Balance game where players roll dice and stack cylinder-shape blocks on the seesaw.
Who's turn blocks falls, he/she is out of game. Player who plays last block wins the game.

These special Golden Ammo pieces are a Kickstarter-exclusive mini expansion for your Crossbows & Catapults game! Once per game, you can fire your Golden Ammo, and if you hit your opponent's general, you win the game immediately!

Race to rescue the animals you need to fill your castles and get the most points. But watch out for the gem-stealing dragon!

Adventure Cats is a family board game for 2-4 players. It is accessible enough for 5-year-olds to enjoy, but strategic enough for adults to enjoy!

In Adventure Cats, players take turns moving their cat miniature from tile to tile on the map. First, drop your gems Mancala-style on adjacent tiles. Then, your cat hops from gem to gem, landing on a tile at the end of the line. Puzzle it out so your cat lands on a tile with an animal token to rescue it! Place rescued animals in one of your castles that has room for that type of animal. Filled castles are worth extra points at the end of the game. Plus, for every filled castle, you get to move the dragon one space at the end of your turn!

When the dragon passes through a cat, it steals a gem. Gems are worth points, so you want to avoid getting hit by the dragon--as well as figure out how to get the other players!

Adventure cats is a co-design with my daughter Verna (7 years old at the time of publishing). She wanted to publish a game with Dad this year. We worked hard on it together and made a game our whole family enjoys! Especially my wife, who always wins.

Part of our goal was to make gameplay exciting and simple enough for my 5-year-old son to be able to play with us and have fun. But also make the game puzzly and deterministic enough to minimize "feel bad" moments that happen due to randomness, and make it so my wife and I can enjoy the gameplay as well. We did our best and hope you like the result!

—description from the designer

This is a reworking of Knight Line for a print and play collection, which ended up different enough to be its own game. There are no stacks, movement follows the 12* protocol, and a line of 5 rather than 4 is required to win

After the starting player adds a piece in their colour to a grid, players take turns adding 2 pieces in their colour to a using chess knights moves from their existing pieces, with the pieces of all players required to be connected (diagonal is OK) at all times. The aim is to form a row of 5 pieces in your colour (in any direction, including diagonal).

The standard game is played on a 13 x 13 grid (medium sized Go board)

Black Orchestra Resistance Expansion is an expansion for the original Black Orchestra game that adds 6 new Conspirators to play and 3 different modules. Each module adds new rules, components, and complexity of the game. Widen the range of difficulties and stories to experience. Any of the new Conspirators can be played with or without any of the expansion modules.

—description from the publisher
family: History: Resistance during WW2 in Europe

description:
In Queteb, players assume the roles of rival architects in ancient Egypt, working together to construct a single, grand pyramid as the pharaoh’s tomb. Though they collaborate on building, only one player will earn the title of the kingdom’s foremost architect.

The game begins with one player placing the first block, after which players alternate turns to add more blocks to the growing structure. Gameplay emphasizes tactical placement and movement, with players “jumping” on blocks to reach higher levels. The first player to complete the pyramid’s fifth level wins. Each architect must think ahead in three dimensions to outmaneuver the other and secure their place at the top!

—description from the designer

Try to build a cube pyramid. The fewer rolls you need to build a pyramid, the more points you score. Over 12 rounds, you try to build as many pyramids as possible. If you have also built a 3 or 4 double in the pyramid, you will receive extra points.

Gleis um Gleis is a free, downloadable tile-laying strategy game. Try to complete railroad lines with five (5) cards in your hand. The longer the completed track, the more points you score. Ideal if you are traveling with the family. There is an extended game mode for a bit more strategy.

Welcome to the city of gems!
In this game, players become skillful gemcutters called "Stars", and with their companions the Jinnees (Star Spirits) will manage a gem workshop.
Polish a precious gem from rough gemstones, then sell it or use it to develop your workshop, in order to fulfill the ultimate dream of offering their masterpiece at the shrine, and be recognized with the coveted title of Al Shira, the Greatest of the Stars.
Go forth! Create sparkling gems with the Jinnees and aim for the top of the stars!

A sidestep on the Brownies series of games, Atelier with Jinnees is a workshop management game for 2 to 4 players in 30 minutes
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
designers: ヨシイケ　シンイチ (Yoshike Shinichi)

description:
Choose your Pet-Nauta, create your battle space and roam the different and colorful planets creating your strategy with one of two goals!!!.... Tame and adopt pets to complete your pet-family or equip yourself with them for the most furry, flea-ridden and bizarre space battle ever!!!

... and you ... Will you choose the way 
of the pet-family or the battle?

How to win?

You can win the game in two ways ... choose the peaceful way and complete your pack by being the first to ADOPT at least ONE PET from each DIFFERENT PLANET into your team ... or use them together with the upgrades to make you stronger and be able to ATTACK your rivals and be the LAST PET-NUT STANDING.
So, CREATE the BATTLE SPACE yourselves, arranging the planets side by side the way you want them and let the game begin!!!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Elige tu Pet-Nauta, crea tu espacio de batalla y recorre los diferentes y coloridos planetas creando tu estrategia con uno de los dos objetivos!!... Domar y adoptar mascotas para completar tu manada o equiparte con ellas para la más peluda, pulgosa y bizarra batalla espacial que existe!!

... y tú ... ¿Elegirás el camino 
de la manada o de la batalla?

¿Cómo ganar?

Puedes ganar el juego de dos formas... elegir el camino pacífico y lograr completar tu manada al ser el primero en ADOPTAR en tu equipo a lo menos UNA MASCOTA de cada DIFERENTE PLANETA ... o usar a estas junto con las mejoras para hacerte más fuerte y poder ATACAR a tus rivales y ser el ÚLTIMO Pet-Nauta EN PIE.
Así que, CREEN el ESPACIO DE BATALLA ustedes mismos, ordenando los planetas uno al lado del otro de la forma que deseen y que comience el juego!!

—description from the publisher

A trivia game with twists, lies and coconuts.

Draw a card and reveal a topic. State how many answers you can list in 1 minute. The next player (or group of players) may pass or raise your bid. If no one wants to raise your bid, you have 1 minute to list your answers out loud.

You can bluff, be genuine or strategically mix fake answers with true ones to fool everyone. If your opponent thinks that you’re lying, they can whoop you with a coconut.

boardgame: Chronicles of Drunagor: Age of Darkness – Dice Tower Hero Class
description:
Available from Dice Tower 2024 Kickstarter Eclipse Pack.

They include the COMMUNITY, ENTERTAIN, REVIEW, and SUPPORT cards.

Dread Nights is a standalone expansion to the 28mm miniature skirmish game Forbidden Psalm, itself based on and fully compatible with the MÖRK BORG RPG system. Players will create a crew of desperate scoundrels and embark upon one of fourteen ready-made scenarios designed to test players’ skill, cunning, foresight, and luck.

Within the full-color, 128-page book you’ll find:
 Rules for building and outfitting your crew of demon-slaying Nightfolk
Random encounters, inclement weather, and infections to ensure no game plays the same
15 fully-statted monsters with unique motivations, keywords, and attacks
Rules for solo and co-operative play
Full conversion rules for both Forbidden Psalm and MÖRK BORG

—description from the publisher

Graveyard Shift is a single-player, 18-card dungeon-crawling game. The goal is the survive through all 17 graveyard cards without losing all of your life!

There is a blue moon rising in the night sky as you arrive for your shift at the local graveyard. A fog rolls in from the forest surrounding the gravestones as you get ready for the night. Suddenly you hear a bellowing noise from somewhere outside. You fill up your trusty lantern with oil, just in case, and light it as you brave out into the night....it is your job to keep the grounds safe, but what will save you from what's out there?

Start by taking your trust lantern, life & oil tokens. Shuffle the graveyard cards and flip one over. Roll the die to determine the outcome of the event on the card, or to defeat a creature of the night; some cards could possess both!
Use the oil tokens to help change the fate of your roll, and survive all the cards without losing your 3 life tokens to win!

This is the first game in "The Graveyard Shift" series, that shares the same title and this edition is nicknamed 'Graveyard'.

—description from the publisher

Embark on an Unforgettable Journey!

Deep within the shadows of ancient lands and forgotten realms, a new chapter awaits the bravest of heroes. The Lost Adventures Quest Pack brings you a thrilling expansion filled with epic quests, legendary battles, and powerful artifacts. The forces of Chaos are rising once more, and only the most daring adventurers can stand against them.

12 Immersive Quests: Traverse through deadly dungeons, haunted castles, and cursed ruins. Each quest is designed to challenge your courage, wits, and strength.
New Enemies and Bosses: Face off against powerful Chaos Warriors, dark sorcerers, and ancient evils that have long been forgotten. Confront Valtrix the Bloodward Sentinel, Kharzak the Warlord, and other fearsome adversaries.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Sparring Dragons: A Postcard Taiwan Conflict
description:
The tensions for Taiwan go hot!
Based on TSR's Hunt for Red October, Sparring Dragons is a postcard-sized reduction of the engine to simulate the conflict. The Taiwanese forces must hold out against the Chinese navy's attempts to land an invasion force on the island and take Taipei. US forces are headed in to help, but they may not be in time.

—description from the designer

As mentioned, the game system uses a condensed version of TSR's The Hunt for Red October. Playable in under an hour. Print-n-Play.

boardgame: Stratheós: Les origines de la mythologie
description:
Discover Stratheós, a captivating strategy game that promises hours of entertainment with family, friends, or colleagues. Dive into the fascinating world of Greek, Viking, and Roman mythologies, where you'll have the opportunity to become the god of strategy. Crush your opponents by deploying varied tactics: be cunning, offensive, fast, or show patience and ambition. Every game is different and provides an opportunity to hone your senses, planning, thinking, patience, and memory.

Stratheós is an engaging game for 2 to 6 players, perfect for sessions of around 20 minutes. Suitable for ages 8 and up, it features evolving rules that ensure every participant can have fun while still having a chance to win.

The Stratheós box set is a true invitation to adventure and discovery. It contains 102 unique cards, each offering a rich and immersive gaming experience. The accompanying 60-page booklet not only provides the game rules but also includes captivating stories highlighting the mythological figures that populate the game's universe.

—description from the publisher

Death on the Rocks is a 1930s murder mystery game for 12-16 guests and one host. A peaceful (if rather peculiar) English village community has been disrupted by a savage murder.

Death on the Rocks is set in 1936, on the notoriously cold and windy Gull Island, just off the north-east coast of England, whose perilous rocks have claimed many a victim. It is 6:30pm on the eve of the winter's Solstice, a traditional day of celebration for the villagers of Little Bleakly – much to the horror of the local Reverend. The wind is howling and the rain is coming down in sheets, but inside the village hall has been cheerfully decorated with brightly coloured flags and bunting.

The needle of the gramophone is poised over 'Music Maestro Please', and many of the villagers have already arrived in the village hall, when Little Bleakly is suddenly plunged into darkness by a power cut. Stumbling along the shadowy and winding paths with candles in their hands, the remaining villagers are on their way when light returns.

The festivities are ready to begin! Except someone is missing: Hugo Malady, the dashing heir to the legendary Malady fortune, who traditionally kickstarts the Winter Solstice Celebrations, is nowhere to be seen! The villagers spread out over the island in search. Soon, a shrill scream is heard from Lighthouse Bay: Freda, the local pub landlady, has discovered the body of Hugo Malady on the beach, lifeless, with a dagger protruding from the back of his neck!

Death on the Rocks lasts for about four hours and is suited to an evening. It is often played over a meal – a finger buffet if possible, to allow guests to circulate and talk in private.

—description from the publisher
The expansion deck for It’s about time includes 50 double-sided cards. A Kickstarter exclusive and not for faint-hearted add-on!

Buku is played on a standard 8x8 chess board with 65 pieces (chips, seeds, cards) of one color. The square a1 (lower left corner) must be dark. Initially each square has one counter.

The game is played by two players, White and Black, facing each other. White is also called Row ("Fila"), Black is also called Column ("Columna"). It is said that white squares are owned by White and black squares by Black.

White starts.

Each turn consists of three steps:

     First a player picks up the contents of a row (horizontal line), if he is White, or of a column (vertical line), if he is Black. It is even permitted to pick up the contents of an empty line, but players must pick up a line completely.
     After that the player sows the counters starting anywhere he wishes. He puts the first counter on the square that was chosen by him, the second counter on an orthogonally adjacent square, the third one on another orthogonally adjacent square and so on. He is permitted to change direction but may not pass the same sqaure twice.
     Finally he captures the contents of any squares of his color, which contain 3 or 4 counters.


The game ends when all squares contain singletons or are empty. The remaining counters are won by the player who owns their square. The player who captured more counters wins the game.

Special rules:

     White gets an additional counter (the 65th one) at his first turn to prevent draws.
     The game also ends when the board position is repeated by a player. Then all the remaining counters are attributed to his opponent.

A connection game played on the curved surface of a cone. The goal is to create a closed loop from the player's edge to the centre, or to connect a path to the centre. The game can be played on a projection, which is isomorphic to the usual Hex rhombus with some cells overlapping or removed.

Hyde & Peek is a game about two rival squirrels preparing for winter. The gameplay centers around a memory-testing tile placement system where two players compete by strategically placing, moving, and flipping tiles on the game area. A special lock token adds another layer of strategy as players must think carefully about which tiles to protect. Players take turns manipulating the tiles on the board, always trying to reveal more of their preferred food type while potentially hiding their opponent's.

The goal is straightforward: be the first squirrel to display five of your food symbols (either acorns or pinecones) on the game area to secure your winter supply and win the game. This creates an engaging balance of tactical placement and memory as players work to achieve their winning condition while preventing their opponent from doing the same.

—description from the designer
A variant of checkers with incomplete information, enchanted dragon eye spaces, and played on a hexagonal board.

Hens and Chicks is a draughts-style game with two types of pieces and a unique capturing mechanic. The winner is the first to land a piece in their opponent's home row or to eliminate all the opposing hens.

The game Fist and the simpler game Hens and Chicks have some mechanics in common, and are both based on Alysa Wishingrad's novel The Verdigris Pawn.
An adaptation of Y on a regular hexagon where players vye to control most of the perimeter of the board.

Hex connection game with captures inspired by Tumbleweed. A cell is controlled by a player if it has more lines of sight to the player's pieces than half of the number neighbours that it has. If an opponent's piece is on your controlled cell, you may capture it by replacing it with your own piece on your turn. The player with a solid connection between their two sides at the start of their turn wins.
A game where enemy pieces mimic your moves while you race to be the first player to reach the back row.
A connection game on an Alquerque board. Place a piece on an eight-way intersection, or two pieces on four-way intersections.

The Smoky Valley is a complex economic and industrial development game set along the historic Lachine Canal in the Griffintown neighborhood of Montréal, Canada.

In The Smoky Valley, you are a 19th-century investor helping to elevate Griffintown as a prominent industrial hub in North America by building and upgrading industries, producing and transforming goods, and shipping products abroad.

Beyond these efforts for Griffintown's prosperity and your personal fortune, you’ll need to stimulate the city’s development to enhance economic activities. You'll also collaborate with other investors to construct the Victoria Bridge and earn valuable bonuses—but only one of you will claim the ultimate reward.

Do you have what it takes to be remembered as the greatest builder in The Smoky Valley?

This new edition of The Smoky Valley offers an entirely fresh experience. All city cards have been enhanced, canal tiles and districts now generate new combos, and canal development opens up new options for direct warehouse and train delivery in McCord and St. Ann.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: D100 Dungeon: The Forgotten Tome Of Curious Rules
description:
D100 Dungeon Book 8: The Forgotten Tome Of Curious Rules adds the following new rules to your D100 Dungeon games:

BUTCHERY – Become skilled at looting the monster parts you would like from table P.

DUAL WIELD – Learn to fight with with two weapons.

WEAPON PROFICIENCY – Become better with the weapons you use in combat.

CHEATING DEATH – Seek out the favour of the gods to resurrect your adventurer when they die.

SECRET PASSAGEWAYS – Secret passages are no longer automatically added, and you have to find them first.

PURSUIT - Chase after those escaping monsters.

MORE MONSTERS – Add new monsters to encounter tables for results below 1 and above 100.

PROPERTY INCREASE – Property prices have risen from those found in Book 4.

NEW RACES – Create a Gnome, Dragon Scar, Half Orc, or a Wood Elf.

SUMMON SPELL CLARIFICATION – A detailed look at how to use the Summon Spell.

SPELL MANA – Switch to using Mana for your magic users.

MAGIC POWER – Suffer Spell Burn damage when failing your spell and scroll tests.

AMMUNITION – Certain ranged weapons have ammunition to use.

THROWN WEAPONS – Details for how to use thrown weapons.

SPECIAL RANGED ATTACKS – Shoot at escaping monsters, and extra rules to take aim.

TREASURED ITEMS – Finer, Greater, Superior, and Exceptional items gain in value.

PARTY PLAY IN THE WORLD BUILDER – Explore the World Builder in a party.

PROPERTY IN THE WORLD BUILDER – Buying property in the World Builder expansion.

DEATH IN THE WORLD BUILDER – Continue building your world after an adventurer dies.

HERBS IN THE WORLD BUILDER – Collect herbs to create some fascinating items.

MINING IN THE WORLD BUILDER – Artisans can mine for their materials.

SKINNING IN THE WORLD BUILDER – Artisans can find materials in the food they hunt.

REINFORCED BELTS – Modify waist items to increase its armour and protection from belt checks.

SPIKED SHIELDS – Add spikes to your shields to deal more damage in combat.

EPIC DUNGEONS – Play dungeons with more powerful monsters, and find epic weapons.

UNIDENTIFIED MAGIC & CURSED ITEMS – Brews, potions, elixirs, and legendary items are found unidentified and could be cursed.

YELLOW EVENTS – In yellow areas the adventurer may have a random event.

EXTRA QUEST TABLE – Play new quests on table QE; with rewards that enhance your items.

HARD MODE & EASY MODE– Play D100 Dungeon with less or more experience rewards.

MAPPING COMPLEX – Replace table M with MC and map some interesting looking dungeons.

MOVEMENT RULES – Rules to move the adventurer using squares, rather than area to area.

HEROIC ITEMS – Adds a new legends table (LA) and some Heroic Items you may find.

ACCOLADES – The adventurer through personal advancement can gain some perks.

MONSTERS AREN’T ALWAYS WHAT THEY SEEM – Monsters are given a personality and the adventurer can earn honour points to spend re-rolling the dice.
Grow root-like formations of stones from seeds. Plants die when they run out of room to grow. The winner is the last player with a plant alive on the board.

Misty: Arco e Flecha 1 is an expansion for the Misty game that offer a new possibility to the game, through the diagonal movement of the card in its window during the activation phase.

This expansions were developed by the author exclusively for Brazil in partnership with PaperGames.

Welcome, rancher, to a hellscape where cattle roam and the stakes are eternal!
Construct your own infernal domain, piece by hellish piece. Master the terrain, raise your herds, and defend what's rightfully yours. Because in Hell, it's not just about ranching; it's about reigning.

Worship, wrangle, and beef up your empire with every roll, every trade. But beware – in Hell, not all deals are fair. Harness the power of blood, bone, fire, brimstone, iron, and the Devil himself. But remember, every card has its price. And with Satan always looming, you must make the right moves or feel his wrath.

Hell is full of twists and turns. Embrace spite, cast curses, and outwit your opponents in a game of diabolical strategy. For those with true grit, rewards await. Build the longest fence, command the largest posse, and show Hell you're its top rancher.

In Cattlers of Satan, the wild west meets the fiery depths of the underworld. Rancher, are you ready to stake your claim in Hell?

Cattlers of Satan is a resource-based trading game in which you and your friends become ranchers in hell, wrangling demonic cattle, building ranches, and outwitting your enemies. The game involves strategic placement, resource management, and a bit of luck as you roll dice, trade elements, and navigate infernal challenges to become the top rancher in the underworld.

Each rancher aims to build up their territory by placing herds, fences, and ranches on the board. Your main goal is to collect Steak Points, which you earn by expanding your livestock, using Devil Cards, and achieving special milestones. Every turn offers opportunities to gather the elements of blood, fire, brimstone, iron, and bone; trade with others; and build up your hellish domain. You’ll need to think strategically about where to place your herds and fences, how to best use your resources, and how to thwart your rivals.

You acquire Steak Points in several ways:

Building Fences, Herds, and Ranches: each new structure adds to your total Steak Points.

Playing Devil Cards: some cards offer Steak Points directly or give you powerful abilities to boost your game.

Ranching Rewards: special milestones like the Longest Fence Line and Largest Posse give bonus Steak Points.

The first rancher to accumulate 10 Steak Points on their turn wins the game. It’s all about balancing your element collection, smart trading, and timely building to outmaneuver your opponents and claim victory in Hell!

—description from the publisher

Tree Folk is a tile-flipping puzzle game with a simple, yet challenging resource management mechanism.

Solo / 2-4 player co-op / 2 player versus


     Start with the trunk tile.
     With 5 random branches in your possession, work your way up.
     Buy new branches using resources inside your tree.
     Complete the tree to win, without running out of resources or obstructing other branches.
     Determine your score using the value on closing branches.


Grow your own gnome home ??

—description from the designer

boardgame: Broken Logic: A Long Road Expansion
description:
It's the summer of 1985, the British 1st Armored Division find themselves in a epic struggle against the Soviet invasion of West Germany. Their job? Counterattack the base of the Soviet salient piercing NATO lines.

Yet, amidst the burning tanks and smoking earth of their counterattack, they stumble upon something far more sinister than they could have imagined. Strange apparitions emerge from the shadows—ghastly Soviet soldiers, resurrected to serve once more. Freakish Soviet vehicles tear across the land, tracks screeching and cannons unleashing unearthly bursts of energy against the British armor. The English soldiers find themselves face-to-face with horrors that defy all reason, a nightmarish threat beyond the scope of any battlefield they had known. They find themselves facing the evil Soviet Regiment X.

Welcome to Broken Logic, the haunting second expansion to the award-winning game The Long Road.  A labor of love, the Long Road has earned praise for its gritty, platoon-level, 1985-era combat intertwined with dark supernatural twists. Make no mistake, the world has turned upside down; British infantry fight side-by-side with lycan clans—some in human form, others transformed into beasts. Even the vampiric Eagle Eyrie Coven has come out of hiding, wielding futuristic weaponry against NATO and Warsaw Pact forces alike.

From British Challengers to American Abrams, NATO’s arsenal seems unstoppable, but the Soviets have unlocked weaponry far more terrifying. Long rumored, thin seams between parallel worlds have indeed been discovered—and exploited. With this newfound knowledge, Soviet forces rip through reality’s fabric, calling forth unspeakable entities to bolster their ranks.

And that’s only the beginning. Soviet engineers have unveiled advanced weaponry that defies comprehension: armored vehicles firing deadly, energy spheres that ricochet into multiple targets, T-72 tanks armed with colossal 135mm cannons, potent enough to obliterate enemy armor… even if they sometimes detonate from the sheer force of firing, and Russian 10-meter-tall bipedal multi-weapon platforms—easy to target but devastatingly armed—that can decimate a British tank troop in mere seconds.

Their infantry? Proceeded by the ravenous Hell Hounds, they attack without fear. Stalin’s old mantra, “Quantity has a quality all its own,” takes on a twisted new meaning. Soviet scientists have mastered the grim art of reanimation, sending the fallen back into the fray as “Reborn.” These soldiers—not dead, yet not alive—fight nearly as well as the living, adding an eerie and relentless advantage to the Soviet front.

Nothing here is logical. Nothing makes sense. Or as one battle-worn soldier said, “Our logic is broken.”

Step into the madness with Broken Logic, the expansion that will push you into the unknown and beyond.

Gameplay

Platoon-level combat: most counters represent 3-4 vehicles, 30 soldiers. Hexes are 150 meters.
Bigger-than-life Heroes: Single-man counters (Legends, Heroes, and Commanders) have special abilities that can turn the tide of battle.
Interactive sequence of play. Little down time.
Powerful Action Cards ramp up your attack, allow paranormal forces to use crazy abilities, or just give players the opportunity to wreck their opponent’s plans with “Take that!” card play.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to the Lands of Arran, where you will compete to attract dwarves of different orders — Shield, Forge, Temple, Talion, Wanderer — to your service, with each character having special skills. Manage your coins well to outbid others and boost your wealth for a long-term advantage.

Nains marries the gameplay of Nidavellir with the comic serial universe of Nains from author Nicolas Jarry and publisher Éditions Soleil, challenging players to discover new strategies and new ways to score.

In Super Fantasy Brawl: Reborn, players select a team of three champions and combine their unique action cards to create a synergistic action deck. The game is faction-free, so players can choose any combination of champions they wish. Each turn, the players use their hand of action cards to maneuver, attack, displace enemies, and claim objectives to score victory points.

However, a player scores an objective only at the beginning of their turn, giving you a chance to disrupt their plans and position your champions to score the objective for yourself...assuming they don't manage to disrupt you. As soon as a player has 5+ points, they win.
Released as part of the 2023 Kickstarter bundle for Kabuto Sumo Sakura Slam, this promo adds a new wrestler named Dr. Bibble and features the game's first metal piece.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
We Go Way Back is designed to be played with family and close friends. You will rely on your history with each of the other players as you create specific clues based on shared memories, inside jokes, and personal details.

At the start of each round, players are secretly assigned another player. Next, a category is selected (such as movies, sports, or music). Using the category as a guideline, each player creates a clue which is read out loud. Players want their assigned player to know the clue connects to them, but do not want other players to make that connection.

—description from the designer

The Spud-pocalypse has arrived, and only the bravest will survive!

In this thrilling sequel, the battle for potatoes heats up with the arrival of the Four Horsemen of the Spud-pocalypse. "Where the Potatoes Burn: Spud-pocalypse" is a fast-paced, chaotically fun card game for the whole family. Watch out! Every move could unleash chaos and the revenge of your opponents. With new apocalyptic potatoes, the fun is guaranteed at every turn.

The Four Horsemen bring along the Spud-pocalypse Dice, adding even more excitement and madness to the game.

The first player to reach the golden potato goal or to collect all four Horsemen of the Spud-pocalypse wins the game.

On your turn, you can play as many action cards as you want, unless you play an alarm card, which will automatically end your turn. You can trade in three matching potato cards for a golden potato.

When your turn ends, draw one card.

Can you survive the Spud-pocalypse and emerge as the ultimate champion in this one-of-a-kind competition? Find out now and get ready for the most chaotic and competitive fun!

—description from the publisher

¡El Apapalipsis ha llegado y solo los más valientes sobrevivirán!

En esta emocionante secuela, la batalla por las papas se intensifica con la llegada de los 4 jinetes del Apapalipsis. "Donde las Papas Queman: Apapalipsis" es un juego de cartas frenético y caóticamente divertido para toda la familia. ¡Cuidado! Cada movimiento puede desatar el caos y la venganza de tus oponentes. Con nuevas papas apocalípticas, la diversión está garantizada en cada turno.

Los 4 jinetes del Apapalipsis traen consigo el dado del Apapalipsis, agregando aún más emoción y locura al juego.

El primer jugador que alcance el objetivo de papas de Oro o logre tener en la mano los 4 jinetes del Apapalipsis gana el juego.

En tu turno puedes jugar todas las cartas de acción que quieras, a menos que juegues una carta de alarma que finalizan tu turno automaticamente. En tu turno puedes puedes cambiar tres cartas de papa del mismo valor para ganar una papa de oro.
Al terminar tu turno, robas una carta.

¿Podrás superar el Apapalipsis y ser el vencedor en esta competencia sin igual? ¡Descúbrelo ahora y prepárate para la diversión más caótica y competitiva!

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

Misty: Arco e Flecha 2 is an expansion for the Misty game that offer a new possibility to the game, through the diagonal movement of the card in its window during the activation phase.

This expansions were developed by the author exclusively for Brazil in partnership with PaperGames.
A mini-expansion that adds 6 new patrons. The Singaporeans have powers that upgrade cards to the next number up.

Kangaroo Rush (カンガルーラッシュ) is a memo card game from Japan. Players take turns to record the fastest round arranging the memo cards to deliver boxing combos.

Kangaroo Rush is a game to compete in your shadowboxing speed. The players, as promising shadow boxers in the world of kangaroos, compete for speed and beauty of combos to win the shadow boxing contest and become the champion.

Use a stopwatch to measure the time it takes to perform the moves on the rush cards in order. Take turns performing the same combo, and the person with the fastest time scores points.

Occasional punches from your opponents may interrupt your moves, however.
Aim for victory by scoring the most points over 4 rounds.
-publisher's summary
designers: Kenta Murayama (ムラヤマ　ケンタ)

boardgame: Northern Fleet: A Proper Naval Game – 1983
description:
Northern Fleet is a print-and-play board game simulating a hypothetical clash between the the USSR and NATO in the Atlantic Ocean and Norwegian Sea in 1983.

Key Points

     Simple and playable rules, an average scenario can be played in approximately 3 hours.
     The core game is developed and ready to go. Delivery guaranteed 2-3 weeks after the Kickstarter ends
     A wide range of scenarios, the shortest takes approximately an hour, leading to a grand campaign including the full Soviet and NATO order of battle.
     Meticulously researched. Literally 100's of hours have been spent to ensure game play is realistic, unit stats are correct and both sides' order of battle (OB) is accurate.

死名をご記入ください (Please write DEATH name) is a word game from Japan. Whilst the publisher has given it a horror theme, it is meant to be a party game, and players can choose any kind of topic they like. In addition, the publisher encourages players outside Japan to play, as the gameplay is based on alphabetic order, not on the language itself.

--publsisher's summary
The objective of this word game is to give names (dead names) to other players and make them lose points in order to satisfy the NG conditions of the given theme.
The conditions of the subject are simple and based on the order of the syllabary, such as “The second from the front in alphabetical order loses points”.

Once you have written the name to give to your opponent, it is trade time! Look at the “dead name” that has been passed around from the person next to you, and think, “This dead name looks okay! If you think this name is dangerous, you can ask for another trade. The player who fulfills the NG condition from the death name cards held by all players loses a point.

Who will be the last survivor after 7 orders...?

出されるお題のNG条件を満たすように、他プレイヤーに名前（死名）をつけて失点させることが目的のワードゲームです。
お題の条件はシンプルで「五十音順で前から２番目は失点」のように五十音の順番を基準にしたものです。

相手に渡す名前を書いたらトレードタイム！隣の人から回ってきた「死名」を見て、この死名なら大丈夫そうだ！と思ったらキープ、この死名は危なそう、と思ったら再トレードを希望しましょう。全員が持っている死名札からNG条件を満たしたプレイヤーが失点します。

7つの命令を終えた後、最後に生き残るのは誰でしょうか…？

フラワーマルシェ (Flower Marche) is a card game from Japan. The designer has simplified their previous game Flower Market into a lighter card game version but kept the tactical choices.

--publisher summary
This is a flower shop in the forest. Inhabitants of the forest come here for beautiful flowers.
Who will be the florist who can make the most profit?

This game is a card game version of Flower Market. The Dutch auction element of the board game version has been removed and the focus of the game is on collecting the cards you want before the other players.

The elements of the board game version are retained, such as advertising decreases profits and excess inventory decreases profits. The early bird gets the worm, as does the selection of customers to purchase and sell flowers to. It's a cute little box and simple rules, but it's a game of interaction bees.

ここは森の花屋さん。森の住人たちがきれいな花を求めて訪れます。
もっとも多くの利益をあげられる花屋さんは誰でしょうか。

このゲームはフラワーマーケットのカードゲーム版です。
ボードゲーム版でのダッチオークションの要素をなくし、欲しいカードを他のプレイヤーより先に集めることにフォーカスをあてたゲームにしました。
宣伝をすると利益は減り、在庫を余らせると利益が減るといった要素はボードゲーム版を踏襲しています。花の仕入れや売るお客様の選択は早いもの勝ちです。かわいい小箱でシンプルなルールですが、インタラクションバチバチのゲームです。
publishers: さとーふぁみりあ (Sato Familie)

description: A food-themed version of competitive patience or spite and malice. Players pick a forty-card deck: Tamale, Italian Beef, Deep Dish Pizza, or Chicago Style Hot Dog. The goal is to be the first to play all of one's cards on three building piles from one's lunch and shuffle pile.
family: Traditional Card Games: Spite and Malice

description:
Introduction:Scauver (from Old English: sċēawere, meaning "watcher", "mirror" or "buffon") is a card game for two to four players. It uses a French deck of 40 cards, excluding the 8s, 9s, and 10s. Each player has enough chips to bet, in an amount agreed upon by the players beforehand. This game began to be created in 2016 by Saïd Galdseid, but it took until 2024 to perfect its rules.

				
				
					Definition:
     The field is the area of play for each player where cards are placed face up.
     A flank is a group of cards that divides the field into two sections: left and right. A flank can contain one or more cards.
     The vanguard is a subgroup of up to three cards within each flank, chosen at the end of the round. A vanguard can contain a single card. The value of a vanguard is the total sum of its cards.
     A vanguard is considered greater if it contains more cards than the opposing vanguard, which will be the lesser.
     A link is a combination of cards formed by each player's vanguards. For a link to be valid, both vanguards must have the same value and must not share any numbers. This value defines the value of the link.
     An amphora is a mandatory initial bet that must be made before the start of each round. The player who shuffles and the player to their left, who cuts, are the only ones who place the amphora. In tournament mode, the value of the amphora may increase every specified number of rounds, as agreed upon by the players before the game starts.


				
				
					Card values:The J, Q, and K cards are worth 8, 9, and 10 respectively, although they can optionally be replaced by the 8, 9, and 10 if the face cards are to be excluded. Aces are worth 1, and the other cards are worth their face value.

				
				
					Scoring the field:The scoring of the field is the difference between the sums of the flanks, multiplied by the value of the chosen vanguards. The player with the lowest score wins the round, and in case of a tie, the hierarchy of the links is applied in order of importance.

				
				
					Link hierarchy:The strength of a link is determined in order of importance:


     Size: by the value of the link.
     Length: by the number of cards in the vanguards (from lowest to highest: 2 to 1, 2 to 2, 3 to 1, 3 to 2, and 3 to 3).
     Height: by the highest card in the greater vanguard, continuing to the lesser if necessary.


				
				
					Initial distribution:Before the first round, the dealer is chosen randomly, who deals five cards to each player. Each player selects three cards and places them face up, while the remaining two stay in hand. The dealer rotates each round, moving to the left.

				
				
					Turns:The player to the left of the dealer starts his turn, performing the following actions in order:


     Draw a card from the deck and place it with your cards in hand.
     Bet, fold, or proceed to the next step.
     Choose one of the cards from your hand and place it face up in either of your two flanks.
     Place your two remaining cards, one in each flank, until four cards are completed in both flanks, ending the round with a final bet.
     Pass the turn to the player on your left.


				
				
					Betting:Before dealing the cards, the amphoras are placed. If a player decides to bet, the other players can:


     Accept the bet by saying "I'm in."
     Reject the bet by saying "I'm out," and lose the round.
     Raise the bet by saying "I raise."


When all players have accepted the bet, the chips accumulate in the "pot." The player who wins the round takes all the chips from the pot.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when a player no longer has enough chips to pay in the exchanges and is eliminated. The winner is the last player remaining in the game.

—description from the designer

Biblical Connections, through much laughter and debate, both challenges your knowledge of the Bible and helps you and your friends grow in your understanding of the overall Biblical narrative and how it all connects! 
There are two core ways to play: “Grafted” a 1-6 player solo/cooperative game mode, and “Sower” a 2-6 player competitive
game mode.

Table Setup:
Shuffle the deck. Reveal 7 cards from the deck, face up, in the center of the playing area to form the Seed Store.
Deal 7 cards to each player (or 4 cards for a short game). Keep your cards in your hands, secret from the other players. Stack the rest of the deck face down to form the Draw Deck.

Gameplay:
On your turn

     Play, Grab and Connect – Take one card from your Hand and any one card from the Seed Store, then connect them along any side to a card on the table in front of you.
     Explain your connection to the other players. Everyone needs to agree that the connection can be found in the Bible.
     Refill Seed Store back to 7 cards.


Your objective is to connect the most cards into the biggest group or ‘tree’ of connections you can! The game ends when you play all cards in your Hand, the player with the most connection points wins!

—description from the publisher

As harsh blizzard descends upon Rodentale, colder and crueler than ever before, great famine also comes to its inhabitants. Creatures scurried to gather as many crops as they can to survive the crisis. Spotting the opportunity, the Merchant Guild of Rodentale held a competition where its members collect and deliver produce to villages in order to boost sales, help everyone stock up on food, and amass as many gold coins as they could to win and be given the title of THE MIGHTY MERCHANT OF RODENTALE!!

Wagoner of the Rodentale is a gateway family game for 2-5 players. The players are the representatives of the Rodentale's Merchant Guild, who need to collect the most gold to become the one who triumphs in this game!

In each turn, the player must choose to spend their actions moving their character, collecting produce, delivering them to the villages, and collecting the most gold! The winner of this competition will be entitled to THE MIGHTY MERCHANT OF RODENTALE!!

—description from the publisher

Game Overview
In Tasty Korea Restaurant, you take on the role of a chef serving customized dishes to guests. Your goal is to gather the right dishes your guests desire, serve them, and aim for the highest score by the game’s end!

Setup
At the beginning, each player receives 3 Guest Cards, which are placed face-up in front of them. Each player also receives 2 Goal Cards. Players look at the Guest Cards in play and choose the Goal Card that best aligns with their strategy, returning the other Goal Card to the deck.

Gameplay
On each turn, players can draw the top card from the main deck—composed of Dish Cards and Event Cards—and place it face-up in the shared space. Players may continue drawing cards one at a time. At any point during their turn, players may pick up one card from the shared space or the newly drawn cards to add to their hand. However, if two Dish Cards with the same name appear in the space, the player’s turn ends immediately, and they do not get to pick up any cards that turn.

If a player successfully picks up a Dish Card without triggering a duplicate, they have the following additional actions available:

Serve: If they have the necessary Dish Cards to fulfill one of their Guest Cards, they can "serve" the guest, scoring that card. When they serve, players announce “Serve!”
Acquire a New Guest: Players may draw an additional Guest Card to serve later. Be cautious, as unserved Guest Cards at the end of the game will count against your score by their point value.
Throughout the game, players will also encounter various Event Cards, which add fun surprises to the gameplay.

End of Game
When the main deck is completely drawn, each player has one last chance to serve their guests. The game then ends, and players total up their scores by combining the points from served Guest Cards and collected Dish Cards. The player with the highest score wins!

—description from the designer

50 extremely amazing puzzles about true records: Everything here is bigger, higher, faster, further, somehow different... but extremely record-breaking! Whether it's gigantic breakfast eggs, huge snowball fights or killer icebergs. And the most fascinating thing is: these racing stories are 100% true!

This is how it's played:

The guessing team variant:
The black stories junior are exciting puzzle stories on cards that have to be cracked together. Only the expedition leader knows the solution to the riddle, as he/she reads the riddle on the front out loud and is also allowed to study the solution on the back in detail. The adventurers now try to find the solution step by step by asking questions, guessing and puzzling.

Riddle pro game variant:
One puzzle card is solved per game. Only the game master knows the solution and the guessing team approaches it step by step by asking questions, guessing and puzzling ... as usual! But watch out: Particularly clever questions that lead to a hot clue are rewarded with point chips. The guessing fox who solves the puzzle receives an additional 3 point chips. Whoever has collected the most point chips at the end of the game wins!

Boost the party vibe with Mini 0–100 Party! This festive, fast-paced game is perfect for any party-loving group. Whether you're planning a chill night in or gearing up for a full-blown celebration, this game is sure to make it an unforgettable evening together. Mini 0–100 Party introduces an exciting twist with 25 video questions, some featuring celebrities! The game’s questions are party-themed, with a fun mix of entertainment, celebrity trivia, and music to amp up the mood. There are also questions about drinks and a bit of cheeky humor.

Malumière is a trick-taking game where cards are played face down.
The back of each card shows a suit-related symbol, making the suit visible.
The size of the symbol indicates the card's number, adding a visual twist to the gameplay.
Players predict the number of tricks they will take and score points based on their bids.

If a tree falls in a forest and no one is around to hear it, does it make a sound?

The year is 2015. Deep within the Nozama rainforest, the uncontacted Nozama Tribe is a society obscured from modern civilisation. Among the tribe’s unique customs and beliefs is its reverence for the Monkey Spirit, an all-powerful force believed to bring both fortune and punishment. Recently, mysterious events have spread fear throughout the tribe, as more of its members suffer from what they believe to be the Monkey Spirit’s curse.
 On the night of the tribe’s ritual to worship the Monkey Spirit, the tribespeople discover their leader’s lifeless body bearing claw-like marks across his back – the unmistakable signs of the Monkey Spirit’s wrath.
 Did the Monkey Spirit exact vengeance, or is there a more sinister human scheme at play? As shadows lengthen and suspicion mounts, the secret behind the leader’s death waits to be uncovered.

Content warnings: violence, death, murder

Welcome to Windcrest, an ever-growing city where the misty canals are graced by majestic flying whales that serve as taxis and freight carriers. Your job is to help make living in and traveling across this city more accessible for the citizens while exporting trade goods to increase the wealth of the nation.

In Canals of Windcrest, players will draft and play location cards that will gain them resources and special actions. Resources are used to manufacture export goods and construct buildings that will enhance the locations where they are built. Navigate the canals with whale taxis to transport citizens while also delivering export goods. Players will also have investors to help them in various ways, including special powers and end game scoring bonuses.

Every successful endeavor will earn you prestige. Can you master the use of your whale taxis and prove your worth to the nation?

—description from the publisher

description:

Five Personal Discovery Promo Cards

A 2-player wargame of the battle that won Texas independence from Mexico. The untrained Texian militia led by Sam Houston defeated a professional army under an over-confident Generalissimo Santa Anna just six weeks after the fall of the Alamo. Had the Mexican army not taken a siesta that afternoon and let down its guard or if Santa Anna had not been occupied with a servant girl in his tent at the time of the Texian attack, the outcome of the battle of San Jacinto might have been very different, especially since General Cos has just arrived with hundreds of reinforcements.

The game is a classic hex-and-counter affair, with about 20-30 counters per side. There are 5 scenarios:

Day One. 20th April 1836. Covers the skirmish fighting on the first day. This can get out of hand and trigger a full-scale battle.

Day Two. 21st April 1836. This is the 18 minutes of fun as the Texians try to overrun the Mexican camp.

The Full Battle. Covers day 1 and day 2, with the effects of the first day carrying over to the next day.

Hypothetical Day Two. This supposes that Santa Anna had not stood his men down and was ready for the Texian assault.

Hypothetical Day Three. Sam Houston was not keen on attacking. If he didn't attack on day 2, Santa Anna's fully rested army gets its chance on day 3.

—description from the publisher

Unify is a simple unification game. It is the sister game to Coalesce.

Components
1 Hexagonal board, size 5
30 Black pieces
30 White pieces
10 Pieces in a third, neutral colour

Contract phase
Player 1 places any number of neutral pieces on the board.
Player 2 chooses a role: Unify (Black) or Divide (White).

Play
Players alternate playing stones, starting with Divide.
If Unify connects all neutral stones, via stones of their colour, they win.
If unification becomes impossible, Divide wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Summoner Wars (Second Edition): Tress Promo Card
description:
Originally available from Dice Tower 2024 Kickstarter Comet Pack.

Description:
Adapt
This unit cannot be targeted by an attack more than once per turn.

Overcome
When this unit attacks an adjacent unit, it has its strength increased by the base strength of its target.

Start with go, distill it down to its essence using the Symple protocol, and you have Itsy.

The rules are very short:

A group is a maximal set of orthogonally adjacent pieces of a single colour. Groups can never merge.

Each turn, in order:


     Grow: Add a piece adjacent to each of your existing groups
     Spawn: Add a piece to the board, forming a new group.


If you cannot complete your turn (usually because a group is surrounded), you lose.

boardgame: Tales from the Red Dragon Inn: 2024 Dice Tower Promo Cards
description:
Originally available from Dice Tower 2024 Kickstarter Comet Pack.

New ally and reaction cards, along with a new companion.

Ally: Pallyhedron
Reaction: The Hat of Vasel
Companion: Pallyhedron

Play
On the first turn, white makes one move; on each subsequent turn, take two moves (12* protocol).

Each move, select one of your pieces and move it in a straight line, over empty spaces only.

At the end of your move, place a neutral piece in your starting space.If you have no legal moves (and only then), pass.

Goal:
When both players pass in succession, the game ends. Players score according to the quantity of neutral pieces adjacent to their pieces. 
The player with the highest score wins.

Originally available from Dice Tower 2024 Kickstarter Comet Pack.

Card Description:
After you use the scoundrel below this

Summary
Inspired by Slitherlink, but ended up more like Libraria.

Setup
Put a random number in each square of the grid.

Play
Each turn, draw a line on the edge of an unclaimed square, or pass. After placing, check any neighbouring squares to see if they are claimed; a square is claimed if it has exactly as many neighbouring lines as the number in it. The player with the most neighbouring lines claims the number. In the case of a tie, the non-moving player gets the number.

Goal
The game ends when both players pass in succession. Players add their claimed numbers, and the highest score wins. Draws are possible, but rare.

Balance
To offset the first player advantage, 1.5 komi (points given to player 2 to start with) seems to work well.

Name
SLLUG is kinda short for SlitherLink-Like Game.

Play as a fungus, building a symbiotic relationship with the roots of a mighty tree.

Building on a tradition of hexagon based strategy games, Fungopia plays to the strengths of classic designs with tightly balanced mechanics and concise playtime of around 30-60 minutes.

You will use spores to spread your hyphy and grow your mycelial network as you grow mushrooms and compete for space and resources.

Instead of roads, walls or castles you will build hyphy and mushrooms.

The game is designed to be easy to learn but hard to master, with branching decisions.

—description from the designer

Murder on the Rocks is an exhilarating card-based tabletop murder mystery game that invites you to an elegant yet eerie masquerade party, where suspense and intrigue are the guests of honor. As the night unfolds, one of the attendees has met an untimely demise, and the responsibility to uncover the identity of the culprit falls to you and your fellow guests. Each player will have to navigate a web of secrets, subtle clues, and misleading deceptions in order to piece together the truth.

The game combines thrilling deduction and strategic play as players investigate the crime, gather evidence, and reveal hidden motives. With every turn, the tension mounts as you analyze the vague center clues, interpret mysterious images, and attempt to decipher the intentions of your fellow players. Is someone leading you astray, or are they an innocent bystander caught in a tangle of suspicion?

Murder on the Rocks features rich storytelling, dynamic scenarios, and cocktail-themed twists that set the mood for every playthrough. Whether you prefer serious investigations or humorous antics, this game will challenge your wit, push your alliances to the limit, and keep you guessing until the very end. Put on your mask, sharpen your deductive skills, and get ready for a night of secrets, suspense, and surprise revelations!

—description from the designer

A darkly comedic board game that takes its players on a satirical journey through the absurd world of celebrities, politics and influence. Create your very own super-star persona with plenty of money, fans and scandals along the way in this cynical celebration of the fame obsessed culture we all know and love.

Gameplay Overview: 
Players begin the game by selecting a class for their character (ex. a Reality TV Star or Podcaster ). 

You may start the game without any money, fans, or any talent whatsoever, but when has that ever stopped someone from thinking they should be rich and famous? 

Before you know it, your character will be battling internet trolls, starting a charity, and paying a lawyer to make those criminal charges go away. And that may all just be on your first turn!

Throughout the game, players will build and add to their character. They could have valuable skills like being Incredibly Good Looking or having No Self-Respect. Advantages like having a Cult-Like Fanbase or a Powerful Family make life a breeze.

No wealthy relatives? No problem! Add some revenue with a Sponsorship Deal or team up with another player to Buy a Sports Team.

As players climb the levels of Influence, Fame, and Power, they will also have to avoid scandals or being Canceled for all sorts of terrible things along the way. You might get in trouble for being a Conspiracy Theorist or having your DMs Get Released will set you back in the game.

But just like in real life, if you have enough fans and money, you can weasel your way out of any scandal. 

Players will have lots of opportunities to gain fans and money, maybe with a Fan Give-Away, or be a Guest On Late Night TV. Players will also get the chance to work with the other players in the game, perhaps by joining them in a Celebrity Boxing match on Pay-Per-View, or to attack players by sending in all your fans to hate on them. 

The game ends when a player reaches the final levels of Power and enters the Final Campaign where if they have enough money and fans they get to become President!

Categories: 
Engine Building Game
Eurogame
RPG Board Game
Humor

A fun brain twister and conversation starter!

What is the opposite of a bridge? What is the opposite of a politician? What is the opposite of ice cream?

Contrarium is a combination of a riddle, a debate or conversation game, and a fun and humourous activity. Players are asked for the opposite of various things. What is the opposite of toast? What is the opposite of ice cream? What is the opposite of a premonition? There are no right or wrong answers. Players try to beat each other by matching their answers to the most common answers which are listed on the cards. It takes only a minute to learn and 10–30 minutes to play, no complicated rules or set-up – just jump straight in and play! Perfect for 2–6 players and can be played in teams with a big group.

The dinosaurs are back! Not only do they roar and run, but they also deliver meals using the funniest transportation.
In Dino Delivery, our prehistoric friends deliver delicious dishes and drinks around the big city.
Help the dinosaurs deliver orders at full speed.

PURPOSE OF THE GAME
In this game, your mission is for the dinosaurs to deliver their food orders as quickly as possible.
To do this, you will have a team of dinosaurs trained in
delivery, who will use their maximum skills. 
The winner of the game will be the player who manages to deliver all the orders for five dinosaurs first.

—description from the publisher (translated)

¡Los dinosaurios han regresado! No sólo rugen y corren, sino que también hacen delivery de comidas usando los transportes más divertidos.
En “Dino Delivery”, nuestros amigos prehistóricos reparten deliciosos platos y bebidas por la gran ciudad.
Ayuda a los dinosaurios a entregar pedidos a toda velocidad.

OBJETIVO DEL JUEGO
En este juego, tu misión es que los dinosaurios entreguen sus pedidos de comida lo más pronto posible. Para ello, contarás con un equipo de dinosaurios entrenados en
delivery, que usarán sus máximas habilidades. 
Será el ganador de la partida aquel jugador que logre entregar primero todos los pedidos de cinco dinosaurios.

—description from the publisher

As a holiday gift calendar, you’ll get part of the game every day until Christmas – ready to play either solo, or against a friend!

boardgame: War for Chicken Island: Not-A-Dodo Promo Card
description:
Originally available from Dice Tower 2024 Kickstarter Doppler Pack.

Card Description
Not-a-Dodo may band with any Battle Car you control as if it had banding (the Battle Car cannot band with other units).

Not-a-Dodo gets +1 speed, strength and armor while banding with a Battle Car.

Originally available from Dice Tower 2024 Kickstarter Doppler Pack.

Card Description
At the start of the game, players agree on using either side of this card.

Side 1: At the end of the game, whoever has the most ISOLATED eggs counts as having spawned 2 extra eggs.  An egg is isolated if it is not adjacent to any other eggs of the same species.

Side 2: At the end of the game, each player who has spawned an egg on a tile of each type of TERRAIN and each type of NEST counts as having spawned 2 extra eggs.

Welcome to Hell. We hope you brought your short game.

In Mini-Golf in Hell, you’re an infernal architect competing to build the most diabolically ironic mini-golf course this side of the River Styx.

Using a wicked mix of worker placement, resource management, and tactical schadenfreude, you’ll draft hazards like Venomous Volcanoes of Fast Food, Blistering Tornadoes of Lawyers, and Mountains of Politicians to torment your unlucky visitors—who, naturally, must suffer for eternity.

Assign your minions to the underworld’s most ironic construction tasks, assemble torturous holes with hazard tokens, and lure damned souls to their doom. Gain brimstone (Hell’s currency, of course) to fuel your twisted ambitions, and score victory points by matching visitors to the most thematically fitting punishments.

Visitors will walk any path you build—but they only suffer when the hazard matches their personal hell. No matching color = no punishment. Build wickedly.

Efficiently manage your course layout, leverage your ever-growing crew of devilish meeples, and strategically play bonus cards that offer hellish one-time powers. When the fires settle, the player with the most points wins... everyone else just roasts.

Bring Jackbox Games Fakin’ It online game to life with the new card game version! A party game that challenges players to try to identify which of their friends is a lying liar. One player among the group will be a ‘Faker’ who must try to blend in with the group.

CAN YOU DECEIVE YOUR FRIENDS? Be a master of deception when you're named the Faker, and a keen sleuth to uncover who among your friends is faking it.

HOW TO PLAY. A card with a task written on it will be passed around the group for each player to read, using special secret wallets. But, watch out! One player’s secret wallet won’t reveal what the card says, so they gotta fake it!

PLAY WITH 5 CATEGORIES. Hands of Truth; Players must either raise their hand up or keep their hand down. Number Pressure; Raise a certain number of fingers, between 1-10. You Gotta Point; Point at another player, or even yourself. Face Value; Make a facial expression. Text You Up - Each player must answer the question on their card out loud, truthfully.

—description from the publisher

NEW ALL NIGHT LONG CONTENT. Explore the brand new edgy content in the ‘All Night Long’ adaptation of the classic Fakin’ it game. Packed with risqué and spicy tasks to elevate the fun!

CAN YOU DECEIVE YOUR FRIENDS?: Be a master of deception when you're named the Faker, and a keen sleuth to uncover who among your friends is faking it!

You Gotta Point; Point at another player, or even yourself. Face Value; Make a facial expression. Text You Up - Each player must answer the question on their card out loud, truthfully.

—description from the publisher

In Roshi, you will place, slide, and push pieces on the board in order to be the first to build a roshi - a 5-in-a-row that includes the roshi stone.  The first player to create a roshi wins the game.

On a player’s turn, complete up to two different actions: place a piece, push an opponent's piece, or slide a piece.  The actions can be completed in any order.

Place one of your game pieces on any empty space.  Push one piece with one of your own. You can only push a piece in a straight line horizontally or vertically, and it can only be pushed one space.  You may push your own piece, your opponent’s piece, or the roshi stone. Slide one of your pieces by moving it from one space to another.  A piece can slide freely around the board through as many spaces as possible.

The roshi stone can be pushed one space, but it can never be pushed outside the yellow roshi zone.  REMEMBER, you are only able to push one piece, and this includes the roshi stone.  The first player to create a roshi - a 5-in-a-row that includes the roshi stone - wins the game.  The roshi can be vertical, horizontal, or diagonal.

—description from the publisher

Gold Panners is a simple game that is played with a standard deck of 52 cards. In it, players are prospectors, panning for gold in four rivers, each one designated by one of the four suits. The object of the game is to accrue the most points by striking gold (taking tricks), and selling that gold at the current price. With each new strike, the supply goes down, the price goes up, and the more money (points) can be made. Do you have what it takes to become the richest prospector in these hills? Well, talk is cheap, and the hills have eyes! Good luck. (You'll need it, LOL.)

—description from the designer

A one page, print-n-play dice-rolling game that pits two Cold War SSN's against each other. To send your opponent to the bottom you must use twelve dice to obtain specific dice results and apply them to "pools" representing Detection, Weapons, and Countermeasures. The first player to score four unblocked hits wins the game.
Cold War Yahtzee, anyone?
