description:
A solitaire game in which a CNBG (USN nuclear aircraft carrier battle group) tries to fend off several salvos of anti-ship missiles.

The game uses two decks of standard poker cards and the counters provided on the postcard.

The player chooses where to place the CVBG assets (cruisers, frigates and destroyers)to screen the carrier and decides how try and shoot down each incoming group of missiles.

The objective is to save the carrier from destruction.

Created and Designed for the 2 player 18 card challenge contest on BGG. You are rival sommeliers (wine experts) facing off to prove who is the best. You will be blind bidding against each other, on the bottles of wine available, and setting them in order in your cellar. Some bottles are best opened early & sour if left too long, others need some time & get better with age. Use your cards wisely and outsmart your opponent.

this game was signed by 25th Century Games and it grew and improved since then. Now it plays up to 8 players and has client cards.

The Farthest Journey is a promo expansion featuring the Beyond the Horizon Module and 2 advanced Scenarios.

The Beyond the Horizon Module expands the core set of Location cards, adding even more variety, and introduces a new Fabled Place called Nethergate.

In The Great Swamp Scenario, you meet a Merman who shares his wisdom with those who keep him company in the heart of the endless bogs.

The Ghostly Forest Scenario is intended both for the competitive play and for The Challenge. The lost Sages have to pass the miraculous forest growing at the junction of two worlds.

The Farthest Journey is compatible both with the core game (required) and the Eve of Times expansion (not required, but recommended).

Dead Spot - A word game with spotty transmission.

Try to re-establish contact and share words with your teammates using preprepared explanations – but be careful: the spotty signal will mean that your explanations arrive incomplete at the other end. Whoever can quickly and correctly reassemble the garbled message to find the original word scores the most points at the end!

What do capybaras eat? What do muskoxen prefer? What snack is a tapirus always happy about? In this funny game you are going to feed cute animals that live in a zoo with their favourite treats.

Each animal has their own dietary habits. Sometimes it’s different from what this animal eats living in the wild — zoo staff add various healthy products to their pets’ usual food to make sure they will be healthy and strong.

Each round players quickly open the Food tiles trying to find the kinds of food shown on a card. The first to open the right tiles will feed the animal and take this card to themself. The first to feed four animals will become the winner.

—description from the publisher

Embark on a whimsical adventure with Oddities, the card game where players take on the role of daring adventurers, each aiming to create the oddest collection of peculiar woodland creatures!

The goal is simple: be the first to finish your collection, but the path there is anything but! Use the unique abilities of your Oddities to strengthen your collection, or deploy Mischievous Misfits to throw a wrench into your rivals’ plans. And then there are the Quirks - chaotic cards that stir up the game, shaking up the game and turning the tide in unpredictable ways. To top it off, changing seasons may limit what Oddities you can collect each turn, forcing you to adjust your strategy and keep an eye on shifting opportunities.

In Oddities, it’s all about outsmarting your opponents while navigating the chaos - all in the name of odd!

—description from the publisher

Every coin has two sides and every “Yes, but” meme has two parts.
Time to pair them up!

Match cards, share the stories they tell and check if its what the author meant. Match three correct pairs to win!

Yes, But is a fun card game based on the meme comics by Anton Gudim! Exchange cards, match pairs and don’t forget to check if you were correct about what the author meant. Match three comic strips before everyone else and you win!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: V8: Death racing game in a post apocalyptic world
description:
Raise your arms, crossing them, and clasp your hands.

“I worship you, lord of the engine, lord of the oil,
lord of the pistons and the gasoline.
I worship you so that you have mercy on my machine
and my steering wheel, and give me the agility to respond
quickly to my pedals and draw my weapon.
I worship you, chrome lord, so that you grant me
reflexes in the changes and to drive insanely in the salty dunes.
I worship you”

Objective: Depending on the missions, the objectives vary, but in general, surviving and eliminating the enemy is the best option.

What do you need to play?


     Terrain: The main thing, a varied terrain of at least 60 X 60 centimeters and depending on the number of points to play. The more points, the larger the terrain should be.
     Vehicles: V8 is made up of different vehicles for the player to choose from for their war games. From Motorcycles, Buggies, Cars, ATVs, Cars, Trucks, Monster Trucks, Small Trucks, Buses and War Trucks, among others. These must be at least 1/64 scale, and can be of any brand.
     Bases: In order to prevent traps from appearing and to blow your head off for doing so, the vehicles must be on the bases provided by the game


and more!

—description from the designer

Object of the game:
Each player tries to expand his influence over Woberg and to have the majority of party members in as many regions as possible. If he succeeds, he advances on the score track.

The winner is the one who is in front at the end of the game and thus becomes mayor of Woberg

Preparation:

     Each player takes a turntable, chooses a color and the corresponding party members and power cards
     A region card is drawn for each player and for the campaign leader
     The players place their candidate mayor and 2 party members in the region and 1 disk on the score track
     The white round counter goes on the counting track
     7 party members are in your possession, the rest are in the recruited area
     The action cards are placed next to the game board. Scoring


Process of a scoring Players Value table

     Setting the turntable For 2 players The 1st number
     Counting Woberg For 3 players The first 2 numbers
     Placing party members in the regions For >3 players The 3 numbers
     Counting the regions


In case of a tie in a region, the players receive the points of the next lower rank!!
In case of a tie in the campaign region, nobody gets the king bonus!

Important Rules:

     Party members are only placed in the adjacent regions of the campaign region


EXCEPTION: party members that are secretly placed on the game board from Woberg).

     Party members are NEVER placed in the campaign region (not even from Woberg)


EXCEPTION: With a special action card

     Party members can be distributed as desired over the adjacent regions and in Woberg. * Party members from Woberg can only be placed in one region.
     Gaining the majority in the campaign region = +2 bonus points,
     Gaining the majority in the home region (for the candidate mayor) = +2 bonus points
     When playing the power cards, the value must always be different


from the power cards already played

     If your recruited area is empty, you may remove party members from the game board!


Game play :

The 7 rounds: Each round consists of 3 actions and a player turn:

Action:
1. Round counter
The black round counter moves down 1 space.
At the end of the 3rd, 5th and 7th round, a general scoring takes place.
2. Turning over the action cards
The players look carefully at the 5 cards and think about the actions.
3. Playing the power cards
The player who was last on the turn, in the previous round, starts.
Then, in clockwise direction, the players play their power card.
The player with the highest power card begins his turn.
Then follows the player with the second highest card, and so on…

Player’s turn:
4. Replenish party members
The player moves party members (number depends on the power card played)
from recruited territory to your possession. The played power card leaves the game.
5. Choice of action card
The player takes one of the face-up action cards.
6. Action
The player performs the special action and places the number of party members
(depending on the chosen action card) on the game board.
The player may choose whether to place his party members on the game board first or to perform the special action first.
Party members are only placed in the adjacent regions of the campaign region.
You may distribute them as you wish over the adjacent regions and Woberg. 7. Discard chosen action card
The chosen action card is discarded to the discard pile.
After rounds 3, 5 and 7, general scoring takes place
Spin for picnic gear and goodies - main course, fruit, vegetable, dessert and more. Be the first to fill your plate with something from each category to win.

In Burning galletitas ("Burning cookies") you will compete in an international amateur baking contest in search of the world's best homemade cookie recipe, but... watch out!

Someone may be boycotting one of your secret ingredients or turning up the oven temperature while you're not looking so  your delicious cookies will be burned to a crisp and you won't win the contest

Add a cookie to the tray. And then another, and another... The color must always be different and the number higher than the previous one. But you can also play hidden cards and special tricks. If you fail, you will take the burned cookies home and you will have to buy snacks for everybody.

—description from the publisher (translated)

En Burning galletitas participarás en un concurso internacional de repostería amateur en busca de la mejor receta de galletas caseras del mundo pero... ¡cuidado!

Alguien puede estar boicoteando alguno de tus ingredientes secretos o subiendo la temperatura del horno mientras no miras para que tus deliciosas galletitas se chamusquen y no puedas ganar el concurso.

Añade una galletita a la bandeja. Y luego otra, y otra... El color tiene que ser siempre diferente y el número superior a la anterior. Aunque también puedes jugar cartas ocultas y trucos especiales. Si fallas, te llevas las galletitas quemadas a casa y tendrás que pagar la merienda

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Path of the Necromancer, a dark fantasy trading card game (TCG) where you take on the role of a powerful necromancer. This strategic and immersive game challenges players to command undead legions, wield forbidden spells, and manipulate powerful artifacts to dominate their opponents. Whether you’re a fan of tactical combat, deep deck-building, or gothic horror, Path of the Necromancer offers an unparalleled experience in the TCG genre.

				
				
					The Core of the Game				
				
					ObjectiveThe goal is simple: outlast and outwit your opponent by reducing their Hero's health to zero.

				
				
					Gameplay Highlights
     Resource Management: Lifeforce is the currency of your power, fueling all card plays and Hero abilities. Each turn, gain lifeforce (up to a maximum of 10) and decide how to spend it wisely.
     Battlefield Control: Play cards strategically to command the field. Summon minions, equip relics, and cast spells to overwhelm your opponent.
     Dynamic Decisions: Every turn offers critical choices about offense, defense, and resource allocation, ensuring no two games are the same.


				
				
					Card Types
     Hero Cards: Represent the player and offer unique abilities that define playstyle. Heroes are central to every deck and can be targeted and damaged by opponents.
     Minion Cards: Serve as your army, attacking and defending with diverse stats, traits, and abilities.
     Magic Cards:
     Spells: One-time effects that provide bursts of power.
     Artifacts: Continuous effects with defense values, functioning like stationary units on the battlefield.
     Relics: Equip to minions to enhance their stats or grant special abilities.
     Land Cards: Grant ongoing benefits but are limited to one per player on the field.
     Events: Temporary effects that last multiple rounds.


				
				
					Gameplay ModesPath of the Necromancer is designed to fit various playstyles:


     1v1 Duels: Classic, high-stakes battles where precision and timing determine victory.
     2v2 Team Play: Partner with an ally to strategize and coordinate your way to victory.
     Free-for-All (2–4 Players): Engage in chaotic multiplayer showdowns, where only one necromancer will rise supreme.


				
				
					Meet the HeroesPath of the Necromancer introduces a cast of iconic Heroes, each offering unique gameplay styles:

				
				
					Ragnar the Malignant
     Playstyle: Swarm and overwhelm.
     Abilities: Ragnar excels at commanding hordes of skeletons. Abilities like Corpse Explosion (deal AoE damage by sacrificing minions) and Reanimate (resurrect fallen allies) make him relentless in battle.


				
				
					Morgana the Dark Enchantress
     Playstyle: Spellcasting versatility.
     Abilities: Morgana dominates the battlefield with Witching Hour (cast spells at any time) and Arcane Infusion (gain bonuses for casting multiple spells). Her flexibility makes her a tactical powerhouse.


				
				
					Blood Queen Valeria
     Playstyle: Vampiric lifegain and aggression.
     Abilities: Valeria sustains herself with Blood Harvest (heal when minions die) and unleashes devastating attacks using Judgment of the Dead, trading health for immense power.


				
				
					Atropal the Soulweaver
     Playstyle: Demonic summoning and tempo.
     Abilities: Atropal thrives on building momentum. With Hell’s Favor (reduce the cost of summoning minions) and Dark Summoning (summon directly from the graveyard), he keeps the pressure on.


				
				
					Deck CustomizationEach Hero comes with a curated deck that synergizes with their abilities, but players can customize their decks by mixing cards across Heroes, crafting endless combinations to suit their playstyle.

For example:

     Combine Ragnar's skeleton hordes with Morgana's spellcasting for a versatile mix.
     Pair Valeria's lifegain with Atropal's relentless summoning for a blend of offense and sustainability.


Deck Rarity Rules:


     Commons: Up to 4 copies per deck.
     Rares: Up to 3 copies per deck.
     Epics: Up to 2 copies per deck.
     Legendaries: Only 1 copy per deck.


				
				
					Community and ResourcesPath of the Necromancer is more than just a game—it’s a community. We’ve built several resources to help players dive into the game and connect with others:


     Discord Community: Join to find matches, share strategies, and stay updated with the latest news.
     Lifeforce Tracker App: Manage health and lifeforce on-the-go with this intuitive app, complete with rules references and tracking tools available for both iOS and Android.
     YouTube Tutorials: Learn the game from setup to advanced strategies via regular video content.
     Tabletop Simulator Version: Playtest the game digitally with full access to Hero decks and future content.
     Website: Visit PathoftheNecromancer.com for gameplay guides, Hero details, news updates, and more.


				
				
					Why Play Path of the Necromancer?
     Meaningful Choices: Every action, from resource allocation to combat decisions, matters.
     Rich Strategy: Combine Heroes and cards for unique decks and creative strategies.
     Community Focus: Join a passionate player base and grow alongside the game.
     Dark Fantasy Setting: Immerse yourself in a haunting, gothic world of necromancy and power.


Path of the Necromancer isn’t just a game—it’s a living, evolving experience designed for players who love strategy, customization, and community. Take your first step into the dark and see if you have what it takes to command the dead.

boardgame: Chicago Shuffle: Popcorn and Chicken expansion
description: Adds two more decks to the game, one Cheese and Caramel Popcorn Mix themed, the other Fried Chicken with Mild Sauce themed, allowing for a fifth and sixth player.

Based on the popular online strategy game from England, the world of Illyriad is transposed to a hexagonal grid of 19 hexagons. Players pick one of four factions: human, elf, dwarf, or orc, and receive a starting city and resource deck. Players take turns deploying their units to harvest resources to buy spells from the market or build more units to engage opponents in battle. The goal is to conquer all the other cities to form one's empire.

A dead professor. A suicide note. A laboratory of death.

At the peak of his career, Professor Wee Thiam Soon, assistant dean of the Chemistry Department at SUN University was found dead in the lab, leaving behind a suicide message posted on the university’s sub reddit forum.

However, the circumstance surrounding his death leave much more to be investigated. Why would this world renowned scientist take his own life? Could this be a red herring to cover up a skilfully plotted murder? Put your deductive prowess to the test to unravel the mystery.

This is an original English jubensha murder mystery game where players roleplay as suspects and deduce the murderer amongst them. Only the murderer can lie!

boardgame: Town Center: Designer's Attic Maps Volume 1
description:
Designer’s Attic Maps are Town Center expansions.

This is the volume 1. 
Each volume will include 6 new maps with new twists and different lay outs. The rules also include 2 Variants.
 The variant is used by all the players. It is compatible with all the maps (except stated exceptions). Players can mix the cities and agree on each map they want to play.
You will visit:
- Boston              - Singapore
- Luxembourg          - Cyclades Islands

Some maps are more difficult than others and like in reality, it is not fair all the time...

A set of three new themed battlefield boards [Coast, Coastal Town, and Lighthouse], for use with Sorcerer and Sorcerer Endbringer. They each feature a new unique action which adds to your options during battle. Package also contains additional markers/tracking components.

—user summary

This expansion requires the Sorcerer Base Game.

These 3 battlefields feature abilities that add even more strategy to the game!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Just Desserts: Recipe for a Single Player
description: Our 2024 Holiday Gift is a "Recipe Card" with the official solo rules for Just Desserts! These recipe cards fold in half and fit perfectly in your Just Desserts box. Now you can play Just Desserts with just one player!

boardgame: HERD: The Dinosaur Survival Card Game
description:
HERD: A Dinosaur Survival Card Game is a beautifully illustrated semi-cooperative card game with the goal of keeping your herd safe from rapacious predators during the Mesozoic Era.

Every player has a Herd card: Triceratops, Stegosaurus, Parasaurolophus, Ankylosaurus, or Brontosaurus. Each turn, Predator, and Prey cards are drawn, and players must fend off the attacks of terrifying monsters before their herd is eliminated! The Countdown Clock ticks down, and if it reaches zero, you’ve won…or, rather, you’ve survived to fight for survival another day.

The goal of HERD is to survive until the end of the game, which is played over five turns. There are three types of cards: Herds, Predators, and Prey.

Your Herd is your responsibility - sacrificing weaker Prey to survive attacks by Predators. Prey cards represent smaller dinosaur species in the vicinity that can distract a Predator or invoke specially evolved defenses and behaviors to protect the Herd from harm.

Whether your Herd survives to the end of the game is dependent upon your tactics, but HERD is also a semi-cooperative game - if you want to have a chance to win, all Herds have to survive!

—description from the publisher

You are a short order cook in a busy kitchen, trying to crack open as many eggs as you can to make the biggest omelette. Avoid bad eggs, crack open more good eggs then your opponents, and hatch a plan.

The dice will roll, and you'll have to predict the the outcome within a range of numbers. a correct guess will move you along the ticket tracker and making your omelette will be easier. An incorrect guess will have egg on your face, and you'll be working harder in the kitchen. Crack open eggs by flipping them over to see what's inside. Get as many good eggs as you can, one egg yolk is one point, but two bad eggs and you lose everything. The later you go in turn order the less eggs there are left to crack open. Some special abilities in the pile will be egg-actly what you need to pull ahead.

After three rounds the player with the most points is the best short order cook in town!

—description from the publisher

The Epoch Environments Expansion introduces two new card types to the gameplay of HERD: Environments and Environmental Hazards. Both of these drastically influence the way the game plays and add a new level of depth and decision-making to your play experience.

Environments are a new card type that a Herd can Migrate to (see Migration below), instilling benefits and penalties reflecting the ecosystem your Herd might find itself in. These effects can provide interesting new quirks to the flow of gameplay, influencing how Prey cards interact with Predators, Predators interact with your Herd, and more.

Environmental Hazards affect a specific, matching environment - wreaking havoc that can be unpredictable. If you suspect an Environmental Hazard may be drawn soon, or don’t want to risk losing powerful Prey cards to misfortune, you may want to Migrate and avoid their effects!

The expansion also includes five new playable Herds (Deinocheirus, Pteranodon, Armagosaurus, Isisaurus, Pachyrhinosaurus), and a ton of new predators and prey to enhance your games of HERD.

—description from the publisher

Papagotchi is a one card board game where you have to feed a virtual pet with dice.
In each turn, the player throws a dice and that dice indicates which of the six tasks available has to comply so that his pet does not die.
Then, to fulfill the tasks you have to achieve a specific combination of dice for each task, such as having even numbers, stairs or certain specific numbers.
To win you need to feed Papagotchi six times, if in three rounds you cannot feed the Papagotchi the game is lost.

Fungus is your life. You grow, sell, and expand your farm in this game of galactic agronomy. Navigate the changing market and gaining as much as you can for this year’s crop. Also, watch for shifting prices of equipment and labor.  As you do you will become master of commerce among the stars and fill the needs of neighboring planets!
Astromycology is an economic farming game - except you are an alien growing fungus in space. Each player buys, plants, and harvests crops and sells them in an ever-changing market.
Game Mechanics: Engine building, changing economies.

—description from the designer

You are the manager of a rock band! You will advise your new band to rehearse and record, find new band members and play new venues. You start small, but with a lot of work and developing talents, your little garage band can become a music influence to rock the ages! Just don’t let the band’s outrageous behavior get bigger than their talent--that spells disaster!
Players must manage rehearsing, performing, and recruiting.
Game Mechanics: Dice drafting, resource management.

—description from the designer

In a noisy forest, the animals gather together and decide to play a game of tag. One animal will be "it" (the ghost/tagger) and must find the other animals. During the game, the ghost can only locate other animals by following their animal calls!
If you get caught, you'll be "it" in the next round.

—description from the publisher

Chicago, 1973 - a city shrouded in violence and corruption, where gangs rule the streets and the police have lost control. Four characters with intertwined destinies each seek their own path in these chaotic times.

Players will choose one character from the story and complete their missions. At the game's end, only those who complete their tasks have a chance to change their destiny in the story.

During their turn, players must choose between drawing cards or using card abilities. Before the game trigger ends, players aim to collect matching pairs of character and item cards with the same numbers, symbolizing the completion of their character's objective. After all players reveal their cards, points are calculated.

—description from the publisher

Your parents are away, and you're allowed to throw a party at home. As midnight approaches, most guests have left, leaving only a few drunk friends passed out around the house. Suddenly, a scream echoes through the rooms - everyone instantly sobers up and rushes to investigate. You find one friend lying in a pool of blood, clearly dead. There's a killer in the house, and just then, you hear the main door lock... You must survive until dawn...

In this game, players take on roles as either the killer or victims, engaging in a deadly game of cat and mouse throughout the house. The killer wins by claiming just one victim, while the survivors must make it until dawn to win. It's going to be a long night.

—description from the publisher

A biohazard incident at a research lab has caused the B.I.G. virus to leak, turning infected humans into zombies. As the city plunges into panic, scientists estimate the world will be completely overrun within 12 days! As survivors caught in this crisis, how will you escape?

This is a ladder-climbing card game where the game ends when a player plays all their cards. All players lose points based on the number of cards remaining in their hands. If a player chooses to PASS during the game, they must draw from the deck to determine how many cards they need to add to their hand.

—description from the publisher

The Era of Dark Genesis has begun. In order to secure the crucial source of magic power, summoners must possess magic gems to maintain their immense strength. Summon your most powerful creatures to compete for magic power!

This is a 28-card battling card game where each card has a unique effect. The players' goal is to compete for magic gems on three paths. In each game, players will receive a random set of nine cards as their personal deck and try to play the most powerful combination of creatures within 6 rounds.

Each round, both players will draw a card and jointly play a card on one of the paths. The cards are revealed and their effects are executed in sequence.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Food for Thought: Yoga Promo Cards
description:
Six promo cards that can be shuffled in with the Activities deck. These cards give the player interesting effects or bonuses to work towards.

—description from the publisher

A mini-expansion to add variety to your Barony game. This pack includes 24 Hero cards that are selected by players to give them asymmetric one-use powers to use during the game.

This mini-expansion will work with Barony: Royal Edition or the original Barony game (however the artwork does not match the original Barony). The cards are language-independent.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Terra Fantasia: A Map to Encounter Monsters
description:
Terra Fantasia is a communication game for children and adults with an Age of Discovery theme. Drawing and story-telling guide players through their journey.

In the 16th century, a European King collected mysterious monsters from all over the world.He was intrigued by the outside world, and was always examining maps and expanding his realm.One day, he summoned the Adventurers.

King: Adventurers, thank you for gathering here today. Work together and bring me back some rare monsters.

The Adventurers form teams and head out on a voyage.Their mission is to capture the precise monster that the King wanted and to bring it back alive.

By using the map together, let’s catch rare monsters and bring them back to the King.

The Objective of the Game
The King secretly chooses a monster from the map, and the Adventurers figure out which one it is and draw a picture of it.
Try to guess the monster that the King chose.After presenting their drawing and the story to the King, Adventurers will be awarded based on their capture.

—description from the designer

Saga: Age of Chivalry sweeps you off to the time of the Hundred Years’ War, a succession of conflicts between
the kingdoms of France and England and their allies that ran from 1337 to 1453. 
This Saga Universe also lets you recreate other armies of the Late Middle Ages, like the Burgundian Ordonnance and their ferocious Swiss adversaries.
This pivotal period saw battles that remain famous to this day, like Crécy and Agincourt, and forged audacious warriors and courageous warlords who continue to inflame our imaginations. 
Turn the pages of this book to march in the footsteps of Joan of Arc, the Black Prince, and Bertrand du Guesclin.

—description from the publisher

Deckfish is an abstract game for two players in which players collect cards by moving 3 pawns around a map made up of a grid of cards. Pawn movement is dictated by the suits on the cards. At the end, the symbols of each suit on the collected cards are counted and the final score is the number of symbols of the suit with the lowest symbols.

The game uses the Decktet system.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: TTMC: Tu te mets combien? – Bonne Bouffe avec Marmiton
description:
The goal of TTMC - Tu Te Mets Combien? - Bonne Bouffe is to be the first to go through the board answering the questions asked. Each score corresponds to a question, more or less complex: the higher the chosen score, the more specific the question. In case of a correct answer, the team will advance the number of squares corresponding to its self-assessed score. For example, if you answer a question correctly with a 6, you will advance 6 squares.

It's funny, accessible to all cooking lovers and there's plenty to do: 162 cards and 1157 questions, with the presence of original cards to spice up the game.

Strong points:

Funny and accessible, for all cooking lovers!
More than 1100 new "TTMC-style" questions, just the way we like them!
In collaboration with Marmiton!

Conquer land, gain tactical victory, learn powerful magics, and grow your empire! A little Catan, a little Risk, and a little Dominion!
The goal of the game is to capture as much territory as possible with military units. The game will end after all 4 seasons have passed and the player with the most territory will win.
Players will generate resources based on the tiles they control. These resources can then be spent to recruit a variety of troops. 
Troop combat is done with dice. Players will roll dice equal to the same number of troops they have in combat, add or subtract bonuses based on terrain, and then calculate hits in an attempt to remove territorial control from their opponent.

—description from the designer

Rackare! is a cheeky party game full of humor. It’s based on the extremely popular American game Cards Against Humanity. After selling over 160,000 copies, here comes Rackare! 4 with 600 brand-new cards. A new game and an expansion at the same time, covering everything from ChatGPT to Brazilian butt lifts!

—description from the publisher

Fyr is a strategic racing game set in a misty, sea-bound world where players navigate steamships through a hidden-tile map filled with obstacles like fog, rocks, and mysterious sea creatures. Players reveal tiles as they progress, unveiling both routes and risks.

Each turn, players can use special cards and interact with lighthouses to manipulate the map or gain strategic advantages. The objective is to complete a set course faster than the others, with the first to finish winning a bonus and ultimately the game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
InvestiCats, the prestigious pet detective agency where you work, has just received a very important assignment. Countess Catafiore's cats, tired of eating cream puffs for breakfast every day, have escaped the mansion and have been spotted frolicking in a nearby forest. Your mission is to find them before your colleagues from the Agency.

Take your turn to search the forest thoroughly. When is your turn? Well, that depends on the die. Marramiau is a game of cats, chaos... and a ball of yarn.

—description from the publisher (translated)

InvestiGatos, la prestigiosa agencia de detectives de mascotas en la que trabajas acaba de recibir un encargo importantísimo. Los gatos de la condesa Gatafiore, hartos de desayunar bollitos de crema cada día, se han fugado de la mansión y han sido vistos correteando por un bosque cercano. Tu misión será encontrarlos antes que tus colegas de la Agencia.

Aprovecha tu turno para buscar a fondo en el bosque ¿Que cuándo es tu turno? Bueno, eso depende del dado. Marramiau es un juego con gatos, caos... y un ovillo de lana.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Grand Heist: Paris, Les Annees Folles
description:
In this semi-cooperative card game players will cooperate together to perform heists, starting negotiations to share the loot. Hire gang members, avoid police officer, planificate the perfect heist! 
Will your share of the loot be fair?
In Grand Heist: Paris you will test your abilities as a diamonds thiefs gang mastermind in a Paris of the 1920ies, and as a convincing negotiator able to find a place in each heist! 
But don´t forget, there will be only one winner...

—description from the designer

Pisando Fundo is a racing game in which players move their cars on a grid, similar to, for example, Monza.
But the method used for movement is quite different. Each player has speed tokens every 20 km/h and, with each new round, must decide whether to accelerate or brake the vehicle, but not both.
Starting from the vehicle's speed in the previous round, the players place markers on the grid, accelerating every 20 km/h or braking at the same rate, until there are no markers left or they choose to stop placing markers, moving the vehicle to the last space and leaving the last token to find out the vehicle's speed.
However, several restrictions are imposed: you can only overtake a vehicle if, when you are next to it, you have an equal or greater speed.
In addition, various spaces on the track show recommended speeds and, if the player passes one of these spaces at a different speed, you receive a damage card every 20 km/h.
The damage cards cause you to lose speed tokens, interfering with how much you can move.

Lizards love the heat and that's why they go to the beach.
Too much heat isn't that cool either, so they fight for that space in the umbrella.
In your turn make the others raise their temperature and have to go to the water to cool off while you stay cool under the umbrella. 
Fast, simple and interactive game where everyone will go against you.
During your turn put cards over the others in correct order to raise from 32º to 40º and push the lizards to dive. When others raise your temperature, you can defend yourself. You also could cool down, break defenses, or manage your hand in other actions.
When a lizard goes twice to water, game is end and the coolest one wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Hobbit: Hunt for the Arkenstone
description:
Embark on an epic quest with Hunt for the Arkenstone, a cooperative board game inspired by the thrilling scenes from The Hobbit: The Battle of the Five Armies.

In this adventure, players assume the roles of the brave members of Thorin Oakenshield's Company, including Dwarves and Hobbits. Working together, they must search the vast lands of Middle-earth to find the legendary Arkenstone and return it to Erebor's throne room.

To succeed, you'll need to explore every corner of Middle-earth, using your character's unique abilities and the support of your allies. Along the way, you’ll discover valuable items to aid in your quest. But beware—Azog and his ruthless Orcs are hot on your trail, determined to stop you at any cost.

Hunt for the Arkenstone is a legendary adventure for Thorin Oakenshield and his Company, brought to life in this complete boxed game. Perfect for any fan of Middle-earth, it offers an immersive experience in a single box.

—description from the publisher

Bad news! You’re in Manhattan and it’s overrun with zombies!

You’ve been laying low, but now it’s time to leave your sanctuary and make a break for the safety of the New Jersey Refugee Camp! It’s been weeks since the outbreak occurred; the city streets aren’t getting any safer – more New Yorkers are getting turned every day; the number of zombies is increasing, and the army are moving in.

To get out alive, you must make it out of Harlem, through Central Park, Midtown and finally survive the Lincoln Tunnel.  Good luck - you’re going to need it!

New York Zombies is a one-page, A4 tri-fold brochure solo game that plays in 25-30 minutes (or less if your character doesn't make it out of Harlem!).

—description from the designer

KAPOW!!

Was that a snake that just flew by my head? In Abracabattle, duel as a wizard with weird and wild abilities, competing to see who will reign magically supreme. Players take turns learning powerful spells from each of the four elements: Snake, Crystal, Feather, and Cheese. Use your spells to create ridiculously powerful combo chain reactions to battle and defeat your enemy!

Abracabattle is a card game about learning magical spells, and using them to fight your opponents in wizard battles. Spell cards are bought and placed into the player’s tableau, and are activated by rolling dice (kind of like in Machi Koro). By buying additional spells with complementary abilities, players will create strong and unique engines, each based around a different strategy and play style.

—description from the designer

Recipe Rumble is a family friendly, easy to learn and fun to play game for all ages. It's a fast-paced game where everyone trades their cards at the same time. Be the first player to collect all of the ingredients on your recipe card to win.

—description from the designer

In this game you will be raising you baby dragon to a youth dragon and then adult dragon and ultimatley taking you adult dragon to the Cliffs of Endless Horizon to ride your dragon and win the game.

This is a dice allocation with a lite deck building mechanic. You will be purchasing cards to collection resources to grow your dragon and rolling dice to attack monsters and purchase location cards.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Vs. System 2PCG: Defenders of Hell's Kitchen
description:
With crime on the rise under the shadowy Kingpin, Hell’s Kitchen is living up to its name. But there can only be one master of hell—and it’s the devil. Join the Man Without Fear and the Defenders of Hell’s Kitchen to take back the streets from rival gangs and calculating criminals. Can you stand the heat of Hell’s Kitchen?

Defenders of Hell’s Kitchen will return to the Defenders and Underworld teams as they exist on the levels of organized crime. This Issue will shine a light on the Kingpin of crime, The Taskmaster, and how the Defender of Hell’s kitchen has evolved in Vs. System 2PCG.


     Return to Hell’s Kitchen with Daredevil and the Kingpin of Crime.
     Each expansion adds support to both The Defenders and Underworld teams.
     9 Main Characters both returning and appearing for the first * time!
     Customize your new team or enhance your existing decks!
     Epic competitive gameplay to pit your team of Allies against your opponents


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Paladins of the West Kingdom: Player Suspicion Decks
description:
This mini card pack includes 56 Suspicion Cards (14 per player), designed to replace the original Suspicion Deck.

Each set of 14 Cards consists of:

3 x No Reward
4 x 1 Tax
5 x 2 Tax
1 x Discard Suspicion
1 x 1 Provision

—description from the publisher
family: Series: The West Kingdom Trilogy (Garphill Games)

boardgame: Vs. System 2PCG: Protectors of New York
description:
Details
Protectors of New York will revisit the Spider Friends and the Sinister Syndicate teams as we return to New York and everyone's favorite borough, Manhattan. This Issue will uncover the devious new plans of the Syndicate as they are foiled by our friendly neighborhood Spiderman and a few new friends. Images will be sourced from the Marvel Comics themselves and feature art from the many recent appearances of Spider-Man throughout the Spider-Verse and The Devils Reign Story Lines.

Highlights:


     Return to Manhattan with everyone’s favorite wall crawler Spider-Man.
     This expansion adds support to both The Spider-Friends and Syndicate teams.
     8 Main Characters both returning and appearing for the first time!
     Customize your new team or enhance your existing decks!
     Epic competitive gameplay to pit your team of Allies against your opponents!


—description from the publisher

Tribal Conquest is a skirmish-sized miniatures wargame where prehistoric tribes clash in a battle for survival and dominance, guided by the ancient power of magical Shamans! Each tribe is led by a Shaman—an all-powerful figure who draws on mana generated by their followers to unleash devastating spells. Whether it's a fiery blast of destruction, a powerful protective shield, or even the ability to reshape the very landscape of the battlefield, the Shaman’s magic is the key to victory!

The tribes themselves are made up of a wide range of unique warriors and specialists, each with their own strengths and weaknesses. From the brave, mana-generating youths to the seasoned warriors and mystical elders, every tribe member plays a vital role. Hunters, preachers, and other specialized units bring their own unique abilities to the fight, making each tribe’s strategy a balance of power, magic, and cunning.

Tribal Conquest is designed to be easy to pick up, yet offers deep, strategic gameplay. The core rulebook is filled with clear examples and diagrams to help players master every mechanic. But the real excitement comes from the ability to customize your game experience! With the option to add or remove Advanced Rules Modules before each battle, no two games are ever the same. The true challenge lies in the tough decisions you’ll face as you carefully plan your tribe’s moves, manage your Mana, and outsmart your opponent!

—description from the publisher

As CEOs of pharmaceutical companies in Perfect Health Company, players control their production lines to carry out their "perfect" plans.

Each player has a profile sheet to track points and resources and a schedule sheet on which they'll move their pawn. The game starts with a planning phase in which you receive resources and mark spots on your schedule sheet.

In the production phase that follows, you roll two dice and pick one to move your pawn. If you move to a space with symbols — e.g., a liquid, capsule, or syringe — you add them to your profile sheet. When you stop at a shop, you can purchase additional points and add skills to your schedule sheet to make spaces more valuable. Moving your pawn faster than the other players or meeting the conditions on bonus cards revealed at the beginning of the game earns you additional points.

After a player completes three laps, the first round ends. Players then have a second planning phase to boost their schedule sheet, then you continue play, with the game ending after a player completes seven laps.

Manipulate your dice rolls, heal your employees, and make the most of the skills you have to carry out medicine production more perfectly than anyone else!

Welcome to HAA!, where you lead your bacteria to become the most widespread and formidable virus. In this fast-paced hand management game, every card you play matters. Draw your hand at the start of the round and use it wisely—there are no extra draws or discards (unless you play a special effect card), so strategic decisions are key. Will you play your cards for immediate points, or set up end-of-round surprises to outmaneuver your opponents? The choice is yours, but choose carefully—only the most calculated and daring player will claim victory in this intense and thrilling competition.

This auction game for four or five players is the 2024 Hollandays Sale Freebie.

Thomas Hobson (1544-1631) was the owner of a livery stable. To prevent his best horses from always being chosen and thus overused, he rotated his horses and required his customers to choose the horse whose stall was closest to the door. This has been passed down to us as the idiom "Hobson's Choice": Take what is offered, or take none.

In this game, players have sneaked a peek at the rotation for the day, and know what horses will be offered in which order. Each player will bid a discrete amount of time. The player who bids the most got up the earliest and will be arriving first, taking the first horse. The player who bid the next highest takes the second horse, and so on, with the player who bid the least taking no horse at all!

These horses each have two scoring criteria, and depending on which horses you've ridden in which order, these criteria might result in positive or negative points. The player with the highest score wins.

—description from the publisher

Legends of ShenZhou is an immersive and strategic board game inspired by the legendary Chinese text Shan Hai Jing(The Classic of Mountains and Seas). This ancient text, dating back over 2,000 years, is one of the most famous works in Chinese mythology, filled with vivid descriptions of mythical creatures, divine beings, and fantastical landscapes. It serves as both a mythological map and an encyclopedia of strange beasts, gods, and the unknown corners of the world.

Set in a fantastical world drawn directly from the Shan Hai Jing, players embark on an epic journey as iconic characters from Chinese mythology. They will explore the mysterious regions described in the ancient text, from towering mountains to vast seas, encountering the legendary creatures and divine figures that have fascinated readers for millennia.
In this game, players must navigate diverse and treacherous maps, using tactical movement to gather skills, earn wealth, and outmaneuver rivals. Along the way, they will encounter legendary creatures from Chinese lore, battle powerful adversaries, and face off against fellow players. Victory comes through strategic decision-making, where players must carefully purchase weapons, shields, mounts, and essential supplies to gain the upper hand in combat.

The ultimate goal of Legends of ShenZhou is to challenge and defeat the divine beasts that guard the path to greatness. Only the most cunning and resourceful players will overcome these mythical foes and claim their place in history as a true legend of Shenzhou.

—description from the publisher

Gate Legend is a deckbuilding rouge-lite adventure board game. Make unique cards, encounter bizarre creatures, discover powerful treasures, use props cleverly, and eventually become strong enough to defeat the final boss the demon lord.
An unstable overlapping land in the universe was once conquered and ruled by a great magician for a long time. He created the great Throne of Void. As the magician disappeared into the other world, countless space cracks formed a door to travel through the other world. Dominating the Throne of the Void has also become the competition target among all parties. Players play the role of legendary heroes, adventure in the Throne of the Void to defeat the final enemy and establish a new order in the void.

—description from the designer

In Synthesis, you play as a scientist exploring the mysterious planet Kepler-452b, aiming to unlock the secrets of newly discovered chemical elements. Each turn, you roll two dice to guide your choices: the bond dice determine the shape of bonds you’ll draw on your personal formula board, while the mutation dice introduce special rules for how you can modify these bonds. As you draw, you’ll use one of four colors—each representing a unique element—and build toward completing hexagonal molecules on your board.

But here’s the twist: it’s not just about finishing molecules. The points depend on the colors used within each molecule, challenging you to create the most effective pattern. Do you complete that green molecule with three green lines now, or wait to make it blue for higher points? Each turn, this small but strategic choice adds a layer of depth, creating a satisfying puzzle that keeps gameplay addictive.

With its vibrant sci-fi theme and simultaneous turns, Synthesis is perfect for groups of up to 8 players. The blend of accessible mechanics and strategic decision-making makes every roll feel exciting, whether you’re new to games or a seasoned player.

—description from the designer

Parasite is a cooperative strategic card game where you need to outsmart each other and stay alive. 
In a stressing situation like this, put to the test your strategy, teamwork and bluffing skills!

Easy to learn, difficult to master.

Everyone wants to find which player is hosting the parasite, but be aware! It can pass from host to host...
The evil alien hates the light, use it at your advantage to gain information from other players.
The campfire is the only thing that prevents the parasite to attack, each card has a “bonfire points” cost that reduces the lifetime of your main light source, if it goes out you might be the next victim.

Fortunally there are many items your group can use to reveal the alien, “the team bag” will be the main deck to find them.

—description from the designer

OBJECTIVE:
Capture your opponent’s hidden king.

Unlike the classic games of chess or checkers, where pieces can only make one kind of move depending on what type they are, the Madcap Knights can take any action needed.

To activate your Madcap Knights, roll two dice: 

     THE CHESS DIE 
     THE NUMBERS DIE


The Chess Die will determine what kind of move is possible on that turn. If the roll is Queen, Rook or Bishop, then the Numbers Die determines how far a piece MUST move as Queen, Rook or Bishop. If the roll is King, Knight or Pawn, the Numbers Die is ignored.

The player then selects any of their Madcap Knights to carry out the action.

Except for the Kings, all Madcap Knights are identical, each with the colors of the opposing players on the reverse side. Each player’s pieces begin with their color facing up at the start.  Players each get 12 pieces, including the king) and deploy their pieces in any formation on their side iof the board.. Place the king symbol face down to hide it. 
Players choose randomly who makes the first move, Capturing the king ends the game.

THE KANGAROO DIE
Whenever a SIX is rolled, the player MAY opt to give up that roll to reroll with the KANGAROO DIE and NUMBERS DIE to get 6 extra MADCAP moves. Only Joker and Double Knight can capture the King.

—description from the designer

Join your favorite Grumpuses in Bugsnax: The Card Game! An easy-to-play card game based on the hit video game.

Welcome to Snaktooth Island! In this strange and far away paradise live the most incredible creatures: Bugsnax!

What are Bugsnax? Well, they’re kinda bug, kinda snack, and all delicious. You and your Grumpus friends have come to the island for the sole purpose of catching and devouring these skittering little morsels. Just don’t think too hard about what it is you’re eating…

Based on the hit video game, Bugsnax: The Card Game is a bite-sized race to eat your fill! Take on the role of your favorite Grumpus and use their unique ability cards, along with your tricky traps, to outwit the other players and overcome the Bugsnax defenses. Be the first to chow down on 6 points worth of Bugsnax, and you’ll be crowned the Snakmaster General!

Play in standard mode for a casual burst of bug-catching fun, or tackle advanced mode for a longer and more strategic experience. Between 13 Grumpuses and 40 Bugsnax, there are a ton of play styles to try and unique situations to overcome. However you like to play, Bugsnax: The Card Game is a treat!

—description from the publisher

One-card game.

After hours of arduous experimentation, a group of students managed to generate electricity with a potato... but it's about to short-circuit! Pass the potato before you get an electric CHIPS!

Double Exposure is principally a 2 player game that can be played with a deck of ordinary playing cards.  Each player is trying to achieve a scoring combination in the cards in their prison before the other player.  Cards are gained through trick-taking, although the rules for this are different than for most trick-taking games.  It is very close to a game of perfect information.  In fact, at the beginning of the game, each player exposes their hand to their opponent--hence, the name.

Double Exposure is a tense, thinky game for two skilled players.  All of the information you need is available to you at the start of the game; your job is to reach your goal, while keeping your opponent from reaching theirs first.  Each hand only takes 20 minutes or so to play.  A "duplicate" option is also included in the rules, that eliminates the luck of the draw.

Finally, a solitaire version is also included, with the player competing against a robot opponent that operates using straightforward rules.  This version isn't as challenging as the 2-player game, but is still interesting, plays quickly, and includes some of the feel of the primary game.

—description from the designer

This expansion introduces a number of new cards and characters based on mythologies from around the world. These new characters have powers which the players can choose to use when they are played. Each power is loosely based on the mystical or magical traits of the "real life" creature. For example, a Phoenix card has an element of rebirth, allowing you to take any one card from the discard pile and bring it back into your hand.

In Objective !nsanity all players race to achieve a handful of objective cards that are face up in the middle of the table. These are designed to encourage new strategies, and when achieved, guarantee you bonus points at the end of the game. Farmers and Animals also gain abilities in this expansion, which only adds to the chaos. More ways to win, more ways to lose, and more fun!

boardgame: Magic Number: The Party Game of Wild Guesstimation
description:
Will you be able to guess the Magic Number? In this new party game, the answer to every question is a number, and the player with the closest guess wins!

Magic Number features hundreds of questions across three categories that will test your knowledge (or lack thereof) of random facts and how well you know your friends. Will your guess be spot-on, just a little off, or mind-bogglingly awful?

With the ability to customize questions, indicated by blue, underlined text found on most cards, Magic Number will always be unique and exciting, no matter how many times you play.

Gather your friends, and let the guessing games begin!

—description from the publisher

Quick Words is a fast-paced word game with plenty of variation! Find and form words from the letters on the dice while the sand in the timer keeps running. The catch is, you won´t get points for any words that appear on your opponents´ sheets!

From the crumbling earth an ancient castle rises slowly over the city of Sedgewick - a dark lord's tomb. Within its confines, untold thousands of Vampires slowly emerge from hibernation. The Night Hunters must enter the cramped confines and vault-like chambers of the Castle to rescue their father and defeat this graven menace.

Night Hunt is a board game for 1 or 2 players, recreating the battles fought between the Night Hunters and Molduran Vampires. One player commands the Night Hunters as they search for their father in the shadows of the Castle, and the other commands the bloodline family of Vampires opposing them. Night Hunt's fast-paced rules simulate the tense atmosphere of a hunt deep into the cramped corridors of the Castle, where split-second decisions are needed for victory.

This story campaign has rules for head-to-head, co-operative, and solo play.

—description from the designer

日本內戰：會津 ("Japanese Civil War: Aizu") is a 1-2 player strategic level wargame to simulate the Battle of Aizu in late Tokugawa period in Japan.
There are 8 rounds in one game. Player commands the Meiji government to conquer the Tokugawa shogunate in kanto region. Imperial Japanese armies may move into the revolt areas and roll the die to attack rebel armies. Be careful, the rebels may ambush you randomly. In the end round, you need to score at least 7 vps by capturing the daimyo of rebel domains and sweep the rebel armies.
Japanese Civil War: Aizu is the third game of the Revolt & Conquer series. You can combine Aizu and Hakodate maps together for playing a Boshin War campaign game with 3-4 players.

—description from the designer

日本內戰：箱館 ("Japanese Civil War: Hakodate") is a 1-2 player strategic level wargame to simulate the Battle of Hakodate in Tokugawa period in Japan.
There are 8 rounds in one game. Player commands the Meiji government to conquer the Tokugawa shogunate in Tohoku region. Imperial Japanese armies may move into the revolt areas and roll the die to attack rebel armies. Be careful, the rebels may ambush you randomly. In the end round, you need to score at least 7 vps by capturing the daimyo of rebel domains and sweep the rebel armies.
Japanese Civil War: Hakodate is the fourth game of the Revolt & Conquer series. You can combine Aizu and Hakodate maps together for playing a Boshin War campaign game with 3-4 players.

—description from the designer

Set during the Normandy Campaign of World War Two, Vanguard places players in control of USA and German forces fighting to defeat one another in a desperate and high-stakes struggle for the French mainland.

The huge range of US and German forces fighting in the Normandy Campaign is represented by beautiful miniatures and bespoke unit cards, which handily track special rules for a streamlined playing experience.

These units are entirely unique, each featuring distinct rules, attack profiles, and artwork inspired by the design team’s passion for history.

Combat is fast-paced and intuitive, thanks to a streamlined dice system that eliminates complex calculations and line-of-sight mechanics.

Vanguard’s unit activation is both fun and intense. Players add Faction Tokens to a shared Activation Bag for each unit they control, before drawing tokens randomly to determine the order these units activate.

This system creates nail-biting, cinematic gameplay where players are never sure which units will activate first!

Vanguard also features a dynamic army-building system. Both players start with limited forces and a requisition income, building up their army and economy over the course of the game through the control of battlefield sectors.

The more sectors a player controls, the more resources their forces receive—but if the Victory Point gap grows too wide, the trailing player can unleash powerful Underdog abilities to even the odds.

Setting up the battlefield is quick and simple.

By arranging nine of the 15 double-sided battlefield tiles into a grid, players can create a visually stunning and unique play area in seconds.

Each tile features beautifully detailed artwork depicting historical locations from D-Day and the Normandy Campaign, including airfields, radar stations, manors, farms, and more.

The solo campaign allows a single player to fight through a historical campaign as the USA, battling across six historical engagements against a fiendish German AI opponent.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Duskmourn: House of Horror
description:
In this expansion for Magic: The Gathering, players explore the world of Duskmourn, a hellish land ruled by Valgavoth, a demonic overlord who feeds on fear and has transformed the entire plane into one gigantic haunted mansion. There is nothing left of outdoors—only the House, and its uncountable horrors.

In addition to the main booster expansion, the Duskmourn: House of Horror offerings also include four preconstructed commander decks, each of which includes an Archenemy scheme deck, allowing them to be played in a one-versus-many variant game mode.

A set of fast play rules for late ironclad/early pre-dreadnought fleet battles of the 1870s and 1880s.

Based on the successful Dahlgren and Columbiad system, the rules include a completely revised stat system to reflect the far larger, more heavily armoured and heavily armed ships of the era. 
Included with the rule are ship statistics for British, French and Italian fleets.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Thunder Road: Vendetta – Choppe Shoppe: Spotter's Tower Promo Card
description:
Originally available from Dice Tower 2024 Kickstarter Atom Pack.

An upgrade card that requires Choppe Shoppe expansion.

Text says: When you drift with this car, do no reveal face-down hazard tokens on spaces it enters this turn. If you end your move on a face-down hazard, reveal it.

Players play as Catholic Church cardinals, trying to get support from other Cardinals around the world. The first player reaching the target number of supporting Cardinals wins and is elected as the next Pope.

Players can perform actions, collect taxes from believers, fly and move from one diocese to another and try to persuade or bribe other Cardinals to get their support. Persuasion is performed using a D20 dice roll.

The players also can boost their character stats or the ones of the other players by going to a pilgrimage to Rome or using action cards. Each player has 3 stats: sanctity, sins and movement points.

This historical and strategy game takes an ironic approach to the Catholic Church, with fun and cartoonish cards reminding real-life scandals and questionable practices attributed to the Catholic Church.

—user summary

In Pontifex sei un cardinale della Chiesa Cattolica e hai un solo obiettivo: diventare Papa!
 Come fare? Devi persuadere gli altri cardinali che sei tu il più “meritevole” e “santo”…
 Ma la strada per il potere non è quella della santità, quindi preparati a una lotta in cui nessun peccato è escluso: comprometti gli avversari con scandali poco cristiani, corrompi gli altri cardinali e usa la politica a tuo vantaggio. Il tutto restando sempre immacolato come una veste papale.
 Un ironico gioco di strategia ambientato nella Chiesa contemporanea, dove la realtà supera la fantasia.

—description from the publisher

In Pontifex you are a Catholic Church cardinal, and you have one objective: to become Pope!
 How? You have to persuade other cardinals that you are the most "deserving" and "saint"...

But the road to the power is not the sanctity one, so get prepared to a challenge where no sin is excluded: compromise your opponents with not-very-christian scandals, bribe other cardinals and use politics at your advantage.
All of this keeping yourself immaculate as a papal robe.

An ironic strategy game set in the contemporary Church, where reality trumps fantasy.

—description from the publisher (translated)
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
GAMEMASTER: Battle Royale is a fully customisable party-style board game that suits any group and occasion.

It features three exciting, fully playable themes:

Family-Friendly Fun: Perfect for all ages, with challenges everyone can enjoy.
Adults-Only Party Night: Add some spice with bold, edgy gameplay.
Classic Trivia: Test your knowledge and prove who’s the smartest at the table.

In each round, players take turns as the Game Master (GM), spinning a wheel to determine the category for that round.
In this fast-paced game, players compete in category-specific tasks to win a category card.

The ultimate goal of GAMEMASTER: Battle Royale is to be the first to collect a card from all the main categories (seven or eight, depending on the game mode). Players can propose trades, build alliances, and make strategic decisions that challenge trust and tactics or be a lone wolf and trust no one.
The game balances luck, creativity, knowledge, and common sense—all while delivering intense one-on-one player interactions for a thrilling, dynamic experience.

Unique mechanics, such as 1-on-1 challenges, Chameleon Cards, and Wolf Tokens, add layers of strategy. These features allow players to steal cards from other players, protect their collection, or use versatile cards to their advantage. The GM oversees fairness, resolves disputes, and ensures smooth gameplay but does not compete for cards during their turn.

With engaging challenges and flexible gameplay modes, GAMEMASTER: Battle Royale delivers a unique experience with every playthrough. Packed with excitement, strategic twists, and unexpected turns, it’s perfect for those who love a mix of trivia, challenges, and strategy. Only the best will claim victory and emerge as the ultimate champion!

Acuarella is a competitive game where you’ll place polyomino tiles with gorgeous cat illustrations. You must add these tiles to your board following the placement rules, trying to collect as many pigments as possible by placing adjoining tiles in the right combinations.
You can also gain opportunity cards that make the game a fun and challenging competition for cat-like artists!

—description from the designer

In Acuarella, a vibrant polyomino placement game featuring adorable feline illustrations, you'll become an ingenious cat artist. Your mission: to create the ultimate masterpiece to support the many kitties living near an urban park.

To achieve this, you'll combine your Cat tokens (polyominoes) on your personal canvas, mixing them to generate new colors (pigments) that will earn you valuable points.

The challenge and fun are intensified with Opportunity cards, which add unexpected twists to the game. Plus, you'll aim to capture adorable "cat photos" to fill the small empty spaces on your board, as each unfilled space subtracts points from your final score.

As a finishing touch, the Cat tokens you've used on your board will transform at the end of the game, allowing you to assemble adorable kitties on the table for extra points.
Get ready to unleash your creativity and help these
furry friends!

The cycle of life as we knew it is being altered, spirits from beyond, ancient warriors awakened from their lethargy, all now room our world. A being of incalculable power from the realm of Hella threatens to enter our world and plunge it into purgatory. We will travel to the gates of the underworld to face the myterious guardian of the dark kingdoms.

Face the Lord of the Underworld.
A new campaign with over twenty hours of gameplay!
Includes 10 new miniatures, 5 runes, a new hero, 3 new territory tiles, 40 cards and 15 tiles.

In a game all about making people feel good about themselves, one player, the fisher, receives compliments from the other players, the fish.

The fish write the compliments on dry erase fish-shaped cards based on prompts from one of three compliment categories: Living (everyday topics), Laughing (light-hearted subjects), and Loving (affectionate themes).

Meanwhile, the fisher writes down what the fish might say about them on a separate dry erase card, called the Catch of the Day card.

Then, scoring, awarded via heart tokens, ensues in three ways: 1) The fisher get one heart for every fish cards they fish into the game box using a toy fishing rod. 2) The fisher gets one heart for every time their Catch of the Day guess matches a compliment they receive from the fish. 3) The fisher awards three hearts to their favorite compliment, two hearts to their second favorite compliment, and one heart to their third favorite compliment.

As a barista at a coffee café, your daily grind is making the perfect cup of joe. But today, trouble is brewing. You are short staffed and running the café on your own. Rent for your apartment is due, and unless you can collect at least 7 tips before the end of your shift, you’re going to be short. Utilize ingredients efficiently and arrange your orders to accomplish this goal. While you're at it, make yourself a few so you can think straight. Full steam ahead!

In this challenging, print-it-yourself solo game, you must place cards that represent coffee ingredients and special orders within a 3x3 grid to score at least 7 victory points.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Prospect Detective: Caso 0090 – La carpa di via Catamone
description:
In case 0090 you will have to investigate the death of a young girl inside her apartment in Via Catamone.

In Prospect Detective you will have to solve a cold case.
The game purpose is to offer a very immersive and realistic experience (no dices, rules or pawns).
Every useful informations to analise and relevant clues are all included in the original case game-folder along with qr codes that links to audio/video recordings.

In prospect Detective dovrete chiudere un caso irrisolto.
Scopo del gioco è offrire una esperienza estremamente immersiva e realistica (nessun tiro di dado, manuali con regole astruse o pedine da piazzare su un tabellone). Tutte le informazioni utili da dover analizzare e i relativi indizi per risolvere il crimine sono inclusi nel fascicolo di gioco che ricalca fedelmente il faldone di un "cold case".
Trovere inoltre al suo interno i codici qr che rimandano alle testimonianze audio e video dei fatti accaduti.

boardgame: Prospect Detective: Caso 0056 – Il delitto della Motocicletta
description:
In case 0056 you will investigate the mysterious death of Marcello, a biker from Prato, killed in cold blood in the parking lot of the company he worked for.

In Prospect Detective you will have to solve a cold case.
The game purpose is to offer a very immersive and realistic experience (no dices, rules or pawns).
Every useful informations to analise and relevant clues are all included in the original case game-folder along with qr codes that links to audio/video recordings.

In prospect Detective dovrete chiudere un caso irrisolto.
Scopo del gioco è offrire una esperienza estremamente immersiva e realistica (nessun tiro di dado, manuali con regole astruse o pedine da piazzare su un tabellone). Tutte le informazioni utili da dover analizzare e i relativi indizi per risolvere il crimine sono inclusi nel fascicolo di gioco che ricalca fedelmente il faldone di un "cold case".
Trovere inoltre al suo interno i codici qr che rimandano alle testimonianze audio e video dei fatti accaduti.

Get ready to make Russian dumplings that your cafe visitors will love! Your menu should include Udmurt pelmeni, Perm's posikunchiki, southern Russia's vareniki, Buryat buuz with sour cream, mayonnaise, ketchup, and vinegar.

Keep an eye on the sauce supply at the serving area and try your best to assemble full portions of dumplings as accurately as possible, minimizing mixing several types of dumplings and sauces.

Become the best chef! But remember, your colleagues want that too.

—description from the publisher

A game based on the Battle of Cannea, Tribun pits two sides against each other in a battle of constantly shifting pieces.

You start out with a Tribun and several stacks of soldiers of varied heights. You can then move, combine, or split your stacks - or attack an opponent's stack. Movement, attack range, and power are each dependent on the height of the stack - single-disk stacks move one/attack one, larger stacks can hop around the hexagonal board in increasingly large steps.

The goal is to attack the stack containing the opponent's Tribun, and beat the leader of the opposing forces into submission.

Since the Strengths and Abilities of stacks keeps changing (whether by choice or by attacks from the opponent diminishing your stacks), the rules are simple, but the actual choices can be quite complex.

—user summary

Promotional trick card game. 
The cards show a specific energy and its advantages.
All 36 cards are dealt. Jokers are to be placed at the end of your pile. One player starts with his first card and calls one of its marked advantages. Other players may choose one of their cards and should (must?) follow suit (= advantage). (This rule is not clear.)
The player who played the card with the most advantages gets all the cards. The player to the left continues. The player with most tricks wins.

—user summary

Pájaros en Celaya ("Birds in Celaya") is a family-friendly game where players take on the role of birds exploring the charming city of Celaya, Guanajuato, México. Fly over historical landmarks, expand your flock, fight to control areas and collect beautiful cards. Through strategic movements and careful planning, compete to grab specific cards and score the most points. With colorful artwork and simple yet engaging gameplay, Pájaros en Celaya brings the city’s beauty to life from a bird's-eye view!

—description from the publisher

Promo building given out by Tony Boydell in November 2024 at his annual Gathering of Chums (hosted at The Kings Arms pub in Newent).

The building is printed on cardstock similar to the cards in Nusfjord; the obverse has an amusing joke regarding Nusfjord, in the style of a pun from Tony's Blog (https://boardgamegeek.com/blog/344/everyone-needs-a-shed).

Purchasing the card requires 5 wood, 3 fish and 2 gold.  When built, it covers a 2 x 2 board area and the card includes a building called The Kings Arms'; the building is worth 4 Points at game end.  The building's instructions include placing an Elder on the tile for use by all players; when the Elder would 'give fish', the owner takes 1 Gold into their reserve.

In addition, The King's Arms counts as a Forested double space for Thin Out and can even be Deforested (if you REALLY need to) :)

Originally available as part of the 2024 Support The Dice Tower Kickstarter campaign's biome pack.

Promo featuring a 1x1 Dice Tower wonder with the wonder card and a Mundo wonder card. The dice tower must be placed adjacent to Natural resources and a road. The bonus for completing this wonder is that it acts as a Tower.

The Mundo card gives extra points for having the Dice Tower adjacent to multiple road pieces.

boardgame: Windmill Valley: Calendar Ability Promo Cards
description:
Originally available as part of the 2024 Support The Dice Tower Kickstarter campaign's biome pack.

Promo cards for Windmill Valley that activate each time your wheel hits or crosses the red spoke (when you advanced your calendar tile). Cards are dealt at the start of the game, and each player gets that benefit throughout the game.

boardgame: The Fox Experiment: Study Promo Cards
description:
Originally available as part of the 2024 Support The Dice Tower Kickstarter campaign's biome pack.

Four promo cards for The Fox Experience that add extra study cards. These cards require a specialization in traits AND require a minimum friendliness score.

Introduction:Lublache (from Irish: lúb-bealach, meaning “loop path”) is a loop game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended board size is 7 cells per side, but boards with 5 or 9 are also valid. Each player has a sufficient supply of identical stones, each with one color on the front and the other on the back.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a maximal set of connected stones of any color. A single stone is also a group.
     A path is a subgroup of stones of any color within a group that traces a continuous line of minimal thickness (one stone wide) without branches. A group may contain multiple minimal paths.
     A loop is a closed path that surrounds empty cells or stones of any color. You control a loop if at least half of its stones are of your color.
     The length of a loop is the number of stones it contains.
     To flip is to replace enemy stones with friendly ones.
     A claimed loop is a loop made up of stones of a single color. Stones in claimed loops cannot be flipped.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty cell that is not adjacent to friendly loops and does not form part of a loop controlled by your opponent. If this action results in you controlling a loop, flip the enemy stones it contains until all are your color. Then, remove all friendly stones adjacent to the new loop.

If you cannot place a stone, you pass your turn; otherwise, passing is not allowed.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when both players pass in consecutive turns. The winner is the player with the longest claimed loop. In case of a tie, the next longest claimed loops of each player are compared until a difference is found. If still tied, the player who placed the last stone wins.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a black stone on an empty point and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer
New cards, the same black humor in this micro-expansion.

Back Tracks is a game you see and hear and feel!  An exciting, interactive game for families or groups that puts your tracing and guessing skills to the test. Optimal for team-building or as an entertaining party game. Move your train along the track by successfully guessing words traced on your back. Play a derail card to create a challenge for your opponents to get them off-track by making it more difficult to detect the correct word.

—description from the publisher

In Ancient Animals, players compare prehistoric animal features in the Top Trump style. The comparison winner gets to spin a spinner to collect bone tiles, which can be assembled into complete skeletons.

5 sekund po bandzie is a game where you think fast and talk fast! Just get it out of your head... or better yet, don't. This is a brand new game in the “5 Seconds” series designed for adult players who aren't afraid of a bit more spicy gameplay. Remember, in this game it's not the questions that are on the bandwagon... it's your answers! The game is designed for at least 3 players over the age of 18.

How to play?
Can you name 3 hard things? It won't do without ambiguous allusions! You have only 5 seconds to give 3 correct answers. With the game 5 Seconds After the Band the excitement reaches its zenith! Draw a card and try to answer the question before the timer runs out. Hurry, you only have 5 seconds.... Gather your friends and test your reflexes in a crazy game where time is not your ally.

—description from the publisher

Ghosts tour the graveyard (Rondel), 
carrying spirits with them (Pick-up and Deliver).

Game objective
Ghosts (players) collect bones, which are worth points, for return to their former selves.
The player with the most bones wins the game.
Can you collect the bones and return to your former selves?

A medium to heavyweight game with a Japanese horror theme that is highly re-playable!

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Kaldheim Commander: Elven Empire
description:
Awaiting Your Orders
