Choose your commander and overcome both friend and foe in this definitive multiplayer Magic format. Team up with Lathril and lead an army of elves to absolute victory. Grow your army to a massive scale and even the mightiest rivals will fall before you.

Lathril, Blade of the Elves

Hailing from a time before the wood and shadow elves were split apart, Lathril represents the glory that the elves hope to return to. She was a natural leader and master tactician whose prowess ensured her people held onto power.

100-card deck with 8 new cards
Deck box
10 double-sided token cards

Plus:

Life Wheel
Strategy insert
Reference card

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Kaldheim Commander: Phantom Premonition
description:
Exile and Ectoplasm

Choose your commander and overcome both friend and foe in this definitive multiplayer Magic format. Call forth Ranar and create a horde of spirits to do your bidding. Your spectral squadron swells every time you send a card into exile, which you'll be doing a lot.

Ranar the Ever-Watchful

In life, Ranar was an honored warrior who refuse to abandon his post in the face of helplessness. Recognizing his bravery, the Valkyries named him the guardian of Istfel and tasked him to guard the gates of the spirit realm as a noble, unyielding soul.

100-card deck with 8 new cards
Deck box
10 double-sided token cards

Plus:

Life Wheel
Strategy insert
Reference card

A semi-cooperative game that replicates Habitat for Humanity's work, as players compete to be the first to finish three homes for low-income families while not depriving other players of the resources they would need to finish their own projects post-game. A truly co-operative variant also requires all houses to be finished on time.

Proceeds from the sale of the game went to support the efforts of Habitat for Humanity in Cape Breton, where the game is set.

The popular question game is now available for all Harry Potter fans - whether small or large, in pairs or in a big group, Muggles, Squibs, witches or wizards - everyone can play! The practical card box in a handy take-along format is ideal for on the go, at home or as a fun party game. With over 60 cards, there are a variety of exciting questions - from challenging decisions to funny scenarios: Would you rather lose at wizard chess or go into the forbidden forest alone? Would you rather be related to Umbridge or Voldemort? Would you rather belong to Ravenclaw or Hufflepuff? How would you decide?

—description from the publisher

Created by Habitat for Humanity of Bulgaria, the game teaches three different classes of skills - family budgeting, housing maintenance and energy efficiency. Players have the option to take side-missions across the hexagonal tiles to go to the healthfood store, or visit the playground or classroom, and they gradually accumulate money, knowledge, happiness and dirt points. The player with the highest number of points cumulatively, wins.

A party game based on fast decision-making, dexterity and action, TeppanDo — 铁板道, "The Art of Teppanyaki" — will grant you a messy but fun game experience. You will have to collect, rotate, twist. and stack tiles to resolve a series of puzzles, just like you used to do in games such as Ubongo. However, this time, YOU CAN NOT TOUCH THE TILES! As a good cook, the only tools you could depend are tweezers and spatulas — plus the wisdom of you and your friend.

Work seamlessly with your assistant for a show-off in the party mode, compete head-to-head in a racing mode, or perfect your skills in a solo challenge. Flip raw ingredients into perfectly cooked dishes, all while avoiding chaotic mishaps like dropping tiles or serving uncooked creations. No matter stacking eggs over mushrooms or flipping wagyu (Japanese beef), every move counts in this culinary showdown.

Every order is a puzzle. You have to find, pick & collect the right ingredient tiles with your tweezers. Then  All tiles must be dropped on your teppan with raw side up, and then flipped to cooked side by using your spatulas. All before your timer runs out!

Finish as many orders as you can before the countdown ends. Each finished order card will bring you and your assistant scores. The more scores you get, the more probable you win the game tonight as a teppanyaki master!

—description from the publisher

Scarab Sands is a highly competitive, fast paced, area control game with almost no down time. Players compete as dead souls inside a pyramid, sending their scarab troops to fight for control over sacred rooms. Only the one with the lightest heart will make it to the Afterlife.

Each round starts with determining movement for scarab troops. This happens simultaneously for all players, by using command arrows on player mats hidden by screens. Movement is resolved in turn for 1) basic units,2) flying units and 3) armored units; Therefore, moving basic units happens almost blindly, guessing the moves of the opponents, while armored troops move when most of the stage is set.  After movement, combat is resolved through dice rolls in every room where two or more players' troops are present, until a single player (or none) controls it. Each room offers rewards for controlling it, such as upgrading troops or getting an obelisk die.

Player starts with 7 heart tokens, and the first one to free all of them, wins the game. There are multiple ways to free a heart, such as (1) controlling the pharaoh room, (2) simultaneously owning the three obelisk dice or (3) spending Ma'at influence points (mostly gained when losing a battle).

Whenever a scarab dies in combat, heart tokens are flipped to their "larvae" side. These tokens are flipped back to hearts in the last chamber of the pyramid, spawning one basic unit for each larvae flipped. Therefore the closer a player is to victory, the less basic units they are going to be able to spawn.

—description from the designer

Daupun (from Welsh: dau ddeg pump, meaning “25”) is a two-player card game: south and north. It is played with a 40-card French deck (from Ace to 10; without J, Q, or K; the Ace is worth 1) and stones for the stakes.

				
				
					Definitions:
     The reserve is the set of five cards discarded by each player in the construction phase.
     A trick is a pair of opposed cards in the battle phase.
     The battlefield is the set of five tricks in the center of the table.
     The position of a trick is the value of each trick according to the order played: the first is worth 5 stones, the second 4, and so on until the fifth, which is worth 1 stone.
     The distance of a trick is the difference between the opposed cards of that trick.
     The value of a trick is (position + distance) × number of tricks won; the total defines the value of the battlefield for each player.
     A crown is 250 stones. The game ends when a player reaches the agreed number of crowns (1, 2, or 3).
     The pot is where the stones of the stakes are placed.


				
				
					Initial deal:Each round assigns at random a dealer and a cutter. The cutter begins the round. Each player receives five cards. The rest forms the draw pile.

				
				
					Structure of a round:A round has two phases, with south playing first (in the next round, the winner of the previous round plays first):


     Construction phase: The goal is that your five cards sum to at least 25. If you do not reach it, you lose all stones gained (unless the opponent also fails to reach 25).
         You draw one card from the draw pile (you now have six).
         You optionally make a stake (the process is explained later).
         You discard one card to keep five and place it face up in the reserve.
         You exchange cards between your hand and the reserve up to three times, only when the reserve has five cards.
    


Upon reaching 25, you may say “I stay” to stop. The opponent may choose between “I stay” and “I leave” (grants one stone to the opponent).


     Battle phase: If both stay, combat begins. The five cards of each player are arranged from lowest to highest and face off 1 vs. 1 in five tricks. A trick is won with the lowest card. After the battle, tricks are removed according to this priority:
         Ties are removed.
         If pairs remain, the lowest ones are removed until leaving an odd number. If it is still even, the lowest one is removed.
    


Possible cases:

     0 ties: the two lowest are removed (3 remain).
     1 tie: the tie and the lowest are removed (3 remain).
     2 ties: both are removed (3 remain).
     3 ties: the three and the lowest are removed (1 remains).
     4 ties: the four are removed (1 remains).
     5 ties: all are removed (0 remain).


There must always remain an odd number of tricks; the only total tie occurs when there are five tied tricks and all are removed, in this case, no player takes the stones from the pot.

				
				
					Stakes:During construction, after drawing and before discarding, you may:

     I stake: place three stones in the pot if it is empty, or one if it is not.
     I stake to N: set the pot at N stones, without exceeding what is necessary to complete a crown.


The opponent responds:

     I’m in: accepts the stake.
     I’m out: rejects the stake and grants two stones to the opponent.
     I raise to N: increases the stake, setting the pot at N stones, without exceeding what is necessary to complete a crown.


				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when a player reaches the crowns agreed upon at the beginning. Whoever achieves that number wins the game.

—description from the designer

Would you rather speak fluent Mermish or Gobbledegook? Would you rather try out the "Gringotts rollercoaster" or ride a Thestral? This magical version of the popular classic game offers you many more difficult choices like this about Harry Potter, Hogwarts and Co. With this game, you will get to know yourself and the world of your favorite hero better than ever before, which is why it should definitely be included at your next game night - because fun is guaranteed!

—description from the back of the box

Würdest du lieber fließend Meerisch oder Koboldogack sprechen? Lieber die »Gringotts-Achterbahn« ausprobieren oder auf einem Thestral reiten? Diese magische Version des beliebten Spieleklassikers hält noch viele weitere solcher schwierigen Entscheidungen rund um Harry Potter, Hogwarts und Co. für dich bereit. Mit diesem Spiel lernt ihr euch und die Welt eures Lieblingshelden so gut kennen wie noch nie, weshalb es beim nächsten Spieleabend auf keinen Fall fehlen darf – denn hier ist Spaß vorprogrammiert!

—description from the back of the box

A horrible crime was committed on the outskirts of a quiet town: escaped convicts kidnapped a family with children and left without a trace. The police are stumped: it seems like these people just... disappeared. All thet's left of them are notes of the daughter and the mother, made hastily.

The police have nobody else to turn to: they consult you, experienced psychics that can travel back in time and change the course of the events.

Study the notes left of the missing people, follow their footsteps and make decisions in split situations. Be careful and you will get a chance to save the live of these unfortunate souls.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Pass auf, was du sagst: Die Teenieedition
description:
Do you like going to school? Are you in love right now?

Would you answer “yes” or “maybe” to these questions? Or rather with a clear “No”? Attention! None of these answers are allowed in this game. And the basic rules of the classic game Watch what you say are explained just as simply.

Only those who answer cleverly enough can score points here. The tricky special cards make the whole thing even more fun. Whether on a school trip, at your next party or just in between - there's plenty of fun to be had here, as well as a confession or two!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Hair coloring is a fascinating and complex process. It is a matter of craftmanship that requires a keen eye, accurate judgment, and attention to detail. Hair Color Game-Colorology is the first board game designed to help you hone your ability to evaluate customers' hair colors.
By looking at hair cards, you will learn to identify hair colors based on actual salon terminology. You will then learn to consider hair color as a space within which to move, by mapping it first, and then establishing color paths with the help of a special color compass with its own rules and cardinal directions.

—description from the publisher

La colorazione dei capelli è un processo affascinante e complesso; è un'attività artigianale che richiede occhio sopraffino, giudizio accurato e attenzione al dettaglio. Hair Color Game-Colorology è il primo gioco da tavolo pensato per aiutarti a migliorare la tua capacità di valutare il colore dei capelli delle clienti.
Osservando delle carte capello, imparerai a identificare il colore dei capelli con la terminologia realmente impiegata nei saloni. Imparerai poi a considerare il colore dei capelli come uno spazio entro cui spostarsi, per cominciare mappandolo, e poi stabilendo dei percorsi colore con l'aiuto di una speciale bussola del colore con le sue specifiche regole e direzioni cardinali.

—description from the publisher (Italian)

Residents of the city can no longer sleep peacefully—now, the streets are ruled by rival cat gangs. Players take on the role of gang leaders, sending their cats out in search of loot.

Each round, you’ll secretly play one cat card. But if you and the other players choose cats that are too powerful and noisy, the homeowners will wake up and chase you off. Your goal each round is to send out the most powerful cat you can without waking the light-sleeping homeowners. To succeed, you’ll need to consider the unique features of each location and anticipate which cards your opponents might play.

boardgame: Thunder Road: Vendetta  – Turbo Tina's Swag Pack
description:
Turbo Tina can see that you all came to party hearty! She's digging the success of this year's Carnival so much she put a special swag pack together as thanks.

Turbo Tina's Swag Pack collects the bonus components (stretch goals) for the Carnival of Chaos expansion. It was given to backers as a reward in the latter's campaign and is available for purchase by the retail buyers.

Notably, the pack includes additional sleeves for Carnival of Chaos and all the cards in the pack, in the same style as the Thunder Road: Vendetta – Sleeves.

boardgame: Fateforge: Chronicles of Kaan – Kin of the Wild
description:
Embark on an unforgettable adventure in Kin of the Wild, where the frozen lands of Northern Kaan await. This expansion introduces Pets—animal companions that fight, explore, and grow with you. These creatures aren’t just tagalongs—they’re mini-characters with their own unique Pet boards, abilities, action dice, and beautifully detailed miniatures.

Your journey takes you to the snow-swept harbor city of Katafygia. The shaman of a forest clan has been kidnapped, and the Kartaçöl Empire’s presence in the region is growing stronger by the day. Fearing an invasion of their forest homeland, the council has sent you to investigate. But you won’t be facing these dangers alone. Your loyal animal companions will be at your side, offering both strength and wisdom as you uncover the dark forces at work in the North.

Along with the introduction of Pets, this expansion brings even more depth to your adventures with two new factions, each more unpredictable and deadly than before, with new leader ability decks to keep you on your toes. Explore fresh terrain with two new floorplan sets—forest and wooden interiors—along with additional story scene tiles to keep the narrative fresh and dynamic. You’ll also discover new rest locations, weapons, and items to aid in your journey.

Whether you’re a fan of deep strategy, rich narrative, or tactical combat, Kin of the Wild offers an exciting new chapter filled with new mechanics and immersive storytelling.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description:
Quatuor is a fast-paced, family game where you try to line up your pawns by moving them around a 4x4 chessboard.

To achieve this goal, each player must place and then push pawns to align 4 of his.  Be mindfull that your opponent can push you out of the way to get his pawns aligned.

In Quatuor, there is no place for luck, just skills.  It's a great game to learn strategy and anticipation.

—description from the publisher

After all the miles clocked in Rallyman: Dirt, it’s time to add new content to shake up the rally world and spice up those special stages. New cars, new tires, new tiles, and new terrain fuse together with new strategic elements in this add-on.

In the fully customizable world of Rallyman: Dirt, the Monte Carlo expansion incorporates all the features of the original 110%, R4, R5 expansions as well as part of the original Co-pilot expansion. The possibilities and combinations become endless as strategy reaches a whole new level.

Take on tougher challenges in Rallyman: DIRT – Monte-Carlo. This expansion includes the original 110%, R4, and R5 expansions as well as portions of the original Co-Pilot expansion that will take your rallies to a whole other level. Get ready for unpredictable and exhilarating rallies that will offer heightened strategy thanks to all new surface types. Tackle snowy terrain and burn rubber on the asphalt with new dedicated tire types and brand-new vehicles.


     Choose between a more powerful or more agile car to help you set the fastest times. 
     Use the bridge to add new variations or chicanes to create obstacles on the course. 
     Mix different terrain types and adjust your rolls while contending with variable limits.


—description from the publisher

Jeu en print and play qui simule la course du Vendée Globe 2024-2025.
Le jeu est gratuit et librement diffusable. Il suffit de télécharger les 4 fichiers (plateau de jeu, pions, feuille de marque et règles).
Les pions représentent tous les skippers actuellement en course (avec une valeur de pronostic établie par le magazine Voiles et Voiliers).
Le but ? Parier sur les bons concurrents qui finiront aux 3 premières places. Sauf que le choix de chaque joueur est secret...Arriverez-vous à faire avancer discrètement vos champions sans attirer les foudres des avaries des autres joueurs ? Aurez-vous du bol dans le pot-au-noir ou au Cap Horn ? Miserez-vous sur un outsider plus difficile à faire gagner mais qui vous rapportera beaucoup de points ou sécuriserez-vous la course avec un favori ?

Print-and-play game simulating the Vendée Globe 2024-2025 race.
The game is free and can be shared freely. All you need to do is download the four files (game board, tokens, score sheet, and rules).

The tokens represent all the current skippers in the race, each with a forecast value established by Voiles et Voiliers magazine.

The goal? Bet on the right competitors who will finish in the top three. But there’s a twist: each player’s choices are kept secret... Can you discreetly advance your champions without attracting the misfortunes caused by other players? Will you have luck on your side in the Doldrums or at Cape Horn? Will you bet on an underdog, harder to secure a win with but offering more points, or play it safe with a favorite?

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Narrative Encounter Pack 1 – The Ghost of Curly Jones
description: This Narrative Encounter Pack contains everything you need to play an Adventure searching for old Curly's treasure and revealing the story of The Ghost of Curly Jones along the way. Included are a Rules card, a unique set of linear, numbered Narrative Encounter Cards with a branching story, Threat cards to add The Ghost of Curly Jones to your Adventures, and a Mine Artifact, as well as The Ghost of Curly Jones Narrative Story, large Enemy Record Sheet, and the Miniature for the Legendary ghostly prospector that haunts the mines.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: One Shot Card Supplement 2024 Edition
description:
Designed specifically for one-off Adventures and Event Games, this set includes a One Shot deck for Loot and Scavenge that emphasizes finding Gear and Artifact cards, as well as delivering Gold and Dark Stone in easy to use, even amounts to track with simple tokens. It also includes Large cards for a simple In-Game Store of Items and Upgrades that can be purchased by Heroes during an Adventure using the Gold and Dark Stone they have collected along the way.

There are 5 different In-Game Store large cards, based on the theme of the Heroes being played. This set also includes game cards for all of the different Items and Upgrades that can be purchased on these In-Game Store large cards.

Though this set is not intended for normal Campaign play (as Heroes will get too many Gear/Artifacts too quickly), it can also be used to give your Heroes a boost if they are struggling, especially for smaller Hero Parties or low level/starting Heroes with a lot of Expansion Enemies added into the mix.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Dicefest 2024 Goody Box
description: The goody box with exclusive Shadows of Brimstone content, available on the Flying Frog Productions webstore during 2024 Dicefest.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: 2024 Dicefest Exclusive promos
description:
These promos were available at the 2024 Dicefest convention, and could be acquired in person by participationg in various activities. Included are:

Map to Curly's Gold - Legendary Gear

Painted Shield - Gear, Shield

Rusty Mouth Harp - Gear

Regal Cape - Gear, Clothing, Shoulders

Widowmaker Lantern - Artifact, Haunted, Light Source

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Dicefest 2024 Web Store Promo Cards
description:
Two promo cards offered on orders of $100 or more on the Flying Frog webstore during Dicefest 2024:


     Savage Things - Darkness (ART-003)
     Roar of the Hell Bear - Darkness (ART-004)

MapGoMap is an innovative educational game designed to help players of all ages learn and memorize geography in a fun and engaging way. It incorporates various modes of play to cater to different learning styles and preferences. MapGoMap utilizes map-thinking logic to enhance the memorization process, making it an effective tool for understanding and recalling geographical locations. Whether it's for educational purposes, family game nights, or social events, MapGoMap serves as a unique approach to learning geography while having fun.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ancient Knowledge: Desert View Watchtower Promo Card
description:
Originally available as part of the 2024 Support The Dice Tower Kickstarter campaign's atom pack.

A promo card adding the Desert View Watchtower to your game. Shuffle it into your deck and play!

Expansion Icon: Watchtower

Promotional cards to start the collection of the game Stickers in a very small booster format.

Welcome to the Stickers’ Collectors World Gathering – the SWoG. Who boasts the most impressive collection? The rarest? The most diversified? You’re here to answer this question. Each of you will do their best to put their hands on the Holy Grail of any stickers’ collector: the supreme title of Greatest Collector of the Galaxy – and nothing less!

In Stickers, players compete to collect the most cards in each family of cards. However, each family is not worth the same! To win, you’ll need to collect the rarest cards of the rarest families!

Stickers is a constantly evolving game: after each game, you’ll discover new rules and new game elements. Your game will evolve according to your choices.

boardgame: Campaign Trail: Jack Pumpkin and Jill Crow
description:
Promo candidate card.

“It’s your season, may as well make a difference while you have people’s attention.”

Candidate abilities:
Starting funds: $60M, Starting registered voters: 6

At the end of your turn, move 2 committed voters from the state in which you are located to any adjacent state.

You have a cap of in number of voters you can have in the states:
States worth 20 EV or greater - max of 11 voters
States worth 19 or less - max 6 voters

Card is double-sided.  Jack Pumpkin and Jill Crow have identical abilities, only the character art differs.

This a a demo version of 10 cards

Little Secret is the game where you have to uncover the intruder. Members of a secret organisation do meetings to verify the identity of the participants with the help of a secret word. Only the Disciples have the correct secret word. Among them are hiding Infiltrators and a Journalist.
It's up to you to unmask them without being eliminated!

Step 1: Each player has a card with secret words that will define their role during the game.

Step 2: By turns, each player will give a clue to their secret word, the aim being to find out who has a different word to theirs (or who has none, in the case of the Journalist).

Step 3: It's time for players to debate and vote to eliminate the Infiltrator or Journalist. You will need to form alliances with other players to unmask the intruder.

Step 4: After a player is eliminated, the game continues until all intruders have been unmasked. Players who have not been eliminated earn points.

Bonus: from 6 players upwards, it is possible to add "Powers" cards to liven up the game.

A simple collection-based card game, you must gather your hamster's favorite treats! Take from the pile in the middle, or steal from other players. Each hamster species has a favorite treat worth 2 points, or the absolute favorite sunflower seeds are worth 3!

—description from the publisher

Originally available as part of the 2024 Support The Dice Tower Kickstarter campaign's atom pack.

Add the Rum Vessel promo card to your deck.

boardgame: Last Light: The Core Promo Card
description:
Originally available as part of the 2024 Support The Dice Tower Kickstarter campaign's atom pack.

Add The Core to your game, giving you more disk rotation opportunities!

Text says: Immediately after the board rotates, you may choose to rotate one disc one extra position.

Originally available as part of the 2024 Support The Dice Tower Kickstarter campaign's atom pack.

Gives every player a once-per-game ability. Every player gets one card from this deck and they may use its power once per game.

Includes 9 cards + 1 rules card.

Originally available as part of the 2024 Support The Dice Tower Kickstarter campaign's atom pack.

Adds the Big Tom tile to your game. Allows you to split your damage dice between multiple enemies.

boardgame: Dicebound Heroes: A roll & write adventure
description:
One-page dungeon crawler is a print&play, roll&write, puzzle, exploration, and role-playing game.

Each turn, players will roll 3 d6 dice, and results will determine heroes movement, magical color, and creatures growth. Players will have to decide which die results go where. Players will spend time exploring maps, collecting treasures, equipping weapons to their characters, and even using collected diamonds to socket them for magical bonuses to their attributes.

Brave heroes have only 24 hours to explore the dungeon. Mission is to eliminate evil and collect treasure for ultimate glory and fame. They have to be very careful; each decision is crucial. Monks will seal the entrance after 24 hours. Heroes must be quick, or they could remain stuck there forever.

More time they spent exploring, more treasures, and more items they found, but closer they are to the end game. Its game of balance and decision-making. Don't be greedy. And of course, heroes cannot visit the same location within the dungeon twice! Evil is lurking and waiting for heroes to make mistakes. They must be quick.

The task was not one to be taken lightly; the goal was not merely to claim the riches hidden within but to cleanse the dungeon of its dark presence. If the heroes failed, the forces of evil would expand, their grotesque forms ultimately breaking free into the world above.

Trick taking card game for 2 or more players where special cards score positive or negative points when taken in tricks. the 2, 10 and J of Hearts score the highest positive points and the 2, 10 and J of Spade score the highest negative points, hence the name of the game
Dutch card game very similar to UNO game but in a Minecraft like theme. Goal is to play all your cards. There are also cards to tease other players.

Gekitou! Rommel Corps was created as part of the "40" series. The concept of the "40" series involves simulation games that use fewer than 40 tokens and each game lasts about 40 minutes. However, with the reprint, some markers were added, bringing the
number of tokens to 50.
The theme of Gekitou! Rommel Corps is Operation "Crusader", launched by the British 8th Army on November 18, 1941. This
operation led to the British forces breaking the siege of Tobruk, which was surrounded by Axis troops.
The game is a duel between two players: one controls the British forces, while the other leads the Axis forces (German and Italian). The first edition of Gekitou! Rommel Corps was published in 2013 in the Wargame Handbook (published by Kokusai-Tsushinsha).

boardgame: Skyrockets: Festivals of Fire – P2 Double Down Promo Card
description:
Originally available as part of the 2024 Support The Dice Tower Kickstarter campaign's atom pack.

Festival Name: Elite Exclusive Invite Only
Event Name: Double Down
Event Number: P2
Hand Size: 4
Starting Timer Positions: All Running
Additional Rules:
One non-countdown timer at a time may be empty without you losing.

The only way to advance the Countdown Timer (when it's empty) is to play a card with colors matching both the Countdown Timer and an empty non-countdown timer.

Originally available as part of the 2024 Support The Dice Tower Kickstarter campaign's biome pack.

Setup: Simply include the Cruise Ship cards in the C-level-pile of Challengers! or Challengers! Beach Cup. Then enjoy the game as usual! Rainbow Set cards have all Set icons. They receive all Set-specific power bonuses and can be targeted by all Set-specific effects of other cards.

Name: Cruise Ship
Set: Rainbow
Base Power: 5
Effect: Put a card from your bench on your exhaust pile. Put the top card from the Level-B-Pile on your deck.

Assembly Cost: 1 Power + 3 Microchip + 2 Sensor or 5 Power + 3 Microchip
Victory Points: 5
Associated Phase: Design, Fabricate, Recycle
Robot Name: Tommy
Basic Action: 2 Power to discard X robot cards and draw X + 1
Upgrade Type: Blue
Upgraded Action: 4 Power to discard X robot cards and draw X + 2

Game Night
4 Gentry + Footman -> £200 See 4 Guests Take 3
-1 VP

Convention
9 or More Gentry + Footman -> VP Card
5 VP

8 power
Attack: If you have the same number of Mindbugs as the opponent, the opponent loses 2 life.

P1/2024

boardgame: AI: 100% Human – Dice Tower Promo Card
description:
DT1
Number: ?
Color: Gaming (blue)
Effect: End of Game 3 VP if 1 column of same number, 7 VP if 2 adjacent columns of same number, 15 VP if 3 adjacent columns of same number
Placement: Middle of tableau

DT2
Number: 3
Color: All
Effect: End of Game 4 VP
Placement: Middle of tableau

Boxo Blox is an all ages block stacking party game.  Be the first player to stack your blocks on the tower without them falling off. Take turns rolling dice, drawing challenge cards, and stacking blocks in ridiculous ways.

Players take turns rolling either 1 or 2 dice. Then matching the number that you roll, with the sum of your blocks. So if you roll a 6, you can stack your #6 block on the stacking base, or you can stack a #1 and a #5 block etc, it just has to equal your roll. If you don’t have the blocks to match your roll, you can’t go and the next player rolls. If you can go, before you stack, you must draw a challenge card and stack accordingly.

To win the game, be the first player to get rid of your blocks by stacking them. However, on your final turn, in order to win the game you have an extra challenge. You must draw 3 challenge cards and play them all together.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: HACK! Simple rules for large-scale fantasy battles with miniatures
description:
HACK! is a set of rules for large armies of fantasy miniatures that is easy to learn and simple to play. 
Completely miniature agnostic, hours of enjoyment await you as you journey into the fantasy regions and struggle for victory against powerful adversaries.

HACK! is an open-ended system in which you can create scenarios for any situation, from any environment you desire. Spells, abilities, beasts are all at your disposal in this simple set of rules. The limits are only imposed by your imagination.

HACK! provides you with a simple, clearly written rules that allows you to resolve any fantasy battle. Included is a small set of scenarios to get you on your way. You can use whatever fantasy figures you currently own. The unit types are listed as general categories, allowing the formation of armies with whatever figures you have available. No need to purchase new sets.

HACK! is ideal for beginning as well as more experienced players. 
The challenge is in the play, not the learning. 
So get ready. Victory awaits….

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Fantasy Hack: Rules for Fantasy Miniature Wargaming
description:
Fantasy Hack:Rules for Fantasy Miniature Wargaming was originally published in 1993. This is a reproduction of that. The book includes the core rules and an appendix for monsters, and spells. The rules provide the ability to do battles with armies or even individual figures. Big or small these rules will be fun for all.

Fantasy Hack will take players to any place or time in the wonderful worlds of fantasy created by Lewis and Tolkien. 
The game system is easily learned and games will run for 2 to 4 hours. 
It is designed for use with miniatures.

—description from the publisher

A vicious card shedding game with beloved simple mechanics wrapped in a pirate theme.

Skuttled! is a fast game of piratical shenanigans where everyone is out to get one another.  One play of the card can thwart even the best laid plans.  Beware the Kraken!

The goal is to get rid of, or to "Skuttle" your hand of cards, to be the first player to go "out."  But it is never that easy!

Hinge is a strategic game of exnihilation for 2 players. What in the world does that mean? It means Hinge is an annihilation game played on an initially empty board. Games like Oust and Lifeline precede Hinge in this category and the former by almost two decades. Hinge however has an entirely unique set of strategic procedures from its predecessors and therefore offers as much as any other entirely new game. Some may find it to have the tactical sharpness of Oust and the visual clarity of Lifeline. A game of Hinge will not end in a draw.  Corey L. Clark invented Hinge in fall of 2024.

Setup: Players will need a hex hex board with a side-length of at least size 6 (size 7 is recommended) and an unlimited number of double-sided discs.

Gameplay: On a player's turn, he may either place a disc with his color facing up on a legal cell or swipe an enemy group.

swiping involves moving a disc around a friendly disc one or two spaces such that the moving disc contacts at least one enemy group at the end of the move (possibly even one it already contacted at the outset of the turn). Swiping results in the complete conversion (that is flipping) of exactly one of these enemy groups to the swiping players color. If you contact more than one enemy group, you must choose which one to capture. At the conclusion of the turn, the group the stone was moved from cannot remain split (that is it must contain all the stones which were in the initial group) otherwise the move is illegal.

Placement is subject to the following restrictions:

     The placement cannot place a disc where it removes all the empty cells adjacent to a group of any color
     The placement cannot start a new group adjacent to an enemy disc


Objective: The objective of Hinge is to remove all enemy discs from the board.

—description from the designer

Star Chart is a deep puzzle game; pass cards around the table, forming your star chart to match as many constellations as possible. Get 15 points first to win!

The game also comes with a booklet of puzzles to solve yourself or with friends :)

Star Chart is a drafting game, each turn all players pick a card from their hand to add to their chart, and one card to pass on to the player to their right. Then, each player checks if they have matched a constellation in the sky or in their hand, and if they have, they can take one and score points.

Then, players may use one of their three once-per-game abilities which allow them to modify their chart and match once more. Finally, players pick up the card passed to them and draw up to 5 cards.

This is then repeated until a player reaches 15 points or the deck runs out.

—description from the publisher

Originally available as part of the 2024 Support The Dice Tower Kickstarter campaign's comet pack.

Setup:
Place the extension tiles that correspond to the colours of the lines in play next to the board.
Extensions are treated the same as standard track tokens - once placed on the board they are part of a player's network and the passenger may move along them.

Gameplay:
A player may spend one branch tile to take an extension tile matching one of their line colours. Doing so does not cost an action.
As an action, a player may place an extension tile on to the board as if it were a track token of the same colour (and under the same restrictions)

boardgame: The UFO Disclosure Game: We are not alone
description:
You have realized that the UFO phenomenon is real, and you want to get to the bottom of it, bringing it to the attention of the whole world. But you can't do it by yourself. You must acquire knowledge and credibility by collecting evidence and persuading individuals with high credibility to validate the evidence, all while avoiding the stigma and potential pitfalls of the path. The whole game is an amazing travel through real UFO/UAP cases that happened worldwide!

Each player chooses a different character who has a specific background related to the phenomenon of UAP/UFOs and starts his globetrotting from the continent where her/his character was born. To fulfil his mission, each player moves on the board, launching a die to travel around the world to collect the best UFO events. At the same time, the player needs to meet qualified persons who could confirm your evidence. Be aware that skeptics, debunkers, and other traps will try to stop you!

As soon as you have an event matching a specific qualified person (e.g., a scientist), you have access to the “Disclosure’s Path." The trivia starts there: you need to prove your knowledge about the topic, answering some questions about the history of the UFO phenomenon while defending your reputation with media!
Though everyone is building their own collection of UFO case investigation, the game offers ways for players to interfere with each other.

The first player who collects three different confirmations can handle the truth and can expose her/his theory to humankind, winning the run for the final disclosure!

—description from the designer

You have finally reached the site where the treasures are hidden. Now you must follow the compass instructions to lift the riches. Be quick, because your rivals are close at your heels.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dust Race: Press Start – Extra Drive No. 1 Promo Pack
description:
Dust Race: Press Start – Extra Drive No. 1 is a promo pack for the game Dust Race: Press Start.

It contains one new vehicle (the "BD-Dream"), consisting of 1 vehicle card, 1 vehicle standee as well as one base for the standee.

It was given away at Essen Spiel 2024.

The largest cavalry action in the American War of Independence, the Battle of the Hook saw Lauzen’s Legion and Lieutenant Colonel Mercer’s Select Battalion of Militia defeat a British detachment of cavalry and Royal Fusiliers who were positioned at a fortification in Gloucester Town led by Colonel Tarletan. Outflank your foes by using the different terrain hexes to your advantage. If you are the British player, deploy your Hessian allies for the advantage. For the American player, team up with the French army for extra firepower. With only 24 turns to play, the average game can be enjoyed in under two hours. The game also has optional rules for players looking for extra depth.

The game offers two scenarios:

- The Battle of the Hook focuses on the core battle between the American and British forces. This is the default mode of play.

- Cornwallis Breaks Out is a "what if" scenario, where General Cornwallis makes his daring escape across the York River, avoiding American captivity. Recommended for advanced players.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description: Add more than than 30 cards to the main parasite deck to increase the maximum number of players, the combos and the fun!

Puzzle Dungeon Intro Deck is a new introductory deck for the endlessly playable monster-hunting solitaire card game Puzzle Dungeon!

New content includes:

     16 Hero cards (double sided)
     5 Monster types (normal/king sides)
     10 traps and treasures
     32 arsenal cards

The most amusing, entertaining and enjoyable time you’ll have with family and friends.

The first player to get 5 consecutive cards on their table deck wins the game!
Start the game by shuffling the cards and dealing 5 to each player — this is your hand. The remaining cards become the draw pile on the table.
At any given time, each player can hold a maximum of 5 cards in their hand, and 5 cards on their table deck.
Play starts with any player and then moves clockwise.

The cards consist of:

Number cards (1-10): These are used for regular play, in order to create a table deck of 5 consecutive cards. We call them Bots.

GOOD GUY (GG) cards: these Guys are wild cards and can be used as any number on your table deck. When played, you must declare the number it will be and you cannot change it. If a GG is stolen then its value can be reassigned. The winning sequence can include any number of GG cards.

BAD GUY (BG) cards: these Guys allow you to snag one card from one opponent’s table deck and bring it to your own hand or table deck.

NOT SO SMART GUY (NSSG) cards: these Guys allow you to take any card from one opponent’s hand and bring it to your hand. You cannot look at your opponent’s hand — you just choose one, and trust your instincts!

JERK (J) cards: these Guys allow you to choose one opponent and wipe out their entire table deck. All their table deck cards go into the discard pile, under the J card.

On a turn, pick ONE of these actions:
Place a number or GG card on your table deck to build your 5 consecutive number sequence. Once you have committed to a sequence, it cannot be changed unless you’re wiped out by a J card.
Play a BG, NSSG or J card by dropping it on the discard pile and telling one opponent the card is for them.
Discard a number card from your hand to the discard pile. You may not discard a GG card. You cannot discard from your table deck.
Pick a card from the draw pile and add it to your hand (but only if you have less than 5 cards in your hand).
You may pick up the top card from the discard pile, so long as it is a number card. You cannot pick up a BG, NSSG or J card from the discard pile.
If the draw pile finishes, take all the cards on the discard pile, shuffle them, and use them as the new draw pile.

—description from the designer

boardgame: S.T.A.L.K.E.R. The Board Game: Personal Stories
description:
Personal Stories delves deeper into the Stalkers' stories, offering diverse tasks that reveal their motivations, haunted pasts, and personal desires. While not necessary to enjoy the main story campaign or Zone survival mode, it enriches the background narratives of the available characters, providing additional depth and insight.

—description from the publisher

There are 2 tracks on the game board (one with white spaces, the other with blue spaces). The game begins at the arrows marked Start.
From the Channel coast, the Germans advance to the track of white dots. From the Mediterranean, the Italians advance to the track of blue dots. The red spaces are English bases that must be taken by the players, which take turns to roll and move. Whoever lands on the letter Ⓐ has to go back to the starting point and start again. Spaces with a letter count as follows:
B = Bunker 3 points
G = Convoy 3 points
U = Submarine 2 points
F = Aircraft 1 point
K = Cruiser 3 points
E = English base 5 points
The points hit are noted and added up. Whoever lands on a letter (i.e. hits the enemy) can roll the dice again as often as he gets a hit. Each party has to play the prescribed round. Whoever scores the most hits is the winner.

—user summary

At the space with the big "A" is the beginning. Each player places a token there. A die is then rolled once and the number of points rolled is moved forwards in the direction of the arrow. When counting the number of spaces to be advanced, the eight streets are also included. Anyone who can place their token in one of the streets that lead to the finish (white streets) will slide directly to the finish. 
Anyone who has to place their token in one of the blue streets will slide out of the game and have to start again from the beginning.
If a player has gone around the outer ring, he must move into the next ring via the two streets in front of his "A", and if he has neither luck nor misfortune there, he must also move into the third or even the fourth. But then, when he has gone through all the rings, he can go to the finish without further ado.
Two tokens should not be in a space. In such a case, the player must roll the die again. If he hits the same space again, he has to miss a turn.
Each player has four tokens, but should only place one in the game at a time. Whoever gets their four tokens to the finish first is the winner.

—user summary

boardgame: The League of the Extraordinary Collectors
description:
In a world where ideas, images, and words take physical form, Dr. Gregory Faustroll’s prophetic monkeys type the future on a skyscreeper-high typewriter: the Oracle. In this detective game you and your fellow Investigators must question this living oracle to answer 8 difficult question about eight surreal characters: the Extraordinary Collectors.

				
				
					OverviewOne player becomes the Oracle, who can cheat knowing all the answers to the 8 questions (peeking the end of the booklet), but can only speak through Rebus— abstract images built from overlapping tiles. The others are Investigators, who must interpret those images, propose meanings, and slowly construct a shared symbolic language between human and machine.
This booklet is not merely a prop — it is the game’s true grammar. In fact the game involves the innovative mechanism of the Referents: a system that players will use to anchor the symbols they see to this book texts and illustrations. Without it, communication between Oracle and Investigators would collapse into pure abstraction.

Your notes, drawings, and guesses become the game’s evolving dictionary that you will use through your 8 adventures.

By the end, you will not just have solved the mysteries —you will have invented a language, one glyph at a time.

				
				
					What Makes it UniqueIn most detective games, events are mysterious but language is clear. In The League, it’s the opposite: the story is obvious, but the language is the mystery.
Other detective games hide the facts — The League hides the language.
Here, the crime is visible, but the words to describe it must be invented.

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events benefit or hinder the players. The first to reach space 100 wins the game.

—user summary
family: Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

description:
An expansion for World Order that adds to the game actual events that took place since 2010, allowing the players to not only see their effect on global relations, but also get the chance to prevent them and change the course of history.

Will you allow Brexit to happen?

Could the tensions in Ukraine have been avoided?

How did the pandemic affect global economies?

Learn about these and many other important events that shaped our times and witness their consequences within World Order!

—description from the publisher

Take on the role of a Robot Doctor working to rebuild your robot patients from piles of parts. Complete beautifully designed robots, scoring different points for each. Who will get the required number of points first?

The game uses an innovative draft, instead of typical card drawing, that is simple to learn and creates excitement and allows for some awesome chained plays.

Special upgrade cards like "Bubble matrix" and "Chromagrapple", or procedure cards like "Poach", and "Clear!" allow for interactions and disruption of other players.

Easy to pick up and learn as you play, for all ages and experience levels!

(Parents: it's an enjoyable game to play with your kids, let them surprise you with the strategies they discover!)

—description from the designer

The SYW Battle Series (SYWBS) games are playable grand-tactical portrayals of battles of the Seven Years War. The series’ intent is to show how command operates on the battlefields, using the weapons and tactics of the period. The system emphasizes command & control and playability.
The games all share the following characteristics:

     Scale = 200-220 m/hex (219-240 yrd/hex)
     Time = 30 min/Turn
     Counter = 1 Brigade, 1 Battalion, 1 to 3 Squadrons, 1 Battery, 1 Leader


Each unit's historical flag and uniform are displayed on Counters, Orders/organizational Displays, and Orders of battle (in Playbooks).

Kunersdorf 1759, the third game in the "SYWBS" series, simulates the battle fought on the 12th of August 1759 in Brandebourg, near Frankfurt an der Oder, during the Seven Years War. General Count Piotr Semionovitch Saltykov commanded a combined army composed of a Russian Army: 60,150 men (70 bns, 63 sqns + 45 Cossacks sotnias) and 201 field guns and an Austrian contingent commanded by FML baron Ernst Gideon Loudon: 19,200 men (16 bns + Grenzers, 36 sqns) and 10 field guns. King of Prussia Frederick II the Great commanded the Prussian Army: 50,200 men (63 bns, 112 sqns) and 160 field guns in total.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Endeavor: Deep Sea – Orca Reintroduction, Underwave Academy, and Robot Wanderer Mini Expansion
description:
Orca Reintroduction: You have teamed up with a faclity that rehabilitates injured and displaced Orcas, preparing them to be returned to the wild. With your help, these majestic creatures can return to their ocean home and thrive!

Underwave Academy: The Underwave Academy isa next-generation training facility specially built for your team to get the underwater field experience and learn valuable new skills.

Robot Wanderer: New technology at your fingertips. Join up with the Wanderer Dock to control the sophisticated upgradeable Wanderer!

Game based on the TV series of the same name. Follow the path on the game board and try to get the entire football team onto your playing field. collect players by answering questions or carrying out activity assignments.

Trixx is an educational word card game that challenges players to expand their vocabulary while engaging in competitive play. Designed for 2-6 players (or teams of up to 12), the game revolves around creating words from letter cards to score points. The longer and more complex the word, the higher the score. The game incorporates a board with special bonus spaces, wildcards, and the ability to steal words from opponents, adding layers of strategy and excitement.

The gameplay is straightforward: players draw cards and take turns forming words. Bonus spaces on the board offer additional points, encouraging strategic placement. The use of wildcards allows for creative word-building, while the option to steal words from opponents adds a competitive edge. The goal is to be the first to form five words, with the player having the highest total score at the end emerging as the winner.

Trixx combines classic word-building elements with modern mechanics, making it suitable for family game nights, classrooms, or casual play among friends. It’s designed to be accessible for players aged 6 and up, making it ideal for younger audiences, as well as individuals with language difficulties or learning delays. The game emphasizes both fun and educational value, promoting language skills in an engaging way.

Ultimately, Trixx is a dynamic and strategic take on traditional word games, offering an inclusive experience that’s perfect for players looking to challenge their minds while having fun.

—description from the designer

Kooksville The Game is a surfing themed board game for kids 9+ to adults. Kook meaning someone who lacks the norms of surfing. 


To play the game: 
Players will choose their surfboard game piece then roll dice to navigate around the board. Some spaces are obstacles where the player must complete before continuing, others are challenges where they can challenge another player to roll a number of waves to catch. Once completed or if there are no obstacles, the player will pickup a card.

Players will gain or lose points depending on the card they draw. The first 3 players to finish gain additional points.

Finished? Don't get too comfortable! Even if a player is at the finish, they can be brought back in the game by a board swap or wave challenge!




Challenges include: 

     Wave of the day: player rolls to go left or right on the board.
     Stingray: player rolls an even number to move forward or they lose points and stay on the stingray.
     Board swap: player can swap boards and places with anyone on the board.
     Wave Challenge: the player chooses another player to roll a number between 2-12, points are gained or lost and spaces depending on the outcome.
     Wipe Out: the player who wipes out loses a turn.





What's included in the Game:

     Kooksville Game Board
     6 Surfboard Game Pieces
     200 Kooksville Game Cards
     Game instructions
     6 Game Dice

Baseleos

The Big Squeeze is your new go-to gateway game: specialize your lemonade stand and curate your menu across two eras in this fresh tableau builder.

Gameplay starts simple: move your meeple to a different location in town and do its action (gain ingredients, gain recipes, spend ingredients to make lemonade, and sell lemonade). But your decisions start stacking up fast: how much will you invest your cash into upgrading your lemonade stand, and how much will you stash away to prepare for the apocalypse?

Post-apocalypse gameplay not only introduces new wild recipes, but new character upgrades that unlock your full potential. Rebuild your stand, and finish bounties across the wasteland to collect fame. Every player has the ingredients for absurd synergies, so it's a race to unleash your combo before anyone else!

—description from the designer

IT’S CRUNCH TIME – Are you ready to go on a spilling spree? Stir, swap, and sabotage your way to success with Cereal Spiller from Ridley’s Games. Cutthroat scheming is required to fill your bowl and win this game. Prepare to lose some friends.

CEREAL SPILLERS HAVE A TYPE – Cereal tokens score points when they are next to other tokens of the same number or color. So, sabotage your competition to fill your Bowl with matching Cereal. But beware: your opponents can get you back!

MIX THINGS UP – Stir and Swap to wreak havoc, seek out the Magic Charm, avoid Floaters, and beware Dead Flies that can ruin your day. Made for those who love eating cereal from the box but never share.

—description from the publisher
