Introduction:Spelde (from Middle English: spelde, meaning "to split") is a drawless game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 7 cells per side, but boards of 5 to 9 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a maximal set of stones of the same color connected to each other. A single stone is also a group.
     To flip is to replace enemy stones with friendly stones.
     To split is to separate a group of enemy stones into two or more independent groups by turning over one or more subsets of stones from the original group.


				
				
					Turns:On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty cell. If it touches enemy groups, you must split all of them; otherwise, the placement is illegal. To split, flip consecutive stones of each group, starting next to your placed stone and ending at the one that separates it into two or more parts.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when a player cannot make any placement; in this case, the opponent is the winner.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a black stone in an empty cell, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer

An educational game wherein young players collect gold coins which are then used to purchase the necessary components to build a mosque. Caution must surround the magpie that will steal coins, setting back progress.

The Board Game Pilgrims of Life takes the players on the iconic Hajj pilgrimage. Each station of the game corresponds to the steps and rituals of the Hajj.

In Pilgrims of Life you take on the role of a group of pilgrims traveling to Mecca and you accomplish the rituals of the holy pilgrimage together.

They learn about the pilgrimage and reflect on their journey through life. What will they discover about each other?

—description from the designer

Mars Escape Party Rumble is a take that, party style card game for 3-8 players.
You've crash landed on Mars and need to gather parts to escape. You must out damage your friends in a huge betting war using weapons found around the crash in order to beat the aliens and take their ship parts. This is easier said than done, as everyone will be collecting sci-fi inspired items and weapons from the crash to give them the advantage, or to drag you down before you can escape. First astronaut to get the required number of ship parts wins the game!

—description from the publisher

Operation Gain is an expansion for SAS: Rogue Regiment, it includes a brand new five part campaign based on an incredible true story, plus 3 standalone bonus missions.
The main campaign sees Operators dropped 150 miles behind enemy lines, tasked with knocking out railroads, trains & ammunition dumps in a desperate attempt to stall the counter-attack of Hitler's fearsome Panzer divisions.
The opening missions are pure stealth, infiltrating the town to meet up with local resistance agents and carrying out vital reconnaissance of their objectives, all in preparation for the main event... taking down a war train packed with troops and tanks bound for the Normandy beachheads!
Leaving a trail of destruction in their wake, a heart pounding escape across the French countryside finally culminates in one of history's most daring & audacious extractions... from the enemy's own airfield!

—description from the publisher

A set of 4 promotional cards (Hippopotamus) for the Polish edition of the game Ekosystem 3: Sawanna.

At the end of the game, each hippopotamus = 2 points for each watering hole in the player's savannah.

—description from the publisher

As an eager entrepreneur, you've high hopes for your new venture - a general store, where you hope to cater to the local townspeople as well as adventurers passing through. The kingdom is experiencing a rare period of peace and prosperity and you intend to capitalise on it!

In Village Emporium, you are aiming to develop your establishment over 24 days so that it can compete with more established local stores. Your success will be measured in Commerce Points (CP), which can be earnt by accruing coin, upgrading your store and serving a wide range of customers.

—description from the designer

The Addams Family heads out to the most haunted cemetery in the United States, St. Louis Cemetery, for some drag racing fun. The cemetery is well known to be haunted by Voodoo Queen Marie Laveau and screams can be heard from her tomb, among other tombs which bring out the spirits of the dead. The family will be racing their family car and hearse car and are ready to dual it out. A simple dice mechanic for two players which includes a fun theme for some racing excitement, and a Addam’s Family fun solo competition.

—description from the publisher

Tuknanavuhpi is a game that has been played for several hundred years by the Hopi of Arizona and neighboring tribes in the U.S. and Mexico. It appears to have roots in Alquerques which was brought to the area by Spanish invaders 500 years ago. It was first recorded by Stewart Culin in 1907.

It’s a jump-and-capture game in the same family as checkers and draughts, but it has its own unique personality. As pieces are moved and captured, if an outer row or column becomes emptied at any time during the game, that row or column becomes off-limits for the rest of the game. The board shrinks as the game goes on!

The game ends either in the elimination of a player's pieces or when no more legal moves can be made. In the latter case, the player with the most pieces still on the board wins. (Or the game ends in a tie.)

Murderous Time Out is the ultimate crime game for all true crime fans! Based on a true case and developed in collaboration with the well-known podcast “Mord auf Ex”, you will be immersed in the dark story of a murder at the Retreat Center Bergesruh. What was planned as a relaxing vacation ends in a gripping criminal case that you can only solve with logical thinking and a keen eye. With high-quality evidence and interactive, digital clues, this game offers a realistic and immersive experience for 1-6 players. Perfect for an exciting evening with friends, escape room fans or true crime enthusiasts. The aim is to discover the truth - but can you decipher all the clues and unmask the culprit?

The Cold War is winding down. You've played your part so far, but now the loose ends are being tied up. Will you get out of the cloak-and-dagger trade alive?

Enter the world of international espionage with Last Shot, a fast-playing game of intrigue and strategy that's never the same game twice. Play your hand wisely to protect yourself. Draw — or steal — a "terminated" card, then choose the right time to eliminate your rivals. The last player standing wins.

—description from the publisher

A Slovak card game for 3-7 players, which is played with a special set of 117 cards, with images inspired by folk ambience and traditional clothing.

Zubatá is played with a deck of 117 cards - 100 cards in four colors, numbered 1-9, with 25 different symbols and two different genders; 16 'aces,' and 1 'Zubatá.' Players are dealt an initial hand of cards, number twelve less the total number of players, and take turns playing one or more cards to either the center stack (the only option at first) or "houses" that players may open throughout the game.

Players must play cards whose number is within one of the card atop the stack; playing a card whose number does not match requires the player to draw a card, while matching the number no draw is required, and matching both number and symbol allows the player to open a house.

Aces allow a player to reverse play order, skip a play, force another player to draw 2, or swap hands with a targeted player, and can be played at any time; however, if they do not match the color of the stack on which they are played, the player must draw a replacement.

The object of the game is to play all of one's cards; alternatively, play ends immediately when any player plays the "Zubatá" card, and the player with the fewest cards remaining in their hand wins. In both cases, the sum total of all other player's hands is their score.

Visit the magic school on the edge of the Shadow Forest! ✨

Over eighteen chapters in Schattenwald, you'll be sent on adventures that get trickier and trickier — teamwork is required here! Find rare ingredients, master magical spells, and stay away from the forest spirits...

Right on Time is all about timing here because the aim is to come in second! In each round, you have to decide which card goes on the discard pile. Play a higher card individually or two lower cards at the same time — timing is the key!

—description from the publisher

Here comes the ultimate reaction test! In Get That Cat, you have to react at lightning speed, aim sharply and...don't hit a cactus. Everyone turns over a card at the same time, then tries to throw the correct disc into the box — only those who hit it correctly score.

—description from the publisher

One legendary stretch of evenings in 1970, Miles Davis and his band of the moment played a rollicking series of sets at The Cellar Door in Washington, D.C. For almost 40 years, the only way to hear these blistering cool performances was via the album, Live Evil, where producer Teo Macero expertly blended them with studio recordings. This album was also the only recording where longtime band member, John McLaughlin, could be heard playing with Miles Davis live.

In this game, players will attend the early evening jam session to collect jazz personnel to form bands before the show begins. The player who creates the best bands will win.

—description from the designer

In the first light of the new year, the tigers gather in the ancient forest to test their strength! Immerse yourself in an epic tournament where timing, memory, and tactical skill determine the path to victory.

In Tai Chi Tiger, all players gather a team of masterful tigers who try to outdo their opponents with graceful movements and powerful combos. If you don't keep the rhythm of the chi, you could lose your tiger — but your remaining tigers will give their team new strength for the next exchange of blows.

—description from the publisher

Get your gear, Hawker; there's a job waiting for you. Safety is not guaranteed.

FRIK is a sci-fi "failpunk" skirmish game for 2-4 players. 
Lead a Team of bumbling idiots on dangerous, classified missions that may or may not be of vital importance. Hunt down traitors. Test experimental devices. Anticipate the worst.


     Critical failure (or FRIK) charts for just about everything!
     Disturb your enemies with Zappers, Rocket Boots, Thwackers, etc.
     10 Missions & randomized Interactable Terrain features.
     Everyone is expendable.


Enjoy a silly "beer & pretzels" game for people who love disaster and low probabilities of success.

—description from the publisher

Spice up your gameplay with this set of game-changing promo cards for Thief's Market!

Available through our webstore, Kickstarter, and at conventions.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Bite the Bullet: You are a legend – The Posse Campaign
description:
This volume 3 expands the "Bite the bullet: you are a legend" series. It gives you a complete map-based campaign, advanced rules, 6 new game maps, 10 new scenarios and 12 new characters.

You need volume 1 and even better, volume 2 to play this expansion.

—description from the designer

Want To Be A SWAT Team Leader?


You can do that. 
But first let’s explain more about the job.
A SWAT team (special weapons and tactics, originally special weapons assault team) is a police tactical unit that uses specialized or military equipment and tactics.
They are equipped with military-type hardware and trained to deploy against threats of terrorism, hostage taking, and in situations beyond the capabilities of ordinary law enforcement, sometimes deemed "high-risk". More on that later.

In Red Ops 5 you are the Leader of one such Team. Your assignments will vary but you ultimately have two things to accomplish. Succeed at your assignment and bring your team members back alive.

Inside you’ll find:

     A set of rules playable for any current type of Urban Warfare. Any.
     A gaming system where no two games will ever play out the same.
     Four different Factions with different agendas that you can play as or play against.
     Stats for over 50 unique Characters that you can play as or play against.
     A game playable with the Counters and Battle Boards we’ve given you or on the tabletop with any figures you may already have.
     A system to track your Career where your success or failure will dictate how well you live when you retire. Assuming you make it there.
     All this and more…


Are you sure you want to do it? Good. As that highly successful football coach says… “Do your job!”

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Fighter Alley: The Ghost of Kyiv – Special edition
description:
A tactical game in which two sides (yellow and blue) oppose each other with fighter aircraft. The goal of the game is to eliminate the opponents' planes. The game is played by either two or four players, with the possibility of three players in which one side is played by two players and one side played by one. The fuel gauge is used for every turn, limiting the amount of turns a plane can stay in the air. Two dice are rolled to determine the flying points. Air cards are placed on the board with different levels: gaining a flight level takes two points of the die throw, remaining at the same level or descending costs one. Bases can also be attacked and put out of order, meaning the planes can't land there. If a plane runs out of fuel without landing, it's taken out of the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Pokémon TCG: Scarlet & Violet – Surging Sparks
description:
The Pokémon TCG: Scarlet & Violet—Surging Sparks expansion evokes laid-back, tropical vibes with new Tera Pokémon ex originally discovered in Alola. Perusing these cards is your very own staycation, complete with calming ocean waves and a balmy breeze.

—description from the publisher

In the card game Blütenreich, your flower meadows grow step by step: draw cards and let beautiful butterflies spread their wings. Each card has its own strategy, so decide wisely and plan ahead: butterfly or flower meadow?

boardgame: Interstellar: The Dice Tower Goorg Promo Pack
description:
Available from Dice Tower 2024 Kickstarter Biome Pack.

Includes 6 new cyborgs: the Dice Tower team uplifted into starfarers.

Special rules span from their frightful inception in a goo fight, to their precious tendency to embark on foolhardy experimentation.

In Jure Grando: The First Vampire, you step into the shoes of the desperate townsfolk of Kringa.

Move around with your chosen townsfolk and explore the streets of Kringa, find items and protect the other townsfolk from Jure. But be careful; each encounter with Jure or failed objective will increase the terror in your town.

Complete your objectives and banish Jure once and for all before it is too late and your once peaceful town becomes a place of the dead.

—description from the publisher

A feast of fists! Everyone showed up for the brawl. Can you push your luck and deliver a beat down to everyone else? The last player standing will be the hero of Brawlopolis!

Hit Me! is a press-your-luck party game with a Super Hero theme.  Players take on the role of 1 one 12 different heroes provided, which will give you a unique ability during the game.

On your turn,  you draw a card from the hit deck and apply the effects.  If it is a hit card,  aim it at the player you want to hit.  If the card is a trip,  be careful not to trip up! 3 trips, and you hurt yourself instead.

There was an original game of Hit Me, designed by the same designer,  this is a revised and more complete version,  now with special cards, characters,  and much more!

—description from the publisher

Microverse is a fast-playing card-driven space 4x game. The game features lightning fast turns (sub 5 seconds most of the time) and a short playtime (15 minutes per player) while packing in all the hallmarks of a full 4x game. Players will guide their civilizations from humble beginnings on a single planet to galaxy spanning empires fighting over choice planets.

Each player has a hand of cards which are tied to one of the game’s four actions: Build, Colonize, Explore, and Mobilize. On a player’s turn, they play one or more of these cards of the same action type to generate resource points that they will later use to perform those actions. These resource points are then increased by the number of worlds they have colonized that contain the matching action icon. Finally, the player performs the action and draws back up to their hand size.

The core game of Microverse boasts 21 playable factions (with many more coming) that radically vary the gameplay. Each faction has their own unique strength, weakness, and research ability. This research ability is tied to a special icon found on worlds as the players explore. Players can choose to colonize these worlds to make their faction even more unique and powerful.

Players compete to be the first to complete one of four victory conditions:

     Expanse Victory: the player must occupy 10 or more sectors as the start of their turn. 
     Empire Victory: the player must have all 6 of their colonies on planets at the start of their turn. 
     Conquest Victory: an immediate win when the player destroys another player’s starting colony. 
     Transcendence. As players explore they will uncover monoliths - evidence of the lost precursor civilization. After a number of these worlds (equal to twice the number of players) are found, the game clock starts and players have only 3 turns left. If no player wins by another victory condition before the clock elapses, the player who controls the most monoliths transcends.


Microverse also contains quick and streamlined combat and political rounds. Every hallmark of 4x gaming is present here, but in a fraction of the time.

—description from the publisher

An abstract strategy game similar in ways to the classic game of Checkers but with one important difference: you can capture your own pieces.

Described by some as "a knife fight in a phone-booth" due to the size of the board, this game rewards carefully setting up the board in such a way that you trap your opponent into making undesirable moves.

Are you willing to sacrifice yourself to get ahead?

—description from the designer

This expansion for Galaxy Postman contains 6 exciting modules that can be integrated into the Core game seperate or in combination to provide even more variety.

Modules:
- Planets
- Scrapyard
- Boost
- Specialist
- First Descent
- Events

—description from the designer
family: Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

boardgame: War in Christmas Village Tabletop Game Rules
description: War in Christmas Village is a print & play tabletop, objective based, miniatures, skirmish game which uses your own Christmas Village as the tabletop terrrain. Defend Christmas Town from zombie hordes, subdue an angry Christmas Treent, and help Santa get his stolen sleigh back from a group of workshop elves gone rogue!

Quiltable: Christmas Quilting is a cozy printable quilting game from the designers of Quiltable: Paper Quilts and Quiltable: The Card Game. Amid the holiday rush of finishing your quilt in time for Christmas, race for the perfect blocks, and make sure to give good gifts to other players in true Christmas spirit!

Throughout the game, gain Christmas Cheer for creating great quilt designs, and giving gifts. Grab blocks while they're available, create great combinations, and use your Christmas stocking to mitigate die rolls.

At the end of the game, the player with the most Christmas Cheer wins!

The game can be played with 1–4 players, and depending on your group can match friendly or competitive styles. The solo game comes with multiple levels of difficulty as you face off against master quilters with increasing levels of skill.

How will you prepare for Christmas as you create the most Quiltable design?

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Watson Elementary School and the start of recess. Select a faction of students looking to make their play for the top of the social ladder. Go forth and stake your claim over the most popular playscape equipment and then leverage that popularity to sway more kids to your cause. Use your faction's unique skills (and the occasional barrage of dodgeballs) to send your rivals packing. Keep a close eye on the Teacher making their rounds. But above all don't forget that recess isn't forever, and before the bell rings only one faction can rule the playground.

boardgame: ヴァルトビルダー (Wald Builder)
description:
Wald Builder is a middle-heavyweight game of 90-120 minutes of resource management and puzzles to improve your status.
In the Flip & Write game genre, each player has their own sheet of paper to write on to develop their city on their own map.
In multiplayer play, you need to keep an eye on other players.In  solo play, you can immerse yourself in your own world.

—description from the publisher

Players take turns doing these 4 Actions on their turn:

     Move one of your Bees the First Time, like a queen in chess (a straight-line in any of the 6 directions) as far as they want.
         Bees can only move through open hexes and stop on open Hexes.
         So, Bees cannot go through hexes that contain other Bees nor Hexes with Gems
    
     Draw a random Gem from the Bag 
     Place the new Gem on the table next to your other Gem.
         Gems must always be visible on the table.
         Place one of your 2 Gems on an open Hex next to where the Bee is located.
         The open hex must be empty (i.e., it cannot contain another bee, nor a Gem).
    
     Move the same Bee a Second Time based upon the Gem Effect.
         Each color of Gem causes the Bee to move differently for the second movement.
         If you cannot move your bee a Second Time, the bee is removed from the game.
         If you cannot move any of your bees the First Time, you lose.
    


It is then the next player’s turn

—description from the designer

Introduction:Sgolte (from Scottish Gaelic: sgoilt, meaning "to split") is a strategy game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexagons (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 6 cells per side, though boards of 5 or 7 are also valid. Each player has a sufficient supply of stones of their color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is the maximal set of connected stones of the same color. A single stone is also a group.
     To merge is to join two or more friendly groups into one group.
     To split is to separate an enemy group into two or more independent groups through captures.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty cell. If your placement merges two or more friendly groups, you must split exactly one enemy group: to do so, remove a consecutive chain of enemy stones in a straight line, provided the last stone removed is the one that causes the split.

Each removed stone becomes a stone of your color and must be immediately placed, one by one, adjacent to at most one friendly group. During your turn, you cannot merge friendly groups unless you split an enemy group.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when a player cannot to place a stone or fails to place all removed stones in their turn; in both cases, their opponent wins.

Before starting, the first player places a black stone in an empty cell, and then the second player chooses their color. This method of balance is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer

Subversia is a quick card game of simultaneous espionage. Everyone plays action cards at the same time to plot attacks, steal intel, and topple their neighbor's regimes. Subversia combines the simplicity and hand management of classic games like Uno, with the deception of hidden reveal games like Coup, and the chaos of party games like Exploding Kittens.

Players manage a hand of 5 Actions, secretly selecting 2 they wish to play during a Round. Simultaneously, everyone will flip 1 of their 2 Actions - those effects taking place instantly. Attack other players, steal their points, flip the direction of play, and more.

Be the first to collect 5 Intel points to claim victory or eliminate all the other factions to rule supreme!

Only one can claim dominion. Will it be you?!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Good Riddance: Booted From the Bash!
description:
A Judged Round Submission Game (a/la A2A or CAH) with a twist! We set out to create a version of this style game that saw two entries every time, without the awkward "which order should I read these in?" conversation. Each round begins with a Judge who reads which type of party we will be attending. Each Player submits one Guest Card and one Hijinks Card. The Judge will then read each aloud who did what and determine which party guest is too much to remain in attendance. This player gets a point for the round and play continues. I know we've all seen this type of game before but I feel the combination of two variable sets adds a chaos element that can lead to limitless combinations. At launch, we have the core version and three small expansions.

—description from the publisher

In addition to the character cards from the base game, you now have the option to choose from 3 additional character cards. With these new characters you will receive 1 of each resource, 1 additional resource of your choice, and 3 starting equipment cards at the start of the game.

Characters includes: Cartographer, Messenger, and Adventurer.

Cartographer:
When another player’s yak ends its movement on the same space as the Cartographer’s yak, that player may not swap maps with the Cartographer. Matching pairs of maps are immediately considered secured for the Cartographer and will be worth 8 points in the final scoring.

Messenger:
If the Messenger’s target space is a city or temple space, they receive 1 compass or 1 yak fodder from the supply.

Adventurer:
Once per turn, the Adventurer can spend any 1 resource to allow their yak to make one right-angled turn during its movement.

In addition to the character cards from the base game, you now have the option to choose from 3 additional character cards. With these new characters you will receive 1 of each resource, 1 additional resource of your choice, and 3 starting equipment cards at the start of the game.

Characters includes: Scout, Thief, and Photographer

Scout:
Once per day, the Scout can add the value of the black die to another die, increasing the number of steps their yak can move.
They must stack both dice on one dice space of their player board. They still only receive the resource from the non-black die.

Thief:
When the Thief ends their movement on a space with another player's yak, they may either steal 1 equipment card from that player (without swapping a map) or steal 1 resource and swap 1 map with them.

Photographer:
Once during the game, the Photographer can place 1 resource from their supply on their character card. From that point on, that type of resource counts as a wild resource for them.

During setup, place 1 market tile each on the space marked by a little tower in the right-hand corner on the 2nd and 4th board of the landscape, counted from the start (In the long game: place them on the 2nd, 4th, and 7th board; no temple tile will be placed on the 4th board in this case).
Shuffle the offer tiles and place one face up next to each terrain board that has a market tile.

Changes to gameplay:
If your target space is a market tile, you receive the bonus shown on the offer tile next to the board that market tile is one. You can only visit each market once per game

Includes: 3 Market tiles and 6 Offer tiles.

Changes in Setup:
Shuffle the new animals with the ones from the base game.

Changes to Gameplay:
You can the ability of an animal once per round. After using it, you must turn it over.
At the end of the round, the animal is turned back to its front.

Baby Pirate: On your turn during Phase I: Nassau City, move the pirate captain once to an adjacent space along an unused alley without using your own pirates, and take the corresponding equipment.

Piranha: When fighting an opponent, you can add +1 to your attack, i.e. you place 1 additional ammo crate from your cargo hold on the opponent.

Kite: When sailing on the Caribbean board, your sailing range is increased by 2 for one turn.

Changes in Setup:
Shuffle the new animals with the ones from the base game.

Changes to Gameplay:
You can the ability of an animal once per round. After using it, you must turn it over.
At the end of the round, the animal is turned back to its front.

Chameleon: In Phase I: Nassau City, after moving the pirate captain, take the bonus from the captain's current square and from another square that is connected to the current one via an alley.

Shark: In Phase II: Caribbean, you do not have to stop at a kraken. If you stop to fight the kraken, it doesn’t fight back, meaning it won’t draw conflict tokens that you need to fend off.

Starfish: In Phase II: Caribbean, after sailing, you may perform 2 different actions in your area.

Changes in Setup:
Shuffle the new animals with the ones from the base game.

Changes to Gameplay:
You can the ability of an animal once per round. After using it, you must turn it over.
At the end of the round, the animal is turned back to its front.

Skeleton: You may take over an outpost from another player and receive the displayed reward, if your ship is in the same area of the Caribbean board. The other player is compensated with 1 doubloon from the general supply and returns their pirate back to their own supply on their ship board standing upright, thus may use them as a sailor during the current round.

Hobgoblin: To occupy a control tower already controlled by another player, you do not need to surpass the number of their ammo crates - you only have to place the same number of crates to take over control (for this you still need to have the necessary sets of sailors and depicted weapons). Take the ammo crates from your supply, not from your cargo hold.

Sea shell: You may sail diagonally once, leaving an area of the Caribbean board through a corner. The kraken won’t stop you, as it doesn’t expect you from there. However, you still need to spend 1 provision and stay within your total sailing range.

A special edition of the popular trivia game, featuring slightly naughty questions aimed at adults and a purple and pink illuminated Smartbox. The Red-light edition includes 50 cards with a total of 100 adult-oriented questions and 1000 blushing answers. The Smartbox for the game has lights, allowing you to play even if not all content can withstand daylight. The Smart10 game is particularly engaging and interesting. The game has fair rules, everyone gets to answer regularly, and it also encourages strategy – plus, each correct answer is rewarded. The game is suitable for 2-8 players or even larger groups playing in bigger teams. The game lasts about 20 minutes. Recommended age is 18+. The package includes batteries. All Smart10 games and question packs' cards fit all Smartboxes! You can also create your own favorite version by combining parts from different question packs. All the best elements of trivia games in one handy game that quickly established itself as a favorite for gatherings!

Smart10 is a trivia game where you don’t have to wait long for your turn. Each question has 10 answer options, and everyone gets to answer each question. Each correct answer earns an answer token, and at the end of the round, points are awarded based on those tokens. If unsure, you can pass on the question and keep the answer tokens you’ve already collected. However, you can lose the game tokens and points for that round if you answer incorrectly – but the points from previous rounds remain. The first player to collect 30 points wins the game.

Who plays it risky? Who deposits answer tokens in time? Who is greedy? Is passing and patience the key? The conveniently portable Smartbox holds 200 questions with answers, score counters, and answer tokens; nothing else is needed. The game is a handy size: easy to store and take anywhere!

Art-themed additional questions for the award-winning and popular Smart10 game. The topics of the questions come from visual arts, architecture, and design, and concern famous individuals and works as well as terms, genres, and techniques in visual arts. For those interested and enthusiastic about art. The deck contains 50 new double-sided question cards: a total of 100 new questions and 1000 new answer options. Exchange the questions in the Smartbox for two special packs or mix the themed questions with some general questions. To play, you need a Smartbox from any SMART10 game in addition to the question cards. All cards from Smart10 games and question packs are compatible with all Smartboxes! You can also create your own favorite version by combining parts from different question packs. The game is suitable for ages 14 and up, for 2-8 players, or even for larger groups playing in bigger teams. The game lasts about 20 minutes. All the best elements of trivia games in one handy game that quickly established itself as a favorite at gatherings!

Smart10 is a trivia game where you don't have to wait long for your turn. Each question has 10 answer options, and everyone gets to answer every question. You earn an answer token for each correct answer, and at the end of the round, points are awarded for those tokens. If you're unsure, you can pass on the question and keep the answer tokens you've already collected. However, you can lose the game tokens and points for each round if you answer incorrectly – but the total point pot doesn't go empty, as points from previous rounds are retained. The first player to collect 30 points wins the game.

STRATE9Y GAME is a unique board game designed for team-based validation of business ideas, guiding players through a structured process to explore and refine their concepts in depth. The goal is to navigate through the 9 essential steps of the business process, allowing players to analyze, discuss, and receive valuable feedback on their ideas from other players.

Game Mechanics:
Players collaborate and utilize coaching questions focused on four key areas: vision, strategy, marketing, and perspective. Each area encourages players to look at their ideas from various angles, prompting in-depth consideration of feasibility and potential. Team feedback is central to the gameplay, as players offer each other constructive insights and suggestions to continually refine and improve their ideas.

The game culminates in a focus on “elevator pitch” skills, where players learn to present their ideas concisely and compellingly, making an immediate impact. This final component is invaluable for anyone who wants to quickly and effectively convey the value of their project.

Why Play STRATE9Y GAME?

Validates Business Ideas: Players learn to analyze, think critically, and work on improving their ideas in a collaborative setting.
Promotes Collaboration and Feedback: Players share their ideas and receive support and fresh perspectives from their team.
Develops Elevator Pitch Skills: Players refine their ability to present their ideas concisely and persuasively, capturing attention right from the start.
Special Initiative:
STRATE9Y GAME is designed to be both engaging and educational. For every two games sold, we donate one to children in foster homes, giving them the opportunity to develop ideas and skills that could shape their futures.

STRATE9Y GAME is perfect for anyone looking to take their dreams to the next level and gain a hands-on introduction to the world of entrepreneurship!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – King Hsss with Tyrantisaurus
description:
Behold the united power of two reptiles! Nobody possesses the strength to stop the formidable alliance between a serpent and a dinosaur. Soon, the entire world shall remble beneath the resounding roar of Tyrantisaurus and the authoritative command of King Hsss. United, they shall exert their dominance over Preternia, bending it to their will!

—description from the publisher

We all know the fairy tale – Red goes into the forest to bring her ailing grandmother some treats. She’s warned by her family not to stray from the path. But she sees some beautiful flowers to pick for her grandmother and cannot help herself. The Big Bad Wolf finds her there, and plans ahead. He asks her where she’s going and runs ahead to gobble up Grandmother, and then Red herself, until they’re saved by the Woodsman. In our version, it’s Red who does the deed, but what if she never makes it? What if she strays off the path too far and never makes her way to Grandmother? She may be old, she may be frail, and she may be ailing, but she is a hunter of old, and she’s forgotten more about tracking and slaying than you’ll ever know. Like a general, she’ll rally the troops to do her bidding, so that she can rescue as many people as she can along the way. If she happens to get a little pushy about sending them into battle, well, her grandbaby was taken from her too soon, and that does something to a person. Something almost as dark as the killer.

Like our other stretch goals, the rules of this are subject to change as we keep developing it. What I started with was the premise that Grandma is ailing, so we removed sprint cards, and healing cards like Short and Long Rest. Instead she will have additional slow movement cards, and cards that will Rally the community to fight for her. When they panic, they run for Grandma, to lead them and offer advice.

Let’s not forget her carpet bag that can carry anything from a wad of tissue to hard candies that have probably been sitting at the bottom for a decade. She’s no fool, being a slow mover. She knows a slipper is a formidable weapon when in the right hands, but that’s letting the killer come a little too close. She needs to set traps that can either do damage or stop the killer and immediately end their turn when they enter the space. Those items (Knitting Needles, Knick-Knacks, and the Good Plates) are precious to her, and the killer will pay for making her lose those gifts from her family. But sometimes you have to make sacrifices, which Grandma knows all too well.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Final Girl: Lore & Scenario Book – Series 3
description:
This 64-page staple-bound booklet includes:


     A backstory for each killer in Series 3
     A backstory for each Final Girl in Series 3
     Tips on playing each Final Girl in Series 3
     A scenario for each killer/location combination in Series 3, with special rules and setup instructions for each scenario


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Summoner Wars (Second Edition): Isle Vekkid Faction Deck
description:
Rokomoko leads the Isle Vekkid from the frontline of the battlefield. As his allies join him in battle, Rokomoko makes their skills his own, turning him into a terrifying threat to his enemies. Dive into battle and confront your foes head on with the might of the Isle Vekkid!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Summoner Wars (Second Edition): Spectral Conclave Faction Deck
description:
The Spectral Conclave are a secret society that have long honed their skills in illusory magic. From out of thin air, their summoner Feyrin calls the ghostly forms of strange beasts into being. These illusions strike at their enemies and then dissipate back into the ether. Confound your enemies as you assault them with your nearly limitless supply of spectral units!

—description from the publisher

The citizens of Babylon are toiling tirelessly to reach the heavens and make a name for themselves. However, God’s purpose for His people is to make His name great. In this dexterity game, players must disrupt the communication of the citizens before they complete their ambitious tower.

Players take turns playing cards to disrupt the language of six different tribes. Each time a card is played, it must be added to the tower, and the tribe workers must be moved up the structure. With a limited supply of walls, the race to confuse the languages is intense. If the tower is completed, a tribe reaches the top, or the tower collapses, you and your team lose. However, if your team succeeds in confusing the languages of the tribes before any of these happen, victory is yours.

—description from the publisher

This expansion for Galaxy Postman contains 8 exciting modules that can be integrated into the Core game seperate or in combination to provide even more variety.

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

description:
The Siege of the Molten Mountain'' is a new turn-based board strategy game. 
It can be played with 1–4 players and can be both story-driven and sandboxed. 
The main objective of this game is to reach the peak of the molten mountain and defeat the Moltouons, a savage demonic race that uses the mountain to shoot lava and death upon the lands. The road is perilous, and throughout the game you will be ambushed, attacked, deceived, and cursed. The players not only want to liberate the lands, but it is also said that the Molten Mountain is the gateway to godhood. Difficult decisions and dilemmas will forge your course. 
Who will be brave enough to conquer the mountain?

If you are a fan of strategy turn-based games, fan of fantasy, medieval and magic as well as real time wargame battles, we got you covered! We tried our best to implement a new kind of wargame-boardgame-strategy game where we try to combine both long campaign movements on the map of the whole world and real time battles.

We tried not to copy stuff from other wargames and board games, so some aspects might seem a little bit off and strange.

In this game, you get to rule a faction, raise its armies and fight your way to the top of the mountain where the Moltouons threat to eradicate life on the world! But some of you might also be cunning, because at the top of the Mountain, there is the gateway to Elloria, the lands of the Gods! You can deceive your way through and get the immense power of the Gods, or just fight the Moltouons back from where they came and shut the door forever!

Choose your faction, create your army and turn by turn go to the top of the Molten Mountain to seal the portal of the Moltouons, winning the game! 
In this game you can both control where your army is going and what is going to be the outcome of the battle!

description from the publisher

DOOM PILGRIM Pocket is a mint tin box version of  Doom Pilgrim solo adventure. More compact and faster fun!

This solo card game is a roguelike adventure in the form of 90 cards (the size a bit smaller than a classic MtG card, so-called mint-tin card) with pictures on the whole front sides and texts on the whole back sides. You represent a doubtful hero struggling through his miserable fate in a grim medieval open world full of mortal dangers and cruelty.

On every turn, you take 3 cards from the top of the deck that represents your future. From the three cards, just based on the images, you choose one path to reject, one path to postpone, and one path to go. The path you have taken is then revealed - you turn the card, read the story, make some decisions according to the text and apply the eventual changes, losses, or gains to your character sheet or just a piece of paper. This way you build your fate of a medieval pilgrim-hero and you try to survive the whole journey to the bottom of the "your future" deck. There are fights, NPCs, equipment, and many more... just as in a gamebook (a very grim & dark one, full of odd meetings) but there is no dice rolling, everything depends on your decisions and on the path you had gone before. For the next play, you will possibly remember what image hides what kind of event or fight, so you may choose your path wiser, according to your current equipment, and fitness.

It contains the same cards as Doom Pilgrim, the grimdark medieval fantasy game. The card texts are shorter and use 1 point smaller font than the classic version, so the narrative aspect of the game is a bit reduced and also card effects are sometimes less complex. On the other hand, the gameplay is smoother and more actionable thanks to it. Some cards were on the contrary equipped with new effects so that we use all of the space the most effectively. Thanks to shorter text and more straightforward effects, this pocket version is better for most of the multiplayer options.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Hello Kitty: Day at the Park – Hangyodon Deluxe Promo
description: Hangyodon is a promotional character for Hello Kitty: Day at the Park. The small package includes the character figurine, its scoring token, and its character card.

Rush to uncover the flag hidden on this treacherous island as its shores shrink and sink into the ocean!

Send out your explores to unearth the island's mysteries, battle opponents and collect valuable loot that could improve your chances. But beware of hidden bombs and fierce storms... Will you be the last one holding the flag?

—description from the back of the box

As large stockyards are established in midwestern and western cites, meat packers respond by building slaughterhouses in these locations, expanding their enormous business empires. In Union Stockyards: Westward Expansion, you will expand westward, developing your strategy by using each city’s unique ability. Your workers can visit the Exchange Building to access new actions, and new Year Cards will add more rounds and more events to the game.
A Solo Mode is also included, where you compete against 1 to 3 BOTs (out-of-town meat packers). BOTs may also be added to multi-player games to alter player count.

—description from the publisher

Djibouti is an exciting card game with 150 cards, each featuring a city from around the world. The goal is to guess the warmest cities based on real-time temperatures from a weather app. The hotter the temperature of the city on your card, the more points you earn. With several game variants, Djibouti offers fun and variety for families and friends.

—description from the designer

Available from Dice Tower 2024 Kickstarter Biome Pack.

This new mini expansion contains a variety of alternate art cards and new character options not previously available in any other expansion. Included in this pack are


     4 new hero art cards: Elani, Hanev, Marorg, and Xoric
     4 new hero ability options: Elani, Hanev, Marorg, and Xoric
     1 new version of the Wyvern Monarch with new abilities
     1 new wyvern, the Royal Rockvern
     2 new event/encounter cards for Monster Pit


"It's Elzra's 10 year anniversary! This content pack adds new cards for the following games: Catacombs Third Edition, Catacombs & Castles Second Edition, Catacombs Conquest,Catacombs Conquest Event Horizon, Wyverns of Wylemuir Second Edition, and Monster Pit "
—description from the publisher

Krieg Am Gardasee, Operations on Lake Garda, 1866 is a set of fast play rules and a campaign system based on LFG’s Steamer Wars, covering the 1866 naval campaign fought out on Lake Garda by the navies of Austria and Italy.

The fast play nature of the rules and the campaign system that is also included allow the campaign to be fought out over a weekend or a few club nights.

The rules are supported by a small range of exclusive 3D models.

—description from the publisher

Apprentices have overcome the Iocus Arcanus, and now they have to prepare as fast as possible for the next challenge, the Draconis Arcanus. But before they'll have to learn the secrets of magic with greater depth and there's only one place in the whole kingdom where they can do that, the Lucerna Library, the place where the best and the most dangerous spells are.

This library will only show its secrets by candlelight, if the candles go out, they will not be able to read any of the books and they will have to leave. The Guardian of the Library will be of great help in your search for knowledge, but in exchange, the Spirit of Oblivion, a greedy being filled with envy will not allow them to take from his lair the knowledge or the wisdom that he was never able to achieve.

Goal of the game is to have the largest amount of books when the last candle in Lucerna goes out.

Lucerna is open! Learn the spells and continue your journey!

—description from the designer

Chrono Core Trading Card Game is a mech based trading card game where you’ll be building your deck with equipment, weapons, configurations (attachments), and support cards to aid you against your opponent in the arena

Starting from a unique deck building process where your equipment cards chassis type must match the same designation as your Pilot and Core cards. Once your deck is ready, you’ll place your Pilot, Core, starting equipment and weapon cards into play. You’ll then go back and forth with your opponent battling it out and destroying each other’s shields to reach one another's vulnerable pilots and reduce their life to 0 and claim victory.

—description from the publisher

Simple deduction game for children. Mysterious events happen in the Animal City, and the players must collect eyewitness clues to rule out the innocent from the list of suspects. The game combines classic roll-and-move mechanics to simple deduction.

With Christmas just a few days away, are you ready to welcome your family and friends? Buy your Christmas supplies and light your Christmas tree, wrap presents for your guests and bake cookies. "Ready for Christmas" is a fun print and play roll and write game for the whole family. Grab some coins and dice and sit by the fireplace to get your house ready for Christmas.

In Ready for Christmas, players compete to be the best host or hostess for Christmas. The game is for 1 to 99 players and ages 8+ and all you need to play it are 4 six-sided dice, a pencil, a game sheet and a coin per player. The game is played in 10 rounds and lasts about 15'. Each round has 2 phases, the Shopping phase where players use their coin to buy supplies and the Action phase where they use dice to perform actions to try to score points by lighting their Christmas lights on their tree, wrapping presents and baking cookies. The player with the most points at the end of the game is the winner and claims the role of the best host or hostess of Christmas.

So, do you think you are "Ready for Christmas"?

—description from the publisher

The snow has fallen and what better way to enjoy it than by building a snowman? Gather as much snow as you can and dress your snowman with cool winter accessories. "Best Snowman Wins" is a fun print and play roll and write game for the whole family. Grab some dice, sit by the window, watch the snow fall and build the best snowman.

In Best Snowman Wins, players compete to build the best snowman for Christmas. The game is for 1 to 99 players and ages 8+ and all you need to play it are 4 six-sided dice, a pencil, and a game sheet per player. The game is played in 10 rounds and lasts about 15'. In each round, players use the dice to perform actions to try to score points by making snowballs and adding them to their snowman and searching the old warehouse for accessories to dress him. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins the prize for the best snowman.

So, may the "Best Snowman Win"!

—description from the publisher

Mini-expansion for Castle Builder that contains 6 challenges, from whom you choose randomly 3 at the start of the game. To claim a challenge you will need for example at least 3 persons in your castle, but if an other player would have more persons then you, he will take over the challenge. Each challenge is worth one coin.

When playing with this expansion, you need one more coin to win the game.
Four additional clue cards.

Journey into the Tanglewoods is the second crowdfunding campaign for the 20 Strong game system, focusing on the new Tanglewoods trilogy. Inspired by 20 Strong, acclaimed designer and artist Manny Trembley collaborated with Chip Theory to create a world where fairy tale characters navigate an ever-changing forest, battling and bribing enemies to confront their ultimate nemesis. The game features flippable cards that reveal different gameplay elements depending on which side is faceup.

The campaign introduces a new core box, containing everything needed to play, including the base rules, 20 dice, a magnetic chip tracker, and the Tanglewoods Red deck, all packaged with red foil accents. Players will navigate the forest, avoid the Big Bad Wolf's pack, and face the woodland's treacherous denizens on their way to grandmother's house. After mastering the Tanglewoods Red deck, players can explore other 20 Strong decks for fresh adventures.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Little Red Riding Hood

boardgame: A Touch of Evil: Dark Gothic – Flooded Marshes Game Supplement
description:
Towering over the sweeping cornfields, the ominous windmill creaks and sways as its blades turn in the dark autumn sky. There are evils that lurk within the fields, and a darkness growing below the mill’s musty wooden floor.

This 15 card supplement for A Touch of Evil: Dark Gothic is themed around the sinking marshes that lay between the Abandoned Keep and the Olde Woods. With new cards for Gear, Allies, Events, and Minions, it also features a new Special Location card type for the Flooded Marshes. Sitting below the center line when revealed, the Flooded Marshes gives an extra risk and reward to players that acquire any of the cards above it…collected from within the tall reeds and grasses that cover the fetid swampy marshland south of Shadowbrook.
family: Game: A Touch of Evil: Dark Gothic

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Orient Express / Amusement Park
description:
Age of Steam Expansion: Orient Express / Amusement Park was available individually and as part of Alban Viard's Age of Steam Map Set 2024.

Amusement Park is a solo map, and the Orient Express map is an original design by Viard not connected with an earlier design in the same setting. On this map, yellow cities follow the route of the Orient Express and are considered connected. You can use the route to transport non-yellow goods long distances. Expensive upkeep costs are represented by additional income reduction each round.

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: France 2078 / Mongolia
description: Age of Steam Expansion: France / Mongolia was available individually and as part of Alban Viard's Age of Steam Map Set 2024.

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Belgium / South Africa
description:
Age of Steam Expansion: Belgium / South Africa was available individually and as part of Alban Viard's Age of Steam Map Set 2024.

The South Africa map is an original design by Viard not connected with an earlier expansion set in the same location. South Africa is one of the biggest exporters of gold. Find gold and you will find victory! Players start with extra money, but every bid made in the auction costs an additional fee. Delivering yellow cubes reduces your shares on the share track.

If you loved the Mad Max films, then you're bound to have a great time with this game! 
Prepare yourself for an amazing racing experience with Vrrroomit. 
This lively and engaging game welcomes players of every age to join in thrilling races using their favorite vehicles across various themed tracks, all while battling opponents along the way.

As you speed ahead, racers have the opportunity to collect items that are scattered throughout the circuits, which enhance their combat abilities. You can charge at your fellow competitors, launch missiles, or push them into fiery pits, removing them from the running altogether. Work together with your teammates to complete those first four laps and seize victory as the ultimate champion of Vrrroomit!

Designed for up to four players, this engaging game allows each participant access to three unique vehicles during competition. These vehicles must be employed wisely according to their distinct characteristics as well as the overall strategy a player selects after choosing a team color.

Vrrroomit is available in a downloadable format featuring PDF files. Each game comes with track tiles, original JPEG maps, vehicle displays, team charts, along with user manuals provided in English, German, French, and Spanish languages.

You’ll find it easy to print these materials either at home or through local printing services tailored for your convenience. Print out all components , build them up and prepare yourself for thrilling racing action! The assembling experience is just as entertaining as playing the actual game.

Enjoy your time playing Vrrroomit!

description from the publisher

boardgame: Scattered Readings: A One-Shot Mission for Zona Alfa-Kontraband
description:
SCATTERED READINGS

Veteran Zone Guide Ulik and his faithful K9 companion Laika take three Institute scientists on a quick field trip to an Anomaly-rich location in the Exclusion Zone.

Easy money for a babysitting job? Or something more dangerous? It's up to you to find out.

SCATTERED READINGS is a one-shot mission for Zona Alfa - Salvage and Survival in the Exclusion Zone. Designed for solo or cooperative play, it also uses rules and material from the supplement, Kontraband.

You will need both of those books to run this game as written.

SCATTERED READINGS - A one-shot mission for ZONA ALFA/KONTRABAND.

description from the publisher

boardgame: Location 39: A Narrative Campaign for Zona Alfa / Kontraband
description:
LOCATION 39 is a narrative campaign for Zona Alfa - Salvage and Survival in the Exclusion Zone. 
Designed for solo or cooperative play, it incorporates rules and material from the independently produced supplement, Kontraband.

You will need both books to run these missions as written.

In this campaign, players control a four-person team of veteran Zone explorers who find evidence of a remote facility not found on any current map of the Exclusion Zone. The siren call of a hefty payday inevitably draws the team deep into the heart of the unknown, to a place marked with the cryptic notation Location 39.

The Institute acknowledges your bravery in undertaking this dangerous venture into the heart of the Exclusion Zone. Good hunting, stalker.

Five Missions + Maps, POI Cards, Stats for Zone Hostiles and Optional Pre-Generated Characters

Use what you have. Play how you want.

description from the publisher

boardgame: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Battlesnap Sewer Slam
description:
A two-player game that uses a simple rock-paper-scissors mechanic and a TMNT theme (including miniatures of Leonardo, Michelangelo, Donatello, Raphael, Bebop, and Rocksteady).

Each player selects a character, puts the character into a plastic "card," and turns a dial to equip the character with either Pizza, Weapons, or Mutagen.  (Pizza beats Weapons. Weapons beats Mutagen.  Mutagen beats Pizza.)

Each player presses a button to activate the "Battlesnap" mechanism, which flips the card over and reveals what the character has equipped.

The winner of the round collects a "slice" (plastic wedge) of pizza.  The first player to collect four slices is the winner.
family: Comic Books: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

boardgame: Grave of the Sun: A Narrative Campaign for Nightwatch Blood and Bone
description:
Grave of the Sun
Is a narrative campaign supplement for the tabletop miniature war game, Nightwatch Blood and Bone.

You will need that core rule book to get the most out of this material.

In Grave of the Sun, five members of the Nightwatch travel to the fictional desert kingdom of Harab where they investigate a mysterious artifact, navigate political intrigue, face death cult assassins and supernatural enemies, and ultimately confront a legendary evil.

MINIATURES:
To run the tabletop miniature wargame portion of this campaign, players will need forty enemy models: twelve of each category of Dark Spawn minion, (Vermin, Horde, and Terror) one Alpha for each minion level and one Atrocity-level monster for the final boss.

Players will also need five Guild Hunters, plus one to three Hired Hands, Animal Companions, or Magic Familiars, depending on player choices.

GM USEFUL BUT NOT REQUIRED
This campaign can be run solo or cooperatively, but it was written with a Game Master in mind - someone who will guide the players through the adventure, set the stage for each scenario, create atmosphere, reveal the narrative challenges, and control the Dark Spawn models during the combat encounters.

The Grave of the Sun scenarios are slightly modified from the standard template to fit the campaign’s story line. In general, there are more low-level adversaries in each mission, with fewer but more dangerous, high-tier enemies appearing later.
Good Hunting.

description from the publisher

boardgame: The Loa of Lockwood Court: A 'Nightmares' Incident
description:
THE LOA OF LOCKWOOD COURT - A 'NIGHTMARES' INCIDENT

The Nightwatch is investigating reports of assaults and attempted muggings in the parking lots at Lockwood Court. The attackers don’t shout or make demands; they groan and stumble, seemingly impervious to reason or injury.

Police informants say there’s a new gang in the city; Haitians carving out territory with the usual combination of fast cash, brute violence, and raw fear. The word on the street is they have set their sights on Lockwood Court.

Gangs, drugs, attacks, disappearances... Are they connected? Maybe.

Time to see what's really going on.

THE LOA OF LOCKWOOD COURT is a one-shot incident for Osprey Wargames' When Nightmares Come - An Investigative Wargame of Supernatural Horror.

You will need that core rule book to get the most out of this material.

description from the publisher

boardgame: Hostile Actions: Warriors Clash Under Alien Suns
description:
Hostile Actions: Simple Rules for Fighting Sci-Fi Battles

Step into a universe of sci-fi warfare with Hostile Actions, a simple and fun miniatures combat game that brings dynamic and engaging battles to your tabletop! Designed for flexibility and ease of play, these rules allow you to use any sci-fi miniatures in your collection, whether you want to play small patrol-type battles with a few squads and a vehicle or two, or larger battles with around twenty or more units per side.

Why Choose Hostile Actions?


     Simple Mechanics: Based on a simple-to-learn D20 system, yet still offering tactical depth that keeps the action thrilling and unpredictable.
     Adaptable Gameplay: Play with any sci-fi miniatures, from any manufacturer. You can have a straightforward meeting engagement or play one of the included scenarios.
     Customizable Armies: Build your forces using the pre-made unit cards, or create your own force using the blank unit cards. All infantry units come with their own unique traits and abilities.
     Varied Force Support Options: Bring extra firepower to your battles with Force Support Cards, including artillery strikes, orbital bombardments, and more.
     Varied Special Unit Options: Make your units different by adding Special Unit Cards to help them on the battlefield. Maybe give them extra armour, additional ammo, or maybe even some combat stims, something to give them the edge in battle.
     All You Need to Start: The PDF includes a comprehensive yet straightforward set of rules, printable unit cards, tokens, templates, scenario options. Everything you need to dive into your next sci-fi battle. Just add your miniatures!


Whether you’re a seasoned wargamer or new to the hobby, Hostile Actions offers a perfect balance of simplicity and strategy, allowing you to focus on what matters most. Dominating the battlefield!

description from the publisher

The Executives is a competitive trick-taking game where players assume the roles of C-Suite executives vying to wrest control of the company from the legacy CEO.

The game unfolds over several turns. The first four turns involve influence bidding followed by trick-taking, while the final turn centers on a pivotal vote.

By the end, either a player or the foreign investor will claim the CEO title. But simply becoming the CEO isn’t enough to secure victory. True success requires cunning subterfuge, strategic alliances, and careful use of your influence to shape the outcome to match your own agenda.

boardgame: Blood Sweat & Iron: Two Free Scenarios No.1
description:
Two free scenarios for Blood Sweat & Iron simple ACW Ironclad river combat rules.

The first is The Convoy Must Get Through and the second is Run Rabbit Run both are nice and simple and a lot of fun.

Playing as either the attacker or the defender will have its own set of challenges.

Although these scenarios have been written especially for Blood Sweat & Iron, I am sure that with a little bit of adjustment they would work with your preferred rule system.

description from the publisher

boardgame: Blood Sweat & Iron: Two Free Scenarios No.2
description:
What you will find in this second scenario pack is two very simple scenarios for Blood Sweat & Iron simple rules for fighting ACW river battles.

The first scenario is called Destroy the Supplies! and in this scenario a Union force makes a surprise attack on Confederate riverside warehouses.

The second scenario is called Break Out which sees a small Confederate force trying to hold a blockade on a section of river.

Both of these scenarios have been written for Blood Sweat & Iron but I am sure that with a little adjustment they could be used with your preferred rules systems.

description from the publisher

boardgame: Eternøl: 15mm Sci-Fi warfare across the Void Horizon
description:
Eternøl is an easy-to-pick-up, fully customizable, miniature-agnostic tabletop wargame at half-scale designed for 15mm miniatures. 
Players build and paint their armies to defeat opponents using ten-sided dice, tokens, and various terrain types in a play area. 
The game rules and vibrant graphic design are presented in booklet format.

description from the publisher

Bloody Hollow is a 28mm solo/cooperative miniatures game inspired by Washington Irving’s The Legend of Sleepy Hollow. 
You will create a band of colonial mercenaries and investigators who have been called upon to help save the now-haunted Hudson Valley.  
Sculpt, bash, or print unique miniatures that wade into the evil mayhem of Bloody Hollow to try to investigate and return the Headless Horseman to the grave before he covers the valley in darkness forever.

Bloody Hollow is a tabletop miniatures game played on a 3' x 3' table. Like a TTRPG everyone brings character sheets but they also need to bring miniatures. In this game 1-5 players play against the game. You can use whatever miniatures you'd like.

Each player will control a single character in either solo or cooperative play, as they seek to uncover the mystery surrounding a murderous apparition - known as The Headless Horseman - who haunts the region. Players will attempt to unravel the mystery of the dreaded ghostly rider before he collects the heads of the terrified villagers and coats the area in darkness forever.

The gameplay is focused on a narrative campaign where characters fight to clear the land of monsters and learn clues to discover how to ultimately confront and defeat the Headless Horseman. As you play through missions, useful villagers may end up in harm's way. If they perish your access to vital information and goods decreases and the town becomes closer to destruction. But if your missions are successful, you'll bring prosperity and peace... if you don't lose your head.

—description from the publisher
family: Books: The Legend of Sleepy Hollow

description:
Lethalpalooza is a strategic card game where players battle other bands and others to gain enough fans to win the game!

Players start by choosing a genre: Rock, Pop, Country, Electronic, Hip-Hop, and Metal. One enemy band card is revealed to the group and seven cards are given to each player and the round begins. A player can either draw one card from the deck, discard two cards and draw two more, or initiate a Battle.

When a battle is initiated the player who initiated it will roll a dice. From there, going clockwise, each player can enter the battle (by rolling the dice) or resign (by taking a card from the deck. If a player decides to roll they must match or surpass the roll before them, adding gear cards or specialty cards to keep them in the battle. When a player is unable to match or beat the previous number they must fold, discarding all the cards they played.

The last player standing in a battle round wins the enemy band's fans and adds them to their score. A new enemy band is revealed and the game continues on. First to ten fans wins!

The great King's bloodline has ended and the realm of Enemier has been thrown into political chaos. As factions fight for power and champions emerge, the future of the realm is up for grabs.

Barnburners and Noblemen brings the worlds of wargames, tabletop diplomacy, and extensive lore clashing together in an epic death match style wargame. Barnburners and Noblemen is a tabletop skirmisher set in a unique fantasy world. Draft your army with a variety of champions from any faction and seek your victory.

On your turn, you will move your armies units, make your attacks, and prepare to defend from the others. Pairing up different units between the factions allows for limitless choices when it comes to your armies makeup for the round.

The last player with surviving characters is the winner!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Hail Caesar: Once and Future King – The Dark Age Sagas: Volume II
description:
King Arthur is often seen as a figure of myth - surrounded by shining knights, devious wizards and magic swords, yet the legends of King Arthur may have some historical basis. 
This supplement delves into the historical sources for Arthur and provides all the rules and army lists you need to field a heroic British force defending against the depredations of the Saxons, Picts, Scots and Irish in the Dark Ages. The heroes and villains who populated the legends are also featured, allowing you to muster your favourite Arthurian characters ion the battlefield.

Scenarios for Arthur’s twelve battles are detailed, as well as his final fight at Camlann where he faced his nemesis, Mordred. A campaign system allows you to play these as a single narrative, with options to play Arthur in a Welsh, Northern, Southern or pan-British context. 
An additional campaign brings to the table top the action described in Geoffrey of Monmouth’s spurious history of Arthur, whose victories culminated in the mass battle of Siesia, where Arthur and his allies faced off against the might of Rome and her allies in the East.

description from the publisher

A fun food party game where players determine who has the best taste!

Each round one player acts as Chef and creates a dish on the board using the ingredient and creation cards.

All players then secretly give it a score. Reveal and compare; anyone with matching scores are rewarded with a slice of pizza for thinking alike!

A highly social game that uses creative flair, imagination, a little luck-pushing.

Either go with your gut or go with the flow... it's all A MATTER OF TASTE!

Suitable for 2-8 players, ages 7-99.

—description from the designer

The purrfect bluffing galactic card game

Abduct cute pets with your UFOs? Combine memory, strategy and lots of bluffing in the funnest family card game?

Katua is a fun card game about strategy, memory, alien ships and cats, lots of cats.
Each player represents an extraterrestrial commander of the Katua Force, made up of expert UFO fleet pilots, who compete among themselves and come from distant planets, to take abandoned kittens and give them a new home.
Join the Katua Force and help them abduct the largest number of pets.

—description from the designer

PONG FOR CATS: Set up the pom-poms on a table or counter, place the cups below with the point values and watch as your cat does – or doesn’t – play the game!

Play against your cat or have the cat play against itself. Either way, we’re pretty sure the cat always wins. Your cat gets to knock pompons into the cups. When it lands on the target, you get points. You can play against your cat or be on a team with your cat.

Put down your phone and bond with your cat. This will be the first time you cheer your cat as he knocks things off of the counter.

Contents:
- 2 sets of rules for you and your cat to read.
- 6 plastic cups
- 6 colorful pompoms
- Point chipboard for each cup

Entropy is the first full size expansion for the hit deckbuilder/tableau builder Chemical Overload. This expansion includes:
40 new potions ranging from level 2 through 6
All new level 7 potions
4 new toxin tracker boards
20 new toxin event cards
2 new side objective boards
6 new objective card sets 
New Hard mode for solo players
Action tokens
and more!

Over 500 new cards to continue you alchemy journey in Chemical Overload!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: トゥルーマリンショーＸ (True Marine Show X)
description:
After completing her sea adventure, Captain Marine became a businesswoman in search of a place to settle down. But then the secret society HoloX attacks her. Yes, it's a hostile takeover!
The captain, or rather the president, splits into three in the shock of her company being targeted. Will she be able to tame the attackers and protect her position as president? Stay tuned!

This is a light and crunchy deck-building game that combines victory points and resources. Don't worry if you don't have friends to play with. Solo mode is also available! You can play this alone or mix it with other sets in the same series.
family: Video Game Theme: Project Shrine Maiden

description:
Introducing Disbarred -- The first ever legal-themed party game where serious learning meets serious fun!

Disbarred is a groundbreaking card game designed to make learning about the law fun and accessible for everyone — no legal background required! We know learning about the law seems boring (we're lawyers!), but it’s essential knowledge for everyone. That’s why we created a game that combines practical legal insights with entertaining gameplay, making it enjoyable for any and everyone.

Dive into a world of pop culture references, criminal law, family law and more, where each card promises exciting moments and builds strong connections among players. Gain legal insights that are relevant to your everyday life—all while having a blast with friends and family.

Join this revolutionary experience! Back us on Kickstarter and help us bring Disbarred to life!

—description from the designer

Crack Word is a competitive game where players use cards (each card a letter) to create words. When a word is completed, the player who contributed the majority of letters takes all cards. At the end of the game, the player who took the most cards wins.
A few special cards add tactical twists.

boardgame: トゥルーマリンショーＲ (True Marine Show R)
description:
Captain Marine's work as president has stabilized, and she's finally reached a turning point in her life. The next treasure she aims for is the ultimate romance, "marriage." The best way to efficiently advance her marriage search is to split up. Which Marine will reach out to ReGLOSS and Nijisanji and receive a wonderful rice shower of blessings!?

New in this game are characters from Nijisanji, the return of EX cards, and even a new ability trigger. True Marine Show R can be played either by itself or combined with other games in the series. It also includes a solo mode.
family: Video Game Theme: Project Shrine Maiden
artists: Karakara shin (空々心) (からからしん)

description:
F.O.R.K. (Fox, Owl, Rabbit, Kale) is a 2 to 6-player trick-taking game. The goal of the game is to capture animals or kale in the food chain.

Players take turns leading a trick.

The active player calls the suit (terrain), and players must follow (if they have the cards) or play a fox (because it is wild). Players play kale cards face up and everything else face down.

After all players have played a card, reveal all cards and resolve from the highest number to the lowest. The player who played the fox can score an owl or rabbit. 
If the owl survives, it can score a rabbit, and so on following the priority chain listed in the rules.

More than one player scoring a card per trick.

Once a player has five scorecards, the game ends and the player with the highest score wins the game.

In a six-player game, players are in a teams of two. 
Team play is optional in a four-player game.

—description from designer

This DELUXE Box FORK includes (source-Kickstarter):

+  Base Game Cards: Fox, terrain, owl, rabbit and kale, and a rule book

+  Plus: Terrain score board, Terrain token, 6 score tokens, and Wild the Kale expansion

+  Expansion Sets: Eagle, Bear, Wild Cat, Mouse, Skunk, and Tortoises

        
        Base Game and all of the expansion sets.

boardgame: トゥルーマリンショーミニ拡張「GoTo5期生」(True Marine Show Mini Expansion - Go to 5th Gen)
description:
This is a mini expansion for True Marine Show. Adds one additional card in each color + 6 EX cards.


    This cannot be played alone. At least one copy of a full True Marine Show game is required.


The 5th generation members of hololive will be added to the EX slot. The power map of hololive will be redrawn! These are all difficult characters to use, so players will need to use their skills to pilot them correctly.

boardgame: Bronze Shield Silver Coin: Ancient Greek Mercenary Skirmish Wargaming
description:
I have great wealth – a spear, a sword and a fine shield to save my skin.
With these I plough, I reap, I tread the sweet grapes
and am called master of my serfs.
-Hybrias, Cretan mercenary poet, 6th century BC

Create your own mercenary captain, build a warband of about 10 soldiers, and undertake campaigns alongside or against your friends. 
Bronze Shield, Silver Coin is an ancients wargame you can play with just a couple of sprues of models. Battles shift suddenly as you backstab your friends and get their soldiers to betray them, just like the ancient world! The perfect skirmish game for those new to ancients wargaming or for using your existing models in a new adventure.

Gather your warband, recruit stalwart companions, then venture forth to fight in scenarios based on the oftentimes dirty history of spears-for-hire in the ancient world. The rules are easy to learn but offer deep tactical choices. Games play quick and brutal to briskly move forward a campaign with friends. Mechanics emphasize surprises, shifting loyalties, and memorable moments. Your warband’s loyalty and courage will be undermined by the enemy and influenced by your captain’s fortune.
