Whether playing solo adventures, cooperating with friends, or clashing with rivals, Bronze Shield, Silver Coin puts you in the mercenary captain’s sandals. Command your forces wisely and write your name in the annals of history! The ancient world awaits - will you seize the glory?


     Simple, flavorful rules emphasize maneuvering, backstabbing, and management of your precious silver supply.
     Characters only have one stat to keep track of, but can be equipped with different weapons and special abilities to distinguish them on the battlefield.
     Use the Intrigue Card system to disrupt your enemy's chain of command. Bribe their troops into inaction, goad them into charging at an opportune time, or send them fleeing from the fight.
     Eight scenarios based on the dirty history of ancient mercenaries.
     More than 20 special abilities to customize your warband.
     Campaign rules let you chain together games to tell the story of your warband.
     Solo and co-op rules included.


description from the publisher
family: History: Greek & Persian conflicts of the 4th century BC

description:
Contra Cubes is an abstract strategy battle game of cube movement, tile rotation and colour linking. The first player or team to successfully rotate and link five of their own coloured tiles wins - try not to link an opponent's tile or unlink your own!

Taking alternate turns, players will draw the top Move card and move both of their own cubes the full amount shown. Rotate the tiles where the cubes stopped ninety degrees in either direction. Players end their turn by placing or removing Link blocks to indicate which tiles currently match.

The first player to place all five of their coloured Link blocks wins.

boardgame: That's Dope: The Party Game for Strong Opinions
description:
Find out how well you really know your friends or make some new ones! That's Dope: The Party Game for Strong Opinions is a party game and icebreaker for any group with strong opinions. Players take turns guessing who voted "Dope" or "Nope" to hilarious, controversial topics.

Each round, one player reads a topic card, then everyone else secretly votes whether they think the topic is "Dope" or "Nope" using one of their black voting cards. If the active player correctly guesses how many people voted "Dope", they win the topic card, and whoever wins the most topic cards after five rounds wins the game.

JOIN THE AXOLOTL CRAZE! Dive into an aquatic adventure where your goal is to build sets, outwit opponents, and rescue the most axolotls. On your turn, play Axolotl Cards into matching sets. A fun and fast game for all ages.

There are 2 ways to play.

WILD - Everyone plays at the same time - full of chaos and laughter!

CHILL - Everyone takes turns. No-pressure & kid approved!

Whatever mood you're in - the mission stays the same: Save the most axolotls to win the game!

Hexcrement is a conservation education board game about dung beetles and their pursuit of smelly treasure. For anywhere up to six players, this game is a well-calculated blend of chance, strategy, and player interaction. Players enter the six-legged shoes of dung beetles, roll dice, move around six hexagonal playmats placed around a central dice tray, land on tokens to search for dung, and map the terrain as they discover the instructions hidden under each token, hunt for their favourite-coloured dung, and plan their fastest route home.

A unique direction rule means that players do not idle nor can they stay in the same column on a playmat unless the instruction on a token tells them otherwise. Players move in a clockwise or anticlockwise direction indicated on their direction coin, which is flipped just like a coin, at the beginning of the game to randomise the direction. But watch out, because a player's direction might change, and the geometry of the board creates some interesting hurdles and dynamics.

Once dung is found, players can battle the dung-carrying beetle by catching up to them, landing on the same space, and rolling dice to see who has the higher roll. Meet it does not beat it. The challenger cannot lose any dung they are carrying (creating an incentive to catch up to beetles carrying dung), and players who are challenged are slightly protected by the 'meet it does not beat it rule.'

With six different coloured dung and eighteen different instructions hidden under 42 of the 48 tokens spread around the dice tray, players will discover some juicy dynamics as the mechanics of the game unfold. The first beetle home with their favourite-coloured dung is the winner. Beetles who do not find their favourite-coloured dung can still win if they have two other colours of dung. All this is part of the environmental education side of the game where players learn that beetles have their favourite flavours of dung, but can still make use of similar resources that they don't favour. Fear not, this is not a scratch'n'sniff game.

—description from the designer

It is August 1914, Europe is about to be engulfed in war for the next four grueling years. Will the war come to a stalemate in the desolate no man's land between France, Belgium, and the German Empire? The Triple Entente—comprising the United Kingdom, France, and Russia—valiantly opposes the might of the Central Powers. The German and Austro-Hungarian forces plan to overrun Belgium and much of eastern France, posing a dire threat to strategic strongholds like Verdun. The landscape will soon be scarred with trenches and bunkers, and now, the time has come to make your move.

In No More Kings: 2-4 players take control of powerful generals, each determined to bring the Great War to a swift end. Players must carefully manage supplies and earn victory points to overwhelm and defeat their adversaries. By implementing powerful policies, stratagems, and advancements, players will dramatically shape the battlefield, paving the way for one alliance to claim the ultimate victory.

Will France and the United Kingdom reclaim the French countryside and liberate Belgium from German occupation, or will the Central Powers prove too formidable, crushing the Entente once and for all?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Disney Winnie The Pooh: Kids Bingo
description: There are 6 different boards that show pictures from Disney Winnie the Pooh in a 5 x 5 grid. And above are the five letters of the word BINGO. A letter die and a picture die are rolled to determine the field. The first person to cover five pictures horizontally, vertically or diagonally is the winner.

Players travel their pawns over a world map with 6 colored continents. Dice decide where you start and how fast you travel. Everyone receives at the start some Unicents money, 6 culture cards and 1 personal mission card. 
There are assignments that you must carry out immediately, and cards that you can play at a later time. At a trading point, you can either buy a culture card from the bank or you can exchange a culture card with another player.
The first person to get three culture cards from each continent and complete his personal mission wins the game.

You learn about different cultures, nature and monuments.

—user summary

Tutto: Torte-Karte expands Tutto! Volle Lotte (Fill or Bust).

The card counts 1500 points, if you set aside 3 dice of a kind two dice showing a 5 and 1 die showing a 1.

It was part of spielbox issue 6/24 in order to celebrate the 30th anniversary of Tutto at ABACUSSPIELE.

boardgame: Битва колод: Чемпион – Капибара (Clash of Decks: Champion – Capybara)
description:
This is a unique promo card of the Capybara Champion - exclusive to the Russian edition.

The card needs a starter kit/ advanced deck to be playable.

Capybara
Mana Cost: 6
Attack: 2, Defense: 3

The special abilities are:
Defense
Immunity
Regeneration

•••

Уникальная промо карта чемпиона "Капибара"  — эксклюзив русского издания.

Для игры необходима стартовая или любая сезонная колода.

Капибара
Стоимость маны: 6
Атака: 2, Защита: 3

Особые способности:
Оборона
Невосприимчивость
Регенерация

Everything is ready for Christmas, but the tricky little gnomes are out to ruin it. Protect the Christmas spirit from the tricky gnomes and bring happiness to your village. "Tricky Christmas Gnomes" is a fun print and play roll and write game for the whole family. Grab some dice to deliver presents and bring happiness, but remember to keep an eye on the gnomes.

In Tricky Christmas Gnomes, players compete to bring the most joy to their village. The game is for 1 to 99 players and ages 8+ and all you need to play it are 4 six-sided dice, a pencil, and a game sheet per player. The game is played in 10 rounds and lasts about 15'. In each round, the tricky gnomes try to make the people of the village unhappy. Players then use the dice to perform actions and try to score points by taking presents from Santa's sack and delivering them to the village houses to make people happy again. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins and becomes the savior of Christmas.

Can you save the Christmas spirit?

—description from the publisher

Welcome to our store! Players move around the board with their shopping lists and the same contents of their wallets. The first person to buy everything on the list within budget will win. Lista Zakupów is a game that develops many social skills through play and teaches:

     counting to 100,
     doing your shopping,
     strategic thinking,
     healthy competition,
     planning.

Peace and quiet my arse! The famous British detective Claire Harper actually wanted to retire to her tranquil villa on Lake Constance to write her biography. But when a dead woman washes up on the shore, she finds herself in the middle of a new murder mystery. Who murdered her best friend's former employee? One to five players aged 12 and over join the master detective in this mystery game. Card by card, they investigate the crime scene, question suspects and collect clues. With simple rules and a compact format - the perfect detective game for crime fans.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Von wegen Ruhe! Eigentlich wollte die berühmte britische Detektivin Claire Harper sich in die beschauliche Villa am Bodensee zurückziehen, um an ihrer Biografie zu schreiben. Doch als eine tote Frau ans Ufer gespült wird, steckt sie mittendrin in einem neuen Krimi-Fall. Wer hat die ehemalige Angestellte ihrer besten Freundin ermordet? Ein bis fünf Spielerinnen und Spieler ab 12 Jahren ermitteln in diesem Krimispiel gemeinsam mit der Meisterdetektivin. Karte für Karte untersuchen sie den Tatort, befragen Verdächtige und sammeln Hinweise. Mit einfachen Regeln und im kompakten Format – das perfekte Detektivspiel für Krimifans.

—description from the publisher

Introduction:Villawar or Villagers and Warriors is a two-player strategy game: Black and White. It is played on the squares (cells) of a square board with an initial setup of 16 villagers in the first 2 rows of each player without warriors. The default size is 8 cells per side, but boards of 6 or 10 are also valid. Each player has 16 double-sided pieces: one side represents a villager (unmarked), and the other a warrior (marked).

				
				
					Definitions:
     A phalanx is a maximal set of pieces of the same color connected in the same orthogonal or diagonal line, composed only of villagers or only of warriors. A single piece is also a phalanx.
     To jump is to move a piece in a straight line, either orthogonally or diagonally, crossing exactly one friendly phalanx and landing on an empty cell or on an enemy piece, with the possibility that there may be cells before or after the jumped phalanx.


				
				
					Turns:White plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, do one of the following actions:


     Move a villager of your color by jumping over a phalanx of friendly villagers and turn them into warriors.
     Move a villager of your color by jumping over a phalanx of friendly warriors and remove them from the board.
     Move a warrior of your color by jumping over a phalanx of friendly warriors and capture by replacement the first enemy piece it encounters in its path.


If you cannot make a move, pass your turn; otherwise, passing is not allowed.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when both players pass on consecutive turns. The player with the farthest villager from their starting row wins (considering each player's starting row as their first row). If both have villagers in the same row, the player with more villagers in that row wins; in case of a tie, the previous rows are compared. If there is still a tie after evaluating all villagers, the same criteria are applied to the warriors.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Rust Belt Express / Union Pacific Express
description:
This is a two map release for Age of Steam 2024. Maps are printed on high quality poster stock front and back.

Both expansion maps are designed to be played in a shorter time, about 60 minutes, with fewer rules than typical expansions.

Rust Belt Express is for 3 to 4 players. 
The beginning of Age of Steam is the original "Rust Belt" map, a true classic. Rust Belt Express is set in a smaller region of this map, with fewer rules, fewer turns, and all the tense decisions ramped up in a shorter play time. Pittsburgh plays a major role on this map and accepts cubes of all colors and pays $2 for per delivery black cube.

Union Pacific Express is for 3 to 4 players. It was originally released for TRAXX MAINLINE magazine in 2022. The only changes to this version are cosmetic graphics changes. 
---From its early roots in Council Bluffs and Omaha, the Union Pacific Railroad grew west and ultimately connected the East Coast to the West Coast. In this expansion map, players focus on building from some of the Union Pacific’s main hubs: Chicago, Omaha, Kansas City, and Cheyenne, as well as from Bailey Yard in Nebraska, the largest railyard in the world.

—description from the publisher

Can you separate fact from fiction in this game of true or false?

Find out how accurate your truth-radar is with this bizarre set of true or false cards. Including 100 crazy questions, score a point for each correct answer as you take turns to guess which statements are accurate, and which are totally fictitious!

Manage the slime that constantly falls into the cylinder! If you create the Legendary Lucky 7 Slime, you win!

Combine slimes to create 'Legendary Lucky 7 Slime'! Legendary Lucky 7 Slime is a slime made up of 7 squares that cannot be drawn with dice. You can only make Legendary Lucky 7 Slime by combining two 6 Slimes.

Stay longer than others without being eliminated!

—description from the designer

Sentando a Pua transports us to the golden age of aviation, putting us in the cockpits of legendary World War II fighters. Feel the adrenaline rush of intense dogfights as we ride classics like the Italian C202 Folgore and the Brazilian P-47 Thunderbolt. With intuitive and accessible gameplay mechanics, even for newcomers, and a detailed historical setting, the game offers an immersive experience that makes us feel like real flying aces. Developed by award-winning designer Hugo Bustilho, known for his attention to historical detail, 'Sentando a Pua' is an invitation to relive the most exciting moments of World War II in the skies.

Preparation of the game:

The board is placed in the center of the table with the hit markers within everyone's reach, and each player (from 2 to 8 players) chooses an airplane and takes his 10 maneuver cards, his d10 die and his plane card corresponding to the chosen plane.

Objective of the Game:

Shoot down the enemy planes.

Gameplay:

Each turn, players choose one of their hole cards and place it face down, next to the board. After all players choose the maneuver cards, they all reveal their cards at the same time, the player with the lowest initiative starts by moving their plane around the board according to their chosen card.

When moving his plane, an enemy plane enters his sights, that player can choose to shoot at the enemy plane.

If the shot is a success, after rolling the 10-sided die (D10) the attacked player puts a hit on his plane card.

After suffering the full number of hits, the plane is shot down.

If the number on the die is higher than the vital number of the attacked plane, that plane is shot down instantly.

Like?

Let's Sit On Enemy Planes!

—description from the designer

Sentando a Pua nos transporta para a era dourada da aviação, nos colocando nos cockpits de lendários caças da Segunda Guerra Mundial. Sinta a adrenalina de dogfights intensos enquanto pilotamos clássicos como o italiano C202 Folgore e o brasileiro P-47 Thunderbolt. Com uma mecânica de jogo intuitiva e acessível, mesmo para os novatos, e uma ambientação histórica rica em detalhes, o jogo oferece uma experiência imersiva que nos faz sentir como verdadeiros áses da aviação. Desenvolvido pelo premiado designer Hugo Bustilho, conhecido por sua atenção aos detalhes históricos, 'Sentando a Pua' é um convite para reviver os momentos mais emocionantes da Segunda Guerra Mundial nos céus.

Preparação do jogo:

Coloca-se o tabuleiro ao centro da mesa com os marcadores de hits ao alcance de todos, e cada jogador (de 2 a 8 jogadores) escolhe um avião e pega suas 10 cartas de manobras, seu dado d10 e sua carta de avião correspondente ao avião escolhido.

Objetivo do Jogo:

Derrubar os aviões inimigos.

Gameplay:

A cada turno, os jogadores escolhem uma entre suas cartas de mão e coloca-a voltada para baixo, próximo ao tabuleiro. Após tods os jogadores escolherem as cartas de manobras, todos revelam suas cartas ao mesmo tempo, o jogador com a iniciativa mais baixa começa movendo seu avião pelo tabuleiro de acordo com sua carta escolhida.

Ao mover seu avião, um avião inimigo entra na sua visada, esse jogador pode escolher atirar no avião inimigo.

Caso o tiro seja um sucesso, após rolar o dado de 10 faces (D10) o jogador atacado, coloca um hit na sua carta de avião.

Após sofrer o número total de hits, o avião é abatido.

Caso o numero do dado seja superior ao número vital do avião atacado, esse avião é derrubado instantaneamente.

Gostou?

Bora Sentar a Pua nos aviões adversários!

—description from the designer (Portuguese)

A team game at speed...

The aim of the game is to be the first team to roll the word “Tammy” with the letter dice. But a few dice are causing problems...

—description from the publisher

Two gladiators compete directly against each other in the arena. Who can act tactically on the field?

The players place marker stones, which score points if they end up in certain constellations.

—description from the publisher

Up to 13 players compete against each other. Who throws their dice best?

The players roll the dice against any boundary and try to get as close to it as possible.

—description from the publisher

Two tactical placement games for 2 players aged 8 and over

In this game, two players try to gain majorities on the roads by placing their own pieces and changing the course of the roads.

—description from the publisher

In this game for two traders, the players use their dice values to buy goods at auctions to be collect larger amounts of them in order to sell them at higher prices in the end.

—description from the publisher

Mystic Rememberland is based on the classic memo game and can be played by up to 1-6 players.

The active player turns over two squares one after the other - for all to see. If these squares are not the same, the active player turns them over again in the same place. Once the player has found a pair, he takes a marker from the supply and places it in one of the empty spaces. The markers have the following effects:


     Eye marker: If a landscape part is turned over that is orthogonally adjacent to such a marker and the active player does not manage to find a pair, he takes the marker as a minus point.



     Foot marker: If a landscape tile is turned over that is orthogonally adjacent to such a marker and the active player does not manage to find a pair, he swaps this landscape tile with a landscape tile that is orthogonally 1 or 2 spaces further away (depending on the number of feet). However, the foot marker remains where it is.


The game ends when all pairs of landscape tiles have been cleared.

—description from the publisher

A dice-based trading game for two players in which goods must be bought, shipped, stored and then sold.

The players load goods into their ship, which are then stored in the warehouse and finally sold. Players receive points if they sell the majority of a commodity on the market. With 5 different goods, a player must therefore sell more of 3 types of goods than the other player.

—description from the publisher

Up to 5 knights saddle up their toads and compete against each other in a jumping contest. Who will be the first to complete the journey?

All players roll the dice at the same time. Whoever rolls the highest number moves their toad knight to the next free space. The other players follow in order according to their values (descending). The player who rolls the lowest value will move the furthest forward.

—description from the publisher

A dice game for up to 4 players in which you have to roll your fish dice to match the food in the water. This also requires a bit of luck

The active player rolls all the dice in the middle of the table so that the dice are placed between the disks on display. If the fish on the dice now point with their heads to a food disk (use a ruler, your cell phone or similar to help), they score points.

—description from the publisher

From the newspaper to the game board, challenge opponents or work co-operatively to solve pre-published sudoku puzzles or build your own in Duo Coup: Sudoku for Two. Place tiles accordingly and be prepared to defend them with logic. The player with the most correct tiles wins.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to your camping holiday. Quickly set up the tent, a cool drink and onto the bath towel by the lake. How lovely. And what is that buzzing now?

A dice game for up to 7 players in which you have to avoid being bitten by mosquitoes. A little luck with the dice helps enormously.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kamigami Battles: Baba Yaga, the Witch
description:
Released via the Japanime Games patreon.

Baba Yaga, the Witch is promo card # KBP-019

Act: Banish a Disciple from your hand to add a Warrior costing 4 or less to your discard pile.
React: If one of your warriors would be Banished, perform Adjudication. If you reveal a Disciple, Discard the Warrior instead.

boardgame: Worldspanner Factions: Promo Pack A Treasures!
description:
Promo pack offered during Worldspanner Factions crowdfunding campaign.

Five unique treasures for your hero to acquire: Shocking Eye, Pegasus, Potion of Acuity, Glitter Soulstone, Ice-Forge Falchion

boardgame: Worldspanner Factions: Promo Pack B Heroes!
description:
Promo pack offered with Worldspanner Factions crowdfunding campaign.

Four unique heroes: Baba Yaga, Nimsea, Ettamerra, Hadbiztos Rozsa

The Dwarven High Council has set up headquarters on Pluto to establish mining operations on the Plutonian moons. As ambitious prospectors, you are vying to collect gems, use them to fulfill Council contracts, and gain valuable shares of the mining operations.

Each round you’ll send rocket probes to Pluto and/or its moons to acquire contracts or gems, respectively. The rocket probe navigation systems are based on an ancient tavern dice game though, so you must choose wisely about which ones launch.

The game takes place over five rounds with players rolling and placing rocket dice, claiming gems and new contracts, and fulfilling contracts to gain valuable shares. After five rounds, the player with the most number of shares wins.

-description from designer

boardgame: Original Remmidemmi "Wie du mir: so ich dir!
description:
The two-sided game board allows for 2-4 and 3 player participation. Each player receives 4 pawns of one color. These are placed on the corners. The players take turns to roll and move in the direction of the arrows of the same color, along the track from "A" on the outside, clockwise to "B", then changing to the middle, anti-clockwise to "C", then changing inwards, clockwise again to "D" of the same color, into the 4 target spaces. After "B" and "C" between the track circles, each player has one safety space. He cannot be hit there. The aim is to occupy your own safety spaces and wait to ambush the passing pawns of the other players. If a player can still move with other pawns, he keeps the or one of the safety spaces occupied. Otherwise he must move on with the or one of the safety pawns on the next throw, provided he does not have to enter the game with another pawn on throwing a 6. 
Whoever gets their 4 pawns to the target spaces first is the winner.

—user summary

Two or three knights compete against each other in direct competition at this year's tournament. Who will manage to knock over the opposing knights?

To do this, players flick one of their own knights in an attempt to knock over other knights. All knights that fall over are out of the game. The players flick in turn until only knights of one color are left standing - this is the winning player, who receives points according to the number of knights still standing.

—description from the publisher

Mathematics can be so colourful...

The active player stacks 12 discs face down behind a screen. Then he names the colours from top to bottom with ascending numbers for everyone to hear. So ‘Red - 1’, ‘Green - 2 ...’. The other players must memorise this sequence.

Now it is up to the active player to set tasks. Many things are allowed, e.g:  Red minus blue = ? (colour), purple + orange = ? (number) or 4 - 2 = ? (colour). Maths and memo...

—description from the publisher

Two players compete directly against each other. Who flicks their tokens best?

Each player receives tokens of one color and tries to flick them so that they come to rest on or near the scoring chips on display. Points are awarded for this. The game is played over 7 rounds. At the start of the game, the players choose a round in which their points count double.

The winner is the player who has scored the most points in total after the 7 rounds.

—description from the publisher

A board game about the Great City of Benton Harbor, Mi.  It's jammed full of outdoor adventures, family attractions, rich history, and of course, Lake Michigan.

Here's a quick idea of how to play! Buy property, collect City Blocks and trade in for a Key to the City. It sounds easy enough - but add in a traffic jam, property taxes, and parking fines,  and it becomes a little more difficult... and a lot more FUN!

Get your game on, Choose your token, and advance to Go, Benton Harbor.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
Dexterity set collection for 1-4 players.

Players pick up cards with food preferences, and then have to use chopsticks to pick up the chips representing the food preferences from a shared hot-pot (very bad chopstick manners, hence the name)

A communication game with a yakuza theme.

The yakuza members are gunning for a promotion by winning the favor of the boss's only daughter.

In this game, players split into roles between the lone "princess" and several yakuza.

For each topic they draw, players must predict whether the princess approves or not.

The first time a yakuza gets it wrong they get a red card, and a second time means they're eliminated. The last man standing wins.

—description from the publisher (translated)

An MMORPG simulator for 1-5 players with cooperative and competitive elements, mostly a race to collect victory points by trading in gathered resources.

Players first customize their characters, and then spend their turns taking either one of two actions: moving their pawn, or spending a resource card.

Players must move their player pawns around the board gathering resources of different types from different spaces, and turn them in them at different shops to purchase the items from that shop. Many of the items available grant abilities to the player, or change the rules of the game slightly. Some are purely victory points or equipment to strengthen their character.

The items purchased have different victory point values, and the game ends after the shops have run out of stock, at which point the players determine who has won.

This is a psychological card game that both adults and children can enjoy and easily negotiate.

A group of friends visit a diner at night. There is something strange about the atmosphere in the restaurant. One of the friends eats the pizza on the table without permission and turns into a pizza zombie! If you are caught by this zombie, you will become a zombie. Choose a good hiding place to avoid being found and escape.

The winner is the person who manages to remain a pizza zombie and not end the game.

I can't give up on my family or my job. Second trick-taking

Moderately Elite is a trick-taking game with must-follow rules.

As an elite sea otter, collect shells from all over the world and aim for success. However, sea otters are creatures that value their families . If you achieve the top score , you will be sent on a business trip overseas and separated from your family.

Maintain a balance between work and family and aim to come in second as a "moderately elite" otter.

The story of the city woven by the subway

This is a game where you place tracks one by one in order and connect stations to create a subway line.
The more lines there are, the bigger the station will grow.

Aim for a high score by connecting as many large stations as possible .

The shortcut to victory is to intersect with everyone in a balanced way.

When the tough battle was over, there was a beautiful railway map on the desk, as if everyone had worked together to create it .

What kind of city would the new metropolis, "TOKYO," have become?

Available from Dice Tower 2024 Kickstarter Eclipse Pack.

1 campfire card
4 tinderblox cards

boardgame: Draft & Write Records: Dice Tower Band Member Promo Cards
description:
Available from Dice Tower 2024 Kickstarter Eclipse Pack.

Three new lead singer cards.

boardgame: Escape from New York: Dice Tower 2024 Promo Pack
description:
Available from Dice Tower 2024 Kickstarter Eclipse Pack.

Four new action cards:

     My Name is Plissken (Snake)
     We're Going with Snake Now (Maggie)
     You're a Special Case (Cabbie)
     Friend of yours, Brain? (Brain)

boardgame: Sammu-ramat: The Dice Tower Promo Pack
description:
Available from Dice Tower 2024 Kickstarter Eclipse Pack.

Contains

     Challenge - Tower of Babel
     Wonder - Tower
     Character - Milla
     Punchboard
     Rules

Take on the role of one of four witches competing in the annual potion brewing contest! The winner receives a 50% off coupon for Hansel’s Housing Goods and the keys to a gently used houseboat. The twist? All competitors must share a single cauldron, making every move critical. A poorly placed ingredient could allow your opponents finish their potions first and take the lead. You must plan carefully, block your rivals, and outmaneuver the competition to win in this dynamic, engaging, and quick competitive board game!

Players take turns pulling a random ingredient from a bag and placing it on an empty location of a three by three grid (the cauldron). After placing the ingredient, the player can complete a potion by matching an exact color pattern to a recipe card that they have in their hand or displayed around the cauldron. If they don't see a match, they may draw a card and then discard a card to search for a more suitable recipe. The first player to complete a number of potions based on the number of players wins.

—description from the designer

Ketupat Rendang is a casual and fun-filled board game that celebrates cultural aspects of the Eid celebration in Southeast Asia. Players play the Dishes card and cook their favorite dishes. The game combines elements of strategy, luck, and cultural education, making it a perfect addition to any Hari Raya celebration.

Player draw a Role card where they have their personal objective. Player then draw 2 Dish card where it contains fire and dishes that they have to cook. Each turn, player will draw a Dish card and play one Dish card on the table to cook their dish.

To cook dish, they need to place it next to a fire. Player can sabotage other player(s) by placing other dish on top of another player's Dish card.

The game end when all Dish card has run out from the deck. Player that cooked the most of their own dishes will be the winner.

boardgame: スクラップ&ライト (Scrap & Write)
description:
You are a planner who has been asked to redevelop a city. Get rid of the old facilities, build new ones, and propose a new city plan.

However, it won't be that simple. You are not the only planner who has been asked to do this. Accept their opinions when necessary, and create the best city plan.

Scrap & Write is a paper and pen game that focuses on puzzles. 
Each player has one plan. This is copied onto a map to create a city plan.

You can use not only your own plan, but also the plans of other players. Each player has a limited number of uses, so you need to plan your moves carefully.

Each round, you need to "Scrap" one facility from your plan, and the plan you copy onto the map gradually gets smaller.

At the end of the game, the player with the highest score is the winner.

—description from the designer

Fortune telling, play and spend your cozy mystical evenings at the Imaginarium: Tarot. A new version of the board game about associations is now 2 in 1. Even more fun, mysticism and imagination!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Гадайте, играйте и проводите свои уютно-мистические вечера за «Имаджинариумом: Таро». Новая версия настольной игры про ассоциации теперь 2 в 1. Ещё больше веселья, мистики и воображения!

—description from the publisher

Dungeon Alliance: Paragons is the second full expansion for the Dungeon Alliance deck-building miniatures adventure game.

This expansion adds the following new materials to the game:


     New Heroes & Upgrades: The expansion includes 4 new female heroes, including each character’s Hero Card, Hero Figure, and 3 Starting Deck Cards. The expansion also includes 8 new Upgrade Cards.
     New Adventure Pack: Adventure Pack 5 (Rise of the Northern Kingdoms) explores Lady Eleanor's quest to reclaim the Fallen Throne of Norr. 
     New Dungeon Tiles & Monsters: 6 new Dungeon Tiles feature 6 new Monsters to contend with during your adventures.
     New Quests: 3 new Quests, including a Final Enemy with its own set of Final Enemy Cards.
     New Artifacts: 3 new Artifact Cards, because you can never have enough!
     Alternate Art Monster Cards: The base game's 15 monsters are presented with updated art. The original art is included as an icon on the card for easy reference.


—description from the publisher

Today we found a new abandoned place where you can collect a lot of useful parts. Hurry before someone else finds it! And if you meet someone else, get ready for a fight. I'm giving you our best robots, but remember - each of them only has four slots for parts, so don't overload them!

Players send their robots to grab useful spare parts. Like in retro games, when robot goes to the cell with the spare part, it gains that ability.

—description from the designer

Whoosh! Soar into space with Roll and Write Revolution as we join Rae Gunn and Rescue Rocket, a duo known for saving all manner of extraterrestrial life on planets that are in danger of destruction from forces like black holes, imperial menaces, and planetary cataclysms.
 Rae Gunn and her trusty ship, Rescue Rocket, with their heroic missions arrive bringing hope to those in dark times. Why just last week they saved a planet of kittens who had run out of tasty snacks! The week before they helped a caffeine-dependent planet construct orbiting greenhouses to grow coffee beans with three-times the caffeination! A month before that they helped a planet full of dinosaurs construct a meteor blocking shield! Quite the feats really.

In the dark depths of space a hero and her trusty robot-powered rocket ship are preparing to save the residents of Planet X. Rae Gunn has been known as a force for hope across the cosmos and now the call for help has gone out from Planet X. Can Rae and Rescue save the day?

Planet X is melting, the magma is out of control and the residents need to escape. They would also really appreciate it if Rae Gunn could help them take some of their precious minerals with them as it is the source of wealth they would need to start their lives somewhere new. Rae and Rescue will also find tools to help them on their quest.

In order to save the residents of Planet X, Rae Gunn and Rescue Rocket will have to place residents into groups based on scoring criteria. Each turn, the player(s) decide if they will use the card for scoring categorization or if the card will fit in one the the previously identified categories for scoring. If they can’t place a card, that’s the end of the game. Paying close attention to each card and figuring out where it goes will lead the player to achieve their lofty goal of being the Savior of Planet X!

Rae Gunn and Rescue Rocket is compatible with other games in the Rescue series including Safe and Hound and Otters to the Rescue. Players will have the option to try those games with  Rae Gunn and Rescue Rocket as a competitive game.

—description from the designer

A new racer for the Dodos Riding Dinos game family inspired by the boardgame award “Quetzalera” given in Roll a Game Expo (México).

Snakes & Ladders: Roll 1 die. If the result is 1, 2 or 3, advance that amount.
Quetza & Ladder: At the end of your turn, you may discard 1 card from your hand. If you do, activate Snakes & Ladders.

This Christmas-themed, laugh-out-loud game is simple to learn and hilarious to play. Players choose a challenge card from their hand, then write the name of the player they want to “gift” the challenge to with the included dry-erase marker, attach it to the challenge, then place it face down in the middle. On each player’s turn, they draw a challenge from the center (if their name is on it, they choose a challenger; if someone else’s name is on it, that’s who they battle). At the end of the challenge, the group votes on the winner, who can then grab a treat token. The first with five tokens wins the game.

—description from publisher
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

description:
Cerro (Italian edition): 1 point for each Cloven-hoofed animal symbol.

Lucca Comics & Games 2024

Dungeon Foury is a SOLO Roll & Write dungeon-crawling adventure in Print and Play format.

It features four missions with increasing difficulty, all set in a single dungeon. The map evolves and changes as you explore, offering a unique experience every time you play.

HOW IT WORKS – A QUICK OVERVIEW 
There are 4 adventures (or rounds) to complete the dungeon. Each adventure involves performing a specific action to awaken the Oracle at the center of the map.

Choose your starting point and move one square at a time. Each move generates, through a dice roll, events and the configuration of the square. You’ll need shovels, weapons, shields, and a bit of luck to reach the Oracle—and then escape.

Once you’ve reached the Oracle, you’ll need to go back trough a different way, to proceed to the next adventure. This action modifies the map for the upcoming mission.

At the end of each round, you can access the Market to trade resources and spend the treasures you’ve collected.

The adventures become increasingly challenging as the map’s complexity grows and the monsters become stronger.
Additionally, we’ll include character sheets with special powers and the option to use your FAVORITE BOOK to gather a phrase from the Oracle each turn.

—description from the designer

Embark on a Trade and Prestige Adventure to the Orient! Get ready to push back the frontiers of trade with bastions and forge lucrative trade routes to the Orient! The Splendor: The Sun Never Sets expansion offers new strategic possibilities and will add tension to your games!

EXPANSION FOR SPLENDOR: The Sun Never Sets is a must-have expansion for the popular Splendor board game, introducing new strategies and heightened tension to your gameplay.
NEW GAME MECHANICS: This expansion adds bastions and the ability to forge lucrative trade routes to the Orient, expanding your options and adding fresh challenges to your strategy.
INCREASED STRATEGIC DEPTH: With new strategic possibilities, players must adapt their approach to win, creating a more intense and rewarding experience.
ENHANCED GAMEPLAY EXPERIENCE: The Sun Never Sets brings new twists to your Splendor games, ensuring that no two sessions are alike with its dynamic new features.
PERFECT FOR SPLENDOR FANS: Ideal for Splendor enthusiasts looking to elevate their gameplay, this expansion adds exciting layers of strategy and complexity to the original game.

Secret Recipes is a game for kids, families, and adults ages 9+. The game is a fun, family-friendly baking-themed card game where players compete to complete delicious recipes and score Cake Points. Secret Recipes is a lively and strategic card game where players compete to become the ultimate master baker by collecting ingredients and completing recipes. The game challenges players to gather the right ingredients to bake Cakes and Cupcakes while using their character's unique abilities to gain an edge. Players take turns asking each other for specific ingredients, diving into the pantry to discover new cards, and revealing secret recipes in their play area. But there's a twist! Once a recipe is revealed, it becomes vulnerable to other players who can steal it by adding the missing ingredients from their hand. With multiple strategies to victory—whether it's cleverly holding onto cards, strategically revealing recipes, or seizing opportunities to steal points from opponents—Secret Recipes keeps everyone on their toes.

Each turn has 3 phases:

     Action Phase
     Play Phase
     Discard Phase


During the play phase, a player chooses between three possible actions: Ask for Ingredients, Deep Dive in the Pantry, or Pick up a New Recipe.

Ending the Game:
Once one player scores 12 or more cake points, each player takes one more turn, including the player who reached 12 points first. For players who want a longer game, they can play until the face-down Ingredient Deck is empty.

boardgame: Skirmish Kids: Small Battles for Young Minds
description:
Fight your own tabletop battles with toy soldiers!

These basic wargame rules put you in command of toy soldiers fighting over a battlefield of your own design.

Kids can easily learn the core rules for soldiers moving and shooting. The more they play, the more they can enhance their skirmishes with advanced rules, giving some soldiers heroic abilities or special weapons. Use them to fight make-believe skirmishes or historical engagements.

An engaging introduction to the wargame hobby kids can explore with their friends and family.

Inside you’ll find:

     Basic rules for skirmishes between small groups of toy soldiers.
     Advanced rules options to add new dimensions to combat as you become more familiar with the game.
     Guidelines for playing games in different historical periods and fantastic settings.
     Six starter scenarios — situations defining how games start — and more with each set of period guidelines.


You will need to bring your own toys to this fight: a crowd of toy soldiers or figures, some six-sided dice, props for battlefield terrain, and a ruler or tape measure.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Mystery Cube: Mystery_Rätsel: Lost Places – OP-Raum
description:
The mystery game for in-between in a small format.

The Mystery Cube "Operating Theatre" contains a richly illustrated room in which 6 exciting puzzles are hidden. Just like in a real escape room, various elements in the room have to be cleverly combined with other clues to find the solution. There are hints and the solution to each puzzle on the corresponding cards. Once all the puzzles have been successfully solved, a digital surprise can be unlocked.

Whether at home, on the go or during a break in between - the Mystery Cube ‘Operating Theatre’ fits in every pocket and has exciting puzzles in store. Your skills and powers of deduction will be put to the test on a tour of discovery through an abandoned building. Will you rise to the tricky challenge?

—description from the publisher (translated)

Das Mystery-Spiel für zwischendurch im kleinen Format.

Der Mystery Cube "OP-Raum" enthält einen detailreich illustrierten Raum, in dem 6 spannende Rätsel versteckt sind. Wie in einem echten Escape Room müssen verschiedene Elemente im Raum mit weiteren Hinweisen clever kombiniert werden, um der Lösung auf die Spur zu kommen. Für jedes Rätsel finden sich Tipps und die Auflösung auf den entsprechenden Karten. Wenn alle Rätsel erfolgreich gelöst wurden, lässt sich eine digitale Überraschung freischalten.

Ob zu Hause, unterwegs oder während einer Pause zwischendurch - der Mystery Cube "OP-Raum" passt in jede Tasche und hält spannende Rätsel bereit. Bei einer Entdeckungstour durch ein verlassenes Gebäude werden deine Fähigkeiten und Kombinationsgabe auf die Probe gestellt. Stellst du dich der kniffeligen Herausforderung?

—description from the publisher

Simple rules: Six pages are enough to describe the game system, which is based on the use of d8. Despite this brevity, the essential elements of a tactical game are covered: initiative, movement, terrain effects, line of sight, shooting and assault, etc.
     Short games: Mecha Fire is designed to be played with few units and on small maps, which speeds up the games and facilitates remote play. On average, it is possible to play three games in one afternoon. Eight types of missions are offered to vary the pleasures. In addition, a campaign mode allows units to accumulate experience and obtain special abilities.
     Army customization: You can not only compose your army à la carte from a points budget, but also create each unit of your army. After assigning points to the characteristics of the unit to be created, choose the systems it will carry from the 43 possible options. For simplicity, you can also use the many examples of ready-to-use armies and units.
     A game accessible to all: The necessary material is limited, because you can use most of the "battlemaps" on the market without any worries. And if you don't have any figurines, links are provided to acquire printable pawns. Finally, the game itself is offered for free payment and under a Creative Commons license.


—description from the publisher (translated)

Couchés sur le sol détrempé à la lisière de la forêt, les soldats de la colonie indépendante de Tradicus attendent l’ennemi. Soudain, ils ressentent les premières secousses ; peu après, ils entendent des piétinements de mauvaise augure, à peine atténués par leurs casques. Puis la terre vibre de plus en plus violemment, comme si elle était pilonnée sans relâche. Le cœur au bord des lèvres à force d’être brinquebalés, tous gardent les yeux rivés devant eux.

Au loin apparaissent cinq titans de métal haut comme des immeubles et brandissant des armes gigantesques. Des méchas impériaux ! Et derrière eux s'élèvent une multitude de colonnes de fumée, signe que la contre-offensive des chars de Tradicus a échoué. Même les vétérans déglutissent, car ils savent que la fin est proche.

Une voix légèrement tremblante retentit alors dans les casques : " Feu à volonté ! Pour l'honneur de Tradicus !"

Mecha Fire est un jeu de combat tactique futuriste avec figurines. 
Il met en scène des batailles entre méchas, qui sont des robots géants pilotés par des humains. Des troupes conventionnelles – chars, infanterie, etc. – peuvent aussi participer aux affrontements.

Les atouts du jeu

     Des règles simples : Six pages suffisent à décrire le système de jeu, qui repose sur l’utilisation de d8. Malgré cette brièveté, les éléments essentiels d’un jeu tactique sont couverts : initiative, mouvement, effets du terrain, ligne de mire, tir et assaut, etc.
     Des parties courtes : Mecha Fire est conçu pour être joué avec peu d’unités et sur de petites cartes, ce qui accélère les parties et facilite le jeu à distance. En moyenne, il est possible de jouer trois parties en une après-midi. Huit types de missions sont proposés pour varier les plaisirs. De plus, un mode campagne permet aux unités d'accumuler de l'expérience et d'obtenir des capacités spéciales.
     La personnalisation des armées : Vous pouvez non seulement composer votre armée à la carte à partir d'un budget de points, mais aussi créer chaque unité de votre armée. Après avoir attribué des points aux caractéristiques de l'unité à créer, choisissez les systèmes qu’elle embarquera parmi les 43 options possibles. Pour plus de simplicité, vous pouvez aussi utiliser les nombreux exemples d’armées et d'unités prêtes à l'emploi.
     Un jeu accessible à tous : Le matériel nécessaire est restreint, car vous pourrez utiliser sans souci la plupart des « battlemaps » du marché. Et si vous n’avez pas de figurines, des liens sont proposés pour acquérir des pions à imprimer. Enfin, le jeu lui-même est proposé en libre paiement et sous licence Creative Commons.


—description from the publisher

Killer Questions is the dead funny ‘would you rather’ party game! Grab some friends, spin the Grim Reaper, then get ready for some of the funniest (and strangest) would you rather questions you’ve ever seen! Each round you vote for which option you’d rather out of two choices… but don’t be the odd one out as it could be the death of you! People will fall, people will resurrect, but only one will be the survivor of these killer questions!

THE ‘WOULD YOU RATHER?’ GAME WITH A KILLER TWIST: Prepare to face off against hilarious “would you rather” questions that will have you and your friends in stitches.
SPIN THE GRIM REAPER: Will the Reaper choose you to ask the next Killer Question?
CAN YOU SURVIVE THE ROUND?: Don’t be the odd one out or face the deadly consequences! Vote for the option you’d rather but be careful as your decision could be your demise.
WHO WILL BE THE ULTIMATE SURVIVOR: Defy death and keep playing to see who can outlast the rest and survived these Killer Questions?
BRING THE PARTY TO LIFE: Invite your friends and family to answer these Killer Questions, where hilarity and high stakes collide for an unforgettable game night.

—description from the publisher

The Mystery Cube ‘Agent's Office’ contains a richly illustrated room in which 6 exciting puzzles are hidden.
