—description from the publisher
Expand the basic Cities game with a new achievement board. That includes the main attractions of the cities of Warszawa and Kraków for even more variety.

You go to the market, choose your ingredients and make the best soup in town! In this game of ingredients and recipes each player attempts to predict what ingredients he or she will have and what soups can be made. If you collect ingredients and can’t make soup with them, you may hear the other players gloat, “No Soup For You!”
No Soup for You is a game where players gain cards with ingredients and a simple 3-ingredient recipe.  They attempt not only to make those soups with the ingredients, but also to predict what soups they will make in future rounds.

Game Mechanics: Card drafting, prediction.

—description from the designer

You ever wish there was a strategy game that didn't take a half hour to set up and hours more to play? 
If so look no further than Missionary Crusades.

In Missionary Crusades you are tasked with creating your own religion in which to spread across the map. 
Easy to learn rules and gameplay allow for easy set-up and an average play time of 45 minutes to a hour. 
Missionary Crusades is a 2 to 4 player strategy game for those 12 and up. 
Crusade against other players to convert the entire map. 
The Game Mat is a map of 14TH century medieval Europe and has 36 territories to convert. 
Game also includes a eight card perk deck that rotates to players throughout the game.

—description from the publisher

A children's game published in Pakistan by Manomama, players move their pawns across a wooden board answering trivia questions about Islam. With a correct answer, they move ahead to the next of 48 spaces to prepare for another question - the first play to reach the trophy at the end of the board wins.

boardgame: Ark Nova: Promotion Team & Capybara Promo Cards
description:
Available from Dice Tower 2024 Kickstarter Doppler Pack.

1 new Animal card (Capybara): Cost $7
Petting Zoo Animal
• Gain 3 Appeal for each Petting Zoo Animal icon in your zoo. 
Water Affinity
• Gain 2 Appeal if the petting zoo is adjacent to a water space.

1 new Sponsor card (Promotion Team): Cost Level 5
• Find a person Sponsor card (instant)
• Each time a person Sponsor card is played into any zoo, gain $2 and 1 Appeal. 
• End Game points: 1 Conservation point if your zoo contains 5 person Sponsor cards.

Available from Dice Tower 2024 Kickstarter Eclipse Pack.

Contains 3 new Citizen cards, 1 of each color (green, yellow, and gray)

Trappers and Traders is based on possible trade interactions between four groups at South Branch House, an historic fur trade post in central Saskatchewan. Players are divided into four groups: Hudson's Bay Company Traders, North West Company Traders, First Nations Trading Party, and Metis Trading Party. Each group gets a set of 21 oversize colour coded cards representing the goods they have to trade. Each card has a picture and the name of the item in English, French, Cree, and Michif. Each group gets a checklist of the items they need to trade for and the groups begin negotiations to make trades. The first group to acquire all the items on their checklist wins.

The players are rolling marbles down a mountain to land on numbers. Players then multiply the number by a chosen number from a multiplication table. If the answer is correct, they place a chip on their bingo card. The first player to complete a row or column on their card wins.

10-Card Moose Evolution Simulator is a (mostly) scientifically accurate solitaire "probability" game so simple, a moose could probably play it. Each turn you must decide if you will keep your moose as is, or if you will seek to adapt it in order to survive an ever changing environment. Would it be useful to be a fast runner, swimmer or even flyer? Deadly antlers could turn you into the hunter. Mayhaps being build like a tank is the way to go instead. Will you grow a Moose population so large it breaks the simulation, or will you watch our deer species go extinct?

Vizzard is a strategy game for 3 to 6 players that simulates battles between powerful magical beings.
The main aim of the game is to be the last Vizzard on the battlefield.
The game map is created at the beginning of the game and it is never the same. Each player chooses his own Vizzard with unique powers,
places his pawn on the map and draws his first spells and the battle begins!

—description from the designer

The game consists of 32 shopping cards that are matched to 4 individual shopping lists.

The first player to complete their shopping list is the winner.

Spire of Dioronna is an amazing modular-designed light strategy game where players get to move around the map and make their way to the exit. Meanwhile, players need to tactfully utilize the map, use map functions to obtain resources and weaken your opponents, ultimately obtain the props they need to win the game!

In this thrilling game, you can collect props and escape with one mind. Alternatively, You can go empty-handed and use the room feature to repeatedly send your opponents back to the starting point. Communication and negotiation are very important in this game.

This game is suitable for 2 - 4 players and lasts about 20 to 40 minutes.  Players must place map cards, move around the map, complete quests, and compete with other players for resources.

—description from the publisher

Adventurer (Dungeons & Drifters) is a solitaire, story, dice game based in a fabled land.  It is the third game in The Drifter series after The Drifter and Star Drifter.

Adventurer is reminiscent of games like Barbarian Prince, The Drifter and Star Drifter, where every game is a unique story experience.

You are a fledgling adventurer.  Now far from home you have set out into this fabled land in hopes of accumulating enough wealth in order to fulfill your quest.  
The terrain of this mysterious realm is covered in dense forests, arid deserts, hazy marshes and rolling grasslands.  Throughout, there are all walks of life, from docile critters, predatory animals, peasants, patricians and ravenous creatures.  There are tales of perilous dungeons, secret tombs and ancient ruins, inhabited by mythological beasts and menacing magic that guard legendary riches.

Armed with your wits and weapon of choice you are ready to explore the highest mountains to the deepest valleys to achieve your goal.

—description from the publisher

Homestead Hyperjump! is an epic co-operative space adventure game set at the dawn of the 25th century. Players assume the role of Homesteaders, intrepid pioneers from across the Sol System whose unique backgrounds and experiences make them ideal candidates for service aboard an experimental Homesteader-class vessel, a cutting-edge starship commissioned by the United Interests of Sol to explore the vast, uncharted galaxy.

Equipped with newly discovered hyperjump technology, you and your crew will navigate anomalies and chart dozens of sectors across three distant star clusters. Along the way, players will encounter strange phenomena, face dangerous foes, and make critical decisions that could shape the fate of humanity.

Each session of Homestead Hyperjump! immerses players in a galaxy brimming with life, danger, and discovery. Your choices will define the journey as you make first contact with alien civilizations, navigate perilous anomalies, and uncover the mysteries of uncharted space. With more than thirty narrative-driven scenarios, sixty branching paths, and countless decisions to make, no two games will ever be the same.

Choose from six key roles — captain, engineer, tactician, doctor, scientist, or pilot — and build your crew from eighteen characters, each with unique abilities essential for survival. Together, you'll manage your ship's critical systems, balance resources, and overcome escalating threats like space madness and hostile forces.

The future of humanity is in your hands. Will your crew rise to the challenge and discover a habitable world to claim as your own — or die trying?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Might & Mettle: Rules for Tabletop Medieval Battles
description:
Simple to play, easy to learn, and quick to resolve—yet rich with tactical choices that keep every match engaging and historically inspired.

Play with:

     any scale of miniatures; 
     miniatures from any manufacturer; 
     hex-based or free formed; 
     a vast army or
     a small skirmish force.


Includes scenario notes to recreate historic battles at:

     Tours 
     Lechfeld 
     Manzikert 
     Stamford Bridge
     Hastings 
     Montgisard 
     Legnano 
     Hattin
     Arsuf 
     Bouvines 
     Courtrai 
     Bannockburn
     Sempach 
     Grunwald 
     Patay 
     Agincourt


—description from the publisher
family: History: Wars of the Guelphs and Ghibellines

description:
Monstrosity is a solo, dice-mitigation memory game for ages 12+ that plays in 10-20 minutes, using only 10 cards, 6 dice, 3 tokens, and 2 cubes.

Your goal is to scale the towering monstrosity and wreak havoc on its eyes, but along the way, it’ll be a struggle to not fall. You’ll need to be alert and dodge the acid spittle dripping from its mouth, hold tight as it tries to shake you off, and all while avoiding the numerous, scientifically altered alien organisms (aka Disturblings) climbing after you!

Monstrosity is part of A.R. Curry's Deadverse, a series of solo, standalone games within a shared universe.

—description from the designer

Monstrosity Expansion expands Monstrosity, the solo, dice-mitigation memory game with several added features:


     9 expansion cards
         1 new Head card
         2 Mutation cards
         2 Nest cards
         2 Joint cards
         1 Goat character card
         1 abilities card
    
     1 Goat character token
     4 cubes
     1 die

The Autumn is part of the 4 Seasons tile series with individual illustrations. You can use the tiles to expand the playing field or as a small mini-expansion.

Autumn is harvest time! That‘s why we pay special attention to the farms and stables in and around Carcassonne in autumn.

Components: 12 land tiles

Mix in the 12 new tiles with the base game tiles.
If you draw an Autumn tile, place it according to usual rules.  You can place a meeple on the autumn tile according to usual rules.  For farms and stable  that are vertically, horizontally or diagonally adjacent to the autumn tile you placed you immediatly receive 2 points per farm or stable.

There is 1 Statue tile in each of the 4 Seasons.  The following applies to all 4 statues. 
If your tile is vertically, horizontally or diagonally adjacent to the statue tile, you may place 2 meeples on this tile in two different areas.

Micro Disturblings is the micro version if Disturblings, the push-your-luck, tower-defense game for one player that’s full of bullets and colorful oddities.

You play as three-armed anti-hero, Bobby Butcher, as he’s handcuffed to an injured sheriff and struggling to fight off the disturbingly cute, lab-altered creatures scampering towards you.

As you scramble to reload the sheriff’s service weapon, you’ll need to strategically reallocate resources to build up your defenses and ensure you’re not overwhelmed.

A Fantastic Strategic Card Game about Broadway and Koalas. Win Auditions and fill your Stage!

An artistic card game that immerses players in the vibrant world of theater. Featuring Koala Actor Cards, Backstage Cards, and Audition Cards.

Players use their own deck in game play card (TCG style).

Koalas On Broadway has skill values for Dancing, Acting, and Singing, as well as a unique KEM (Koalas Entertainment Metric) score.

Players begin by “koalifying” their Actor Cards for auditions by meeting skill requirements. Once qualified, they compete against opponents to win auditions using their skills, supported by strategic Backstage cards that provide extra powers.

Winning an audition means placing the cards on your Stage and Backstage. The game ends when one player fills both their Stage and Backstage, but the winner is the player with the highest combined KEM score.

Showstopper Wild Cards add twists to the gameplay, potentially upending strategies. Some auditions will be worth winning, some you’ll want to avoid, and others you’ll be forced to win—making every decision a part of the drama!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Camping Season: Holiday Season Mini Expansion
description:
Decorate your campground with the Holiday Season Mini Expansion! This expansion will only be available on our Kickstarter campaign, and this fun pack adds 10 "Holiday" New Season Cards to the deck, as well as 24 Holiday Tokens to place around your campground!

—description from the publisher

With this mini expansion, your campground will have the opportuity to offer your guests to go on unique excursions!  Exursion cards will add Excursion tokens into your Guest Bag.  When pulled, they act as wild tokens, able to be placed in any site, simulating taking that family on an excursion.

Desolation is a solo/co-op exploration, dungeon crawling, map tile placement, skirmish boardgame set in the great World War Two zombie apocalypse.

In Desolation you will build a hardened crew of survivors that will progress in skill, acquire new equipment and customize weaponry from battle to battle. The game comes with its own field book where you record your crews’ skills, weapon loadouts and campaign progression.

Your play area is made up of 4-inch by 4-inch map tiles, randomly placed as you progress through each hellish mission. Unique tile placement and removal rules maximise the high-tension experienced in ferocious combat versus a ravenous and infectious zombie horde.

You are pitted against the award-winning Zombie Tango Oscar game AI which controls the swarming undead. Your zombie opponents include the ubiquitous Beta zombie along with a hierarchy of more dangerous foes such as the Kugelfisch, Armoured, Alpha, Alpha Prime and Omega zombies, each with their own unique powers.

There are multiple missions, each modified by environmental conditions and foe composition. These are combined to form numerous campaigns.  Each campaign tells a unique story with its own objectives and bespoke missions.

Whether you play as one-off battles or as a full campaign, Desolation delivers a nail biting, highly tactical solo board gaming experience with unlimited replayability.

—description from the designer

The carriage wheels creak over the bumpy old road - a difficult, dangerous path that runs through forests and swamps, outposts and plagued villages. A path haunted by greedy bandits and supernatural creatures. Your job it to escort it safely to its final destination.

Roads of Peril is a quick dungeon in card format co-produced by PocketLands and Ganesha Games. Each deck is a standalone thematic dungeon to explore including its own quest, unique monsters and magic treasures. Fully compatible with “Four Against Darkness” game. It is an official product for Four Against Darkness, and the core Four Against Darkness rulebook is required to play.

The deck contains:

17 tarot-size, full color cards
Instructions are included on the cards
2 unique monster cards
2 unique magic item cards

In Overgrown, players attempt to reclaim an abandoned city with new growth and wildlife, while competing for the resources needed to do so.

Inspired by tile-combination & match-3 games, Overgrown offers a fun twist on fighting the tide of tiles with clever placement and combination: each player determines the tiles their opponents receive from where they place their own tiles!

You and your friends are the forces of nature, attempting to transform polluted and abandoned cities into lush paradises brimming with wildlife. Each turn, players gather the elements needed to regrow flora and reintroduce fauna from a shared pool. Unfortunately, the elements are limited and mixed in with pollution so not every city will fully revive.

Unlike other tile-laying games, you control the drafting pool not only by what you take, but also how you use it, giving you plenty to think about as your city slowly recovers. At the end of the game, the player with the most verdant city is declared the winner.

—description from the designer

A separate pack of three promos were sent along with the Kickstarter version of Motto Tanto Cuore.

All three are extra-large High Class Maid cards.

MP01 - Nena Wilder
MP02 - Claire Saint-Juste
MP03 - Noëmi Santorini (Alternate art)

Gastro Dash is a competitive turn-based race to the bottom (er, toilet). 2-6 players play survival cards to alter the road ahead and sabotage everyone else on the road. Players can push their luck by trusting a fart to conserve (or when they're out of) their survival cards.

—description from the designer

A cooperative game set in the Klondike, where engineers are staking claims to gold deposits and then building mines and arranging railroads to get the precious resource to a smelter where it can be refined for sale. The goal is to find, transport and process as much gold as possible.

Bored of small talk? Or ever feel like socialising is hard work?

Introducing Popularity, the party game where social influence is everything and reputations are built or broken with the flip of a card!

Inspired by Truth or Dare, Uno, Snakes & Ladders, and a dash of Cards Against Humanity, our new game Popularity is all about making socialising fun again. 
Compete or co-operate to climb the social ladder through sheer force of personality!

Will you choose to boost your own reputation with favours and friendships or trash your rivals with grudges and gossip?

Will the frontrunner betray their allies to become the last one standing… or perhaps the group outcast will leap to victory at the last second?

There are plenty of paths to victory… but be careful as, whatever your strategy, Twists of Fate can turn winners into losers at any moment!

—description from the publisher

A easy playable game where you observe air balloons and collect points through rounds.

Each air balloon has fractions of colors. It's up to each play to complete the fraction of colors and gain points on their points.

First to a given point wins the game.

—description from the publisher

You can’t get enough of the little Fluffies, but you can’t find a game round right now? Or you just want to play a quick round for yourself?

With these rules, you can also play Time Trouble in solo mode! Just download the PDF and get started!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Debutante's Ball at Dramaton Hall
description:
Meet Esther Villiers, daughter of Lord Villiers, third Duke of Beaumaris. As the date of her Debutante's Ball approaches, the eligible and the great of Britain travel to Dramaton Hall eager to attend the biggest social event of the year and forge new connections.

Manipulate a grid of cards to strengthen your recollection. The more cards you score, the easier bidding for memories will be. Bid against the deck to remember the events of the Debutante’s Ball, and then forge connections between the nobles and commoners at Dramaton Hall, aiming to meet plot points to create the most compelling story.

—description from the designer

Binge-watch energy meets trivia night in
How I Met Your Series!

This TV-inspired party game is packed with hilarious prompts and tricky questions that test your knowledge of all the shows you love to quote, rewatch, and obsess over.

Compact and travel-friendly, it’s perfect for sleepovers, parties, or game nights with friends. Ages 14+.

The Germans have invaded Norway. The royal family must escape across the rugged countryside, evading capture at every turn. Will the king defy German demands for surrender, or will he find a way to escape, inspiring hope for Norway's freedom?

Crown & Courage is a solitaire-only game,  bringing clever, low-randomness eurogame-inspired mechanisms together with a fresh, new perspective provided by the best wargamer traditions of illustrating history's unique challenges through evocative gameplay.

—description from the designer

Roll 10 six-sided dice to construct your pyramid and score the most points. Plays similar to Yahtzee and Farkle.

—description from the designer

Sever is a territorial game of pure placement for two players – Black and White – played on an initially empty hexhex board.


     Starting with Black, the players take turns placing a stone of their color on an empty cell. 
     When the board is full, the player with the higher score wins. Ties are not possible.
     Each stone is worth 1 point for its owner or their opponent depending on its adacency count. The adjacency count of a group is the number of groups adjacent to it. A group scores for the opponent of its owner if it is adjacent to a group with a higher adjacently count, otherwise it scores for its owner. 
     Experienced players can use the pie rule to balance the game: One player places a black stone anywhere on the board, and the other player decides whether to play as Black or as White.


—description from the designer

Smacked Down is a 2-player, miniature agnostic, tabletop skirmish game set inside the world of independent professional wrestling.

The game relies on a d6 system and is meant for fast character creation, fast rules, and fast game play. Players are guided through Smacked Down's robust character creation suite utilizing tables to generate looks, name, and moveset so players can jump into the action as soon as possible.

Ideal for small or cramped living arrangements, all that is necessary to play Smacked Down is a 12" x 12" grid, two 28mm scale models, and 6d6. Smacked Down is the ideal game for a in between longer games as a pallet cleanser or it can be stretched out with multiple games to simulate running your own wrestling show!

Included in the core game are 3 scenarios for play - Standard Match, Submission Match, and Last Man Standing Match. This gives players a chance to switch up game play with quick, digestible rules.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A custodial capture game with sliding dice inspired by Hnefatafl.
Surround your opponent's die with your dice to weaken it.
Dice slide the number of hexes equal to the pip count on top. When the sum of your dice surrounding an opponent's die is higher, the opponent's die is weakened by 1.
The game ends when one player cannot capture the opponent's strongest die.

—description from the designer

With the Goliath Shit Happens Family Edition you are assured of an evening full of hilarity and fun with the whole family. This game challenges you to rank life's events in order of misery - from a flat tire to a broken relationship. It's up to you to decide which is the worst and score points. The cards have funny illustrations and provide the necessary discussions at the table. The Family Edition is suitable for young and old and guarantees a fun gaming evening. Show how well you can assess the misery in life and beat your relatives in this hilarious game.

A ten-card expansion that adds new events, treasures, enemies, friends, magic items and spells, and allows for a fifth and sixth player.

Content: 1 new event, 1 new treasure, 3 new baddies, 1 new friend, 3 new items, and 1 new magic spell.

A one-on-one card game where designers are placed using color combinations to build five-tiered pyramid tableau.

The winner is the one who can accumulate the most CP (Creative Points) by using the designer's abilities to create combos and get in the way of their opponent.

You can discard designers you don't want and draft those you need in the "break room" row.

If you rush, the quality will drop, but if you seek perfection, it will take time.

—description from the publisher (translated)
This fourth expansion contains 120 new cards and add three new type of units: Djinns, Dwarves and Giants. It also provides support for Witches.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Welcome to the game where it’s all about likes and followers! You’ve just quit your job to fully commit to becoming an influencer. Your confidence is sky-high—you’ve got 200,000 followers and just landed your first two brand deals. With 100K in your pocket, the goal is clear: to become Sweden’s most powerful influencer! But can you survive in a world where fame is a commodity, and every move is as calculated as it is anxiety-ridden? Can you outshine your frenemies without losing your authenticity? Challenge yourself and your friends in this sharp, satirical board game and find out who truly has what it takes to dominate the influencer world!

—description from the publisher (translated)

You embark on a journey to the country where you were born - the country of your parents. You have never seen them again since they left you as a toddler with your grandparents in a distant land. Your grandparents never told you why. Now you set off for Vigoria to find your parents and finally lift the veil of your past. But you realize that this land is very different. Much more dangerous! Animals and plants are much bigger and more aggressive here! You have no choice: if you don't want to end your longing search for your parents in Vigoria prematurely, then you need animals like these to fight your way through. You need Vigormon!

In Vigormon, 1 to 2 players tame unusually large and aggressive animals and plants - namely Vigormon! All Vigormon are based on real animals and plants, which have impressive characteristics and fascinating behavior. Things you never knew about these species before! Your Vigormon have attack moves and abilities that are based on a real-life background. You experience a story-driven campaign. Your decisions have a clear influence on the story. The open world allows you to tackle the challenges you want to face first.

To do so, you need to put together a perfectly attuned team. However, this is not that easy, as there are no Vigormon that are clearly better or worse than others. Rather, it is important that you cover as many creature types as possible with your team, and that they have one of six common archetypes and synergizing roles to gain additional bonuses, and much more to strategize! With so many options and so many different Vigormon, you can discover a huge range of different strategies.

In quick battles lasting around 15 minutes, you will then face off against opponents who stand in the way of your search for your parents in Vigoria. You'll soon realize that Vigoria needs you much more than you expected. And if you don't have a whole evening to spare, then the flexible game duration will suit you just fine! You can pause the game at any time outside of a battle. And long set-up and tear-down times won't get in the way either. By storing all your Vigormon cards, tokens and other cards from your team in trays with lids, you can get everything out and put away very quickly!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Keep the Heroes Out!: Playing with Fire
description:
Emberlings are little firebrand monsters, eager to turn your dungeon into a smoldering ruin. Playing with fire sounds fun, but with these hot-headed pyromaniacs you'll need to plan ahead - or you'll be calling your dungeon insurance company real soon! Beware! Hugging an Emberling is a guaranteed way to get burned! Additionally, since fire is treated as a resource, warriors are tough enough to extinguish two Fire meeples upon their arrival.

After all the Emberlings have played their tactic cards, but before the heroes' phase, you must resolve the following for each Fire in a room (including those with an Emberling):

     Deal 1 Damage to a hero
     Discard a resource
     Deal 1 damage to a monster


If a room has no more flammable objects, the fire burns out and you must remove the Fire or Emberling meeple from the dungeon and return it to your supply.

The Playing with Fire! Expansion includes 3 promo-packs: The Quijote pack, the Year of The Dragon Pack and the Very Jolly Pack.

Interstellar Extraction Corporation just got a new mining permit for a sector that is rich in asteroids with a generous amount of metals and other rare minerals, but there is a problem. That sector is swarming with aliens that will tear every ship to shreds if they find it too close to their nests. If we want to get to the valuable materials we cannot wake them up.

You’re one of many driller operators in Interstellar Extraction Corporation. You are tasked with excavating as many minerals as possible without alerting the Swarm. Will you accomplish your task and come back rich or will you have to leave everything behind, running from the aliens.

In Space Driller, create patterns to drill through the asteroid, collect materials, explore the asteroid mines and avoid the alien eggs.

boardgame: Path of Civilization: Alcatraz Promo Card
description:
Promo card given for free at conventions.

Alcatraz is a blue Level I Wonder

Card number is GW-49

When you acquire Alcatraz you get 2 sages. The card ain't worth any VP.

Up to 6 players compete against each other with a billy goat standing in front of them to flick it as close as possible to the rock crystal in the centre, but without letting the wobbly playing piece fall over. This is made difficult by the fact that you can only flick the billy goat against its rear end, but it likes to turn when you flick it.

The winner is whoever brings their billy goat to the crystal standing up first.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Box of Kittens Promo Card
description:
Promo card for Extra life 2024

Text on card:
Box of Kittens
Prize
When you win this Prize, add it to the Prize Bin, then flip a coin:
Heads: "So soft!" +1 fortitude
Tails: "So sharp!" -1 fortitude

Meet the Marauders, the most fanatical warriors of the Moonrakers. Known for their relentless aggression and unmatched firepower, they embody shoot-first, ask-questions-never. Diplomacy? Sutlety? Forget it. These specialists turn every skirmish into chaos, breaching defenses and overpowering foes. Prepare to dominate the battlefield with their devastating prowess. Unleash the fury of the Marauders in your next Moonrakers adventure.

Introducing a brand new Moonrakers micro-expansion: the Marauders.

Includes 5 new Crew, 5 Contracts, and 5 Ship Parts, along with card sleeves.

—description from the publisher

As builders of the realm, your job is to team up and put together a village for the sprouting fae population. Each turn you'll chose a tile from your hand and name one resource that's NOT on the tile for you partner to place face down. Then flip up the tile and rotate to try to match and string together and produce the resources you’ll need across the land to build all the buildings that make a village work. You may build one building from the market by spending the resources required after each placement. Get them all built in time for the harvest and you win!

—description from the designer

boardgame: RPage!: The Lair of the Adorned Worm
description:
RPage!: The Lair of the Adorned Worm is the first adventure introducing this micro system highly inspired by good old RPG games.

Just print one single sheet of paper, front and back, fold it like a pamphlet in three parts, and grab a few components, then you are ready for Rpage! an entire dungeon crawler game with old-school feels played entirely in one single paper pamphlet! Venture deep into the dungeon, reach the end,
return and emerge alive, and preferably richer. Get a highscore and a high Rank. Play again if you want to get a higher Rank!

The Lair of the Adorned Worm is the first adventure introducing this micro system highly inspired by good old RPG games.

To play you will need the pamphlet, a miniature or pawn (to be the adventurer on the map), 3 six-sided dice, 2 markers or cubes and a pencil and eraser.

d5escription from the designer

The world of the outlaws was not all fun and games - what's more! The outlaws gathered in the tavern and often compared their strengths with each other. Which outlaw would win this time?

The aim of the game: Players play as a well-known outlaw, a real-life character from the 19th century in Hungary, who is now pitted against each other to earn Virtus Points (VP). The aim of the game is simple, the player with the most VP at the end wins the game.

—description from the publisher (translated)

A betyárok világa nem csak móka és kacagás volt, sőt! A csárdában összegyűlő betyárok sűrűn összemérték erejüket egymással. Vajon most melyik betyár fog győzni?

A játék célja:
A játékosok egy-egy jól ismert betyárt alakítanak, akik a XIX. század valós karakterei voltak Magyarországon, és most egymással mérik össze erejüket, hogy Virtus Pontokat (VP) szerezzenek. A játék célja egyszerű, az nyeri a játékot, aki a végén a legtöbb VP-vel rendelkezik.

—description from the publisher

Goal
A tactial boardgame to become the new king of Eldoria. Win 3 trials and you can take the Crown.

Game-play trial 1
All the nobles get a Quest Card in front of them at the start. They also receive 4 random cards as there are: Red Crown, Green Crown, Red Owl and Green Owl. Lay those 4 cards on your quest card in a square.
The first trial is played without any Joker or disrupter cards.
Each player gets 3 other cards for holding in the hand. Each player puts one of these cards on the game board and takes a new card from the general deck of cards.
Each player is trying to get the same layout of his quest cards on the board. You can also win as you competitor is doing it for you.

The game-board has 5x5 squares. As soon is the row horizontal, vertical or diagonal is filed with 5 cards, all the 5 cards are removed from the board and place underneath the general deck of cards.

Game-play trial 2 
Same as trail 1 but now in the general deck of cards are added Jokers which represent bot of the colors of a Crown card or a Owl card.

The 5 on a row is not validate anymore.

And the disruptor cards: Scepter Horizontal, Scepter Vertical, Scepter Diagonal and the Golden Explosion, removes the cards on the Gameboard in their specific way.

Game-play trial 3
Same as trial 2, but no is also back the 5 on a row rule.

End game
As soon as a player has his quest found on the Game board he wins a trial. When you won 3 trials you will be the new king of Eldoria.

Variations
If you want to play short rounds and learn the game play trial 1
If you are more of an expert and has more time, trial 3 will expect more of your patience and tactical skills.

SIXEM Sour Patch Kids is the perfect play on the mischievous and well-loved candy brand!  It’s fast-paced and full of sour and sweet moments sure to bring laughs and fun to your game night table.  Players must roll fast, trying to achieve a run of 1 through six on their dice. Then players mark Xs on their boards corresponding to the colors and numbers of their dice.  The first player to have six Xs in a row on their board wins!  But there’s a twist:  If you roll an X already on your board, you must either be sour and erase that X from another player’s board or be sweet and mark that X on an opponent’s board.  It’s quick, strategic, and full of fun!

All is groovy on Disco Island where sifaka lemurs dance to the music made by Disco Ball Mountain, until one day the mountain turns out to be a volcano. Disco Inferno!

In this turn-based game, match the symbols on your cards to those on the board in order to cross lava streams. Land on another lemur to send them back to the start and flip your lemur to its GROOVY side, allowing it to move twice as quickly across the lava. Draw the Boogie to Safety card to escape quickly offshore!

The first player to race 3 of their lemurs to safety offshore wins!

Players choose a Cooking Challenge, which consists of 2 related recipes (e.g. breakfast, burger night). Each team includes a head chef (adult) and at least 2 sous chefs (child). Each recipe includes an Ingredients card, 3 Mystery Ingredients cards, 3 Mad Skills cards, a number of recipe cards, 3 Game Change cards, and a Judge’s scorecard. Place these cards on the corresponding spaces on the board. Teams then proceed around the board resolving cards. Mystery Ingredients cards are blindfold taste/smell tests. Mad Skills req performing a cooking task (e.g. chop a pickle slice into 4 even pieces), answering a question, etc. When a team reaches the Recipe space, they follow the instructions on the recipe card, actually making the recipe with real ingredients. 3 steps will require team to draw a Game Changer card, which adds an additional challenge. When a team reaches the Judge’s Scorecard, they judge their own recipe using the criteria on the card.

Players take turns placing a cloth boneless chicken on a plastic chair then slamming the launcher to launch the chicken onto the fence. Score 3 if the chicken is hanging over the top rail, 2 if it it lands on one of the lower side rails, and 1 it it lands on the lower middle rail.

Game includes a deck of cards, each with the picture and name of an item. Some of them (e.g. bread) are mentioned in the nursery rhyme Old Mother Hubbard and some (e.g. cabbage) are not. In the first part of the game, the reader reads the Old Mother Hubbard nursery rhyme aloud. If a player has one of the items mentioned, they discard it. The first player to discard all their cards wins. If no one has done so by the time the reader finishes the rhyme, proceed to part 2. The reader now reads the next part of the text, pausing every time they reach a blank. The next player in turn fills in that blank by naming and discarding one of their cards. For example, the first line in part 2 is "Oh my poor little dog/It grieves me to tell/What wondrous adventures/That doggy befell/One day he was sick/He had swallowed ____". The next player might complete the line with "a poodle" and discard that card.

"Traveler! It's been so long. You've seen so much and found so much more. Why don't you join me again for another quick game?"

(Genshin Tarot is required to use this expansion)

Genshin Tarot Volume 2 brings us back to Teyvat for another 60 to 90 minute solo or co-op deck upgrading board game.

Each player creates a party of 4 Genshin Characters, dealt randomly from a pool of 22 playable characters (with 14 more added with the first expansion.) Each character brings several cards unique to them to your deck, forming a totally distinct deck for each player.

Each round is split into player and enemy phase. Play happens simultaneously during the player phase, where each player will use the cards in their hands to take down the waves of enemies in front of them or offer help to allies. On the enemy phase, surviving monsters will attack players, and enemy ranks will be replenished.

Once any one player defeats all of the enemies in front of them, they trigger the arrival of the boss. The players win if the boss is defeated, but lose if any player ever hits 0 HP.

Will you and your friends take command of your own fate? Or falter in the face of Teyvat's challenges.


Genshin Tarot Volume 2 is an expansion for Genshin Tarot set to go on preorder on November 30th and for distribution during November 2025. Alongside this comes The Cups Minor Arcana Expansion which adds an additional 14 playable characters.

Follow on twitter to see the latest updates!

The Cups Arcana Expansion is the third expansion for Genshin Tarot, the Fan Made Genshin Impact Board Game.

Add 14 additional playable characters to the base game, each represented by an authentic cups suit minor arcana tarot card and illustrated by a different renowned artist.

Bring Wriothesley home, along with Kamisato Ayaka, Gaming, Heizou, Kirara, Sayu, Xianyun, Layla, Furina, Wanderer, Lynette, Navia, Nilou, and Shenhe!

Taking Space is a cozy 54-card pattern-laying deckbuilder for 1 player. In Taking Space, you’ll be charting out patterns in the night sky by drawing cards one at a time from your deck, placing them in ways that fulfil a variety of scoring conditions. All cards have a time cost, and once you’ve reached or exceed your time limit, the round ends. The higher you score, the more rounds you’ll have before Daybreak. Between rounds, you’ll upgrade your Play Deck by choosing cards to add that uniquely twist the strategies and pattern laying, from new scoring conditions to rule-breaking effects. When Dawn finally comes, you get one final round with double the time limit to create the ultimate final starchart, which determines your Final score! At the end of the game, you’ll earn trophies that will in turn make future games a little more tricky, and even permanently unlock new stargazing items that grant you very special abilities.

—description from the designer

Waging Peace invites board game players to exercise strategy, experience cooperation, and practice diplomacy in the context of international geopolitical conflict. Players navigate the global sphere of government and law, as well as nuclear energy and rocket science, determining what is ethical and just in their pursuit of peace. The objective of the game is to obtain any one of 3 "Peace Checks" Resource Peace Check, Power Peace Check and Strategic Peace Check. With sufficient resources , the winner influences other players to live in peaceful competition rather than embroiled in conflict. Each player has the choice to place a marker at the Peace Space or Justice Space to begin. From either space players roll the dice and proceed along the playing area of the board claiming Resource Sectors, Nuclear Forces Spaces and Outer- Space Units. Each player may do business and conduct diplomatic relations with the players according to the rules and procedures of the game.
The player who acts as a peacemaker not only demonstrates tact and patience, but also effective negotiating skills in order to form alliances. The first player to obtain one of three Peace Check wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Sauna Masters: They Just Don't Get It
description: Two-player battle card game where each player becomes the “master” of a shared sauna and goes head-to-head with their opponent for ultimate control. Players play cards to adjust the temperature, tweak the humidity, and aim for ultimate relaxation before your rival does. All the cards, board, and pieces are waterproof to facilitate play in an actual sauna.

The human race is on the verge of extinction. 
Survivors of the virus must band together to defeat the Zombies and save our planet. 
Not all Survivors have the same goal in mind, though. 
Some are after world domination and will stop at nothing to take you down.

Trust no one.

—description from the publisher

Who throw a tantrum more often?
Can he apologize?
Who is more argumentative in your relationship?
And you? Are you always blameless?

Ciche Dni ("Silent Days") is a game for a couple or even up to four couples at the same time, in which you can write what you are usually afraid to say.

Players take turns answering questions about knowing their partner. In this time other players have the opportunity to bet on whether the question will be answered correctly. Correct answer allows to move forward by on board. The winner is the pair which one will first arrives on final field of board.

Kto częściej strzela fochy?
Czy on potrafi przepraszać?
Kto w Waszym związku jest bardziej kłótliwy?
A Ty? Czy Ty zawsze jesteś bez winy?

CICHE DNI to gra dla pary lub nawet czterech par jednocześnie, w której możecie napisać to, czego zwykle boicie się powiedzieć.

It's family Sunday! Your grandma promised to bake a delicious pie for you and your cousins, The Wondernauts! But she needs fresh berries, so she turns to you for help. 

Excited by the challenge, you grab your hang gliders and head out on an adventure through the skies. Your bird friends will be there too! You’ll soar over lush landscapes, searching for the sweetest berries to earn an extra slice of pie!

But hurry! Dark clouds are gathering on the horizon. A storm is coming! You have to return before it hits, or risk losing all your berries!

—description from the designer

Power Bulletin! is a short card game designed to be played with 2 to 4 players for 10 to 15 minutes. The game's goal is to eliminate opponents by removing cards from their hands or by having the most powerful hand once the deck runs out. Do YOU have the Power?!

boardgame: Quest for El Dorado: Expansion Collection
description:
Quest for El Dorado - Expansion Collection combines all previous add-ons and promo cards for the Quest for El Dorado in a convenient package.

It includes Heroes & Hexes, Dangers & Muisca and Dragons, Treasures & Mysteries add-ons, as well as promotional packs "Binoculars", "Telegram", and "Letter of Recommendation".
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Poesía a la Carta: León Gieco
description:
A poetic encounter with the work of the beloved Argentine singer-songwriter León Gieco. Verses and stanzas that form part of his popular songbook.

Three forms of play: "poetry on demand", "poetic oracle" and "playing the game". Think of a question and poetry will answer you. The game brings together a selection of poems by León Gieco, verses and stanzas that are part of his popular songbook. It is the fourth title in the Poesía a la Carta series and the first made with song lyrics. Because poetry and music go hand in hand.

boardgame: A Most Resolute Action: The Battle of Fort Meigs, May 5, 1813
description:
The War of 1812 brought conflict once again to the state of Ohio and the Northwest Territory. The War of 1812 began badly for the US. In August, a two-thousand-man US army in Detroit was surrendered to the British and their First Nation allies by General William Hull without a shot being fired. General Harrison’s army, that was meant to reinforce and relieve General Hull, was forced to retreat back into Ohio following another defeat south of Detroit at the battle of River Raisin in January. Reforming south and east of Toledo, Harrison constructed a fort along the Maumee River, near present day Perrysburg, Ohio in February 1813.

In the Spring the British, Canadian and First Nation forces under the command of British General Henry Proctor advanced from their positions in Michigan and laid siege to Fort Meigs on May 1st. On May 5th General Harrison ordered two sorties by the US garrison to destroy British siege guns that were east and west of the river.

Can you do as well, or better? Learn and enjoy!

—description from the designer
family: Series: Battles of the Old Northwest (High Flying Dice Games)

description:
Like a game of pictionary with pretend poo, Skid Markz is a wild and wacky game where you draw with a dog's doo-doo to craft clues for your friends.

Draw a bone card to get the secret word; next, roll the die to see what extra challenge will be added (like drawing with your eyes closed or using only straight lines). Then take up Scoot the Dog by his leash and try to draw your secret word by guiding his butt all over the dry-erase board before time runs out. The player with the most correct guesses is a Skid Markz champion!

—description from the publisher
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

boardgame: Mine Turtle: Uncensored – The Naughty Forty
description: A NSFW 40 card expansion that adds adult only missions to your Mine Turtle deck.
family: Versions & Editions: Adult Versions of Family-Friendly Games

boardgame: Mine Turtle: Reloaded – The Bombastic Booster
description: 40 extra missions adding more probing questions and tricky challenges to your game time.

boardgame: Chris Kemp's Not Quite Mechanised: Tabletop Operational Wargaming 1914-1945
description:
All the information that you need to start playing operational level wargames on the tabletop with miniatures is here. 
Suitable for two players or many. 
Games can be played at four scales of resolution: Regimental Scale Order of battle(RSO),Divisional(DSO), Corps(CSO)and Front or Army(FSO). 
Games can last a couple of hours or a day to suit your preference.

The book contains comprehensive rules and orders of battle for three World War Two armies, British, German and Soviet.

An example of a game turn is included to show how the rules work and a quick reference sheet with copies of the combat table are included for personal use.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Blossoms of Blood: A game of immortal conflicts in the Desired Land
description:
When Divine order is lost, mortal chaos reigns
In a land shaped by the August Will, Humans and Sainted Beasts once lived in harmony. Then the moon was lost and the sky ran red as blood. 
Thereafter, the Demons came.

Blossoms of Blood is a competitive miniatures game set in a world inspired by East Asian mythology and tradition.

Players assume the role of warlords, leading bands of loyal fighters in a struggle for the power of the Divine Blossoms. Wise animal spirits, ferocious demons, and warriors both devoted and depraved are theirs to command.

This document includes everything needed to start playing Blossoms of Blood right away, including the core rules, scenarios, and three complete faction lists from which to raise a warband. 
Using an innovative system that employs playing cards instead of traditional dice, players battle in a game of triumph or ruin.

—description from the publisher

Alice has fallen into Wonderland once again, but this time everything is different. She is locked in a magic tower by the evil Queen. The only way out for Alice is to figure out the correct combination of three magic locks. Are you ready to embark on a Wonderland Words adventure?

This family cooperative game blends captivating gameplay and amazing abstract artwork. Each adventure fosters creative communication, out-of-the-box thinking, and teamwork. Wonderland Words is more than a game - it's an enchanting experience!

—description from the publisher

Sport game of Stage cycling race.
All cyclists registered in a real stage race (e.g. Giro, Tour, Vuelta) participate in this game. Paper miniatures are included. The entire race is made up of stages divided into sectors approximately 10 to 20 km long each. Each individual stage is managed through the use of Fast Action Cards. During the stage, miniatures and a board are available, so it is possible to show the gap cyclists from the main group at any time. Specific cards and tables are provided in the cases of falls, sprints, extreme weather and time trials.

The game is a free PNP

—description from the designer

Each round in Labyrinth Go!, the four labyrinth dice reveal which treasures must be collected. Using your own path tiles, you try to connect the treasures shown on the dice with the center of your game board. Whoever does this most quickly earns the most points.
family: Components: Game Box Used In Play
Versions & Editions: Dice Versions of Non-Dice Games
Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

boardgame: Flieg Schweinchen, flieg!: Lass sie fliegen
description: A kid's game where you bounce pigs of a trampoline, trying to hit a hole in the middle.

A kids' game in which you try to catch bouncing rabbits with a net. The game is battery-powered and features a rotating playing field with nine rabbits that get launched in random order. Players need to catch the rabbits with their nets in mid-flight. Rabbits that land without being caught are placed back in the playing field for the next round. The game is over once all nine rabbits have been caught, and the player with the most caught rabbits wins.

In a slightly advanced variant of this game, different colored rabbits are assigned 1, 2, or 3 points, and the players sum up their score over 5 rounds.
A kids' game where you navigate a spinning top around a maze on a board by holding the maze in your hands and skillfully tilting it.

A game for kids where you build funny faces by rolling dice and filling the slots on your board with matching pieces. Players take turns rolling the dice and then try to fill in one of the six slots of their board with one of the available pieces that match their roll result. Whoever can fill all six slots of their character first wins.

A re-implementation of the game Tumblin' Monkeys using the Minions from Despicable Me instead of monkeys, and a rocket instead of a palm tree. Players drop minions through the top, trying to get them to catch on to the colored sticks inside the rocket, while subsequentially removing a colored stick of a random color, trying to avoid dropping any minions while doing so. A player has to keep any minions that drop all the way out of the bottom of the rocket during their turn. At the end of the game, the player who dropped the fewest minions during their turn wins.

Three people take part in the game. Each player takes on a weapon type (submarine, bomber, fighter plane) in the form of a spinner and receives the corresponding 16 game pieces, which are used to show his successful hits by covering the ship and plane symbols of the attacking fleet on the board.
The game is played in the order of submarine, bomber, fighter plane, from the outer 6th to the inner 1st zone. First, all enemies in the 6th zone must be destroyed, then in the 5th, 4th and the remaining zones. Only when an individual weapon type no longer finds any objects to destroy in a zone can it move on to the next zone. The submarine can only destroy ships, the fighter pilot can only destroy aircraft, but the bomber can destroy both aircraft and ships.
To overcome the opponents, it is necessary to spin exactly a 6 in the 6th zone, exactly a 5 in the 5th zone and so on. If the required number is not reached with one rotation, the player can rotate from the 6th to the 3rd zone a second time. This also applies if the spinner stops on a red or blue space.
If the sum of these two rotations then results in the required zone number, then success is achieved.
If a red or blue space is reached again on the second rotation, the attack fails.
To destroy ships in harbors and aircraft over land, two rotations are always necessary, and one rotation must result in the zone number, but the other must show a red space.
The player who uses up his playing pieces first is the winner.

—user summary

In KeyForge: Prophetic Visions you will use foresight and scrying to foil your opponents. Why? For more Æmber, of course!

KeyForge: Prophetic Visions is the ninth KeyForge set featuring the same great game system with tons of new cards and new game mechanics. It features these exciting new elements:


     Prophecy cards, an entirely new card type
     Fate effects
     200 new cards for the Dis, Logos, Sanctum, Saurian, Shadows, Star Alliance, and Untamed Houses


Beware, the rebellious House Redemption has been spotted usurping House Sanctum in select packs!

—description from the publisher

The official Answer The Internet Card Game from KFC and Feitelberg from their podcast - KFC Radio - is finally here. We took 500 of the best questions from the KFC Radio Podcast and the first season of Answer The Internet on YouTube, and we put 'em in a box. Three different types of questions - all featuring the best hypotheticals, Would You Rather, Marry Fuck Kill, and classic thought provoking questions from the Internet. Box includes 500 cards: - 200 "Answer The Internet" cards- 150 "Poll the Internet" cards- 150 "Debate the Internet" cards The game comes complete with the questions and rules for gameplay so you can play with friends and crown a winner. You can also just grab a stack and use them as a conversation starter. No matter how you choose to play, formally or informally, ATI is going to be the must have item this fall/winter/holiday season for your house party, pregame, bachelor/bachelorette weekend, game night, or anywhere you're hanging with your friends.

In Live and Let Pie players each have their own kitchen board where they bake pies.  They gain ingredient tiles from the truck on the main board.  They sell pies in the bakery of the main board. The player who best meets market demand will make the most money!
Choose the crates of fruit you will take to your kitchen and turn into delicious pies! As you make and sell more pies you will also be able to upgrade your kitchen.  This will allow you to bake more pies, store more crates and win the contest to become the best pie maker in town!
Game Mechanics: Tile drafting, engine building.

—description from the designer

Joint All Domain Operation (JADO) is a Chinese wargame. From the box: The global battlefield manual wargame system focuses on global confrontation operations and multi-service campaigns in the sea, land, air, space and power grid under high-tech conditions in modern warfare. As a general wargame system, it can support the armed forces of various countries in the 1990's to the near future to conduct theater-level scale simulation confrontations in various battlefield environments around the world.

The "Sea and Air Core Kit 01-A" and the "Land Core Kit 01-B" are a basic kits that can support commanders of both sides to directly conduct simulated confrontations, including a set of sea, air and land combat maps, corresponding unit counters, rule books and other necessary accessories.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Age of Colors is a civilization-building card game inspired by color theory. Players manage colored cards representing different aspects of their empire—such as economy, military, and culture—and strategically combine them to purchase powerful cards, complete events, and gain victory points. Each turn, players use cards to either increase their skills or fulfill card requirements to acquire new abilities and bonuses.

The game involves hand management, resource optimization, and set collection, where players aim to maximize the potential of their cards and outmaneuver opponents. The goal is to accumulate the most victory points by the end of the game through wise decisions, strategic purchases, and completing various in-game objectives.

—description from the designer

CREATE 3 HILARIOUSLY UNHINGED SCENARIOS: Flip 3 Action cards and 3 Object cards. Then everybody secretly pairs up each Action and Object card to choose who (or what) they’d fork, milk, kidnap, and more.

MAKE QUESTIONABLE CHOICES: Would you fork Abe Lincoln. Start a cult with the player to your left. Kidnap a kinky mime. Take turns revealing all 3 of your pairings and explaining what the fork you were thinking.

THERE IS NO JUDGE. YOU WILL BE JUDGED, THOUGH: No judge, no voting. In this game, majority wins. Will your friends make the same forked-up choices as you. If so, you all score 1 point. Score 5 points first to win.

ADULTS-ONLY, NSFW PARTY GAME: This not safe for work game is a shamelessly raunchy party game for game nights to remember. It’s easy to learn and quick to play—jump right into a game.

—description from publisher

This trivia game consists of cards for all 194 countries of the world. Each card contains on the foreground the following information: 2) name, 3) area, 4) language, 5) inhabitants, 6) capital, 7) biggest town, 8) currency, 9) highest mountain, 10) number of neighbour countries, 11) GDP and 12) continent. On the background the flag and shape of the countries can be found.

For a two-person game the rules are as follows: Every player gets an equal number of cards in front of him with the back of each card visible. Now two dice are rolled and the result can be odd or even:

In the odd case numeric facts are checked. Each player selects an own card and compares the corresponding category of the dice sum on their cards. The person with the higher number (3: area, 5 inhabitants, …) wins all these cards and puts them on a separate score pile.

In the even case each person selects a card from the opponent and the players have to give an answer to the corresponding category (2: country name, 4:  language, …). If only one gives a proper answer this person gets both cards on his score pile and all cards from the jackpot pile. Otherwise the cards are added to the neutral jackpot pile.

Winner is the person who has the most cards in his score pile.
It is a Monopoly but with places from city Białystok, Poland.

Air Force Wargame Indo-Pacific is simple educational wargame framed around a multi-domain air centric fight in INDOPACOM. It is intended to be an introduction to Air Force Wargaming, demonstrating the basic tenets of airpower and multi-domain combat. It requires players to use strategic thinking and decision-making skills to achieve campaign objectives though the use of aircraft squadrons and a bevy of multi-domain enablers such as cyber, space, long range fires, maritime, SOF, and other assets.

The game is intended for 2-8 players (on two teams) and is designed for rapid play so that players can execute multiple Air Tasking Orders (ATOs) in a single sitting. It is a d4 based system with a simple range map and off map boxes for standoff strikes and air bases. Players each turn choose postures and select squadrons and enablers to use the fight before playing out the turn. Asymmetric hidden missions of each side dictate the scoring of victory points for different objectives, and at the end of the game the player with the most victory points wins.

boardgame: Die Schule der magischen Tiere: Auf die Plätze!
description:
A fast-paced reaction game set in the world of "Die Schule der magischen Tiere". An exciting race featuring characters from the popular book series. For 2 to 4 players, ages 7 and up.
A great birthday gift – not just for fans of "Die Schule der magischen Tiere". Board game with game board, playing cards, and point tokens. The board game inspired by the beloved book series "Die Schule der magischen Tiere" by Margit Auer, with illustrations by Nina Dulleck.

boardgame: ASL: March Madness 2022 The Grumble Jones Pack
description:
A scenario pack for Advanced Squad Leader.

A collection of 8 scenarios designed by K. Scott (Grumble Jones) Mullins.  The Kansas City ASL group playtested and balanced the sceanrios.

The Scenarios are as follows:

MM74 Hänert's Line - Vas’kovo, Russian, 23 July 1941. A classic Russian attack over open ground with the Germans defending in foxholes. Do not delay. Russians need to move fast. 4.5 Turns

MM75 Woodland Pursuit - Near Bol’shoe Kosarevo, Russia , 21 April 1943. Russian Partisans come out of hiding to go on the attack and attempt to capture equipment and exit. Sneaky partisan movement, but beware the German Shepherds. 6.5 Turns

MM76 ILLI’s Block - Ortona, Italy, 23 December 1943. The Canadians need to fight through a stiff German defense on this Deluxe Board. The Germans have some unique setup options to thwart the advancing Canadians. 5.5 Turns

MM77 Sake At Sunrise - Mount Tenjo Road, Guam, 26 July 1944. Bolstered by sake, Japanese attempt a pre-dawn attack to infiltrate the Marine lines and capture their command/supply tent. A great introduction to Night Rules. 7 Turns

MM78 A Day At The Museum - Arnhem, Holland, 19 September 1944. Tight street fighting action on a Deluxe Board. The British have to find the right balance of fighting or retreating. 6 Turns

MM79 Race For The Wolfheze - Ginkel Heath, Holland, 19 September 1944. British paratroopers rush to defend crucial defensive positions around their landing zones from a rapid German response. 7 Turns

MM80 Roll Jordon Roll - Schoenberg, Belgium, 17 December 1944. Faced with the advance of German forces the Americans have to decide whether to fight or flight. 6 Turns

MM81 Hanson’s Bridge - Bago River, Negros, The Philippines, 29 March 1945. An intense firefight across a bridge, which the Japanese are trying to destroy, and the Americans need intact. 7.5 Turns

Relatively fast, free print-and-play minimalist convoys campaign game to keep Britain alive and build up for D-Day, opposed by U-boats, Kondor(/Vulture) etc. bombers, and the usual suspects:  Scharnhorst, Bismarck, a pocket battleship, a raider, etc.
Half-year turns. NO GRID. }:-)
3 convoy lanes to lay out with 1 or more convoys per lane plus any escorting battleships or (escort) carriers.
Technology/operations advantage can shift from turn to turn.
Enigma (although there is no way to keep it secret from the German player. :-/ )
German Admiralty code breaking.
Murmansk Run.
Sudden death win, if the Allies get enough for D-Day in Summer 1944 or - for a 2-game-win Strategic Victory - even Summer 1943.
(24 shipfuls to be accumulated, but hungry Britons and visitors eat 2 each turn.)
Interesting to play solitaire, randomly composing the convoys and Atlantic patrols.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Alpha Team Mechanized Battle Force Go!
description:
A giant monster is attacking the city! You and your friends must use the power of teamwork to overcome evil... with awesome!

Alpha Team Mechanized Battle Force Go! is a co-operative game with pick-and-pass style card drafting where players work together to defeat a giant monster. Draft cards to defend against monster attacks and to pull off combos with your teammates to save the city.
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description: A 9-trick (3 tricks x 3 phases) battle of chaos and order will proceed! If the order of wins and losses for all phases matches the order of wins and losses for tricks in phase 1, Order wins!

The game is played over 2 rounds of 8 battles, with the Mining Ore Battle and the Drifting Ore Battle repeated.

In the Mining Ore Battle, you play a must-follow trick-taking game to acquire randomly revealed ores. You place drifting ores on an island of the same color as the card that won the trick.

In the Drifting Ore Battle, you play a may-follow trick taking game to collect the ores that have accumulated on the island.

A two player abstract strategy game that requires you to tactically utilize your pieces in conjunction with one another to eliminate the opponents pieces. Pay close attention to your opponent's movements as you formulate your own path to victory.

Released into the creative commons under CC-BY 4.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/)
Source code (currently trying to digitize the game) is open source under GNU GPL Version 3 (https://github.com/GreenAnts/Amalgam_Webgame)

A die decides which building the player can build. The following applies:
1 = residential building 
2 = private home 
3 = hotel or shop 
4 = school or church
5 = town hall or train station
6 = factory or gasworks
Now the construction of the buildings is decided by rolling the dice. Each player always rolls two dice at least three times. He can only build if he rolls the components in order. He can only start if he rolls the number 1, then 2, 3, etc. The pips on each die count individually. If 1 and 2, 2 and 3 or similar are rolled in one roll, the player can build the components in question together. The player can roll the dice in succession as long as he keeps up with the last number, i.e. more than three times. For the large buildings: school, town hall, train station and factory, the player must roll each number twice.
Once a player has completed a building, he is rewarded with points and the opportunity to immediately roll a die to determine the next building and begin construction. If the building selected is already being worked on or completed by another player, he must take the next lowest building.
The game ends when all components are used up. The player with the most points wins the game.

—user summary

The rifle beats even the whip, therefore it is the strongest card in the game.

A puska még a karikás ostort is üti, így a legerősebb kártya a játékban.

Test your knowledge of the TV Show Friends with 100 new questions in this second edition.

The box contains 50 Quiz Cards, each with a "fan question" (worth 1 point) and a "super fan question" (worth 2 points), plus one card with instructions.

The first player/team to score ten points wins!

2-4 player worker placement, tile placement and resource management game. Players build their town over 5 rounds by placing tiles in their 5x5 tile town to gain resources and points

—description from the designer

boardgame: Tatort Meer Junior: Rätsel um die Robbenbilder
description:
Tatort Meer Junior: Rätsel um die Robbenbilder is a crime game for children, where the children help Manni, the seagull to solve a case:

"Hi, I'm Manni, the seagull. I live in the coastal town of Nordseil and I need your help to solve a crime. Valuable paintings are on display in the seal station. But suddenly the paintings are being smeared, moved and even stolen. Help me find out who is after the paintings and why!"

No internet required. No game material is destroyed.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Sharpen your pencils and prepare to test your powers of deduction in the solve-it-yourself, who did it game of Sleuth! During a roller-coaster ride aboard the Oriental Express a deadly crime has been committed … and only you can solve it! You all play the role of a famous detective - from Sheerluck Homes to Missing Marbles - as you move from carriage to carriage solving clues to catch the criminal.

boardgame: Triple Scoops with a Cherry on Top
description:
In Triple Scoops with a Cherry on Top, you and your friends will cooperate or compete to serve ice cream.
Customize your deck then play in ruthless cutthroat competition, team up with another player for 2v2 action, or work together as a fully cooperative scooper squad! As a modular card game, the variations of play experiences are endless! 
With chaos and sabotage on the menu, can you scoop your way to victory!

—description from the publisher

Future Invaders is a physical card game set in a war-torn future Solar System, where four factions are caught in a needless war: Humans, Invaders, Organics, and Pirates.

Designed to be beginner-friendly, the game features simple rules and straightforward gameplay. For seasoned card game enthusiasts, it offers rich tactical depth, challenging your strategy, deck building, and drafting skills.

—description from the designer

With the collaboration of morning man Martin Dalair, at the helm of the WKND 91.9 morning show in Quebec City, Gladius arrives with a new feature never seen before: a quiz show where bluffing is in the spotlight. Fool your opponents with surprising claims, true or false.

Goal of the game:
Put doubt in your opponent's mind and convince him to trust you.

How to play:
All players take a game card containing two statements: one true and one false. In turn, each player addresses the player of his choice.

Choose from the two statements offered the one that you think will allow you to fool the player being questioned. Put doubt in his mind and convince him to trust you. The key to success: being credible.

In the end, he must choose whether to trust you or not. As soon as a player has read his statement, all players can try to convince to help... harm... or create confusion as soon as possible.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Avec la collaboration du « morning man » Martin Dalair, à la barre de l’émission matinale de WKND 91,9, à Québec, Gladius arrive avec une nouveauté jamais vue auparavant : un jeu-questionnaire où le bluff est à l’honneur. Dupez vos adversaires avec des affirmations surprenantes vraies ou fausses.

But du jeu:
Semez le doute dans l'esprit de votre adversaire et convainquez-le de vous faire confiance.

Comment jouer:
Tous les joueurs prennent une carte de jeu contenant deux affirmations : une vraie et une fausse. À tour de rôle, chaque joueur s’adresse au joueur de son choix.

Choisissez parmi les deux affirmations proposées celle qui, selon vous, vous permettra de duper le joueur interrogé. Semez le doute dans son esprit et convainquez-le de vous faire confiance. La clé du succès : être crédible.

À la fin, il doit choisir de vous faire confiance ou pas. Aussitôt qu’un joueur a lu son affirmation, tous les joueurs peuvent intervenir pour aider… nuire… ou créer la confusion la plus totale. Si le joueur interrogé tombe dans le piège et que vous réussissez à le duper, vous gardez la carte de jeu ; sinon, c’est lui qui la garde.
 Les cartes APPEL À TOUS
Lorsqu’un joueur prend une carte APPEL À TOUS, tous les joueurs jouent en même temps. Il s’agit d’un jeu de vitesse. Pour jouer, placez vos deux mains derrière la tête, et écoutez le joueur annoncer le thème et lire sa carte. Si vous croyez avoir entendu un élément qui appartient au thème, soyez le premier à appuyer sur le « buzzer » et remportez la carte.
 Le joueur qui accumule plus de cartes gagne la partie.
Mentir est un plaisir et vos mensonges pourraient vous aider à gagner la partie!

—description from the publisher

Quermesse is a festive card game for two to six players, where the goal is to be the first to set up three stalls (whole, without burning them!). To do this, the player must complete the ‘bingos’ with the items needed to set up the available stalls.

Discover the excitement of the animal kingdom with Animal Duel !

Ready to live a fast-paced, dynamic adventure full of strategy? Animal Duel is the new board game that will conquer your afternoons and test your cunning and speed. Perfect for players ages 8 and up. This duel of animal cards on the board offers quick games of just 10 minutes, making each confrontation a vibrant and addictive experience.

Animal Duel is easy to learn, but difficult to master. Every decision counts and every move can lead you to victory or defeat. Become a master of animal strategy and show who is the real king of the fauna.

Release the animal inside you!

—description from the designer
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
Available from backing Secret Cabal 2024 Kickstarter campaign.

Five extra cards celebrating Americana: Johnny Appleseed (Friend), Mothman (Enemy), Route 66 (Mystery), Jackalope (Beast), Apple Pie (Trinket).

Be the Queen or the King of the Cones!

There’s a line out the door at the ice cream store. Complete ice cream cones to serve the customers. The more toppings you put on ice cream cones the more points you score. Certain toppings are harder to place than others and are worth more. If you are the only one to put all toppings on one cone, you get bonus points. Roll the dice, top the cones and strategize for the most points.

—description from the designer

In this must-follow and trump trick-taking game, teams compete to win tricks, all while avoiding the dreaded 4s in the deck. Along the way, when players lose tricks, they can upgrade their cards to the trump suit. Teams are trying to control the flow of the trump suit, and they’re competing to catch tricks, but not all tricks, since 4s are negative points.

In trick-taking parlance, this is a mix of trick-taking and trick avoidance. Your team wants tricks, but the right tricks. Namely, you always want to avoid tricks with more than one 4. But otherwise, it is always good to take tricks. There's a balance to be had: you want (and indeed need) to take tricks. But you don't want to overdo it, because you’ll end up capturing the cards worth negative points.

This is the teams version of Kansas City: The Trick-Taking Game.

—description from the designer

Push your luck as an 18th century hunter risking your life for valuable furs attained from a dangerous alt-history wilderness!

Frontier Gambit is a light-weight, dice-rolling adventure game designed for 1-4 players who seek high-stakes competition and thrilling exploration. Set in an alternate 18th-century wilderness, players take on the roles of hunters venturing out to capture wild game, but lurking in the shadows are dark creatures waiting to challenge their bravery and skill. Will you risk the unknown for the rarest rewards?

—description from the publisher

description:
In Subtropical Japan, players acquire and sell different fruits that have the best market in Spring, Summer, or Fall. Fruits sold at their optimal season receive the best price. However, through the market where players acquire fruits, the seasons will steadily, but not precisely, advance and you could miss your opportunity to sell.

The player who sold their fruits for the most value at the end of the game wins.

Split Realms takes place over 4 rounds. In each round, the players each draw 5 tile-cards, and split them into two groups - offering both to their neighbor. Their neighbor chooses one group and returns the other group. A player then places their tile-cards into a growing tableau.

Lastly, in each round, the players score points. During the setup of the game, players choose one scoring criteria for each round. Some make the color tile you have the most of worth a certain number of points, or maybe the one you have the third most of. Others effect the value of a symbol that appears on some tiles.

The player with the most points after 4 rounds wins.

Through hell and back Campaign for Through Hell and Back. This will increase the size and enjoyment of the game. Using this Campaign expansion you will fly 3 squadrons each represented by one bomber against a set of 24 missions. It will be rough and resources are limited. How will you finish your campaign? Tattered and worn or unbruised and triumphant?

Campaign system uses a new tracking system that handles three crew and reserves. There is also a bomber repair mechanic and track. New mission mechanics create an engaging and challenging mission.

—description from the publisher

Sharpen your knowledge by correctly guessing the characteristics on the various animal cards.
Players each have 16 characteristic tiles and must guess the characteristics of animals shown on picture cards. Each player marks 1 or 2 characteristics with guessing chips on their grid based on the animals shown and compares these to the animal's actual characteristics revealed on the back of the card. The goal is to be the first to complete a row, column or diagonal with your own marked tiles and shout “Bingo!” Whoever shouts “Bingo!” twice wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Dragon Dragonella guards her stolen treasure. Will the players manage to win back the precious cups from her? Everyone plays at the same time: Who is the fastest, strikes at the right moment and doesn't get caught by Dragonella? Fast-paced fun with three levels of difficulty, where the first person to collect the required number of cups wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: My Very First Games to Go: Leo the Party Lion
description:
Leo the Party Lion loves a party and he always needs different supplies. The lion cards show what supplies needs to be found. Who can remember where these 6 things are, wins a lion card. The first player to collect 5 lion cards wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: My Very First Games to Go: Pete Ahoy!
description:
Penguin Pete wants to splash about in the paddling pool. Remember the symbols on the face-down tiles to help him find his way. Work together and use your memory skills — if Pete makes it to the pool before the rain starts, everyone wins!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: My Very First Games to Go: Mine or Yours?
description:
How adorable – the three cuddly toys are waiting for someone to help them get dressed. They all need a hat and socks. They also need a snack but they don’t want to get dirty.
