Work together to find the objects matching the color of the three cuddly toys before all the stains are uncovered!

—description from the publisher

DECK 52: Final Bus is a fast-paced solo card game set in a radioactive wasteland. Strategize to survive enemies, obstacles, and dwindling resources as you fight to reach a safe haven. Play standalone missions or link multiple missions together for a deeper adventure, where choices and outcomes from one mission carry over to the next. Progressively harder challenges keep the journey tense and engaging.

—description from the designer

Wherever there are shiny and sparkling things around, the Flipkins are never far away! The curious jungle creatures are just waiting for children to throw their coins. But look closely: Do the characteristics shown on the coins match the Flipkins on the cards? Then start grabbing! The player who collects the most Flipkins cards wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Summer, sun, the beach, and – of course – ice cream! The seal Robbi Gelato skillfully balances a tray of ice cream on his nose. The ice cream scoops must be placed so they don’t slip off the tray when Robbi moves. The players who successfully place all their ice cream on the tray by the end of the game win!

—description from the publisher

Race to put puzzle pieces together in order to create complete animals matching the mission card. Play in 3 levels of difficulty.

Gameplay:
Each player picks a set of puzzle pieces and transparent tokens to mark completed animals.
Flip a card to start.
Race to score by putting puzzle pieces together to create matched animals corresponding to the mission card.

—description from the publisher (translated)

四方位拼圖空間挑戰
隨身攜帶的思維遊戲

觀察顏色和圖案, 拼出對應的小動物, 3個難度

玩法:
每人拿取一套拼圖紙皮和一套透明標記物
翻開一張卡片, 迅速組合版圖, 拼出題卡中所有目標, 贏取題卡上的分數.

—description from the publisher

Created by Teachers

How I Win was made: I Win was developed by teachers from China, the US and the UK, with the goal of truly and uniquely integrating the rules of phonics into the rules of the game. Teacher Anna (Structured Literacy Teacher Credited by IDA.org), a Chinese teacher with over 14 years of experience, created the first version of this game in her own classroom based on the needs of here ESL students. The game was continuously refined and updated over two years of classroom testing and discussion with American and British colleagues.
I Win is suitable for both Chinese and Western children, ESL students and native speakers.
It was launched in North America in 2022 and won the love of many parents and children!

The game blends phonics education into a UNO gameplay.

—description from the box
publishers: Anna's Place Culture Communication Co. Ltd

description:
Who will win the title of Coco-Ninjutsu master? All at the same time and as quickly as possible, mime and repeat the ninja techniques shown, then slap the central card before the others to win it! Just don't get ahead of yourself! Coco Ninja masters will slow down the fastest!

Form a Fruit deck and select 3 Technique cards (27 different Technique cards).
Each turn, a player flips over a Fruit card featuring symbols (pineapple, blackberry, watermelon).
Each Fruit symbol is linked to a Technique card on the table, indicating an action (gestures and words).
Each player must then perform the required actions as quickly as possible, before slapping the card to claim it.
Each time a player collects a Fruit card, they also receive a Coco Ninja card, which requires them to perform additional actions in subsequent rounds.
Claim 2 cards to get 1 Medal card.
Earn 2 Medal cards to win the game.

—description from the designer

Bronze Island is a strategy board game for 2 to 4 players, set in the Bronze Age collapse period (c.1,200 BC).​

In this game, you will take on the role of a leader of a Bronze Age civilization, trying to survive in a changing world. To win, you will need to build your tableau of cards, representing different cultural and technological achievements of that time. You will also gather resources from the map and fight for territory and influence. You will face random events affecting your civilization and the world around you, such as wars, famines, migrations, and natural disasters.

—description from the designer

In Fraudville, players will take on a role of elected council members, scheming to siphon the city's budget for personal fortune and prestige. While making money is easy, getting too greedy can trigger a corruption scandal, forcing someone to take the fall. With only four years in office, make your moves wisely!

Players take turns proposing city projects. These can be funded collectively by bidding facedown cards as investments. Once bids are revealed, if enough funds are raised, the player with the highest bid takes the credit. Any surplus is siphoned from the budget and shared among all contributors.
Later, players can use their earnings to buy fortune, which grants prestige but comes with annual taxes. Mismanaging cash flow might force players to sell their hard-earned assets for peanuts—or lose prestige in disgrace.
The game ends after four years or when the city’s budget is depleted. In the latter case, a corruption scandal unfolds, and the greediest players are arrested for their crimes.

Experience the ultimate music selection from the radio station Bayern 1! With more than 150 cards you can immerse yourself in the biggest radio hits of the past decades. Enjoy the melodies as you try to put the songs in the correct chronological order. You take it in turns to complete this challenge until someone has sorted ten songs correctly and becomes the “Hitster” master!

Step into the spotlight with Talent Jam, the ultimate party game that turns every living room into a stage for family fun! Players ages 8 and up will dive into 120 unique talent challenges across multiple categories, from silly songs to creative art, bringing out the star performer in everyone.

Watch as confidence blooms and creativity soars while players showcase their abilities. Perfect for family game nights, classroom activities, or group settings, Talent Jam creates an encouraging environment where even the shyest participants can shine.

Each round, players vote for their favorite performance. Who should you vote for? The funniest performance? Most creative? Weirdest? You decide! The player with the most Talent Tokens after 4 rounds is the winner.

This laugh-out-loud experience isn't just about entertainment either – it's a powerful tool for building social skills, fostering creativity, and developing artistic talents. Whether at home, school, or camp, each round brings new opportunities for self-expression and connection.

—description from the publisher

The Fortuneteller is a new Titan of the Hyperborean Strain. It has been subjected to the waves of paracausal energies emanating from the Delphi Timelock, and has gained some mild precognitive abilities. This Titan will give you a new way to influence some of your draws, like the BP card one – but of course, everything has a price. The verdicts of the Moirai can be bent, but they bounce back. The mechanics of the Fortuneteller deal with manipulating Fate.

The Fortuneteller is compatible with both Aeon Trespass: Odyssey and Twelve Sins of Herakles.

The Fatesinger is a new Titan of the Siren Strain, excelling at aquatic movement and fate manipulation, through the Spiral keyword and the new Grand Spiral ability.

The Fatesinger is compatible with both Aeon Trespass: Odyssey and Twelve Sins of Herakles.

The Pathfinder is a new Titan of an unknown Strain. It is rather lithe, though it makes up for it agility and ease of traversal through difficult terrain. Its kratos abilities focus on supporting others, with early warnings and the provoke keyword, though the Pathfinder also has a built-in Camouflage move, so it can start the Battle from a favorable position. Last, but not least, the Pathfinder is the first Titan you can also deploy on the Voyage/Journey Map, where it can scout the road ahead for you (though forfeiting some of its Battle prowess and gaining Danger).

The Pathfinder is compatible with both Aeon Trespass: Odyssey and Twelve Sins of Herakles.

Sjørøver kamp (Pirate's Gambit) is a fast-paced, pirate-themed card game of mischief and fortune for 2-6 players aged 5 and up.

This game is meant for children and families, and is not suitable for those looking for a strategic card game for adult players.

Set in the Golden Age of Piracy, players must play their pirates, treasures, ships and pirate hunters to outmaneuver their opponents. Players must bluff, trade, and play strategically to outlast their opponents and claim victory.

Featuring vibrant artwork, caricatured characters, and accessible rules, Sjørøverkamp offers a fun, competitive experience for families and children.

Prepare to sail the high seas and embrace your inner rogue!

—description from the designer

The Oldtimer is a new Titan of the Dreamwalker Strain. It is old, and fragile, and survived far longer than its stronger counterparts. It’s slower, but moves more deliberately, and is able to utilize diagonal movement for Reactions. It’s Kratos table is very technical, with Diversion and Rouse, but it can also relive its glory days with the Precise keyword. Finally, this old Titan has a strange affinity for Cryptexes.

The Oldtimer is compatible with both Aeon Trespass: Odyssey and Twelve Sins of Herakles.

Skyward Odyssey contains 3 mini campaigns, set after the conclusions of Cycles III, IV and V of Aeon Trespass: Odyssey, with a slew of new rules for adventures and Voyage, as well as a sheet of new tokens, cards and other components.

Skyward Odyssey includes all new content, all new stories and new mechanics, including new ways of traveling.

On your turn, you will play a card and introduce a new rule - while following that rule as well as all previous rules. If you make a mistake, you're out.
The last player standing wins and becomes the leader of the secret society.

Action Card expansion for the 2nd edition! A 9-card Action Card set with brand new, unique Action Cards, such as, "Sandbags", "Let 'em Have It", "Airstrike!" and "Veteran Medic". These cards will expand the standard deck with abilities unique to the opposing forces NVA/VC or United States/ARVN/ANZAC.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: The World Crisis: First World War in Europe
description:
The World Crisis is a grand strategy game which uses many of the gaming systems developed for A World at War and its prequel games Gathering Storm and Storm Over Asia. It simulates the military, economic, political, diplomatic, research, and production aspects of the First World War and lets the players find out for themselves what might have happened if:

     Germany had stood on the defensive in 1914 and attacked in the east.
     Austria-Hungary had deployed its forces differently in 1914.
     Germany had remained true to the spirit of the Schlieffen Plan.
     Turkey and Italy had entered the war earlier, later, or on a different side.
     Germany had pursued a more aggressive naval strategy.
     The Gallipoli campaign had succeeded—or hadn’t been undertaken at all.
     Italy had broken the Austro-Hungarian front.
     Bulgaria, Rumania, and Greece had pursued different policies.
     Germany hadn’t attacked at Verdun, had followed its original plan, or had won the battle.
     Russia had collapsed earlier or hadn’t collapsed.
     The U.S. had remained neutral.
     YOU had been in command.


The World Crisis uses many of the game systems developed in A World at War, Gathering Storm, and Storm Over Asia, but it also includes the following innovations:
Placement of ground units on Army Charts, similar to Task Forces in A World at War, recreating the fog of war for both attacking and defending forces until combat occurs.
An integrated movement and combat phase, giving ground combat the flexibility of naval combat.
Multiple rounds of ground combat, with counterattacks and reinforcement for both sides, emphasizing both the importance of achieving results at the outset of a battle and the importance of staying power for the defense.
The control of terrain features in hexes still under enemy control, depending on combat results.
National cohesion levels as the crucial measurement of major power resistance.

Components:
    13 Full-color Countersheets
    Two 22" x 30" Full-color Mapsheets
    120 Random Event Cards
    Eight Player Aid Cards
    Scenario Cards
    Research Record Sheets
    One 40-page Rulebook
    One 24-page Battle Manual
    Eight 6-sided Dice

Time scale: 3 months per turn
Map scale: 60 miles per hex (Europe)
Unit scale: Corps, individual ships, and naval squadrons
Players: 2+

—description from the publisher

Vil Du Hellere? ("Would You Rather?" - in Danish) is the only party game in the world where you will find out if you agree or not that "always waving at everyone in traffic" is more tolerable than "eating two pounds of sausage a day".

In each round of Vil Du Hellere? you will generate a new and crazy dilemma (A and B) each consisting of three randomly drawn statements: one good, one half-bad, and one really, really horrible.

The active player reads the statements aloud and chooses which dilemma (A or B) they would rather do in real life. The other players then discuss which of the two dilemmas they think the active player chose. If they guess correctly, the players collectively score 1 point.

The game ends once each player has been the active player three times. The points are counted, and the players refer to the scoring chart on the last page of the rulebook to check how well they scored.

—description from the designer

Are you The Type who's been backstage at a concert? Or are you the type who loves to bake? - and can the other players guess which statement on this quirky scale you've chosen as the **most** true about you?

In each round of Er Du Typen? ("Are You the Type?" - in Danish) the active player has to choose between two randomly drawn statements (A or B) in four pairs on the table. They do so by placing either an "A" or "B"-token face down below each pair to indicate their choice.

Because the statements in each pair is drawn randomly, the active player might find both to be true - or both to be not true. In either case, the player must still choose the statement they think describes the type they are the **most**.

The rest of the players then discuss which statements they think the active player chose in each pair. For each correct guess, the players score 1 point. If they guess all four pairs correct, they also score a bonus point.

After eight rounds, the game ends and the players count their points. They then refer to the chart on the last page of the rulebook and check how well they've done - followed by a statement from "The Expert" on how well the group knows each other.

____

The randomly paired statements in Er Du Typen? makes quirky, random scales - from "Is passionate about design" to "Spends too much time in the shower". This makes the game fun to keep discovering repeatedly, even with the same group of people.

boardgame: CATSS: Combined Arms Tactical Simulation System
description:
CATSS is a military simulation system, a wargame, which aims to simulate modern and contemporary combined arms land warfare (from World War II to the present days).
CATSS stands for Combined Arms Tactical Simulation System.

CATSS is a game system. 
The rules allow simulating most ground combat, of all intensities: from high-intensity warfare to guerrilla warfare and air-to-surface operations.

CATSS is played on 1/100,000 laminated operational maps. It is a wargame that uses real maps, by marking and drawing the symbols of units as Staff officers do.

CATSS is currently available in digital version. 
Rules are available online.
Tables and maps are downloadable in PDF format (to print yourself or to play on computer with PDF files).
The game Modules are downloadable in PDF format.

Printed (paper) version of the rules, tables and maps will be available soon.

It is also possible to play on computer by drawing on scanned maps in PDF format and with the unit sheets updatable through PDF forms.

In addition, with CATSS App, it is possible to install the rules on your smartphone or tablet as a native application, which makes the consultation of the rules even easier and faster when playing.

There are 4 types of rules: 
- the Basic Rules
- the Advanced Rules
- the Air Rules
- the Guerrilla Rules

For the moment, only the Basic and Advanced Rules are available.

Basic Rules: these rules cover unit activation, hierarchical organization, communications, morale, missions, deployment, movement, infantry fire, anti-tank fire, artillery fire, assaults, logistics (simplified).

Advanced Rules: these rules allow to simulate the more technical aspects of modern warfare. They cover special artillery ammunition, engineering, logistics (advanced), electronic warfare, guided munitions, trains and railways, breakdowns and repairs, etc.

Air Rules: these rules bring the 3rd dimension to the CATSS system. They cover air combat, ground support and bombing, detection, radar and anti-aircraft fire, aerial observation, helicopters, air transport, paratroopers and drones.

Guerrilla Rules: these rules allow to simulate low intensity fights. They allow to alternate, in the same scenario, between guerrilla actions and high intensity warfighting. These rules cover ambushes, raids, patrols, special forces, posts and garrisons.

The rules are currently only available in French. They will be available in English soon (in 2025).

CATSS is a sophisticated game system, meaning that it is possible to accurately simulate almost all types of military operations at the divisional level, but it does not sacrifice on gameplay.
The time required to play a scenario depends on the number of units involved and if the advanced and aviation rules are played; a scenario with a few battalions playing only the Basic Rules can thus be played in one evening, when a scenario involving multiple divisions and playing the Advanced and Aviation Rules may take several days (or more).

CATSS, in addition to showing the effects of fighting (an artillery barrage that surprises a Battalion advancing on a road is often deadly: it’s a fact) also allows to understand and apprehend the processes that cause these effects (what makes such a barrage lethal? in relation to which environmental and technical factors?).
So, when you have assimilated the rules and the game system, you will realize that you will have a much better understanding of the impact of contemporary weapons (modern and less modern) and the possibilities of action of fighting units on military operations.

Whether it’s infantry fire, anti-tank fire, artillery fire or air bombing, each type of fire has been very precisely modeled, taking into account the results of analyses, experiences and norms established by the military and experts in this field since World War II. For example, the volume of fire and lethality of an infantry company can be quantified and calculated in relation to the weapons used, the number of ammunitions spent, the duration of firing, the distance from the target and the terrain on which it is located. Regardless of the simulated period, because it is the types of weapons used and their use that determine their effects in relation to their environment.

For experienced wargamers who want to know where they are going, we could say that CATSS is the land-based side of Larry Bond and Chris Carlson’s Harpoon Naval warfare simulation (the paper version, not the computer game). The CATSS approach, both as a simulation and in-game, is very similar to Harpoon. You can therefore consider that CATSS is to air-land warfare what Harpoon is to air-naval warfare.

For those who want to classify CATSS in a type of game, let’s say it is a hybrid game and it lies halfway between miniatures wargames with complex and sophisticated rules, to more classical map wargames, and to semi-professional simulations, but with the particularity of drawing units and information directly on the Map.

CATSS is a rich, sophisticated game that is both simple and complex (but not complicated). It allows to understand and study modern fights in a precise and original way, with enough seriousness to pretend to be a simulation, but also with enough "fun" to pretend to be an exciting game!

A 1985 trivia game dealing with 3,700 questions on New Brunswick, PEI, Newfoundland and Nova Scotia. Points are allocated based on 1 point for a correct answer to Question A, 2 points for Question B, 3 points for Question C. The game sold at least 5,000 copies in its first year. The box contains only the cards and a scoring pad - and the highest score wins.

Wombo is a deckbuilding game where you are competing to be the first to form a WOMBO combo! Each player will take turns buying Letter cards for their deck and playing them for their abilities. Once you are able to spell WOMBO in your hand, shout WOMBO to win!

A game with cards and many puzzles waiting to be solved to get out with your new bike before being locked in a store - just like in a real Escape Room! Solve puzzles that require logical skills, mathematical thinking, observation skills and creativity. The cards hide useful clues to continue the adventure and exit the pyramid. The game includes a set of puzzle cards, a game board and useful items to find the clues.

The pond is bustling with excitement! Help wriggly Taddlies grow into playful Froggos. Roll lots of tadpoles and have them jump onto land as frogs. The player with the most frogs at the end wins!

A dice-based collecting game.

—description from the publisher

The dog goes “moo,” the cow goes “baa”? The mischievous Mixalotl has mixed up all the animal heads and bodies! Quickly restore order to the chaos by turning over the animal cards and making the right animal sounds – or grabbing the Mixalotl. The player who collects the most cards wins.

—description from the publisher

How well do you know Europe? Test your knowledge of a total of 30 European countries with a colorful mix of questions from a wide variety of subject areas. Correct answers are rewarded with chips. Whoever collects the most chips wins!

—description from the publisher

Few can match Sebastian Seastar when it comes to playing catch as he has five arms to tag his sea creature friends with. And yet, they still keep challenging him. Players lower Sebastian with the fishing rod, trying to tag as many sea creatures of the color they rolled as possible, earning shells for every friend they catch. The first player to collect 9 shell tiles wins.

—description from the publisher

The animals are putting on sensational performances at the magical forest festival. But there’s a lot of hustle and bustle, and the shows end quickly. Who can keep track of it all and remember the most superstars? Enjoy a spectacular show full of fun and surprises. The player with the most points after 12 rounds wins!

—description from the publisher
The game covers the life of Moses, from his birth in Egypt to Mount Sinai. Question cards, a new game mechanism and the lovingly illustrated game board make Moses an interesting game.

The busy chipmunks are foraging for food. Can you fill your greedy chipmunks' cheek pouches with as many acorns as possible? But watch out: only 11 acorns fit in each of the hungry mouths. Whoever gets too greedy will lose all of their acorns and go away empty-handed. The first player to capture the most acorns three times wins the game!

—description from the publisher

Spike, Bang, and Pop! The porcupines are throwing a fun balloon party. After all, as porcupines, they enjoy nothing more than making balloons go pop. But the competition is fierce, because everyone throws themselves into the balloon-popping fun at once. With each turn, you try to outbid the other players to be the first one to get rid of your spike cards. Whoever manages to do this wins the round. And the first player to win two or three rounds wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Family & Friends introduces 53 new cards, divided into 5 new types (Events, Knowledge, Personality, Goals and Kittens), 2 new neighbourhood tiles and 8 cubes of a new resource, offering more difficulty and new ways to play Cathood. 
Combine the content as you like or play with all elements at once.

—description from the publisher

Quick Hangman is a twist on the classic game of Hangman where any called out letter applies to everyone's hangman - including your own!

To start, draw a card to determine your three-letter secret word. Everyone then takes turns calling out any letter that they think might be in another person’s word. If the letter is in your word, write it down on your Hangman Card. If it isn't, you can draw a line on your Hangman. Remember, any letter called out applies to everyone's word, that includes the player who called out the letter!

Players can win – and take – a Hangman Card when one of three things happen: if someone suggests the last missing letter of someone's word, if someone guesses another player's whole word, or if you are able to complete your own hangman drawing, which stops any other player from stealing it.

Whoever has collected the most Hangman Cards at the end of three rounds wins.

Can you think the same to win the game?

To play Same Brain, you'll first draw a category card from the deck. Then, secretly list three items on your whiteboard from the given category in the order that you think is most appropriate.

You'll win a point when you list the same item in the same rank as another player - so try to think about the answers that your fellow players would've put when making your list.

The first player to 12 points wins!

Bring everyone together with As One!, a fast-moving, mind-matching party game that will see you and your team match attempt to say the same word at the same time to win!

To play, everyone gets five random word cards. Your teammate puts one of them face up. It's then your turn to put down a word, try and pick a word that relates to the one your teammate put down. On the count of three, you both shout out a word that connects them!

Will you both shout out the same word? You'll have three chances to match with your team mate, the earlier you match the further you'll get to move your counter.

The first team to cross the finish line wins.

Primordial Energy is the source of all existence, an ancient power that shapes society, technology, and even spirituality. That is the world our Ancestors left us, a world where only the most perceptive minds can channel this omnipresent force. Over time, different schools arose, each channeling this energy in a different way: alchemists, metamorphs, elementalists…

Once every five years, the floating city of Aeria hosts the “Great Tournament,” a highly anticipated event to crown the new Champion.

Take on the role of an apprentice of one of five prestigious schools and put your command of primordial energy to the test!

In Elements: The Great Tournament, dive into a dynamic and highly strategic deckbuilding 2.0 experience, for 1 to 5 players.

The essence of the game is rooted in a powerful mechanic: transmutation.
Use your cards for their instant effect or combine them to cast stronger spells. Every decision becomes a question of balancing immediate impact with long-term growth. A vibrant, strategic and truly unique deckbuilding experience.

All 5 schools (factions) feature a unique gameplay. Starting with the initial draft, shape your apprentice’s identity by preparing your synergies ahead of the first round.

In ELEMENTS, each game will be different: explore a universe brimming with tactical possibilities where every session will reveal new combos.

Faced with the unpredictable nature of the Grand Tournament, turn your adaptability to your advantage and let yourself be guided by the thrill of discovery.

Will you win the favor of the Grand Masters?

—description from the designer

Love Conquers All is a cooperative tile laying game that abstracts the challenges of relationships for 1 to 5 players.

The board represents a relationship. There is a deck of cards including challenges and love cards. Each challenge corresponds to a shape of tile and brings a rule with it (e.g. I must play the largest card in my hand). When you play a tile, you must follow that rule and keep following it until somebody plays a love tile touching your challenge and flips it so it no longer affects game play.

The theme of relationships is reflected in how the challenge cards affect play. "Sickness" and "Injury" prevent you from playing on the board edges. "Travel" requires you to play at the edge. The challenge cards aren't called "problems" because they just bring a difficulty to the situation. the "Children" card must be touched by all plays because anybody who knows somebody who becomes a new parent knows that until they get enough help (love) there will be a lot of focus on the kids.

Players win collectively if they can get through the deck without running out of legal places to place tiles.

—description from the designer

boardgame: A volta ao mundo em 80 dias: A grande corrida!
description:
At the end of the 19th century, the great feat of the gentleman Phileas Fogg has already traveled the globe and there is no one who does not know that it is possible to travel around the world in 80 days. In order to break this record, some brave gentlemen and ladies organized a great race. Not only against Phileas Fogg's time, but also against each other.

Become a competitor in this great race around the world. Increase your fame in the game to get more help during the event, buy transport cards in the market to improve your personal board and create movement combos, thus optimizing your travel time. Choose your route carefully and try to execute it in the best possible way to be the great winner of the greatest race of the 19th century!

—description from the designer

Imagine: You're sitting comfortably on the couch, the credits are rolling, and suddenly you hear the first notes of your favorite film soundtrack. It's exactly this feeling of nostalgia and excitement that our latest Hitster expansion brings to the gaming table. With cards covering the unforgettable tunes from films and series of the last decades, you can test your knowledge and have a lot of fun.

The gameplay remains simple as usual: A QR code is scanned and you have to prove yourself as a real cinephile by placing the song in the right place on your imaginary timeline of film history. From blockbuster beats to TV tunes, can you nail the hits?

Whether you're old hands with the original game or fresh faces diving into the world of Hitster for the first time, this expansion is your ticket to an evening of excitement, laughter and maybe a little bragging rights about your impressive soundtrack knowledge. Come and find out which one of you really calls the shots!

In Toy Story: おもちゃの多すぎるゲーム (Toy Story: Toy Overload), you're challenged to remember the names of all the toys that players have in their hands. Well, you don't have to remember them all yourself because everyone's working together, so it'll be easy...maybe.

Try to work through all seven levels of gameplay.

September 5, 1977, Cape Canaveral. The Voyager 1 probe takes off to explore the Solar System. Its mission is to photograph the gaseous planets, their moons, and our Earth-from afar. Guide the probe on this epic journey by sending radio signals to it, but be careful not to mess up its delicate computer systems or use up too much battery power!

Bon Voyage, Voyager! is a cooperative game you may also play solo.
The goal is to get Voyager to the end of the Path in front of it. Each space on the path shows the value the die must have to move onto it. Play a card on the A-E piles to change the value of the die, then move Voyager to the next space along the Path if the space and the die show the same value. If 3 or more of the cards on top of the piles have the same color, “Noise” is generated. Noise allows more actions to take place, but damages Voyager.
By managing the cards and the use of Noise as best as you can, help Voyager complete the Path before the deck runs out or the probe is destroyed by damage.

This game has been published by DV Games in a limited edition, as a reward for winning the 2024 edition of the Gioco Inedito award.

—description from the publisher

Crowns and Cutthroats is a trick-taking duel for two players. Will you win the trick and take one step closer to your foe or does the moment require the talents of one of the city’s many townsfolk? It will take guile and quick-thinking to preserve the royal bloodline or bring the monarchy to its knees.

—description from the publisher

TerraForge is a combination ECG/Tabletop Wargame where you build a deck of cards, then fight over the void of space by using those cards as energy and lands that you can build out from your TerraForge. The game incorporates elements of Tactics Games, Card Games, and a bit of spatial elements.

OLD DOG NEW TRICKS:
TerraForge uses a combination of familiar with new twists and builds off of what you might know from other games but does it in interesting ways, such as building literal lands from the cards you play as resources, tactical combat and big action plays.

BUILD YOUR UNIVERSE:
Create lands based off of the cards you play for resources, Connect those lands to your enemy's to give your Units a path to Victory! The board starts as an open expanse with just your TerraForge floating in the void of space. Summon lands by sacrificing the memories of your previous home to establish lands for those that make the perilous journey to a new Universe and fight for a place all their own.

BIG COMBOS, BIG PLAYS:
TerraForge is balanced around wild swings and big plays. The game starts out with the Creation Phase, where players establish their lands for three turns. Once the game starts in earnest players will already have 3 lands to build their army and set up combos for their attempts to seize victory.

INNOVATIVE HEALTH SYSTEM:
TerraForge has a unique system of recording your health: Lands played increase your health, but when you're damaged those lands get corrupted, stripping the life and your control of those lands on the board but not reducing the energy those cards supply.

—description from the publisher

In this 27 page Spooktober Rules Packet, new rules are introduced for both Fallout: Wasteland Warfare and Fallout: Factions, designed to bring some of the most terrifying Wasteland denizens to your tabletop.

This free PDF contains:
New Vault-tec Approved Profiles for Fallout: Wasteland Warfare & Vault-Tec Simulator:

     Deathclaw
     Deathclaw Matriarch 
     Fog Crawler
     Shipbreaker
     Centaurs
     Super Mutant Behemoth
     Swan
     Wendigo Colossus
     Wendigos


Let them Fight! a custom scenario for Fallout: Wasteland Warfare
The Bigger They Are… Rules for using Goliaths in Fallout: Factions, including brand new Goliath Profiles:

     Deathclaw Matriarch 
     Frank Horrigan
     Fog Crawler
     Super Mutant Behemoth
     Wendigo Colossus


Shadow of the Goliath a new scenario for Fallout: Factions
The Replicated Ganger a new generic Ploy for Fallout: Factions
Centaur Rules bring the eponymous creatures to Super Mutant Crews in Fallout: Factions.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Last Call at the Crow Bar
description:
Welcome to the parched town of Avery...
Where the locals have found shelter from a dust storm in The Crow Bar. You find yourself caged in with the brooding souls steeped in a veil of smoke, when a whirl of incriminating evidence rushes in!  Orville the barkeep looks on with increasing suspicion as you scramble to preen yourself clean of crime before he calls the birds in blue!

Last Call at The Crow Bar is a crime noir-themed card game centered around avian characters. Players assume secret identities and aim to minimize incriminating evidence against themselves while attributing it to others. The game accommodates 2 to 6 players and typically lasts between 30 to 45 minutes.

boardgame: CHAINS Disc Golf Board Game: Raptor's Knoll
description:
Raptors Knoll Disc Golf Park officially opened on June 1, 2019 at Jackman Wetlands Park in the Township of Langley. Built and maintained by volunteers, the course spans approximately 40 acres of forested and open areas, including significant elevation changes, great views, good parking, and a bit of seasonal water.

This 18 hole course expansion features original photography, 2 pin positions, and teepads for Red, Blue, and Gold, making for lots of replayability.

The advanced mechanics featured on this expansion showcase the potential for capturing the nuances of a real life disc golf course, and sets the standard for what could be accomplished by a talented illustrator and course designer utilizing our Course Designer Kit.

—description from the designer

You and your friend love chocolate so much. Like, SO much. And you'll fight over any number of chocolate bars for that theobromine high.

Over the course of the game, draft tiles from a bar of chocolate (7x5 grid) using a split mechanic- wherein you must break off a line of chocolate, and if it is less than 3 squares (you're not greedy), you collect those squares. Scoring is determined by a random rubric drawn at the beginning of the game.

The peaceful Shire has a multitude of resources to dig up and transform into amazing goods to sell to Middle-Earth! And while you try to make your fortune digging up resources and building factories, the Hobbits are aware of their land’s value, and expect their share of the wealth by charging you to build on their land and helping transport resources and goods to your desired locations! But you think you can outsmart them and their pesky fees! And watch out for Mount Catastrophe…it’s due to erupt at any time.

Developed for the 2024 Print n Play Solitaire Contest, FtH is a solitaire loan-taking-developing-shipping-selling-tile-flipping game heavily inspired by the Brass series...without the need to play multiple seats or against an automa rules set.

—description from the designer

Snail Antennae is a trick-taking game with unusual rules.

In this game, you combine two cards to play in tricks.
You have to reveal your cards in your hand in descending order of numbers, when you win a trick or when you play a trump combination.
However, you have to be careful not to reveal all the cards in your hand, or you will lose!

You can play a lot of trump combinations as long as there are concealed hands, and you can also play carefully not to burst.

Please enjoy a challenging trick-taking game with hand management.

Tales is a storytelling-based party game that incorporates cards from Cards Against Humanity. Players use their cards to fill in blanks within prewritten story templates, creating humorous or unexpected narratives.

During gameplay, each participant selects cards to respond to secret prompts without knowing the context of the overall story. Once all prompts are completed, the story is read aloud, revealing the collective result of the group's choices.

The game includes a leather-bound book of story templates and a collection of matchboxes containing small prompt cards.

boardgame: Through Hell and Back: Expansion Pack 3
description:
Elevate Your Aerial Combat Experience!

Introducing the latest expansion pack, designed to take your missions to new heights:

Enhanced Enemy Aircraft Card design: Featuring a new landscape backdrop and BF-109G artwork. It's compatible with all existing content.
    Legendary Mustangs: Introducing the iconic P-51s of the 352nd Fighter Group, 457th squadron with exclusive card designs.
    Strategic Bonus Cards: New options to streamline your missions and boost your squadron's effectiveness.
    Dynamic Incidents: Face additional challenges that will test your pilots' skills.
    Expanded Bomber Fleet: More B-24 options to diversify your bombing runs.

Unlock new strategies, face greater challenges, and immerse yourself in an even richer wartime narrative. Your next mission awaits!

—description from the designer

This is a compact edition of Alhambra that contains 4 expansion modules:

     Vizier's Favour - from Alhambra: The Vizier's Favor
     Camps - from Alhambra: The City Gates
     Bazaars - from Alhambra: The Treasure Chamber
     Falconers - from Alhambra: The Falconers


Note that this only contains these 4 modules, not any of the other modules that are included in the original expansions.
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
All aboard for an unforgettable adventure through the heart of Romania! In this map for Rolling Trains you'll embark on a captivating journey from the bustling streets of Bucharest to the mist-shrouded peaks of Transylvania with Brasov city. Your tracks will wind near lush forests, medieval castles, and charming villages. But this is no mere sightseeing tour – you're here on business! The lifeblood of your railway empire is iron, the most precious cargo in the land. Load up your wagons and strategize your way to victory as you traverse this sun-drenched land. All aboard for an unforgettable adventure!

Provided components:

     map sheet of Romania in color,
     map sheet of Romania in lowink.

Prepare for the scenic adventure in the “Land of Tswana” with a map of Botswana for Rolling Trains. Get ready for the heat of the vast African savanna and travel through the richest cities in Africa. Your cargo will find its way to cities like Gaborone, the capital of Botswana, picturesque village of Shakawe or vibrant city of Lobatse. Load up diamonds and copper and traverse between mountains and lakes creating a railway of your dreams.

Load up your wagons and strategize your way to victory as you traverse this sun-drenched land. All aboard for an unforgettable adventure!

Provided components:

     map sheet of Botswana in color,
     map sheet of Botswana in lowink,

Prepare for an adventure in Egypt, the land of bright sun and vast dunes. Lead your trains along the Nile river and expand the rails from Cairo to Alexandria. Crude oil is a precious resource there that can boost your points, but it will also attract your rivals. Can you master the art of transportation and become the most respected train operator in Egypt?

Provided components:

     map sheet of Egypt in color,
     map sheet of Egypt in lowink,

Venture through Slovakia's breathtaking landscapes as you build a vast railroad empire. From the rolling hills to historic cities, your task is to connect towns with seamless rail lines, face the challenges of mountainous terrain and deliver goods all across the country. Can you master the tracks and transform Slovakia's rail network into a thriving transportation hub?

Provided components:

     map sheet of Slovakia in color,
     map sheet of Slovakia in lowink.

Embark on an epic journey through the Andes and jungles of Peru. Build an expansive railroad network through Peru's diverse landscapes, from the Andes mountains to the Amazon rainforest. Overcome natural obstacles and connect vibrant cities and fill their warehouses. Will you create the most efficient and profitable railway system in the heart of South America?

Provided components:

     map sheet of Peru in color,
     map sheet of Peru in lowink,

Enrich your game and change your strategies with this expansion for Tale of Ale: Viking Saga Begins featuring three modules for your Grand Raid preparations. They can be combined together or played separately.

Fleet Reinforcement
New Viking Clan arrived at Skjoldhavn harbor awaiting your deliveries. Expand your game to play on the same sheet with 5-6 players. New fleet changes the way you will strategize with different way of fulfilling the orders

Overseas traders
Traders from across the sea come into the village to exchange their goods. This module adds an alternative village sheet with different Trading Market to change the way you play between games.

Delivery Plan
Delivery orders are already at your doorstep, now you only need to fulfill those orders. This module gives you an already filled Harbour Sheet for a quicker setup so you can start playing as soon as possible.

Provided components:

     Delivery Plan game files,
     Fleet Reinforcement game files,
     Overseas Traders game files,
     rulebook.

Expand your game by adding new ways of trading goods during your journey through the village.
This expansion for Tale of Ale: Viking Saga Begins brings additional way to trade for resources your game, depending on your position on the Village Path.

Fill the barrels with Ale and sharpen your weapons, only you can assure the success of the grand raid!

Provided components:

     New Village Sheet for multiplayer game
     New Village Sheet for solitaire game,

New Traders arrive in the village and the Jarl demands more goods. Enrich your game with longer play, small change of rules and new Trading Post Sheet in this expansion for Tale of Ale: Viking Saga Begins.

Fill the barrels with Ale and sharpen your weapons, only you can assure the success of the grand raid!
Provided components:

     alternative Village Sheet,
     additinal Trading Posts Sheet,
     rulebook.

Set out onto a grand journey. Sail your drakkar throughout Scandinavia searching for resources and runes. In this add-on for Tale of Ale: Viking Saga Begins players will gain an additional way to acquire produce and runes. Players will travel with one Drakkar, expanding the game with more player interaction as well as giving you more things to strategize around.

Fill the barrels with Ale and sharpen your weapons, only you can assure the success of the grand raid!
Provided components:

     Scandinavia map sheet,
     rulebook.

A pack of 3 modules for Builders of Sungrave that will bring your game to the next level. Builder of Sungrave expansions consists of:

Architects module
There are changes in buildings’ costs. There is a new type of building cost that requires spending a number of resources shown in a circle in a number of different types shown below it. Players must spend at least 1 resource in each type they.

Renumeration
In this module there are additional rewards for constructing buildings that will help you in construction of the town of your dreams. Bonus a player gets, depends on the row in which the building the player has constructed is placed.

Specialists
This module lets your settlers, that were sent into the districts, to be sent into already constructed buildings giving you additional ways to score points.

Provided components:

     3 new game sheets,
     rulebook.

The fate of the island is uncertain. The winds of fortune will change into hardships and you will need to be ready for every complication. Will you take advantage of the Sungraves golden age or will you yield because of setbacks?

A pack of positive and negative event cards to spice up your Builders of Sungrave game. This expansion will change your tactic and force you to strategize on the fly. Each Event Card has its own unique effect that applies to all players.
Provided components:

     9 events cards,
     rulebook.

Royal advisors roam the paths of Sungrave. These wandering mentors visit cities, moving from one to another to help the Builders with the construction. Use them to your advantage in your mission of becoming the governor of Sungrave.

This Expansion for Builders of Sungrave consists of Advisor Cards that give you new ways to acquire resources and construct buildings inside of the districts. During the game players will have access to only one of the advisors and pass them to other players at the end of the round.

Provided components:

     6 advisor cards,
     rulebook.

boardgame: Builders of Sungrave: Castle of Sungrave
description:
Foundations for the Sungrave castle are set and workers were already sent to construct the towers. That won’t stop you and your competitors from a bit of rivalry. This time you compete to see whose materials will make up the largest part of the wall.

Castle of Sungrave is an expansion for Builders of Sungrave that brings an additional objective into the game.You will bring resources together with other players to the castle construction every time when you recruit one of the settlers. Doing so will not only bring you more renown but will also bring you closer to becoming the governor of the Sungrave Island.

Provided components:

     castle game sheet,
     rulebook,

Welcome to the Realm of Chosŏn - visit Korea map expansion for Dice of Steam.

Unleash the power of Steam-Powered companies. Their tracks await to be laid across the land! Explore Historic Cities from Najin - a hidden gem nestled in the rugged mountains, waiting for ambitious rail tycoons in the North-East, to Mokpo - a port city with dreams of prosperity, fueled by steam and ambition in the South-West.

The most important cities here are:

     Seoul: bustling heart of Korea, where ancient traditions meet modern progress.
     Pusan: A coastal gem, gateway to the East, and hub of maritime commerce.
     Pyongyang: A city of intrigue, where secrets whisper through the steam-filled streets.


Between these cities lie crucial rail lines that can grant additional market value to companies. Claim your ticket, unleash your entrepreneurial spirit, master the art of steam, and leave your mark on the history of 1900s Korea.

Provided components:

     Korea map sheet in color version,
     Korea map sheet in lowink version.


—description from the publisher

Railroad sprays steam throughout Iberian Peninsula- visit Portugal map expansion for Dice of Steam. Start the technological revolution, your tracks are already waiting to be laid down across the land.
Visit the land from Lagos on the south to Beautiful Vlana de Castello in the North

The most notable cities are:
Lisbon - Bustling capital full of ancient monuments and technological advances.
Braganca - Lively city build on a medieval castle on the north
Porto- Located along the Douro River, Historic Centre of Porto, Luiz I Bridge and Monastery of Serra do Pilar
Faro- Scenic city full of parks and vast gardens beloved by many species of aquatic birds

Between these cities lie crucial rail lines that can grant additional market value to companies.
Smell the faint scent of steam in the air, and feel the excitement building within you. The tracks of new lands await your touch, may fortune favor your decisions!

Provided components:

     Portugal map sheet in color version,
     Portugal map sheet in lowink version.


—description from the publisher

Set your rails in Ardennes with the Belgium map expansion for Dice of Steam. Explore Northwestern Europe from Flemish to Waloon region. Architecturally, Belgium was a blend of historic charm, seen in its medieval towns and grand squares, and modern advancements, particularly in the Art Nouveau movement, which flourished in cities.

Create the rail network in such cities like:
Brussels - a rapidly growing metropolis known for its blend of historic architecture and modern institutions.
Antwerp - a thriving port city and a major center of trade, known for its bustling docks and vibrant diamond industry.
Liege - an industrial powerhouse, known for its steel production and its position as a major hub in Belgium's mining and manufacturing sectors.

Between these cities lie crucial rail lines that can grant additional market value to companies. Claim your ticket, unleash your entrepreneurial spirit, master the art of steam, and leave your mark on the history of the 1900s Belgium.

Provided components:

     Belgium map sheet in color version,
     Belgium map sheet in lowink version.


—description from the publisher

Expand your horizons with a new map for “Dice of Steam”. This time you’ll create a railway in a beautiful place known for tulips, delftware and windmills, Netherlands. The country’s economy was marked by a thriving shipping industry, agriculture, and the beginning of industrialization, with modern infrastructure such as railways and canals expanding throughout the country.

The most important cities are:
Amsterdam - a bustling metropolis known for its maritime trade, as its ports remained central to the Dutch economy, alongside thriving industries in textiles and diamond cutting.
Leeuwarden - a modest yet vibrant city in the northern Netherlands, serving as the capital of Friesland with a strong regional identity.
Eindhoven - a small, industrial town in the southern Netherlands, primarily known for its growing role in the textile industry and its proximity to key trade routes.

Between these cities lie crucial rail lines that can grant additional market value to companies. Claim your ticket, unleash your entrepreneurial spirit, master the art of steam, and leave your mark on the history of the 1900s Netherlands.

Provided components:

     Netherlands map sheet in color version,
     Netherlands map sheet in lowink version.


—description from the publisher

Build the rail network across Scandinavia. Sweden, experiencing industrial growth, particularly in mining, manufacturing, and forestry, while Norway, with its rich natural resources, relied heavily on shipping, fishing, and timber exports. Both countries maintained a deep connection to their rural pasts, with traditional agriculture and craftsmanship still central to life, but also began embracing modernity through expanding cities, railways, and an emerging sense of national identity.

Most important cities would be:
Stockholm - a rapidly growing metropolis, transitioning from a small, historic city to a modern, industrial capital of Sweden. The city's economy was expanding with developments in trade, manufacturing, and infrastructure.
Kristiania - experiencing rapid industrialization, with key sectors such as shipping, timber, and manufacturing driving its economic growth, while modern infrastructure like railways and factories began reshaping its landscape.

Provided components:

     Sweden-Norway map sheet in color version,
     Sweden-Norway map sheet in lowink version.


—description from the publisher

You play as an experienced adventurer, a legend, who must enter the Legendary Dungeon and defeat the monster that is threatening to destroy the realm. 2D6 Legendary Dungeon is an expansion for 2D6 Dungeon: a dungeon crawler, print and play, roll and write game designed by Toby Lancaster.

You will need the 2D6 Dungeon core rules, two different colour six sided dice, an additional two six sided dice, a pencil and a dot grid sheet to draw the map on, and the Character Sheet (provided at the rear of the book). If you know how to play 2D6 Dungeon then you’ll be able to jump straight into this new expansion. Rooms are generated the same way with several additions, and advanced combat has a few key updates which build upon the core rules. The dramatic narrative is enhanced by the addition of Spectral Energy.

What’s in 2D6 Legendary Dungeon?

2D6 Legendary Dungeon is an approximately 200 page book that comes in digital, paperback, hardback, spiral bound and faux leather Formats.

A set of rules that are comprehensive and clearly written that have gone through a series of drafts and proofreads, after being comprehensively tested.

A large selection of narrative tables providing an endless combination of experiences and unique adventures while exploring the dungeon.

A set of rules that shows you how to generate a level 10 character if you do not have one already.

New rules covering Spectral Powers and the application of Advanced Combat.

Each level is themed and has its own set of creatures and narrative to be discovered. This connects up to create an overarching story that is woven into the lore of the setting.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Lord of Conspiracy: Light and Darkness
description:
Embark on a journey full of dangers, random locations, random encounters, watch out for ambushes, face monsters, hunt animals, pay for services and trade. Enter the darkness and explore ruins, caves or underground. Keep your team in mind and meet their basic needs. And find the Lord of Conspiracy!

In Lord of Conspiracy: Light and Darkness, players lead a team consisting of a hero and three lieutenants. The goal of the game is to find the Lord of Conspiracy or defeat all the Leaders responsible for the unrest in the kingdom. Players choose one of four teams such as dwarves, elves, halflings and humans. It is the player who decides which character in the team becomes his hero and what equipment the team has to start with. Unique equipment gives the player unique abilities. After choosing their hero, they must explore their world, have adventures, and collect testimonies and witnesses to in order to deduce who the Lord of Conspiracy is. The first player to do and - and to defeat his bodyguards - wins.

—description from the designer

Community Garden is a tile-placement game with a bid-for-turn-order mechanic. Players are all trying to complete the shapes of vegetable plants in a garden before anyone else does it. Setting yourself up to do so without setting up another player creates tension. The bid cards also perform special moves so you must weigh their functionality vs going earlier in the turn order to hopefully succeed.

This is a game from the Great Campaigns of the American Civil War family.  It covers three campaigns that occurred in the final months of 1863.

The first is "Lee's Last Offensive" in October, in which Lee advanced on Meade's positions near Culpeper VA with the intent of driving forward toward Bristoe Station.

The second is Meade's attack across the Rappahannock River in early November.

The third is Meade's Mine Run Campaign at the end of November, which was halted almost immediately in real life because of delays in crossing the Rapidan River.

The game includes a countersheet with counters needed to play the seven scenarios that are included in the game.  It uses the maps from Stonewall Jackson's Way or Stonewall Jackson's Way II; no new maps are included with this game.

The game is sold with The Skirmisher #4, and the rules are included in that magazine
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Great Campaigns of the American Civil War (Avalon Hill/MMP)

description:
Promo card of the legendary monster which was available only during Russian crowdfunding campaign.
As in the base game, you should inflict 3 damages during your fight with Inferno. But this creature is extremely difficult to deal as his response damage is rather high and his vulnerable areas are located in inconvenient places.

boardgame: Dawn of Iron: The Battles of Galveston & Sabine Pass – Expansion Pack
description:
This Dawn of Iron battle pack expansion covers the battles to control the Texan coastline at Galveston and Sabine Pass in 1863. Texas was one of the Confederacy's main links to the outside world through which they could export cotton and import arms and supplies through the Texas-Mexico border and port cities such as Galveston. The Union's Anaconda plan to blockade the Confederacy required it to tackle the Lone Star State's immense coastline. Their hopes would rise with the capture of Galveston only to suffer a series of embarrassing defeats forcing them to re-evaluate their naval strategy...

Inside you will find:

     History & Maps of the Battles (5pgs)
     Fortification rules (1pg)
     2x Historical Scenarios (Galveston and Sabine Pass)
     10x Ship Cards (Contains formats for A4 and US Letter paper sizes.)
     2x Fortification Cards (Contains formats for A4 and US Letter paper sizes.)
     6x New Upgrade Cards (A4 and US Letter paper sizes)


About Dawn of Iron Expansion Packs

This is an expansion for Dawn of Iron: The American Civil War at Sea. Inside you will find some history and maps about a famous naval event during the American Civil War, some scenarios for you to recreate it, and ship cards that you can use with this pack and your other games. You will need a copy of Dawn of Iron: The American Civil War At Sea, 1861-65 to fully use the contents of this pack.

—description from the publisher

Craft My Agenda is a semi-cooperative real-time game where players will negotiate to use nature-based solutions to protect an urban coastal region facing flooding. Players will join their resources to score for safe urban regions. Each player receives a hidden scoring card that determines their scoring goals. Some hidden goals will be shared; others will not. Players should be able to convince other players to do actions that benefit all involved players (or not). Negotiation, bluffing, and manipulation are involved.

Craft My Agenda is the third analog game of the Craft My Cost project, which was developed at University College Dublin to address climate change and coastal planning using nature-based solutions through games. This serious game aims to address the role and effect of stakeholders' hidden agendas in real-case planning and decision-making processes.

Craft My Agenda is a free print-and-play game requiring some cubes and dice to play.

—description from the designer

Explore the Boss cave and collect patterns of numbers of different colors. This will help you gain extra power to defeat the boss after you explore his lair. Then, you roll and add your power to defeat the boss.

Magic Pen is a print-and-play roll and more giveaway game. It integrates with the Game Creator Set board game, expanding the Magic Flick game to show an example of games made with pen and paper with just a die.

—description from the designer

This is a cooperative paper-and-pencil game that appeared in Sackson’s book Beyond Competition. 
The playing field shows grid of circular spaces connected by lines along with 6 triangular starting spaces numbered 1-6. 24 of the circular spaces are heavily outlined and represent rescue spaces. Players use a deck of standard cards (A-6 only) to determine what number (0-5) to enter in each rescue space. This represents the number of people to be rescued in that space. Players also use the cards to determine their movement speed (2-5) and starting triangle. Each player uses a different colored pencil. On your first turn, move from your starting a number of spaces equal to your movement speed then mark off 1 space on the shared time track. Then fill in one of the 4 quadrants of the circle you landed on. On subsequent turns, you may leave a circle from any direction that has a line leading out of it, provided you can move your full movement speed and land in an available space. For each quadrant that is marked off on a circle you leave, add 1 to your movement speed for that turn. If you land on a rescue circle, fill in the entire circle with your color and rescue the number of “people” in the space. Check off that # of boxes on the “rescues” track. Mark off 1 time each time you move. Each rescue circle may only be visited once. Each other circle may only be visited four times. If, on your turn, you have no legal move, you may move to any unused triangle and start from there. If all 6 triangles have been used, you are out of the game, but other players continue playing. If the players rescue all 60 people before 60 time has expired, they win the game. Otherwise, they lose.

Get ready to pitch your way to the top of the VC world with this thrilling new game! First, you'll need to determine who's going to be the dealer - that all-important VC - by each player pitching why they'd be the best for the job. The group will then vote on the best pitch, with any ties settled by a coin flip.

Once you've got your dealer, shuffle the deck and get ready to play! Place all cards face down in a pile, ready for the game to begin. Depending on your preferred game variation - whether it's 1:1, teams, or sudden death - the rules will differ slightly, but the objective remains the same: to come up with the most innovative ideas possible based on the nouns drawn from the deck.

So let your imagination run wild and show off your best entrepreneurial skills as you battle it out to be crowned the ultimate VC champion! With a new game every time, there's no telling what kind of amazing ideas you and your competitors will come up with.

—description from the designer
Add dynamic weather and unexpected twists to your races with this mini expansion! Each turn brings new effects that can change the course of the game, keeping every race unpredictable and exciting.

This first scenario pack includes twenty more* scenarios to further expand the enjoyment and playability of Dawn of Battle.

Battles in this set include:

Muye, 1027 BCE
Qarqar, 853 BCE
Pelusium, 525 BCE
Cunaxa, 401 BCE
Leuctra, 371 BCE
Crocus Field, 371 BCE
Asculum, 279 BCE
Magnesia, 190 BCE
Vosges, 53 BCE
Thapsus, 46 BCE
2nd Adrianople, 378 CE
Ad Decimum, 533 CE
Volturnus, 554 CE
Ashdown, 871 CE
Clontarf, 1014 CE
Manzikert, 1071 CE
Dorylaeum, 1097 CE
Hattin, 1187 CE
Liegnica, 1241 CE
Falkirk, 1298 CE

Note that the battles included in this scenario back were included with the Worthington edition of Dawn of Battle and only "expand" the scenario list of the Designer's Edition of Dawn of Battle.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Cthulhu: Death May Die – Forbidden Reaches
description:
Cthulhu: Death May Die returns with an all-new core box, Cthulhu: Death May Die - Forbidden Reaches!

In addition to new Investigators, Episodes, Elder Ones, and Monsters, Forbidden Reaches introduces Campaigns, a dynamic new mode that can be played with any Episode and any Elder One, unfolding across a three-act structure, creating a unique narrative experience with every playthrough.

boardgame: Unfair: Themed Merch Outlet Promo Card
description:
Promo card originally given out at CMON Expo 2017 when demoing the game and as in-store promo with purchase for Australian retail stores.

Shuffle this card into the relevant deck(s) during setup and proceed as normal.
Add no more than one copy of any sneak peek or promo card.

Themed Merch Outlet (Attraction - Store)
Once per Guests Step:
Gain 4 extra coins for each theme icon on this attraction.

With the release of the Unfair ABDW expansion, this card have now OFFICIALLY EXPIRED. New version is available:


     Themed Merch Outlet (B-movie Theme)

How about some more heroes? With more heroes you can get a lot of variety out of your games. You can also play them in solo (either as or against them) and even play 3-4P team games.

The mystic hermit Rin Mana has spent their entire lifetime unraveling the secrets of reality itself. Long years of isolation have distorted their moral compass even as their mind expanded with world-warping power. Now they crusade to remake the Neververse according to their own inscrutable principles.

Components:

     Rin Mana Hero set: 1 Hero, 1 Health, 4 Action cards.
     Rin Mana AI Sheet: for solo play, requires Imperator expansion.



What is the mark of a true champion? Legendary heroes clash across the Neververse to find out.

Battlecrest is a card-based tactical skirmish game for 2 players (1-4 with expansions). With perfect information, players must truly outwit their opponent to win.

Characters are composed of 6-card sets featuring a hero card, a health tracker, and action cards. Occasionally a minion or item will replace an action card.

The map is built of 6 cards as well, with the cards randomly placed in a set pattern.

Players take turns maneuvering their characters around the dynamic, and often dangerous map while unleashing powerful attacks. As action cards are used they open up new possibilities and empower the character’s battlecrests. Build up to decisive strikes or refocus to get powers back. Every character has a unique set of actions and preferred tactics, so no two battles play the same.

—description from the publisher

Step into the untamed wilderness of the Lost Jungle, where every decision could mean the difference between victory and defeat. Barbaric: Into the Lost Jungle brings fresh challenges, innovative mechanics, and a thrilling roster of Riders and Gears that promise to elevate your Barbaric experience to new heights.

At the heart of this expansion lies a new map with three unique entry points and a dynamic tide system. The ebb and flow of the waters will force you to rethink your strategy with every turn, as paths open and close unpredictably, testing your adaptability.

But the jungle isn’t just about navigating terrain—it's about surviving its dangers. The jungle is home to Morayzon, the monstrous electric eel Queen, and her army of deadly ranged-attacking minions. Together, they will stop at nothing to protect the treasures of the jungle, creating a relentless threat for every player.

For the first time, team play comes to Barbaric! Partner up with an ally in 2v2 battles, where teamwork and coordination are the ultimate keys to success. Use the new Riders and Gears or mix them with characters from previous versions of Barbaric to create endless combinations of strategies and playstyles.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Barbaric: Heart of the Grand Canyon
description:
Venture deep into the Grand Canyon, where danger lurks at every corner and teamwork is your only path to survival. In this fully cooperative expansion, players must join forces to retrieve the precious atomic core from the formidable Honetia, the queen bee, and her swarm of lightning-fast minions.

The canyon presents more than just hostile enemies—it challenges players with dynamic special missions that must be completed each round. Success demands collaboration, strategy, and adaptability as the hive becomes increasingly dangerous. For those seeking an even greater challenge, the Mega Minions Mode amplifies the ferocity of Honetia’s minions, making them stronger and more relentless than ever before.

The new map immerses players in the harsh and perilous landscape of the Grand Canyon, with three entry points leading into Honetia’s hive. Navigate through crumbling rockslides, which not only damage players but also block essential pathways, including underground waterways. Every step forward is fraught with obstacles, making your mission all the more daring.

Whether playing solo or with up to four players, Barbaric: Heart of the Grand Canyon offers a fresh and immersive cooperative experience. Combine Riders and Gears from this expansion with those from other Barbaric sets to create endless possibilities and bring new dimensions to your gameplay.

Do you have what it takes to unite your team, survive the canyon’s unforgiving hazards, and emerge victorious against Honetia and her swarm? The Grand Canyon awaits your courage—will you rise to the challenge or fall to the hive?

—description from the publisher

Step into a new chapter of the Barbaric universe with The Challenger, a mini-expansion that introduces two remarkable Riders and their unique Gears, bringing fresh dynamics and strategies to your games.

Meet Aleanor, a skunk renowned for her mastery of perfumes and her ability to manipulate others, turning the tide of battle with precision and control. Alongside her stands Alfredo, the noble archer prince of the northern deer tribe, whose unerring aim and agility make him a force to be reckoned with.

Their Gears add even more depth to the battlefield. Neptune, a powerful snail-like machine, excels in relentless pursuit and boasts impressive defensive capabilities, while Gemini, the seahorse Gear, shifts effortlessly between stone form, offering stability, and diamond form, unleashing devastating power to outsmart opponents.

While this expansion requires a core game to play, the addition of these Riders and Gears seamlessly integrates with other Barbaric sets, enhancing gameplay with limitless possibilities. The Challenger promises to inject new life into your Barbaric matches, creating unique challenges and opportunities for creative strategies.

The stage is set, and the challenge awaits. Will you rise to it?

—description from the publisher

You are one of the great Ninja clans competing for glory. It is a trick-taking game for 3 to 6 players where you must always win the trick whenever possible. When you win a trick you will have the opportunity to choose a mission. After certain rounds the Ninja with the most points wins.

Sos uno de los grandes clanes de Ninjas compitiendo por la gloria. Es un juego de bazas de 3 a 6 jugadores donde siempre que sea posible deberás ganar la baza. En el momento de ganar una baza tendras la oportunidad de elegir una misión. Luego de ciertas rondas gana el Ninja con mas puntos.

Peace Conference is a cooperative paper-and-pencil game that appeared in Sackson’s book Beyond Competition. The game is played with a deck of 24 cards (4 each of values 1-6. You may use the A-6 of a standard deck or cut out the cards on the back cover of the book). The scoresheet/playing field shows 4 large boxes labeled A-D, where each P writes their initials. In the middle are 6 grids of boxes (known as “accords”), each showing a different two-letter combination of the letters A-D )(AB, BC, etc.) There are 4 girds showing all the 3 letter combinations (ABC, ABD, ACD, BCD) and one grid showing ABCD. Winding around the grids is a conflict track (aka the “path to destruction”) with 100 spaces. For a four-player game, deal out 4 hands of six cards. Each P secretly selects one card from their hand and places it facedown on the table. The cards are then revealed simultaneously. If any two cards have the same value, a “conflict” has occurred – mark off 1 box on the conflict track. If three cards have the same value, mark of 3 boxes. If all four have the same value mark off 4 boxes. If 2, 3, or 4 cards in sequence are played (e.g.5-6, 3-4-5, or 1-2-3-4), those players are in “accord” - check off a box in the grid corresponding to the players who played the sequence (i.e. If player A played a 1 and player C played a 2, you’d mark off a box in the AC grid). Only mark off a box for the longest sequence(s) played. If two separate two-card sequences were played (e.g. 2-3 and 5-6), mark off both grids. Now each player plays another card from their hand in the same manner until all six cards are played. Cards previously played are left faceup on the table as a reference/aid. Players may not divulge or discuss the contents of their hands. After all six cards have been played, reshuffle the deck, deal out the cards again, and continue. The players win if a) all the boxes of any 3 of the two-player accords are marked off, b) all the boxes of any 2 of the three-layer accords are marked off, or c) 15-boxes of the four-player accord are marked off. The players lose if the last box (box 0) of the conflict track is marked off. For games with 2 or 3 players, one or two players will control a dummy hand.

Search is a cooperative paper-and-pencil game that appeared in Sackson’s book Beyond Competition. The playing field is an 8x11 grid of squares representing an haunted house. Each square has from 1 to 3 dots in it. Each room is connected to one other room by a corridor. Six of the rows and six of the columns are marked by triangles with the numbers 1-. Each players uses  different colored pencil. Players determine their starting room by drawing two cards from a deck of 24 cards #d 1-6 (4 of each value) then circling the dots in one of the rooms where the row and columns indicated by the triangles correspond to the cards drawn. A player may never enter a room occupied by another player or connected to such a room by a corridor (if a player would start in such a room, they draw again). 
    On subsequent turns, each player must move as follows. 1) if they are in a room and they have already visited the connecting room, they my move to any adjacent room (obeying the rule above) and circle the dots in that room. Except that they may move to  room that has the same number of dots that are in ANY player’s (including their own) current room. If they are unable to move to a room, they lose their turn and continue to lose their turn until they can move to another room or until the game ends. 
2) if they are in a room and they have not visited the connecting room, they must circle the dots in the connecting room, regardless of the number of dots in that room. Each time  player moves to  new room (by either method), they color in the circle of room they left, so that the room with the circled dots always represents their current position. They also check off a number of boxes on their score track equal to the number of dots they circled. Players must move if they are able to. 
The game ends when no player is able to move. The players’ is equal to the number of the highest checked box of the player who has advanced the farthest on their track minus five times the difference between that player’s position and the position of the player who has advanced the fewest spaces. If that score is positive, the player’s win; else they lose.

Round 'N' Round is a cooperative paper-and-pencil game that appeared in Sackson’s book Beyond Competition. The playing field shows a winding, coiled track of spaces. The game uses a deck of 24 cards (4 each of values 1-6). 4P game, each P uses a different colored pencil (or 2 can play, each controlling two different colors). To start, each player draws a card. Starting from the innermost space (the one marked with an X), the player who drew the highest colors in that many consecutive spaces in his color. The other players do the same in reverse order of the number drawn. Then six cards are dealt in a row. On a player’s turn, they may select the card at either end of the row and fill in that many consecutive spaces on the track. When there is only 1 card remaining, deal out 5 more to the right of it. When the deck is depleted, reshuffle and deal out cards until there are six.
    There are two rows of boxes above the playfield – a “+” row and a “=” track. Each time you color a square, if you form a line of adjacent spaces of that color (not including diagonally), fill in that many spaces on the “-“ track. If you form a line of 3 adjacent spaces (not including diagonally) of three different colors, color ONE space of the “+” track. If you reach the end of the “+" track before you get to the end of the “-“ track, you win. If you reach the end of the “-“ track, you lose. If reach the end of the main track before you reach the end of the “+” or “-“ track, whichever track you were farthest ahead on determines if you win or lose.

Introduction:Edrala (from Scottish Gaelic: eadar-dhealaichte, which means "various" or "different") is a a drawless diversification game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended board size is 5 cells per side, but boards of 4 or 6 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a maximal set of stones of the same color connected to each other. A single stone is also a group.
     The size  of a group is the number of stones it contains.
