					Turns:Black plays first and then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty cell adjacent to at most one friendly group. If you cannot do so, pass your turn; otherwise, passing is not allowed.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when no player can place stones. At the end of the game, each player receives one point for each group of their color that is different in size, plus an additional half point for the player whose largest group is smaller than the opponent's. If the tie persists, the next largest groups of each player are compared, in descending order. If all sizes match, the player who placed the last stone wins.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a black stone on an empty cell and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer
A trading card game where the best divorced dad wins. Take turns playing cards and attacking with beast. Use tools and terrain cards to gain advantage. Deplete your opponents cans to win.

In this special 10-page Companion Supplement for Fallout: Factions, you will find everything you need to start fielding loyal Animal and Robot Companions as part of your crews.

Attached to your Champions, Companions are added via the new Companion Perk type, that can be taken when you hire a new Champion. Once the Perk is taken, your Champion has a friend for life (regardless of how short that may be), and you have a brand new model to help you lay claim to the Wasteland!

The rules presented in this supplement are for both Casual games of Fallout: Factions, and Tournament play.

This free PDF contains:


     New Companion Perks


        Roboteer
        Creature Tamer

     New Fallout: Factions Robot Companion Profiles:


        Mr Handy
        Protectron
        Eyebot
        Securitron (Mk 1.0 and Mk 2.0)
        Nukatron
        Mr Frothy

     New Fallout: Factions Creature Companion Profiles:


        Brahmin
        Bloatfly
        Bloodbug
        Deathclaw
        Mongrel
        Radstag
        Yao Guai

     New Rules and Weapons to fully bring your Companion models to the table


—description from the publisher

boardgame: BULL'S EYE: Strategic Air Combat Board Game
description:
Strategic Air Combat Board Game

This game is played by up to four players on a circular board.
The game is played over a total of 13 rounds, and the player who is closest to the center of the game board at the start of each round is awarded victory points and resources to augment his or her actions.

However, being positioned near the center of the board has the disadvantage of making you an easy target for other players.
This game must be played while keeping track of the positions of players other than yourself.

Victory points and resources to reinforce your actions are awarded to players who damage their opponents or take aggressive actions, as well as position checks at the beginning of the round.

Conversely, points are deducted for players who stay outside the game board and take passive actions.
This is repeated for each round of the game, and the player with the highest total points wins the game in the end.

Any player who is shot down the specified number of times before the end of the 13th round is eliminated from the game at that point.

The player who has earned the most points at the end of the game (13 rounds) by continuing to act aggressively by making full use of the movement and attack systems that take advantage of the characteristics of fighter planes on a board with a dynamically changing environment wins.

boardgame: Sengoku Folio 9: The Battle of Hasedo Castle
description:
The Battle of Hasedo Castle is a Japanese doujin (fan-made) game produced by Ludens Faber and is game 9 of his Sengoku Folio series. The game simulates the battle between Uesugi and the Date/Mogami allied forces in 1600.

The game is a typical wargame, with movement and attack values, zones of control, a CRT for combat results, and a turn track.

The game includes a map, rules, historical guide, and mounted counters. The counters need to be cut out.
publishers: Dōjin Club Ludens Faber (同人会ルデンス・ファベル)

description:
It’s 2am, everyone’s asleep, but your tummy’s rumbling... A quick trip to the kitchen in the dark, what could go wrong?

IT’S SUPPER TIME!

Sneaky Supper is the ultimate push-your-luck game where stealth is key! Tiptoe through the kitchen, grab snacks, and collect ingredients to whip up a mouthwatering meal. Maybe you’ll munch on some potato chips for quick points.

The game ends when someone hits 20 points, but only the sneakiest player with the most points will claim victory.

But watch out! Drop your utensils or accidentally step on the family cat, and it’s game over... with your midnight feast confiscated, and the whole house awake (and angry)!

Can you create your dream supper without waking everyone up? Or will you stumble and fumble your way to bedtime empty-handed?

Let the sneaking (and snacking) begin!

—description from the publisher

As the mayor of a new town, you decide where and what gets built. Place buildings and roads to fulfill the requirements of Plans. As you grow, so do your progress tracks, granting you rewards the higher the go. Do your best to build the best city, as the player with the most prestigious city wins the game!

To play Scribble City, you need three things: A city deck, a map pack, and colored pencils.

You can mix and match city decks and map packs for different gameplay experiences. Each city deck and map pack comes with their own unique mechanics and features, offering a variety of ways to play.

—description from the publisher

Embark on a journey through serene lakes and vibrant rivers in Lakefinity, a rare card game where strategy meets the beauty of freshwater aquatic life. Entire game library features over 300 fish from around the World and Starting Pack gives you 31 cards: 21 common, 6 uncommon, 3 rare and 1 epic. Only first two types of cards are present in the Starting Pack, while 4 last cards are random (10 possible cards of Rare & Epic levels).

Collect, trade, and build the perfect fish ecosystem as you  discover new fish species from around the World and outsmart your rivals with a deck of 31 cards you pick yourself. With stunning artwork Lakefinity offers fun for families, gamers, and nature lovers alike.

Goal is to gather biggest winning deck by challenging other player/s by picking one of several fish traits off the card. With each starting round you have 3 fishcards in your hand that you draw from your deck.

An extra challenge comes from the ability to match an opponent's card value. When this happens, players take turns stacking cards with the same trait until one player's card has a higher value. The winner then adds the entire stack to their winning deck.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Final Exam: Grow Up Not a Cheat, Growing Good
description:
The final exam is upon us! Our hectic high school life is full of activities. There is no time to spare for cramming. Our only hope rest upon the shoulders of “Straight-A Stephen” to send us answers to the questions and escape the hawklike praying eyes of “Patty the Teacher’s Pet”. Our quest for full marks has begun!

The student with the highest score after 5 5 rounds is the winner.
Each player get 1 Answer Sheet and 1 pencil
Shuffle role cards. It have Teacher’s Pet/Star Pupil/Straight-C Students. Deal 1 card to each player. They peek at the card and place it face down
Each round lasts 3 minutes 

     Straight-C Students: must pick up hints from (unknown)Star Pupil and write the correct in the Answer Sheet
     The Star Pupil must send hints the answer they got (A/B/C/D) to other students without getting caught by the Teacher’s Pet
     The Teacher’s Pet must caught the Star Pupil wile picking up the answer themselves 


End of game
The student with the highest score after 5 rounds is the Winner.

—description from the publisher

ART TOY fans rejoice. The new Riqaa collection is coming out this year! With 9 cute characters in high demand. Secret items pop up once in a blue moon, but oh what a treat it would be if we could lay our hands on one. Now is the time to find the Dream Set and the Secret items!

Each player chooses 3 different dream set cards face up
Deal 3 Blind box cards to each player face down below dream set
And play an action card to find Blind box card that match dream set.
The round ends immediately when the second Closed card is draw
Play 3 rounds, after 3 rounds the player with the highest total score is the winner.

—description from the publisher

Morbopoly as an adult party game of dark humor. The goal of the game is to "achieve a life as miserable -that is, as similar to your real life- as possible". To do this, players must cross the entire board collecting Coupons of Distress, Illness, Tragedy, etc. It's the morbid heir of Monopoly, Game of Goose, and The Game of Life.

Advance through the road of our life in the board collecting as many disgrace coupons as possible. The game ends when a player reaches a death square (unless said square indicates otherwise). At that moment, everyone's coupons are counted. The player who has more will be the winner.

A game in which 2-4 people have to keep running. It is about to keep oneself the possibility of moving your own meeple as long as possible.

45 discs are laid out in 7x7 format, leaving any 4 free spaces. Each player places his/her meeple somewhere in that grid.

The starting player rolls the dice and moves his meeple the corresponding number of fields. He may only move orthogonally, but may make as many hooks as he wishes.

He removes the red disc from the field where he ends his move. This reduces the number of possible fields for the rest of the game. A player who can no longer move according to his dice roll is eliminated. Winner is the last player that could move.

—description from the publisher
The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The players start at Bremerhaven and the first to arrive on space 66 (Leningrad) wins the game.

Dreadnought: Mini is a simple miniature wargame, using any miniatures, and data cards.

The game is played in a series of turns, which are broken up into two phases: Control and Repair Phases.

Fleets have their own abilities.

Objective: The game ends when only one player has ships in play. That player wins the match.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Black Friday 2024 Web Store Promo Cards
description:
Two exclusive promos received from ordering $100 or more worth of product from the Flying Frog webstore during the 2024 Black Friday Sale. Included:

Vampiric Hunter: Darkness - Every Hero must roll a D6 for each wound they have on them. On the roll of 1 or 2, take 1 Sanity damage ignoring Willpower. Any hero KO'd by this also gains a Bitten marker (if you have the rules for Bitten markers).

Steel Lock Box: Gear - Use 2 Grit to open, rolling a D8 on the chart below then discarding this card:
1: Trap, take 2D6 wounds ignoring defense.
2-5: Gain D6 starting side bag tokens.
6-7: Loaded, draw D6 Loot.
8: Draw 2 gear and an artifact card.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: XL Warden Mk III Gladiator Enemy Pack
description:
Buried in the desert sands of the Blasted Wastes, the Wardens are giant robotic guards, constructed with a single purpose...to prevent anyone from ever escaping this harsh alien prison world. Towering above the inhabitants of this inhospitable land, the Warden Mk III Gladiators rip through the rusted metal hulks that scatter the surface, using their arsenal of deadly weapons to lay waste to all in their path!

This Enemy Pack contains everything you need to add the XL-sized Warden Mk III Gladiator into your games of Shadows of Brimstone!  Includes alternate Hands and Helmet to customize your model.  Also includes the large Enemy Record Sheet for it, counters, and all of the Threat Cards needed to add this deadly robotic Enemy to your games of Shadows of Brimstone.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Dark Stone Magazine Issue 1 Promos
description: The first issue of Dark Stone Magazine is 64 pages filled with articles and features focusing on your favorite games from Flying Frog Productions. This issue also includes six exclusive game cards and a huge double-sided 20×30" poster. New to this issue are Threat Deck Recipe cards, which are suggested enemy combinations for themed adventures.

boardgame: ツチノコドコノコ (Tsuchinoko Dokonoko)
description:
Are those two really connected?

Tsuchinoko Dokonoko is a speed action game in which you must quickly find two connected cards among the cards that are turned over one after another.

Recommended for families with elementary school children and casual/light groups.

When a player notices that one of the cards on the table is connected, they quickly take one of the two cards!
The other card can be taken by another player. In other words, two players can take it... how nice!

That's all the rules? But it's full of complications!
It's a game where you just have to find two pieces that connect, but it's hard to "find" them?

     Colors are inverted
     There are legs and there are not
     The distance of the connections is subtle


This complexity slows down your sense of speed and leads to repeated mistakes.

Let's make a Tsuchinoko/Lizard with the parts you collected at the end of the game.
Before you know it, all you've collected is the head.

その2枚本当につながる??
『ツチノコドコノコ』は、次々とめくられるカードの中からつながる2枚を見つけて早取りするスピードアクションゲームです。

小学生のいるファミリー層や
カジュアル・ライト層のグループにおススメ♪

場にめくられたカードの中で「つながる!!」と気づいたプレイヤーは2枚の内1枚を急いで取ります!!
もう1枚は他のプレイヤーが取ることができます。つまり、2人取ることができるってこと…優しいですね♪

ルールはそれだけ？でも、ややこしさはいっぱい!?
つながる2枚を見つけるだけのゲームですが、その「見つける」が難しい??
●色が反転
●足があったりなかったり
●微妙なつなぎ目の距離感

このややこしさが、スピード感を鈍らせ、お手つきを連発します。

ゲームの最後に集めたパーツでツチノコ/トカゲを作りましょう。
気づけば、集めていたのが頭だけ…なんてことも。
publishers: ぶれけけゲームズ (Brekeke Games)

description:
A frog jumps in! Memories fly away!?
Frog Cycle is a memory-based game in which you rely on your loose memory to connect and acquire the growth cycle of a frog.

The point is that the sudden unexpected events will blow your mind!

飛び込むカエル！！吹っ飛ぶ記憶！？
カエルサイクルは、ゆる〜い記憶を頼りに、カエルの成長サイクルを繋ぎ、獲得する神経衰弱系ゲームです。

突然訪れるハプニングタイムのおかげで、記憶が吹っ飛んでいくのがPoint！！
artists: おーつぼじゅうじん (Jyujin Ohtsubo)
publishers: ぶれけけゲームズ (Brekeke Games)

description:
Market Day is a drafting game where the cards you collect are scored across 4 criteria. Certain colors of cards will be worth a different number of points based upon how many of that color are in a public tableau; certain ranks of cards will be worth points, depending upon how many of that rank are in players' collections; certain cards are just worth points; and special scoring cards.

The drafting mechanism resembles a "pick and pass" draft, where each player has a hands of cards, chooses 1, and passes the others. However, rather than passing the cards, the unchosen cards are collected, shuffled - the designer suggests a wash shuffle - and then hands are redealt.

The game lasts until the hands have dwindled and the player with the most points wins!
artists: Yamauchi Rock Boy (ヤマウチロックボーイ)
publishers: ハレルヤロックボーイ (Hallelujah Rockboy)

description:
In the second expansion to the game, a shady figure appears in the Caravanserai: he is the Opium Dealer. Illicit goods open up new and tempting ways to fulfil orders, but getting caught can easily lead to failure. 
Trading with different currencies adds an exciting new dimension to the function of the Money Changers. 
The added features come with a set of new ability cards, some of which can be used with the Dervish expansion. 
The Opium expansion is only playable with the Caravanserai base game.

The first expansion to Caravanserai adds a new character to the game. The old Dervish spends his days in the shade of the North Gate, often overwhelming passers-by with empty chatter. But if you are there at the right time, he can give you valuable advice. 
The box contains a Dervish miniature and 24 cards. 
The expansion is only playable with the Caravanserai base game and integrates well with the Opium expansion.

boardgame: We Didn't Build Decks at All
description:
Over a series of games, build a progressively more ridiculous 6-card deck out of We Didn't Playtest cards.  Playable on its own or in conjunction with most other Playtest games.

You start by drawing 8 cards and picking 6 to be your personal deck.  Then you play Playtest almost as normal.  Instead of win and lose instantly ending a game, they award or subtract health.  If anyone hits 0 or 8 health, they are eliminated.  At the end of 3 full rounds, the remaining player with the most health wins.  Then you prepare for a new game with additional cards available to pick your best 6.  Each round gets more and more high level, and allows you to adapt to the other players' decks.

A single game takes 1-5 minutes.  A campaign of games takes 15-30 minutes.
family: Game: We Didn't Playtest This At All

description:
Color Chord is a set collection game for 4 or 5 players that uses a "batting" mechanic - where cards are revealed simultaneously by the players and cards which share a rank have something special happen.

In Color Chord, if you play the same number as someone else, you each take your card and place it face up in front of you. Player that played unique numbers take two cards from a face up market, and then add their played card to the market.

If a player collects cards of the same rank in front of them, those cards are discarded face down. The game ends with the deck of cards is exhausted, and the player with the most points wins.

Sosuto is a climbing game based on prime factorization. Play proceeds clockwise and on your turn you must, generally, play a higher card than the last person. You may play any higher card that shares a prime factor with the last card played; you may also play a lower card if it is one of the prime factors. The cards are color coded to aide in the math.

A round ends when either a player has played all of their cards, or a player has collected a certain number of penalty cards from not being able to play. Players lose points based upon the cards left in their hand. The player with the most points after a series of rounds wins.

Skirmish combat rules for Vietnam War where you take command of a US platoon fighting the Viet Cong insurgency. 
Uses 10-40 troops per side, from 'grunt' privates with their M16s to squad and platoon support weapons and up to fire support requests from mortars, artillery, and helicopter gunships. Includes campaign system to play through 12 months 'in-country' in command of your own platoon.

Detailed US platoon creation and support fire options using a points-free system. Scenario creation for ten missions, including routine security patrols, search and destroy missions, ambushes, hunting fugitives, clearing a hot LZ, assaulting an enemy strongpoint, and surprise VC night raider attacks on US bases.

Rules include: Movement, Suppressing Fire, Targeted Fire, Explosive Weapons, individual and unit Morale, Medics and enemy 'Hazard' actions, such as Return Fire, Ammunition Checks and Booby-Trap detonations. Vehicle rules for the common armoured fighting vehicles of the war, with support from M113 APCs, M48 Patton tanks, and 'Huey' helicopters.

All scenarios include options and rules for: variable game sizes (small, medium or large), from small firefights to a full platoon in action, variable 'threat levels' setting how many VC might be encountered, the presence of civilians on the battlefield, 'search points' and optional scenario sub-plots for an added twist, like finding a downed pilot, or the arrival of unexpected VC reinforcements.

Variable and unknown VC forces to encounter, divided into three threats: Local Guerrilla forces, the Regional 'Militia' Force and the National 'Army' Force, as well as all manner of ruses, booby-traps and defences.

—description from the publisher

Our King lies ill in the tavern after enemy shamans unleashed a deadly curse across the valley. You are the only member of the King's party with basic alchemy skills and the ability to navigate the skies in your air balloon. Only you can save the valley: heal the villagers, hold back the orcs, find a cure, and revive the King! 
Choose your character. Decide whether to excel in foraging, potion crafting, skyship navigation, astronomy, mysticism, or other unique specializations. Adapt to changing circumstances, and switch between classes as needed.

—description from the designer

66 fleurs de printemps ("66 Spring Flowers") is a roll & write game designed and sold in aid of Accessijeux, a French non-profit organization dedicated to making board games accessible to visually impaired players. Only 1000 copies of the game has been made available, only sold by French webstore Philibert on 29/11/2024 (just like 61 feuilles d'automne and 16 lueurs d'hiver the previous years).

In 66 Spring Flowers, roll dice and complete as many activities before Spring ends. Gather flowers, hang out the laundry, grow plants, watch bees, have a picnic on the grass and party with wild animals!

Each turn, a player rolls 3 6-sided dice (not included in the game), and each player chooses one of the rolled value to participate in one of the 5 activities, another to increase their flower counter to unlock bonuses, and the third one to activate special events for each activities. Use your dice smartly to unlock ladybirds and score more points than your opponents.

Gardens is an abstract strategy game for 2 players. The goal is to be the first to place 8 stones of your color into the High Garden.

Gameplay:
Players move and stack stones of their color across the board, aiming to control key positions and capture their opponent's stones. Movement is horizontal or vertical, with stones dropped along the path. Players must balance offense and defense, managing limited stacking in their Home Garden and leveraging special rules for towers and staircases.

Key Features:
Tower Control: Towers with 5 stones block movement.
Capture Mechanic: Gain control of opponent stones by stacking on top.
Staircases: Special rules add layers of tactical depth.

Victory:
The game ends when a player successfully places 8 stones in the High Garden.

—description from the designer

Believe is a creative storytelling and bluffing game for 2-6 players. Use your cards to craft incredible tales—true or false—and deceive your audience to win.

Gameplay:
Players take turns as storytellers, drawing cards to inspire their stories. The storyteller secretly decides if their tale is true or a lie, marking their choice with a coin, and tells their story within a 3-minute time limit. Other players ask a single question collectively before individually guessing whether the story was truthful or not.

Scoring:
Points are earned based on how successfully the storyteller fools the audience. The more players misjudge your story, the higher your score. The game ends when a player reaches the point goal, adjusted for player count.

Key Features:

Storytelling Meets Strategy: Combine creativity with cunning to craft convincing or outrageous tales.
Audience Interaction: Players must work together to question the storyteller, then rely on their intuition to make individual judgments.
Dynamic Play: Flexible components like the number of cards drawn and varied player roles keep the gameplay fresh.

—description from the designer

MUKBANG noun.(from Korean) ​a video, especially one that is live-streamed, of a person eating a large quantity of food while talking to an audience.

- definition from Oxford Dictionary

MUKBANG: THE YOUTUBE CRAZE – Inspired by the YouTube craze, Mukbang is the high-intensity, randomized memory game where you match food cards to Mukbang sounds coming from our adorable, electronic cheeseburger-shaped gaming unit.

PRESS THE BURGER BUTTON RANDOMIZER – Press the burger button randomizer to get a Mukbang sound – could be a slurp, crunch, belch, or another intriguing noise!

MEMORIZE & MATCH TO THE SOUNDS OF MUKBANG – Turn over your cards and remember which ones are where for your next turn. Memorize and match 7 food cards to the sounds of Mukbang to win!

LAUGH WITH FRIENDS & FAMILY – This hilarious game is fantastic for friends or the whole family. You can even include real foods or make your own extra food cards and put your own spin on it!

RULES:

     Get a sound.
     Turn cards to find a match. 
     Remember where they are. Match 7 to win!


—description from the publisher

Mischievous fox Kitsunedon and tanuki Tanukyun are having a tile-placing duel, but they have a trick up their sleeves. PoCon! plays similarly to Othello, but with a tricky twist, each player has four trap tiles which are the same on both sides!

On a turn you place one of your tile, flipping all opposing tiles that you enclose. When flipping, you always flip tiles starting in a line starting from the tile just placed. However, if you flip over a double-sided trap tile, you immediately end your turn before any more tiles are flipped over (if your move cause two lines, you choose which line to flip first).

Once all tiles are placed, the tiles are counted up, and the first place receives 3 extra points. The person with the higher score wins!

There are two rule sets included. In Wai-Wai mode your tiles are shuffled, so you don't know which ones are trap tiles. In Trick mode you can strategically plan where to play your trap tile. Both modes can also be played with a 4 player team mode.

15 Lantern Years

In celebration of 15 years since the release of the first Kingdom Death figure, we revisit the starting survivors of Monster, Allister, Zachary, Lucy, and Erza. Fifteen lantern years into their existence the pursuit of survival is replaced with a quest for perfection. Life and death still remain balanced on the edge of dice roll but the grizzled survivors have endured beyond expectation.

The 15th Anniversary Survivor figures are accompanied by an expanding armory of weapons wrought with purpose to take advantage of fortune and tenacity. A Blacksmith settlement location extension and a host of new tools and resources allows settlements to craft the wrought weapons of the 15th anniversary survivors.

The 15th lantern year of this journey has hardened our resolve. Kingdom Death has taken darkness of the past lantern years and crafted a path forward. Our journey continues to draw random events, impossible showdowns, and the rich rewards of bringing new stories to the world of Kingdom Death. Let's forge ahead together into 15 more lantern years of darkness!

—description from the publisher

The Syncretic Vestments are a promo gear card that were included with Priestess Percival, a miniature that itself is not part of the game.

The Syncretic Vestments say: When you block or deflect a hit, gain +1 strength token. Limit once per round.

With two red affinities, the vestments gain: While you have any +1 strength tokens, swords in your gear grid gain Provoke.

Throughout history mankind has been pondering the things that are not of this material world. To make sense of the unknowable and to seek wisdom in matters greater than humanity have proven themselves to be a favoured pastime again and again. Several kings, queens, emperors and empresses were no exception to this rule. Both in legendary stories and known history there are examples of rulers who sought council from persons who were acclaimed, at least by themselves, to be in touch with the otherworldly. As was the legendary Arthur counselled by Merlin, so was queen Elizabeth by John Dee and, perhaps most famous, Nicholas the second by Rasputin.

Whether any of these figures had any of the powers they claimed to have or whether these are the product of hearsay, self-aggrandizement or embellishment, we leave to the discretion of the individual reader. Yet here in BelloLudi Spellbound they certainly are the stuff of wonders!

Stand-alone game or supplement?

In fact: both! With these rules we aim to add an extra layer of magic and monsters to the game for those who love a bit of mythology or weird war in their games. This add-on can be applied to any BelloLudi rules or period you like the most: they are designed to tack on to all other BelloLudi books, be it a skirmish game or big battle. 
Spellbound can also be played as a standalone game with a couple of Casters and/or Monsters per side. As Casters are able to drum up some otherworldly minions, the table might soon be crowded with supernatural lackeys to give you some regiments to command about! Please note that Spellbound is an expansion as well as a stand-alone game. We give the basic rules in this booklet but for advanced rules you still might want a regular BelloLudi ruleset for shooting, melee, etcetera.

In consideration of the fact that not everyone may have a selection of fantasy miniatures, we have tried to make these rules as agnostic as we could. The purpose is very much that people can just play with whatever miniatures they happen to have available.

And now, without further ado, let’s add some magic to the table!

—description from the publisher

In the spring of  2018, Beyoncé became the first ever Black woman to headline Coachella, an annual multiday music and arts festival in California that had been ongoing for nearly 20 years. Understanding the opportunity to put Black cultural expression in the spotlight, she organized a 26-song set with over 2 hours of performance steeped in historically black college and university (HBCU) life and Black feminism. Beyoncé spent months prepping the choreography and symbolism of the show with groups of steppers, gospel singers, majorettes, drumlines, marching bands, and more from many HBCUs. This process is documented in the film, Homecoming, which won a grammy for Best Music Film and is widely aclaimed as the best concert film of all time.

This game celebrates those who performed alongside Beyoncé and the intense training they underwent in preparation for the show. Players will play tricks and increase the power of their cards by learning from the skills of those cards better than themselves. At the end of the game, the player with the most well-trained performers wins.

This is the second game in the Greatest Shows on Earth series.

—description from the designer

Get ready to race to finish line in the space-themed game with the crystal ball to dice or indicate 6 universe events. You can play free way or team up to chase opponents by hitting them to the base.

Watch out for the 6 universe events, it can trap you or accelerate speed. The first player to get all the 3 crafts to the Finish Zone wins the game.

Space-Themed Board Game
Experience classic Ludo reimagined for the modern age! In this exciting adventure, be the first to navigate all 3 crafts to the finish zone.

Free Way 1v1 or Team Up 2v2
Partner up with a friend to challenge another team or engage in free-for all and race to the finish zone!

6 Universe Events
Experience 6 thrilling cosmic surprises that randomly trigger with every roll!

     TELEPORT
     SWIRL
     WORMHOLE
     ONE MORE DICE
     EXCHANGE
     METEORITE


Smart Crystal Ball
Located at the center of the board, the crystal ball acts as a dice and indicates universe events.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Electronic Versions of Non-Electronic Games

description:
Tripleto is a bidding game played with standard deck of cards.
The catch is it's bidding system - lost bid is added to subsequent bids. Therefore - placing high bet and still losing may be actually good. It is 3 player only quick filler with simple rules.

Соображарий: Чебурашка ("Think It Up!: Cheburashka") is another version of the popular board game Think It Up!, this time dedicated to Cheburashka. You have to remember as many words as possible quickly and name them so that they meet certain conditions.  Which of you will be the best Cheburashka fan and win this fun and easy game?

A participant opens a card, reads what's on it aloud, and places it next to a stack of tasks. Then, they demonstrate the letter map so everyone can see. Now that the rules are set, everyone tries to name a word that matches the open task starting with the given letter. Whoever names the word first gets the task card.

Also, in the game, there are special cards called "Two Words!!" and "Three Words!!". When these cards are revealed, you can name two or three words, respectively, following the current task.

The final round is announced when the last letter of the task or the final task is revealed in the deck. At the end of the round, players count their cards. Each player earns one point. The player with the most points wins!

There is also a team mode called "Together With Parents", which is great for playing with families. In this mode, each player works as part of a team, teaching children how to work together. Everyone wins or loses together.

—description from the publisher (translated)

•••
"Соображарий: Чебурашка" – это ещё одна версия хитовой настольной игры "Соображарий", на этот раз посвящённая Чебурашке. Придётся как можно быстрее вспоминать различные слова и называть их, да так, чтобы они подходили под определённые условия. Кто из вас окажется самым преданным фанатом Чебурашки и победит в этой весёлой и простой игре?

Участник игры открывает карту, читает вслух, что на ней, после чего размещает рядом с колодой заданий. Далее он таким же образом демонстрирует карту буквы, чтобы все могли увидеть её. Ну а теперь, когда условия для слова заданы, каждый участник спешит назвать слово, которое подходит открытому заданию и начинается на открытую букву. Тот, кто оказался проворнее остальных и первым назовёт подходящее слово, забирает себе карту задания.

Также в игре присутствуют особые карты "Два слова!!" и "Три слова!!!". При открывании таких карт можно назвать два и три слова соответственно, также следуя текущим заданиям.

Последний раунд объявляется перед тем, как будет открыта последняя буква или задание в колоде. По окончании этого раунда игроки подсчитывают свои карты заданий. Каждая из них приносит 1 очко. У кого больше очков – тот лучше всех соображает, тот и победил!

Правила предлагают командный режим "Вместе с родителями", которые отлично подойдёт для совместной игры в кругу семьи. В этом режиме всем предстоит работать в команде, что научит детей совместному взаимодействию. Участники побеждают или проигрывают все вместе.

—description from the publisher (Russian)

boardgame: Альтушка для скуфа (An alt girl for skoof)
description:
Dear skoofs! We are pleased to welcome you to the physical version of the Internet resource Skufuslugi, which was developed for use in case of complete Internet outages. Charming ladies will come into your offline life and deliver notifications personally. Collect as many Altooshka ("alt girl") cards as possible and receive government benefits!

Альтушка для скуфа ("An alt girl for skoof") is a simple card game for those who are tired of tank games and want the company of charming girls. Receive the attention of beautiful government-provided girls, collect cards and get victory notifications!

Shuffle the deck of alt girl cards and give each player 4 cards. Place the remaining cards face down on the table, and next to them are the victory notification cards. During your turn, point at any player at the table and ask them if they have any alt girl cards. If so, they will give you all their cards for that alt girl and if not, you will draw a card from a shared deck. Remember that you can only ask about alt girl cards that are in your hands.

When you have collected 4 of the same alt girl card, place them in front of you and tell your friends. Take the top card from the stack of victory notifications. Congratulations, you have received a payout and are closer to winning!

When one player picks up the last card of victory notification, the game is over. Count the number of notifications you have collected from all cards and the winner will be the one with the most number!

—description from the publisher (translated)

NOTE:

SKOOF - In Russian contemporary slang, the word means a kind of nasty man about 30-50 years old, looking older than he actually is, due to bad physical condition, obesity, baldness, smoking, bad job, ugly wife and three kids etc.

ALTOOSHKA - literally means alt girl, but in more Russian way. Alt girls are supposed to be girls that wear alternative fashion (goth, emo, etc.), listen to non-mainstream music, and are usually left winged.

•••
Уважаемые скуфы! Нам отрадно видеть вас на целлюлозно-физической версии интернет-ресурса скуфуслуги.рф, которая была разработана специально на случай тотального отключения интернета. Очаровательные девчушки ворвутся в ваш аналоговый быт и вручат уведомления лично в руки. Собирайте как можно больше альтушек и получайте государственные льготы!

"Альтушка для скуфа" – это простая настольная игра для тех, кто устал от танчиков и нуждается в компании очаровательных барышень. Получите всё внимание красавиц, предоставленных государством, соберите одинаковые карты и получите победные уведомления!

Перемешайте колоду альтушек и раздайте всем игрокам по 4 карты. Оставшиеся положите лицевой стороной вниз в центр стола, а рядом – карты победных уведомлений. В свой ход покажите на любого скуфа за столом и спросите, есть ли у него карты конкретной альтушки. Если есть, он отдаёт вам все карты этой альтушки, а если таковых нет, то вы тянете одну карту из общей колоды. Но зарубите на носу: спрашивать можно только карты тех альтушек, которые у вас самих есть на руках.

Когда соберёте 4 карты одной и той же альтушки, выложите их перед собой, похвастайтесь товарищам своим достижением и возьмите верхнюю карту из стопки победных уведомлений. Поздравляю, вы получили выплату и стали на шаг ближе к успеху!

Когда кто-то из скуфов заберёт последнюю карту победных уведомлений, игра закончится. Подсчитайте, сколько уведомлений со скуфуслуг вы получили со всех своих карт, у кого окажется больше, тот и победил!

—description from the publisher (Russian)

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces traffic situations hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach space 85 wins the game.

The game was published between 1945 and 1953.

—user summary

Random people, common ground! Can you find the common ground between two kinds of people? In this hilarious party game, a judge will lay out two wildly different traits. Players choose from cards featuring an individual or group that fills the Inner Circle. The judge then decides which card found the most common ground and wins the round. Add some spice with 69 adult content cards! For 3 or more players ages 18+. Spin Master brings the fun home by offering a range of games for adults, card games for adults, adult games, kids toys & games including baby toys, toys for girls, and board games. Collect your favorite stuffed animals, fidget toys, RC cars, action figures, dolls & accessories and more. From special gifts for men to baby & toddler toys for kids at heart, make your birthday gifts a breeze by shopping our diverse catalog selection. Still hungry for more, explore kids games, pool toys, outdoor games, and monster trucks for any time of the year. Get creative with arts and crafts, building toys and RC cars and take the action to the table with jigsaw puzzles for adults or card games.

—description from the publisher

In this fishy family friendly betting and bluffing game, players take on the roles of fibbing fishercats to try to convince the others that they caught the biggest fish of them all!

Players will go fishing for their initial hand, strategize when to play their larger cards, and make wagers to try to win the most Purrency.

And if a simple betting and bluffing game isn't enough, players have the option to add the powerful catnip cards to the game, allowing them powerful one time use abilities that may sway the course of the game.

At the end, whoever has the most Purrency will be crowned the legendary Purr-fect Fishercat!

—description from the designer

The party game of extremes & everything in between. See how similarly you & your friends feel about ALL the things. Flip the top Spectrum Card for everyone to see (ex. Green Flag to Red Flag). A judge pulls a number card showing a number between 1 & 10 & gives their best hint to help everyone guess the number right on the scale. On the count of 3, everyone reveals their guess. If someone guesses right, both the player & judge get a point. First to 7 wins! Includes “Adults Only” pack.

—description from the publisher

Not everyone’s got the temperament to be a Belter. Plenty of folks crack when the grav cuts out and the lights go off. Plenty more jump at shadows when their flash starts to flicker. Others are too busy watching their O2 to see opportunities.

But that isn’t you. If you can only rely on your gear, your smarts, and your grit, you’re going to make a stack of cred out here among these hulks. Or you’re going to die trying. Either way, you’re going to do it alone.

No one’s coming looking. No one knows you’re here.

HOW IT'S PLAYED

Dead Belt is played by building a Belter and taking them out into the Belt to scavenge randomly-generated starships, using things you already have laying around: a six-sided die, a deck of common playing cards, and a few tokens of whatever sort happen to be close at hand.

With a dozen unique ship deck plans, over 100 flavorful prompts, and plenty of character stats to help you avert certain death, no two ships will ever feel the same. You’ll board these derelict starships, navigate barriers, dodge threats, monitor your air-supply, and salvage as you go.

You’ll deal with all the dangers lurking onboard these starships, push your luck, and finally return to spend your hard-won booty to secure better equipment, improve your skills, pay down your crippling debt, and hopefully, maybe, eventually set yourself up to live out your dreams far from the Belt.

There are three ways to play Dead Belt: Solo, Co-Op, and Rivalry.

Solo is the classic experience, you're truly all alone out there and no one is coming to help you.

In Co-Op, you and a friend join forces, allowing you to push your luck a little further, knowing they'll be there to have your back when things go sideways. Don't make the mistake of thinking this makes the game easy. Start pulling wild stunts and you're just as like to take your partner down with you.

In Rivalry, you and a friend dial the difficulty level up to 12, actively sabotaging each other as you race for the juicy payday hiding on every ship. This mode is not for the faint of heart.

Legend of the Unbound Star is a single player high fantasy dungeon crawler card game, inspired by rogue-lite video games. Built around a randomized booster pack of 18 cards, players fight their way through each room of the dungeon, defeating a variety of monsters in order to reach the boss at end of the dungeon. The randomization of the booster pack is the vehicle for the rogue-lite element of the game: Each pack contains a unique combination of cards, similar to how rogue-lite games have unique gameplay experiences during each play through. As players play through their bespoke experience they'll have opportunities to learn the nuances and characteristics of their cards, simulating players gaining game knowledge with repeated play throughs of rogue-lite and rogue-like games.

Upon opening the pack, a player selects their hero and class to play and then the dungeon and boss combinations to play against. The rest of the cards represent the dangers, events, and treasures that the player will encounter as they move through their world.

Combat is dice-driven, rolling and adding results to a character or monster's stats to see if a strike or ability is successful. If a six-sided die is not readily available then the game has a built-in random number generator on the backs of each Monster card, a player flips the top card over in a cardinal direction, choosing whether or not to rotate it 90° one way or the other first, and then registering the top-most number on the card's edge as their rolled result. This helps make the game completely self-contained, with all components necessary to play included within the pack.

As a player makes their way to the boss of the dungeon they have chances to acquire loot from each enemy they defeat, building up their strength, experience, and skills with each piece they acquire. Upon reaching the boss they may choose to face it or, if they're not ready just yet, take their chance at running one more level instead, flipping over the boss and dungeon cards to generate a new level to play through. The game ends when the boss of the dungeon is defeated or the player dies trying.

—description from the designer

Triple Gem, is a mindful journey into the heart of Buddhist teachings, where players can choose to compete or collaborate and walk the middle way. Learn what it means to seek refuge in the three jewels, Buddha, Dharma, and Sangha. Begin by alleviating your own suffering before extending compassion to others. Gather sacred offerings such as light, incense, tea, fruit and flowers. Discover sutras that guide you toward enlightenment and share your wisdom within the community. Uncover your inner Buddha with mindfulness and illuminate the path for all.

—description from the publisher

In the Clue Junior: Marvel Avengers edition game, players set out discover what Loki stole from Avengers Tower, who he tricked to get it, and when he escaped. Players roll the die to see if they can move their hero character pawn, look under other hero pawns, or peek under equipment tokens for clues. Kids can aim to save the day, marking the notepad as they find clues, eliminate choices, and eventually solve the mystery. Avengers fans will love the featured Marvel characters: Iron Man, Captain America, Hulk, Black Widow, Black Panther, and Captain Marvel. Ready to announce a conclusion? Go for it! The player who figures out Loki's Big Trick is the winner. The game for 2-6 players is a fun game for Marvel fans, and game night with the family.

THE ULTIMATE GAME NIGHT EXPERIENCE Transform any game night with this immersive, competitive strategy game. In just 45 minutes, you and your friends will be hooked, whether they’re Bitcoin fans or total newcomers. Laughter, friendly competition, and excitement take center stage.

QUICK TO LEARN (15 MIN), ENDLESS STRATEGY (AVG 45 MIN TO PLAY) Learn the game in just 15 minutes, but enjoy deep strategy and evolving gameplay that keeps you coming back. Each round presents new challenges and decisions, offering a fresh experience every time.

ENDLESS REPLAYABILITY No two games are alike. The dynamic, fast-paced gameplay ensures multiple rounds of engagement, making it a go-to choice for fun, unpredictable entertainment that never grows stale.

EARN SATOSHIS AS YOU PLAY Manage liquidity, open channels, and earn routing fees as you make smart moves. It’s not just about winning—it’s about skillfully navigating blockchain-inspired challenges to maximize your Satoshi stash.

THE BEST, MOST VISUAL WAY TO UNDERSTAND BITCOIN LAYER 2 This game is an incredibly immersive way to explore Bitcoin’s Layer 2 technology, while keeping things fun and lighthearted. Enjoy yourself while gaining insights into real-world blockchain mechanics—learning is just a bonus.

PERFECT FOR STRATEGY GAME AND BLOCKCHAIN FANS Whether you love strategy or have a passion for blockchain, this game strikes the perfect balance between entertainment and education. It’s suitable for both tech-savvy players and casual gamers alike.

FACE REAL BITCOIN CHALLENGES Overcome routing challenges such as network slowdowns, and griefing attacks in a fast-paced environment. The gameplay is exhilarating, and you’ll find yourself learning about blockchain without even realizing it.

STEP INTO THE FUTURE OF FINANCE As you play, you’ll gain valuable insights into Bitcoin’s Lightning Network, all while enjoying a memorable gaming experience. Who knew learning about finance could be this engaging?

—description from the publisher

Städquiz is a educational trivia game. The theme is about professional cleaning. The game contains 60 cards. On each card there is either a question or a picture. The cards are drawn one after the other. The players can write down the answers to the questions on a paper, or write down what a picture represents. One point is giving for each right answer. When you gone through all the cards, the player with most points are the winner.

In Lost Pirate, players become captains navigating dangerous waters, protecting loot, and facing unpredictable challenges. The game is compact yet rich in strategic depth, featuring 18 double-sided cards with loot on one side and events on the other. Players aim to gather resources, form rewarding symbol combinations, and manage the effects of events while striving to survive the treacherous waves.

The gameplay revolves around collecting resources (Bombs, Food, and Water), fulfilling event requirements, and strategically using loot card effects. Players alternate turns, placing cards in their play area or under the event deck, ensuring high replayability and dynamic strategies. The winner is determined by the amount of loot and points accumulated by the end.

Put A Finger Down is the official party game from the viral trend! Draw from the deck, share stories, and see who will have all five fingers down first!

PUT A FINGER DOWN: is the viral-trend-turned-party game that will have everyone talking!

PLAY WITH ANYONE: meant for 2+ people and simple to play, Put A Finger Down is the perfect card game to spice up holidays, parties, date nights, and more.

HOW TO PLAY: Hold up five fingers and read the card aloud. If it’s a DOWN card and applies to you, put a finger down. But! If it’s an UP card and you’re *that* person, take the penalty by putting a finger up. First with all their fingers down wins!

WHAT’S INSIDE - 400 cards, including 100+ spicy cards packed separately (just in case). For 2-20 players Ages 18+

ENDLESS FUN: each group brings their own fun stories and experiences, making Put A Finger Down the ideal game for gifts, stocking stuffers, & group activities.

—description from the designer

Spitfyre is a fun 'Historo-Fantasy' miniature aerial wargame in which 10mm scale miniatures are used to depict the everyday struggles of standard fantasy races such as Orcs and Dwarves fight out battles with 20th century weaponry.

Dick Garrison Rapid Launch is a stripped down but fully functioning version of our forthcoming The Amazing Interplanetary Adventures of Dick Garrison, Pulp SF wargames rules for 28mm scale miniatures.

You're the doctor in this S.P.E.C.I.A.L. Edition of the classic silly skill game! Operate on Vault Boy as you remove his Dogmeat Breath, Bottle "Knee Cap," "Adam" Bomb and more. Get paid in bottle caps when you successfully operate as a "Curie" or "S.P.E.C.I.A.L.ist." But be careful not to touch his insides, or you might lose your license.

The game was at least given away as a promotional gift by one sauna facility, Sauna 5 Mei in Leiden, in 1982 during the Open Days from September 6 to 11. It is possible that it was used by other facilities as well.

The Dutch Sauna Association, founded in Amersfoort in 1969, merged on October 2, 2012, with the Sauna and Thermen Business Group of RECRON to form Sauna and Wellness Netherlands.

It's a variation on Goose Game with some alternative routes. The rules are titled "The Great National Sauna Game," which does not match the title on the box.

Heist Night takes your next themed party to a new level with hilarious challenges designed to connect you to your friends and create memorable party experiences!

Guests take on a special agent identity and discretely complete tasks throughout the party. The host, well-versed in the language of espionage, will guide your band of thieves throughout many clandestine operations. In the box, you’ll even find party prep ideas, so you and your guests are fully prepared to pull off the perfect score!

boardgame: Karak II: Imperium – Minimus Promo Character
description:
Package contains one promo character card and new tiles

MINIMUS - Pilgrim

MINIMUS used to be a Praetor – the commander of an elite unit of Praetorians, the Emperor’s bodyguards. When the Dark General attacked the country, it was this unit that put itself in the front line of resistance, and Minimus was at its head. How­ ever, the Dark General’s cruel magic overwhelmed Minimus‘ comrades and bound them to the service of evil. Minimus was the only one who resisted the spell and managed to break free of the evil power. He escaped and embarked on a harrowing journey to save all men of honor and avenge his brothers. He builds shrines across the land, serving as a refuge for the defenseless and a place where all those brave enough to stand up to evil gather. When there are enough of them, Minimus will stand at their head and lead them into battle against his former brothers and the one who enslaved them.

boardgame: Polar Vortex: The Battle for Alaska's North Slope
description:
“According to a State Department representative, Russia is not getting back Alaska, which was sold to the United States in the 19th century. This is it, then. And we’ve been waiting for it to be returned any day. Now war is unavoidable.”—January 28, 2024 comment by Dmitry Medvedev, current Deputy Head of the Russian Security Council and close confidant of Russian President Vladimir Putin.

As Vladimir Putin’s regime continues, and as the war in Ukraine demonstrated, there are increasingly more reasons to be concerned with Russia using military force to intimidate and coerce any nation it deems a rival or a threat. Putin himself has frequently claimed that Russia reserves the right to “protect” the rights and interests of Russians and Russian property anywhere in the world, and this includes those in Alaska. As conflicts involving Russia endure or escalate in Ukraine and elsewhere, ways to “protect” Russia’s interests by lashing out and taking revenge cannot be ignored. One very cold place where matters can quickly turn hot is Alaska’s North Slope where the terminus of the Alaskan Oil and LNG Pipelines, and the tanker loading facilities for loading those materials, are located. Taking this vital economic resource would be a highly prized Russian military objective, and one that is vital for the US to defend.

Wargames can be excellent vehicles to explore “what ifs?” Can you do as well or better than your counterparts? Learn and enjoy!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Borrowing The Army Campaign: Campaign wargames rules for campaigns in the American Civil War Eastern Theater
description:
My Dear McClellan, if you don’t want to use the army I should like to borrow it
for a while.
– Abraham Lincoln

Borrowing The Army is a pair of rulesets and a map. These rules allow you to refight the major campaigns of the Eastern Theater of the American Civil War. 
Put yourself in the shoes of Jackson, Lee and Grant and refight history; hopefully you can do better than your historic counter-part.

The campaign ruleset sees you organising your forces in theater, getting your corps and divisions into marching columns at the outset of the campaign. These can maneuver and skirmish their way into position to fight a decisive battle. The campaign rules and map are simple enough to learn, but will give you the opportunity to create your own context for a pivotal engagement.

This is where the tactical ruleset comes into play. These rules focus on cohesion and pressure. How cohesive and functional will your army remain as the pressure of combat wears it down? 
They are something of a sequel to my He Did What Heroes Do ruleset, but the maneuver unit is the brigade rather than the division. These are relatively quick to play (you will only need a tape measure and a fair few D6s) and allow you to fight out the battle both sides have created the conditions for.

The campaign ruleset comes with 9 scenarios with full orders of battle, campaign assets and notes for historical or alternate deployments:

     1861 - Manassas
     1862 - The Peninsula 
     1862 - The Seven Days
     1862 - Northern Virginia
     1862 - Maryland
     1862 - Fredericksburg
     1863 - Chancellorsville
     1863 - Gettysburg
     1864 - Overland


As always, there are plenty of scenario specific special rules to bring to life the situations each side faced. I mention it several times in the rules, but if you want to use my map and campaign rules to generate a battle to fight out with a different ruleset go for it!
So, the board is set and the pieces have been moved. But will history be rewritten? 
Good luck general.

—description from the publisher

Other rules can be used for the tactical part of the game
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
On the eve of the critical battle of Santa Cruz, in which the Japanese ships outnumbered ours more than two to one, I sent my task force commanders this dispatch: ATTACK REPEAT ATTACK.
– Admiral William Halsey

This is a scenario for my ruleset There's The Effing Scheer, and its expansion Striking Effectively First, which allows you to wargame early- to mid-war WW2 naval combat.

The gap between the battles of the Eastern Solomons and Santa Cruz contains a very dramatic shift in the balance of power in the South Pacific, one which was accomplished without a single plane howling down on a flat-top. The Americans had sent The Big E back for repairs, but still had Wasp, Hornet and Saratoga on station. Two Japanese submarine torpedo spreads later and Wasp had to be abandoned and Saratoga is sent home. This is as much as the Kido Butai achieved at Midway and the Eastern Solomons combined (sinking one carrier and damaging another).

And this is what fascinates me about WW2 carrier warfare, despite the overwhelming power of Japanese surface units; Henderson Field and two American carriers were enough to keep these at arms length. Halsey turns this up to 11, not only is he outnumbered in almost every area, he continues going on the offensive with his only two carriers. The fog of war with carrier warfare means that even outnumbered 5-2 in carriers, the Americans could still feasibly pick apart the Japanese force. Unless of course the Japanese spot them first...

There are some similarities to this scenario and the Eastern Solomons in terms of objectives, but the forces are different. The Japanese should be able to defeat the American carriers and secure Henderson Field, but there is always that scary question mark over their objective with the vagaries of 1940s carrier warfare. 
Will Henderson Field hold out and harry the Japanese carriers?
Will the Japanese battlecruisers get a chance to face off against South Dakota? 
Who will spot the enemy so that they can strike effectively first? 
I suppose this all comes down to you.

Happy gaming

—description from the publisher

Are you tired of boring dinner parties and dull game nights?

It’s time to spice things up with bad advice, your new favorite card game.

Designed to create moments that are equally uncomfortable and hilarious, bad advice is the adult party game your friends will rave about. If you like dark humor and irreverent jokes, this is the best game for you.

Packed with 30 cringe-worthy situation cards and 120 inappropriate advice cards, bring bad advice to your next social event to unleash chaos.

Each round a player will read out a situation card and each player will submit the funniest and worst advice card from their hand. the baddest advice wins.

description:
In Hell Raisers in Kanawha County, two players take on the role of two mining companies — Cabin Creek Consolidated Coal Company and Paint Creek Collieries Company — while the third player takes on the role of the uprising Labor Movement.

The game begins in early 1912 when the miners at Paint Creek demanded increased wages, increased transparency surrounding their payment, abolishment of Company-owned stores, acceptance of the union, and their right to free speech and peaceful assembly. The estimated cost of these demands was 15 cents (worth $3.66 today) per worker per day and was promptly refused. The workers of Paint Creek went on strike, and Cabin Creek followed shortly after.

Within the next year, martial law would be declared three times within the region. Countless people were evicted from their homes, the legendary labor leader Mary "Mother" Jones was sentenced to twenty years in penitentiary for inciting riot by reading the constitution, and an armored train with mounted machine guns was used to attack the strikers.

Hell Raisers offers an asymmetric twist on a Pax-style game. As one of the game's two mining companies, you'll concern yourself with speculation and mining logistics as you fight for larger market share. Meanwhile, the labor player will attempt to drum up popular support for their demands, shining a light on incredible acts of abuse and corporate maleficence while organizing strikes — but they must be careful. If they go too far, they may find the corporations bringing in paid thugs or even the national guard!

Hell Raisers includes modes for one, two, and three players and is easily playable in 60-90 minutes.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Before the game begins, a postal manager is chosen from among the players; he leads the game, manages the cash register and delivers the postal items.

Each player now pays 5 coins into the cash register and places his figure on number 1. Then each player rolls the die once and moves his figure forward. The postal manager shuffles all the postal items before the game begins and places them face down in a stack in front of him. As soon as a player lands on one of the red spaces, he picks up the top postal item and delivers it to the player.

If the player receives a postal order or a money letter, for example, the postal manager pays him as many coins as are stated on the postal item.

If, on the other hand, he receives a payment order, he must pay as much into the cash register as is stated on it. Postcards and letters cost nothing. However, if someone receives an unstamped letter or postcard, they must pay two coins or 1 coin for postage; for packages, 1 coin for delivery.

The game was published about 1900.

Whoever reaches number 30 counts from number 1 and so on in a circle until all postal items have been delivered. The postal manager then takes the contents of the cash register as his salary. Now the game starts again until every player has taken on the role of manager. At the end, the player who has received the most coins from the post office wins.

—user summary

description:
The Biscuit Sisters and Gravy Brothers are a feudin' and a fightin’, and you'd be wise not to get in the way of their sibling scuffles! They are the two new groups introduced in this set, the first booster pack expansion for Squirmish: The Card Game of Brawling Beasties (3.0 edition)!

While this set has enough cards to play a game of Squirmish by itself, it is even better with a deck of 36-70 cards. The Squirmish card game is for two to four players ages twelve and up. This set includes 24 new game cards featuring new characters with new, unique abilities and loads of hilarity.

It’s a very old game from the 1960s or 1970s.

This game featured a course in the shape of a bicycle, consisting of 216 main spaces (excluding detours). Each player had a team of cyclists. A die was rolled to move forward by the number shown, except for the mountain section (blue area), where there was a value correlation (e.g., a roll of 6 allowed moving 4 spaces, and rolls of 1, 2, or 3 allowed moving 1 space).

There were two types of cards—blue and pink—that were drawn when landing on orange spaces. Pink cards allowed advancing the indicated number of spaces (start), while blue cards were bonuses kept for later use, triggered by event spaces (e.g., punctures or chain breaks).

There were many types of spaces with different effects.

—user summary

I Served with Heroes Revised 1.1 is a World War II squad-based wargame with roleplaying elements. 
Each model is a soldier that you control on the battlefield in squad vs. squad combat. 
Now, with any wargame, you can never get the realism, but you can get close to what you are trying to achieve, so each soldier has a personality that you can name, have skills, and feel the loss on the field when he dies, unlike the massive platoon-sized games where you don’t care if one dies.

The revised update includes the following:

     Two new armies (Japanese and Russian, including the Germans and the Americans)
     New rules on diseases in the game playing in the jungle
     New skills for your troopers to have
     Army abilities on the battlefield
     Weapons for each army type
     Squad fireteams revised
     Sheets for each army type


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Realms: Rise of Empires – Scenario 11B
description: This backer-exclusive expansion for Star Realms Rise of Empire includes a scenario envelope, oversized scenario card, rules card, Victory Point card, and 3 Hero cards.

A real estate trading game based on the Monopoly game but with local Detroit themed products and places.

Theme
Players take the part of property owners, attempting to buy and then develop their property. Income is gained by other players visiting their properties and money is spent when they visit properties belonging to other players. When times get tough, players may have to mortgage their properties to raise cash for fines, taxes and other misfortunes.

Gameplay
On their turn, a player rolls two dice and moves that number of spaces around the board. If the player lands on an as-yet-unowned property, he has the opportunity to buy it and add it to his portfolio or allow the bank to auction it to the highest bidder. Player may build branch offices on these spaces, generating even more income from opponents who land there. If they land on a property owned by another player, they must pay that player rent according to the value of the property and any branch office on it.

Goal
The goal of the game is to be the last player remaining with any money.

Get ready for Ferro Sequence—the ultimate board game for family nights, outdoor fun, and travel. Designed for 2-12 players, Ferro Sequence is easy to pick up and hard to put down, bringing a blend of strategy and chance that’s fun for teens, adults, and everyone in between. With its magnetic folding board (19.95" x 15.5") and 135 strong magnetic chips (50 green, 50 blue, and 35 red), this game lets you play smoothly on any surface, even uneven ones, without worrying about pieces slipping out of place. To play, just draw a card, place a chip, and aim to get five in a row while blocking your opponents. But keep an eye out—the wild cards can change the game in an instant! Ferro Sequence includes two decks of cards and complete instructions for effortless setup, whether you’re at home or on the go. It’s a thoughtful gift for board game lovers and a must-have for any game collection. Bring the fun wherever you are with Ferro Sequence!

—description from the publisher

You love reading books and enjoy spending time at your local library. But who would have guessed it could transform into a nightmare after midnight? As the darkest hours unfold, navigate the eerie halls of the haunted library, relying on your knowledge, dwindling stamina, and defense (thick books) to make it through the night!

Escape the Living Library is a game that only needs a sheet to print, and 7 dice to play. The player will go through 6 floors of the haunted library (featuring 6 sections: art, history, science, literature, fiction, and horror) and try to make it alive by escaping without running out of the vitals. Use a pair of dice for roll-and-place actions, 3 dice to track your Knowledge, Stamina, and Defense, and 1 die to represent yourself as you explore the library, dodging spooky surprises! The 7th die is for the re-roll mechanism.

It's a game where both strategy and luck are key, so choose your moves wisely. Otherwise, before you know it - you might end up as one of the library's ghosts!

—description from the designer

Test your mettle in this competition to be the greatest burger chef of them all! Compete with other players in this fast-paced puzzle game to see who can complete the ever-more complicated orders to perfection in the shortest steps possible. Think you can do better than other players? Step up to the grill and prove it!

Complete the burger orders in the fewest steps possible to earn fries. Claim your title as the greatest burger chef by winning 5 fries!
. 40 unique challenges, hundreds of solutions.
. No down time.
. Play with up to six players. Solo mode included.

—description from the publisher

Fast set-collection game where you should pick animals of 3 different colors by placing your dice on piles of cards during auction phase. Auction is rather simple (the higher dice result always wins). Each color consists of 18 cards and you can collect certain card and receive victory points only if all of your previous cards of the same color are less or bigger. For example, if you have 6 and 9 cards of the red color in your collection, you can pick 5 or 10 of the red color. But you can't choose 7 or 8.
Points are awarded for the different sets (same color, same value, consecutive values in the same color).
All images are taken from the Lion King movie.

Introducing Dangerous Space, the followup to the breakout 2023 success Dungeon Pages.

Dangerous Space is a dungeon crawl experience in futuristic starships.

This solo, roll & write game has a tactical emphasis on dice management and careful placement.

With more puzzle-optimizing challenges than Dungeon Pages, combined with an all new tech tree, you’ll find yourself racing the clock, dodging enemy laser blasts and confronting some deep space terrors.

2025 Year-Long Adventure Set

Every Friday in 2025 you will receive a new Dangerous Space sheet. Each new sheet will bring a 2024 character and ship back, but with an all new mission. There are 4 mission types, each very different from what we've seen so far in Dangerous Space.

Missions will have you defusing bombs, delivering kits, repairing the ship, and dealing with quarantine outbreaks.

Includes: 

     4 new mission types for all existing heroes
     Delivers every Friday in 2025


Features: 

     Fully playable on its own or combine with existing Dangerous Space sheets

boardgame: いきものボックス (IKIMONO BOX) Vol.1
description:
This box contains two games.
"Savanna Ten" is irresistible for dilemma and crunching interaction on which square to place one of up to three animals in order to become King of Savanna
"Kani nari Ebi," is new style shogi, we use crabs that can move as many squares as we want, or shrimp that can move only one square diagonally,  aims to win by utilizing tricky 'sea currents' and 'bonding'.

boardgame: Uncrossing Stars- a solitaire roll & write survival game
description:
The Story: 
“Then I defy you, stars!”
Oh Romeo, oh Romeo, you did not just say that, oh Romeo!
As the stars, you will try to mitigate Romeo’s doom as he defies everything that could have otherwise gone right!

In this solitaire roll & write game, time everything right, so that Romeo safely arrives in time for Juliet to wake up and for them to elope before Verona finds out!

—description from the designer

boardgame: What's Eating the Danish Prince?: A solitaire roll & write crisis management game
description:
It’s not easy being the Prince of Denmark. While struggling with the grief of your father’s death and your mother’s marriage to your uncle, a ghost suddenly appears, claiming that your uncle killed your father! While you try to keep your sanity, you need to verify the ghost’s claim, test your uncle’s guilt, make your mother see sense, deal with your girlfriend, all before your uncle deems you too dangerous and has you shipped away to England!

In this solitaire roll & write, complete all five tasks of the Danish Prince before he goes all out mad, runs out of alibis, or gets shipped off to England.

—description from the designer

FACER is a unique card game that turns facial recognition into a fun and strategic challenge. Set in a world where every card features a distinct face, FACER tasks players with identifying similarities between these faces using information from a machine learning algorithm. The result is a game that blends strategy, perception, and a touch of data science in an accessible and engaging way.

In FACER, each card showcases a unique face on the front, while the back provides similarity scores to other cards in the deck. Gameplay is flexible, offering two main modes: Competitive Mode, where players vie to assemble the strongest hand of similar faces, and Cooperative Mode, where players team up to find increasingly similar pairs. Both modes challenge players' observational skills and strategic thinking.

Whether competing or cooperating, FACER offers a quick-to-learn experience with surprising depth. Players of all ages can enjoy the thrill of discovery while gaining an intuitive understanding of how machine learning works. With endless combinations of faces and strategies, every game is a fresh and exciting puzzle.

Ideal for game nights, family gatherings, or casual play, FACER is more than just a game—it’s a journey into the art and science of facial recognition, made fun for everyone.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Cheese Security, Inc.: A solitaire movement puzzle game
description:
Stop the Rats. Avoid the Traps. Protect the Cheese!

Cheese Security, Inc. is a solitaire movement puzzle on a grid where your choices influence the Rats' movements and the Rats' movements provide you with your limited choices. Complete the winning conditions quickly because the Rats run on a timer. And every time the timer runs out, the Rats increase the number of losing conditions on the grid.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dino Mites: A solitaire skirmish puzzle game
description:
Mites on DInosaurs! Dino MItes! Move the Knight Mite and plant charges to defeat the enemies of the Realm. Or just duel. Either way, claim victory with a bang!

In this solitaire skirmish puzzle, outwit the Boss Mite through well placed and well-timed bombs and tar pits... Before the Boss Mite outwits you.

—description from the designer

Symmetric abstract game for three players. Assemble triplets of cards to move players on the board, make them draw or discard cards or give them or take the gems. Acquiring gems of all three colors means winning the game.

A Story You Must Experience

In 1995, as the Czech Republic is still recovering from the dissolution of Czechoslovakia, you find yourself in the midst of a police investigation. Lieutenant Matěj Sláma, a promising investigator of the Prague Criminal Police, is found dead in Smíchov, shot with a single bullet. Was it an accident? Or a calculated murder tied to dangerous forces in the shadows?

Your mission is to unravel this complex case and uncover the truth. The mysterious murder of a police officer becomes a symbol of the struggle between justice and lawlessness, against the backdrop of a growing web of organized crime. AN INTERACTIVE DETECTIVE GAME!

Step into the dark world of the 1990s, where hope and danger collide. Prepare for the thrilling detective game “THE 90s,” which immerses you in the gripping story of Lieutenant Matěj Sláma’s murder. Delve into the evil lurking behind the facade of the newly formed Czech Republic and bring justice to a fallen officer.

A Unique Experience

1 FOLDER, 32 AUTHENTIC PIECES OF EVIDENCE, plus 2 videos introducing and concluding the story, 8 interrogation audio recordings, 1 radio broadcast recording, an authentic authorization letter, a soundtrack playlist, interactive elements, and much more.

What Makes “THE 90s” Stand Out?

•    Realistic Evidence: Receive a sealed evidence package, including a real bullet casing, Matěj Sláma’s car keys, and interrogation audio recordings.
    •    Detailed Materials: Investigate various evidence types such as newspaper articles, autopsy reports, personal files, business cards, flyers, receipts, tickets, and photographs that will help you unmask the murderer.
    •    Online Elements: Interact with websites designed to mimic the aesthetics and functionality of the 1990s. Use contact information, listen to interrogation audio recordings, tune in to radio shows, and analyze video footage to gather additional clues and information. Once you believe you have solved the case, submit your solution via the STATE POLICE INTRANET, where you’ll verify your answers and determine whether you’ve identified the correct perpetrator(s).
    •    Immersive Storytelling: Every detail has been meticulously crafted to draw you into the narrative and create an authentic experience you’ll love. Players feel like real detectives solving a criminal case, working with evidence to unravel an unforgettable and captivating story.

Who Is the Game For?

This game is perfect for mystery and crime story enthusiasts aged 13 and up. Ideal for individuals, friends, family gatherings, as a themed party game, or a team-building activity. The game can be played only once. However, once completed, you can reset and reorganize it to share the experience with someone else.

Get Ready for the Challenge!

Uncover the secrets lurking in the shadows of the 1990s. Order “THE 90s” and embark on a thrilling detective journey that will lead you to the shocking truth!

—description from the designer

boardgame: I Want to Talk to the Manager!
description:
Everyone has a bit of "Karen" in them, in this new party game find out who really is the biggest Karen/Ken of the group.
Players vote on who they feel will react in the most "Karen" kind of way to various scenarios that many of us go through daily: 

     Been cut off in traffic?
     Didn't get the sandwich you ordered?
     Just feel you need special attention some days?


We've all been there! But who is the biggest Karen of the game?
Find out here!

—description from the publisher

In Decolar Corp., players take on the role of cadets aiming to reach incredible (and dangerous) destinations across the galaxy. Each round, they must carefully plan their moves by playing F.L.U. cards (the ship's precious fuel) onto the table.

Some cards are played face-up, while others are placed face-down, keeping their information hidden from other players. Each card provides a certain amount of F.L.U. and may trigger special effects under specific conditions, adding tension and unpredictability to every decision.

When a player believes they have enough fuel to complete the journey, they declare "Vai Decolar!" (Take Off!), claiming the role of Pilot. At that moment, a frantic race begins: the other players scramble to be the first to hit the launch button and secure the position of Co-Pilot.

Blast off toward the stars and, with a bit of luck, make it to your destination!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Welcome to Poltergeist! Become a paranormal investigator, go into the house to get the objects and learn the secrets of poltergeist. But be careful, ghosts can ruin your game.
