A game where you will try your luck and collect the objects to get the highest score possible, a race against time where you will try to become the best investigator.

Unleash the cryptid hunter in you. Test your wiles against up to 4 other players. Control 1 of 5 unique cryptid hunter cards as you vie for success, fame, and survival in the ancient misty hills of West Virginia. This game is easy to play, hard to win.

This is the revision of our original West Virginia Edition! This solid 90-card deck lets you test your wiles against up to 4 other players. Control 1 of 5 unique cryptid hunter cards as you vie for success, fame, and survival. Playing is easy. Winning is not.

Have you ever wondered what it may be like to be a cryptid hunter? How cool it would be to prove to the world that some elusive, mythical creature of lore actually exists? Now you can.

Players control a unique cryptid hunter card as they collect leads, evidence, encounters, and items. All the while, competing to be the first cryptid hunter to survive 3 encounters OR collect the most fame points.

This deck is an updated revision of our original West Virginia edition, Cryptid Hunter: The Elusive Card Game.

—description from the publisher
Try to get holes in as few rolls as possible. Get exact matches for style points that can be used to reuse spent dice.

The prince of the night has deployed his most powerful weapon. The Moroi is the terrible result of a mighty Draco's transformation into a Vampire. A lethal mix that has given birth to a nightmarish creature now placed in defense of the Castle... will you be crazy enough to face it?

Сборная солянка ("Hotchpotch") is a fast card game with the goal to recieve minimum number of penalty points. It is played over a series of rounds (depends on the number of players).

During each round, players play cards from their hands, arranging them into groups based on the same value. The player must play a combination of cards so that the value of the cards they played is equal to or higher than the value played by the previous player. If the player cannot meet this requirement, they must play the same number of cards with the lowest value. The last card remaining in the player's hand that has the highest value is their penalty point.

The game called Hotchpotch because all cards represent food ingredients and by discarding them you all together "cook this dish".

Pssst . . . hey, guess what? The pigeons are listening! We know it sounds ridiculous, but it’s true. Have you ever wondered how news outlets get their scoops? It’s all thanks to those flying sources competing to break the next big story. And now, you’re one of them!

In this spy pigeon racing game, your mission is to navigate your winged witness along its flight path and back to News Coop where you create the headlines! But beware: watchful drones lie in wait, and your competition will do everything possible to sabotage your journey across London’s skies. You must be prepared to respond in kind, so let’s see what you’re made of.

This game is for gossips and people who will do what it takes to win. After all, there’s nothing like a bit of healthy competition.

In Timetable Tag, you compete with your family and friends in a game of Tag across Europe: Draw cards, move on the gameboard strategically and sneak to your destination!

And be quick, because the chasers are always right behind you!

boardgame: Fortune and Glory: Tools of the Trade
description:
The Tools of the Trade Supplement is a Web-exclusive for Fortune and Glory and features 15 new game cards and one Cover card. The cards are exclusive to this supplement and will not be available anywhere else.

Traveling the globe and visiting every dark corner along the way can be dangerous, but your Hero will succeed if they have the tools of the trade!

The new cards in the Tools of the Trade Supplement are designed to be shuffled right into the existing Fortune and Glory card decks.  It includes new Gear cards, Common Items, City cards, and Event cards.

—description from the publisher

Trifle is based on the classic Italian card game, Briscola. You can play it competitively with older children or as a breezy pastime with other adults.

It is a may-follow trick-taker. Which means, you can always play whichever card you want. No restrictions.

Cards are worth the number printed on them (if a card doesn't have a number, it is worth zero).  Your goal is to capture the most card points. Catch more than 30 and you win a game. Win three games to win a match.

—description from the designer

Life and Beyond is a strategic board game that immerses players in a journey of personal growth and financial success. The game begins with players starting from modest beginnings, navigating life’s milestones, and striving to achieve their goals through thoughtful decision-making and calculated risks.

The gameplay involves rolling dice to progress, drawing cards, and managing resources like money and assets. Players can negotiate, form alliances, or compete directly with each other, mirroring real-life dynamics of cooperation and competition. The balance between ambition and strategy is key, as every choice impacts the path to success.

The ultimate goal of the game is to build a life of abundance while overcoming challenges. With its emphasis on balance and replayability, Life and Beyond offers players a unique experience every time, revealing insights about their personalities and strategies.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Mission Control: Critical Orbit – Crisis Command
description: Crisis Command is the first large expansion for Mission Control: Critical Orbit.  It adds (1) three new City Boards (one for each mission control station); (2) a deck of System Check event cards for use by the Mercury III player; and (3) three new Conduit tiles with new shapes and mechanics.

In Overlords in Training you take on the roles of aspirant Overlords. Attack other players via the Fighters you've acquired, hunt the Giants Deep for Crystaline to boost your options, protect your basses from destruction, and prepare to duel against the Boss himself, Mike!

Overlords in Training uses 1 of 4 playmats to represent your trainee.  On your turn you can utilize the cards to battle your opponents, dig for crystalline to power your character, draw up to three cards, or reserve your action to fight the boss later.

Koinobia is a cooperative game of strategy, created and illustrated by the Brothers of the Taizé Community. It is a simple, yet intriguing journey into the life of the monastic communities founded by Abba Pachomius in the fourth century, in Egypt.

—description from the publisher

There is little more fun than playing: board games, card games, solo games, cooperative games, from beginner to expert games.

With Twist it could be even more fun. Because after playing a few times, you might want to play with a twist. Many people come up with their own rules or variations. In Twist, you bring a new twist to each game. You choose a card from one of the categories and apply the new or extra game rule.

—description from the publisher

Prairie Wolf, Old Man Coyote, Song Dog...soon to be gone, faded out into the past, into only our memory. Or is its heart without end — extinction with a smirk, snickering at the poison and the traps — remaining against the onslaught, the mad march of time? Heat rising from the cities, warming the carrion ideas that fill its belly, the Prairie Wolf curling up beside us, Old Man Coyote dancing across our progress, and the sweet secure Song Dog serenading itself into us forever.

Time and the Coyote is a head-to-head, asymmetric game that pits the trickster Coyote against the progress of Time. Time lays out its cards, attempting to discover and contaminate Places of Power as the Coyote uses its dreams and cunning to protect the Places of Power. At the end of the game, the entity that controls the most Places of Power wins.

—description from the designer

A cartoony, kitchen & household themed family board game, where the objective is to collect 5 eggs before the other players. It is fast-paced with a high degree of uncertainty.  The game mechanics are made to be simple and easy to learn, being a roll and move game with a simple deck of cards.

The game begins with everyone in the Front Hall, and each player draws a hole card. A player's card determines whether or not they are a chicken. A player can choose to reveal if they are a chicken in the Bathroom to collect an egg. Players can also accuse other players of being a chicken when a player is in the Kitchen. Players either gain or lose eggs when they make correct accusations, and if a player is caught as a chicken in the kitchen they lose an egg.

The game can change pace rapidly, and can have swift lead changes. In larger play groups (4-6 players), it is very difficult to determine who will win at most given points in time. It is made so there is a large amount of luck, with some strategic decisions that can be made. However, the benefit or drawback from a player's decisions are only realizable in hindsight. This creates an exciting and fun environment for players of any level of board-game background experience. Great for beginners and intended to be taking purely for fun.

Jujuman is a single-sheet roll-and-write game of exploration and potion crafting. Each player becomes a mystical potion maker navigating a hex-based map.

On a turn, three dice (red, green, and black) are rolled for all players. Each player uses the dice to simultaneously fill potion bottles with strategic number placements, move across the map, and collect ingredients like mushrooms, feathers, and worms. Players score points by completing potion rows, collecting ingredients, and invoking the magical power of the number nine, with special abilities granted by ingredient rewards that can modify dice values.

Playable from 1-99 players, Jujuman offers a unique blend of dice management, spatial navigation, and magical theme, challenging players to become the most skilled potion maker by balancing precise bottle filling, strategic movement, and ingredient collection.

—description from the designer

Fear has settled in on the community of Dirty Ankle with the continual increase of UFO sightings reported in the media. As local prankster or mischievous aliens, players will want to add to the panic by creating crop circles on farms in the community. Can you complete three crop circles overnight without getting caught by the authorities?

Cleephas Spangler is back! Yes, that lovable, affable redneck from Dirty Ankle (Fire Ant Mow Down) is back for another madcap adventure. No longer content with mowing over fire ant hills and tipping cows, he has set his sights on fooling his neighbors that an alien invasion is descending on his town by creating crop circles. Little does he know that aliens are really here and creating crop circles as well.

Crop Circles is a two player, dice drafting, roll & write game where each player take turns drafting dice and filling in the patterns that match the dice chosen. As your crop circles get filled in, player’s choices become more limited. If a player cannot fill in a shape on any of their crop circles, they will fill in a box containing the walkie talkie icon. This indicates the authorities (police or AFS) are one step closer to catching a player. If a player fill is their sixth walkie talkie icon, they lose their game. If a player completes three crop circles, they win.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Golden Cup: Extra Champion Cards Set
description: Promotional item with three new champion cards. One for each deck: gold, silver, and bronze.

Running a hot dog truck is a daily struggle.

When you’re busy, you can’t keep up with demand, and if you make too much, customers might not show up.

It’s a great feeling to be able to provide the right amount of food on days when you can’t predict the volume.

In a world where the power of gemstones drives everything, they are not just beautiful objects but possess immense power that can control nature and the world itself. These gemstones are divided into seven main types: Ruby of Fire, Sapphire of Ice, Emerald of Wind, Garnet of Earth, Amethyst of Darkness, Topaz of Light, and Diamond of Time.

Gemmage Festival is the name of the unique magic tournament where magical girls are chosen from gems. To compete in a magic competition in a variety of different skills, such as speed of casting, summoning, magic gems, and the size of magic. The first magical girl to score 30 points will be the winner of the festival.

—description from the designer

PinchBowl is a light dexterity and strategy game inspired by American football, where players "pinch" a die with a controlled flick to advance on the field and score points through Touchdowns, Field Goals, and daring 2-Point Conversions.

Combining finesse, tactical decision-making, and a touch of luck, PinchBowl recreates the thrill of football in a quirky print-and-play format. Each team balances stamina management, risky Fumble and Pick calls, and precise execution to outplay their opponent.

The core mechanic revolves around advancing the die by achieving the same face (Scramble), the opposite face (Pass), or either (Passing on the Run). The unique pinching action adds a layer of physical skill to every play, transforming the game into a lively mini-sport.

Welcome to the Revolution!
Transport yourself to the universe of George Orwell's “Animal Farm”: The farm is on the boil and it's time to elect a new leader. Use all your influence and persuasion during discussions, navigate the intrigues that will arise, while trying to create a fair and equal society.
Make decisions, face moral dilemmas and find out if you have what it takes to lead the revolution!

boardgame: Feluś i Gucio grają w emocje
description:
Another game in the series, but the first with such a diverse set of games and activities! Ideal as a stand-alone educational package and a supplement to the book "Feluś and Gucio learn about emotions". The game supports proper emotional development and helps parents in difficult conversations with their child about emotions.

The 3 basic types of games in this set are: memo, charades and conversations. This type of activity is particularly conducive to learning to recognize, name and express emotions, which is the main goal of the game.

We all have feelings. We feel each of them differently. They are all necessary and equally important to us. That is why it is worth getting to know them and being able to talk about them. This game makes it easier.

—description from the publisher

The box includes 2 games and 2 activities:
Game 1: Memory with tiles featuring various emtions.
Game 2: Charades, in which players present and guess emotions.
Game 2 (advanced variant): Charades with talking allowed, in which players present or describe specific situations that includes particular emotions (from drawn tile) and a specific sense (from rolled die).
Activity 1: Conversation about particular emotions and situations.
Activity 2: Matching emotion tiles with particular situation card.

—user summary
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: This manual introduces Outworld, a dangerous realm of monsters and heroes. It is the land where the heroes' exploits take place. This manual provides everything you need to tie the quests together. Now your heroes, at the end of each quest, will embark on dangerous journeys; they may visit small villages or the Kingdom Capital, spending their hard-earned gold on new items, equipment and magical items. Along the way they may meet friends who will try to help them or enemies who will try to thwart them.

Introduction:Bleven (from Middle English: beleven, meaning "to believe") is a strategic card game for two or four players (in teams), played with a French deck of 40 cards (the 8s, 9s, and 10s are excluded; or J, Q and K can be replaced by these). White stones (1 point) and black stones (5 points) are used for betting.

				
				
					Definitions:
     The field is the set of three cards held by each player.
     The reserve is the set of discarded cards from each player, placed face up.
     A trick is a pair of cards faced off during the trick phase.
     A crown is a unit of victory equivalent to 30 stones.
     The declarer is the player who makes the initial declaration. 
     The replier is the opponent to the left of the declarer, responsible for responding to bets. If the replier resigns, the opponent to the right of the declarer becomes the new replier.


				
				
					Card values:
     J, Q, and K are worth 8, 9, and 10 respectively.
     Ace is worth 1.
     Other cards retain their nominal value.


				
				
					Initial distribution:The dealer and cutter are chosen randomly. The cutter starts the round. Each player receives three cards, forming their initial field. The remaining deck is placed as the draw pile.

				
				
					Discard phase:Starting with the cutter, do one of the following:


     Draw a card from the deck and discard one of your cards face up into your reserve, always keeping three cards in your field.
     Declare if you have 15 or more points by saying "I declare it" instead of drawing and discarding. The other players, starting from the left, say "I stay" if they wish to play their cards or "I leave" if they prefer not to. After you draw, you may not declare that turn.


If a player has five cards in his reserve, he has the option of saying "I stay" or "I leave."

				
				
					Declaration and bets:The declarer makes a declaration:


     I bet X to Y tricks: Bets a certain amount of stones and predicts how many tricks they will win (0, 1, 2, or 3).


Predictions:
The declarer can specify if they will win tricks with or without position:


     Without position: Declare only the number of tricks (0, 1, 2 or 3), with normal points based on triangular scoring.
     With position: Indicate which tricks you will win. Each correct position counts as an additional trick in triangular scoring, increasing the score.
         If you declare 2 tricks, you may choose 1 to 2 specific positions (e.g. "1st and 2nd").
         If you declare 3 tricks, you may choose 1 to 3 specific positions (e.g. "1st, 2nd and 3rd").
    


The replier responds to the challenge:


     I deduce: Accepts the bet and believes declarer's prediction.
     I suspect: Accepts the bet, but doubts declarer's prediction.
     I deduce with X plus: Raises the bet, but remains the contestant.
     I suspect with X plus: Raises the bet and questions the prediction, remaining the contestant.
     I deduce with X plus to Y tricks: Raises the bet and bets a new equal or greater number of tricks, becoming the new declarer.
     I suspect with X plus to Y tricks: Raises the bet, questions the previous prediction, and bets a new equal or greater number of tricks, becoming the new declarer.
     I'm in: Only used in the trick phase. Indicates that a player accepts the bet.
     I'm out: Declines the challenge, automatically losing the round and giving 1 additional stone to each opposing player. In pairs games, the turn to respond passes to the partner.


				
				
					Trick phase:Both players face their cards up in ascending order (from lowest to highest).


     The declarer places his lowest card.
     Players to the left respond with his lowest card.
     The winner of the trick starts the next one. Ties are resolved in the following suit order: Spades > Clubs > Diamonds > Hearts.


If the declarer guesses his number of tricks correctly:

     The declarer wins the points and the bet if the replier does not guess correctly.
     The declarer only wins the points if the replier guesses correctly.


If the declarer does not guess his number of tricks correctly:

     The replier only wins the bet.


Three tricks are played. The player or team that fulfills the declared prediction wins the round.

After the first or second trick is played, any player may initiate a side bet. This side bet allows the initial prediction to be changed or introduces new bets:


     I bet X: A new bet is made without modifying the declaration.
     I bet X to Y tricks: A new bet is made and the declaration is modified. The person who says this will be the new declarer unless he is already the declarer.


Opponent's response: If the declaration was not modified, he can respond "I'm in" to accept the bet or "I'm out" to reject it, otherwise you can respond by affirming or questioning it just as you do after a declaration. You cannot declare your position at this phase.

In team games, if a member has less than 15 points at the start of this phase, he or she may not play his or her cards. He or she must place them face up on his or her field, and his or her partner will be solely responsible for competing against the team's tricks. However, if the game is played with two players, anyone who does not reach 15 points at the end of this phase automatically loses the round.

				
				
					Score:Both predictions, with or without position, use triangular scoring:

Prediction without position: It is calculated by adding the sequence of natural numbers from 1 to the number of tricks correctly played.


     1 trick: 1 point (1)
     2 tricks: 3 points (1 + 2)
     3 tricks: 6 points (1 + 2 + 3)


Prediction with position: Points are calculated without position and the correct position is added as an additional trick:


     1 trick + 1 position: 3 points (1 + 2)
     2 tricks + 1 position: 6 points (1 + 2 + 3)
     2 tricks + 2 positions: 10 points (1 + 2 + 3 + 4)
     3 tricks + 1 position: 10 points (1 + 2 + 3 + 4)
     3 tricks + 2 positions: 15 points (1 + 2 + 3 + 4 + 5)
     3 tricks + 3 positions: 21 points (1 + 2 + 3 + 4 + 5 + 6)


Partial correct guesses with position:

     If declarer wins the tricks but not the positions, he scores only the points for the tricks.
     If he wins the positions but not the tricks, he scores the points for the correct positions.
     If he guesses both the tricks and the positions, he scores the combined score.


				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when one of the players accumulates the agreed number of crowns at the beginning (1, 2, or 3). The winner is the one who first reaches that amount.

—description from the designer

Get ready to hustle in That’s Crypto Bro!

Your goal? Earn the most points by collecting hidden coins. Use action cards to peek at values, steal from players, or rig the market. But be careful – values can crash or skyrocket at any moment! ?

Think you’ve got what it takes to be the top Crypto Bro? Grab your copy now and find out!

Introduction:Cuiquindi (from Old Norse: kvikindi, meaning "creature") is a strategy game for two players: white and black. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 6 cells per side, but boards of 5 or 7 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A creature is a maximal set of connected stones of the same color. A single stone is also a creature.
     The size of a creature is the number of stones it contains.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first and then they alternate. On your turn, perform the following actions in this order:


     Move a stone of your color across connected empty cells, provided that it touches exactly one friendly creature in each of the cells moved. Single-stone creatures can't be moved.
     Place a stone of your color in an empty cell in a straight line toward the moved stone, without jumping over other stones and without merging two or more friendly creatures. The placed stone cannot be adjacent to the group of the moved stone.
     Pass your turn if you cannot perform actions 1 and 2 in that order; otherwise, passing is not allowed.


				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when both players pass on consecutive turns. At the end of the game, points are calculated by adding up all creatures of different sizes, with half a point going to the player with the largest creature. In the case of a tie, the next largest creature is awarded up to a difference.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places one black creature of two stones and one white creature of two stones, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the two adjacent stones pie rule.

—description from the designer

Discover new gameplay modes, a solo mode, and updated card versions in this expansion of the 2024 bestseller *Gods*.

All the characters from *Destripando la Historia* and some new ones await you in this thrilling addition. The base game is required to play this expansion.

Decrypt is a cooperative deduction board game for 2-4 players where you fight monsters, complete quests, and loot the crypt. Using a Clue-inspired deduction system, players must figure out which items exploit each monster’s weaknesses while managing their resources and health.

The deduction system in Decrypt requires players to determine which three items are needed to defeat each monster. Each player knows the weaknesses of their assigned monster but cannot directly share this information. Players receive feedback on how many correct items they used during combat, allowing them to narrow down the possibilities through trial and error. Players are encouraged to share their trials, deductions, and theories with one another.

The game is played on a modular board, where you construct the dungeon tile by tile, creating a unique crypt layout in every game. The game’s second phase begins when players find the staircase, flipping the board to reveal the Catacombs. This new level increases the difficulty, with stronger monsters, higher rewards, and specific objectives such as rescuing a damsel or defeating bosses.

The "Stands" system ties health, inventory, and armor into one mechanic, requiring players to make strategic choices about what to carry and what to leave behind. Each character token has three slots which can hold armor, coins, chests, and more. These not only affect your armor value, but are used as your character's health. Each time a character takes damage they remove one of their stands. When no stands remain, they are sent back to the start of the Crypt.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Lâche pas la savonette Extra Large
description:
The Social Card game where there are no winners, just one loser.
Don’t Drop The Soap is a dark humored, fast-paced card game of hot potato set in a prison shower. Players take the role of prison inmates who are, literally, trying to screw each other over. The objective of the game is to not be the player who drops the soap.
Each turn players must play one action card from their hand. The game ends when a player runs out of options and is forced to play a soap card. Although you may never win a game of ‘Don’t Drop The Soap’ you can influence the game in such a way that you decides who loses.

Are you skilled enough to get the booty you want? Or will you end up as someone else prison bitch? You do what you gotta do in here, just Don’t Drop The Soap.

Don’t Drop The Soap is made up of a single deck of 59 Cards. The game is designed to be played by 3 – 6 players. The rules are very simple; Play a card from your hand and do what the card says. Most rounds last around 10 to 15 minutes, giving ample opportunities for revenge and grudge matches.

Contains Deathrow the first expansion for Don’t drop the soap with additional cards!
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Take on the role of squirrels racing up a tree in Scurry Up!. Simultaneously compete for valuable spaces on branches. If too many squirrels jump into a space, they knock each other off, and nobody gets the nuts, berries, or flowers!

This fast paced family strategy game is for 3-6 players and plays in about 20 minutes.

—description from the publisher

Become the host of the hugely popular in Poland YouTube channel MaturaToBzdura. Ask your friends questions from one of the six categories. However, not only knowledge but also cunning is important. You can win the game by smart betting as well.

In each round, one player becomes the Host, while the others answer the question. There are two ways to win: Knowledge-scoring the appropriate number of points - and Cunning-discarding most of your tokens. First, the Responders bet on which of their three cards and which difficulty level the Host will choose. If they bet correctly, they discard their tokens. Then the Host reads the question. For a correct answer, Responders receive points depending on the question's difficulty. Wrong answers allow the Host to discard tokens. The game ends when either player has gained enough Knowledge points or when either player has discarded so many Cunning tokens that they can no longer bet.

boardgame: Twilight City: Maurice – The Cooperative Expansion
description:
The partisans in Twilight City had been waiting for months for the arrival of the agent who had infiltrated the regime's system and uncovered its weaknesses. Maurice is here, and with his help and everyone's cooperation, the overthrow of power can finally succeed!

The expansion brings new goal cards, character tokens, Maurice's miniature figure, and a brand new cooperative game mode. A real game changer!

boardgame: Hail Caesar: The Wars of the Roses
description:
Battle for control of England’s future in the bloody Wars of the Roses!

This supplement lets you fight through the savage medieval civil wars that raged across England in the Fifteenth Century using Hail Caesar. Fought between the great houses of Lancaster and York, the Wars of the Roses would see hugely bloody battles, bitter familial feuds, and the downfall of the Plantagenet dynasty, and go on to inspire the hugely popular Game of Thrones.

Late Medieval warfare is ideally suited to wargaming with Hail Caesar, and this tome is packed with rules for representing the massed ranks of archers, billmen, and men-at-arms so common in English armies of the period, and the tactics they used. 
Accompanying these rules is a wide selection of scenarios allowing you to re-fight some of the most dramatic battles ever fought on English soil – Tewkesbury, St Albans, Towton, and of course Bosworth – as well as plenty of historical inspiration for painting and modelling!

—description from the publisher

This expansion introduces new, sophisticated wine types to the game, allowing you to produce and sell rosé and dessert wines too, with the usual accurate theming of the base game. It also adds a new building card: the Delicatessen shop, and an extra action in each season, as well as a new feature that lets you choose one more action during the year.
 The Rosé & Dessert Wine expansion is only playable with the Cuvée base game.

Hungarian description:

A Cuvée új kiegészítőjében lehetőséged van rozé- és desszertbor készítésére és értékesítésére is. Mindez természetesen az alapjáték híresen pontos témafeldolgozásával történik. A Rosé & Dessert Wine emellett hoz egy új épületkártyát, a Delicatessen nevű csemegeboltot, valamint az Intéző nevű funkciót, amellyel egy extra akció érhető el.

Csak a Cuvée alapjátékkal együtt játszható kiegészítő.

Travel to Hogwarts this Advent!

Have you been waiting for years for the one letter that will turn your life upside down? With this Advent calendar, your long-awaited dream will finally come true.

Open a letter every day and face the puzzles and tasks contained in it. Whether it's magic potions, a broom flying lesson or fortune-telling: with a little skill and a pinch of luck, you can succeed in the entrance exams.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Klappentext

Reise diesen Advent nach Hogwarts!

Wartest du seit Jahren auf den einen Brief, der dein Leben auf den Kopf stellen wird? mit diesem Adventskalender wird dein lang ersehnter Traum endlich wahr.

Öffne jeden Tag einen Brief und stell dich den darin enthaltenen Rätseln und Aufgaben. Ob Zaubertränke, Besenflugstunde oder Wahrsagen: Mit etwas Geschick und einer Priese Glück gelingt es dir, erfolgreich bei den Aufnahmeprüfungen abzuschneiden.

—description from the publisher
family: Misc: Advent / Christmas / Jule calendar game

description:
The Deluxe Edition contains the base game Food Chain Magnate and the expansion Food Chain Magnate: The Ketchup Mechanism & Other Ideas in a single box featuring entirely new artwork, deluxe wooden tokens, and tabletop organizers!

It is planned as a one-off print run and there is no retail release coming for this edition.

So what does the Deluxe Edition Offer?

- All new artwork for every component
- Employee Organizer and Storage solution
- Screen-printed restaurants & coffee shops
- Wooden food tokens in full-color heat-transferred detail
- Storage trays for your food tokens and money bills
- Triple-layered Milestone Trackers
- Both the base game and expansion in a single box

The Deluxe Edition focuses on wood and punchboard components and does NOT include miniatures from the Special Edition, also part of the same Gamefound campaign.

In Поэзиум ("Poetrium"), participants compose a collective poem while trying to understand one another. The storyteller chooses one of the laid-out story cards, and the other players attempt to guess which one it is. To give them a clue, the storyteller places a line card in front of the players, which will serve as the opening line of their shared poem.

The storyteller's goal is to ensure their story card is guessed, but not immediately — it should take a few turns. Each guesser’s objective is to be the first to correctly identify the chosen card.

Players take turns either trying to guess the card or adding new line cards to the shared poem. The storyteller then indicates whether the added line cards align with the chosen story card. If the storyteller finds a line card to be a good clue, they move the token to the "checkmark" cell. If not, it goes to the "cross" cell.

If a player guesses incorrectly, they lose one point, and the guessed card is flipped face down. If the correct card is named, the round ends, and points are tallied. The guesser receives points equal to the number of their tokens on the line cards. The storyteller receives the same amount. All other players gain points based on the number of their tokens on line cards placed in the "checkmark" cells.

Поэзиум also includes a two-player mode, where the players are joined by a virtual opponent — the great poet Alexander Sergeyevich Pushkin.

Same as Grouille, new questions, new box.
Grouille! is a trivia game from Quebec. Every player starts with 10 questions cards. The winner is the first player to get rid of all their question cards.

Question cards are discarded after being read out loud. The player who shouts the correct answer before everyone else gets to read the question card next.

Many cities want to contribute to the protection of cryptids. To do so, they make a request to protect a certain type of cryptid and promise to sponsor its resettlement. Thus, every time you resettle the cryptids upon a city request you get a money reward or invaluable experience!

The mini expansion "CITIES" includes 12 cards: 11 gorgeous city post-cards which were designed to add up even more variety to the game mechanics + 1 memo card.

This is the Medieval theme ADULT PARTY/ DRINKING GAME that allows "drunk you" to use your possible wit and strategy to dominate and rule over your friends. Using mystery cards and seemingly underhanded tactics to turn the tide of the game. Flip through each card of your rows until you overturn an Advance Card. First player to overturn an Advance card in their last row becomes the new king/winner. The last player in the game is labeled as the peasant/loser. With a bit of help from the luck gods, you may just become the king of DOORZ!

Once you are crowned king by the game the fun continues as you will be able to make 1 rule for all of your subjects to follow per round. We love this game because it brings a totally new experience to each time you play by way of game mechanics and your creativity in messing with your own friends. The objective of the game is for you to power trip over your friends by making them do hilarious and embarrassing tasks or maybe even the occasional shot... just remember "It's all fun and games until it's your turn!"


    This is a Party/Drinking Game for adults NOT a trading or collectible card game*


—description from the designer

Welcome to the Renaissance, where merchants' thirst for gold is rivaled only by their desire for silver. As you amass your initial wealth, your first move is to dispatch prospectors to delve into the depths of silver mines, extracting this coveted resource.

With silver in hand, you can now invest in the expertise of scholars and artisans, gaining crucial advantages over your rivals. Moreover, you'll embark on a mission to improve the harsh conditions of mining by constructing Underground houses and Cathedrals within the mines themselves.

But your ambitions know no bounds. As you expand your operations, you'll venture into new territories, previously beyond your reach, sending your prospectors to uncover even more lucrative mines. Are you ready to navigate the treacherous waters of Renaissance commerce and claim your place among the elite merchants of the era?

Tiletum: Prospect for Silver introduces a unique resource, Silver. To gain it, you need to utilize a new type of action that controls your Prospectors, moving them on Underground maps in search of sources of this precious material. Moreover, Silver allows you to acquire Technologies that vastly enhance your game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dragon Dale: Die Magie der Runensteine
description: Adding some abilities to the rune stones.

boardgame: The Witch, the Scoop, and the Magic Soup
description:
Every once in a while, your friend, who is a witch, invites you and other witches to come together for a fun little potluck party. Everyone brings what they find the most delicious: crunchy spiders, spicy mandrake roots, freshly gathered toadstools — to throw all of it into one gigantic cauldron and brew the best soup ever!

There’s a tiny problem, though: some of the food, now bubbling tantalizingly in the golden broth, just isn’t there for you to enjoy. Really, be careful — or you’ll get a big, nasty wart on your nose in no time! And a single wart isn’t half bad, but what if you get five? You’ll have to leave the party early, that’s for sure!

The game consists of matches between pairs of players interchanging clockwise. During each such match both players will be using Magic Scoops to pull Food Cards from the Cauldron as quickly as possible. Each player can take part in up to 6 matches over the course of the game.

Each match is divided into 2 phases:


     Race - players simultaneously use magnetic scoups to get foodies from the cauldron, this takes 3-5 seconds
     Snack - check what you got on your scoups and see whether your witch could eat that or there would be any special effects


—description from the publisher

The World is played with a standard 78-card Tarot deck. You play as Wisdom, charged with creating a new world out of the four elements and the energy of the universe. Each turn you will draw one energy card and place it in one of the four corners of the new world, attempting to fulfill the element card placed there. Each element has a special property which will help to fulfill it. In addition, Wisdom herself has two special abilities: to move energy from one element to another, or to remove an energy card completely.

—description from the designer

Bird Dog is a trick taking game akin to Spades, Hearts, and Turn Up Euchre.

It was developed by Nathanial Meadow and friends in 2017.

In this game, players are trying to capture tricks and collect pairs of face cards.  Points are awarded for face card pairs rather than the number of tricks.  This along with the inclusion of the Bird Dog card makes for a rather interesting trick taking game.

Bird Dog uses a 24 card Euchre deck that includes the 9’s – Aces.  Seven cards are dealt to each player.

Once the trump is determined the card that is the same rank and color as the trump bid card becomes the Bird Dog which is the highest ranking card for the hand.

At the end of the round, players look at the cards they have captured.  Any face card pairs are worth points. First to 11 points wins.

This is the train Dixie told me about... the 6:10 through Canyon Diablo. On board is my way out. The solution to my problems. One last heist.

Canyon Diablo is a quick ‘Press Your Luck’ solo card game set in the late 1800’s Wild West. Get the combination for the safe and get away to win the game!

—description from the designer

The year is 2060. You are a netrunner - a skilled hacker with a cybernetic interface implanted into your body. You’ve been hired by a nameless information broker to break into Akimi Cyber Tech (ACT) - a Mega Corp that manufactures the most sophisticated Cyberware in the world. The risk is high but so is the reward. Obtain the 5 data blocks the broker is after and get paid.

You begin the game on the first floor of the Akimi Corporation. There are 5 Floors to complete before facing the final boss. The goal of each floor is to obtain one data block and one key card which gives you access to the elevator. Once you have obtained these two items you progress to the next floor. Keycards can be discovered in locations you explore or by killing an enemy that is carrying one. You earn data blocks by hacking into the computer network and bypassing encrpytions at ACT.

Data Fortress is a difficult but rewarding solo adventure that will have you coming back for more.

—description from the designer

Minihex is a tribute developed by Rômulo Machado to the game «The Mini Quest» by Robertson Sondoh Jr.

Defeat the Evil that will arrive in the world! To do this, you will need to earn money by completing 4 quests, and thus buy equipment that will make your character stronger for the final battle against the Evil!
You can play alone or with up to 3 players!

Components Needed (double or triple according to players count):


     6 six-sided dice
     5 cubes of 8 mm
     1 miniature with at least 25 mm base, and heroic aspect!
     1 miniature with at least 25 mm base, and evil aspect!

Each Player is a member of the Kaboomski Family, a family of Salvoslavakian spies working undercover in New Clearland. As they meet their neighbors, they work against each other to secure the most Intel for their Fearsome Leader.

The game is divided into 2 phases, Scheme and Action. During the Scheme Phase, a new neighbor arrives at the door. In a randomized order, the players play cards face down from a selection of Espionage, Interrogation, Sabotage, and Manipulation Cards. Players must deduce what cards their opponents played and what effect that might have on the cards around them. During the Action Phase, the cards are revealed and resolved, right to left. Whoever gains the most Intel learns that Neighbor's Secret, a one time use ability to help win the game.

After 3 rounds, each with 2 Neighbors, are played, whoever has the most total Intel wins!

Welcome to Valles Marineris International Park!

Valles Marineris was established in 2236 with the goal of creating a place for families and outdoor enthusiasts to appreciate the incredible and bizarre beauty of one of Mars’ most treasured landmarks.

On a hiking trip, a violent dust storm blankets the planet, forcing your group to take shelter. You must locate waypoints and rush back to base camp before your oxygen runs out.

Players will be given Heading cards, each with a trail color, a compass heading and distance. Using these cards players will work together to meet objectives given on the waypoint cards. If players are unable to continue finding objectives, they must draw a hazard card which will deplete their oxygen level faster. Three equipment tokens are given to each player which you can use at crucial times to help you reach base. The game ends when you are able to reach base and still have oxygen remaining. Three trails are included with different difficulty levels.

Rules for a head-to-head game are also included where players will go against each other attempting to complete the most waypoints. Whichever player has the most points at the end is the winner.

—description from the designer

36 is a seeding game where 2 or 3 players strive to get rid of their counters as soon as possible.

The board is modular, created by assembling 9 different tiles into a 3x3 square with 36 cells. There is also an advanced mode where there is more freedom in the arrangement of tiles.

MATERIAL


     9 square tiles with different distribution of red, white and black areas (see 'files' section).
     36 counters in 3 colours (12 each).
     One pawn for each colour.


—description from the designer

There is an old shipwreck in the coral reef. Dive down and play Tic Tac Toe with the sea creatures! As soon as you cover three animals in a row with shells, you get points. The special thing: It is not only scored on one level, but also when animals swim directly on top of each other!

Aim of the game 
You try to form as many rows of three as possible and get points for doing so. The first person to reach 10 points wins. The game ends early when a water level is completely covered. In this case, the player with the most points wins.

As a member of an elite order of mages, you must harness your magical skills to defeat the dark sorcerer Dalthor. Roll dice to cast a variety of spells—each with unique effects and risks—strategically planning your moves to outwit and overpower
Dalthor’s dark magic.

—description from the designer
It’s a variant of Parcheesi on map of The Netherlands. Instead if dice you use cards and you travel from one Nivon location to another.

Humans have successfully established major colonies across the lunar surface. In Rock Stars, you take on the role of a hired mercenary for the Asteroid Defense Force (ADF), patrolling your sector of space above the Moon and eliminating threatening asteroids with an arsenal of weapons at your disposal. The work is treacherous, but the financial rewards are high. Will you become the wealthiest mercenary in the ADF?

Players take turns drawing Asteroid cards and have 3 rounds to destroy it using their Weapons dice before it collides into the moon. Each asteroid has a composition of minerals (iron, ice, or silicon) that you will have to eliminate. Each side of your dice has a weapon that will remove that mineral. Beware of malfunctions that will freeze dice from play. Use your mercenaries special ability to create stasis fields around the asteroid or even hurl one into your opponent! The player with the most credits at the end of the game after subtracting penalties is the winner!

—description from the designer

You are jolted awake by a blood-curdling scream, followed by an eerie silence. Heart racing, you light a candle, its flickering flame casting trembling shadows as you move cautiously through your darkened house. There, on the floor, is your father, gravely wounded - his life fading with every breath.

Outside, your once peaceful village now teems with nightmarish creatures—horrific beasts that slither from the forest’s edge. But the worst has yet to come: your sister is gone, taken by a monster that can exist only in the darkest corner of the woods. A shadowed fiend that feeds on fear, preying upon the
young and vulnerable.

With the village in chaos and time running out, there is only one path before you and your faithful dog. Into the cursed heart of Ruinwood, where horrors unimaginable lurk. It is there, in the depths of that forsaken place, that you must venture—if you are to bring her back.

Explore Ruinwood by moving your hero along 13 connecting mini Maps by placing two Exploration cards beside two compass headings. Choose one card to resolve and move in that direction. Exploration cards include things like enemies, loot, healing pools, mushroom patches, traps, or clear paths. Battle enemies by expending Stamina points and flipping over multi purpose Outcome cards to determine damage. Upgrade your hero's stats, cast spells, and find upgrades to help you battle your way through 5 unique levels and over 20 enemies in the dark forest in your search for your sister.

—description from the designer

Temple Dice is a push your luck, dice stacking game where you must use all of your wits as an explorer & discover the most gems.

But beware... The temple is crawling with snakes & deadly traps!

Each player begins their turn by rolling 7 custom dice and will attempt to stack water, earth, and sky stones (dice) - each with different point values. Water stones must begin the stack and you may play as many of the same type as you’d like. Earth stones must always be placed on top of water stones, and sky stones must always be placed on top of earth stones. Temple guards and snakes will freeze dice from play unless you roll a machete! At the end of 3 rolls players add their total points for the round and the player with the highest score earns a gem! The player with the most gems at the end of the game wins.

-description from designer

Experience the thrill of the catch - day or night, rain or shine! Play with friends or by yourself. With several tournaments to compete in, 7 unique species of fish to catch, and many lures and tackle to enhance your gameplay, we’re sure you’ll have a great day on the lake every time you play!

To begin, players first select a tournament to compete in. Tournaments take place over the course of one day. After choosing a boat, you can set off to locate fish using your fish finder cards and lure cards. Use dice to 'fight the fish' as each fish will have its own strength depending on the size and species. Certain time of day and lures are better at catching fish. Lures and tackle can be upgraded by selling caught fish at the Bait and Tackle Shop. Manage your fuel by filling up at the gas station. Bring your largest catches back to the Tournament location at the end of the day to determine the winner!

—description from the designer

Tripped is a sprint across North America, players traveling to various cities before sprinting home for the win.

The game site notes, "The game gets competitive immediately as each player takes off to a different city in each of the 6 longitudes, racing to get back home first. This could take you to places you already know (NEW YORK, TORONTO, MEXICO CITY), to places you’d like to go (BERMUDA, ANCHORAGE, SANTO DOMINGO), to places you can’t pronounce (QAQORTOQ, INUKJUAK, UTQIAGVIK)."

A turn consists of 1. Taking Trips, 2. Playing Tripped cards, and 3. Drawing more cards.

Kampf is played by two players on a board that is divided into 81 equal-sized, alternating black and white squares and has a dash-dotted line.
There are a total of 36 pieces in the game: 18 white and 18 black, which are set up as two armies to fight against each other. Each of the two armies is made up of ten higher combat pieces (2 planes, 2 submarines, 2 airships, 2 tanks, 1 flag, 1 general) and eight soldiers.
The aim of the fight is to force the opposing party's main piece, the flag, to surrender.
The different pieces have different rules for movement and capturing. Each player may only move one piece at a time. A piece that is touched must be moved. You can only capture one piece at a time with each move. The capturing piece is always placed on the square of the captured piece. All main pieces (except the soldiers, who can only move and capture in a forward direction) can move and capture forwards and backwards.
If an attacking piece could be captured by an enemy piece after it has captured, the attacked piece is considered to be covered. Covered pieces may not be captured. If a piece attacks the enemy flag, i.e. if it makes a move which would enable it to press the enemy flag on its next move, this attack move must always be accompanied by the cry "Kampf" so that the opponent is made aware of this attack.

—user summary

You are an agent to the stars. Your objective is to cast your actors in as many roles as possible. By casting your actors in the most roles you will win movie cards with point values.  The agent with the most points at the end of game wins and will be declared the most powerful agent in Hollywood!

A movie is cast by revealing a movie card. Each agent is then dealt five actor cards. Each actor has a certain star power along with skills they have. 5 role cards are revealed, and agents must decide which actor they would like to try out for a role. Whichever agent successfully casts their actors in the biggest roles wins that movie card. A new movie is announced and play continues until an award is handed out for best film!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Moby Dick: Wrath of the Seas
description:
Dive into the epic tale of Moby Dick, based on Herman Melville’s American classic.

In this asymmetric strategy game, players take on the roles of either the legendary white whale Moby Dick, or the daring crew of a whaleboat.

As Moby Dick, you control the mighty white whale and other whales appearing in the ocean. You score points by guiding whales safely to feeding grounds, by killing whaleboats crewmembers or by releasing whale bodies killed by sailors. Moby Dick alternates between lurking beneath the waves and emerging dramatically, leaving the crew to deduce the whale's location.

As the crew, you control a whaleboat to hunt whales and bring them back to the Pequod, your main boat, to extract precious oil. Sailor players use cards to move through dangerous waters and ride currents, while trying to keep the boat steady and escape Moby Dick’s attacks.

Will you harness the power of the ocean as Moby Dick, or brave the waves as the relentless whaleboat crew? The hunt is on!

—description from the publisher

Toppa is a minimal 2 player tabletop game that can be surprisingly strategic. With super fast set-up and quick gameplay (each game only lasting 5-10 minutes).

Every Toppa game starts with players setting up the board their opponent will start the game with, placing 9 color chips (3 of 3 different colors) into the 3x3 grid and covering them with the stackable checkers. Saving the 3 extra checkers for later potential gameplay. Once that's done, players exchange boards.

The object of the game is very simple, find the 3 green chips in the board in front of you. With each color you reveal, a new action or event happens.


     Reveal a green chip. One step closer to winning!
     1st Red chip revealed. Rotate the board in front of you 90 degrees.
     2nd Red chip revealed. Rotate the board in front of you 180 degrees.
     3rd Red chip revealed. Exchange boards with your opponent!


Will your memory help you find the green chips?

—description from the publisher

1610: Notorious Pirate Captain Henry Douglas discovered, half buried, an old fiddle whose music had the power to command all the creatures in the sea - including the mighty kraken. He played the instrument and summoned the monster to destroy his adversaries vessels, and amassed a fortune in plundered gold and jewels. The wanted pirate, nearing capture, hid the fiddle on the island from which it came, along with his stolen treasure. A map was drawn, but the Captain was caught by his enemies before he could return. Sailing back to port to face judgment, the ship was swallowed by the kraken and the Captain was never seen again.

100 Years Later: Sailing around the waters of Haven Harbor has become treacherous. The story of Captain Douglas, his magic fiddle, and the mighty kraken has become legend. A legend you know first hand to be true. As a young buccaneer you had encountered the kraken, who destroyed your ship and killed many of your crew. You have made a vow to find Captain Douglas’s treasure and destroy the monster who now commands the sea.

Players will choose a Captain and a ship and move along a randomly created board trying to locate 3 pieces of a treasure map from 3 factions, the Cannibals, the Black Fangs, and the King's Army. Moving your ship requires you to place two Exploration cards beside your compass and resolve one. Exploration cards may contain enemy ships, storms, clear skies, or even the dreaded Kraken! Battle occurs by deciding how many shots from your cannon you'd like to fire and flipping over multi use cards to see how much damage you've dealt. Once the 3 pieces of the treasure map have been secured, you must sail to Treasure Cove, get the Magic Fiddle, kill the Kraken and win the game!

—description from the designer

Propaganda is a negotiation game for 3-5 players. In it 2 players take photos of dice they've rolled onto the board and use those photos to try and convince the Superpowers (the other players) that they're the one losing and needs the most help. The Superpowers then each award points to one player, based solely on the information the other players have given, with the goal of evening the scores. But players can use cropped photos, half-truths, and information to make their case.

—description from the designer

boardgame: CLOSE THE CASE: Wo ist David M.?
description:
Everyone in Kremen knows the charming start-up founder David Mechtig. The centre of attention, he always finds the right words to get to his destination. But now nobody knows where he is. David has disappeared. But why?

As the son of an influential family, envy is never far away. And certainly not everyone is happy with his leadership style. Or is it something else entirely? One thing is clear, only real professional detectives can solve this case. Dive into the 2nd case in the ‘CLOSE THE CASE’ series and put your skills to the test!

Physical and digital evidence and clues await you to analyse during your investigation. It's all about keeping track of all the details in the files, websites, videos and much more evidence.

Work as a team to solve the mystery at the end: Where is David M. and who is responsible for his disappearance?

You are the last hope!

Prepare yourself for an epic showdown of Ultraman! Keep your Ultraman characters in the arena as you outwit and defeat your opponent during training.

Let the game commence!

Two players select their preferred Ultraman standees to participate in the game across two modes: Basic mode and Advanced mode. In Ultra mode, players have the opportunity to utilize the special and distinctive abilities of each Ultraman.  This brings immense enjoyment to Ultraman enthusiasts, just like you and me.

boardgame: Mini Meeple Melee: Fire and Ice
description:
Mini Meeple Melee: Fire and Ice is a stand-alone expansion for Mini Meeple Melee. It can be played on its own or combined with the base game for more variety and a larger player count. Combining both versions increases the player count to 8.

This expansion adds 6 new classes, a new boss, and 2 new terrain types.

Base game description: 
Mini Meeple Melee is a combat arena game you can carry anywhere! 1-4 players battle in 1 of 3 different modes to the death. You can fight in free-for-all fights, team matches, or boss battles. For such a small package, the battlefield is surprisingly modular. Select your terrain, place the crates and magic circles, and position the tins. Then you're ready to drop in and start brawling!

Each turn you can perform 2 actions. Basic actions include moving, resting, or taking cover. You can also use 1 of your characters unique skills, usually to attack your opponents.

This game is quick, fun, & simple. So it's great for anyone, anytime, anywhere!

—description from the publisher

Let's go on an adventure!

This expansion for Mini Meeple Melee completely changes the gameplay from a free-for-all battle arena to a semi-cooperative dungeon crawl!

The Adventure mode is for 1-4 players
Players will work together to defeat each randomly generated dungeons boss and their minions. Players can search the battlefield for potions to help them in their fight, but be careful! The battlefield is littered with deadly traps and wondering enemies.
While team work is necessary, the player that defeats the most enemies and collects the most potions will be the true winner!

Enemies can be controlled by a "dungeon master" or fully automated.

1-8 players, if you own both the base game and the Fire and Ice expansion, can that on waves of enemies in Survival mode.

This expansion requires the Mini Meeple Melee base game OR the Mini Meeple Melee: Fire and Ice stand-alone expansion to play.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Chapolin Colorado em: Pepe, Já Tirei a Vela!
description: In the episode Chapolin Colorado em: Pepe, Já Tirei a Vela ("Chapolin We See, Brains Don't Know"), an insane inventor and his faithful sidekick Pepe put their diabolical mind-switching tests into practice. They just didn't count on Red Thumb's cunning to thwart their plans! A surreal plot that ends with a barking scientist, a talking dog and the arrest of the controversial inventor. That's where our adventure begins... Follow me the Good Ones! In "Pepe, I've already taken the candle!", each player takes on the role of an ambitious scientist who returns to the scene of the crime to steal the "genius" projects left there. The first player to leave the lab with three blueprints or the first to survive Chapolin Colorado wins the match. Use cards to open or close doors, push opponents, walk, hide or brainswitch with other characters, and you can even control the puppy Tirôles or Chapolin Colorado. It is also possible to play in pairs!

This is a card game based upon the Dragonlance Saga.  It was only released in Japan.

The game includes 108 cards.  The cards consist of Characters, Dragons and armies. Rules are in Japanese.

Simultaneous strategy game.

Embark on a simultaneous treasure hunt! Sail in a changing archipelago full of cannibals, corrupt trading posts and voodoo mysteries. Arm your ship, recruit, plunder and trade while being careful of the East India Companies. The outcome is never certain and each turn is thrilling!
For games full of bluff and suspense! Choose your navigation in secret then move all at the same time. No wait for the others' turns!

After navigation, depending on whether ships are in contact or 1 away, there is boarding or cannon fire. Then, everyone plays their actions at the same time.

To win, 2 different epic objectives:

Unearth the hidden treasure of Armand the chatterbox before the others OR collect 25 gold coins. The passage of the Rougeaud of the East India Companies at the Bastion Rouge is an event not to be missed because its capture then brings 15 coins.

Islands with unique actions, infinite combos, asymmetrical ships. Players explore unusual and personal tactics.

Each game is unique thanks to the modular board, the asymmetry of the 10 ships and the 24 unique buccaneers. As exciting with 2 players as with 3, 4 or 5, experts and youngsters alike will be in their element.

Currently the game is in French with other languages options possible depending on the KS success

---

Jeu de stratégie en simultané.

Embarquez dans une chasse au trésor en simultané ! Voguez dans un archipel changeant truffé de cannibales, de comptoirs corrompus et de mystères vaudous. Armez votre bateau, recrutez, pillez et commercez en prenant garde aux Compagnies des Indes. Le dénouement n'est jamais certain et chaque tour donne le frisson !
Pour des parties pleines de bluff et de suspense ! Choisissez votre navigation en secret puis bougez tous en même temps. On n'attend pas les tours des autres !
Après la navigation, selon si des bateaux sont au contact ou à 1 de distance, il y a abordage ou tirs de canons. Ensuite, tout le monde joue ses actions en même temps.

Pour gagner, 2 objectifs épiques différents :

Déterrez le trésor caché d’ Armand le baratineur avant les autres OU amassez 25 pièces d’or. Le passage du Rougeaud des Compagnies des Indes au Bastion Rouge est un évènement à ne pas manquer car sa prise rapporte alors 15 pièces.

Des îles aux actions uniques, des combos infinis, des navires asymétriques. Les joueurs explorent des tactiques insolites et personnelles.

Chaque partie est unique grâce au plateau modulable, à l'asymétrie des 10 navires et aux 24 boucaniers uniques. Aussi palpitant à 2 joueurs qu'à 3, 4 ou 5, les experts comme les plus jeunes seront dans leur élément.

You and other players have been invited to participate in the Grand Worldwide Quilting Blitz! You will face off against any number of opponents as you test your ability to plot the best quilt design, combine quilting patterns, and plan ahead. At the end of the game, the player with the most points wins!

The Patchwork Planning expansion adds several layers of strategy and dice mitigation you can use to help you as you design and sew the highest scoring quilt!

—description from the publisher

This is an expansion for Mini Meeple Melee featuring:
-New Components- A gold coin, gold dice, extra terrain cards, and a sleeve to hold the base game and both other expansions.
-Terrain tiles- 40 magnetic tiles for players customize and design their own battlefield layouts. 
-3 new mini game modes- Mini games designed from ideas from crowdfunding backers.

     Crate Toss- Toss magnetic crates and aim for the bullseye.
     Obstacle Course- Roll and race your way to the finish while slowing down your opponents.
     Mini Meeple Gamble- A minimalist reworking of the standard game with a press-your-luck twist to combat.


—description from the publisher

This is an abstract game about pattern matching, achieved by dropping colored chips into one of 7 vertical channels. The patterns to be matched are determined by cards drawn from a target pattern deck. The first to match 5 patterns wins.

Gameplay is reminiscent of the popular Connect Four games, with some innovative twists. The target patterns consist of several of 4 different color 'dots', either in a straight line or an angle. The chips are two-sided with different colors on each side: dark green with pale yellow OR dark blue with pale red-rose. Each player rolls a die each turn to determine which color they must play. Players know the opponent's target, depicted in the mirror colors on the reverse of the target cards, and thus play defensive blocking while seeking to create their own target pattern.

After a pattern match occurs, play continues until one player attains a fifth match. Chips are not removed from the grid board unless the grid is fully filled.

Players should expect longer gameplay time and more complex planning than in Connect Four.

Easy game in learn, Easy game to play in 5 minutes.
Guess correctly where the questioner wants to go and which they will do there in 5 minutes.

The Player can guess correctly, Where the questioner wants to go, will get that card is point.

But who can guess correctly, which they will do there, will get that card and steal point.

And then who get that card is point, is the next questioner of the next game.

—description from the publisher

An old research station stands before you. Back in the day it was used to observe alien specimens and invent new medicine based on what they find but years have passed and the station is now devoid of life. There has to be some research left by the scientists before they left that explains what happened here. Will you manage to find it before the station falls into a complete ruin?

Explore the Andromeda Nexus in the new map expansion for Diceolated in both solo and multiplayer versions. Unlock rooms, scan the keys and explore the research station to the fullest.

—description from the publisher

Visit the residential space station that orbits the Centauri Beta. Explore the empty halls and untouched for years rooms and find the abandoned data before the life support systems fail completely.

Explore the Centauri Beta in the new map expansion for Diceolated in both solo and multiplayer versions. Unlock rooms, scan the keys and recover data before time runs out.

—description from the publisher

After the exploration of the space station data that was recovered will be placed inside the codex. Fitting data fragments connect into additional data that can be recovered.

This Expansion for Diceolated adds 9 Codex Cards that add an additional way to earn points at the end of the game. This expansion is compatible with both multiplayer and solo variants of the game.

—description from the publisher

Explorers have prepared additional data fragments that can unlock specific rooms while exploring the space stations. Add those fragments to your scanner and unlock the previously unavailable rooms.

This expansion for Diceolated adds 6 color cards and 6 symbol cards that will help you in your investigation to your game. This expansion is compatible with both multiplayer and solo variants of the game.

—description from the publisher

Explorers have found a way to recover more data! Data fragments in the portable scanner will be able to create data glyphs that when used with a proper key, recover more information.

This expansion for Diceolated adds 6 Glyph Cards that add an additional way to earn points at the end of the game. This expansion is compatible with both multiplayer and solo variants of the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Taco Gato Cabra Queso Pizza: Cabra promo
description: Argentinian promo for Taco Cat Goat Cheese Pizza: the GOAT (in honor to Messi)
family: Series: Taco Cat Goat Cheese Pizza

description:
Only a small number of Supervolcanos exist across our planet, but have the potential to prove catastrophic for mankind if ever to erupt.

You have been contracted by the government to tackle the Supervolcano threat. Establish your operation and harvest super heated steam from deep inside the volcano.

Balance the challenges of building a heat network and growing local populations, whilst keeping the magma chamber pressure under control. Fail to meet your quota and the resulting eruptions and quakes will see those precious populations disappear!

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Mandalorian: Adventures – Clan of Two Expansion
description:
The Mandalorian is tasked with reuniting The Child with its own kind, but danger lurks everywhere. Will he find the Jedi, or will Moff Gideon get what he seeks?

In The Mandalorian: Adventures – Clan of Two Expansion, you work together to complete thrilling missions inspired by Season 2 of The Mandalorian. Play as four new characters, each with wildly different play styles, skills, and tricks. Put your skills to the test with four story missions on four new locations in a new mission book — doubling the number of maps in the game — and four diverse mercenary missions.

The expansion also introduces new enemy types, ongoing events, a duel deck, and more that can be integrated with the base game for more variety.

Armoured Clash is a tabletop wargame of epic land battles, using highly detailed miniatures to represent massed battles across the globe. Set in the alternate late-nineteenth century of the Dystopian Age, players become generals for their super-science-fuelled nations that clash over resources and power.

There are many ways to start your journey into Armoured Clash with one and two player Introductory sets, Faction Starter sets and a wide range of expansions. Pick your faction and fight for dominance with a variety of fantastic infantry, cavalry, aircraft and tanks. Showcase your mighty forces with spectacular behemoths such as landships and super heavy tanks.

Lead your Force to victory. And may the best general win.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In Dictator for Life, Ancient Roman Generals compete against one another, trying to gain and keep control of the Dictatorship in Rome. Resources are earned by fighting battles against foreign enemies, and the Dictator may not leave Rome, so a formidable army and a healthy gold reserve is needed to defend your Dictatorship from rival generals. On their turn, the Dictator may buy up to 5 senators. The first to buy 10 senators is declared Dictator for Life and wins the game!

Winning battles against foreign enemies will win you gold, troops, weapons, and tactics cards. The tactics cards are where the most interesting mechanics take place - use Testudo to negate all projectile weapons. Declare it a Holy Day and throw a lavish festival in Rome, preventing other generals from marching on Rome. Sacrifice a Sheep, appeasing the gods and preventing disasters from befalling you while out on campaign.

The majority of the battles will take place against foreign enemies, who are encountered by overturning cards from the Bellum deck while you are on campaign. But the most exciting battles take place between the Dictator and a general who marches on Rome to depose them.

For an added bit of fun and drama, generals give a short speech to indicate that they are marching on Rome. The speech can be as simple as, "I march one Rome!" or whatever overblown oratory masterpiece you can deliver in one minute or less.

The game plays at its best with 3 players playing 3 rounds, with the winner being determined by the overall senator number. This setup leads to more long-term, calculated decisions. With the 3 player setup, large battles between two players will often cause them to drain most of their resources. This is a benefit to the player who was not involved, often giving weaker generals their chance to strike.

—description from the designer

Square Deal is actually a collection of 14 card games that are played with a regular deck of cards except that they are SQUARE. Square cards are ideal for games that are played on a grid such as 5x5 (like Virus vs. Virus), 7x7 (Trapped), 6x6 (Chesstego) and 3x3 (Tic Tac Ten). The square cards not only fit better on most tables, they also lend a symmetry to the games not available with rectangular cards. Some of the games require markers, such as Meeples, which are used strategically to move around the playing field. Most of the games are 2 player, but there are some 3 and 4 player games.

—description from the designer

Reedride is skating in Frisian.
You skate from left to right and from right to left over the game board.
The winner is the one who has the biggest lead in seconds after 3 rounds.

Each skater gets 2 boost pastilles
a skating cone
5 cards to start
If a crash is drawn when the 5 cards are handed out, this card expires and
you can take another card in its place
You take the last 6 cards from the individual card pile and shuffle the blank card between them
The round ends when someone draws the blank card

When it is your turn, you first take a card. If you forget to draw a card before you discard one card or boost pastille
you must continue this round with 4 cards
You will also receive a penalty card (yellow card)
If this happens again, you will receive a red card and you will not be allowed to play this round .
You will receive a 20 second penalty this round

You must play out a fall immediately
In the event of a fall, you choose who you take with you in your fall. Left, right or all those who are just behind you

You may use boost pastilles and thereby discard your lowest card.
You may use these if you are skating next to or just behind someone
You skate the same km/h as the player who is just in front of or next to you. You place your pawn one in front of the player who just played and who was just next to or in front of you
The next player can also do this if he plays a boost pastille

In the peloton
As long as you skate 2 km more or less than the peloton, you stay in the peloton.
e.g. peloton play 38. If you play 38, 39 or 40, go to the first black circle next to the red circle
If you play 36 or 37, go to the first red circle next to the black

Out of the peloton, you must skate the same amount of km/h as the peloton skates
If not, you go back or forward a number of seconds.

When it is your turn, you place your skating pawn on the other side of the center line.
If you are on the right, go to the left and so on.
This is to clearly see who still has to play this turn.

The inside has priority over the outside. Who skates in starts at the same place second

If you are more than -17 seconds behind the peloton during the game, you will be removed from the
game board and you will not play this round anymore.
You will receive a 20 second penalty time this round.

Peloton card is a joker card
You can use this as:
Chasing peloton Peloton + 2
Stopping peloton Peloton - 2
Riding in the peloton You stay in the peloton

The peloton counts for the points. This is disadvantageous if you finish behind the peloton and advantageous if you finish in front of the peloton.

—description from the designer

boardgame: シャルロッテの秘密 (Charlotte's Secret)
description:
シャルロッテの秘密 ("Charlotte's Secret") is set in a village threatened by Charlotte, a tailor who uses souls to create enchanting garments, turning admirers into mannequins. The villagers must identify and exile Charlotte before she dooms the entire village. Players must work together to unveil her identity amidst deception and save the village.

This trick-taking game involves 3 to 4 players who are assigned secret roles—either as "villagers" or as Charlotte. Over eight rounds, players play cards strategically, using special powers to influence outcomes. In the final "witch trial," players aim to have Charlotte exiled and compare collected cards to determine if the villagers prevail. It's a game of deduction and strategy.

Transform into an incredible hero of Greek Myth! Embark on a quest into the Labyrinth in search of relics, returning them to the entrance in order to become the Champion of the Gods. Borrow the powers of the Greek Gods along your quest to retrieve their relics, all while avoiding the dangerous Minotaur. 
This epic quest has three phases. Each features a different task for the heroes to tackle. 
Clotho: The Weaving
Heroes collaboratively construct the board using the Labyrinth tiles. 
Lachesis: The Allotment
Heroes travel the Labyrinth searching for specific God Relics, one relic at a time. 
Atropos: The Inevitable
Heroes race against each other to collect any remaining relics and earn the title of Champion of the Gods.

In Entrenched: Kaiser Kompanie, you take on the role of a German commander during the brutal trench warfare of World War I. This version of the game introduces a more rigid strategic approach, where foresight and long-term planning are crucial to success. It’s a 1 vs 1 or 2 vs 2 battle that can be played in conjunction with Gallia Guards, adding new dimensions to the fight for dominance.

Unlike the French forces, your deck is pre-set, giving you a solid foundation to build from, but only later in the game will you have the opportunity to expand your options with additional cards. This controlled structure demands you anticipate your opponent's moves and adapt your strategy accordingly, thinking several steps ahead to overcome their defenses.

In addition to the formidable armored units, your arsenal now includes zeppelins, which can turn the tide of battle from above. The game’s deckbuilding mechanics remain at the core, but with added complexity.
