Every decision counts, and you’ll need to carefully balance offense and defense, considering the limited flexibility early on. Victory is still achieved by capturing your opponent’s trenches and earning victory points, but the path to success in Kaiser Kompanie requires a mastery of patience, precision, and foresight.

—description from the publisher

In the not-so distant dystopian future…

A chaotic Cyber War rages across the vast digital expanse of the web. Society is starkly divided between the Cogs in the relentless grinding machinery of the five ruthless Mega-Corporations that rule the system, or you’re the few remaining Renegades who prowl the lawless fringes of the dark web, just beyond the reach of the Mega Corps.

The Societal Rift has created the ultimate high-stakes game of hunter and hunted. Only a few elite renegades rise to become heads of international hacking syndicates, powerful and resourceful enough to thwart the Mega Corps system of oppression and to strike fear into the Very hearts of these diabolical corporate giants.

In this high-stakes game of power and deception, the line between hero and villain blurs, but one thing remains certain: the future belongs to those who control the code.

Are you ready to capture all the monsters?? In this exciting adventure, players will use their mental math abilities and multiplication skills to capture the most powerful monsters using a combination of capture cards.

—description from the publisher

This is a math education focused card game, which is designed for elementary school age children. While it primarily is intended to 'gamify' the memorization of math facts, it is in fact a simple, introductory card game for young children.

There is a shared pool of math problem cards in the center. On each turn, the player uses their digit cards to provide the correct answer to a problem and claim that card. The player's card hand and the problem cards are refilled after each turn. Once a player has claimed 5 problem cards the game ends. We play that everyone gets an equal number of turns. Each problem card claimed is worth a point value based on relative difficulty of the problem. Highest score wins.

Control cannons and fight invaders coming from space.

This is a board game based on the game Space Invaders.

The players control their 'cannon' to fight the enemies from space (Invaders) together.

The game ends once all invaders have been defeated.

The rank of the players is then determined by the points they have received.

But be careful: if even one invader reaches the bottom row, the defense fails and all players lose!

"Thank You, Santa" is a trick-taking game for 2 to 5 players.

Players are siblings who love Santa. They look for socks of the perfect size for the gifts to ensure Santa doesn't mix things up. Additionally, they try to make their thank-you letter to Santa as noticeable as possible to ensure it is received. Will the siblings' wishes reach Santa?

The number of tricks won represents the size of the gifts. The two cards placed above the fireplace represent the size and color of the socks. If the two cards are of the same color, the letter can be delivered.

When the siblings' wishes reach Santa, he whispers a "thank you" (victory points) to them while they sleep and heads to the next house.
publishers: 卸売センターサンエイ (Oroshiuri Sentaa San'ei)

description:
Forty Crowns The Card Game is a trick-taking card game for 2-5+ players, featuring a trump suit mechanic. Each set of cards belonging to the same suit, however, is different from the others, with a unique distribution of values and some special powers on certain cards.

The goal of the game is to collect in your pile the highest number of cards displaying one or more crowns, which represent the points to be counted at the end of each game.

The deck consists of 50 playing cards + 4 instruction and score-tracking cards. Forty Crowns can be played as single games or with cumulative scoring over a series of games, either in free-for-all or team play.

The setup is quick: just shuffle the deck and deal 3 cards to each player. Each game lasts only a few minutes.

boardgame: Munchkin Warhammer: Age of Sigmar – Dire Domains
description: The Age of Sigmar is an epic setting in which heroes, gods, and monsters clash upon the fantastical battlefields of the Mortal Realms. Until now, you’ve had to imagine how those realms impacted your games of Munchkin Warhammer Age of Sigmar. Now you can explore 20 of these wondrous locations with the Dungeon and Portal cards found here. Betray your friends in Anvilgard, explore both sides of Hammerhal, and strike dangerous bargains with the Undead in Ulfenkarn. Take a tour of the Mortal Realms for near-infinite fun!

There are too many monsters, and their kings are growing restless. As a brave monster hunter it is your duty to defeat them and this time you got friends!

Puzzle Dungeon: Pets is an add on for the endlessly playable monster-hunting solitaire card game Puzzle Dungeon! Pets and Mascots are coming to Puzzle Dungeon.

New content includes:
50 Heros 
20 Monsters
10 Traps and Treasures
80 Pets
80 Mascots

boardgame: Sengoku Folio 11: The Battle of Okitanawate
description:
The Battle of Okitanawate is a Japanese doujin (fan-made) game produced by Ludens Faber and is game 11 of his Sengoku Folio series. The game simulates the battle between Shimazu and Ryuzoji in 1584.

The game is a typical wargame, with movement and attack values, zones of control, a CRT for combat results, and a turn track.

The game includes a map, rules, historical guide, and mounted counters. The counters need to be cut out.
publishers: Dōjin Club Ludens Faber (同人会ルデンス・ファベル)

description:
FloraSphere TCG is a captivating plant-themed trading card game that combines strategy, science, and storytelling. Step into a world where nature and elemental forces collide in this 1st edition FloraSphere TCG, the ultimate trading card game that merges strategy, resource management, and tactical combat. With over 300 unique cards in the 1st Edition 2024, every deck you construct and every battle you fight will unfold in new, unpredictable ways. Whether you're challenging a single opponent or facing off with up to four players, FloraSphere delivers dynamic, exciting gameplay in every session.

Set in a rich, interconnected world, FloraSphere highlights the intricate relationships between flora, creatures, and environmental elements. Players can harness resources like sunlight to grow powerful plants and summon mythical creatures. With beautifully designed cards inspired by real botanical species, scientific principles, and elements, each game is a journey into a living, breathing universe.

FloraSphere stands out with its dynamic gameplay mechanics, including elemental synergies, adaptive evolution, and thematic campaigns that allow players to explore environmental challenges and uncover deep lore. Created by a family-owned nursery, FloraSphere celebrates the beauty of the natural world while offering a fun, educational, and engaging experience for players of all ages.

—description from the designer

Burglar’s Blitz is a strategic push-your-luck card game where players take on the roles of burglars attempting to sneak into mansions, steal valuable loot, and escape before the police arrive. Thematically, it immerses players in a high-stakes heist scenario filled with risk, competition, and clever maneuvering. Each player must manage their limited loot bag capacity, make critical decisions about which items to take, and contend with the unpredictable effects of action cards.

The gameplay revolves around drawing and managing cards. Players can draw cards from the deck or a junk pile, adding them to their loot bag or discarding them based on their value and utility. Action cards introduce dynamic elements, such as guard dogs that delay turns, burglar alarms that accelerate the police arrival, or getaway drivers that offer a chance to escape unscathed. Players must decide whether to play it safe and secure smaller rewards or risk it all for higher-value items while potentially getting caught.

The game consists of four rounds, each ending when all players flee the scene or when the police arrive and bust everyone left in the mansion. Players score based on the value of the items in their loot bags if they successfully escape. The winner is the player with the highest total score after all four rounds. Tie-breaking rules ensure that even in close games, a decisive winner can emerge.

With its blend of thematic elements, strategic decisions, and interactive gameplay, Burglar’s Blitz offers a fun and engaging experience. It rewards players who balance risk and reward while outmaneuvering their opponents to claim the title of the ultimate mastermind.

—description from the designer

台灣最大戰爭:客家 ("Taiwan's Largest War: Hakka") is a strategic level wargame to simulate the Yiwei War in late Qing dynasty period in Taiwan.

There are 7 rounds in one game. Player commands the Meiji government to conquer the Republic of Formosa in Taiwan. Imperial Japanese armies may move into the revolt areas and roll the die to attack Qing armies. Be careful, the hakka and Hokkien militia units may ambush you randomly. In the end round, you need to score at least 7 vps by capturing the generals of rebels and sweep the rebel armies.

Taiwan's Largest War: Hakka is the fifth game of the Revolt & Conquer series. You can combine Hakka and Hokkien games together for playing a Yiwei War campaign game with 3-4 players.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Vagrantsong: Dogcatcher Scenario – Throw Me a Bone
description: When a haunted dogcatcher sets his sights on the Silver Ferryman, those lively pups are in for a supernatural chase! Dive into the Lost Pups Scenario. It's not just about the pursuit. Alongside the eerie Dogcatcher Haint, you'll find an array of loveable pups for the Runaway and intriguing new skills for other Vagrants.

台灣最大戰爭:閩南 ("Taiwan's Largest War: Hokkien") is a strategic level wargame to simulate the Yiwei War in late Qing dynasty period in Taiwan.

There are 7 rounds in one game. Player commands the Meiji government to conquer the Republic of Formosa in Taiwan. Imperial Japanese armies may move into the revolt areas and roll the die to attack Qing armies. Be careful, the hakka and Hokkien militia units may ambush you randomly. In the end round, you need to score at least 7 vps by capturing the generals of rebels and sweep the rebel armies.

Taiwan's Largest War: Hokkien is the sixth game of the Revolt & Conquer series. You can combine Taiwan's Largest War: Hakka and Hokkien  games together for playing a Yiwei War campaign game with 3-4 players.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Vagrantsong: Shadow Scenario – Amigo and the Devil
description: Starring the enigmatic (and real world) folk artist, Amigo the Devil, this scenario will have Vagrants fight for their very souls.

boardgame: Muskets and Springfields: Wargaming the American Civil War 1861-1865
description:
Muskets & Springfields is designed for playing big battles in the American Civil War and is not model scale dependent and uses the player’s current basing system. 
The game is set at the operational level. The player is the army commander with sub command groups. This will be typically a Corps. In these rules a Corp is made up of several infantry brigades, mounted cavalry, and artillery batteries. If you wish you can also include Native American Indian warbands.

The basic unit in wargaming terms is the infantry brigade, which are grouped into Corps or Divisions. The game system uses grids as the unit of measurement. The game space is broken into several square grids which represent 300 yards in ground scale. Taking a balance of the various drill guides of the period this is approximately equal to 600 men in two ranks. For a typical 6x4 playing space it is recommended a ratio width x depth of 1.5 x 1. A typical 6x4 table provides the following 12 (3600 yds) by 8 (2400 yds) of battlefield.

Morale is handled at the corps level and attrition is held at the individual unit base. These rules have three levels of morale. This is not the usual average, veteran, elite often used. Instead, the format is designed to reflect the actual state of mind on the day. These are Unknown, Nervous and Steady. In a game, unless representing specific historical units all bases start as Unknown. The actual morale state is not known till the unit takes damage. The player then rolls against a chart which provides a score for the unit being Nervous or Steady. This is dependent on the year being played and if Confederate or Union. When a unit fails a morale check this is recorded against their parent corps. Once a corps reaches its break point it will then flee the battlefield. Attrition is held at the unit level. This represents loss of cohesion, battlefield casualties or supplies running low. A unit can absorb 6 hits before it is automatically destroyed.

To facilitate a clean flow of play these rules also facilitate the use of sharpshooters and skirmishers. Turns use a bag-pull system in which it is possible for the non-active player to able to interdict the play.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Bushidan: Miniatures rules for small unit warfare in Japan, 1543 to 1615 AD
description:
The later Sengoku period is the era of warfare depicted in classical Japanese movies such as The Seven Samurai, Ran, and Kagemusha – a colourful period that has a very distinct difference in the arms, equipment, tactics and culture from that seen in Europe at the time. It is a period that sees the dominance of firearms in warfare, and the evolution of increasingly complex small-unit tactical systems. But there still remained a heroic ethos, where leaders and the bearers of age-old honoured names would still try to seek out worthy adversaries to battle blade-to-blade.

While massive armies did meet and clash, such encounters were surprisingly rare. The endemic warfare of the period was dominated by raids, small battles, incursions, and the clash of clan warbands. Small families battled one another for local regional dominance, seeking the leverage to become major powers. Meanwhile, rearms and armour improved in an increasingly lethal arms race.

Bushidan allows players to control the forces of samurai families, ikko-ikki covenants, pirates, bandits and warrior monks – or even forces engaged against the Japanese of the era such as the Ming Chinese and Joseon Koreans. 
The game focuses on the small-unit tactical systems that were developing to meet the demands of the new age of warfare. Players must plan their tactical ‘box of tricks’ with care, and choose the ferocity, style and cunning that their forces will bring to the battlefield. The game includes a campaign system to allow players to pit their Bushidan against one another in longer conflicts.

This is a colourful period for wargamers to paint and to model. The rules are intended for use with 28mm figures but can easily be played with smaller scale, and a typical army might have 8 or 9 units, each of 4 to 16 figures each.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Bismarck's Wars: Wargaming Rules for the Period leading to the Franco-Prussian War, 1859-1871
description:
Step into the tumultuous era of the mid to late 19th century, where the fate of Europe was forged in the fires of war. Bismarck’s Wars offers an immersive wargaming experience that brings to life the strategic brilliance and military innovations of the period leading to the Franco-Prussian War. 
This comprehensive rulebook is designed for both seasoned wargamers and newcomers alike, providing a rich historical context and detailed gameplay mechanics.

Historical Context and Significance:
Explore the intricate political and military landscape of the period leading to Franco-Prussian War, as conflict reshaped Europe and set the stage for modern warfare. Delve into the ambitions of Otto von Bismarck, whose strategic genius and ruthless diplomacy unified the German states and challenged the dominance of France. Understand the pivotal battles, from the opening Italian War via Schleswig Holstein, Austria, Denmark to the decisive victory at Sedan and the siege of Paris, and how these events influenced the course of history.

Core Rules and Mechanics:
Bismarck’s Wars features a streamlined set of basic rules that balance simplicity with tactical depth. Players will command a single Corps, manoeuvring their forces across a grid gaming terrain to outwit and outfight their opponents. The rules incorporate innovative mechanics from Muskets & Springfields, ensuring a dynamic and engaging gameplay experience. Whether you are leading a daring cavalry charge or coordinating a devastating artillery barrage, every decision matters.

Playing the game:

     Game Level: Engage in battles at Corps level, managing your brigades by standard playing cards. The playing card system gives a random activation of individual or several brigades and supports not the turn and turn about method of other rule sets giving a flexibility in the scale of your engagements.
     Game Duration: Designed for both quick battles and extended campaigns, with game durations ranging from 1 to 4 hours depending on the players chosen scenario.
     Base Sizes: These rules use a grid system as the unit of measurement. The game space consists of square grids each representing 300 yards on the ground. The grid size adjusts according to the scale of the miniatures in play. This gives you flexibility with miniature sizes used and allowing you to adapt existing collections easily for example Cavalry base for 100mm grid is a 100mm square base allowing you vignette your figures.
     Table Size: although the book uses a 6’ x 4’ table for demonstration this can be adapted for different sizes, providing ample space for manoeuvring and strategic planning.
     Solo Play: the rules work well with the playing card activation system for lone play, giving an ability to develop your game play and tactics 


Scenario-Specific Rules:
Immerse yourself in meticulously crafted rules that recreate the key battles and campaigns of the Wars that forged Europe. The rules are designed to challenge your strategic thinking and adaptability, offering unique objectives and victory conditions. From the first conflicts in Italy to the final confrontations around Paris, these rules provide endless replay-ability and historical insight.

Visual and Educational Appeal:
The rulebook is illustrated with photographic examples of painted miniatures. These visual elements not only enhance the gaming experience but also serve as a valuable educational resource. With an overview of the era and the influence of historical characters, inspiring a deeper appreciation for the complexities of 19th-century warfare.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Rise of Byzantium: Fighting the Early Wars of Byzantium with the Three Ages of Rome
description:
In the fourth century CE the Roman Empire was still the most important state in both the east and the west of Europe. It was coming under increasing pressure from migrating groups. This pressure would break the empire into two halves. The western half did not survive but the eastern half of the empire, based in the city of Constantinople, would renew itself. It would grow to become a famous empire in its own right. For almost a thousand years it would fight against pressures from both inside and outside the empire. It finally succumbed to the power of the Ottoman Turks in the fifteenth century CE.

From the third century CE, the Roman Empire had been fighting against the Sassanid dynasty of Persia. Neither side had managed to achieve a decisive victory. The period covered by The Rise of Byzantium continues this long running conflict. At the end of the period there was a decisive victory but it did not belong to either the Byzantines or the Sassanids. The victor would be a new power, the Arabs, that emerged from the southern Arabia.  They were driven by religious fervour and would sweep all before them over the next 200 years.

During the early part of the period covered by this expansion, the armies of Byzantium set out to recover some of the lost territories of the Roman Empire. Sometimes they were successful and re-established control, but in most cases any recovery was only partial. Byzantium alone was not always strong enough to hold off the pressures of the migrating groups. As with the later Roman Empire, there were often times when Byzantium needed its allies to be able to defeat these invaders.

These migrating groups would include traditional enemies such as the Franks, Goths, and Vandals. During the period new migrants would arrive such as the Avars, Bulgars, Lombards and Slavs.  
Each new threat was dealt with in its turn and the Byzantine army gained in experience. At the start of this period, the Byzantine army looked much the same as the Late Roman army, but by the period’s end it had changed to a more flexible and resilient structure based on a series of provincial forces.

This book provides an expansion to the Three Ages of Rome wargames’ rules. The six historical scenarios span ‘The Rise of Byzantium’ and link directly into the last period of the Three Ages rules. The new army lists introduce armies from across the period of the expansion set. They complement the armies in the original set of rules enabling players to recreate more battles from the ancient period.

As in the Three Ages, in battle nothing is certain but generals that practice their skills are more likely to be victorious.

—description from the publisher

The following is translated using Google Translate, the original text can be found at the bottom.

Hamsters fight for sunflower seeds!

Collect sunflower seeds while increasing the number of their allies.

As the owner, work with your hamsters to collect 10 sunflower seeds faster than anyone else.

Not only are they cute, but this game also requires strategy! !

Unique hamster cards
Each of the 48 hamster cards has its own unique characteristics.
Not only their appearance, but also their name, weight, and size all have their own unique profile.
Use cards to acquire seeds, draw cards from the deck to gain an advantage, and cooperate with your hamsters according to the situation to advance in the game!

Unique conditions for activating effects
Some hamster requires meeting certain conditions for activating effects:
"There are three or more males on the field"
"There are two or more hamsters on the field with names of five or characters"
"There are three or more hamsters on the field weighing 60g or more"

Many of these depend on the profile of the hamster.

Observe the hamsters on the field carefully!

《A big comeback with a goods card!?》
By paying hamster cards on hand or valuable sunflower seed(s), "goods cards" can get in the way of your rivals, so you can't tell who's going to win until the very end!
Don't give up and do your best to make a big comeback! !


     If you like hamsters
     If you're looking for an exciting game
     If you're into strategy games


Please enjoy Hamlog and have fun while relaxing! !

—description from the publisher (original text)

1ハムスターたちのタネ争奪戦！
個性的なハムスターたちは
仲間を増やしながら「ヒマワリのタネ」を集めてきます。
飼い主のあなたは、ハムスターたちと協力して誰よりも早く「ヒマワリのタネ」を１０個集めましょう。

可愛いだけでなく、戦略性にも頭を悩ませるゲームです！！

《個性豊かなハムスターカード》
４８種類のハムスターカードは、それぞれ個性的な特徴を持ちます。
「見た目」はもちろん、「名前」「重さ」「大きさ」のすべてが固有のプロフィールになっています。

カードを使ってタネを獲得したり、山からカードを引いて優位に立ち回ったりと、場面に応じてハムスターたちと協力し、ゲームを進めていきましょう！

《効果の発動条件も個性的》
効果発動の条件には

「場に♂の子が３匹以上いる」
「場に名前が５文字以上の子が２匹以上いる」
「場に重さ６０g以上の子が３匹以上いる」

など、ハムスターのプロフィールに依存するものがたくさんあります。

場にいるハムスターたちをよく観察しましょう！

《超重要なコンボ要素！！》
手番に使えるカードは基本的に１枚ですが、『もう１枚カードが使える』と書かれたカードを使う事で１手番に２枚以上のカードを使ったコンボが可能です。
コンボを繋いで大量得点や大逆転を目指しましょう！！

《グッズカードで大逆転！？》
単なる「癒しゲーム」で終わらないのがハムログ。
コストを消費することで使える「グッズカード」でライバルの邪魔をする事ができるため、最後の最後まで勝負は分かりません！
大逆転を目指して諦めずに頑張りましょう！！

・ハムスターが好きな方
・盛り上がるゲームをお探しの方
・戦略ゲームにハマっている方

ぜひハムログで癒されながら盛り上がってください！！

—description from the publisher

For fans of quests, ciphers, and mysteries! For detective enthusiasts and admirers of the "Once Upon a Time in Chicago" series! For those who dislike cooperative games and want to finally put their minds to work, racing to uncover secrets and treasures against friends rather than with them.

An adventure game about treasure hunting in the small town of Goodwood, where the "gold rush" reaches unprecedented heights. But we all know that not everything that glitters is gold...

The unique "parallel search" mechanic ensures no one gets bored: two teams search for treasures simultaneously but can significantly interfere with each other.

With the atmosphere of the Wild West, distinct Native American magic, and five unique treasures to battle over with opponents, the game offers a thrilling experience. And if you enjoy playing board games solo, this game supports that too.

Onward to Freeman Dodgson's treasures!

Legions of Heimland is a war-themed board game released by Blisshouse Studio in 2024. The goal of the game is for 2 to 4 players to accumulate legion points by using various mercenary cards, hero cards, and building cards, as well as by attacking opposing players to collect the most points

—description from the publisher

boardgame: На Слово На Слово (On the Word on the Word)
description:
This is an educational, party game in which players are drawing letter cards from the deck and trying to write up to 10 words that start with that letter.

The point of the game is for each player to write as many unique words as they can, so they can gain more points than the other players.

—description from the publisher

Ово је едукативна и забавна игра у којој играчи извлаче слова из шпила и покушавају да напишу до 10 речи које почињу извученим словом.

Поента игре је да играч напише што више уникатних речи и на тај начин добије више поена од других играча.

—description from the publisher (Serbian)

Regulus High-Fantasy Arena-Sports is a highly tactical skirmish boardgame. To win the game, player must score 10 victory points by: knocking-out opponents, score public objectives or have their characters standing in the Regulus zone, a king of the hill center of the map where points can be scored at the end of three rounds.

The unique initiative system lets players strategize when a character starts their turn. Playing a low numbered initiative card will let a character start early, dishing out some hard hits or eagerly avoid any potential danger.

When a character starts their turn they may do up to two actions, which include: movement, attacks, spells, laying down traps, healing and pushing and pulling your enemy's to spaces they don't want to go. Position your character's just right and you can even attack with powerful off-turn Reactions!

Regulus features 4 asymmetrical teams of 3 characters, which can be customized even further with equipment cards. Characters can bring 3 equipment cards with them, adding a once per game action and a lasting passive per card. This vastly changes the playstyle of a character and will lead to new and exciting gameplay opportunities.

—description from the publisher

In this cooperative game players try to liberate the good "nisser" (gnomes) from prison before the evil Filias gets crowned as king!

Each player is either a human or a gnome, and there are two human pieces and two gnome pieces, and each player can only move pieces of their own kind.

Six cards begin face-down (each representing a prisoner gnome), and players have to land on specific spaces to flip them face-up. The card will show where to have two pieces to liberate the gnome (for instance by having a gnome on a green spot and a human on a purple spot - with each prisoner having different requirements).
family: Misc: Advent / Christmas / Jule calendar game

description:
A game about being influencers and winning subscribers for your social media channel.

The board is pyramidal, with four concentric levels. On your turn you roll the die to move clockwise or counterclockwise (your choice). If you hit a square with a subscriber token on it, you flip the token and perform the associated action. If you land on someone else, they get sent to their start square on the level and you take one of their subscribers.
If (at the start or the end of your turn) you have enough subscribers to advance a level, you move to your start square on the next level (if you have too many subscribers, the excess tokens get added to the "award pool" of extra subscriber tokens.

You win by being the first to advance to the fourth level.

The game is app-driven.

Flip a card from the hand while saying the indicated sequence, until there is a match between what is said and what is shown and PLASH! hands to the pile. The last one to place it takes everything.

The player who manages to run out of cards in the hand will be the winner.

Additionally, there are 3 cards that force you to make fun gestures before throwing the slap.

Voltear una carta de la mano mientras se dice la secuencia indicada, hasta que haya coincidencia entre lo que se dice y lo que se muestra y ¡PLASH! manos a la pila. El último en colocarla se lleva todo.

El jugador que logre quedarse sin cartas en la mano será el ganador.

Adicionalmente hay 3 cartas que te obligan a hacer divertidos gestos antes de lanzar el manotazo.

boardgame: Der Rätsel- und Kreativblock für Taylor Swift-Fans

Taylor Swift is a music legend who inspires millions of fans with her songs. But how big is your fan passion really? Do you know which albums Taylor has reached number 1 in the charts with? Do you know her best friends? And can you answer which records the singer has broken? Then this fast-paced card game is made for you! But can you also name three matching terms for a category in just nine seconds? Face the questions and test your knowledge of the superstar! Are you ready for the challenge? The perfect gift for Swifties!

boardgame: Stadt, Land, Exmatrikulation: Der Spieleklassiker für Student*innen
description:
Whether you're at university, in a shared flat or in a café - with this exciting city, country, exmatriculation game, you'll bring a large portion of fun to every round! You can test your knowledge and put your creativity to the test in 52 categories. The game pad with an anti-cheat ABC turntable features exciting topics - perfect for all students, whether procrastinators or nerds. From simple questions to tricky categories - there's something here for everyone. 
Funny term paper title, canteen menu and reasons for being late - can you always think of something suitable? Take up the challenge and be surprised by the wide range of categories. Test yourself and your fellow students - which of you always has an answer ready and can fill in the categories the fastest? You can test your knowledge and put your creativity to the test in 52 categories. The game pad boasts a cool design and exciting topics - perfect for backpackers, all-inclusive travellers and anyone who is plagued by wanderlust. Factual knowledge, creativity and speed are required to be the first to shout ‘Stop!’. Are you ready? Then grab your pencils, get set, go!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Stadt, Land, Extreme: Das Spiel Potter-Fans
description:
City, country, extreme - the card game for every Harry Potter fan. 
Immerse yourself in the magical world of wizardry. The card game works like City, Country, River - but in a different and new way. There are no letter cards, but two different types of categories that are combined. The green category card indicates a property that must match the red category card with characters, rooms or events. For example, you have to find a word that is ‘typical’ for ‘Hermione’. 
Factual knowledge, creativity and speed are required to be the first to get rid of your cards and win. Are you ready to test your magical knowledge? Then grab your cards, get set, go!

—description from the publisher

Become part of the magical universe and feel like a real sorcerer's apprentice at the world's most famous school of magic! Test your magical knowledge with 300 exciting questions and find out if you are a true Potter fan. Work your way through all seven school years alone or together with other sorcerer's apprentices. The practical game templates for the joint quiz are easy to download via QR code. You can also use the magic house test to find out which house the talking hat categorises you in. Are you ready for your exam? 
Take the ultimate quiz and puzzle your way through the various levels of difficulty. Take your ZAG and UTZ exams and prove that you are a true wizard! All solutions are encrypted and can only be revealed with the decoder film. So cheating is out of the question! Magical puzzle fun for Potter fans and anyone who wants to become one. The additional fun facts offer exciting insider knowledge for real fans!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Das inoffizielle Quiz für Star Wars-Fans
description:
Explore the vastness of the galaxy, visit the various planets with your spaceship and test your knowledge of one of the most successful film franchises of all time. Are you a hardcore fan or would you rather watch the films again? 
Take on the ultimate quiz and solve the puzzles at various levels of difficulty. Answer all the questions correctly and prove that you are a true fan! The practical card box in a handy take-away format is ideal for travelling, at home or as a fun party game. The perfect gift for all fans of the Star Wars films.

boardgame: Stadt Land: Das Spiel für Marvel-Fans
description:
The classic game in a new format as a fun card game: with this exciting City, Country, Marvel edition, you'll bring a big dose of fun to every round! You can test your superhero knowledge and put your creativity to the test in over 60 categories. With its cool design and exciting themes, the card game will win over every Marvel fan, big or small. From simple to tricky categories - there's something for everyone here. 
Funny superpower, smaller than Thor's hammer and villain? Dive into the world of superheroes and test yourself and your friends - which of you is the biggest Marvel fan and is the quickest to find an answer that matches the letter and category? Factual knowledge, creativity and speed are required to win. Are you ready to immerse yourself in the world of superheroes? Then grab your cards, get set, go!

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Schmuddelfing!
A vanilla ice cream or a fresh apple? ‘Toad dirt!’ The Olchis shake their heads. They'd much rather have mummy's stinky cake or a nice rubbish bath in the rusty bathtub! In this fun reaction game, you slip into the role of the Olchis and have to react as quickly and correctly as possible according to the cards drawn. Who can make the fewest mistakes and be the first to get rid of all their cards? 
Whether you're at a birthday party, travelling or at home, this fun and cheeky reaction game is guaranteed to keep boredom at bay and provide plenty of fun. But be careful: danger of laughing fits!

boardgame: Nenne drei: schon vorbei! – für Kids!
description:
Types of fruit, films with animals, things in the children's room - can you name three matching terms for each category in just twenty seconds? Are you quick, clever and creative? Whoever answers correctly gets the card and whoever has the most cards at the end wins the game. The rules are very simple and the fun can begin immediately. The categories are a colourful mix - there's something for every child! 
At a get-together with your friends or at the next birthday party - this ingenious word game is guaranteed to keep boredom at bay! Whoever has the most cards at the end of the game wins! Are you ready to test your knowledge?

Introduction:Liflina (from Old Norse: líf and lína, meaning "life" and "line" respectively) is a drawless game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square board. The recommended board size is 19 points per side, but boards of 13, 15 or 17 are also valid. There is also a location outside the board called prison. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

This game is based on Leblin.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A lifeline is a maximal path of empty points in an orthogonal straight line from a stone to the board edge or to a friendly stone. Stones can share their lifelines among several friendly stones without the need for empty points between them.
     A territory is one or more empty points connected orthogonally. You own a territory if all stones connected to it are of your color.
     An incursion is a player's placement into the opponent's territory. An incursion is minimal if it causes exactly one enemy group to be moved to the prison.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then they alternate. On your turn, do exactly one of these actions:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty point and then move all enemy groups without lifelines to the prison.
     Remove an enemy stone from the prison. If the prison contains stones of both colors, remove pairs of stones of opposite colors until only stones of one color or none remain.


At the end of your turn, there should be no friendly stones without lifelines. You cannot make a minimal incursion if your opponent has just made a minimal incursion anywhere on the board.

				
				
					End of the game:The player who made the last move wins the game.

To balance the game, before the starting, the first player places several black stones in the prison, and then the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called komi stones. For handicap games, the weaker player takes black and opens by placing on the board a number of black stones proportional to the skill difference between the players.

—description from designer

boardgame: Renatio et Gloriam: Many Enemies, Much Honour
description:
This book contains 62 lists covering all the main armies of the period; 

     Habsburg Austria, 
     Italian States, 
     Trastámaran Spain, 
     Valois-Orléans France, 
     Vasa Sweden, 
     Huguenot France and 
     the Dutch Republic.


—description from the publisher
family: History: War of the League of Cambrai

description:
In this game the players act as the curators of the museum tasked with creating an exhibition that will interest the most people. The goal is to gather the remains of prehistorical animals, display them to appeal to the customers and promote the exhibition among enthusiasts with interesting facts.
In the game you collect a tile each turn and try to place it to fit your tableau best.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Cthulhu: Death May Die – Atlach-Nacha Elder One Box
description: This expansion introduces the terrifying new Elder One and its Hatchlings, which can be integrated into any Episode from any Season of Death May Die, adding fresh horrors and strategic depth to your gameplay.

In a world where cities are in danger. Where evil and villainy prey on the weak. There's only ONE hero who can them all. That hero's name is... someone you and your friends can't decide on. There's only one way to resolve this... FIGHT IT OUT WITH YOU HEROES!

Heroes Aren't Born is a fighting deck building game for 2-4 players all about battling other heroes and making yourself feel powerful in every game

The goal is to strategize your attacks and defenses against your opponents while learning new powers to determine who should be the main character of the comic book.

—description from the designer

Monty Hall is a 2P game, where players draw cards or play cards, attempting to deduce whether the last card is a car or a goat.

On your turn, you either play a card - with effects such as stealing a card, or arranging the top two cards of the deck - or draw the top card of the deck.

The bottom card of the deck is either a goat or a car. If you draw the bottom card and it is a car, you win a point; if it is a goat, your opponent gets a point. The first person to get three points wins.

Hexblem: Beating the Unbeatable is a competitive roguelite adventure board game for 1–4 players.

In each session, players choose 3 biomes, the player count, and the difficulty. The app then generates a unique map, guides setup, and drives a run built around exploration, resource management, interactive adventures, and an escalating countdown toward the arrival of the Unbeatable.

The game alternates between Exploration Rounds and Management Rounds. During Exploration, players interact with Hexblem tiles through the app, resolve cards from the corresponding decks, face quick combats, trigger events, and uncover branching Adventures. During Management, they spend resources to level up, buy cards, exchange essences, complete missions, and improve their build.

Hexblem is designed around evolving interactive content. The app includes 30 Adventures in version 1.0, built from interconnected phases that can change order and outcome depending on player decisions and performance. Boss encounters follow a similar approach, with 25+ phases across up to 6 stages.

If the Unbeatable is defeated, players make a final Energy count and the player with the most Energy wins the game. Defeating the Unbeatable grants a major advantage, but it does not automatically guarantee victory. If no player defeats it before the countdown reaches zero, the Unbeatable wins.

The app is part of the core design. It handles tutorial and setup flow, map generation, progress events, reward randomization, final pressure, and content growth over time. One phone is enough for the table, internet is not required during play, and no user data is collected. A simplified no-app mode also exists, but the app-supported version is the original intended experience.

Instead of relying on scripted sessions or fixed routes, Hexblem aims for replayability through changing maps, branching Adventures, shifting rewards, escalating boss fights, and free app updates that add new content over time.

As environmental pollutants and ecosystem threats continue to rise, the world faces an alarming reality: air pollution, increasing levels of chemical gases, deforestation, widespread dangerous diseases, and conflicts over land, soil, and food. Some even predict future wars over air itself. The Earth is no longer a sustainable home—it’s time to look beyond.

In response, visionary space investment companies have launched search teams to Planet 442 to explore the potential for cultivating vegetables in its soil. These companies are now competing fiercely to establish sustainable farming practices, driven by missions assigned to astronauts by their organizations. The race for survival has begun—who will build the future of humanity in the stars?

Game Overview:  
In Farmoony, players take on the role of engineers working for competing space companies, tasked with building the most sustainable and efficient farms on Planet 442. The game is designed for 2 to 6 players and unfolds over multiple rounds, with each round offering strategic opportunities to earn points and disrupt competitors. The ultimate goal is to score the highest points by fulfilling missions and strategically managing resources.

How to Play:  

     Each player begins with a set of Farm Cards and Tool Cards, while Mission Cards are shared among all players.  
     At the start of the game, three Mission Cards are revealed and remain the same for all players throughout the game.  
     In each round, players select a Farm Card to place on their grid to fulfill mission objectives.  
     Tool Cards can be used to enhance the player’s farm or hinder opponents by affecting their progress.  
     Missions require players to create specific patterns or combinations on their farm grids, such as aligning elements of water, soil, or energy.  
     Players take turns performing actions like placing cards, using tools, or completing mission objectives.  


Winning the Game:  
The game continues for three rounds, with players earning points by completing missions and optimizing their farm layouts. The player with the highest points at the end of the game wins. If there’s a tie, the winner is determined by the number of unused tokens or additional objectives completed.

Farmoony combines resource management, strategic placement, and competitive interaction to create a dynamic and engaging board game experience. Who will emerge as the ultimate space-farmer and secure the future of humanity on Planet 442?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Renatio et Gloriam: Great Kings, Grand Alliances
description:
This supplement covers warfare in the Great Northern War and the War of Spanish Succession.

It includes 49 army lists, starting in 1700 with the Swedish armies of Charles XII and the Russian legions of Peter the Great, and extending through to the War of the Spanish Succession. The supplement also provides detailed army lists for the Anglo-Dutch alliance, France, Spain, the Holy Roman Empire, and Portugal.

—description from the publisher
family: History: War of the Spanish Succession

description:
This supplement to the Renatio et Gloriam Rules covers warfare during the Thirty Years’ War and the British Civil Wars.

The book contains 55 army lists and enables the gamer to create wargames armies for matched play and for competitions. Starting in 1618 with the Catholic League, the lists cover the armies of the Thirty Years to the end of the British Civil Wars in the early-1650s.

—description from the publisher

Take That Toy! is a may-follow trick-taking game where the players want to acquire at least 1 card from each suit. Trump is randomly determined.

If a player collects at least 4 cards of a single suit, those cards are collected, shuffled, and re-dealt among the players.

If a player has won 1 of each suit, the game immediately ends; if they players play all of their cards, the person with the most suits won wins.

Loud Rock Spa is a trick-taking game about humming at the onsen after attending a rock festival, and trying to make memories with the other concert goers there - while not being too loud.

It is a must follow game, with no trump suit. Each round there is a volume limit for the spa. The winner of each trick will be the player with the highest rank, regardless of suit, and they earn "volume" equal to their rank minus the second highest rank.

At the end of a round, players with a volume higher than the limit are put on the blacklist, and the player with the highest volume that doesn't exceed the limit earns 1 VP.

The first person to earn 2 points wins.

boardgame: Dodos Riding Dinos: Dodo Dash – Ovododo Idol & Ovododo Charm Promo Cards
description:
Available from Dice Tower 2024 Kickstarter Eclipse Pack.

Ovododo Idol
- Once per turn, whenever a projectile launched by another Racer doesn't hit, you may draw 1 card or advance 1 hex

Ovododo Charm
- Take all Racer cards in play, including yours. Shuffle them and randomly deal 1 to each player. Until the end of the race, players keep playing with their new Racer.

Arc-en-Ciel is a cooperative trick-taking game for exactly 4 players. The suits of the cards are visible on the rear, and it includes card passing.

The game takes place through a series of missions of episodes (e.g. each player needs to win at least 2 tricks.)

The players earn points depending upon how well they accomplish the tasks.

Gameplay

     Players take turns moving one Larva at a time.
     Larva can only move by jumping over other Larvae.
     Players can jump over their own Larvae or their opponents’ Larvae.
     To Jump, a Larva must start in the Hex next to the first Larva it jumps overs.
     To end a Jump, there must be an empty Hex to land in.
     Each Jump must be in a straight-line.
     Each Jump is over all Larvae next to each other in the line (i.e., the Jump can be any length until an open Hex).
     Multiple jumps are allowed in a single turn, as long as there are Larvae to jump over and empty spaces to land on.
     Larvae cannot jump backward.
     Larvae are not captured (i.e., no Larvae are removed from the board during the game).
     In the rare event a player cannot make a move, they must pass their turn.
     Jumping is not mandatory (i.e. you can stop jumping at any time and do not need to continue jumping 


if you do not want to).

Game End

     The first player to move all their Larvae into the back two rows, on the opposite side of the board, wins the 


game.

     This game is based upon the classic game Halma.


—description from the designer

Oh Tannenbaum Promo Card: 2 points per card attached to this card. When the 3rd winter card is revealed, you can shuffle it back into the deck.

Limited to Germany.

“Le Ndem !” whose name is taken from Cameroonian slang meaning “to have worries” or “to mess around”, is a board game that skillfully combines creative challenges, collaborative actions, and fun trials. Designed to be accessible to all ages, it is played with a die, eight pawns representing symbols of luck, and a 40-square board full of surprises. The goal is to be the first to reach the end of a course strewn with obstacles.

Players advance their pawn according to the result of the die and must follow the instructions of the square they arrive at; Sometimes, they must undergo a fun test: say a difficult text, sing, dance, etc. Sometimes, they are required to change the position of the pawns, etc.

The aim of the game is to create a friendly atmosphere between the players rather than to win.

Each player chooses a pawn, and half of the players are mice and the other half are frogs. The players roll the die in turn, and the first time they roll the die they cannot roll more than 3 to be allowed to start. The mice play on red spaces, the frogs on yellow spaces. On some spaces events benefit or hinder the players or the earn or lose tokens.

Spaces 7 to 9, 17 to 19, 23 to 25 are battlegrounds; whoever comes up with these numbers has to wait until one or more players have reached the battleground in question. Whoever rolls more than 3 first is allowed to move on first and receives 3 tokens from the player who stays behind. Whoever comes to the battleground and has no one behind them pays 3 tokens into the cash register and moves on with the next roll.

Whoever reaches No. 51 first is the winner.

—user summary

Player become a princess to order their knight to fight by placing your knight card into the battlefield.

Both player have a 4x2 Battlefield grid consist of two different part. Knights fight and placed on the front line and princess stay in the support line on the back.

Player play their knight card, casting spell card from their hand, activate knight and princess's ability in play and order their knights to attack opponent's knight. Your knight's position is important to protect your princess, otherwise your opponent can attack you.

When your princess's health reach to 0, you lose the game.

剣戟は地平線の果てまで止まらない。

王女たちが求めるのは、異界から召喚されたたったひとつの奇跡。
手にした者は世界をも統べるという。
光によって文字を紡ぎ、あらゆる問いに答える石――《モノリス》

boardgame: Elder Garden: 眷属との契約 (Elder Garden: Pact with the Elder's Kin)
description:
Introduction to the "Pact with the Elder's Kin" Expansion
(Literal translation: Contract with the Familiars)

Hello there! It's great to see you've been captivated by the world of ELDER GARDEN. It seems the evil deity 'Elder' has caught your interest, and their familiars are intrigued by your devotion. Why not give them a try? It's not a bad deal, though I can't say they're exactly good guys.

As you journey to revive the evil deity 'Elder', you'll encounter the deity's familiars. They seek to test your true devotion. By awakening ancient souls buried within the earth and forsaking your allies, you can fulfil their desires. Only by overcoming these trials will the familiars acknowledge you (or perhaps mock you) and grant you a fragment of their power.

So, go forth! Make your offerings! Fear nothing! All for the sake of our god!

New Element 1: Familiars
Familiar cards are the main feature of this expansion set.

Familiars lurk within the spirit veins. By establishing a base in a spirit vein, you can form a contract with a familiar, which will gradually and dramatically reward you.

When you build on a spirit vein space for the first time in the game, you acquire a familiar card. However, to truly befriend a powerful familiar and fully utilize their power, you must demonstrate your power to the familiar by offering them a vast amount of anima.

New Element 2: Anima
Anima is what was once a human soul that has been materialized through forbidden secret arts. Anima emits an eerie orange glow and distorts the surrounding air.

To obtain anima, you must delve into new secret arts. You will perform rituals using sacred texts.

New Element 3: Sacred Texts
These are sacred texts of a heretical religion that record ancient secret arts. The pages, filled with forbidden knowledge and terror, describe the methods of summoning the souls of those buried in the earth and materializing them as anima.

Of course, the rituals are not easy. The sacred texts demand a vast amount of blood (and your suffering in offering that vast amount of blood). You will crave blood even more than before you knew of the familiars.

Level 1 Sacred Text Card: Starts the game placed on the altar. However, if you can suppress the urge to be dragged into the sacred text by its immense demand for blood and instead advance your understanding of it in exchange for your own mental strength, you will be able to touch the depths of ancient secret arts and draw even greater power from the sacred text.

Sacred texts become more powerful as they are strengthened. A level 3 sacred text is powerful enough to determine the outcome of the game.

New Element 4: Trials
You have become aware of the existence of the evil deity's familiars. They have also sensed your presence. They will test you from the beginning of the game, even before you make a contract.
You will be given a trial at the start of the game. If you can meet it, you will receive a reward corresponding to the trial.

If you give up on the heavy burden imposed on you, the familiars will not easily let you go. They will force you to struggle before an unattainable trial. And the blood that clings to your hands when you finally succumb to the trial...

Unique Additional Cards
The expansion also adds cards for those who aim to revive the evil deity, as well as sanctuary relic cards. These unique effects will create even more challenging and interesting gameplay for players who are already familiar with ELDER GARDEN.

Let's introduce Felix, a new character.
For Felix, who travels through dreams, the perception of reality is ambiguous, and he has no attachment to his own relics. As he travels, he drops his relics, which are blessed by the world.
If he has one relic or less, he is stuck and can't do anything? That's correct. If he wanders aimlessly and loses all his relics, he will lose all his options and fall into an eternal nightmare without ever waking up.

However, when he reaches the sanctuary, a second journey essentially begins.
Let's introduce Felix's companion relic card.

Mimic a powerful sanctuary relic card!
Upon reaching the sanctuary, you lose your life in exchange for gaining a powerful ability. This is a natural consequence.
However, because Felix's perception of reality is ambiguous, he continues to travel even after death when he reaches the sanctuary.
Felix can travel through the sanctuary relic cards that would normally require a lot of preparation to obtain, and mimic various sanctuary relics!

We have also added new sanctuary relic cards and event cards.

boardgame: Heiho: The Art of War – Wargames rules for large battles of Sengoku period Japan, 1560-1615
description:
As swift as wind
As subtle as forest
As fierce as fire
As unmovable as mountain.
– The Fūrinkazan banner of Takeda Shingen

Take command of the massed armies of your clans!
Battle to the death for control of Japan.

Heihō – The Art of War is a set of wargame rules for play with miniature figures. It covers the vast, spectacular battles that shook the length and breadth of Japan at the end of the sixteenth century.

These are the games rules that will let you play the battles of Kawanakajima and Sekigahara! Control armies that are Napoleonic in scale, and that use sophisticated tactical systems that were unique to Japan.

Lay your battle plans. Command your subordinates! Allay their treacheries. Blend spearmen, shot, archers, artillery and cavalry into mixed-arms forces, or concentrate your cavalry to sweep the enemy from the field!

Designed for any scale of miniatures, the game is well suited to smaller scales such as 2mm, 6mm, 10 and 15mm. The rules depict massed battles of the period, which saw armies with strengths from 20,000 to 100,000 men.

The game reflects the unique mixed arms deployment styles of the period, as well as the often elaborate battle formations. Games are played on a table divided into square grids – or not, if players want to use more traditional measurement and movement.

Troops are based in ‘bases’ of several figures on a single base. There are also ‘elements’ or half bases used to support larger formations (archers, cannon,supply units etc). Base sizes can be of any size desired – so no need to rebase troops that have been based for other systems.

Games systems create unique characteristics for clan troops, and for their commanders. The various great commanders can also have rivalries that may interfere with the best laid plans!

Ippon!
To the death!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Renatio et Gloriam: The Sun King's Wars
description:
Renatio et Gloriam is a set of tabletop miniature rules that allows you to fight large-scale battles of the "Pike and Shot" historical period. It brings to life the epic battles between the formidable armies of Louis XIV and the resolute forces of the League of Augsburg.

Renatio et Gloriam: The Sun King’s Wars contains list for European armies from 1640 to 1699, including - Bourbon France, Habsburg Austria & Spain, the Dutch Republic, Palatinate-Zweibrücken Sweden, Brandenburg-Prussia & Orange-Nassau England.

Renatio et Gloriam: The Sun King’s Wars offers a deep dive into one of the most captivating eras of European warfare. Command legendary generals, outmanoeuvre your opponents, and alter the course of history. 
Are you ready to uphold the grandeur of Louis XIV or defend the balance of power with the League of Augsburg? The choice is yours!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Renatio et Gloriam is a set of tabletop miniature rules that allows you to fight large-scale battles from the Renaissance. Explore the vast and diverse landscapes from the mountainous terrains of Japan and the sprawling lands of China to the tropical jungles of Southeast Asia and the historic expanses of India.

This supplement covers armies and warfare in:

     Japan – Sengoku Jidai
     China – Ming and Qing Dynasties (including the Imjin War)
     Southeast Asia – Thailand, Myanmar, Vietnam, Cambodia & Laos
     India – Muslim kingdoms in the Indian subcontinent and Marathas and Sikh Empires and Kingdoms.


Whether you are a seasoned wargamer or a history enthusiast, Renatio et Gloriam: Warring Dragons offers an unparalleled journey through some of the most colourful empires in history. 
Prepare your strategies, lead your troops, and shape the future of empires!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Swords of God: Wargaming Battles of the Crusades
description:
Swords of God is a narrative wargame centred on the Age of Crusades (1100-1400). 
Rather than attempt direct simulation of the physical aspects of warfare, the rules instead take inspiration from chansons de geste and other romantic literature of the time and aim to create a faster-flowing, more abstract struggle still worthy of a song.

The game aims to fit battles of around 20-30,000 men a side onto a 6’ x 4’ table, but any size of table can be used if your unit bases are smaller than the Impetus standard of 120mm by 40mm, or more numerous than the sands of the sea! 
Centred as it is on the command and control of key personalities in each army, any size of battle is possible with the easy-to-use points system provided. The game also provides a quick chart to help you pick the right size of table for your battle.

Inspired by psychohistorical studies of the Crusades like Avner Falk’s Franks and Saracens and Vincent Baker’s seminal Apocalypse World rules engine, the game’s deep but simple mechanics promote meaningful tactical choices while ensuring the action always moves forwards.

Among other things, it features:

     A unified mechanic based on the roll of 3 six-sided dice.
     Gridded movement to get the action started quicker. The sides of each square are equal to half the width of a unit base.
     Scenario generation that takes the table’s terrain into account.
     More than half a dozen sets of special rules based on the army commander’s relationship with the divine. In an era of personal oaths and charismatic authority, these special rules extend to their army as well.


Army lists for campaigns in Iberia, the Baltics, the Languedoc, Bohemia and the Levant feature curated spreads of generic unit types, while the Faith Trees available to generals of any religion grant special rules based on their emotional connection to the divine and the religious war that has ensnared them. Each one gives an army a different feel, providing a great deal of replayability even within the same campaign.

—description from the publisher

The goal of the game is to be the first to build the personal BLDR structure (Bridge, Airport, Museum, or Skyscraper).

The game is played with a deck of cards that is essentially a typical deck of playing cards with four Wild Cards added, and a deck of action cards.  The action cards include MONEY, which is used to purchase each section of the structure; ROBBER, which steals two action cards from another player; WRECKING BALL, which removes one completed section of another player's structure; and POLICEMAN, which is used to block robbers and wrecking balls.

Players take turns drawing cards and then discarding sets of two or more, or runs of three or more to gain action cards.  The larger the set, the more action cards received.  The strategy comes in deciding whether to discard smaller sets to get action cards more immediately, or to hold onto cards to get larger amounts of action cards.

On their turn a player draws a card and then may exchange as many sets or play as many action cards as they choose to.  Once the active player passes, the next player takes their turn.  This continues until one player completes their BLDR structure.

My aim with these rules was to create something which allowed you to fight larger battles, where each base is a battalion. 
The rules cover the period pretty much from the development of the magazine rifle onwards, but you can doubtless fudge that. The aim is to allow you to fight several different sorts of game. 
Yes you can have the clash of regular armies. But you can also have the sort of counter-insurgency battle where a regular division might have to sweep an area. Or where an insurgent might tell off forces to attack a government firebase whilst preparing to ambush the forces attempting to relieve the isolated garrison. All on the same wargames table.

Because of the nature of these games I wanted to include Logistics, but I wanted to do so in a simple form which was both relevant but also didn’t take too much book keeping. So a lot of your artillery support is just the expenditure of logistics points.
I’ve included rules for solo play, and the three campaigns included can also be played solo or against an opponent.

—description from the publisher

"Guidance comes in many forms," whispered Eagle Woman. "The raven touches the sun, flies far, and sees all.  Nothing like the whispers of war to bring allies together."    Whispers follow E.B. Farnum in Deadwood as he returns from a meeting with the territorial governor.  Deep in the mines, the Deadwood Miner's Alliance plot to take back what they see as theirs.  Feet walk the corridors of the Bai Long Tea Shop as Hao Te Zui trains his followers.  In the Badlands, whispers guide Marshal Bass Reeves toward a fateful showdown with Bill Hickock.  Through Worm Canyon, acolytes feverishly chant as Ursula, the Worm Queen, leads a bloody sacrifice.

This new expansion introduces a second dual faction dude, as well as an updated Law Dogs Fort 51 and new iterations of several returning cards as voted upon by our own players, including Inner Struggle and Doris Powell.   Also included is a brand new Solo scenario!

This expansion for the Doomtown Expandable Card Game includes 240 cards (4 copies each of 60 unique cards).

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Wisdom of the Council Squad Pack
description:
Expand your Star Wars: Shatterpoint adventures with the Wisdom of the Council Squad Pack! Featuring the wise and formidable Jedi Masters Kit Fisto and Ki-Adi-Mundi, this squad pack brings powerful new allies to your tabletop battles.

Features

     Iconic Jedi Masters: Add Kit Fisto and Ki-Adi-Mundi to your squad, each bringing unique abilities and strategies to your Shatterpoint games.
     Enhanced Gameplay: Incorporate the wisdom and combat prowess of these Jedi Masters to outmaneuver your opponents and achieve victory.


Contents

     Miniatures: Detailed miniatures of Kit Fisto and Ki-Adi-Mundi, ready to lead your squad.
     Character Cards: Cards detailing the abilities and stats of each Jedi Master, adding depth and strategy to your gameplay.
     Additional Components: Various game elements to integrate these characters seamlessly into your Shatterpoint missions.

Race to the finish in this letter-matching board game, Alphabet Crossing! Join a band of adorable animal pals as they match letters in a race across the water.

This is a Go-Fish variant where players match upper and lower case letters. For each match, they move their animal one space toward the finish. Additionally, players have the oppostunity to draw a "Hop Forward" card to have their animal move one space. First animal to land on the final spot is the winner!

—description from the designer

Dungeon Map Maker is a solitaire roll-and-write game played in two distinct phases. 
In the mapping phase, you will act as a scribe, mapping out the floor plan of a dungeon by choosing which rooms, tunnels, and caverns to draw. In the exploration phase, you will act as an adventurer, pursuing a main quest as you delve into the corners of the dungeon.
Set yourself up for success by carefully crafting your map, then challenge yourself against the worst of the dungeon!

—description from the designer

Peatris is a polyomino roll-and-write game about how Peat Bogs help sequester carbon and mitigate climate change. Every round, players have to decide how to fit in carbon polyominos into their peat bog. They also need to raise the water level to make sure the carbon is wet so it doesn't burn. The player who fills up their bog the best and avoids getting burned is the winner!

Instead of using the carbon deck, you can use the web app.

Designed and developed in partnership with the Foundation for the Philippine Environment (FPE).

—description from the designer

For slave-gardeners who already have the Collector’s Core Box and want to face a new Threat, while adding some fresh new twists to the game!

The First Garden as well as the upgrade pack (a dozen errata cards and some errata stickers to correct texts in the dialogue book and the Threat leaflets), and the stretch goals from the Kickstarter campaign will be pre-packaged inside the Season 2 Collector's box which includes the free expansion Vehegor’s Poem, a new 15-hour campaign unlocked during the Kickstarter, in the spirit of the "curses" from The 7th Continent.

Vehegor's Poem is a new game campaign, but it is not a Threat! Instead, it is a “Curse”!

For those of you who are not familiar with our previous game The 7th Continent: its story campaigns were called Curses, which work in a slightly different way to the Threats in The 7th Citadel.

It does not require a Threat Booklet, but it does require having the base box as well as the Threats "The final throne" and "The first garden," since it uses the Dialogue Book “A”. It also starts after the introductory scenario “A new beginning”.

It is open-world: this adventure gives you total freedom to explore however you wish. You will initially have only a very vague idea of how to accomplish the task that falls to you to lift this curse. You’ll need to explore the Collapsing Lands to find out...

It is lengthy: reaching each of the key stages of the adventure and overcoming the curse will require at least fifteen hours of play.

It is demanding: returning to the Citadel will not allow you to regain life points, nor to unlock your "Hope reborn!" cards.

The First Garden is a new Threat that will take you to whole new regions of the Collapsing Lands. Includes:

     1 Threat booklet (44 pages, 14.8×21 cm (A5), two-sided full-colour stapled)
     1 Dialogue Book "A" (80 pages, A5, two-sided full-colour perfectbound)
     1 rule sheet (A5, single-sided full-colour)
     2 Citadel leaflets (Achievement Trackers) (A4, two-sided full-colour)
     126 cards (unique ID numbers K1460 to K1585) (80×80 mm)

Roll 4 Rum is a strategic dice-based adventure game where players sail across a hex-based map, aiming to visit at least five harbors before reaching the final destination. Dice rolls determine fate events, movement, and encounters with obstacles like whirlpools, fog, and sea monsters. Players collect resources, including fish, flotsam, and pirate gold, to maintain their crew and ship. Points are earned through exploration and challenges, and the player with the most rum points at the end wins. Strategic planning and resource management are crucial.

—description from the designer

These elite Lobotomy 1 miniatures are now playable in Lobotomy 2 with the included Daily Nightmare cards! With this pack, you will be able to play two additional scenarios.

1x Big Bad Wolf,
3x Piggy
3x Nun
4x Angel

boardgame: Lobotomy II: Manhunt – Lobotomy 1 Monsters: Nightmare
description:
These nightmare miniatures from Lobotomy 1 are now playable in Lobotomy 2 with the included Daily Nightmare cards! With this pack, you will be able to play four additional scenarios.

1x Executioner
1x Faceless
1x Venoma
1x Tire

With the Styria school atlas game, you can discover Styria in a fun way and train your spatial orientation. Through exciting tasks and tricky challenges, you collect points and learn more about this beautiful province. Grab your teammates and dive into the Styrian adventure!

The door creaks open, the footsteps stomp loudly, and the eyes all are averted on you. Some are just curious, some are more than that. You get to see a couple of familiar figures too. It looks like you aren't the only one with a story, everyone just feel on edge tonight. All it takes is a little kick-start to set it all off...

and the light suddenly goes off. The guns start blazing, the blades start flying, and you too aren't going to let this chance slip away. Tonight brawl has just begun.

Welcome to Blackout Bar Brawl! You step into the bar with a personal vendetta, and tonight’s the night to settle the score. The bar is shrouded in total darkness,  and in the midst of this blackout, all hell breaks loose. No one knows who’s an ally or an enemy, but one thing is certain: it’s every person for themselves in a wild, all-out brawl!

The game ends when you successfully kill your target with your own hands, OR survive until the end because your target was accidentally killed by someone else, making everyone in this bar the new object of your revenge.

—description from the designer

boardgame: OrcQuest Warpath: Beast in the Woods
description: In Beast in the Woods our brave Orc Heroes are called back to the Bloody Woods to help deal with a marauding pack of vicious Gnolls and Wargs. Who leads these fierce, flea-bitten dogs? Will the Orcs be strong enough to face what this pack deals out? And more importantly, what kind of Loot do they have?

This micro-expansion contains a trove of 13 cards that will surprise even the saltiest sea dog. They can be easily folded into any games of Ahoy, whether you're playing with the base faction or expansion content.

At the start of the game, players are given two cards and asked to pick one. Choose wisely though, as they can radically transform your plans! (Some of it may be cursed. Don't say we didn't warn you.)

boardgame: TKKG Junior: Escape-Adventskalender – Die Jagd nach den Weihnachtsganoven
description:
Advent, how wonderful!
TKKG - that is: Tim, Karl, Klößchen, Gaby and their dog Oskar - enjoy the cozy atmosphere in the festively decorated town. But is it really so peaceful? A strange observation at the Christmas market leads the friends to a mysterious message. What's behind it?
The detectives investigate - and you're in on it! Solve the case with TKKG! Read how the story continues each day and unravel a tricky task to help solve the case.

The detective tasks
Exciting search, logic and word puzzles - clever text puzzles - fun number puzzles - ingenious combination puzzles and much more!

Contents:
1 word puzzle behind each of the 24 doors
24 envelopes with the exciting story in 24 chapters and a puzzle for every day
Small tools for solving the puzzles - such as magnifying glass, coding disc or cube
A large-format poster with TKKG Junior
Every day a sticker for the poster that completes itself gradually

In addition, all detectives need a pen, paper, a brush, water and a device that can read QR codes (e.g. a smartphone with corresponding app). With fun, excitement and lots of adventures, the investigations start


Klappentext

Adventszeit, wie wunderbar!
TKKG - das sind: Tim, Karl, Klößchen, Gaby und ihr Hund Oskar - genießen die gemütliche Stimmung in der festlich geschmückten Stadt. Doch ist es wirklich so friedlich? Eine merkwürdig Beobachtung auf dem Weihnachtsmarkt führt die Freunde zu einer geheimnisvollen Nachricht. Was steckt dahinter?
Die Detektive ermitteln - und du bist dabei! Löse den Fall mit TKKG! Lies dazu an jedem Tag, wie die Geschichte weitergeht, und enträtsele jeweils eine knifflige Aufgabe, um bei der Lösung des Falls weiterzukommen.

Die Detektivaufgaben
Spannende Such-, Logik- und Worträtsel - pfiffige Texträtsel - lustige Zahlenrätsel - geniale Kombinationsaufgaben und vieles mehr!
family: Misc: Advent / Christmas / Jule calendar game

description:
Be the first player to complete all 12 rounds in this Rummy Style game. In case of a tie, the player that completes the 12th rounds with the highest score is the winner.

Each round all players are dealt 12 cards. The goal is to complete your objective and get rid of all your cards to score the round.

In Rummathon Royale, players compete to form SETS & RUNS while cleverly disrupting their opponents' strategies. Each round presents new challenges as you balance building your hand & attacking others. The game’s dynamic mechanics ensure that every move counts, keeping the tension high until the final card is played. With the right mix of skill, luck, and deception, you can outsmart your rivals & claim victory.
