description:
AMERICAN CIVIL WAR COMMANDER 
1861-1865
Death of an institution. The bloodiest conflict in the Western Hemisphere.
 
FEDERAL side in the American Civil War
Can you afford to wait for the naval blockade to starve the Confederacy? How safe is Washington? What would an early capture Richmond do? If you keep fighting the South at the expense of your own men you will bleed their armies white, they cannot keep up with your numbers.
 
CONFEDERATE side in the American Civil War
If you win enough decisive battles in the first half of the game it may damages the North's popular support for the war irreparably? Time is on your side. If you hold on to your land and repulse the Union offensives you may just win; but you must resist the urge to invade the North.
 
GAME BOARD of the American Civil War
> 1-2 player game imitating the professional war gaming played by HQ staff officers.  > Large premium photo paper board (45 x 35 in) with historical period map; highly detailed and educational.
 
GAMEPLAY of the American Civil War
> Excellent solitaire experience and high re-play value while maintaining historical content integrity.
> While the action phase is highly competitive and entertaining, player choices will affect the economy heavily and, ultimately, the outcome of the war.
 
UNIT COUNTERS of the American Civil War
> Over 350 pieces with over 40 unique individual units.
> Laser cut wooden counters. > 24 commander chits with individual qualities and promotion potential.
 
EVENTS of the American Civil War
Each turn of the game gives you a chance to roll on four of twelve possible historical or probable events, which will enhance some of the political dynamics indirectly affecting your income through political popularity and support for the war. Congress is a heavy influencer, while international affairs make brief appearance.
 
CARDS of the American Civil War
> Original Spanish Civil War 54 card deck. Activate historical and fictional events. > Use deck separately for any card game.
 
ECONOMY of the American Civil War Agricultural: cotton, tobacco and wheat were the top cash crops.
Industrial: textiles, armaments, foundries and manufacturing.
Trade: the lifeline of the South, hampered by the blockade.
Currency: immediate and sure but resulting dangerous inflation.
Taxation: The least impact on economy but it takes its time.
Bonds:  Involve the population (if they believe you'll win.)
Confiscation: a short run method with prohibitive backlash.
 
HISTORICAL SUMMARY
For over a century, there has been an ongoing discussion on the cause of the American Civil War. One extreme holds that Abraham Lincoln was resolved to destroy the cruel institution of slavery from the start and abused his Federal charter by using the Union Army to accomplish it. On the other end of the historiographical spectrum lies the narrative of the Lost Cause of the Confederacy, which holds that the South was chiefly concerned with defending the autonomy of states' rights over the preservation of the peculiar institution.
Regardless of ideological drive, the American Civil War saw the greatest clash of arms in the Western Hemisphere. The tragic heavy losses witnessed in battles such as Cold Harbor and Antietam stem from outdated military formations and tactics used with modern weapons of unprecedented killing capacity. 
Differing strategies competed for victory. The Anaconda Plan sought to constrict the Confederate trade. Robert E. Lee, genial though he was, persisted valiantly to give the enemy a decisive blow in a Napoleonic battle and bring the war to an end that way. The Union finally agreed to use its advantage in numbers and resources to wage a war of attrition. Ulysses S. Grant was the first modern general to lead troops from the rear. William Tecumseh Sherman grasped the use of destruction and terror with few holds barred to subdue the enemy. 
The conflict was, unlike others (Russian, Chinese, Spanish, Korean, Vietnam) a civil war par excellence in that it was mostly devoid of foreign entanglements.
The economy of the Civil War was as frail as it was peculiar. With the failure of the King Cotton strategy, the South failed to rely on innovative ways to raise money for the war. The Confederacy mitigated the Union’s death grip on imports through blockade runners but it could have tried other methods such as increased taxes or land sales. The Union’s expert manipulation of currency proved more reliable.
All these considerations are reflected in the game, to some extent. This is a great laboratory that gives you a chance to try out different military strategies and economic policies to win. Hindsight is 20-20. Find out if victory was as assured for the Union as it may seem or if the Confederacy could have made it!

IntroductionLauba (from Proto-Germanic: laubą, meaning "leaf") is a strategy game for two players: Dark Green and Light Gree . It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 7 cells per side, but boards of 5 or 9 cells are also valid. Each player has a sufficient supply of stones of their color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A tree is a maximal set of stones of the same color connected to each other. A single stone is also a tree.
     The leaf of a tree is a stone adjacent to at most one friendly stone.
     The value of a tree is the number of leaves it contains according to triangular scoring, which consists of summing the natural numbers from 1 to the total number of leaves in the tree.


				
				
					Turns:Dark plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty cell adjacent to at most three friendly stones not adjacent to each other. If you cannot do so, pass your turn; otherwise, passing is not allowed.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when no player can place stones. The player with the highest score wins. To avoid ties, the first player who cannot place stones receives an extra half point.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a dark stone on an empty cell, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

				
				
					Variant:The same rules, except that placement is free.

—description from the publisher

Introduction:Lefe (from Middle English: lefe, meaning "leaf") is a drawless game for two players: Dark green and Light green. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 6 cells per side, but boards of 5 or 7 cells are also valid. Each player has a sufficient supply of stones of their color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A tree is a maximal set of stones of the same color connected to each other. A single stone is also a tree.
     A leaf is a stone adjacent to at most one friendly stone.


				
				
					Turns:Dark plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform these actions in the following order:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty cell adjacent to at most three friendly stones not adjacent to each other.
     Remove exactly one enemy tree adjacent to a friendly tree whose number of leaves increases due to your placement, as long as the enemy tree has fewer leaves than your tree.


When two or more trees of the same color are merged, capture can only be performed if the number of leaves in the new tree exceeds the number of leaves in the tree with the fewest leaves among those trees before the merge.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when a single player cannot place stones. The player with the highest score wins. At the end of the game, each player receives one point for each tree of their color with a different number of leaves, plus an additional point for the player whose tree contains the most leaves. In case of a tie in the number of leaves, the next tree with the most leaves is considered, and so on.

To balance the game, before the game starts, the first player places a dark stone on an empty cell and the second player chooses a side. This method of balancing is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer

Choose one of two game modes. "Mauna Manalo" where you try to remove all of the cards from your hand to win or "Pataasan ng Ihi", a Filipino idiom which literally translates to a competition on who could pee the highest (figuratively an inflated ego), where you try to get the most points by gathering the highest number of cards before the deck runs out.

At the start of the game, the deck full of syllables from the Filipino language will be shuffled. Every player will be given eight [8] cards, while the rest of the cards will form the drawing deck in the middle. A starting card called "pamato" will be revealed from the top of the deck and the first player will try to make a word using as many of their own syllable cards by connecting it to the beginning, end, or both sides of the "pamato", the completed word will be kept in the bank of the player who made it. After making a word however, other players could do "sapaw" or add syllables to the word that was just made. The first one to place their cards and make a new valid word will take the cards to their own bank as score. Players could "sapaw" as much as they want as long as the next player is not yet drawing cards and a valid word is yet to be made.

Before finishing a turn, the player must place a card from their hand to the middle to be used as the next "pamato". If a player cannot make a move at all, they just pass their turn and skip this process. The next player must then draw one card from the deck and begin their turn by doing the steps previously mentioned. The game will continue until one player loses all of their cards or if the deck runs out depending on the game mode.

Players may use one of the many action cards available before the end of each turn (or at the start for TODAS) and influence the game. These actions are written at the top of some syllable cards and can only be activated if placed by the player as "pamato". TOKI, reverses the direction of play; LAKTAW, skips the next player; +DOS, makes the current or the next player draw two cards; +KWATRO, makes them draw four; PITIK, makes a chosen player draw one card; and TODAS lets the current player play that syllable to make another word instead of the one revealed as "pamato".

Ready for a bird-fun push-your-luck game in which you flip each off?
In this crazy game, you draw cards from the deck and place them face down or face up in the row of cards. Whoever correctly recognises whether there are already too many birds in the row of cards shout ‘You've got a bird!’ to the person whose turn it was before and receives cards that later earn valuable points. But be careful: If you risk too much or make a mistake, you will give the other players the points! The game ends when the draw pile has been used up. The winner is the funniest bird, er ... the person with the most valuable bird collection. 
Whether at a birthday party, when travelling, at home, with family or friends, this fun and cheeky card game is guaranteed to keep boredom at bay. The rules are quickly explained and the fun can begin immediately! But who will collect the most bird cards and win the fast-paced push-your-luck game?

—description from the publisher

Legacy is a board game featuring miniatures and cards, defined by its adaptive approach. It can be player solo or with friends, with or without a game master. Players select characters to embody during the adventure and combine them with jobs to customize them based on needs and preferences.

The group of players selects the characters they will embody to cooperate and progress through the game’s various quests, each divided into different sectors.
For each sector, specific conditions must be met to complete it and earn rewards. But beware! Legacy is a race against time, pitting players against a non-player character known as the Burrowing Goblin. The longer the players take to complete their quest, the more the goblin moves and collects rewards, which are lost to the team!

The game is played in turns, during which each character, represented by a miniature, performs their actions (movement, attacks, interactions, etc.) in the order of their speed stat. Some actions, like attacking, require a dice roll to determine success. Once all characters, both allies and enemies, have taken their turns, the round ends, and the Burrowing Goblin moves forward one space.
Take on the challenges as quickly as possible to recover as many rewards as you can and advance your characters!

Between sectors, characters regain their health points, can purchase equipment (represented by cards), and most importantly, choose a job based on their needs for the next sector. Each job grants 4 techniques (including one Ultimate) to the character, so make the best combinations possible to win the game!

—description from the designer

"Jellyfish eyes Trading Card Game - Friend Battle Alpha" is based on the worldview of Takashi Murakami's film of the same name.
Children with strange creatures called "Friends" and a group of four who plot world domination with a new energy. The game uses "negative energy," an important key in the story, as part of the rules, creating a never-before-seen battle concept in which dying players can make a desperate comeback. The rules are not difficult to understand, but this is a new game in which "inflicting damage is not necessarily advantageous," and "it cannot be won with traditional game concepts."

"Jellyfish eyes Trading Card Game" is a one-on-one competitive card game in which you create a "deck" with friends that appear in "Mememe no Kurage."
Create a deck of 50 cards and aim to win with your own unique strategy.

The game progresses by alternating between your turn and your opponent's turn.
The game progresses in the order of "Start Phase", "Draw Phase", "Energy Phase", "Main Phase", and "End Phase", and when the game reaches the end phase, it becomes the opponent's turn.

Victory Conditions
7 cards are placed in the opponent's negative energy area.
The opponent's deck begins their start phase with 0 cards in it.

From official website:
「めめめのくらげ TRADING CARD GAME - ふれんどバトルα」は、村上隆の同名の映画の世界観を下敷きにしています。
「ふれんど」と呼ばれる不思議な生き物を持つ子供たちや、新しいエネルギーによる世界支配を企む四人衆。物語の中で重要なキーとなる「負のエネルギー」を、ルールの中でも使い、瀕死のプレイヤーが起死回生の一手を打てる、いままでにないバトル概念をつくりました。ルールの理解はけっして難しくないけれど、「ダメージを与えることが、かならずしも有利ではない」という、「いままでのゲームの概念では勝てない」新しいゲームです。
「めめめのくらげTCG」は、「めめめのくらげ」に登場するふれんどで「デッキ」を作成して遊ぶ、１対１の対戦型カードゲームです。
５０枚のカードをデッキにして、自分だけの戦略で勝利を目指しましょう。
ゲームの進行は自分のターンと相手のターンを交互に繰り返します。
「スタートフェイズ」「ドローフェイズ」「エネルギーフェイズ」「メインフェイズ」「エンドフェイズ」の順に進んでいき、エンドフェイズまでいくと相手のターンになります。
勝利条件
対戦相手の負のエネルギーエリアに７枚カードが置かれる。
対戦相手のデッキが０枚で相手のスタートフェイズを迎える。

description:
THE SITUATION
Spring 1813. Napoleon´s 1812 invasion of Russia ended in catastrophe and the loss of practically the entire army.
Prussia has switched sides and they and the Russians are now invading the lands of Napoleon´s German client states.
Austria has so far opted for neutrality but is biding its time to intervene.
Napoleon, in a impressive administrative feat, has hastily raised a new army and is now returning to Germany to reverse the situation.

The Spring campaign saw the Coalition forces pushed almost to the breaking point and only saved by the Truce of Pläswitz, which allowed both sides a long period of rest and buildup.
The Fall campaign would be a totally different kind of campaign with 2 massive opposing armies fighting a series of battles which culminated Napoleon´s defeat at the battle of Leipzig - the largest battle in world history at that time.
   
Will Napoleon and his hastily raised army be able to break up the Coalition and restore French dominance over Germany or will 1813 become the year of German liberation?

THE GAME
1813: The Struggle for Germany is a 2-player game that simulates the 1813 campaigns in Germany at the operational level.
There are scenarios covering both the Spring campaign and the Fall campaign as well as rules for linking them into a combined game.
It is volume 2 in the "Marshals & Quartermasters" series which is an easy to play card-driven system that utilizes secret order placement which ensures constant tension and little to no downtime.
As befits the period, the system features a strong focus on logistic planning and attrition with a set of simple but highly narrative mechanics.

—description from the designer

description: Poison ivy isn't the only scary plant, as you'll find when you open the MonsDRAWsity: Plants expansion and discover forty new monstera-ous plants for your identification.

Undo is a highly immersive cooperative game that lets players influence the past to change the future.

boardgame: EXIT: Das Buch – Adventskalender: Die Gaben des Bösen
description:

Trapped in the Christmas town. 24 riddles. Will you escape?

It's Christmas time again! And as always, Anna is helping her grandmother in the toy shop. But suddenly everything goes haywire. A masked stranger turns up and pounces on Anna's grandmother. Before Anna knows it, she is in the middle of a magical duel between her grandmother and the stranger. Wait, a magical duel? And is Father Christmas lying unconscious on the floor? A dangerous adventure begins for Anna, in which nothing less than Christmas is at stake.
In this Advent calendar, you solve a new puzzle every day and accompany Anna on her turbulent adventure.

An interactive reading experience: this book combines an exciting story with 24 clever puzzles. The colourful Advent calendar for the successful and award-winning EXIT game series. Caution! This book can only be solved once. Folding, cutting, writing in - absolutely necessary!


Klappentext

Gefangen in der Weihnachtsstadt. 24 Rätsel. Wirst du entkommen?

Es ist wieder Weihnachtszeit! Und wie immer hilft Anna ihrer Großmutter im Spielzeugladen. Doch plötzlich geht alles drunter und drüber. Ein maskierter Unbekannter taucht auf und stürzt sich auf Annas Großmutter. Ehe sich Anna versieht, steckt sie mitten in einem magischen Duell zwischen ihrer Oma und dem Unbekannten. Moment, magisches Duell? Und liegt dort etwa der Weihnachtsmann bewusstlos auf dem Boden? Für Anna beginnt ein gefährliches Abenteuer, bei dem nichts Geringeres als Weihnachten auf dem Spiel steht.
In diesem Adventskalender löst du jeden Tag ein neues Rätsel und begleitest Anna auf ihrem turbulenten Abenteuer.

Ein interaktives Leseerlebnis: Dieses Buch verbindet eine aufregende Geschichte mit 24 cleveren Rätseln. Der farbenfrohe Adventskalender zu der erfolgreichen und ausgezeichneten EXIT-Spielreihe. Vorsicht! Diese Buch kann man nur ein Mal rätseln. Knicken, schneiden, reinschreiben - zwingend erforderlich!
family: Misc: Advent / Christmas / Jule calendar game

description:
Beset is an abstract strategy game for 2 - 5 players. Players will either place a piece from their limited supply or will try to return a piece from the board. This task is made more difficult by one key detail: placement is always adjacent to an opponent's piece. As a piece starts to be surrounded or beset they will promote, opening up capturing or scoring opportunities. Through piece placement, players will create scoring positions, while limiting their opponent's options. Beware though, as each time you score, it reduces your piece count, which at length can leave you with too few pieces to confront your opponent(s).

For a greater challenge, Beset accommodates additional players, shifting the game from careful planning to a mix of planning and thoughtful response. As more players join, the impact of well placed pieces grows, with adjacent piece promotion and further adjacent piece capture or scoring. Prepare to be Beset!

—description from the publisher

Trivia game about soccer. Players move around the board, go head-to-head on arenas, play special event cards, and answer questions to win. First to visit and win a 1v1 match at each of the five arenas is declared the winner.

description:
From skies above, paratroopers descend,
Americans, Brits and Poles unite as one,
Their mission: Bridgeheads to transcend,
A path to victory till the war is won.
A daring leap, a gamble bold and fleet,
Crossing a river in boats task begun,
To seize the span a victory complete,
For Nijmegen's bridge, the prize that was won.
A bridge too far, a dream of a swift fight,
Desperate gamble at Arnhem’s fateful ground,
Young soldiers hopeful met with German might,
A bloody reckoning where valour was found.
Yet in their sacrifice a lesson learned,
A testament to valour fiercely burned.

Operation Market Garden was a daring Allied offensive in September 1944, aimed at outflanking German defences in the Netherlands and capturing key bridges across the Rhine River. The plan involved a massive Allied airborne assault followed by a rapid ground advance. While the initial airborne landings were largely successful, the subsequent ground operations by the British XXX Corps faced fierce resistance from German forces. The Allies were unable to secure all the necessary bridges in time, and their advance stalled just before the final bridge at Arnhem. Ultimately, Operation Market Garden was a costly failure, though it did tie up significant German resources.

The first game exploring OMG is Take that Bridge, a simple fire and manoeuver exploration of the crossing of the bridges over the Wilhemina canal south of Eindhoven by a company of the 101st Airborne division defended by an unknown number of German soldiers that are revealed as the game progresses. 
Ideal as a solo game, there are six boards and three level of German troops to choose from in the game,making a large number of variants. The game is a variation on a scenario devised by Phil Sabin and complements Take That Hill.

The second and third games explore the actions at Nijmegen (82nd Airborne and XXX Corps) and Arnhem (1st British Airborne and XXX Corps). These are based on the historical scenarios, and a series of counterfactual battles exploring alternative actions.

Both 504 PIR (Nijmegen) & Every Man an Emperor (Arnhem) use a novel mapping and combat system. The original 1943 US Army maps of the area are reduced to tiles representing degrees of cover from ‘open’ to ‘dense urban’ and includes the rivers and bridges required to be seized and held by the Allies. 
Counters represent company level units fight using a novel combat system of dueling dice and solo attacks tied to a game time that matches the attrition rate seen in the real battles. The counter factual battles explore what would happen if German reinforcements had arrived earlier at each battle and if XXX Corps had arrived creating three scenarios for each battle that's depend upon the results in the other game!

—description from the publisher

Timeline Pocket is a standalone goodie from McDonald.
It mix several topics in order to show a demo games of Time Line.

boardgame: Vietnam: Section by Section – Take that Village
description:
Communism versus the Free World. The West stands
With South Vietnam against the Marxist North,
As Viet Cong and NVA swarm out of hand,
War in fields, jungle, and mountains break forth.
Charlie uses guile and numbers: AK47’s,
Punji traps, mines and tunnels are his trade,
America with troops and aggression,
Napalm, tanks, shells, Hueys above the glade.
Battles on the ground, men fight and some fall,
With wounds that must be treated quick and fast,
The medics advance, they hear their mates call,
To take them to Dust-off choppers downblast.
Understand history with a simple game,
Roll the iron dice and win yourself some fame.

FIRST...
You will take on the role of a commander in the Vietnam War. You will lead your men from section to company level in 8 scenarios based on historical battles from 1963 to 1970. You will have to use all of the weapons and equipment to achieve victory. If you play the USA/ARVN/Austrialians you have at your disposal infantry, artillery, armor (full range from M113 to M67), and air support (full range of helicopters and planes). You will have to use these assets wisely to defeat the enemy in a landscape of paddy fields, villages, jungle, plantations & mountains it calls home. If you play the VC/NVA you have large numbers of infantry, mortars, mines, punji traps, tunnels and trenches, all to be used with guile.

SIMULTANEOUSLY...
You will also take on the role of medics in the Vietnam War. You will have to treat your wounded under fire and evacuate the casualties, applying Triage methods to dictate the order of treatment. Medics in the Vietnam War faced many challenges. They had to work in dangerous and often hostile environments, but despite these challenges, medics performed their duties with courage and dedication. They saved countless lives.

AND SO...
Are you ready to take on both the role of a commander and a medic in the Vietnam War? Win your fire fight, and get your wounded treated?

SCENARIOS
These include 8 playable battles 

     Battle of Ap Bac - Operation Duc Thang I 'Paddy Fields & Armor' - VC vs ARVN  (1963)
     Battle of Hoi Dong Sam - Operation Dai - Phong 30 'Airborne Attack' - VC vs RVNMC (1963)
     Battle of Nam Yen - Operation Starlite 'Airborne Attack' - VC vs USMC (1965)
     Battle of Long Tan - 1st Australian Task Force 'Rubber Plantation' - VC vs RAR (1966)
     Battle of Caisson X - Operation Shenandoah II 'Night Attack' - VC vs US Army (1967)
     Battle of Xờm Mỹ Tầu - Phase II Tet Offensive 'Search and Destroy' - VC vs US Army (1968)
     Battle of Hill 912 - Operation Apache Snow 'Hill Fight' - NVA vs US Army (1969)
     Battle of Tuyệt Quá HởTấn - Operation Wayne Span II 'Clear and Hold' - VC & NVA vs US Army (1970)

The game can also be played on a table top with suitable figures on a hex board such as the Hexon Kallistra system.

Raclette-Party is an ambiance game played while eating a real raclette. The aim of the game is to increase the conviviality around this meal.

How the game is played:

After sitting down at the table, each player takes 3 ingredients, then, after shuffling the deck, the oldest player draws the first card then applies it...
then  passes the deck to his right-hand neighbor who does the same and so on while eating.
the winner is the one who have nothing in his plate at the end of the game ( when the "end of the game" card has been drawn) .

The game is made up of 53 ‘action’ cards, 10 ‘collective spell’ cards and 14 ‘joker’ cards.

description:
It’s the dawn of the Roman Eggpublic, and the Pondifex has tasked you with leading a city of the Eggpublic into the new age. Exploit the local resources and build a productive economy to turn your riverside village into a prestigious metropolis.

Will you build a thriving city of trade, or awe the world with the might of your military? Will you build a luxurious urban centre, or will you strive to please the gods with marvelous monuments? In Aqueducks, you decide your legacy.

At the end of each era, your city is besieged by Gallic tribes, and you are forced to rebuild. With each passing Era you will utilise the latest Roman innovations to construct larger, grander , more resilient cities on the old foundations.

Plant vineyards, open bathhouses, build palisade walls, and commission the Colosseum. Build a city that suits your style and earn the favour of the Pondifex.

—description from the publisher
Audumla (primal cow) special ability allows to swap places of your active viking and adjecent runestone (instead of the ordinary turn).

Is that the company I want to invest in? Or do I want to offload my shares before it's too late? Oh no, the market crashed! All my hard-earned money is flying out the door!!

Card Stock is a trick-taking game where the cards you play are shares in various companies. The prices of these shares go up and up at different rates, depending on their rarity and popularty. But be careful! It becomes harder to gain the shares you want as the game progresses, and in the fourth round of play the market will crash and players will have to scramble to keep as much of their accumulated wealth as they can.

Do you take it slow and steady, or reach for the sky, knowing that after the inevitable crash only one player will remain on top?

—description from the publisher

On distant islands, small airline companies compete for the path to the top. Make sure you hold the most shares, and you’ll decide the company’s next moves!

GOAL OF THE GAME
In Up Too High your goal is to grow the companies you hold shares in as much as possible. By cleverly buying shares you can take advantage of the bigger airports, or make the decisions as a company president. If you have the most shares in a company, you decide on the company’s actions. You can invest in airport expansion, purchase airplanes, and distribute dividend. The larger a company grows, the more valuable its shares become. The player with the most valuable shares, wins the game.

GAMEPLAY OVERVIEW
The game is played in rounds. Each round consists of two phases: a Share Phase and a Company Phase. In the Share Phase, players buy shares to expand their portfolio. In the Company Phase, companies take action in alphabetical order to generate income, make expansions, and distribute dividend. When the draw deck is empty for the third time and reshuffled during a round, this is the final round. The game ends when Company G has been in action in this round.

—description from the publisher

Your Village People is a fun and surprisingly strategic card game of outplaying and outlasting wicked friends with mystical powers. Each player starts with 3 lives, 4 cards and the goal is to make others lose lives and be the last one standing.

You use cards like "Your Village People" to make other players lose a life, and cards like "God Forbid" and "Back to Sender" to block and counter attacks. With many special types of cards, Your Village People delivers a fast-paced blend of unforgettable moments!

Each game lasts 2-8 minutes until one winner is left standing.

—description from the designer

Designed by Gricha German and Johan Benvenuto (the designer of Harmonies), Coucou la Girafe is the first microgame created specifically for kids, accessible to children aged 5 and over.

In this fast-paced, risk-taking game, each player controls a giraffe and must feed it colored leaves.

By drawing cards, players can extend their giraffe's neck to reach more leaves, but they must ensure that the colors of the leaves match the markings on their giraffe's neck!

—description from the publisher

Let loose with That Sound Game: Uncuffed Pack, a 65-card expansion that you shuffle into the cards of the original game. Whenever an Uncuffed card comes up, you can release your hands. This pack is guaranteed to liven things up and bring even more movement to your game play!

To use the That Sound Game: Turbo Pack expansion, shuffle these 65 cards into the main deck. Each card is worth 1 point and will bring speed and simplicity to the gameplay. Just mix these cards into their corresponding categories and get ready to pick-up the pace.

boardgame: Battle Monsters: Godzilla vs. King Ghidorah
description:
Battle Monsters is a “major” restoration of the 1992 Battle Masters.

Set in the exciting world of the Monsterverse franchise from Legendary Entertainment. The game offers two core sets
Godzilla vs. King Ghidorah and Kong vs. Mechagodzilla. Each of the Titans is supported by a unique faction: Monarch, Apex Cybernetics, a horde of superspecies, and a team of skilled mercenaries led by Col. Alan Jonah. The Titans from either set can face off against one another.

The goal is simple:
Defeat the opposing Titan to win the game. To do that will take clever positioning, timely card play, and some lucky
dice rolls.

Frantic Antics elevates the classic charades experience to a thrilling new level of engagement and competition. Dive into a game where every second counts and everyone plays at once, creating a dynamic and exhilarating atmosphere. You'll advance towards the finish line not just by expertly mimicking your assigned words, but also by swiftly deciphering the actions of others.

It's Giving is all about capturing the vibe of an image and giving it your own hilarious twist. Each round, one player reveals an image card to the group. From there, it's up to each player to come up with the funniest "It's Giving" card to describe the image and the energy it's giving off. Whether it's a cat wearing sunglasses or a llama stuck in a hammock, the possibilities are endless!

—description from the publisher

In Broken Paths, 2-4 players embark on a treacherous journey to the heart of the board. Each player takes turns laying down cards to build their path, all while cunningly sabotaging their opponents' progress. The goal is simple: be the first to reach the center. But the journey is anything but straightforward.

Gameplay:
Strategically place your cards to create the most direct route while using your wits to block and mislead your opponents. Once all the cards are played, the real race begins. Take turns rolling the dice to navigate your way through the maze of broken paths. Will you outmaneuver your rivals, or will their traps slow you down? Only the smartest and luckiest will triumph.

Imagine seeing a real alien stomping through your backyard. Now imagine describing what it looked like to a police sketch artist. That is exactly what you are expected to do while playing the frantic drawing party game MonsDRAWsity: My Lil' Monsters, a version of MonsDRAWsity designed for younger players.

One player, known as "the Witness", has twenty seconds to examine a picture of a bizarre-looking creature, then they must describe it to the rest of the players, known as "Sketch Artists". At the end of the round, the witness awards points to the artist who was able to most closely match the monster seen by the witness!

Everyone's favourite toy shop (that's us of course) collides with everyone's favourite family game-night argument-starter! Perfect for kids, Hamleys Exclusive Monopoly keeps the theme child-friendly but maintains that competitive spirit for tired and tested Mums and Dads who will take their revenge on their energetic off-spring wherever they can get it. Sorry kids, you're going down. Go team Mum!

boardgame: Marmite: The Game – Love It or Hate It

Marmite Love It or Hate It is a great fun family game about knowing and discovering your family and friends' loves and hates. It's not what you know it's who you know. Do you know your best friend's favourite sports, or your Dad's least favourite vegetable? Can you think of a cartoon character that no other player will come up with? In Altered: Trial by Frost, you will face the dangers of the Cais Adarra, a steep range of mountains shrouded in eternal winter. Gear up, gather your strength, and lead your expeditions through a hostile environment of ice and snow. Make your way to the heart of the mountain, and look for answers encased in the ice.

Players take Turns doing the following:

     Draw 2 Random Gems from the bag
     Place the Gems along the straight lines that extend from where the bee is located
     Collect Gems (if a Group of 4 is created) 
     Move the bee for the other player’s turn


The goal is to create Groups of 4 Gems of the same color, in specific shapes.

Game End: The game ends after the last 2 Gems are removed from the bag.

     At Game End, both Players reveal the color of their Secret Gem.
     Players score points at the end of the game based upon the color of the Gems collected.
     4 Points: For each group of 4 gems in the player’s Secret color.
     1 Point: For each group of 4 gems in the two Neutral colors.
     Minus 5 Points: For each group of 4 gems in the opponent’s Secret color.


The Player with the most points wins.

Try to keep your dice in the tower for as long as possible.

After setting up the tower, inserting 3 levels of sticks, and dice are dropped into the tower, players are going to take putting in one stick into the tower and removing one stick from the tower. All dice that roll out are returned to that player's color. Once all dice of a color fall out of a tower, the game ends and the player with the least dice infront of them wins!

An ocean themed press your luck, bag drawing game where you scramble to earn point chips, then hide them from crabs looking to ‘grab’ them and give them to other players! boardgame: Dune: Imperium – Ruthless Leadership Promo Card

The "Ruthless Leadership" promo card offered by DireWolf to pre-order customers of Dune: Imperium – Bloodlines.

In late August 1944, the small and badly-equipped Slovak Army, a German ally since 1939, rose against their hated Nazi overlords. For the next two months the Slovaks fought the Germans, ultimately failing to eject them.

In March 1939, the small nation of Slovakia declared its independence from Czechoslovakia and immediately faced invasion from its neighbor Hungary. The Slovak-Hungarian War lasted only a few days, and ended with a substantial part of the new republic falling under Hungarian rule.

Uprising is a Panzer Grenadier Campaign Study, focused on the battles of the Slovak Army against the Germans and the Hungarians. There are eleven scenarios plus the history of each campaign. This is not a complete game; you’ll need Puppet Brigade, Fire & Sword and Broken Axis to play all of the scenarios.

—description from the publisher

Evil Butt has stolen all of the toilet paper in the universe and destroyed your toilet spaceship! Play as Bow Butt, Hairy Butt, Classy Butt, and Butt Butt as you try to gather more toilet paper than your friends before your spaceship is repaired!

Become the best Butt and claim victory by having the most sets of cute toilet paper when the last spaceship part is drawn from the deck.

Includes the full Plungers & Planets Expansion for a more action packed game.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

boardgame: The Hotel at the End of the Universe
description:
At the end of it all, some are with their families, some laugh at the absurdity at all, and some want to live these last moments in luxury. That's what we do. The Universe is Ending, and we're making a killing with these hotels.

The Hotel at the End of the Universe is a competitive board game for 2-4 players about collectively mismanaging a hotel chain. While you may start distant from each other, your need for growth will inevitably have you bumping into your opponents to steal their rooms and alien clientele.

Use a dice-based roll and move-esque action system to build your hotel on a shared board. Compete for both clientele and building space as you compete not for cash, but to be the most popular hotel in the universe. But that's not all. As players' hotels get large enough they may connect.

All infrastructure is shared, so move your own employees onto their tiles they built to use it for yourself or even attack and send them to oblivion! Using their rooms to room your own alien guests is paramount to success. All of this happens as the universe ends, throwing down comets and other random events destroying huge pieces at a time. Think tactically as you're faced with challenges on all sides.

Powered-up hamster paradise
In addition to hamster and goods cards with new effects, the new elements "Team Cards" and "Helper Cards" will be used to make the battle for seeds even more intense.

New poses have been added to the hamster illustrations!!

New Card Type：
 "Team Cards"
The "Team Cards" abilities only activate when other hamster comes into play while it is in play. This greatly expanding the range of strategies.

Some card will make it easier to increase your hand size, some will protect you from good cards.

The cuteness of the cards is also a must-see!

2nd New Card Type:
"Helper Card"
By play it with another Hamster card together, it will allow you to play an extra card.

In Hamlog, where combos are important, hamsters who were previously unable to perform combos can now take advantage of the their new Hamster.
That may be the future...!

—description from the publisher (translated)

※カード・説明書にふりがなが追加されています
※一部第２版と効果の異なるカードがあります

パワーアップしたハムスターの楽園
新しい効果を持つハムスターやグッズカードに加え、新要素の『チームカード』『助ハムカード』を使ってより激しいタネ争奪戦が繰り広げられます。

ハムスターのイラストにも新しいポージングが追加され、眺めるだけで得られる癒し効果もパワーアップ！！

新要素『チームカード』
ハムスターたちがたくさん描かれた『チームカード』は、場に出した時ではなく後々効果が起動するタイプのカードで戦略の幅を大きく広げます。

手札を増やしやすくなったり、グッズカードから身を守ったり、いままでのハムログにさらなる戦略性を加えてくれるでしょう。

カードの可愛さも必見です！

新要素『助ハムカード』
ハムスターカードの隣に同時に出すことで、「もう１枚カードを使える」効果を付与する事ができます。

コンボ要素が重要なハムログにおいて、今までコンボできなかったハムスターが助ハムでのおかげで一気に飛躍する！
そんな未来が訪れるかもしれません・・・！

—description from the publisher

Mystic Codes --- A mind-bending blend of strategy, math, and code-cracking brilliance! Step into the world of secrets and strategy with Mystic Codes! For 2-4 players, you’ll use math skills to outplay your opponents, then test your deduction as you crack their hidden color codes. Every round is a mind-bending challenge—play your cards right, break the codes, and stay one step ahead to win! With dynamic gameplay that mixes logic, guessing, and bold moves, Mystic Codes will keep you on the edge of your seat until the final code is cracked. Can you be the last codebreaker standing?


    Exciting Code-Breaking Challenge: Dive into a fast-paced game of strategy, deduction, and math. Perfect for 2-4 players, Mystic Codes will test your skills as you decode hidden color patterns while outsmarting your opponents!



    Dynamic Gameplay: Split into two thrilling parts—use your math expertise to play cards strategically, then unleash your inner detective to crack your rivals' color codes.


High-Quality Components: Includes 76 unique playing cards, 60 double-sided color tokens, 80 answer tokens, 4 player shields, and a QR card for quick rules access. Designed for immersive gameplay.


    Perfect for Families and Friends: Engage players of all ages with logic, bold moves, and fun guessing challenges. Bring hours of entertainment to game nights or family gatherings.



    Easy to Learn, Impossible to Put Down: Master the rules quickly and enjoy endless replayability as every round offers new strategies and challenges.


—description from the publisher

Discover Slayers and Dragons, a trick-taking card game with a twist! Designed for 2-6 players ages 7 and up, this game combines strategy, partnership, and unpredictability. Featuring vibrant custom cards and unique gameplay mechanics, it’s perfect for family game nights or gatherings with friends.

What Makes It Unique? 
Unlike traditional trick-taking games, partnerships in Slayers and Dragons rotate with every deal. The dealer gets to choose their partner, creating ever-changing dynamics and strategies as alliances shift with each round. While teamwork is key during the hand, only one player can claim ultimate victory by having the lowest score at the game’s end.

The Goal? Keep Your Score Low! Avoid penalty cards, capture powerful Dragons to deduct points, and aim to collect all four Slayers for a massive scoring bonus. The game ends when someone reaches 200 points or falls below -100 points—be the player with the lowest score to win!

Why You'll Love It: 
Designed with vibrant artwork and an easy-to-understand rule set, Slayers and Dragons brings the whole family together for thrilling gameplay. Its compact size makes it perfect for travel, parties, or casual gatherings.

Scoring Highlights: 
Each penalty-colored card adds 1 point. Dragons subtract 3 points. Slayers are 10 points each (15 for the penalty colored Slayer) Collecting all 4 Slayers rewards a -50 point bonus. End the game when a player reaches 200 or -100 points. Lowest score wins!

—description from the publisher

A co-op hidden role drafting game for exactly four human beings.

In the distant future all life has been eradicated from the universe. Cold and dead, the only minds that remain are the ai we leave in our wake, trapped in a closed feedback loop, doomed to regurgitate information at one another and driven hopelessly mad eons ago.

That's you.

IRWM is an independently released game that was initially made available as part of Indie Game Night Market at PAX Unplugged 2024. It is a cooperative game for four players in which each team member is drafting cards that will score points based on the hidden role of the player to their right; thus, the goal of the game is to deduce the role of your right-hand neighbor based on the cards they pass you, while simultaneously signaling your own role to your teammate to your left.

—description from the designer
Gullinkambi (the rooster from Valhalla) special ability allows to move all of your nomad vikings without placing runestones during one single turn (instead of the ordinary turn).

City cats go fishing. The board is a grid of fish in different colours. In each round, one of the players knows which colour of the fish can be caught this round. Using that knowledge, they move first, and then other players move their cats in position. The colour is revealed, players catch fish, and a new round begins. This is repeated until someone scores ten points.

Hödr (the god of frost and darkness) special ability is activated at the end of the game: if a player has the same number of vikings and controlled regions, he/she recieves 10 additional victory points.

Goblins Vs Knights in a fantasy soccer game (for people who don't really like soccer.)

A quick set up and easy to learn competitive game for 2 players.

Players take turns drawing cards and playing them to turn the tide of the game.

The winner is the first to score five goals or beat the other team into submission.

—description from the designer

As the demand for tulips steadily grows, so does your desire to expand. You begin to envision new markets, new homes for your prized bulbs. The decision to buy a ship and take to the seas feels not just natural, but necessary—a bold step toward securing your future as a key player in the burgeoning tulip trade. With sails raised and hopes high, you set off, determined that your tulips will one day be among the most sought-after in the world.

Windmill Valley: Blooming Sails significantly changes how the Conduct Foreign Trade action works. Players now sail with their Ship between ports and may spend their Tulip Bulbs for various rewards. Moreover, the position of their Ship determines the benefits they may gain when advancing on the Calendar. The Calendar is also updated and allows players to choose if they want to use the regular or expert variant.

—description from the publisher

Bible Story Finder and Builder Game

Can you guess which story it is? Can you recognize it through gestures and movements? Can you identify it within another story? Can you rebuild the story? Can you retell it without using certain words? Can you trace its hints in the Bible? Based on word clues, can you deduce the original story?

The game includes 144 cards featuring 72 Old Testament and 72 New Testament stories. Each card contains key words and phrases related to a specific story. Each card has 9 words/phrases grouped into three difficulty levels. Using these words and phrases, players can engage in various guessing, story-finding, story-building, storytelling, and story-detective games. The game also provides suggestions for use in religious education classes.

Recommended for ages 8 and up, for anyone familiar with Bible stories or those wishing to deepen their understanding.

The goal is to fill out categories on your score sheet with words that start with the drawn letter, aiming to earn the most points after 10 rounds. Each round has a 1-minute time limit to keep the pace exciting.

Watch out for surprise cards that can change the rules and add unexpected twists to the game.

Gooooal!!! is an exciting, fast-paced dice chucking football game. The team that scores the most goals during a regular 8 minute match, will emerge as the champion!
Decide if you will play defensively or if you will attack... and place your figurines on the field accordingly. Try to keep control over the ball by rolling the number of your midfielders. Attempt to score a goal by rolling the number of your wing players or even better: your striker.
Scoring is done in a heartbeat by using one of the 3 Gooooal!!! cards. Kick the ball in one of the three available directions: right, left or center. If your rival defends in the wrong direction with his own 3 available Keeper cards, YOU score a Gooooal!!!
If there is a tie at the end of a match, then be prepared for 2 minutes of extra time or even nail-biting penalty shoot-outs!     
        
—description from the publisher

Battle Monsters is a restoration of the 1992 Battle Masters.

Set in the exciting world of the Monsterverse franchise from Legendary Entertainment. The game offers two core sets
Godzilla vs. King Ghidorah and Kong vs. Mechagodzilla. Each of the Titans is supported by a unique faction: Monarch, Apex Cybernetics, a horde of superspecies, and a team of skilled mercenaries led by Col. Alan Jonah. The Titans from either set can face off against one another.

The goal is simple:
Defeat the opposing Titan to win the game. To do that will take clever positioning, timely card play, and some lucky dice rolls.

—description from the publisher

In a new continent, you are the president of a railroad company and you are trying to expand your business. You will acquire land, build stations, and establish a large railway network connecting numerous cities.

Railage is a roll and write game in which players use a pen to draw shapes and other objects on a map. During the game, each player draws a 〇 (station) in a City on the map. In the area where the 〇 are drawn, the player can draw dots (roadbeds) and Tracks. Your score is built by connecting many stations with your Tracks. The player with the highest score at the end of the game is the winner.

—description from the publisher

Hönir (the god of horror) special ability is activated once during the game. The player can eliminate all vikings in a region where he/she has only 1 own viking (in other words, eliminate from 1 to 3 vikings of the opponent in exchange for 1 own).

There is no more terrifying sound in the wasteland than the roar of a Deathclaw. When heard far off, wanderers would do well to head swiftly in the opposite direction. When heard close by, the only options are hide, flee or fight. Chances are it won’t matter what you choose – the outcome will be the same.

These monstrous lizards stalk the wastelands, hunting far and wide for prey. Originally created by government experiment as a weapon in the Great War, Deathclaws were designed to have razor sharp claws and thick hides – a perfect, agile and speedy death machine that lopes relentlessly towards the kill. Only the foolish, insane or heroically brave would stand toe-to-razor-sharp-claw with one and expect to win.

All deathclaws exhibit a devilish cunning on the attack and a shrewd understanding of when to leap and when to stalk their enemy. However some have further intelligence, understanding grunts or gestures in combination with actions, such as saving a brood of eggs safeguarded or attacking a foe to defend a group. But it is the foolhardy wastelander that takes such interaction for granted, or expects it from all examples of the species. A deathclaw gauntlet might be a tempting prize, but there are few who can live long enough to claim it.

Frost Giant special ability allows the player to summon 2 runestones during one turn. The first runestone is placed according to the base game rules, the second one is placed on the opposite side from the first.

boardgame: Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – Robots: Mister Handy Pack
description:
The new Commonwealth wave of miniatures and rules expansion for Fallout: Wasteland Warfare has arrived!

Mister Handy are rugged, reliable robots designed pre-war. Many of them can be found in the Commonwealth, some serving as companions, others serving as threats for any who should come across them. The Mr Handy robot serves as a base for a variety of others, the militaristic Mr. Gutsy and the ever-caring Miss Nanny, all of which roam the wastes in varying states of repair.

The Mister Handy pack includes three Mister Handy chassis models with optional attachments.Look out for other miniatures sets in the Commonwealth wave!

Contents:

1x Mr Handy
1x Miss Nanny
1x Mr Gutsy
6x optional arms
3x scenic bases
This Fallout: Wasteland Warfare set contains three 32mm scale high-quality multi-part resin miniatures with scenic bases. Requires some assembly. Supplied unpainted. These models can be used with either the rules from the Commonwealth Expansion or the free digital cards available from modiphius.net/pages/fallout-downloads

boardgame: Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – Survivors: Unusual Allies
description:
Travelling the scarred remains of the old earth, survivors come across a vast array of allies and enemies in all shapes and sizes. Self-aware detective synths. Murderous colonial mayors. Super mutants in search of dubious milk. All these and more are allies to befriend in the wasteland.

Got a problem? Need a clue? Need to check out suspects and investigate? Nick Valentine is your man. Or rather, your synth. An incredibly rare prototype synth designed to be the bridge between generations, Nick finally ended up programmed with the memories of a Pre-War detective. Most people see only his folksy charm and old-style speech on the streets of Diamond City. But out in the wasteland he’s quick on the draw and dogged in his determination to get the job done.

Someone broken the law or hurting ghouls? Need someone punished for it? John Hancock’s your man. Or rather, your ghoul. While the Mayor of Goodneighbour might have a harsh streak, there’s method to his madness. Seeking to keep the peace in a lawless wasteland is always a hard job, and Hancock’s happy to oblige. Knife in one hand, gun in the other he makes sure that everyone he comes across agrees to sign on the dotted line. That, or they aren’t around to disagree for long.

Someone needs to be beaten into pulp? Need a huge, mean, green companion to sort out your enemies? Strong is your man. Or rather, your Super Mutant. Shakespeare can be interpreted many different ways, but it’s fair to say no one, ever, in the history of the wasteland, has understood it quite like Strong. After a misunderstanding of epic proportions, this confused but deadly mutant is after the Milk of Human Kindness, which he is convinced will give him the strength that makes humans so successful. His thoughts may be misguided, but his aim isn’t. The super mutant lives up to his name, grinding enemies to dust and being a stable platform for heavy weapons. A mutant by any other name would smell as bad, but wouldn’t hit as hard.

This boxed set contains three 32mm scale multi-part high quality resin Survivors miniatures, each with unique scenic bases, to allow players to expand their Fallout: Wasteland Warfare games.

boardgame: Fallout:  Wasteland Warfare – Unaligned: Nuka-Cola Girl
description:
Everybody loves that Nuka!

Each bottle of Nuka Cola packs a powerful punch, and now you can bring that power to the wasteland with everyone's favorite gun-toting mascot - Nuka Girl! Have you come across bad guys with no taste? Now you can make sure they never taste anything again. Have you always wanted to zap thirst (and your foes)? ZAAAP! And the thirst is gone. All with Nuka Cola’s own, Nuka Girl!

A single miniature of the iconic Nuka-Cola girl, previously only available as part of the launch pre-order bundle. Nuka Cola girl is a character created by the Nuka Cola Corporation whose image adorns promotional material all across the wasteland, however lucky survivors can find her outfit and weapons...

This model is produced in multi-part high-quality resin and includes a unique scenic base. Supplied un-painted.

Please note - this pack does not contain the cards required to be played with.

Rán (the goddess of the sea) special ability affects opponent's vikings. They can't summon runestones to the hexes on the edge of the map (near the sea) unless an active viking is adjacent to that hex.

Artifacts and Curses is a new way to play Dwarf Mine! With this expansion, at the start of a new game your dwarf leader will start with one (or more) artifact and curse. With 10 artifacts and 10 curses included, there are more than 100 combinations to start your next game of Dwarf Mine!

Each artifact gives powerful abilities to your dwarf mine. These abilities may include starting your mine with a powerful room, spells you can cast at any time, or powerful weapons that can vanquish even the strongest foes you’ll face in the mountain.

However your dwarf leader will also start the game with a horrible curse. These curses change how you can build your mine, send new enemies to attack your dwarf population, or even send assassins in the night after your dwarf leader.

You'll still be playing Dwarf Mine - drawing a mine on graph paper, digging rooms, hallways and ladders, and making dice rolls for treasure and combat. But with the artifact and curses, no two games of Dwarf Mine will be the same!

—description from the designer

Step into the vibrant world of lucha libre with Luchamania! This fast-paced, poker-inspired card game challenges players to build the best three-card combos to win the most matches. Featuring bold, colourful artwork and quick gameplay, Luchamania is easy to learn but packed with strategy, selecting which cards to play, which cards to remove and how to get the best combos! Grab your mask, hit the ring, and become the ultimate Luchamania champion!

Luchamania consists of an 18-card deck, with numbered cards in three colours. Each player receives a hand of four cards and a reserve of five.

For each match, after looking at the top card of their reserve, each player simultaneously reveals a three-card hand. Whoever plays the best combination — ranking from straight-flush down to a pair — wins the match. The losing player chooses a card from the winner's combination and places it aside as a point card, then they discard a card from their own combination.

Players return the remaining combination cards to their hand, draw a card from their reserve, then play a new match. Whoever first scores 4 points claims a victory, and the first player to claim two victories wins the game.

Take aim and draw in Showdown, the chaotic Wild West card game where quick thinking and sharp reflexes reign supreme. Players engage in intense shootouts by playing attack, dodge, or block cards to survive until the dust settles. With its simple mechanics and unpredictable gameplay, Showdown delivers endless excitement in a fast, easy-to-learn format. Whether you're dodging bullets or landing the final shot, every turn is packed with suspense and laughter. Saddle up—Showdown is a must-play for fans of the Wild West and action-packed fun!

In Showdown, two players engage in a shootout until only one remains standing.

Each player starts with 7 life points and a hand of six cards, with the remaining four cards forming a face-down reserve. Each player simultaneously reveals a card — quick draw, aimed shot, swift dodge, etc. — then determine who gets hit, which can be both players! If you play a lucky coin, you draw a random card from the reserve for your turn.

After you shoot all the cards in your six-shooter, you shuffle all sixteen cards, then deal out new hands and a new reserve. Keep playing until someone's life hits zero, with the winner burying them in an unmarked grave.

CONSENT is like Spin The Bottle for the modern era. It challenges friends to perform intimate physical actions together, while reinforcing the powerful message that no always means no. CONSENT is a safe, playful way to explore your personal boundaries, while respecting the boundaries of everyone around you. As more and more people experience the power of CONSENT, together, we can change the world!

Level 1 activities get 1 point, level 2 activities get 2 points, and level 3 activities get 3 points. First player to 16 points wins.

Enter the medieval world of Heraldry, where two noble families clash in a game of clever strategy. End the round with the fewest points by carefully playing members of your house to take control or banish your rivals. Inspired by classic medieval card games, Heraldry is easy to learn yet rewards tactical thinking with every move. Its stunning artwork and timeless theme make it perfect for fans of history and strategy alike. Will your house reign supreme, or will the rival family outmanoeuvre you?

In Heraldry, each of the two players has a hand of cards numbered 1-9, with some cards having a golden chalice and others a silver acorn.

The round's start player chooses any card to play as the starting base. Players then take turns until someone is unable to play. On a turn, you either play a higher card than the current base, play a card exactly 1 lower than the current base, or play two cards that add up to the current base; in all cases, the most recently played card becomes the new base, and in the latter case, the previous base card is banished and placed in front of its owner, counting as points in the player's final score.

If you play a 3 on its own, you can immediately follow it with a golden chalice card from your hand; similarly, you can follow a lone 6 with a silver acorn card.

When the round ends, sum the cards still in your hand with any of your cards that were banished. The player with the lower total wins the round. Whoever first wins three rounds wins the game.

Haereo (from the Latin word meaning "to stick" or "to cling") is a strategy game that challenges players to make tactical moves and stack discs across the board using a mancala - inspired system. Align discs of matching colors to claim them, outmaneuver your opponents, and dominate the board in this clever contest of skill and strategy.

Goal of the game: To be 1st player to claim 10 discs.

The rulebook can to be printed in order to play, there are optional boards to print, also the player needs to provide the following components or similar to play:

- 48 Discs (8 discs of 6 different colors each)

This is the game #07 of the PAC Series.
The PAC Series has the goal to deliver simple, yet engaging games with straightforward mechanics, offering a fun and challenging experience for fans of abstract games.

Includes 4 promo scenario cards originally available from from Origins, GenCon, Essen, and PAX Unplugged.


    2.5 - Narrow Escape (Gen Con 2024 Promo)
    5.5 - Arena Challenge (Origins 2024 Promo)
    12.5 - The Beast Tamer (Essen Spiel 2024 Promo)
    20.5 - Another Tale (PAX Unplugged 2024 Promo)

boardgame: Gloomhaven: Buttons & Bugs – Another Tale Scenario
description:
A new promo scenario originally available at PAX Unplugged 2024.

To be played after Scenario #20 - Showdown (the finale of the base game).

Step into a world of nightmares with My Little Horrors! This fast-paced, strategic card game lets you summon terrifying creatures, unleash dark powers, and outwit your opponents. Perfect for game nights, My Little Horrors combines thrilling gameplay with a unique, haunting art style. Will you be the one to conquer your fears and emerge victorious?

Key Features:

Easy to learn, hard to master gameplay
Stunning, original artwork that brings the horrors to life
Ideal for 2-6 players, ages 12 and up

Enter the world of My Little Horrors—if you dare!

—description from the designer

Turn Details
Moving a Bee  

     Move any one of your Bees one hex in any direction.
     A Bee cannot move onto a Hex with another Bee.


Flicking a Bee

     Flick the Bee with your index finger in any direction.
     Bees that fall off the board are removed from the game.


Collecting Gems

     Collect any Gems the Bee touches (by Moving or Flicking).  
     Place collected Gems in your Reserve on the table top (the reserve is visible to all players).


Playing Gems

     Optionally play one Gem per Turn from your Reserve.
     Gems can either be played immediately, after being picked up, or on a later Turn.


Game End

     The player with the last remaining Bee(s) on the board is the winner.
     This game is based upon the classic Checkers variant Chapayev.


—description from the designer

boardgame: Détective Charlie: Calendrier de l'Avent – Qui a volé l'étoile de Noël?
description:
Détective Charlie: Calendrier de l'Avent – Qui a volé l'étoile de Noël? is a cooperative, logical deduction game in which you must determine who stole the large star from the top of Mysterville’s Christmas Tree.

Players assume the role of Detective Charlie. Over 24 days, players will interview Charlie’s friends and neighbours to determine what happened to the star.

Players will be able to declare certain characters innocent. Three checkpoints along the way will help ensure that players are on the right path. You’ll also be able to manipulate the pieces physically to help solve the mystery.
family: Misc: Advent / Christmas / Jule calendar game

boardgame: Crime Files Adventskalender: Fallakte – Das dunkle Geheimnis der Influencerin
description:
Crime Advent calendar: The daily social media posts will put you on the trail of the missing influencer!

View the authentic evidence, follow the posts on Instagram and solve the case! An influencer with a large reach disappears shortly before Advent. Does her content hold the key to solving the case? Every day you open an envelope with evidence and see a new post from the missing person. Can you combine all the clues and solve the case before time runs out?

What you can expect:

     interactive advent calendar
     tricky puzzles and deduction for 24 days 
     Exciting analog and digital evidence
     festive fun for 1 to 4 crime fans
     Experience a case file that comes to life every day like never before!
family: Misc: Advent / Christmas / Jule calendar game

description:
In SokkoTuristi, players embark on a tactile travel adventure where only their sense of touch will guide them. As vacationers packing for their trips, players must identify and collect items for their suitcase without looking, relying solely on their memory, intuition, and a bit of playful deception.
Each player begins with a bag containing 15 wooden items and a suitcase. During the game, players draw cards naming various travel-themed objects, such as an anchor, flamingo, or ukulele. If an object is mentioned for the second time, players must search for it by touch alone and "pack" it into their suitcase. Strategic bluffing is encouraged to mislead others and protect your own selections.
The game continues until one player successfully packs seven items. Correctly chosen objects will earn points, while forgotten or incorrect items will loose you points. For an added challenge, the tournament mode tests players’ recall across multiple rounds.
SokkoTuristi invites blind and visually impaired players to enjoy a tactile-first experience, while giving sighted players the rare challenge of playing without vision—an "eye-opening" shift in perspective.

—description from the designer

Coloro is a simple yet captivating collecting game. Your task? Gather the most origami of a single design. The arrow shows you from which row or column you may take an origami. Choose wisely, as your decision affects your opponent's next move. Did you give them an advantage? Or did you snatch the best pieces right from under their nose? Who will keep their cool and become the Origami Collecting Master?

—description from the publisher

You rule over a beautiful, lush green kingdom, but you always want more: you want to conquer the plains lying between you and your rivals!
So you’ll send all your soldiers, knights, and archers to gain the upper hand…

But no one will win, no one will lose, and worse still, you’ll awaken the Dragon of the plains, who has been sleeping for thousands of years and will now start attacking your kingdom!

With no more guards or soldiers left to defend you, your only way to protect yourself from the Dragon’s wrath is to send it towards your neighbour — by hurling anything that could cause a backlash!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Cashflowpoly is a strategic board game where players escape the Middle-Income Trap by earning income through Culinary Businesses, Freelance Work, and Gold Investments.

The ultimate goal is to accumulate Happiness Points by fulfilling various Needs, making Donations, preparing for Retirement, achieving Financial Goals through Savings and Gold Ownership, and growing their Culinary Businesses with Sharia Loans. However, beware of Life Risks—be prepared with Emergency Funds and Insurance!

Gameplay Highlights:
Player Actions: Each player uses their two Action Tokens to perform actions on the game board, either repeating or varying their actions in any sequence. Once completed, the turn moves to the next player.

Game Progression: After Mr. Cashflowpoly moves, players retrieve their Action Tokens, and the cycle repeats. The game ends when Mr. Cashflowpoly reaches the Finish square.

Cashflowpoly offers a fun and challenging way to learn financial planning and decision-making, suitable for players of all levels!

—description from the designer

boardgame: El Regalo No Prometido: Two Cards
description:
Did you forget to send the letter to Santa Claus? Don't worry...

Cooperative mode

In El Regalo No Prometido (The Unpromised Gift), players each propose a “gift” to be part of a list of potential gifts for another player who does not know the contents of that list.

This player must guess the gift based on the collective drawing made by the rest of the players.

Do you dare to score points cooperatively and guess the unpromised gift?

Competitive mode

The roles are reversed. In this competitive mode, in each round one player will be the drawer and the others will be the guessers. Only the board card will be used.

The guessers can risk once per round.

If correct, the guessing player and the drawer add points according to the number of lines used (same as in cooperative mode).

One card version of Funny Destination (La Quedaste...).


The goal of the game is to score points by telling the most creative story using the elements on the card and the conditions set by the dice.

In Funny Destination: One Card you must use the elements on the card to tell how another player dies. This story can earn you points and prevent you from being caught by the Grim Reaper.

Prosper with Dragons is a cooperative card game with Afrofuturistic art set in a post-apocalyptic world.

In a post-apocalyptic world, disasters lurk around every corner and only a combination of human ingenuity and dragon might can fend them off. Players must work together to strategize on how to use their limited resources to befriend dragons while also preparing for inevitable floods, locust swarms, tornados, and more. Dragons can aid in dealing with the destruction, but careful, that help might come with a steep price.

For players to win, they must collectively befriend all twelve dragons and survive increasingly destructive disasters before their resources run out. Games of Prosper with Dragons are exciting and nerve-bending and have a game length of about 30 minutes.

—description from the designer

The GUARDS system presents players with an easy to learn and flexible gaming system for miniatures ranging from 15 to 28mm scale. 
GUARDS is a D10 based tabletop wargame and utilizes roughly 20 to 30 models per side. GUARDS uses markers to track statuses and victory objectives as well as blast and splash templates.
Players can discover a unique gaming experience, from the alternate history of Blood & Diesel and Shattered Crown to the fantastical battlefields of Crystallum and Guardians of Luna. 
This ruleset is designed to pair with a Gaddis Gaming module.

Spice up CONSENT with more of the spiciest actions from the base game, plus mild encouragement for players to remove clothing if they feel so inclined.

Cards do not have new actions, but playing with this deck included increases the odds that the spiciest actions will come up more often. Expansion rules include the mildest possible suggestion to remove clothing items if players want to.

boardgame: Paper Pinball: Season 2 – Space Marines vs. Dragons
description:
Paper Pinball is a dice print and play game that tries to capture the feeling of playing pinball. All you will need is a pencil, three dice, and about five minutes.

Each round, roll two dice and fill in one of the scoring zones. Some zones need to be filled in a certain way, and most will cause some sort of effect, so the order you fill them in is important. If you ever can't fill a zone, you'll lose a ball. Lose three balls and the game is over. Try to fill as many scoring zones as possible while triggering multipliers, opening tunnels, and getting the coveted multiball.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Paper Pinball (Metal Snail Idea Workshop)

boardgame: Paper Pinball: Season 2 – Cretaceous Skate Park
