In Detective Meow you help Gabby Girl and Pandy Paws catch up with Cakey Cat before he reaches the Sprinkle Party. Find on the map as many clues as possible, which will help you reach Cakey Cat. Detective Meow is a cooperative game that tests your observation skills and collaboration between players. On the board of Gabby's Dollhouse look for clues, which would bring you closer to catch up with Cakey Cat. If you manage to catch up to him you win the game!

—description from the publisher

Zaczynając od pierwszego gracza, muuusicie dokładać kolejne karty z reki na stos wykładania. Dokładane karty muuuszą pasować do tej na wierzchu stosu kolorem, symbolem lub cyfra. Dołożone karty należy kłaść jedna na drugiej tak, by zawsze na stosie wykładania była widoczna tylko jedna karta. Możecie wyłożyć naraz po kilka kart, o ile wszystkie pasują tym samym elementem do wierzchniej karty ze stosu wykładania.

—description from the publisher (Polish)

Starting with the first player, you must add cards from your hand to the play pile. The cards you play must match the top card of the pile by color, symbol, or number. The cards should be placed one on top of the other so that only one card is visible on the play pile at any time. You may play multiple cards at once, as long as all of them match the top card of the pile by the same element.

—description from the publisher

The players' task is to collect the most valuable treasures from the attic. The player who gathers the most points from the goal cards wins. Add cards to the treasures hidden from prying eyes, trying to claim them for yourself. During the game, not only will a good memory come in handy, but also the ability to bluff and anticipate your opponents' tactics.

—description from the publisher
 Zadaniem graczy jest zdobycie najbardziej wartościowych skarbów ze strychu. Kto uzbiera więcej punktów znajdujących się na kartach celów – wygrywa. Dokładajcie karty do ukrytych przed wścibskimi oczami skarbów, próbując zagarnąć je dla siebie. Podczas rozgrywki przyda się nie tylko dobra pamięć, ale też umiejętność blefu i przewidywania taktyki przeciwników.

—description from the publisher (Polish)

Step into the high-stakes world of startups and investments with Modern Mint, the strategy board game that dares you to fulfill your entrepreneurial dream and mint a fortune. While many board games explore fantastical realms, Modern Mint pulls you into the tempestuous seas of innovation and investment. It's a strategy board game that finds adventure in the real world, where the stakes are as high as the rewards and you can build an empire from your wits and daring.

				
				
					Game OverviewIn Modern Mint, 3 to 6 players step into the roles of visionary Founders or shrewd Investors to compete and collaborate over a series of game rounds, each representing a full business year. The goal is simple: become the wealthiest player by building the most valuable business empire. Four Founders will work to turn their dreams into reality, while two Investors will use their capital and influence to back the most promising ventures for a handsome return.

The game unfolds through three key phases each round:

     Fundraising Phase: Founders pitch their grand visions to secure capital. This is a dynamic stage of negotiation where deals are struck, partnerships are formed, and investors decide who to back. Will you give up equity for a cash infusion or bootstrap your way to the top?
     Action Phase: With capital in hand, Founders get to work. Grow your company by launching new verticals, upgrading your team, investing in PR & Marketing, or seeking guidance from powerful Mentors. An innovative action-point system creates a strategic tension: the more you do this year, the later you'll act in the next, forcing you to balance short-term hustle with long-term positioning.
     Revenue Phase: The moment of truth! See how your business decisions paid off as you calculate your earnings. But beware—unpredictable Market Event cards can shake things up with economic booms, recessions, or industry-wide disruptions, forcing all players to adapt on the fly.


				
				
					Key Features
     Real-World Inspired Gameplay: Experience the authentic challenges of entrepreneurship. Negotiate deals, manage resources, and balance risk versus reward under pressure. Every choice carries weight, mirroring the real founder’s journey from a bold idea to a thriving enterprise.
     Dynamic Player Roles: Play as a visionary Founder, building a company from the ground up, or as a powerful Investor, funding startups and shaping the market. The tension and synergy between these roles create a rich, interactive experience.
     Engaging and Social: Modern Mint is designed for memorable table dynamics. Alliances will form and crumble, friendly rivalries will emerge, and you'll find yourself cheering, gasping, and laughing as fortunes are made and lost. It's the most fun you'll have screaming at your friends!
     Strategic Depth and Replayability: With multiple paths to victory, unique company ventures to launch, and unpredictable market events, no two games are ever the same. You'll learn, adapt, and refine your strategy with every play.
     Business with Heart & Brain: More than just a game, Modern Mint is a celebration of bravery and smart decision-making. It teaches strategic thinking, adaptability, and negotiation in a way that is both entertaining and empowering, leaving you inspired for your next tabletop adventure.



—description from the publisher

It's summertime in Maple Valley, and the river market is open for business! The narrow streets and waterways are bustling with vendors hawking their wares and with customers in search of a good bargain. The market is growing, with new businesses opening all the time. Pick your path carefully, hire the right help, and set up a shop (or two) of your own in this quick, lively game for the whole family!

River Market is the new standalone game set in the cozy world of Creature Comforts. As forest critters, players take turns moving around the river market, activating the buildings they visit, hiring helpful new employees, and adding their own new businesses. Gameplay is quick, and before you know it, the market will be closed for another day. Will you make the biggest contribution to growing the river market? You'll have to play to find out!

—description from the publisher

Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C22 is a Medium difficulty exploration mission for the original zombicide.

Material needed: Season 1.
Buildings and car wrecks are blocking the way. You could use maintenance tunnels to make your way to precious supplies but...Are you afraid of the dark?

Designed by Jeffrey Alley

18xx set in the Papal State Marches region, Central Italy.

The game starts in 1855, right before the Unification, when the Marches were part of the Papal State at the time of Pope Pius IX. He was the first Pope to embrace railways, after the missed opportunities of Gregorio XVI - he was so enthusiastic he had a personal train built for him.

The game opens with an extended 1822/Amun Re Auction for Papal and Episcopal Concessions, Minor Companies and Privates. 
Concessions allow you to lay track with terrain discounts or steal money from the Pope. Minors can't lay track and have to rely on Concessions.
Privates are randomly attached to Concessions and give different powers, active either before (Papal Phase) or after the unification.
An Enthusiastic Investor is attached to each Minor Company, which can convert to a share of a certain Major Company or help floating it.

After the Auction the Papal Phase starts, which consists of a variable number of "Papal" Operation Rounds (2 to 7).

In these the Papal Concession will prepare the advent of the Strade Ferrate Romane, the National company, laying tracks from Ancona and preparing the treasury for train purchases, all the while stealing money for personal gain. 
The other players will use their Episcopal Concessions to lay track for their Minor Companies, starting inland near the mountains in poorer areas but running their trains and earning money right away.

It's up to the Papal Concession player to judge when the Minors are earning too much money and if the Strade Ferrate Romane has enough money and track to start, and to pull the trigger for the Unification of Italy. The Papal Concession converts to the SFR presidency, while the other Concessions have to guess when the Unification happens in order to convert to SFR shares (or be burnt by the Pope if they mistime that).

After the Unification the “standard” 18xx starts. The pre-game helps creating a different starting map and player positions for each game.

The game will progress through historical events like the Ancona bombardment, the Financial Crisis, the advent of the “Autocarro” (+4 Train) and end with World War 2 and a vote for the First Republic.

1855 is an incremental cap game with loans, variable interest, government bonds, auctions for failed companies, Minors being acquired by Majors, a National Company and Destinations.

And Geese. Very important. Bribe material for the antitrust official and bid tokens for the Auction.

PnP files and manual are linked in the forum, in English and Italian
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Shrapnel: The Official BattleTech Magazine is your map and compass for discovering heavy-hitting tales of BattleMech combat on the battlefields of the Hinterlands!

The Hinterlands region of the Inner Sphere is packed full of surprises: shifting loyalties, shady characters, selfless heroes, and more. Get lost in the uncharted chaos of this region with articles forecasting the future of Duke Vedet Brewer’s Vesper Marches and the independent planet of Blue Hole, a playable campaign for Barcelona, planet digests, technical readouts, and more—all by veteran BattleTech authors and some new trailblazers!

—description from the publisher

A whole family of moles has made the farm their home! The new neighbors prefer to live underground, so their eyesight is far from perfect. During their excursions topside they sometimes visit the garden patch and unwittingly snatch a vegetable... or two.

Finding the mole responsible is not an easy task! But luckily the hardworking bumblebee is always somewhere nearby collecting pollen. If anyone can help, it’s him! However, even though he can tell you some of the details, it’s up to you to solve the case!

—description from the publisher
publishers: Verlag Arbeitsmittel für Lehrer und Schüler

description:
Shrapnel: The Official BattleTech Magazine is your survival guide through the explosive minefields and incoming missile salvos in pulse-pounding tales of BattleMech combat on the battlefields of the 31st century and beyond!

Navigate the dangers of any battlefield and find your way to victory with tools like a technical article about ’Mech-identification systems, a tribute to the exploits of fallen heroes of the Fronc Reaches’ Colonial Marshals, a look at the meaning of chivalry in the Order of the Saber, an in-depth history of the Jade Phoenix OmniMech, unit digests, technical readouts, and more—from BattleTech veterans and the newest recruits!

—description from the publisher

Choose your menu, set your meeples according to your needs, and serve the diners while messing with your competitors.

Each turn roll the dice to get ingredients and the table you will serve, depending on your location you might get 1 or 2 ingredients, or none if you are not in the dice location but the other players will get resources. If possible get the order to the client and score points, first one to get 10 pts wins.


Elige tu menú, consigue los ingredientes en el supermercado y atiende los comensales, no te olvides de afectarte a tu competencia

Cada turno tira los dados para conseguir ingredientes y la mesa a la que vas a servir, dependiendo de tu ubicación puedes conseguir 1 o 2 ingredientes, o ninguno si no estás en la ubicación del dado pero los demás jugadores conseguirán recursos. Si es posible haz llegar el pedido al cliente y consigue puntos, el primero que consiga 10 pts gana.

A party game for BIG-EARED detectives!

Who’s been chomping the cheddar in this fun family game for 4 to 8 players? Are you Pinky the cheese thief, Spud the cheese-pushing cat, or an innocent mouse? It’s a game of paw-pointing mouse deduction for ages 5 and up!

Who Stole My Cheese? is a social deduction game for 4 to 8 players. Each player is dealt a secret role card — one player will be Pinky, the cheese thief, and another will be Spud, the cheese-pushing cat.

Each round, players close their eyes while Pinky steals one piece of cheese and Spud moves another. When players open their eyes, they discuss and debate who they think Pinky is. The player with the most votes loses a cheese. Play continues until either Pinky steals and banks 5 cheeses or Pinky is caught.

—description from the designer

Embark on an adrenaline-fueled adventure through Italy's scenic landscapes in Lupin III: The Italian Adventure! You and four other daring thieves have just stolen a priceless gem from Italy’s most powerful criminal cartel. Now, you’re on the run with Inspector Zenigata of the ICPO hot on your trail! Outsmart rivals, face high-stakes decisions, and use clever strategies, high-tech gadgets, and a touch of luck to make your getaway.

This game is perfect for fans of thrill-packed strategy and anyone who loves the Lupin III universe. With iconic characters, unique abilities, and dynamic gameplay, it combines the thrill of a heist with the challenge of evading capture in an immersive, replayable experience. And to complete the adventure, all games come with officially licensed sleeves and playmats, enhancing your gameplay with high-quality accessories.

Each session feels like a new escape as you roll dice to strategize, confront unique Opposition cards, and unlock powerful upgrades with character-specific packs, acrylic standees, and collectible cards. The high-quality components, stunning artwork, and immersive playmats capture the pulse-pounding world of Lupin III, making every move feel like part of the story.

3, 2, 1 Piñata! is a fun game based on a piñata theme. You’ll collect candy cards, play piñata-themed cards, and even use a piñata stick.

In this game, you want to collect candy cards.

To get a candy card, you’ll usually play a single card with a number that adds or subtracts from “the count”. If, on your turn, the count reaches 0 or 10, you score a candy card. But beware, the count applies to the whole group. As you bring the count closer to 0 or 10, you increase your opponents' ability to score as well!

You can also play cards that steal, protect, or sabotage other players to stop them from winning.

And lastly, there's the piñata stick card. There's only 1 in the entire deck. The person who has this card will earn double the candy when they score, but they can also lose double the candy. Take possession of this card at your own risk!

The goal is to get 5 candy cards. The first player to do that wins the game!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Cthulhu: Death May Die – Extra Campaigns
description:
Campaign Decks are one of the unique features of Forbidden Reaches. These sets of cards add new depth and plotlines to your gameplay, allowing you to connect any 3 Episodes and transform it into a battle with higher stakes against an Elder One that gets ever stronger.

That is, of course, not enough for Investigators desiring more stories to tell, so the Extra Campaigns Expansion features 3 new Campaign Decks, diving deep into the mythos lore and telling different stories: The Rescue, The Hidden Enemy, and Keeper of Reality.

boardgame: La Guerra per la Sicilia 264-241 A.C.
description:
La Guerra per la Sicilia 264-241 A.C.
Roma e Cartagine si confrontano per la prima volta. 
Una guerra senza respiro, combattuta sulla terra e sul mare. 
Il vincitore non solo otterrà la Sicilia, ma si candiderà a diventare la potenza egemone sul Mediterraneo per i secoli successivi.

---

Rome and Carthage confront each other for the first time. 
A war without respite, fought on land and at sea. 
The winner will not only obtain Sicily, but will also be a candidate to become the hegemonic power in the Mediterranean for centuries to come.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Operation “Case Blue” was intended to lead Axis troops to conquer the oil fields of the Caucasus, but it ended in the Stalingrad Pocket. Considered the decisive battle of World War II, Stalingrad was the culmination of the German operation to complete the invasion of Ukraine.

—description from the publisher (translated)

L’Operazione “Fall Blau” doveva condurre le truppe dell’Asse alla conquista dei pozzi petroliferi del Caucaso, ma si esaurì nella sacca di Stalingrado. Considerata la battaglia decisiva della Seconda guerra mondiale, Stalingrado rappresentò il culmine dell’operazione tedesca  per completare l’invasione dell’Ucraina.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Crabs in a Bucket: Shrimpocalypse is the first expansion to the base game, and features ten new Jokers, a special new suit called the Seabunnies, and an entirely new card type called the Four Horsemen of the Shrimpocalypse. In this variant of the game, players are trying to stop their opponents from reaching zero cards while simultaneously trying to stop opponents from winning the game by triggering the Shrimpocalypse. Each player is capable of playing one of the Four Horsemen of the Shrimpocalypse, to advance the game state towards an abrupt conclusion as the player to play the final shrimp in order wins the game through Shrimpocalypse. Use the powers of the Jokers and Seabunnies to stop them!

—description from the publisher

La Battaglia di Pavia: Febbraio 1525 is a simple Tactical level micro board-wargame.

The Battle of Pavia marked a moment of transition in military strategies, which would subsequently be characterized by the widespread use of firearms, as well as an important change in the composition of the troops, which now included a more homogeneous distribution of infantry, cavalry and artillery, visible simultaneously in the French and Imperial armies.

—description from the publisher (translated)

La battaglia di Pavia segnò un momento di passaggio nelle strategie militari, che saranno d'ora in poi caratterizzate dal largo utilizzo delle armi da fuoco, nonché di importante mutamento nella composizione delle truppe, che prevedeva ora una distribuzione più omogenea della fanteria, della cavalleria come dell'artiglieria, visibile contemporaneamente nelle armate francesi e in quelle Imperiali.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
This Vudù expansion features new curses which are Comics- and Manga-themed.

Also, it includes new artifacts and permanent effects.

Vudù expansion that adds a new mechanic: Parallel Worlds. When activating a new Parallel World, the first player that violates the World rule will make the rest of the players to earn points.

This expansion also includes new curses cards.

Digital Frontiers is a medium to heavy weight Euro style board game for 2 to 4 players, where each player starts with a conventional food and beverage company. These companies produce goods each turn and they can sell these products to markets; but the players aim to develop digital automation projects for six different departments of their companies, so that they produce more and better products, expand more, build more and have better reputation all in the most effective ways. In the end, the players earn victory points for their automation projects, their expansion, their earnings and their technological achievements.

—description from the designer

In Color Cats, players expand out from a central tile, aiming to make a large contiguous grouping of their color.

Each player gets a matching deck of cards, shuffles them, and places two of them face up in front of themselves. On a player's turn, they take one of these cards and place it next to where their cat figure is on the board, and walk their cat onto the adjacent space - activating any actions shown there.

The game continues until plays have played all but one of their cards, and the person with the most points wins.

Points largely come from having the largest group of adjacent spaces of their color. There are extra cats, point chips, and special actions.

Merchants of the Bazaar is a trading game for exactly 6 or 7 players.

In the game, the players are split between traders who sit at stalls, and traveling merchants who visit the different traders. The game takes place in real time and the players are trading cards which score differently depending upon the player's role in the game.

At the end of the game, there are two winners - one from each roll.

Nossete Catamquero is a team-based game that takes place on a see-saw. Teams are randomly assigned, and each team is affiliated with one side of the see-saw.

During the game, players simultaneously choose a card from their hand to play, and then they are revealed. The cards take effect in ascending order, and each card shows an effect - such as adding gems to the lighter side, or moving gems from the heavier side to the lighter side.

Once the players have played several of their cards, the round ends and the team whose side is heavier earns a point. The first team to earn two points wins.

Choice a Gift is a cooperative game themed around giving gifts. Each round, two setup cards are combined to form an occasion where you might give or receive a gift - such as "a company bonus that makes you sad" or "inheritance you want to return".

The players secretly choose a gift card from those in their hand, and these are combined with one from the deck. These cards are shuffled and given to the players, with the extra card being to the right of the active player.

The players look at their cards, and can choose to keep them if they think it is likely a gift that a player contributed to match the theme, or otherwise draft their cards - aiming for the erroneous gift to end up in the extra position to the right of the active player.

If the players can successfully do these for a few rounds, they win!

The Second is an auction game about coming in second.

Each player receives a lot of coins, and places them behind a screen. Each auction has a minimum bid that increases as the game goes on.

The players simultaneously place a number of coins in their fist and then reveal them. A series of payments take place, but the player who bid the second most will acquire the majority of the coins bid.

The game continues until only 2 players have coins left, and the person with the most coins wins.

Dice game, played with 6 D12 of different colors, and some scoring sheets.
The starting player rolls the dice, and marks numbers off on their scoring sheet that correspond to the numbers rolled, the black die is wild.  The other players can also use the black die rolled as wild. Then the next player rolls all the dice and the same thing, they use all the dice and the other players can use what was rolled on the black die as wild.  When you start a dice line, after that, you can only go up from that number, or down.  Players cannot go back to fill in a skipped number.

If they cannot or do not wish to use a die, they can mark a Ditlin, and reroll.

If they mark 3 numbers in the same line in a roll, they get a Heikai Bonus.

End game is triggered when the Heikai challenge is met (completed dice lines) varies by player count, or when a player has taken their 5th Ditlin.  Players then calculate their scores with Bonuses and Ditlin penalties, the highest score wins.

Mahesa "Mr Dude" Koroshiya is a time traveling World Leader who records major events revolving around a group of kids known as The Nays, throughout various timelines. Izumi, a Party Sanctuary World Official, has tasked Mr Dude with an assignment to create a chronology of everyone in The Worlds who has come in contact with The Nays on their journey to become World Leaders.

The Nays have worked together and had conflicts during their times in The Worlds. Mr Dude contacted some fellow time travelers and important figures in The Worlds to help him gather together as many people as they can to help him with this project.

The group must work together and sometimes compete against each other to find the best matches and ultimate team who will work together during the final "incident". Many World Leaders have alluded to the return of the first Nay, CourtNay, who plans to bring ruin and destroy The Worlds in revenge.

It's up to Mr Dude and the others to find the best team who will work together in the end to defeat CourtNay and seal her back away in the Timeless Abyss. Mr Dude calls upon Val, Clove, Yaguzi, Malik, and Rotle to help him through this journey. Each of them has a different approach to finding the ideal candidates. When they do find their winning team of their chosen challenge, they'll bring the winners along with them on their Linkship.


     To start, Mr Dude tasks participants with guessing the chosen character based on a series of hints and questions leading to the traits and types mentioned.
     Val pulls everyone together to find a team that shares at least 1 trait in common together.
     Clove looks for groups who share common traits and builds upon those.
     Yaguzi brings conflict into the group to fight until all forces have gone to battle.
     Malik tests the limits of the group and selects the one who can match the chosen Nay the most to build upon a team of those in common.
     Rotle leads the final battle against CourtNay by bringing the elite winners of the previous rounds together. Those who can't relate to the M, S, and R team leaders will be defeated by CourtNay and sent into the Timeless Abyss.


Now it's your turn to go forth and help Mr Dude and the others find these teams to bring to the final battle!

—description from the designer

In Bloodlines, players take on the roles of influential families vying for power and territory in a richly detailed setting inspired by the historical dynamics of 10th-century Western Europe. Each family line, or dynasty, has unique attributes, strengths, and weaknesses that can influence their strategies throughout the game.

As the game unfolds over multiple turns—each representing approximately five to ten years—players will expand their influence through diplomacy, assassinations, alliances and marriages, and military campaigns.

Players can also invest in technological advancements, religious power and cultural influence or simply amass more wealth than opponents. These options may strengthen their position and give them an edge over their opponents. Special events, such as plagues, revolts, and natural disasters, may take place, adding unpredictability to the game and requiring players to adapt their strategies on the fly.

Victory is achieved by one of two conditions: controlling four nations or having the highest score at the time of reaching the pre-agreed number of turns. This culminates in a rich tapestry of storytelling, where players' decisions shape the course of their families’ legacies and the fate of nations.

In Bloodlines, players are encouraged to immerse themselves in the historical context and narrative, making each game experience unique as they craft their stories of ambition, betrayal, and power in a turbulent time. The interplay of strategy, chance, and player interaction creates a dynamic environment where every choice can alter the trajectory of dynasties and the world around them.

Triumviratus is an expansion of the ONUS! series that gives you the opportunity to take command of the armies of Gaius Iulius Caesar, Gnaeus Pompeius Magnus, Marcus Licinius Crassus, and the civitates of Gaul, as well as an extensive selection of entirely new allied and mercenary forces, to immerse yourself directly in the armed conflicts that changed the course of the history of Rome, Europe and the Middle East between 59 and 44 BC.

A complete volume in the form of a new Compendium that, apart from offering a historical overview of this exciting period and all the art created exclusively for this expansion, will also offer an impressive variety of scenarios with which to recreate the numerous battles and sieges that marked the Gallic War, Crassus’ campaign in the East or the Civil War between Caesar and Pompeius, in order to get the best out of these army boxes. Thus, from the battle of Bibracte (58 BC), through the ambush of the Sabis River (57 BC), the battle of Carrhae (53 BC), the difficult siege of Alesia (52 BC), the battles of Dyrrachium (49 BC) and Pharsalus (48 BC), to the final outcome of the battle of Dyrrachium (49 BC) and Pharsalus (48 BC). ), to the final culmination of Munda (45 BC), we will soon be able to present a complete selection of campaigns and scenarios through which to travel the winding paths and dusty battlefields of the years 59-44 BC, to relive History… or change it decisively and forever, through the powerful armies that enter the fray at the hands of ONUS! Triumviratus.

—description from the publisher

Triumviratus es una expansión flexible de la serie ONUS! que te ofrece la oportunidad de tomar el mando de los ejércitos de Gayo Julio César, Gneo Pompeyo Magno, Marco Licinio Craso y las civitates de la Galia, así como de una extensa selección de fuerzas aliadas y mercenarias completamente nuevas, para sumergirte directamente en los conflictos armados que cambiaron el curso de la historia de Roma, Europa y el Próximo Oriente entre los años 59 y 44 a.C.

Un completo volumen en forma de nuevo Compendium que, aparte de ofrecer un recorrido histórico sobre este apasionante periodo y todo el arte creado exclusivamente para esta expansión, ofrecerá así mismo una impresionante variedad de escenarios con los que poder recrear las numerosas batallas y asedios que jalonaron la Guerra de las Galias, la campaña de Craso en Oriente o la Guerra Civil entre César y Pompeyo, a fin de poder sacarle el mejor partido a estas cajitas de ejército. Así, desde la batalla de Bibracte (58 a.C.), pasando por la emboscada del río Sabis (57 a.C.), la batalla de Carrhae (53 a.C.), el difícil asedio de Alesia (52 a.C.), las batallas de Dyrrachium (49 a.C.) y Pharsalus (48 a.C.), hasta el desenlace final de Munda (45 a.C.), próximamente podremos presentar una completa selección de campañas y escenarios a través de los cuales recorrer las tortuosas sendas y polvorientos campos de batalla de los años 59-44 a.C., para revivir la Historia… o cambiarla de forma decisiva y para siempre, mediante los poderosos ejércitos que entran en liza de la manos de ONUS! Triumviratus.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

This epic continuation of Kingdom Legacy lets you build your fleet to cross the ocean. Rumors of rich lands draws your attention. Will you survive the harsh seas?

This is an expansion for Kingdom Legacy, even though it has even more cards (160) than the base game. It counts as 2 expansions on your box, so make sure you have 2 more spaces on your score track before playing!

—description from the publisher

The army box of Iulius Caesar allows, naturally, to put in command of your forces the well-known Roman proconsul and dictator, paradigm of all the later Roman generals and emperors. Alongside him also ride the resolute Titus Labienus -whose loyalty to the Republic will lead him to abandon Caesar and fight under the banners of Pompeius in 49 B.C.- and the energetic Marcus Antonius, right-hand man of the proconsul during the Civil War. The remaining 37 cards of this box of 40 offer the player emblematic units such as the legion X Equestris -true elite of the armies of the proconsul- the fierce XIII Gemina or the Gauls enlisted in the V Alaudae, as well as a good quantity of legionary infantry resulting from the reforms of Gaius Marius -known since then as the elite of the armies of the proconsul, as a result of the same, as “Mario’s mules” -, mercenaries like the skilled Cretan archers and allies of the Caesarian forces like the fearsome Germanic horsemen, a group of troops with which they aspire to bring Gaul, Rome itself… and the world to its knees!

—description from the publisher

La caja de ejército de Julio César permite, naturalmente, poner al mando de tus fuerzas al archiconocido procónsul y dictador romano, paradigma de todos los generales y emperadores romanos posteriores. Junto a él cabalgan también el resolutivo Tito Labieno –cuya lealtad a la República le llevará a abandonar a César y militar bajo los estandartes de Pompeyo en 49 a.C.– y el enérgico Marco Antonio, mano derecha del procónsul durante la Guerra Civil. Las 37 cartas restantes de esta cajita de 40 ofrecen al jugador unidades emblemáticas como la legión X Equestris –verdadera élite de los ejércitos del procónsul– la arrojada XIII Gemina o los galos alistados en la V Alaudae, así como una buena cantidad de infantería legionaria resultante de las reformas de Gayo Mario –conocida desde entonces, a consecuencia de las mismas, como “mulas de Mario”–, mercenarios como los diestros arqueros cretenses y aliados de las fuerzas cesarianas como los temibles jinetes germanos, conjunto de tropas con las que estas aspiran a poner de rodillas la Galia, la propia Roma… ¡y el mundo!

—description from the publisher (Spanish)
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
Pompeius Army Box puts this great Roman general at the head of his army, as well as his sons, Pompeius Junion and Sextus Pompeius, along with the Republic’s outstanding hero Metellus Scipio, determined to defend the Roman State from Caesar’s ambitions… and in favor of their own! To accomplish these objectives, the experienced Pompeian commanders count on a plethora of Mario’s mules, antesignani “special forces”, and numerous conscripts to overwhelm the enemy, but also on allies harvested by the powerful gens Pompeii in Hispania, such as the Iberian scutati and the Hispanic Vernacular legion, ready to serve their patrons to the death. Scipio added to these hosts the Numidian infantry and elephants recruited in Africa -sometimes in very persuasive ways-, to crush anyone who dared to oppose them, no matter how powerful or how many victories they had won in battle.

—description from the publisher

La caja de ejército de Pompeyo pone a este gran general romano al frente de sus huestes, así como a sus hijos, Pompeyo el Joven y Sexto Pompeyo, junto al prócer de la República Metelo Escipión, decididos a defender al Estado romano de las ambiciones de César… ¡y a favor de las suyas propias! Para cumplir con estos objetivos, los experimentados comandantes pompeyanos cuentan con una plétora de mulas de Mario, “fuerzas especiales” antesignani, y numerosos reclutas tirones con los que avasallar al enemigo, pero también con aliados cosechados por la poderosa gens Pompeya en Hispania, como scutati iberos y la hispana legión Vernácula, dispuestos a servir a sus patrones hasta la muerte. Escipión suma a estas huestes la infantería y elefantes númidas reclutados en África –a veces por modos demasiado persuasivos–, para aplastar a todo el que ose oponérseles, no importa cuán poderoso ni cuántas victorias haya cosechado en batalla.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
Crassus’ army box leads the player to take up the banners of the legions that the “richest man in Rome” formed to fulfill his dream of conquering the opulent Parthian Empire. He is accompanied by his son, Crassus Iunior, a skilled and courageous general, who had been trained in Caesar’s first campaigns in Gaul. His archenemy, Surena, responsible for his defeat and death at Carrhae, shares a box with both of them, ready to put his genius at the service of the Parthian armies. Crassus’ legions do not know what is coming, but with the help of the fearless vigiles and numerous eastern mercenaries, such as the Galatian legionaries and the Rhodian slingers, they may be able to defeat fate and change the history of the world. If not, the survivors of Carrhae can always make a name for themselves in the service of the armies of the distant Han Empire, beyond the other end of the Orb known to the Romans.

—description from the publisher

La caja de ejército de Craso lleva al jugador a tomar los estandartes de las legiones que el “hombre más rico de Roma” formó para cumplir su sueño de conquistar el opulento Imperio parto. Le acompaña su hijo, Craso el Joven, diestro y valeroso general, curtido en las primeras campañas de César en la Galia. Comparte caja con ambos su archienemigo, Surena, responsable de su derrota y muerte en Carrhae, listo para poner su genio al servicio de los ejércitos partos. Las legiones de Craso no saben lo que se les viene encima, pero con la ayuda de los arrojados vigiles y numerosos mercenarios orientales, como los legionarios gálatas y los honderos rodios, puede que sean capaces de vencer al destino y cambiar la historia del mundo. Si no, los supervivientes de Carrhae siempre podrán labrarse un nombre al servicio de los ejércitos del lejano Imperio Han, más allá del otro extremo del Orbe conocido por los romanos.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

The army box of Vercingetorix offers the possibility of placing at the head of the best armies of Gaul – and of the Celtic world as a whole – those nobles and magistrates who fought most fiercely against the invasion of Iulius Caesar. Thus, Divico, leader of the Helvetii and first to confront the legions, Boduognatus, commander of the combined forces of the Belgians, who almost crushed the Romans in the battle of the Sabis River (57 BC) and, of course, the young, astute and brilliant arvernian noble Vercingetorix, able to unite and raise the whole of Gaul against Caesar in 52 BC, to the dismay of the Romans. With them come the cream of Gaul’s forces, from noble horsemen and Gallic aristocrats armed to the teeth, highly motivated druids or hardened Helvetian infantrymen, as well as bands of Gaesatai mercenaries and Gallic infantry… ready to send the Romans back to their pestilent and overcrowded city with a good kick in the ass!

—description from the publisher

La caja de ejército de Vercingétorix ofrece la posibilidad de poner al frente de las mejores huestes de la Galia –y del mundo celta en su conjunto– a aquellos nobles y magistrados que lucharon con mayor denuedo contra la invasión de Julio César. Así, Divicón, líder de los helvecios y primero en confrontar a las legiones, Boduognato, comandante de las fuerzas combinadas de los belgas, que a punto estuvo de aplastar a los romanos en la batalla del río Sabis (57 a.C.) y, por supuesto, el joven, astuto y genial noble arverno Vercingétorix, capaz de unir y alzar a toda la Galia contra César en 52 a.C., para espanto de los romanos. Con ellos acude lo más granado de las fuerzas de la Galia, desde jinetes nobles y aristócratas galos armados hasta los dientes, druidas altamente motivados o endurecidos infantes helvecios, así como bandas de mercenarios gaesatai e infantería gala… ¡dispuestos a devolver a los romanos a su pestilente y atestada ciudad de una buena patada en el trasero!

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

The Allies box, finally, is filled with hordes of new mercenary units, allies and vassals of the powers of the era, coming from all corners of the Roman world, ready to serve the highest bidder, their partners -according to the winds of war and history- or their patrons, whoever they may be. Unyielding Hispani Soldurii, agile Iberian Caetatri, the Massylii guardsmen of the Numidian kings, Gaetuli horsemen from the North African deserts, Cantabrian warriors from the mountains of northern Hispania and even Cilician pirates are at the disposal of all the contenders of Triumviratus… and of all of ONUS! Many of these units are not exclusive to the 1st century BC and the time of Caesar, but can also be used to reinforce your Hispanic armies on the battlefields of the Punic Wars, to shore up the multi-ethnic hosts of Alexander the Great’s successors in the East, and even to provide Trajan and his general, Lusius Quietus, with the kind of North African auxiliaries and symmachiarii with which to spread terror in Dacia and Parthia. Cosmic Odyssey is an innovative tabletop game that blends the thrill of interstellar exploration with educational gameplay. Designed for 2-4 players, the game takes you on an epic journey across the galaxy, where you’ll colonize planets, face cosmic challenges, and master critical thinking through strategic play.

With an average playtime of 45-90 minutes, Cosmic Odyssey offers an immersive experience for players aged 7 and up, ensuring fun and learning for families, friends, and enthusiasts alike.

Our creations
Our team is creating a saga for this beautiful board game, we have already finished it and it will be available for all of you, the second one is in the modeling phase, since the characters and other things will not be the same, we are creating a story and new data so that based on the first one "Cosmic Odyssey Vol. 1" will be in charge of giving you the bases and Vol. 3 "One Last Chance" of Cosmic Odessey will be our last and the best delivery that we will have in Nova Learning Co.

Components
Every piece of Cosmic Odyssey is crafted as a downloadable 3D model, allowing players to personalize their game pieces through 3D printing. To protect our designs, access is granted to backers who contact us directly.

Your support will fund a 3D printer to produce high-quality, tactile components for Cosmic Odyssey and future expansions. By investing, you're fueling the evolution of accessible, customizable games that bring people together.

Inside the Box
Custom Galaxy Board: Explore unique planets and star systems.
Exploration Cards & Tokens: Manage resources and discover the unknown.
Spaceship Models: Ready for printing, with stunning detail.
Rulebook: Clear, engaging instructions for all skill levels.

How to Play Cosmic Odyssey
Cosmic Odyssey is a game of strategy, choices, and discovery. As a space explorer, your mission is to navigate the cosmos, gather resources, and expand your galactic influence—all while making meaningful decisions at every turn.

Gameplay Overview
Starting the Journey:
The game begins at a central hub, the Cosmic Nexus. From here, you venture into uncharted space by placing hexagonal tiles, each representing new areas like planets, black holes, or asteroid fields.

Decision-Driven Exploration:
Each turn offers 2-3 choices, allowing players to shape their journey. Choose a hex tile from your deck and decide its placement to chart your path forward. Every decision has consequences, leading to diverse strategies and endless replayability.

Role of Cards:
Exploration Cards: Determine the resources, challenges, or surprises found on newly explored tiles.
Event Cards: Introduce dynamic changes, such as solar flares or alien encounters, affecting all players.
Skill Cards: Enhance your character's unique abilities and improve your strategy.

Meet the Crew
Every player assumes the role of a unique spacefarer, each with specialized skills:

The Engineer: Expert in crafting advanced technology, granting mechanical advantages.
The Scientist: Excels in research and unlocking new abilities.
The Explorer: Gains bonuses for rapid movement and discovery.
The Trader: Masters in bartering and managing resources efficiently.
These characters ensure varied playstyles, encouraging teamwork or competition depending on your strategy.

Learning Through Play
Cosmic Odyssey seamlessly integrates educational elements:

Planetary Knowledge: Discover real-world facts about planets, stars, and cosmic phenomena through gameplay.
Critical Thinking: Decisions shape your path—should you risk entering a black hole to secure rare resources?
Strategic Skills: Manage resources and adapt to ever-changing circumstances.
Victory Conditions
The game ends when all hexagonal tiles are placed, or a player achieves their unique mission objectives. Points are earned for resources collected, objectives completed, and tiles explored. The player with the highest score claims victory and the title of Supreme Galactic Explorer!

Prepare for an adventure where every decision matters and no two journeys are the same.

—description from the designer

A Forlorn Hope is an abstract simulation wargame of a typical trench assault, modeling those attacks that were conducted during the First World War (1914-1918). The player represents an attacking regiment of troops consisting of three battalions, with each battalion made up of two or three assault companies (depending on the number of players).

The game uses a "press-your-luck" design philosophy that will challenge you with tough decision-making and risk-taking throughout the game. The goal is for the player(s) to drive their forces across No Man's Land in the quickest and most efficient manner possible to achieve the best level of victory.

A Forlorn Hope is designed both for solitaire and multiplayer co-operative play. Numerous scenarios are included, starting with a basic assault scenario (which is ideal for learning the intricacies of the game system), then adding multiple historically-based scenarios simulating actual battles from World War I that offer a slightly more complex and layered gaming experience. Each scenario features singular aspects of the historical battle it is simulating, and each will therefore be a unique gaming experience.

—description from the publisher

Find the birds with the house before time runs out

boardgame: A Dam Too Far: 617 Squadon's Dam Buster Raid, May 1943
description:
Operation Chastise, commonly known as the Dambusters Raid, was an attack on German dams in the Ruhr industrial area. Carried out on the night of 16/17 May 1943 by 617 Squadron RAF Bomber Command, later called the Dam Busters, using special "Bouncing Bombs" developed by Barnes Wallis, who also oversaw the modifications of 19 Lancaster bombers specially adapted to carry the “Upkeep” bouncing bomb ordinance.

The player allocates 19 randomly chosen crew members to their Lancaster bombers. These crew members are the best of the best, and consist of the Front Gunner, Pilot, Navigator, Engineer, Bomb Aimer, Wireless Operator and Rear Gunner. These crew members can help at certain stages of the mission. The outward-bound journey will be a matter of pushing your luck with the aircraft for the best results. Will you risk trying to get a better attack value or lose an aircraft from flak or flying so low you hit power cables? On the bomb run it will be the challenge of balancing height, speed and position as you approach with the available “Steady” counters to achieve the perfect bomb release point. It’s all about accuracy; if your bombers come in too fast or too low, they will miss the target. Taking a bomb run will be a higher or lower risk choice. How many bombs will the dams take before they are breached, how many dams can be destroyed or damaged, and how many aircraft will make it back on the homeward bound journey? All these factors will determine if the mission was a success or a failure. Can you do better than the historical counterparts?

—description from the designer

A positive affirmation adventure card game designed to boost confidence in kids 6+ through actionable affirmations. It’s easy to learn, perfect for 2-10 players, and takes 15-60 minutes to play.

Board game featuring the Julia Donaldson & Axel Scheffier character, The Highway Rat (and friends) from the World of The Gruffalo. Art of box, board and character playing pieces is true to Axel Scheffier's distinctive work for the books. A compact and attractive package (box is 27cm x 21cm x 5cm), the board folds into four and features a built-in spinner. The look and feel of box, pieces and board are all excellent and add a great deal to the atmosphere of the game particularly if you are a fan of the books and characters. Children who follow the work of Donaldson & Sheffier will love it.

The aim of the game is to be the first player to find four different cakes and puddings and race to the finish without the Highway Rat catching you.

Preparation phase is shuffling the 16 pudding cards and distributing 4 cards to each of the 4 card spaces on the board. Each of up to 4 players choses a character playing piece and places them at the start. The Highway Rat piece is placed on his own special start space across the board.

Youngest player goes first, spinning the board's built-in spinner. The spinner has two concentric number rings: an outer numbering 1,2,3,4,5,6 and an inner numbering 1,2,3. Each player moves their own playing piece according to the outer spinner ring number and moves the Highway Rat piece the number of spaces indicated on the inner ring. The player can move their piece or the Highway Rat first but must move both in a clockwise direction. Players must move to the next empty space if their spin lands them on an already occupied space.

There are a number of special spaces operating in a snakes and ladders mode: spin again, miss a turn, lose a card and Lucky Clover spaces enabling a shortcut path to be taken; the Lucky Clover shortcuts are also sanctuaries as the Highway Rat may not follow their lighter coloured spaces. If a player lands on a Card space they take the top card and if it is a cake/pudding they require for their set of 4 (3 different delicious looking cakes and some fun Gruffalo tarts) they keep it else return it to the bottom of the pile.

If any player lands on The Highway Rat start space they spin again just for the Rat giving him an extra turn.

The mechanics of the Highway Rat's pursuit are quite clever. He can only move a maximum of three spaces to the player's maximum of 6 but he gets to move at each player's turn thus he does run them down.

If the Highway Rat lands on the exact space of a player's piece then that player returns to Start and they lose all the cakes/puddings they have so far collected.

The Winner is the first player to collect all 4 cakes/puddings and arrive at the party at the End and shout "Stand and Deliver !". Only a player with all four cakes/puddings can enter the special area of the End/Party and the Highway Rat, the notorious cake/pudding thief, cannot enter the Party at all, he's not invited.

This charming game is mostly spin and move with some decision as to whether to move one's playing piece or the Highway Rat first. Rules are simple, clear and watertight. Fun and tension reign in the group as the Highway Rat tries to steal their cakes/pudding and send each of them back to the start.

Contents:

Game board with built-in spinner

4x playing pieces with stands

Highway Rat piece with stand

16x cake/pudding cards

Rules sheet

What’s your favorite movie? How about your best friend’s favorite? Your brother? Your sister? Just how well do you know what THEY know?  
 Welcome to ‘The Cast List’: The movie deduction game where your memory of which actor was cast in which movie becomes more important than ever. 
 Designed by a former world champion of movie trivia, ‘The Cast List’ puts the tools in your hand to try and stump your opponents with well placed strategy and movie knowledge. 
 When it’s your turn, your opponent will pick a card at random with 5 movies on it. There is 1 common actor between those 5 films. They’ll then pick 2 movies listed on the card and read their titles to you. It’s then up to you and your team to determine which common actor appeared in both films. If you don’t guess correctly in the 30 seconds given, don’t worry. There are 3 more movies on the card to help you solve the ‘cast list’ puzzle. However, be careful how many clues you ask for. Each additional movie you hear before answering correctly reduces your score by 1 point. 
 The player or team asking the questions gets to pick the order of the movies they want to ask, testing their knowledge of what the other player or team knows.

—description from the publisher

This game, designed for the KCTTS's 2024 Christmas party, is a mashup of a draw-and-discard game and a one-hand trick-taking game. Players compete to win tricks, but they also have to manage their hand, which controls what they score at the end of the game.

The deck is Christmas themed and consists of eight suits (Christmas Trees, Snowmen, Presents, Ornaments, Santa's Hat, Reindeer, Bells, and Stars).

Players start with 5 cards, and before they play into a trick, they will draw 2 and discard 1.  The winner of the trick is the person playing the highest of suit led, though the low card in each suit beats the high card.

Players will collect cards from tricks over the course of the game. At the end, they will still have five cards in their hand. They win points for cards won that match the suits of those hand cards, but they lose points for won cards for which they didn't keep a card in hand.

—description from the designer

Harmijan (from Proto-Germanic: harmijaną, meaning "to imitate" or "to repeat") is a strategy game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexagons (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 6 cells per side, but boards of 5 or 7 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A creature is a maximal set of connected stones of the same color. A single stone is also a creature.  
     The size of a creature is the number of stones it contains.


				
				
					Turns:The black player goes first and then they alternate. On your turn, perform the following actions in order:


     Move a stone of your color through empty cells connected in a straight line, provided that on each of the crossed cells it touches exactly one friendly creature and any number of enemy creatures. Single-stone creatures cannot be moved.
     Place a stone of your color on an empty cell in a straight line towards the moved stone, without jumping over other stones and without merging two or more friendly creatures. The placed stone cannot be adjacent to the group of the moved stone.
     Pass your turn if you cannot perform actions 1 and 2 in that order; otherwise, passing is not allowed.


The movement can end on a cell occupied by an enemy stone, removing it from the board; this is considered that the creature has bitten the enemy creature.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when there are no available moves for both players; in this case, each player receives a point for the sum of all stones of all larger creatures with the same shape (regardless of symmetry), plus the size value of those creatures. In case of a tie, continue with the next largest creatures with the same shape, and so on.

To make the game fair, before the game begins, the first player places one black creature of two stones and one white creature of two stones, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the two adjacent stones pie rule.

—description from the designer

boardgame: My First Theme Park Board Game
description:
A thrilling new theme park is needed, and it’s up to you to design it! Compete with friends to create the most exciting park filled with rides, attractions, and decorations. The player who designs the most impressive park will win the prestigious Best Theme Park award!

In My First Theme Park, players draw attraction cards each round and place matching ride or attraction tile onto their park boards. But watch out! Strategic planning is key—covering up decorations might cost you points, and running out of space could leave you with missing details.

With its bright and colorful design, this game offers a fun and creative puzzle-building experience. Kids will develop shape recognition, visual matching, and strategic thinking skills while they play. Minimal reading makes My First Theme Park perfect for kids ages 5 and up.

—description from the publisher

IntroductionVadrantir (from Irish: uachdranachd nan tìr, meaning "dominion of territories") is a territorial unification game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 6 cells per side, but boards of 5 or 7 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stackable pieces in their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is the maximal set of connected stones of the same color. A single stone is also a group.
     The size of a group is the number of stones it contains.
     A territory is a maximal set of connected empty cells. You own a territory if the largest group adjacent to it is your color, and in case of equality the next largest groups are compared; if they are all the same size, the territory does not score for any player.
     The value of a territory is the number of empty cells it contains.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color non adjacent to friendly groups unless the group formed by the placed stone is at most one stone larger than the largest adjacent enemy group.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when exactly one player cannot place stones, and the player with the territories of the highest value wins. To prevent ties, half a point is awarded to the player with the largest group. In case of a tie in size, the next largest groups are considered.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a black stone on an empty cell and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer

boardgame: La Battaglia di Eluet el Asel 1941
description:
On December 13, 1941 in Cyrene, Major Alessi Edoardo, commander of the 1st Paratrooper Battalion CC. RR., received orders from the Deputy Chief of Staff of the Higher Command of the Armed Forces of North Africa to report to the German command the following day. To receive instructions regarding the use of the battalion in the area of ​​Eluet el Asel, located south of Berta at the point where the two tracks come together, one from Chaulan and the other from El Mechili-Murtaba. And to deploy in defense on the positions astride the crossroads; ordering the Italian officer to defend to the bitter end. 
To understand the importance of the events - and therefore to explain how a battle such as Eluet el Asel, could in certain circumstances produce formidable historical consequences - it is necessary to frame and relive History. 
For this reason, the wargame of the battle offers the possibility of "telling" and "reviving" the events of those men, Royal Carabinieri paratroopers of the 1st Battalion at the crossroads of Eluet el Asel. With the intent of making the events of one of the many feats of arms that occurred in the Second World War known, in a new and different way. 
From here this new project that places the "player/simulator" on the battlefield to relive the fourteen hours of the clashes, perpetuating the heroic behavior of the Royal Carabinieri paratroopers, representing the essence of the inner soul of every modern Carabiniere paratrooper.
Foreword by: Colonel Salvatore Demontis - Commander of the 1st Carabinieri Paratroopers Regiment “Tuscania”

—description from the publisher (translated)

Il 13 dicembre 1941 a Cirene il Maggiore Alessi Edoardo, comandante del 1° Battaglione paracadutisti CC. RR. riceve dal Sottocapo di Stato Maggiore del Comando Superiore delle Forze Armate dell’Africa Settentrionale, l’ordine di presentarsi il giorno successivo al comando germanico, per ricevere disposizioni circa l’impiego del battaglione nella zona di Eluet el Asel sita a sud di Berta nel punto ove confluiscono le due piste provenienti l’una da Chaulan e l’altra da El Mechili-Murtaba, e disporsi a difesa sulle posizioni a cavallo del bivio; ordinando all’ufficiale italiano la difesa ad oltranza. Per capire l’importanza degli eventi - e dunque per spiegare come una battaglia quale fu Eluet el Asel, possa in determinate circostanze produrre conseguenze storiche formidabili - bisogna inquadrare e rivivere la Storia. Per questo il board-wargame della battaglia offre la possibilità di “raccontare” e di far “rivivere” le vicende di quegli uomini, paracadutisti Carabinieri Reali del 1° Battaglione al bivio di Eluet el Asel, con l’intento di divulgare e far conoscere gli avvenimenti, per lo più sconosciuti ai tanti, di uno dei molti  fatti d’arme che ci furono nella Seconda guerra mondiale, in maniera nuova e diversa. Da qui questo nuovo progetto che, pone il “giocatore/simulatore” sul campo di battaglia per rivivere le quattordici ore degli scontri, perpetuando l’eroico comportamento dei paracadutisti Carabinieri Reali, rappresentando l’essenza dell’anima interiore di ogni moderno Carabiniere paracadutista.

description:
Linimasa Card Game'' (LCG) is a card game with an Indonesia historical theme. There are three ways to play LCG: Tautan Tokoh (Historical Connections), Memori Waktu (Memory of Time), and Kalayuda (Chronological War).

The objective of LCG Tautan Tokoh is for players to correctly guess the sequence of historical events related to Indonesia’s independence and connect historical figures to each event.

boardgame: Ermine: Battles in the War of the Breton Succession, 1342-1364
description:
The death of Duke John III of Brittany in 1341, just 4 years after the beginning of the Hundred Years War would spark a dynastic conflict between Joan de Penthièvre, his niece, and John of Montfort, his nephew.
Both sides would be backed by the Kings of France and England, respectively. Charles de Blois, Joan’s husband, was the primary military leader of the French-backed side, and would be present in almost all of the battles during the conflict. Meanwhile, the Montfort faction would see numerous different ambitious and capable military leaders, who were regularly outnumbered by the French troops.
The war was characterized by a series of military engagements and shifting allegiances, as both factions sought to solidify their claims. The conflict not only featured traditional battles but also included skirmishes, sieges, and political manoeuvring, drawing in various local noble families, as well as notable mercenaries.
As the war unfolded, it became intertwined with the broader context of the Hundred Years' War, further complicating the regional dynamics. Ultimately, the war concluded with the Treaty of Guérande in 1365, which recognized John de Montfort’s son (also called John de Montfort, you’ll find that most of the major political players during this conflict were called either John or Joan) as the Duke of Brittany, establishing his lineage and ending the immediate succession crisis. This resolution would have lasting implications for Brittany's political landscape and its relationship with both France and England.
This entry is echoing Richard’s Berg design intent of the original Men of Iron, whose focus was to highlight the re-emergence of infantry on the battlefield at the beginning of the Hundred Years War. It will cover the 6 major battles during the War, as well as an extra one which saw many of the commanders and knights of the Breton conflict take part.

description:
Guarida Goblin will test your mental agility, dexterity and ability to identify patterns in a fast and fun way. Ideal to enjoy with family or friends! Can you interpret the clues faster than anyone else and discover what object the Goblin is chasing?

Each card will give you five hints of the three object you can grab: a magical yellow lamp wityh a genie, a red scroll of spells with a feather, and a blue sorcerer's hat with a magical wand.

Each card will give a number of things related to the objects, and you must catch the object who has a number or related clues to the card as fast as you can to get a point.

The first player to get to an previously specified number or points, wins.

Collect event cards as you travel through the different Cycle worlds. Combine them to open up time lines with mission cards. Become a Futurocyclist by earning Patch Points.

Event cards are collected to create timelines and match the mission cards described on the mission cards. Each event card describes an event in the history of cycling, from the Draisienne to... the future bicycles of 2080.

Zilight Wild West is a solo play wild west zombie card game.
Apart from the rules game book, that explains all the rules you need to play this game that sees you battling zombies in the Wild West in 1880, you need a regular deck of cards, or a custom deck is available for for a smoother gaming experience. 
Add some regular d6 dice and you will be fighting for your life in Dead Man's Canyon in no time!

You draw cards that have different functions acc. to the suit:
Diamonds: Locations
Clubs: Equipment
Hearts: People
Spades: Opponents

You search the locations for equipment, meet good or bad people and battle the Zombies! Combat is dice based as are resolutions of stamina or persuasion against people and events. Everything uses either d6 dice or d3 dice (roll your d6 and divide by 2, rounding up).

DIF-Spelet is an engaging trivia board game that invites enthusiasts to delve into the rich history and culture of Djurgårdens IF, one of Sweden's most storied sports clubs. Founded in 1891 on the island of Djurgården in Stockholm, Djurgårdens IF has a legacy spanning over a century, with significant achievements in football, ice hockey, and numerous other sports.

The game features 1,200 meticulously crafted questions, offering a comprehensive challenge to both die-hard fans and newcomers eager to learn more about the club. These questions are categorized into six distinct areas:

Football: Explore the triumphs and trials of Djurgårdens IF's football team, including memorable matches, legendary players, and historic milestones.

Ice Hockey: Delve into the icy arenas where Djurgårdens IF's hockey team has battled, highlighting key victories, standout athletes, and pivotal moments.

Other DIF Sports: Discover the breadth of sports under the Djurgårdens IF banner, from athletics to bandy, and the club's contributions to Swedish sports history.

General DIF Knowledge: Test your overall awareness of the club's heritage, emblem, colors, and its evolution over the years.

Rivals: Examine the intense rivalries that have fueled the competitive spirit of Djurgårdens IF, particularly against local adversaries like AIK and Hammarby.

Stockholm Knowledge: Assess your understanding of the city's geography, culture, and landmarks, emphasizing areas significant to Djurgårdens IF's identity.

Developed in collaboration with DIF Historia, a dedicated group committed to preserving and promoting the club's history, the game ensures accuracy and depth in its content.

DIF-Spelet is designed for 2 to 6 players, making it an ideal choice for family gatherings, friendly competitions, or social events. Players take turns answering questions, earning points for correct answers, with the ultimate goal of demonstrating their superior knowledge of Djurgårdens IF and its storied past.

Whether you're a lifelong supporter or new to the world of Djurgårdens IF, DIF-Spelet offers an entertaining and educational journey through the annals of one of Sweden's most successful sports clubs. It's more than just a game; it's a celebration of tradition, rivalry, and the enduring spirit of Stockholm's sporting community.

Feel like a Fool, is a new and funny party game: two teams will compete to be faster to fill their boards with all the vowel tokens needed.
