Preparation:
Place the two Ying-Yang center cards in the center of the table and in a horizontal position, forming the Ying-Yang symbol (there are two possible configurations).
Shuffle the 10 "Ages" cards and place them in a deck face down.
Place the 10 Karma cubes within reach of the player.

Objective:
Place the 10 "Ages" cards around the central Ying-Yang cards in a 3x4 rectangle, recovering or managing Karma to prevent it from running out before completing the arrangement.

Game play:

     Turn setup


Draw the top three cards from the "Ages" deck and place them face up.
Roll the two dice (one blue and one withe).
Select and place a card:

Choose a card from the three revealed.
Choose one of the two dice (blue or white) to determine the number you will use.
Restrictions:
The number on the die must be the same color as the number on the chosen card.
The value of the die cannot exactly match the number on the card (there must be at least 1 point difference).

Karma cost:
Subtract in Karma cubes the difference between the value of the chosen die and the corresponding number on the card.

Placing the card:
Place the chosen card adjacent and horizontally to one of the two central Ying-Yang cards or to another card already placed.

Recovering Karma:
If the half circle of the placed card matches the color of the central Ying-Yang card, recover 1 Karma cube.
If a circle is also completed (two half circles of the same color of adjacent cards), recover as many Karma cubes as complete circles are formed.
You cannot recover more Karma than you have lost in previous turns.

Repeating the process:
Follow the same procedure with the two remaining revealed cards.
Once the three cards are placed, draw three new cards from the deck and repeat the process.
When there are only three cards left in the deck, reveal and place the first two, leaving the last card as the final card of the game.

Victory and Defeat Conditions:
Victory: Complete the 3x4 layout around the Ying-Yang center cards, placing all the "Ages" cards and keeping at least 1 Karma cube.
Defeat: If you run out of Karma cubes before completing all the cards, you lose the game.

Strategic Notes:

     Carefully manage your Karma and prioritize placing cards that match the half circles on the center or adjacent cards in color.
     Avoid choosing dice whose Karma cost is too high, especially in the early stages of the game.
     Consider the future layout when placing cards, as completing colored circles can be essential to regaining Karma.


Good luck on your path to enlightenment, Monk Pangu!

—description from the designer

Preparación:
 Coloca las dos cartas centrales Ying-Yang en el centro de la mesa y en posición horizontal, formando el símbolo Ying-Yang (existen dos posibles configuraciones).
 Baraja las 10 cartas de "Edades" y colócalas en un mazo boca abajo.
 Coloca los 10 cubitos de Karma al alcance del jugador.

Objetivo:
 Colocar las 10 cartas de "Edades" alrededor de las cartas centrales Ying-Yang en un rectángulo de 3x4, recuperando o administrando Karma para evitar que se agote antes de completar la disposición.

Desarrollo del juego:

     Preparación del turno


Roba las tres primeras cartas del mazo de "Edades" y colócalas boca arriba.
 Lanza los dos dados (uno rojo y uno negro).
 Seleccionar y colocar una carta:

Elige una carta de las tres reveladas.
 Escoge uno de los dos dados (azul o blanco) para determinar el número que usarás.
Restricciones:
 El número del dado debe ser del mismo color que el número en la carta elegida.
 El valor del dado no puede coincidir exactamente con el número en la carta (debe haber al menos 1 punto de diferencia).

Costo en Karma:
 Resta en cubitos de Karma la diferencia entre el valor del dado elegido y el número correspondiente en la carta.

Colocación de la carta:
 Coloca la carta elegida adyacente y en horizontal a una de las dos cartas centrales Ying-Yang o a otra carta ya colocada.

Recuperación de Karma:
 Si el medio círculo de la carta colocada coincide en color con el de la carta central Ying-Yang, recupera 1 cubito de Karma.
 Si además se completa un círculo (dos medios círculos del mismo color de cartas adyacentes), recupera tantos cubitos de Karma como círculos completos se formen.
 No puedes recuperar más Karma del que has perdido en turnos anteriores.

Repetir el proceso:
Sigue el mismo procedimiento con las dos cartas restantes reveladas.
Una vez colocadas las tres cartas, roba tres nuevas cartas del mazo y repite el proceso.
Cuando queden solo tres cartas en el mazo, revela y coloca las dos primeras, dejando la última carta como la carta final del juego.

Condiciones de victoria y derrota:
Victoria: Completa la disposición de 3x4 alrededor de las cartas centrales Ying-Yang, colocando todas las cartas de "Edades" y conservando al menos 1 cubito de Karma.
Derrota: Si te quedas sin cubitos de Karma antes de completar todas las cartas, pierdes la partida.

Notas estratégicas:

     Administra cuidadosamente tu Karma y prioriza colocar cartas que coincidan en colores con los medios círculos de las cartas centrales o adyacentes.
     Evita elegir dados cuyo costo en Karma sea demasiado alto, especialmente en las etapas iniciales del juego.
     Considera la disposición futura al colocar cartas, ya que completar círculos de colores puede ser esencial para recuperar Karma.


¡Buena suerte en tu camino hacia la iluminación, monje Pangu!

—description from the designer (Spanish)

boardgame: Through Hell and Back: Plane Expansion Pack 2
description:
This second Aircraft Pack for Through Hell and Back includes:


     14 Missions attacking a major airfield in Cognac, France (double sided bombsight & dossier cards)
     11 Escort cards for the 78th FG 82 sqdn. With new mechanics to make them more powerful.
     14 Enemy Fighter cards for BF-109 from JagdGeschwader 2. With new stacking and focus mechanics.


Escorts now have Attack Bonuses (if the Escort has a vector that matches an enemy fighter). Enemy fighters might have a special option for damage done by the bomber (by rolling natural doubles the player can stack the dice to connect to a critical slot and destroying the enemy more easily). "Escort Predators" can ignore the bomber and instead focus on Escorts.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Hostile Space: Anime Inspired Space Combat
description:
Hostile Space is a space mecha combat game where the focus is on maneuver within three-dimensions.

It is the dawn of The New Age for humanity as the Solar System has been colonized with the help of the Fold Drive. Four Great Powers hold humanity together by cooperating through an extra-governmental organization called the Concert of the Solar System. Between the Great Powers an assortment of Free Holds, or small colonies; struggle for survival in the shadow of the Great Powers. The Great Powers compete for their spheres of influence with these Free Holders to gain commercial and political benefit within the Concert. It is a time of cut-throat colonial rivalry and Great Power tensions. It is a time of conflict.

In games of Hostile Space, players will manage and maneuver 2-12 units per side that represent Combat Suits, interceptors, support ships and more. This is a small-scale, model-vs-model level of engagement where the fighting is up-close and personal. The focus is on engaging and evading by pivoting, turning, moving, and changing heights while managing your fire arcs and ammo. Mechanics are resolved using six-sided dice, maneuver cards, and measurement for tactical game play. Life is cheap compared to hardware.

The primary design goals for the game are:


     Scale and model agnostic – Use the models you want to use
     Maneuver is critical
     Player skill piloting is key
     Death can come from any direction, up, down, left, right, behind, or straight ahead!
     A campaign system where you can create and build your own Ace pilots
     Military focused mecha; not super-robot, Kaiju, or Over-the-top mecha


Welcome to Hostile Space.

boardgame: A Herança de Cthulhu: Board Game
description:
A Herança de Cthulhu - Board Game is a survival and exploration board game for 1 to 4 players aged 18 and up. In this game, a group of Survivors must escape Madison City after their refuge is destroyed, facing hordes of Cultists, madness-stricken individuals, and terrifying monstrosities serving the Great Old Ones.

Players navigate through missions, explore the city, gather resources, and manage threats like hunger, madness, and the ever-present danger of noise, which can attract monsters or trigger catastrophic events. Cards are central to the gameplay, representing events, items, and monsters, while dice rolls are used to resolve skill tests, attacks, and other actions. Survivors take turns performing actions such as moving, attacking, or searching for supplies, while monsters react to their movements and bar the way forward.

As if these challenges weren't enough, something truly sinister is brewing in Madison City—an ominous force that could not only block the Survivors' escape but also spell disaster for the entire world. Success requires strategy, cooperation, and the will to endure as players battle through missions or embark on a full campaign to uncover the city's darkest secrets.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Indomitable Survivor: Thumping Timpani Ledla
description:
Thumping Timpani Ledla is packaged in a graphic box that includes:

1 x Thumping Timpani Ledla plastic miniature
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 30mm base and insert
1 x Living rules glossary card
1 x Pattern card Thumping Timpani
1 x Pattern gear card Thumping Timpani
1 x Pompous Face Indomitable resource card
1 x Large matte art print

Spewing and hacking, Ledla stumbled out of the kaleidoscopic cloud crying many-hued tears. From within the obscuring smog, wretched cries of guilty conscience expired invisibly. She collapsed, clutching the flaccid face,  the springy resonance of the waxy monster hide hitting the ground played a note of absolution to her fading consciousness.

The indomitable monsters that stalk the darkness unchallenged tend to grow features not normally found in younger specimens of the species. Survivors brave and lucky enough to survive encounters with these monsters gain indomitable resources. These unusual components inspire new configurations of gear worthy of these hard won treasures.

boardgame: I AM DONE: Certificate Promo Card
description:
This promo card was part of the Spielbox 7#24.

If you win the game with the Urkunde (certificate) card in your hand, you may write your name on it. In following games, when this card is played by anybody, every player has to praise the player with his name on the certificate and give him one of their hand cards.

Dice Apocalypse is a 2-4+ players one-page party game.

In order to play, you will simply need a few sets of polyhedral dice (better to have one for each player), a timer, various snacks, a plate each and from one to three friends to play with.

Before you play, gather at least six different types of snacks. Chips, popcorn, gummy bears, nachos,chocolates, cookies... whatever you can find! It’s the end of the world, you don’t exactly have the luxury to pick what you want to eat! If the only thing you have handy is broccoli, you’ll need to make it do.

Place the six snacks on the table in what we’ll refer to as central storage from now on. At the beginning of the game, those resources are no one’s. Who could have predicted that you’d find yourself needing to stock up on food all of a sudden? For this reason, when playing you will obviously need to wait until your opponents have collected some resources before you even think about stealing from them.

Both when taking resources from the central storage and when stealing them from your opponents, you’ll need to act with a “while supplies last” method. If the units run out before the number on the dice, you’ll have to be happy with whatever you managed to take.

Each community of survivors has an arsenal of seven weapons, each represented by a polyhedral die and equipped with an effect that will allow the user to gather resources from the central storage or to steal them from their opponents.

You can only use one weapon per turn and the used weapons will be discarded by the player, who will only be able to use them again when the whole set has been used. Since the turn begins by eating 1 unit from your resources, a player that finds themself starting their turn with an empty plate has lost.

In this game players will be playing tricks in a classical way and using the result of the trick to either move and get resources or upgrade their die/runner so it can get more resources.
The prototype of this game was made using the Game Creator Set by Creative Toys and Micael Sousa.

Marathon Mayhem is a running themed board game where players race 42km to the finish line. You must manage your energy levels while overcoming obstacles in order to reach the finish line.

Each player starts with 8 energy tokens. On your turn you have the option to either run of rest. To run, exchange energy tokens for distance. To rest, stay in the same position on the board and gain 2 energy tokens.

Players also pick up and play 1 action card on each turn. Action cards can be used to either benefit yourself or disadvantage your fellow runners.

—description from the publisher

Wardens is an all-new miniature fighting game where teams of 5 characters face off against each other in a dynamic environment with powerful unique abilities.  The figures are 3" tall, making for a large and exciting experience on the table.
Bullet: Fan features four new heroines with all-new abilities for Bullet!  These four heroines were also designed by the fans of the game.

4 new rival heroines from Orange Juice shoot-em-up video games arrive on the scene! Fight with chains, powerups, cannons, and... playing cards?! Battle against each other or cooperate against a heroine's powerful boss form!

Combine this expansion with any Bullet base game for even more replayability!

—description from the publisher

La Clef : Tome 2 - Les Chutes d'Est-Rive is a book of riddles illustrated with invisible ink.

On each double-page spread, a riddle begs to be solved!  There are 75 riddles inside! There are no instructions, so you'll have to find out what the text and image are hiding. Some riddles will require you to wander through the book, or refer to others.
One game, one story

Return to the magical world of La Clef for the continuation of this epic saga, where 75 new riddles await you on every double-page spread. Observation, reflection and deduction will help you rise to the challenge. To accompany you, a UV lamp will help you reveal the elements hidden in the invisible ink!
A new promo heroine included in the Bullet: Cubed crowdfunding campaign.

IntroductionTirioga (from Welsh: tiriogaeth, meaning "territory") is a drawless game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended board size is 9 points per side, but boards with 7 or 11 points per side are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones in their color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a maximal connected set of stones of the same color. A single stone is also considered a group.
     The size of a group is the number of stones it contains.
     A stone sees a cell if it is connected to that cell by a straight line without enemy stones in between.
     The rooted stone of a group is the first stone of that group placed on the board during a turn.
     The branched stones of a group are all stones placed after the rooted stone within the same group during the same turn.
     A new group is a group placed during a turn and is composed of a rooted stone along with all branched stones that follow it; otherwise, it is an old group. A new group may contain no branched stones.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a rooted stone on an empty cell, followed by branched stones with which you will form a new placed group of size N, where the rooted stone sees N friendly groups. Branched stones may be placed on empty cells or cells occupied by enemy stones to capture them by replacement. If you cannot place stones, pass your turn; otherwise, passing is not allowed.

				
				
					End of the game:The player who occupies more than half of the board wins.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places one black stone and one white stone on empty cells, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the two-stone pie rule.

—description from the designer

IntroductionSinfrid (from Irish: sineirgíocht frith, meaning “anti-synergy”) is a drawless game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexagons (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 7 cells per side, but boards of size 5 and 9 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is the maximal set of stones of the same color connected to each other. A single stone is also a group.
     The size of a group is the number of stones it contains.
     A stone sees a cell when it is connected to it through a straight line, with no enemy stones in between.
     The rooted stone of a group is the first stone of that group placed on the board during a turn.
     The branched stones of a group are all stones placed after the rooted stone within the same group during the same turn.
     A new group is a group placed during a turn and is composed of a rooted stone along with all branched stones that follow it; otherwise, it is an old group. A new group may contain no branched stones.
     A grafted group is a new group resulting from a placement that connects two or more friendly groups.
     To flip is to replace friendly stones with enemy stones.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a rooted stone on an empty cell, followed by the branched stones with which it will form a new placed group of size N, where the rooted stone sees N friendly groups. At the end of your turn, flip a grafted friendly group.

If there are not enough empty cells to place a group, you may fill them by placing a smaller group of your color in that maximal set of empty cells to make it fit.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when players can no longer place stones, and the player with the fewest stones of their color on the board wins.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places one black stone and one white stone on any empty cells, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the two-stone pie rule.

—description from the designer

IntroductionMeuda (from Scottish Gaelic: meud, meaning "size") is a drawless game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexagons (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 9 points per side, but boards with 7 or 11 points per side are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a maximal set of stones of the same color connected to each other. A single stone is also a group.
     The size of a group is the number of stones it contains.
     A stone sees a cell when it is connected to it through a straight line, without enemy stones in between.
     The rooted stone of a group is the first stone of that group placed on the board during a turn.
     The branched stones of a group are all stones placed after the rooted stone within the same group during the same turn.
     A new group is a group placed during a turn and is composed of a rooted stone along with all branched stones that follow it; otherwise, it is an old group. A new group may contain no branched stones.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, you place a rooted stone on an empty cell, followed by the branched stones with which you will form a new placed group of size N, where the rooted stone sees N friendly groups. All placed stones are placed one by one and must be adjacent to at most one friendly group. If you cannot place stones, pass your turn; otherwise, passing is not allowed.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when neither player can take any action. At the end of the game, players score one point for each group of their color with a different size, plus an additional half-point for the player with the largest group. In case of a tie in size, the next largest groups are considered.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places one black and one white stone on any empty cells, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the two-stone pie rule.

—description from the designer

boardgame: You Can't Say "Umm": After Dark Pack
description:
Describe filthy things without…err…ah…hesitating

Spice up your next game of "Umm" with 100 Word Cards that are not safe for whippersnappers.

—description from the back of the box

This is a story set in the Arctic. Ice is melting. All players choose an animal piece -- polar bear, a polar cub, a seal or a seal pup. Try to move cleverly on the solid ground. The player who can move on the ice tiles until the end is the winner.
The game rules are very easy to understand and it is both strategic and accidental.

—description from the publisher

Pavlova is a may-follow, trick-taker played with dominoes. The relative strength of the suits may change from trick-to-trick but the players’ choices will dictate how the strength will change.

You can play any tile you wish, in whichever orientation is best. Each tile is worth the difference from its highest number to its lowest number (e.g., a 6-1 tile is worth 5 tile points).

There are 35 tile points to capture each round. However, only the player that takes 18 or more of these points will score victory points.

—description from the designer

This is an expansion set that expands the story and game from the Blood Recall card game line. Two new characters, [Cherry Blossom] and [Indigo of the Wings], who inherited the wishes of Scarlet and Blue, have been added as new Blood Recallers, and a new Variant Rule (Succession Battle) has been added, which can only be played with a combined set of the base game and the Last Poem expansion.

In Shiratama Jong, your goal is to collect your most kawaii Shiratama cards for making two groups of absolutely kawaii Shiratamas.

His kawaiiness is tremendous and countless you know, but in this game the amount of kawaii depends on how many Shiratamas of the same pose are in your hand.

Each turn, you draw a card into your hand with which you can combine with the other cards in your hand to create 1-2 groups. Your goal is to play 2 groups to win the deal.

In the advanced mode, you'll want to group the same rank or same pose to score points!
publishers: しらたまゲームズ (Shiratama Games)

description:
Futon, Mochi, and Tengu. How do you explain this words in English? A game that explanes Japanese subjects in English!

Use only English!
There is a NG word that is prohibited to use. (If the title is "Sakura", the NG word is "cherry blossom")
Ask answers to more questions!
If you tell us, it can be done in any English!

The subjector will explain the Japanese language in English only. However, we do not use the NG word on the bottom of the title. Others will be subjected in order of earlier people. 2 responses to each person. When the correct answer, the subjector and the right answer will receive 1 card. Each card gets 1 card, and the first person to take 5 points wins.

boardgame: Management Strategy Board Game Camp President
description:
Utilize limited resources as the owner of the campsite, and aim for the most popular campsite!

In the limited management resources, we build facilities, expand our campsites, make our own goods, do crowdfunding .... YouTuber comes to the store, and that popular campper is appointed ambassador. Do ...

While the commitment exploded, there is not enough manpower and money, it becomes a short -term fund, manages the luck ... the risk hedging is sweet, and the campsite that I made is also in the business crisis ...

Although the rules are simple, the feature of this game is that you can learn the sense of money and business skills just by playing deep and playing deep.

This expansion contains 6 special cards to play Valhalla in teams: 2 vs 2 or 3 vs 3. 
Team play has 2 modes: all members of the team win (co-op team victory) or only one member of the team wins (the one with the most glory points).

Linimasa Card Game: STEAM (Science-Technology-Engineering-Arts-Mathematics) is a card game that invites all players to play and learn about the history of STEAM—Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, and Mathematics.

There are three ways to play: Linimasa Tautan Tokoh (Character Connection Timeline), Linimasa Memori Waktu (Time Memory Timeline), and Linimasa Kalayuda (Kalayuda Timeline).

The objective of LCG Linimasa Tautan Tokoh is for players to correctly guess the sequence of historical events related to Indonesia’s independence and connect historical figures to each event.

In LCG Memori Waktu, players guess historical figures based on revealed event cards. The first player to place three of their Player Cards wins the game.

In LCG Kalayuda, players arrange a series of events in their play area. Each correct sequence earns points.

boardgame: Batman: Le Sauveur de Gotham City
description:
After months of preparation, the Joker has orchestrated the escape of the twelve most wanted supervillains from Blackgate Prison and Arkham Asylum, who are Batman’s most formidable foes: Bane, the Penguin, Scarecrow, Two-Face, Deathstroke, Mr. Freeze, the Riddler, Poison Ivy, Harley Quinn, Ra’s al Ghul, Man-Bat, and Killer Croc.

Together, they’ve devised ten schemes to destroy Gotham City. Will the Joker and his accomplices manage to wreak havoc on Gotham City and escape before Batman and his allies apprehend them?

Randomly select one of the Joker’s ten evil schemes and then create your own scenarios. Choose your side and embody a villain or a hero to either save or destroy the city!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Après des mois de préparation, le Joker a fait évader de la prison de Blackgate et de l’Asile d’Arkham douze Super-vilains les plus recherchés qui sont les pires ennemis de Batman : Bane, le Pingouin, l’Épouvantail, Double face, Deathstroke, Mr Freeze, l’homme mystère, Poison Ivy, Harley Quinn, Ra’s al Ghul, Man bat et Killer Kroc.

Ensemble, ils ont mis au point 10 projets pour détruire la Ville de Gotham City. Le Joker et ses acolytes parviendront-ils à détruire Gotham City et à s’enfuir avant que Batman et ses alliés ne les arrêtent ?

Tirez au sort un des 10 projets maléfiques du Joker et créez ensuite vos propres scénarios. Choisissez votre camp et incarnez un vilain ou un héros pour sauver ou détruire la ville !

—description from the publisher

IntroductionDalen (from Welsh: dalen, meaning "leaf") is an annihilation game for two players: Dark green and Light green. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 7 cells per side, but boards from 5 to 9 cells are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

This game is based on Duile.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A tree is a maximal set of stones of the same color connected to each other. A single stone is also a tree.
     A leaf is a stone adjacent to a single friendly stone.
     The size of a tree is the number of stones it contains.
     A stagnant tree is a tree that cannot increase its size.
     To flip is to replace a stone with a stone of the opposite color.


				
				
					Turns:Dark plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform the following actions in order:

1. Place a stone of your color on an empty cell adjacent to at most one leaf of a friendly group.
2. Flip all enemy trees adjacent to a stagnant tree of any color, if those enemy trees have an equal or smaller number of leaves than the stagnant tree.

				
				
					End of the game:The last player with stones remaining on the board wins.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a dark stone on an empty cell, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

				
				
					Variant:Captures are only made by the tree that cannot increase its number of leaves.

—description from the designer

Play as gods who wish to create an ideal world from elements such as water, minerals, animals, etc... 
This cooperative game is played without communication, requiring deduction to guess the partners' intentions and actions! 
Players take it in turns to place one of the elements on the different continents, while trying to respect its needs.
At the end of the game, we'll check whether each element has been ideally placed to score the most points and enter the pantheon of the gods!

—description from the publisher

A Dungeon Crawler game in which players search for items that will help them escape from the sanatorium. Players win if they manage to escape in time, even if someone is left behind.

Fuga do Sanatório Moreau é um jogo cooperativo de aventura em que cada jogador controla um animal vítima de experiências genéticas e que transforma suas peculiaridades psiquiátricas em habilidades especiais para escapar do Sanatório da terrível Doutora Moreau.

Nessa aventura colaborativa, cada ação e decisão conta para que todos possam fugir do Sanatório da Doutora Moreau. A ação fica por conta dos perigos e oportunidades que aparecem a cada nova sala revelada, cada uma com mecânicas e itens próprios.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
INTERNET FAMOUS ADULT PARTY GAME: Just as bonkers and raunchy as our YouTube series, this Fear Pong game is a game we probably shouldn’t have made, but you wanted it. Be prepared to do something you thought you never would! Ages 21+

HOW TO PLAY: Set up a game of drink pong. Place a dare card under each cup. When a ball lands in a cup, you can either do one of the dares and keep the cup, or refuse both dares and drink. (Alcohol consumption is not required to play this game.)

215 OUTRAGEOUS DARES ON COASTERS: Each of the 54 double-sided spill proof coasters includes 2 dares—some you’ve never seen before, some from your favorite Fear Pong episodes, and all unthinkable

6 BONUS CUSTOMIZABLE COASTERS AND 2 BALLS: Includes 6 double-sided blank cards for your own sick imagination to fill in, and 2 balls to hold, throw, and clutch for courage

BASED ON VIRAL VIDEO SERIES BY CUT: The Fear Pong game, based on our hit YouTube series, challenges you to push your limits and dares you to be your wildest self. If that’s too much, stick to regular drink pong—but where’s the fun in that?

—description from the publisher

An adventure board game about the "Silent Unseen" fighting the occupiers during World War II.

Surviving World War II was no easy feat—let alone as a soldier of the Polish underground army, constantly exposed to danger. Can you rise to the challenge?

To handle the difficult tasks ahead, you'll first need to undergo comprehensive training, as you never know which skill might prove useful to defend against occupational threats or strike against the German war machine.

During training, you'll build your deck of cards, which you will use during operations in the 16 districts of the Home Army. These districts are marked on a large, colorful game board featuring a map of occupied Poland. They are the locations where battles against the occupiers take place across various aspects of underground operations, from living in secrecy to executing military missions.

In addition to the game board and training cards, the box includes 4 other decks of cards—covering mission deployment orders, flight conditions to Poland, and tasks to accomplish—along with player boards, 12 wooden pawns, 32 wooden markers, and 80 cardboard tokens.

INTERACTIVE ADORABLE SKUNK: The game features a cut skunk with a tail that lifts to spray water, adding a surprising and playful element as players take turns.

DICE ROLLING GAMEPLAY: Players roll dice to determine their actions — rolling a 0 means no flowers need to be pressed, while rolling a 1 or 2 requires pressing that many flowers, introducing an element of chance and strategy.

SURPRISE WATER SPRAY: The skunk’s tail sprays water randomly when a specific flower is pressed, creating an unpredictable and exciting outcome that keeps players on their toes.

—description from the publisher

Keep the monkeys from dropping in this fun twist on two classic games: Dropple and Barrel of Monkeys! Randomly insert the sticks through the barrel, then add the figures so the monkeys’ arms hook onto the sticks. Take turns rolling the color dice and carefully pulling out the matching sticks without letting any of the monkeys fall – if any do, add them to your pile. If you get the Golden Monkey, remove five red monkeys from your pile and put them back into the barrel. Once all the sticks have been removed, the player with the fewest monkeys wins!

HOW TO PLAY:

     Start by inserting the sticks into the barrel - then, just add monkeys!
     Carefully pull out sticks without letting any of the monkeys inside fall


Also includes classic Barrel of Monkeys design!

—description from the publisher

This expansion contains 20 cards. Some of them are dealt to the players at the start of the game, some of them are shuffled to the main deck.
Týr's wrath cards are not played by themselves but with warrior cards. They reinforce warrior cards in different ways - add additional strength, reduce activation requirements, grant some extra bonuses and so on.
If the associated warrior card is discarded or goes to Valhalla, the Týr's wrath card is just discarded.

The basic rules are the same as the Camp President, this expansion just adds more options.

Lunar Campsite is not just about building a campsite on the moon!

While building a campsite, you can collect meteorites, develop energy sources, cultivate fields and start a self-sufficient life. You can also develop rockets, set up a travel agency and travel to other planets. This is a solo-only game where you can get into all sorts of different things.

Mega Empires: The Special Buildings is an expansion set that is compatible with Mega Empires: The West (2019), Mega Empires: The East (2021) and Mega Civilization (2015). It contains a set of 18 unique miniatures of ancient buildings historically associated with each of the empires in the game that add depth to gameplay, along with a set of rules.

This expansion reimplements Western Empires: The Special Buildings Expansion and Eastern Empires: The Special Buildings Expansion and combines them into a single set. The product contains revised rules (also available as download) and a board for storing the buildings during play.

—description from the designers

Just when you think you’ve got victory in hand, the screen shakes and you hear that daunting music: it’s the final boss fight!

Use Final Bosses 3-card expansion to add one of the unique final bosses to your level. If you can get past the boss, you’ll immediately win, but it won’t be easy. Each two-stage boss has its own requirements and combos that must be completed to defeat first its starting form and then its final form to truly beat the game.

Select one of the 3 boss cards and add it to your level, replacing the 6th card in the level row. The boss first appears on its starting form. Play through the level as normal, using button presses to defeat the boss’ starting form.

Once defeated, you may take advantage of the boss’ bonus ability, then flip the boss to its final form and place it at the end of the level row. Continue playing until reaching the boss again.

The final form has a specific requirement as well as two specific combos that must be completed to achieve victory. Defeat the final boss to win!

This expansion also adds 4 unique achievements to make bosses even more challenging. Earn every achievement to master the art of boss battles!

Need a power-up? Use these high-tech gadgets to boost your abilities as you make your way through the level.

Equipment Drop 4-card expansion adds new equipment to add variety to your starting equipment. With new combinations and wild buttons, you’ll have all-new ways to navigate the level and make it out of the space base.

Shuffle all available equipment cards together and randomly select two for your starting hand. Play as normal.

The new equipment cards introduce wild buttons. When these are activated, you may use your current level card’s ability and then use any button of your choice. Equipment with this effect can instead be used for a double-button, making them versatile gadgets to get through the most difficult spots in the level.

This expansion also adds 4 unique achievements that reward clever use of equipment. Earn every achievement to demonstrate your gadget mastery!

Honey Queen is a tile-placement game for 1 to 4 players, where you’ll compete, or collaborate, to collect honey of various colors. Each player has 6 bees to strategically place on a shared board that is built together by adding tiles to form paths of matching honey types (pure or mixed) and combining half-flowers to transform honey. Players score points on their personal scoring boards, and upon reaching milestones, they can optimize the points of previously placed bees. With both competitive and cooperative modes, this abstract game challenges you to create routes and maximize your score through tactical decisions.

—description from the publisher

Slime Quest is a game of agility and dexterity where you will need to be the best slime hunter.
In Slime Quest every player is a brave adventurer heading into a dungeon to capture slimes and exchange their keys for valuable rewards.

Eighty-four slime cards are shuffled face down in the center of the table and placed in a jumbled pile (the dungeon). Each player takes one card face down (places it in front of them on the table) and the game starts at the count of 3 (all players turn over their card at the same time).
The turn is simultaneous and continues until the end of the game. Each player will turn over cards from the center to try to place slimes sequentially, always on the right side of the last one placed in his playing area.
The slime cards that you do not use in your row of slimes will remain in the center face up.
At any time you can discard to the center of the table the last card of your row to change it for another one.
The game ends when a player announces that they have completed a row of slimes:
12 slimes with 2/3/4 players
9 slimes with 5/6 players
At that moment the player who finishes shouts DONE and no one else can place another slime in their personal row.
Then, the rows of all players are checked from left to right and cards will be eliminated if one does not match the laying conditions.
In the end, the winner is the one who has the most treasure keys within the slimes they keep in their row. In case of a tie, the winner is the one who has the most keys with the fewest cards.

Laying rules
Starting with the card that is turned over at the beginning, cards must be placed to the right, always of different color than the previous one, and taking into account the following rules.

There are six types of slime cards in the game:
Green: The next card must have the same amount of objects as the green slime.
Blue: The next card must have more objects than the blue slime.
Brown: The next card must have fewer objects than in the brown slime.
Red: The next card must have an odd number of objects.
Purple: The next card must have an even number of objects.
Gold: Gold slimes are a wild card. Regardless of their number of objects, they always fulfill any conditions for the placement of cards of the other colors.
However, to place a card to their right (of another color), their special condition must always be met.

Two players go head to head, one as a loyal agent, the other as a traitor, competing to save or sink their detective's agency.
In Mimic Mind we are transported to 1980, to a Chicago detective agency. Among us there is a loyal agent who seeks to save the agency, a traitor who seeks to undo the agency and a double agent who can help you or your adversary in their mission.
This game has 18 detective cards (6 blue, 6 red and 6 yellow).
Shuffle the deck of 18 cards and place it on one side of the table leaving a space in the center to build a grid of cards that can grow up to 3x3 during the game.
Each player chooses a color: Blue (loyal agent) or Red (traitor agent).
Yellow (double agent) will be a wild card color that both players can use to win.
The Blue player will always start and then their opponent. Each player in their turn will be able to perform one of the following four actions.

     Place an Agent: Take a card from the deck and look at it in secret, then place it in the playing area, face down (if there are cards, you place it adjacent to a card in the center, within the 3x3 boundaries).
     Replace an Agent: Take a card from the deck, look at it secretly and replace it with a card from the playing area, face down. The other card you removed is placed underneath the deck.
     Swap Agents: Swap two agents in the playing area by keeping them face down.
     Reveal Agents: You can reveal up to 3 agents as long as the one following the first one is of the same color.


A player wins immediately when they succeed in revealing a 3 in a row of their, or the wild card color.
In the case of revealing a 3 in a row of your opponent's color, your opponent wins.
A 3 in a row is a vertical, horizontal or diagonal row of cards of the same color. A combination of your color with a card of the wild card is not allowed.

You're searching for cats in CATOrCAT, but you never know exactly how many cats you'll find, which is a problem since you're trying to collect a precise number of cats each turn.

Both players have objective cards numbered 3-9, and to set up, shuffle the cat cards and lay out five piles of eight cards with the cat images on top.

On a turn, reveal an objective in your hand, then take a cat card from the top of a pile and reveal the number on its reverse side. That number will match either the number of whole cats on the front of the card or the number of whole and partial cats; each card appears four times, with its two values appearing twice. If the number doesn't exceed your objective, you can draw again. Ideally the sum of the revealed numbers will match your objective, allowing you to score it; otherwise, you discard it.

Once both players have tried to meet all of their objectives, whoever has scored the most wins.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Nieuport 23
description:
This is the Wings of War Series III World War I airplane pack of the Nieuport 23 plane.
The Nieuport 23 was basically a Nieuport 17 with a larger engine.

The premise of Vegan Not Vegan is simple. The deck contains of 56 cards. One side of the card shows a food product or a drink. The other side tells you if, yes or no, the product is vegan-friendly.

Choose how many cards you want to play with. Show a card, and the group of players have to decide whether or not they think the pictured product are vegan-friendly or not.

For example, do you think Oreos are plant-based? What about marshmallows?

This true or false vegan quiz isn’t as straightforward as it sounds. And you’ll be surprised at the products that have purely plant-based recipes and the ones that don’t.

The player with the most correct answers wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Introduction:Marbanta (from Irish: marbhántacht, meaning "stagnation") is a drawless game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended board size is 9 points per side, but boards with 7 or 11 points per side are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones in their color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a maximal connected set of stones of the same color. A single stone is also considered a group.
     The size of a group is the number of stones it contains.
     A stone sees a cell if it is connected to that cell by a straight line without enemy stones in between.
     The rooted stone of a group is the first stone of that group placed on the board during a turn.
     The branched stones of a group are all stones placed after the rooted stone within the same group during the same turn.
     A new group is a group placed during a turn and is composed of a rooted stone along with all branched stones that follow it; otherwise, it is an old group. A new group may contain no branched stones.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a rooted stone on an empty cell, followed by the branched stones with which it will form a new placed group of size N, where the rooted stone sees N friendly groups.

				
				
					End of the game:The player who cannot place any stones during his turn wins.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places one black stone and one white stone on empty cells, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the two-stone pie rule.

-description from the designer

Roll or Stand: Jurassic Adventures is a cooperative and solo roll-and-write, print-at-home game for 1–100 players. It combines Blackjack-inspired mechanics with thematic route-building puzzles, offering a fresh twist on the roll-and-write genre. Embark on a replayable narrative campaign featuring 3 comic book-styled scenarios, each with multiple possible outcomes. Explore 12 unique puzzles, each thematically tied to a cinematic scene.

Each turn, you roll 2 dice and add the results to your previous rolls, aiming to get as close as possible to the target values without busting (going over), similar to Blackjack. Once you choose to stand, you extend lines in the active puzzle to work toward fulfilling its objective.

Note: Each scenario is contained on a single sheet of paper, with no crafting required. All you need is one pen per player and two standard six-sided dice to play the game.

—description from the publisher

They live in the walls. As soon, inside you.

Enter into the forbidden crawlspaces of the Shepard, where a universal scourge has been allowed to fester.

This is a new Campaign Expansion, introducing a new narrative campaign, new Intruders, new Boss, new Spacers and Suits, new Gear, new Locations and much much more. All content from the Expansioncan also be used in modular custom campaigns.

Shadows have shadows, and desperation is big business.

This Vestige Scrap Pack features 12 new cards to shuffle into your collection. Expansion content, requires Vestige to play.

Introduction:Launiz (from Proto-Germanic: laubinassuz, meaning "leafiness") is a drawless annihilation game for two players: Dark green and Light green. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 6 cells per side, but boards of 5 or 7 cells are also valid. Each player has a sufficient supply of stones of their color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A tree is a maximal set of stones of the same color connected to each other. A single stone is also a tree.
     A leaf is a stone adjacent to at most one friendly stone.
     A sprout cell is an empty cell adjacent to a leaf.


				
				
					Turns:Dark plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform these actions in the following order:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty cell adjacent to at most three friendly stones not adjacent to each other, as long as you do not occupy a sprout cell of an enemy tree if it is the only one that would allow it to form an additional leaf that surpasses all your trees, unless you can also do so.
     Remove exactly one enemy tree adjacent to a friendly tree whose number of leaves increases due to your placement, as long as the enemy tree has fewer leaves than your tree.


When two or more trees of the same color are merged, capture can only be performed if the number of leaves in the new tree exceeds the number of leaves in the tree with the most leaves among those trees before the merge.

				
				
					End of the game:You win if the last enemy group is eliminated from the board.

To balance the game, before the game starts, the first player places a dark stone on an empty cell and the second player chooses a side. This method of balancing is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer

The Kickstarter edition contains the base game plus 3 promo packs that will not be included in the retail edition:

Hero Equipment Promo Pack - 6 equipment cards, 1 for each hero in the base game and Sindra & Finkel Expansion.

Events Promo Pack - 26 event cards to shuffle into the monster deck to introduce both positive and negative effects, adding replayability.

Threats & Resistance Promo Pack - 16 cards used to alter difficulty and replayability and also introduces a campaign mode spanning 5-7 games.

boardgame: Fate: Defenders of Grimheim – Sindra & Finkel Expansion
description: This expansion feature 2 new miniature heroes and their own unique 40 card decks with abilities, equipment, and events.

The battlefield has never looked like this!

A time of danger, a secret mission, mysterious tech, and now you and your platoon of rangers are in a strange world inhabited by orcs, sorcerers, and dragons!

Forgotten Ruin: The Adventure Wargame takes you to the strangest battlefield yet, pitting state-of-the-art firepower against fantastical monsters. Just hope the orc horde runs out before the ammunition does!

Based on the Forgotten Ruin book series, the rules are powered by the ‘Five X’ game system from Five Leagues from the Borderlands and Five Parsecs from Home, unleashing the same solo adventuring thrills. Command your squad while the game rules control your enemies as they move and fight. You can even play with a friend by splitting your squad into two fire teams or designating one of you to control the enemy like a Game Master.

This adventure wargame offers an exciting mix of tabletop tactics for miniature figures and randomly generated campaign play, providing a unique, story-rich experience.

—description from the publisher

A world peace conference was held with various Sentai heroes. A monster appeared there! I'm not ready to fight, so I'll push it onto someone else! But at worst, when I come here, my usual companions are not there, so the five heroes there gather together and defeat the monster with their special moves!

This game is trick-taking. Each card you acquire is worth one point. However, the monster card (number 7) is worth minus 10 points. To make this worth 0 points, you must collect cards of the five suits.
The human card (number 1) can transform into any hero of any color. This is a game in which you acquire the cards you want while adding up your fighting power with weapons (number 2), machines (number 3), power suits (number 4), etc.

It's a lot of fun to see the reversal when you defeat the monster.
Play with 55 all-unique cards and shout out the hero's name!
publishers: Joynt Game Factory (ジョイントゲームファクトリー)

description:
The canteen-friendly extension of the cult game STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN on the topic of work and career.

The ultimate game block with 26 creative and fun categories for an unforgettable lunch break with colleagues. Fun guaranteed!

Brings color to the gray everyday working life.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Die kantinentaugliche Erweiterung des Kultspiels STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN rund ums Thema Arbeit & Beruf.

Der ultimative Spielblock mit 26 kreativen und lustigen Kategorien für eine unvergessliche Mittagspause unter Kollegen. Spaß garantiert!

Bringt Farbe in den grauen Berufsalltag.

—description from the publisher

Magnate is a 3-6 player card game in which the players take on the roles of the 0.01%. Their goal is to collapse the 'elected' government (past the point where money-printing will save it) and to take control of the anarcho-capitalist 'utopia' that unfolds following the governmental collapse.

This is a game of two halves.

During the first phase of the game the players must hoard as many commodities and pay as little tax as possible. Players play minions which they use to tactically attack other the players and the government. The government functions as a non-player entity. The goal of the first phase of the game is to strategically build up your position while cooperating with the other players to take down the government. Beware! The government can, and does, win if players focus too much on building their own positions.

In the second phase, triggered after the government is bankrupted, a new deck of player cards are unlocked. Hasn’t everyone wanted their own private air force from time to time?

Within an anarcho-capitalist tundra, players fight over an increasingly scarce resource base. The game continues until only one Magnate worthy of the name is left (in possession of commodities) and wins.

—description from the publisher

A game of quick maths, deception, and trickery! Just like politics! Anuar claims he has the numbers to form a government! Help him get enough seats! Rally as many politicians as you can to your side and win the election!

The Story
It is election season! Anuar is attempting to form government but he doesn't have the majority support!

Help Anuar to gather sufficient numbers to form government! Rally as many politicians as you can to your side and win the election!

How to Set Up
Every player draws cards 
3 players • 7 cards each  
4 players • 7 cards each
5 players • 6 cards each 
6 players • 5 cards each

Each player can choose to change any number of cards from their hands once before game start. Aim for a good balance of number and target cards!


        Maximum number of cards in hand is seven (7)cards. At the end of each turn, if more than seven (7)cards are in hand. 'declare assets" and discard extra cards. Discarded cards do not go back into the deck. 
    


Sequence of Play

     Draw card from deck and put it face up in the middle. 
     Any player declare 'I HAVE THE NUMBERS' and puts a minimum of two cards down. Number cards face down. target card face up. 
     Other players challenge, steal or pass. 
     Winning player keeps the set. Losing players draw another card from the deck. 
     Declare assets al end of the turn. Only seven cards allowed in hand. Discard extra cards. 
     Next player repeats step 1 to 5. 
     When deck runs out, final phase begins. 
     Players begin to draw a card from any other players' hand and put it face up in the middle. 
     Repeat steps 2 and 3 
     Winning player keeps the set. Losing players discard one card from hand. 
     Game ends when no more cards are left or no more cards can be played. 
     Player with most target cards win. 


Types of Cards
Number cards - Card Value is from 1 to 6
2x Muhidin, 2x Jibby, 2x Isabri, 2x Shishamudin, 2x Adam Baby, 2x LGE,
2x Bong Muktar,2x Masabu, 2x Mama Rose, 2x Kayjay, 2x Rena Harum, 2x Abang Jopeng, 2x Zamidi, 2x Hanayo, 2x Hadiwang, 2x Amarslan, 2x S. Sadig, 2x Tajraman

Target cards - Target card where number cards must fulfill via truth or lie toward the target
6x Anuar (7), 6x Anuar (8), 6x Anuar (9)

Wild card -  
2x Mahadir 

    Wild cards value can be anywhere from 1 to 6 only, 
        Wild card cannot be used as a target card 
    


How to Win
Player with most target cards win. To break ties, total up the numbers on the target cards.

If total result is still a tie, means no majority is formed and there is a hung parliament. A new game will commence!

Politikus (Rats as politicians) test your political animality.

Are you crafty enough to climb the party ladder? Greedy enough to reap government projects? Animal enough to stab your own friend?

Because no matter how close our relationship is, the Prime Minister's chair does not fit two person.

How to Setup

     Place the rank cards on your left hand side.
     Party Cards at the bottom together at the first rank in a straight line.
         Objective is to reach to get the prime minister post first.
    
     Place these cards in the drawpile 


Face Down - Sentiment (Conservative and Liberal), Projects, Dark Side
Face Up - Frog Party & Cash for salary

Choose your party

     Each color on the Party Card represents a player (Politikus)
     At the start, each player can choose whether to join the Conservative Party or The Liberal Party.
     However, they can change their current party to the other during the game. 
     If the change of party is more than 2 times, the player must change to the Frog Party according to their color.


Drawing Salary
On each turn, players only get to perform one action only from these 3 playout;

     Draw Salary according to their rank 
     Do a project (Draw Project Card only, hand one (1) Dark Side card to another player)
     Play Sentiment (Draw Sentiment Card only)


Getting Promoted
Players can be promoted following these 3 playout which was obtained during drawing salary. Players only can use 1 playout per turn.

     Pay with cash obtained from salary towards the price of the next rank; eg pay 10K cash to become Department Head from Branch Head. 
     Play the Project Card (Collect reward from project value and if it fulfills the price of the promotion, pay the rank for the promotion).
         For every project card taken by the player, player must pass a dark side card to another player
    
     Play Sentiment (Place sentiment card according to their distinguish party , use sentiment card to pay  for the rank)


Dark Side

     Players who hold other players dark side card can expose the player at anytime.
     Once the player is exposed, the exposed player must pay the fine as stated in the dark side card.
     Failure to pay the fine will result in a demotion.


Becoming Prime Minister

     First player to reach the Prime Minister rank will be the winner.
     However, the Prime Minister must come from a party majority in which the player is in (Conservative or Liberal according to the number of players).
     If the player did not have the majority, the player can switch party or harass other players to join the player's party.


Switching Parties

     Each player can switch parties twice (2). On the third switch, players must replace their character card with the frog party according to their colour.
     For each switch players must; 
         Pay the fine according to their Rank/Salary
         Dispose all sentiment card and get demoted.
    
     Players can switch party at anytime in the game.
     If a player do not have enough cash to switch parties, they can sell a project card at hand to the bank, however the dark side card stay with the player which gotten it during drawing project card.
     Frog Party cannot play sentiment cards
     Bribery and any other transaction can be practice as it is done in the real world of politics as well as in this game.

A fairly fast abstracted World War 1 Atlantic war campaign game.  Like Atlantic Convoys 1939-45, no grid necessary.  (Max. of 3 convoy lanes plus Murmansk Run.  Independent sailers ... sail independently, as does the hard-to-catch special Transport convoy.)
9 half-year turns.  Half as many dreadnoughts and battle cruisers (in proportion) for playability.
Convoy and Grand Fleet escorts linked as in the rationale for the Admiralty opposing convoys, until losses became so horrific in 1916, by which time there were more escorts/destroyers.
So if destroyers are stripped from the Grand Fleet early in the war, German torpedo boats get that many free shots at British ships out in the North Sea.
See descendant Nick Jellicoe's new book, Jellicoe's War:  The U-boat Threat in World War One and the Question of Convoy.
But if Britain starves, the German player wins a Smashing Strategic Victory worth 2 game wins.
Plus, the British/Allied player must keep Russia in the war.
Freighters/freight units can instead be sailed independently, but are subject to losses depending on the density of German U-boats, the raider, and battle cruisers which break out into the Atlantic or up against the Murmansk Run (and the British did get major tonnage to Russia in WW1, until the Revolution).

Same surface warship combat result system without the "distraction" of air warfare.
Trying to keep the German High Seas Fleet bottled up in the North Sea (which has a blown up chart for fleet battles) is a game in itself, albeit helped by Room 40 codebreaking.

The second Damage result has a special little bright red blossom in it for British battle cruiser and even (to a lesser extent) battleship shell magazines. }:-)

In 1917, the US Navy comes to save the British, and in 1918 sets up the can't-be-done North Sea Mine Barrage between the Orkneys and Norway which was unexpectedly successful - many U-boats just disappeared.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Seal bearers are ancient architects of elemental energy flows between realms. They make strategic allies in preparing crucial battlegrounds in the different worlds. They make their homes in nexus worlds and extend a powerful web of influence across all the connected domains within it.

TKG ARENA: The Seal Bearers is a game set up for 2-4 players.

Components required for this game:

WORLD TILES    48
LIFE TOKENS:    30 from each element for each player (Default)
OINKS    1 element for each player as marker and placeholder for the LIFE TOKENS

Winning Conditions

The objective of the game is to have the most VICTORY points at the end of the game. All ties are joint winners.

Ending Condition

The game ends when any player is able to connect 9 WORLD TILES immediately.

Setup

a.    Shuffles the 48 world tiles. Randomly distribute 3 WORLD TILES and 1 OINK (as player marker) to each player. Each player will choose one as his/her starting WORLD TILE and placed his/her OINK player marker on it. Return the remaining WORLD TILES to the stack.
b.    Put 30 LIFE TOKENS of each element (Total of 120) into an opaque bag. Each player randomly draws 3 from the bag and puts it on their OINK (player marker) which itself is placed on their starting WORLD TILE. 
c.    Designate five spaces A, B, O, C and D. Distribute the remaining WORLD TILES into these spaces as evenly as possible as agreed upon by all players. All WORLD TILES in spaces A, B, C and D should face up showing details about the WORLD TILES and WORLD TILES in space O should be faced down.

A B O C D

d.    A starting trade zone containing 3 LIFE TOKENS of each element (with a total of 12) should be set aside within easy reach of all players. When the bag for drawing LIFE TOKENS is empty, return all LIFE TOKENS in the trade zone to the bag setting aside these starting numbers - 3 LIFE TOKENS of each element to remain in the trade zone.
e.    Players are to build their worlds in a 3 x 3 grid by connecting them. Connection is established between 2 WORLD TILES when they shared at least one common insignia. The starting WORLD TILE can occupy any position in the 3 x 3 grid.
f.    Players are to take actions to draw LIFE TOKENS (of 4 elements) from the bag (and place them on their OINK player marker), trade existing LIFE TOKENS from the trade zone, procure
WORLD TILES, move around, refresh and seal WORLD TILES to connect the worlds to achieve as many VICTORY points as possible before the game ends.
g.    The youngest player starts first. Play commences clock-wise in turn order from the starting player once he/she is determined.

On each player’s turn 
Players can perform ONE of these actions during their turn.


     Draw 3 random LIFE TOKENS from the bag and place them on their player marker. The player marker can hold up to 6 LIFE TOKENS. Players need to discard excess LIFE TOKENS down to 6 after this action.
     Trade any 2 LIFE TOKENS from their OINK player marker for 1 LIFE TOKEN of their choice when available in the trade zone. Players can perform multiple trades at this rate in the same action. E.g. Trade any 4 LIFE TOKENS for available 2 LIFE TOKENS of their choice.
     Procure a WORLD TILE by discarding matching LIFE TOKENS to the insignias inscribed on the WORLD TILE from the spaces A, B, C and D into the trade zone and connect it to their current collection. Players need to ensure that their WORLD TILE must be connectable orthogonally adjacent (sharing at least one common insignia) to the WORLD TILE the OINK player marker is currently residing or else this action cannot be taken. A WORLD TILE can also be procured by discarding any 2 LIFE TOKENS into the trade zone and the bottom WORLD TILE from the stack in space O is revealed. If it can be connected (obeying the rules above) to the WORLD TILE the OINK player marker is currently residing, it can thus be placed, otherwise it needs to be instantly discarded to the top of the stack of WORLD TILES at space O just like any procured WORLD TILE that cannot be connected immediately.
     Move their OINK player marker (including all LIFE TOKENS placed on it) to an orthogonally adjacent WORLD TILE. 
     Seal a WORLD TILE by moving all matching LIFE TOKENS from the OINK player marker to the insignias of the WORLD TILE it is on. Players cannot seal WORLD TILES partially.
     Refresh all WORLD TILES by discarding any 1 LIFE TOKEN. Move the top WORLD TILE of each stack in the spaces A, B, C and D to the bottom. For the WORLD TILE in space O, take its bottom tile and place it on top of the stack faced down.


Scoring
When the game ends (by connecting 9 WORLD TILES), VICTORY points for each player is scored in the following ways and summed up as a final.


     Tile Scoring: Each WORLD TILE sealed is scored according to the number of insignias it has. Each insignia scores 1 point. E.g. A WORLD TILE with 3 insignias will earn the player 3 points. Any connected but unsealed WORLD TILE scores 0 points.
     Diversity Scoring: All LIFE TOKENS used to seal the WORLD TILES are parsed and for each set of LIFE TOKENS comprising of 4 different elements, 2 points are scored.
     Connecting Scoring: Each valid connection between WORLD TILES will score 1 point.
     Majority Scoring: The player with the most LIFE TOKENS of an element scores 3 points and the second place scores 1 point. This will be calculated respectively for each of the 4 elements. LIFE TOKENS placed on WORLD TILES as well as the player marker are all considered for this.
     Path Scoring: Each player considers the max number of singularly connected WORLD TILES (connected to only 1 other WORLD TILE) in a sequential linear path within the 3 x 3 grid.  For each WORLD TILE connected this way, 2 points are scored. The minimum length for this linear path must be 3 WORLD TILES and only one longest path is scored. Connected WORLD TILES need not be sealed to be considered for this scoring.


All the different scorings are added up to form the total final score for each player to determine the winner.

Panomachea (pān-ō-mäk-e-ä) is an original, all-out mythological battle game. Capture the most cards to win. Fight with the gods, heroes and monsters of Ancient Greek myth.

This is the kind of game that you'll remember playing. It gives you every opportunity to pull off those staggering wins you'll lord over your friends for years, and all but guarantees you'll eventually face the crushing defeat that can only come when you're you're feeling too secure about victory.

Panomachea is simple in concept. Your opponents have cards, and you want them. Because each one of those cards is worth sweet, sweet points.

So you send your Fighter Cards out to capture theirs by having a higher Power Level than the others.

Each player chooses a Fighter Card and plays them in order. There's always a World Card active that boosts one of the four suits. Once all the players have laid down their Fighter Cards, the highest overall Power Level captures all the cards on the field. However, it's not as simple as just having the highest Power Level card. Tyche (Ty-kee) cards, World cards, individual Fighter Card abilities and other captured cards can sway the round in your favor.

But since there's always a World Card active that boosts one suit and not the others, and changes through multiple methods throughout the game, there's always advantage and disadvantage on the table.

But that's not the only agent of chaos in this deck. Each card also has their individual Power Level, a unique ability that allows advantages against specific cards (for example, Athena steals victory from Zeus, and Odysseys can't be captured by Poseidon), and players also have Tyche (chance or fortune) cards that allow the tide to be turned in their favor by a strategic play.

Homeostasis is all about trying to keep Bill, a guy that does stupid things, alive. Mr. Bill is constantly doing stuff that is throwing his system out of whack. You will play the role of his hormones in trying to get things closer to where they should be. Bill wont make this easy. Many of the hormones work together and some are even required for others to work.

At the start of each round a Bill card is flipped. Bill has done something and his homeostatic variables of pH, blood glucose, and blood pressure have changed as a result. Players have hormone cards that impact those same variables. They choose which ones to play to make Bill's situation less dire. At least until Bill does something else. Your goal is to get through all of the Mr. Bill cards and keep him alive.

Linimasa Card Game: 25 Prophets and Messengers is a card game designed to learn the timeline of events from 25 Prophets and Messengers in an easy and fun way.

Objective of the Game: Players take turns guessing the order of event cards over 3 rounds. At the end of each round, flip over the remaining player tokens and calculate the score. The player with the highest total score after 3 rounds wins the game.

Reveal one event card as the reference for players. Then, guess if the card on top of the deck happened before or after the reference event.

If it’s about a different Prophet or Messenger, place the card horizontally. If it’s about the same Prophet or Messenger, place the card vertically.

After placing the card, flip it over. If the card order is incorrect, adjust it to the correct sequence. Then, the player places their point token.

The winner is the player with the most points.

Mengenal Emosi ("Emotion Explorer") is a card game where players guess the emotion card (happy, sad, angry, disgusted, or scared) chosen by the active player based on a selected situation card. For each correct guess, players earn points from the active player. The winner is the player with the highest points.

BEST COLD CASE FILE GAME - An almighty snow storm has hit the Science Research Station, high up Mount Mortimer. 9 staff members were trapped there until the storm calmed. Tensions were growing, fights were breaking out, and on December 3rd, 2022... Dr Achenbach was found dead with a fatal stab wound to the neck. Play detective to crack this gripping murder mystery game, and bring the criminal to justice.

PHYSICAL EVIDENCE - Inside the case file, you'll find police reports, suspect photos, witness statements, newspaper articles, a coroners report, and more.

SEARCH THROUGH A POLICE WEBMAIL - In addition to the physical evidence supplied inside of the case files, you'll need to access a police webmail to prove the prime suspects innocent or guilty - but it's not easy...you'll need to be a smart investigator to gain access to the online portal!

A GREAT NIGHT IN - With approximately 3 hours of game play (depending on your skill level) and a difficulty rating of 4/5 this "whodunnit" style thriller will provide a fun night in for all who choose to participate. If you can't crack the case without a secret clue, hints and answers are available online.

—description from the publisher

Explore the wonders of Africa, with this fun, educational card-shedding trivia boardgame by Aideeology Games.

Dive into the rich cultures, landmarks, and history of all 54 African countries and discover the diversity of the second largest continent through this delightful game of trivia and strategy.

Perfect for Any Gathering
Whether it's for festivals, game nights, office parties, or school events, this game is designed for 2 - 12 players and only takes 15 - 20 minutes to play.

What’s in the Box?
One beautifully illustrated quad-fold board  
Two decks of vibrant country and question cards  
A comprehensive Answer Booklet and Instructions Manual

How to Play
Setting up is a breeze: simply unfold the board, shuffle the cards, and distribute the country cards among the players.  
Each turn, players display a country card and address trivia questions related to it.  
Answer correctly to eliminate your card.  
If you answer incorrectly, you must take your card back.  
The first player to eliminate all their country cards wins the game!

Need a Hint?
No worries! The game board includes helpful guides to assist with tougher questions, ensuring everyone can keep up and enjoy the game.

As you play Welcome to AFRICA, prepare for unexpected twists and strategic choices that make each game a unique adventure keeping the rich culture of Africa alive.

—description from the publisher

Play the 2017/2018 World Cup or race all time great skiers as you race the Slalom snow event.  In this solitaire game, you can play a single event, or do a full season.  The designer gives you a method to rate modern day skiers.

This is a pen and paper game, where dice determine the outcomes.  Each skier is rated on control and speed.  Each race of the slope is divided into sections, allowing realistic split times to be recorded.

The skier with the shortest duration after two times through the Slalom wins the event.

KNJO is an abstract strategy game for two people, where players compete to be the first to place 4 tiles in the same line, either vertically or horizontally.

Components

    1 7x7 grid board
    6 tiles for each player (6 white and 6 black)
    numbering tiles (from 0 to 6)


Rules
During the turn a player must perform two mandatory actions in the following order:

     Move a tile orthogonally in any direction and combination, this tile must move the number of squares indicated by the numbering tile that indicates its row, which is located at the side of the board. Considering the following: 
        A checker cannot move over squares occupied by any other checker. 
        A piece can move through squares it has already moved through.
    
     Exchange the number tile of the row used (of the row from which the checker to be moved was chosen) with any other number tile.


—description from the designer

Equal parts hunters and hoarders, Scavengers turn civilization’s broken remains into . . . broken remains with purpose.

This Vestige Scrap Pack features 12 new cards to shuffle into your collection.
The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach the finish wins the game.

With the Hammer is an asymmetric wargame covering the German Peasants War in Thuringia in 1525 along with the exploits of one of its most famous protagonists - Thomas Müntzer. With the Hammer will come with wooden pieces, counters, 2 rulebooks, and historical commentary by Professor Andrew Drummond, and a 22×17 inch map.

Each player is a peasant or a noble; the peasants win as a team, or the nobles win as a team. There is no true solitaire system, but like most wargames, it can be soloed two-handed.

Kaijus From Space! is a co-operative tabletop game in which up to five players dive into the chaos and city-wide destruction of a battle between giant monsters from outer space and heroic human defenders.

Players take on the role of unique kaijus, each with dynamic skills and support moves that evolve throughout the game, becoming increasingly more powerful and bizarre. As kaijus take damage, they grow angrier and stronger, shifting between three different forms that provide different advantages and dangers.

However, the human armies defending the city will fight back with a variety of countermeasures, ranging from unpredictable event cards to robotic champions and towering mechas, ready to face the monsters head-on. Players have to work together and act in synergy with their teammates to neutralize the defenses, damage the infrastructure, and earn enough destruction tokens to win the game.

Inspired by Japanese monster movies of the 1970s and 1980s, Kaijus From Space! mixes playful humor and a moderate level of complexity with gameplay that aims to strike a balance between accessibility and strategic depth. Pairing the social fun of a party game with mechanisms that reward planning and shared tactics, it focuses on physical interactions between players, whose real-world actions can affect the game and vice versa.

Stomp through the city and wreak havoc, taking advantage of overpowered moves and obvious plot holes. Knock down that pathetic skyscraper, and crush the puny humans!

—description from the designer
