description:
Castle Rock is a 2-6 player fishing game played with dominoes.
Every turn players play a domino from their own supply into the central tableau, where through a mechanism similar to capturing in Hnefatafl, dominoes are captured.
Through clever play, it is possible to chain multiple captures in a single turn.

At the end of each round, players score points equal to the number of dominoes they captured, minus the dominoes left in the tableau.

First player to reach 50 points wins.

Vom Werden Des Brotes (1920), is a board game that conveys the journey of bread to its players. The aim is to reach the end point from the starting point as in other games. Meanwhile, all stages of bread are presented to the players with a story. The most striking points here are that this bread-making/production is called “Segensreiche Arbeit”, that is, blessed work. Apart from this, it is described as “Landmanner”, that is, countrymen, for working farmers.

Soar into the thrilling skies of the First World War, where courage meets cunning in a deadly ballet of wings and gunfire! In this fast-paced air combat game, you'll take command of daring fighter pilots, lumbering bombers, and sharp-eyed scouts, weaving through clouds, evading anti-aircraft fire, and outmaneuvering foes in a relentless struggle for dominance.

The game is designed to be played in 20 to 30 minutes, it is a fast-play, hex based, IGOUGO game.

Within these pages you will find all that you need, the rules, maps, aircraft data sheets, counters, scenarios and campaign set up. One can start playing on the A4 sized scenario maps included ahead.

The counters (also included ahead) can be cut out and pasted on card-stock paper or MDF bases, one may also use alternative counters, models or simply plot units using light pencil markings.

Turn sequence:

     Attacker moves
     Attacker fires
     Defender moves
     Defender fires

This book explores the background of the Cyprus conflict and details the armed forces involved, including regular units from Turkey and Greece, the Greek Cypriot National Guard, and Turkish Cypriot militia units. British and UN forces were also present on the island. It outlines the military operations conducted on land, at sea, and in the air, drawing on interviews with veterans and published memoirs.

Additionally, the book offers modelling suggestions for the various forces involved and presents eight scenarios to re-enact key actions on the tabletop. 
These scenarios are illustrated with wargames, maps, and orders of battle that can be adapted to popular wargame rules—providing gamers with everything they need to recreate this fascinating, albeit tragic, conflict.

—description from the publisher

In this broken world, where loyalty and leverage are currency, some spend freely, searching for a better deal at others’ expense.

This Vestige Scrap Pack features 12 new cards to shuffle into your collection. Expansion content, requires Vestige to play.

Operazione C3 is a simulation game that aims to reconstruct the planned, but never realized, invasion of the island of Malta by the Axis forces in the summer of 1942. The game is played in turns, each representing one day of real time. The map scale is 1.5 km per hex, and the units represent represent battalions and companies that would have taken part in the campaign.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vestige: Cultivation of Power Scrap Pack
description:
The Botanists guard a secret bloom, an untapped energy, a whispered key to humanity’s future, one not powered by the vitality of the flesh.

This Vestige Scrap Pack features 12 new cards to shuffle into your collection. Expansion content, requires Vestige to play.

boardgame: Junior Escape Game: Flucht aus dem Zoo
description: Players work together to help animals escape a zoo by solving a series of short puzzles in this family-friendly escape game.

The Expansion Set is an exciting addition to the game Mars Expedition: SOL43, offering players new challenges and opportunities to enhance their gaming experience. With this expansion, players can extend their mission on the red planet with additional sols, simulate the intricate task of Mars soil analysis, and expand the adventure for up to 4 players.

This is a trick-taker in which teams are battling to win the majority of holes on a 9-hole golf course  Each hole is a par 3 and will be played over 3 distinct tricks: Drive, Chip and a Putt.

The rank of the cards will shift depending on the trick being played.  A team wins a hole by winning at least 2 out of the 3 tricks by hitting the best shot.  As soon as a team has won 5 holes they have won the game!

The Stack is a streamlined modern skirmish game, set in the world’s ungoverned spaces and conflict zones. It uses two decks or ‘stacks’ of standard playing cards to run the game and is intended to be played at a squad to platoon level, using individually based 15-28mm figures. The game is setting-agnostic and can be used to play anything from late Cold War to near future.

description:
Inside this can you will find classic beer games from around the globe. From time-honored classics like beer pong to raucous newcomers like Edward Fortyhands and a few international games. This tallboy is the cure for party boredom.

This can includes:

     50 Beer game instruction cards
     Ping pong ball


—description from the publisher

Five classic drinking games get a presidential twist! Beat the odds and pass the laws at the "Battle of Gettysburg," bet drinks on the "Secret Legislation," test your honesty with "Lincoln's Top Hat," and more in games of endurance, luck, and liberal libation. With two shot glasses and an illustrated playing card deck, this set is sure to unify any party in good spirits, no politics required!

Includes:

     2 shot glasses
     1 deck of 54 standard playing cards
     1 instruction book


You must be of legal drinking age to play these games. Please drink responsibly.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vestige: Followers of the Codex Scrap Pack
description:
Silent architects of the liberation of the flesh, the followers of the Codex write humanity’s rebirth from the shadows.

This Vestige Scrap Pack features 12 new cards to shuffle into your collection. Expansion content, requires Vestige to play.

IntroductionFengros (from Scottish Gaelic: fèin-sgrios, meaning "self-annihilation") is a drawless game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexagons (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 4 cells per side, but boards of 3 and 5 cells per side are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is the maximal set of stones of the same color connected to each other. A single stone is also a group.
     The size of a group is the number of stones it contains.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, place all friendly stones captured by your opponent in the immediately preceding turn onto any empty cells, followed by an additional friendly stone. If your opponent captured none, only place your additional stone. Then, remove all enemy groups adjacent to any largest friendly group.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when only stones of one color remain on the board. If you do not own those stones, you are the winner.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places one black stone on any empty cell, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer

Further explore the remnants of civilization with nine new map tiles, each offering unique actions and strategic twists. By swapping these tiles into the game, players unlock new tactical choices and possibilities for resourceful play, creating an ever-shifting landscape that demands adaptability. Every tile introduces new avenues to leverage and outmaneuver opponents, making each session feel like uncharted territory. Prepare for the unexpected and sharpen your strategies, the sprawling remains of this world just got sprawlinger.

Expand the struggle for leverage with everything you need for 5th and 6th players: cubes, discs, pawns, dual-layered dashboards, and personal GameTrayz player trayz for two more players. All these components fit in the game's main box.

Shell We? is a family-friendly, vibrant, and strategic tile-placement game by Reiner Knizia.

The goal is to be the first to play all your tiles or minimize penalty points when the game ends. With its easy-to-learn rules and intriguing mechanics, such as bonus actions, starfish tiles, and optional bottle tile effects, Shell We? provides an engaging experience for players of all ages and skill levels.

Set against the backdrop of a serene beach, players race to arrange colourful shell tiles on the board, following specific placement rules to outsmart their opponents and gain strategic advantages.


     Each shell tile is marked with a specific color and number. Tiles can only be placed on spaces on the board that match both their colour and number.
     A tile must be placed adjacent (horizontally or vertically, but not diagonally) to another tile already on the board.
     Starfish tiles act as wilds and can be placed on any space next to an existing tile. If another player has the matching shell tile, they can replace the starfish tile with the correct one.
     Placing tiles in specific ways, such as on "pearl spaces" or in consecutive order (ascending or descending), can trigger bonus actions, like placing additional tiles or gaining special effects.


Whether you're a seasoned gamer or a casual enthusiast, Shell We? offers a versatile gameplay experience enhanced by variant rules, bonus effects, and beautiful components. Choose from different board layouts, explore the Message in a Bottle, and dive into this seaside strategy adventure!

—description from the publisher

Beaver House is a fast-paced and strategic family board game where players assume the roles of beaver builders competing to construct the perfect house for a particularly picky customer. Designed by Eddie Lee and published by Korea Boardgames Co., Ltd., this game combines quick thinking, precise placement, and a touch of luck.

With its charming artwork by Makoto Takami and dynamic gameplay, it invites players to balance speed with strategy as they arrange material tiles on their construction boards. Whether you’re racing to complete your board or planning your next move in apprentice mode, Beaver House offers an engaging experience for 2-4 players ages 8 and up, with games lasting around 20 minutes.

At the start of each round, an order card is revealed. This card specifies the placement rules for that round. Players then race to arrange their material tiles on their board to meet the conditions outlined on the order card (e.g., certain tiles cannot touch, or specific tiles must be placed in designated slots). The first player to complete their board grabs the hourglass and flips it, giving the other players a limited amount of time to finish. Once the time runs out, players check their boards in the order of completion. Only players who have correctly met the conditions win the order card. The first player to collect 4 order cards (or 3 in apprentice mode) wins and becomes the ultimate beaver builder!

—description from the publisher

Swap, flip, and stack the cats! Move cat cards from the sofa to the shelf, or the shelf to the playtime area. Make two rows match with each other and score minus(-) points. The player with the lowest score wins a bottle. Collect two bottles to triumph in this adorable and strategic cat game!


     Packed with irresistibly cute (and totally relatable) cat illustrations.
     Easy-to-learn gameplay with a clever stacking twist for added depth.
     Stacking: Score double minus(-) points and an extra turn, but only at the risk of a big penalty!


—description from the publisher

When your kind barely stood upright, their empire spanned the stars. Yet, even they could not prevent the rot from corrupting the tissue of reality.

This is a new Campaign Expansion, introducing a new narrative campaign, new Intruders, new Boss, new Spacers and Suits, new Gear, new Locations and much much more. All content from the Expansioncan also be used in modular custom campaigns.

What heinous experiments has Earth Government been conducting here? Or was it the Corps? Either way, it is all Theirs now. And their Design transcends your understanding.

This is a new Campaign Expansion, introducing a new narrative campaign, new Intruders, new Boss, new Spacers and Suits, new Gear, new Locations and much much more. All content from the Expansioncan also be used in modular custom campaigns.

A high-energy party game that will have everyone screeching, laughing, and hooting in no time. Grab your hand of three bird cards and frantically imitate their calls while simultaneously listening for similar calls around the table. Match calls with other players imitating the same species to collect the card, racing to make the most matches before all the cards run out!

—description from the publisher

Wild Pong is a funny card game for 2 or 4 players in which you will discover what it feels like to play a wild game of ping-pong.

Wild Pong is a card game that successfully represents the table tennis experience through hand management and stroke compatibility mechanics that build a balanced, exciting and strategic game.

Players will have service cards to start each point and must alternately play hit cards that are compatible with the hit previously played by the opponent. The player who does not have a hit card that allows the point to continue will lose the point and the player who scores 11 points will be the winner of the game.

You will also have 10 special players who have a special strength or ability but also a weakness. Your game tactics and the possibilities of the match will vary!

—description from the publisher
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description: The Stack Combat Pack builds on the combat system set out in the core ruleset. It provides players with additional options to customize their stands’ weapon loadouts and allow for new gameplay.

The Stack Vehicles Pack builds on the Core rules. Vehicles add another dimension to your game. They are faster, tougher and generally better armed than infantry. They can quickly dominate areas of the table, apply concentrated firepower, and rapidly adapt to your opponent’s actions.

The Social Media game! Show that you are completely up to date! You can easily find your way on the internet and you are incredibly popular on social media.... but now you really have to prove it: collect the most LIKES during this game, by using your smartphone, tablet or laptop to be the fastest to find a video, the shortest route from A to B or the answer to a random question. Be the first to put on a song and of course sing it along as loud as possible. Also make sure that you get the most reactions on Facebook or Twitter in an original way, and your victory is a little closer. But of course it doesn't all happen online; offline you are also incredibly social, so there is no shortage of dice, truth or dare, old-fashioned bell-ringing and other funny assignments!

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Vestige: The Ascendent Machine Scrap Pack
description:
To the Forged, the obelisk offers salvation, and humanity is a flaw to be purged. Every gear they turn grinds us closer to a future where only the machine remains—unyielding, perfect, eternal.

This Vestige Scrap Pack features 12 new cards to shuffle into your collection. Expansion content, requires Vestige to play.

—description from the publisher

Take a break from the real world and escape into this mystery for approximately 2-3 hours of solving with a difficulty rating of 6/10.

Jack Frost is the personification of Winter. Folklore says that he is the one who paints the leavs in fall and creates the fern like tendrils of frost on windows. Over the years, Jack Frost has been portrayed more and more as a trickster. Most of his tricks are harmless, but remember to be on guard: Jack Frost can be as cold and ruthless as winter itself.

Tournament Arc is a comedic strategy card game where you draft and coach a team of ragtag student athletes to win the ultimate sports competition! But what sport exactly? You'll find out along the way!

Build a story with your friends as you train your team and hinder others with a delightful blend of strategy and chaos! No knowledge of sports or anime required.

The game consists of 3 phases: drafting, training, and competing. In the drafting phase, players draw and pass athlete cards to build out their initial team of 3 athletes. In the training phase, players take turns playing episode cards to buff/debuff athletes and activate events while building a story. Half-way through the training phase, the sport card is revealed which determines which stats matter and how many athletes will be sent out per team. In the competition phase, players total their respective team scores according to the active sport card to determine the winner.

—description from the publisher

WHO FRAMED SANTA CLAUS? – A CHRISTMAS MYSTERY GAME TO SOLVE 'Twas the night before Christmas, and throughout the whole town, no criminals were stirring and the crime rate was down. Or so you thought...
You have just settled down for a long winter's nap when the tranquil quiet is broken by the ringing of your phone. Much to your surprise, the police have arrested Santa Claus for delivering a bomb disguised as a present. Santa claims he didn’t know about the bomb and has specifically requested you to clear his name. Failure to exonerate Santa will mean that he is unable to complete his annual gift-giving duties, leaving millions of children disappointed on Christmas morning. If ever there was a case that demanded your attention, this is it. Time to grab your coat and head out into the snowy night to find out Who Framed Santa Claus?

Christmas is about to be ruined, and there is only one person who can save it:  you. The Catalytic Cheer Converter has been stolen from Santa's sleigh.  Without it, the sleigh can't fly.  Since there are less than 24 hours until Christmas, that's a huge problem! If the missing part of the sleigh isn't recovered soon, this year will be one without presents.  Santa has tasked you with discovering who stole the Catalytic Cheer Converter and retrieving the missing part.  Can you save Christmas in time and put an end to this Merry Mischief?

In Summer 1960, you want to empty your hand to score as few points as possible.

To set up, lay out the nine flower cards in a 3x3 grid, and deal each player a hand of seven cards. On a turn, play a card onto the matching flower space — but only so long as the number on your card doesn't already appear in that row and column. If you can't play a card, draw from the deck, playing it immediately if possible and otherwise adding it to your hand.

If you notice three cards of the same number on the board, whether after you play or on someone else's turn, you can call "Trio" and claim those cards from the board.

When a player's hand is empty, everyone sums the value of cards in their hand, then subtracts 1 for each trio they collected.

For the second round, the only change to gameplay is that when someone collects a trio, you discard those cards, then give the cards in your hand to your left-hand opponent.

After two rounds, the player with the lowest score wins.

You’ve all been naughty, but which one of you will get caught?

You and your classmates are becoming restless and messing around in the classroom while the teacher is writing on the board. However, the teacher can hear you and it is really starting to annoy him. Sooner or later, he is going to turn around and catch one of you in the act; it had better not be you!

It is a card game which is played in 3 rounds. Players receive 6 cards will play 1 card from their hand simultaneously during each turn. Each card has a number which will have 2 functions:
 1. The card will be worth that amount of point when played.
 2. A common token will move forward on the teacher's board. The 
 number of spaces it moves forward will be equal to the number 
 on the card. 
Players continue playing until the teacher snaps. The teacher snaps when the token on the teacher’s board reaches:
  2 players : 20 points 
  3 players : 30 points
  4 players : 40 points 
  5-6 players: 50 points
The player whose card causes the teacher to snap will lose 3 points in the first round, 4 points in the second round and 5 points in the third round.

Count your points at the end of the final round, and whoever has the most points wins the game.

The game contains several cards with special abilities which adds interaction, surprise and strategy to the game. Play your cards at the most opportune moment and anticipate your classmates' moves in order to win!

—description from the designer

1-4 players work together to build a 3D city out of building blocks in this cooperative puzzle game.

You'll need to work with your allies to orient each building block so that it is in the right place for your skyline, without upsetting theirs!

Skyline is a puzzle game with multiple puzzles of ascending difficulties, composed of specific sets of skyline cards. Try to complete each puzzle at all, and then try to beat each puzzle's high score by having multiple blocks leftover.

7 copies were made available at the 2024 PAX Unplugged Indie Games Night Market.

—description from the designer

Brazilian card game, release through crowdfunding in 2018. Based on a local comic book, in the game you fight your opponents in a "king of the hill" battle.

The players begin the game diving the 13 heroes equally (5 for 2P, 4 for 3P or 3 for 4P). Each hero has an ability and a strenght. 3 heroes of each player start the game in the table, while the remaining are placed in the reserve.

Each player receives 5 battle card, that adds strength or changes the game in some way. Then they take turns placing those cards on their heroes. This is called the "Movement Phase".

In the "Fighting Phase", the first player chooses an attacker among his heroes and a defender among the opponent´s heroes. Numbers are compared; the highest wins, the lowest is removed from the game. The winner (or both heroes in case of a drawning) and flipped sideways. Every battle card is discarded. The defending player then chooses another of his heroes to attack, and so on, until every hero has fighted once (and no more than once).

Then each player refresh his heroes, take heroes from the reserve (if any), receives 3 new battle cards, place then in his heroes and a new battle phase begins, until only one hero survives and is declared the winner.

boardgame: No Turning Back: The Battle of the Wilderness 5-6 May 1864
description:
No Turning Back is the next game in The Gamers' Line of Battle (LoB) series covering the first epic confrontation between Grant and Lee, the Battle of the Wilderness in May 1864. The battle is a very misunderstood battle with the general narrative that both armies were stumbling to find each other in the thick woods. Units get lost and confusion reigns. While the precepts of this confused melee are generally true, there is much more to this battle that No Turning Back will showcase based on extensive design and research. There was maneuver at the operational level as both commanders prodded their forces forward to hit an open flank. Grant was seeking a general offensive to bring to bear the entire weight of his large army. Lee was not looking for a general engagement in the Wilderness, but had no choice when the shooting started and deftly engaged his outnumbered army. What is not often known is that both sides had multiple chances of obtaining victory only to be undone by the tide of fortune at the last minute. Do you have the dogged determination as Grant or Lee to snatch victory in the bloody thickets?

Game specific rules to handle the terrain that dictated how the forces fought include: WIlderness terrain type, command & communication difficulties, different trail types, and guides. Unique command situations are likewise represented in the game with Grant's chain of command with Meade's Army of the Potomac and Burnside's independent corps, Hancock's pseudo-wing command, Lt. Col. Sorrel's ad hoc division, Ewell's stoutness then passiveness on successive days, and A.P. Hill's inability to get his men dug in which nearly spelt disaster for him. While cavalry action was minimal, the actions of two cavalry regiments, the 1st NC and 5th NY, proved pivotal and their exploits are captured in the rules.

The map is accurately rendered from a survey conducted immediately following the war that laid out the myriad of trails that led the armies astray. The Order of Battle is presented with accurate regimental strengths with contributions from a leading Civil War scholar. No Turning Back will show the Wilderness in incredible detail at the regimental scale.

Scenarios:
The Battle of the Wilderness: Traditional Start (All 4 maps)
The Battle of the Wilderness: Early Start  (All 4 maps)
A Weird, Uncanny Contest: Warren’s Attack (parts of 2 maps)
The Earth Had Swallowed Them: Saunders Field (1 map)
Death Was in Every Shot: Wright’s Attack (1 map)
Death Holds High Carnival: Hancock vs. Hill (1 map)
Victory Without Triumph: Day 2 Start  (All 4 maps)
Driving Them Most Beautifully: Southern Flank (2 maps)

Components:
Line of Battle rulebook (v3.0)
Two Line of Battle Charts and Tables (v3.0) booklets
No Turning Back game-specific rulebook
Two No Turning Back specific charts (Terrain Key and TEC)
Four 22”x34” full color game maps
1,960 counters
Box and dice
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Mr. Popokpok: Disposable Diaper Waste Management
description:
In Mr. Popokpok, players explore the challenges and solutions related to managing disposable diaper waste through two engaging game modes:

Game Mode 1: Fact or Hoax Challenge
Players take turns drawing statement cards related to disposable diaper waste management. Each player secretly decides whether the statement is a fact or a hoax. Once all players have made their choices, they reveal their answers simultaneously. Correct answers earn 1 point, and the player with the most points at the end wins the game.

Game Mode 2: Process Guessing Race
In this mode, players compete to guess the correct stage of diaper waste management based on the cues given by the active player. The active player stops the sound of a crying baby at a specific stage in the process. Using two tokens, other players place their guesses on the process cards they believe match the stopping point. Correct guesses earn 1 point for both the active player and the guessing player. The game ends when all players have had a turn as the active player, and the one with the highest score is declared the winner.

Mr. Popokpok combines fun and education, raising awareness about environmental issues in a family-friendly and interactive way!

—description from the designer

Players must safeguard four private body parts represented by Private Body Part Cards. A player loses if they fail to protect their private parts twice, earning two Protection Cards.

How to Play:
Each player selects a Body Part Card and places it facedown in front of them.

The main player announces: “The body part I must protect is…” and all players reveal their chosen cards simultaneously.

Players race to guess which body part appears most often by quickly slapping the correct Answer Card.

The fastest player wins the round and discards one card. Other players take back their played cards.

The role of the main player rotates clockwise.

The game ends when a player receives two Protection Cards, symbolizing the need for increased awareness against child sexual abuse.

—description from the designer

Roll-Over! is a THRILLING push-your-luck roll n' write - all you need is 7 dice and a pencil!

OBJECT OF THE GAME:
Use guts, luck, and strategy to roll DARING dice combos, totaling the highest score by the final round! 
Every turn is tense, as you have to decide each time whether you'll STOP and SCORE, or ROLL FOR MORE!

HOW TO PLAY:
In 'ROLL-OVER!', you are rolling six dice to try to make hands that are familiar to players of 'Yahtzee' or 'Zilch', but with twists that make it consistently EXCITING and TENSE, from the first roll to the last! In our game, instead of rolling a set number of times, you can roll as many times as you wish! However, each time you roll, you MUST be able to arrange your dice to make something MORE VALUABLE than what you previously had on the table! So you may start with a pair for 5 points, but by rolling your dwindling pool of remaining dice, you may go to three of a kind for 15, then a full-house for 30, until you're deciding if you should take a BIG SWING by rolling your FINAL DIE, get TWO TRIPLES for 75 POINTS! But if, at any time during your rolls, you're NOT able to make something that's worth more, you BUST, and get NOTHING!

OTHER EXCITING FEATURES INCLUDE:
- BONUSES for bigger hands, which further encourage you to PUSH YOUR LUCK! But they're LIMITED, so grab them EARLY!
- Using all SIX of your dice is a 'Rollover', that allows you to BANK your score, and KEEP ROLLING for even MORE POINTS! With bonuses that grow EXPONENTIALLY!
- A BRILLIANT catch-up mechanism called BUST BOOSTS (okay, the name for them isn't all that brilliant) - special, one-time-use powers that unlock each time you BUST, which increase your chances of getting those HUGE HANDS later on!
- NEW TO THE LATEST VERSION: 'LONGSHOT BETS' - When ANY player (the 'shooter') chooses to roll their FINAL die to try to make a BIG HAND, you can BET on them to SUCCEED! So if THEY win, YOU WIN! You've never cheered so loudly for your friends to do well!

A gameplay how-to video helps see the game in action! We encourage you to leave reviews, questions, and comments! Enjoy!

'-description from the designer'

A nautical/pirate 4x game featuring historic, mythical and sci-fi themed factions. You build the board through exploring as the game progresses. Your charter is to achieve your asymmetric goal before anyone else can complete theirs.

To accomplish this extraordinary feat; you must sail your fleet with or against the wind die with prowess, navigate with your faction-specific instrument cards to discover the seas then the shores as you play, and take to your cannon to exterminate your adversaries ships or settlements.

boardgame: Gangsters! A Complete Gangster-Era Tabletop Game
description:
You run out of the bank and jump into a waiting car. “Go, go, go!”
Frank punches the gas and you take off, a couple of bullets whizzing by.
“Dang candy a$$ security guards. Get a real job!” you yell back at them.
Frank hangs a quick left, horn a honking, and you hear the sirens coming closer. 
Some to the left and some behind you. 
Frank runs a red light, missing a car as it careens out of your way. 
You smile and think to yourself…
What a good life.

Gangsters is a stand-alone game set in the Roaring 20’s but with a little tweaking and subbing out of weapons it’ll work for 5150 New Beginnings too and other THW games as well.

In this mini-campaign you are a Gangster with the job of robbing banks. Or a G-Man tasked with bringing the Gangsters to justice. The mini-campaign continues until the Gangster is killed or imprisoned or the G-Man is killed or retires.

Play it on the tabletop with any figures you may already have. Play it on the Battle Boards and Counters – we’ve given you both. Play it any way you want because after all…

It’s Your Game!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Western Desert Force: Operation Compass – North Africa Campaign
description:
Focusing on Operation Compass in 1940. This British operation originally planned as a raid on Italian camps in Egypt, turned into a much longer campaign across North Africa. This battalion level scenario pack contains 6 exciting miniatures war game scenarios written in campaign form and playable by 2-4 players.

The scenarios include data sheets for vehicles and uses Fist Full of TOWs 3 rules. Scenarios can be easily converted to your favorite game system.

The six scenarios can be played as a campaign. A campaign score sheet is included, enabling players to track the campaign progress and final score.

These scenarios put you in the Gasoline Cowboy seat, making you and your gaming partner(s) the tank drivers as you play through the Western Desert Force: Operation Compass - North Africa Campaign.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
This Cicada wrester comes with three musical notes they'll use as they build their harmonies.

First made available as a promo for the Kabuto Sumo big box accessory.

Celebrate nearly 50 years of heavy metal history with the ultimate game for Iron Maiden fans! MONOPOLY: Iron Maiden lets players relive the prolific English metal band's worldwide live performances as they buy, sell, and trade the most memorable performances over the decades, such as Legacy of the Beast Tour, Somewhere Back in Time Tour, and more! Give the band's equipment a go and take custom tokens like Flying Helmet or Steve's bass around the board to set up Arenas and Stadiums. Open the Box and Feeling Lucky? Cards will test your dedication on the road for the most Eddie Bucks in the end to win!

—description from the publisher

Help! the water playground is drying up. The pump is broken and the mole is making molehills and blocking the water from flowing. You are a team of Uniekies (Unique-ies). Every Uniekie has a disability and a superpower!

Can you work together to make the water flow again? Use each others special powers and (dis)abilities to make the pump work again and save the water playground so that everybody can play in the mud again!

Game was developed with children, and made possible by Stichting het Gehandicapte Kind (Children with Disabilities Fund) and the Speeltuinbende. Special thanks to Arsenaal Doesburg.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Two players take on the role of Hanbok dress designers and design a collection of Hanbok for a fashion show. Hanbok is a traditional Korean garment, and in Hanbok Runway, you'll create a modern take on it.

Each hanbok set is created by layering a fabric card and a style card.
There are 10 different style cards and 30 unique fabric cards to create 300 different designs.

When you play a set:
(1) Choose one of two colors from the fabric cards to gain an influence token, and 
(2) Apply the ability of the style card. 

    Some influence tokens are set face-down, and some even give you negative influence points.
    The abilities of style cards allow you to convert numbers, get information, etc.


You can play 0-2 sets per turn, and the game ends when someone makes 10 sets or when 2 types of influence tokens are removed.

Influence is compared for each color, and whoever scores 1 or more and has a higher influence than the other player gets victory points for that color. The player with the highest total wins.

You will fall in love with the simple rules, quick setup, intense competition, and above all, the beauty of Hanbok.

The game catapults every Harry Potter fan straight onto the Quidditch pitch at Hogwarts! In this Harry Potter card game for adults, teenagers and children aged 6 and over, Quidditch sets are collected. Memory skills are also required to avoid bludgers and find the rare golden snitch.

The aim of the game is to form as many Quidditch sets as possible and collect points. A Quidditch set consists of four Quidditch field cards and one Quaffle, Bludger or Golden Snitch each. One point is awarded for Quidditch sets that are equipped with a quaffle. Three points are awarded for the Quidditch set with the Golden Snitch. One point is deducted for each Quidditch set with a Bludger. The player with the most points wins the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Diggers' Resolve: The Battle of Milne Bay, August-September, 1942
description:
“I don’t think I have ever given any troops a tougher job than this.” –Commander of Guinea Force, Lieutenant General Sydney F. Rowell about the upcoming battle for Milne Bay.

Although the Japanese called off their invasion of Port Moresby following the battle of Coral Sea in May 1942, the high command of the Imperial Japanese Navy still wanted to continue with operations to gain control of all of eastern New Guinea. To that effect the Japanese navy, without any approval or support from their counterparts in the Imperial Army, decided to conduct Operation RE to take control of Milne Bay at the eastern end of New Guinea. The Japanese believed the area to be only weakly held by militia and a token force of Australian reservists. However, thanks to the US code breaking activities, the Australians were alerted to the new Japanese threat at Milne Bay and had rushed two brigades of the Australian 7th Infantry Division, veterans of the battles in North Africa, and part of a third to the region. What ensued was a battle that resulted in the first Allied victory over Japanese land forces in the war.

Can you do as well or better than your historical counterparts? Learn and enjoy!

—description from the publisher

IntroductionHirdalan (from Old Norse: hirð land, meaning "territorial group of warriors") is a strategy game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 9 cells per side, but boards of 7 or 11 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stackable pieces of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a maximal set of connected stones of the same color. A single stone is also a group.
     The size of a group is the number of stones it contains.
     A territory is the maximal set of connected empty cells. You own a territory if the largest group adjacent to it is of your color, and in case of a tie, the next largest groups are compared; if all are the same size, the territory does not score for either player.
     The value of a territory is the number of empty cells it contains.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone on an empty cell such that the group of the placed stone is at most one stone larger than the largest enemy group, excluding itself if before placement it was the largest group.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when exactly one player cannot place stones, and the player with the territories of the highest value wins. To prevent ties, half a point is awarded to the player with the largest group. In case of a tie in size, the next largest groups are considered.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a black stone on an empty cell, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer

Form your Bear Paws to obtain the coveted Honey Jar for your Bear-tastic friends with Bear With ME!!! In this fast-playing card game, the goal is to collect the best combination from the Bear Runs to make your Bear Claws. Score points for making Full Bear Claws with your Bear friends. Through strategy and a little luck you will be ready when the Bear Paw appears.  But be sure to empty your hand of high points or else lose the points you gained.  Gather the most points and consider yourself the bear-ist of friends. Earn 3 Honey Jars so you and your friends can be proclaimed the Bear-tastic buddy.

A 88 card game for 2-4 players with a single deck. Can expand to 8 players with two decks. 
The goal is to have the highest points in front of you and the lowest in your hand. These only a set of three in front of you counts for points but all cards in your hand will be negative. A pair of the same card in front of you is worth zero. Once one player has 3 rows of three or more in front of them the round ends. Highest score gets the Honey jar. 3 jars and you and your bear friends win.

—description from the publisher

In Evoke, players are members of different warring guild factions and were thrown into a dungeon for illegal use of magic. Someone cast a spell which erased everyone's memories and now players can't remember which guild everyone is a member of!

Use your time and resources wisely to try to learn about the other players and yourself... and escape with who you assume are your fellow guild members.

Players will need to figure out who to trust and when to provide correct (or false) information to influence the outcome of the game. Each player starts the game with 3 memory cards which contributes to the loyalty of a faction. Over time, more information will be revealed, but trust will need to be placed without perfect information, since there is a small window of time for escape. Players who are chained up at the end of the last round will not contribute to their guild's win condition.

—description from the designer

Roll the six dice to discover the placement of the six column pieces, then race your opponent to complete the grid first. Whoever wins, then moves to the next (harder) level. Whoever completed the square when playing the 'wizard level' is crowned the winner.

boardgame: The War of the Rohirrim: Battle of Edoras
description:
The Battle of Edoras brings the Hill Tribes into open conflict with the proud warriors of Rohan as the fractious and dishevelled tribesmen mount a dedicated campaign. A once noble Lord, now an exile of Rohan, Wulf rallies the Hill Tribes to his banner, but the princes Haleth and Háma, sons of the noble and mighty King Helm Hammerhand, lead the Rohirrim in defence of their home and holdings.

The War of the Rohirrim – Battle of Edoras, is the ideal way to begin, or continue, your adventures in Middle-earth. Take command of the mighty warriors of Rohan, or the bitter and aggressive tribesmen of the hills, both led by characterful heroes, and reenact your favourite scenes from The Lord of the Rings: The War of the Rohirrim. This box contains 56 miniatures split into two balanced armies – one Good, one Evil – and everything you need to play, including a rules manual, dice, and accessories.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Paper Pinball is a dice print and play game that tries to capture the feeling of playing pinball. All you will need is a pencil, three dice, and about five minutes.

Each round, roll two dice and fill in one of the scoring zones. Some zones need to be filled in a certain way, and most will cause some sort of effect, so the order you fill them in is important. If you ever can't fill a zone, you'll lose a ball. Lose three balls and the game is over. Try to fill as many scoring zones as possible while triggering multipliers, opening tunnels, and getting the coveted multiball.

Paper Pinball is a dice print and play game that tries to capture the feeling of playing pinball. All you will need is a pencil, three dice, and about five minutes.

Each round, roll two dice and fill in one of the scoring zones. Some zones need to be filled in a certain way, and most will cause some sort of effect, so the order you fill them in is important. If you ever can't fill a zone, you'll lose a ball. Lose three balls and the game is over. Try to fill as many scoring zones as possible while triggering multipliers, opening tunnels, and getting the coveted multiball.
