Each round, roll two dice and fill in one of the scoring zones. Some zones need to be filled in a certain way, and most will cause some sort of effect, so the order you fill them in is important. If you ever can't fill a zone, you'll lose a ball. Lose three balls and the game is over. Try to fill as many scoring zones as possible while triggering multipliers, opening tunnels, and getting the coveted multiball.

boardgame: Paper Pinball: Season 2 – Escape the Vent
description:
Paper Pinball is a dice print and play game that tries to capture the feeling of playing pinball. All you will need is a pencil, three dice, and about five minutes.

Each round, roll two dice and fill in one of the scoring zones. Some zones need to be filled in a certain way, and most will cause some sort of effect, so the order you fill them in is important. If you ever can't fill a zone, you'll lose a ball. Lose three balls and the game is over. Try to fill as many scoring zones as possible while triggering multipliers, opening tunnels, and getting the coveted multiball.

In this colourful trick-taking card game you have to avoid winning any points! It is based on Hearts, but is not played with regular cards:


     6 suits when played with maximum 6 players
     suits: red, black, green, blue, yellow, orange
     red suit (little devils) - 1 point each
     black suit - the 3 highest cards are points - Black Spider, Black Snake, Black Dragon
     green suit (mouse) - permanent trump suit
     4 Safety cards - can always be played to avoid winning a trick

Introduction:Esgros (from Scottish Gaelic: sgrios, meaning "annihilation") is a drawless annihilation game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 5 cells per side, but boards of 4 or 6 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones in their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is the maximal set of connected stones of the same color. A single stone is also a group.
     The size of a group is the number of stones it contains.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, perform the following actions in order:


     Place a stone on an empty cell so that the largest group grows only if it is the only one that can; otherwise, any other group may surpass it by the minimal possible size.
     Remove all smaller enemy groups adjacent to the group of the placed stone from the board.


				
				
					End of the game:You win if the last enemy group is eliminated from the board.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a black stone on an empty cell, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

				
				
					Notes:This game is inspired by Mark Steere's Oust, as an attempt to make a similar game but with only one stone per turn, in addition to making better use of the space.

—description from the designer

It's a race against time and each other as players deal and steal cards to collect the highest value White Elephant gifts by the end of the game. You never know what crazy gifts you'll get in everyone's favorite wacky, tacky, gift-giving card game! Players have two minutes each round to quickly pass, draw, and discard cards in a mad dash to collect the highest value gifts! Once all players have had a turn as the reader, the game is over. Players reveal their hands and add up their individual points. The player with the most points at the end of the game is the winner. Win it all, or walk away with the booby prize!

—description from the publisher

A boardgame with four mini games. Set in a thief school, the goal is to gain the most coins by winning mini games.

In the first mini game each player picks a random token. The players then roll a die and pass the tokens around according to set rules. After three rounds, the player with the highest valued token wins.

In the second mini game the players roll a die and move on a board competing to be the first to reach the finish.

The third mini game is building a maze using shuffled tiles. The player to use the least number of tiles to build a path from start to finish wins.

The fourth mini game is a memory game where the players have to memorize the position of eight code tiles and point them out in a specific order. The player to remember the most tiles wins.

The quintessential clue game!
Challenge others to be the first to discover who is hiding behind the IDENTIKIT. But be careful: the score decreases after each clue revealed!
A fun deduction game to discover 100 mysterious characters, including famous women and men, sports champions and superheroes.

—description from the publisher (translated)

O jogo de pistas por excelência!
Desafia os outros a serem os primeiros a descobrir quem se esconde atrás do IDENTIKIT. Mas atenção: a pontuação diminui após cada pista revelada!
Um jogo divertido, de dedução, para descobrir 100 personagens misteriosas, incluindo mulheres e homens famosos, campeões desportivos e super-heróis.

—description from the publisher

Laiva on lastattu takes your child on a journey into the world of letters and words. The game involves spinning a wheel and searching for words or pictures that begin with the letter indicated on the wheel. The hourglass shows the time available. The words are loaded on board the ship. The first player to fill his ship wins the game. The game develops the child's reading skills in a varied and gradual way. The child learns to recognise the initial sound and letter of a word, progressing to reading whole words. The joy of discovery and fun games for a long time!

Halla, the promised land of the Church of Transcendence. Or so they said. Hollow promises, and a hollow world. Nothing here but death and despair. And Her. Do not follow Her.

This is a new Campaign Expansion, introducing a new narrative campaign, new Intruders, new Boss, new Spacers and Suits, new Gear, new Locations and much much more. All content from the Expansion can also be used in modular custom campaigns.

—description from the publisher

Trick King is a trick-taking game. A trick-taking game with a skateboarding theme! Players become skateboarders, and the player who strings together the best tricks and gets the most points wins! This is a trick-taking game with four suit colors and must-follow numbers from 5 to 13. A special feature of Trick King is that when you play a card, you can turn an unnecessary card face down and add it from your hand to increase the number.
By turning a card face down, you can increase the number by "+1". (You can make a 10 card "11" by adding one face-down card! You can turn any number of cards face down to increase the number! Even if another player leads the highest number, 13, you can push your own card! By adding a card to your hand, you can increase the number you played from your hand! (However, there is a disadvantage in that you will not be able to participate in the final trick!)
The "Grab" card, one of the skateboarder's tricks, allows you to "recover a random card from the discard pile" if you lose with a grab card (10, 11)! You can also get a new card or increase the number of cards in your hand by losing!

A trick-taking game with some very tricky psychological warfare! Will you be able to become the Trick King?

In this edition of the revolutionary quiz game, there are 200 questions and 2000 possible answers about Luxembourg.

However, the principle of the game remains unchanged: you have to guess the correct answer from 10 possible answers. The many different topics and categories with questions offer a lot of variety and excitement.

IntroductionLidalan (from Old Norse: lið land, meaning "territory of group") is a territorial game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 7 cells per side, but boards of 5 or 9 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a maximal connected set of stones of the same color. A single stone is also a group.
     The size of a group is the number of stones it contains.
     A territory is a maximal connected set of empty cells. You own a territory if the largest group adjacent to it is of your color, and in case of a tie, the next largest groups are compared; if all are the same size, the territory scores for neither player.
     A live group is a group that either has at least one adjacent empty cell that is part of the friendly territory, or, if it does not have adjacent empty cells, is equal to or larger in size than the adjacent enemy group; otherwise, it is a dead group.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty cell not adjacent to friendly groups, unless the group of the placed stone is larger by one than the size of any adjacent enemy group. If you cannot place a stone, you pass your turn; otherwise, passing is not allowed.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when neither player can place stones. The player with the highest score wins, calculated as one point for each empty cell within friendly territories plus one point for each stone in dead enemy groups.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a black stone on an empty cell, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer

Each player takes the role of one of 9 available characters, each with distinct personality traits and a special ability. The game unfolds with the discovery of a crime that has taken place. Each player draws three random cards with sentences they need to use in a made-up story, showing their alibi to everyone else. Suspicions arise, questions are asked between the players, and one of them - whose story is the least convincing one - gets convicted. That player draws a card from the "convictions" pile and then has to perform a certain bizarre activity on the next round(s).

Това е игра, в която има разкрития, а може би дори престъпления. Изходът от нея ще зависи от това дали имате добро въображение и дали умеете да разказвате интересни и убедителни истории. Играта е за минимум трима души, но ще е по-интересна, ако участниците са петима или шестима. Максималният брой участници е девет, колкото са и героите.

After an important investigation was closed, the decisive evidence disappeared. A brown briefcase contains T.H.E. Plan and four agents are suspect. As a secret agent, your mission is to unmask the real thief, find the briefcase and get a key to the office where it is hidden. Move quickly around the city, take the tram for advantage, gather information, and avoid the beam of the searchlight!

—description from the publisher

PlayPension is a simulation game that will help young people to understand the importance of long-term savings, teach them how to manage small income and expense budgets and introduce them to simple tools so that they can learn to deal with unforeseen events in the future.
The aim of PlayPension is to help young people understand the concept of savings, but also to promote the importance of teamwork since decision-making is done by consensus. The ease of access, together with its simple operation, make it an effective educational tool to play in class.
This game is aimed at young people aged 16 and over.
The winning team will be the one that manages to reach the indicated savings goal.

Sgibun (from Scottish Gaelic: sgìre buidhne, meaning "territory of group") is a strategy game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 7 cells per side, but boards with 5 or 9 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a maximal set of connected stones of the same color. A single stone is also a group.
     The size of a group is the number of stones it contains.
     A territory is a maximal set of connected empty cells. You own a territory if the largest adjacent group belongs to you, and in case of a tie, the next largest groups are compared; if all are the same size, the territory does not score for either player.
     The value of a territory is the number of empty cells it contains.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty cell not adjacent to groups of any color, unless the smallest adjacent enemy group is exactly the same size or exactly one stone smaller than the group of the placed stone.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when no player can place stones, and the player with the territories of the highest value wins. To prevent ties, half a point is awarded to the player with the largest group. In case of a tie in size, the next largest groups are considered.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a black stone on an empty cell, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Enormity: The Glorious Empire of the Thousand Suns Campaign Expansion
description:
They come to convert you in the name of their God-Emperor. Failing that, their holy flamethrowers are ready. The Shepard will be cleansed either way.

This is a new Campaign Expansion, introducing a new narrative campaign, new Intruders, new Boss, new Spacers and Suits, new Gear, new Locations and much much more. All content from the Expansioncan also be used in modular custom campaigns.

—description from the publisher

"Gotcha! Tried to rob me, ugly little monster? Now I will rob you! Let's take a look into this trashy backpack of yours… Oh? Where did you get those boots from, some bloody lord? Good leather… sturdy but soft as hell… Should fit just right! Here goes the left, and here goes… another left. My goddess, you Rotlings are so damn stupid you reach cleverness from the other side." — Borius Chennay, famous bounty hunter

The Looter Rotlings are mischievous creatures, tainted by the Dimrot. After the Breaking of the Sun, they started to emerge from area known as Rotlands. They  prowl the civilized realms, pilfering all sorts of items. No one knows what they need them for, but they seem to serve the Overmind of the Rotlands.

Drawn to chaos and eager to use it as a cover-up for their thievery, Rotlings sometimes appear on the outskirts of battles. That’s why, in terms of gameplay, the Looter Rotling will be a small optional objective that can be introduced into any Combat.

Upon meeting certain conditions, players will be able to access its loot and finish the Combat with bonus rewards. Of course, this won't be easy — Rotlings are elusive, and their thieving nature may lead to the mysterious disappearance of some of the Party's assets. Additionally, the chance to catch a Rotling will be time-limited, and it will have few different setup and activation variants, bringing more tactical challenge and diversity into your encounters.

—description from the publisher

Dragon School Heroes is a simple competitive roll-and-move game where the goal is to reach the Dragon's Cave and defeat him first by dealing 3 damage.

In this game you become one of the five heroes with some speacial abilities.
Roll dice and move across the game board. Each time you'll be landing on Event tiles. If the tile is closed, open it and resolve its effect. If the tile is open, just perform its effect. Effects make you move further, move back or take Treasure cards.

Once you reach the Dragon Cave, you'll be rolling dice to see whether you deal damage to the Dragon or not. The Dragon will be striking back making you move backwards on the board. Your special abilities and Treasure cards will help you deal more damage.

The player who deals the 3rd and final damage to the Dragon wins the game.

boardgame: Finders Seekers: Route 66 – Rags to Riches
description: Explore Route 66 in search of multiple stolen high performance muscle cars in this at-home escape room.

Awaken the trickster in you! Introducing another 18+ game from Black Mamba, this time offering a fresh twist on the classic game of Blackjack (21) like you’ve never seen before. Build your own deck throughout the match with the most unexpected cards inspired by pop culture and other famous games, giving you a wide variety of effects to help you “outsmart” your opponents and break the casino’s bank!

Manipulate your results without going over 21 in this thrilling deck-building game for 2 to 4 players, where cheating is allowed—as long as you don’t get caught, of course...

Sadhana: The Path, is a climbing card game designed for exactly three players, where they take on the roles of Lamas (Tibetan monks) seeking balance on their spiritual journey toward enlightenment. Players perform Sadhana (meditative practices) using Yidam (guiding deity) cards throughout their many reincarnations, striving to reach and tame the elephant meeple—an allegory for "The Great Mind"—on a path formed by cards.

Sadhana is a race for wisdom, where the winner is not the one who reaches the end first (transcending to the state of Buddha), but rather the one who successfully tames their mind by maintaining balance on "The Path" for the benefit of all beings.

boardgame: Гарри Поттер: Кубок факультетов (Harry Potter: House Cup)
description:
Гарри Поттер: Кубок факультетов ("Harry Potter: House Cup") is a family set-collection game with the goal to score the most victory points after 3 or 4 rounds (depends on the number of players).

Each round certain number of cards are revealed from the main deck and are distributed among 5 piles (days of the week). After that players bid for the piles using closed bids system. This process repeats 4 times each round and then all bids are revealed. For example, there are 5 cards in a pile (on Monday) and the opened bids are the follows: 1 for the red, 3 for the green and 2 for the yellow player. The red will be the first to take only 1 card from the pile, the yellow will be the second to take 2 cards and the green will be the last (he will take only 2 remaining cards). If the bids are tied than the sequence of placing the bids matters (the first player who placed the bid wins).
Cards obtained during the bid phase have different effects but mostly they grant victory points for being collected in sets.

The art and setting of the game are inspired by Harry Potter universe but all images are unique (not taken from movies).
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Oganika Arcanum: Diamonds and Duals is a standalone but complimentary set to Oganika Arcanum: Hex16, providing a set of thematically linked puzzles – the 36 Diamonds – contextualised within the fantasy culture of Or, a slowly fading Empire located on the continent of Embra, on the world of Imbril. 
Aspects of the Empire of Or, its culture and history, are explored and detailed in 4 accompanying booklets included in the set, and an additional game mode Dual, along with a dualling board, counters, timers, and instructions, is also included.
As with Hex16, Diamonds and Duals is not just puzzles and games but an open invitation for GM’s and RPGers to develop scenarios, adventures and campaigns within the landscape of Or.

—description from the publisher

Forfort is a little card game of 124 cards (31 cards per player) to build fortifications, attack your opponent and win!  Players play tug-of-war, destroying their opponent forts while building their own.

Your deck (donjon) is in the center of your fort. Use a card to build on one of 8 placements or discard (with symbol) to apply its effect (catapult, army, drawbridge, etc.). You have an extra bonus if you associate two symbols one next to the other in your fort.

You win if you finish building your fort first or you build the most fortifications.

GATHER NEWS FOR THE SOCIETY PAPER: You are the eyes and ears of Lady Whistledown as you uncover who is embroiled in the latest scandal of the heart, how they were caught, and where their tryst occurred.
EXCLUSIVE CHARACTER MOVERS: Choose a favorite Bridgerton character: Daphne Bridgerton, Simon Basset, Queen Charlotte, Eloise Bridgerton, Anthony Bridgerton, or Benedict Bridgerton.
REVEAL SECRETS WITH SCANDAL CARDS: Roll doubles or the magnifying glass icon on the die and draw a Scandal card. You'll get clues faster, but you may be asked to divulge your own secrets or deceive others.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

description:
Get ready for an adrenaline-pumping paragliding adventure! This unique board game will take you to the mountains, on the ground and into the air. You will become a brave pilot-racer, who will try to reach the destination ASAP, flying or hiking.

You will follow the routes given by placing the start, finish and turn points anywhere on the map that is put together in many different ways. You can take advantage of the speed of a paraglider in the air or safe movement on the ground.

The task will be hard, because the weather in the mountains can change unexpectedly. Various weather changes on the route will make you flexibly adjust your tactics.

You will learn to calculate the height needed to fly over the mountains and use your energy efficiently when hiking on the ground. Moreover, you have to learn how to use the portion of luck, that you have been given at the beginning of the day, at the right moment.

—description from the designer

Do you think you and your friends have no secrets from each other? You don't have to wonder about that anymore, now you can find out! Psychotest, a social party game in which you reveal all your cards to each other. What do you think of yourself from years ago? How do you feel about .... the abandoned cat? What will you do in an extreme situation? Look at each other carefully and be alert, because this is a high-risk game.  Place your bets on your answers and see if honesty will win!

Players collaborate to protect Mia by helping the cats build towers to defend against monster attacks.

How to Play:
Phase 1: Build the Tower
At the beginning of their turn, the player selects 1 Roof Card from 2 available choices in the Roof Card deck. This ensures the player always has 2 Roof Cards in hand. Refill the selection if the taken card is not from the deck.

Phase 2: Attack the Monsters
Each player must reveal 1 Attack Card from the Attack Card deck and activate its effect.

Phase 3: Defend Against the Attack
Monsters advance according to their movement steps. If there is more than one monster on the path, they move one at a time in any order.

End of the Game:
The game ends with all players losing if all Life Cards are depleted.
All players lose if the Roof Cards run out and there are still monsters alive on the path/nest.
All players win if all Monster Tokens are depleted, meaning all monsters have been defeated or there are no more monsters on the path/nest before Roof Cards run out.
After winning, each player counts and compares the total value of the defeated monsters (gem icons on Monster Tokens). The player with the highest total points is the winner.

—description from the designer

Tirnabun (from Scottish Gaelic: tìr na buidhne, meaning "territory of group") is a strategy game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 7 cells per side, but boards with 5 or 9 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a maximal set of connected stones of the same color. A single stone is also a group.
     The size of a group is the number of stones it contains.
     A territory is a maximal set of connected empty cells. You own a territory if the largest adjacent group belongs to you, and in case of a tie, the next largest groups are compared; if all are the same size, the territory does not score for either player.
     A live group is a group that either has at least one adjacent empty cell that is part of the friendly territory, or, if it does not have adjacent empty cells, is equal to or larger in size than the adjacent enemy group; otherwise, it is a dead group.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty cell not adjacent to groups of any color, unless the largest adjacent enemy group is exactly the same size or exactly one stone smaller than the group of the placed stone.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when neither player can place stones. The player with the highest score wins, calculated as one point for each empty cell within friendly territories plus one point for each stone in dead enemy groups.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a black stone on an empty cell, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer
An axolotl card game. Call out "eatz a lotl" when 2 piles match to take the pile. Whoever gets all the cards wins.

Introduction:Licled (from Old Norse: lykkja leið, meaning "loop path") is a loop-stalemate game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended board size is 7 cells per side, but boards of 5, 6, 8 or 9 are also valid. Each player has a sufficient supply of identical stones, each with one color on the front and the other on the back.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a maximal set of stones of any color connected to each other. A single stone is also a group.
     A loop is a closed path of stones of any color, with no forks and one stone wide along its entire length.
     You control a loop if more than half of its stones are your color. If a loop has an equal number of stones of each color, the active player controls it.
     A claimed loop is a loop of a single color.
     To flip is to replace enemy stones with friendly stones, and is optional only if it involves doing so with stones from claimed loops.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform these actions in the following order:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty cell.
     Flip all enemy stones contained in any controlled loop formed by this placement, until they all match your color, provided that stones from claimed loops only do so if the resulting loop is longer than all those previously claimed loops.
     Remove all friendly stones adjacent to the new loop, except those that are part of claimed loops.


At the end of your turn, there must be no stones adjacent to friendly loops.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when a player cannot place a stone; in that case, that player is the winner.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a black stone on an empty point, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer

Push your knowledge to the limits in this speedy game of complete answers! Pick a category card - anything from "Zodiac signs" to "Members of One Direction". Write down as many answers as you can, then race to grab one of the bonus cards.

Reveal the answers - players score a point for each correct one. But if anyone lists every possible answer, they score maximum points and are The Completist!

Quick to learn and fun to play, this game is perfect for all players who think they know it all!
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

description:
An educational family game. Players take turns rolling the dice and moving across the board-map of Bulgaria. There are 4 different types of spots: Geography, History, Feats or Culture. Each type corresponds to a deck of that type. Once a player lands on any field, the rest of the players draw a card for him and read the question written on the back side of the card out loud (answers present on the card). If the player answers correctly, they get the outlined number of point. If they fail, they lose that number. The 4th category type (culture), asks the player to either sing or recite a certain song/poem. An additional leaflet with song/poem texts is provided within the box.

Нова Образователна настолна игра "Опознай България"
Разработена по Метода за учене чрез игра,
ти помага да научиш 100 факта за историята, географията и великите постижения на България, докато се забавляваш с твоите близки.

boardgame: Enormity: The Blasphemous Species Campaign Expansion
description:
La! La! Rise darkness against the tyrany of light! La! La! Down with the false God-Emperor! La! La! Let the reign of the black gods of the stars begin anew!

The Goatmen are known to make bloody sacrifices to their dark gods, in exchange for arcane wisdom and unholy powers. One if these powers, one you suspect is tied to some sort of DNA manipulation, is the ability to brew strange concoctions, some of which can turn willingsentient beings into hulking monsters, like the Wendigo.

It’s hard to pinpoint where in the Goatmen’s hierarchy the Wendigo sits. Undoubtedly, this boss Intruder, wielding an ancestral shub cannon, is used as a siege engine, one capable of scuttling spaceships – a feat made easier by the fact that the Wendigo can survive in the void of space as readily as in the Shepherd’s atmosphere.

This is a new Campaign Expansion, introducing a new narrative campaign, new Intruders, new Boss, new Spacers and Suits, new Gear, new Locations and much much more. All content from the Expansion can also be used in modular custom campaigns.

—description from the publisher

This Chop Bot box adds 12 scrappy, personality-filled bots forged from grit and scrap and sometimes sass, ready to shake up your Vestige strategies (or help you come up with one in the first place). Each of these bots will be presented in gorgeous holofoil. It also features a small neoprene Chop Bot market mat to get those cards off the table and onto something a little more stylish.

—description from the publisher

Infamous, elusive, and endlessly toying with you, the Scarlet Sage is always two steps ahead of you. Or right behind you. Or, it seems, nowhere at all. Confront her with caution.

Nemesis packs introduce non-player opponents into Vestige. They can be used in competitive, multiplayer, and solo modes.

These packs are quite different than scrap packs. Each nemesis pack introduces a non-player opponent into the game who then competes for victory with minimal upkeep. A pack contains their nemesis card (a sort of main, personality card that features the ongoing effect they have on the game world as well as the unique win condition they are striving toward) and 11 other cards that enable that character to do whatever it is they do. Typically these other cards go into the little stacks that go off when players reach trigger spaces on the scoring track, but they can do other things as well.

—description from the publisher

Encrypted is a word game where you draw cards containing encrypted words that you try to solve as fast as you can.

The game comes with 400 cards in three different categories: Dirty (18+), Party and Culture. 
The game can be played as individuals or in teams.
There is a timer that can be used to put pressure on the players to solve as many words they can before the timer is up.

Read the card aloud. Everyone else will see the meaning except yourself. Can you understand the hidden meaning before the time runs out?

Try saying "Armour nid he yot" out loud. Do you hear the encrypted message? If you're feeling a little silly, that's only natural, because you just said "I'm an idiot".

Split into teams or play one-on-one. Decrypt as many cards as possible before time runs out, enjoying plenty of fun and laughter along the way!

Racing Game board game for 2-4 players. Simple and funny game that introduces the colors and teaches the rules of the game. Includes a detachable board, 4 wooden car-shaped pieces and 1 colored die. Roll the dice to see the color and advance with your car to the next square of the same color... Who will get home first?

—description from the publisher

Pleas and protests mean nothing to Bone. He wants what he wants and takes it by force. And what he wants - is control.

Nemesis packs introduce non-player opponents into Vestige. They can be used in competetive, multiplayer, and solo modes.

These packs are quite different than scrap packs. Each nemesis pack introduces a non-player opponent into the game who then competes for victory with minimal upkeep. A pack contains their nemesis card (a sort of main, personality card that features the ongoing effect they have on the game world as well as the unique win condition they are striving toward) and 11 other cards that enable that character to do whatever it is they do. Typically these other cards go into the little stacks that go off when players reach trigger spaces on the scoring track, but they can do other things as well.

Nemesis play can be introduced in competitive games, and is at the heart of cooperative and solo play. If you are getting this game for coop or solo, you probably want all the Nemesis characters.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Lands of Evershade: Lost in the Depths
description:
Descend to the long-lost realm of the Yawning Depths and discover story that started before the Breaking of the Sun.

Since the first Alfs emerged from the Yawning Deths, the way back to this dark world has been hidden. Now, a small expedition can finally brave this forgotten frontier. Looking for a new home for the surface races, they instead discover facts about the Breaking of the Sun that can shake the world…

This is an Advanced level Adventure 10-15 hours long that contains 3 Chapters (3-5 hours each).

—description from the publisher

Journey as ambassadors to mysterious Rotlands - the place of congregation for all infected with Dimrot, who now live governed by mysterious consciousness. The Dimrot hates sunlight more than anything, but is the enemy of your enemy always a friend?

Rotlands Crusade is  our second Add-on Adventure, this time aimed at experienced Characters and full of difficult challenges. It also introduces a new enemy: the dangerous Puffshroom Colossus who can severely punish careless attackers.

This is an Expert level Adventure that contains 3 Chapters (3-5 hours each), for a total playthrough time of 10-15 hours.

—description from the publisher

Who Said That? is a game that challenges your knowledge and wit, turning classic quotes into moments of laughter and friendly competition.

The object of the game is to have fun while creating the funniest ending to a quote. Each player takes a turn as Judge. The Judge tries to determine the correct ending to the quote; which is the funniest attempt; and who originally uttered the quote. Quotes are not just from comedians, anyone who has said something funny, or profound, is fair game in this game.

Play the classic Loteria game in a whole new way! Players race to build a grid of cards by playing cards with matching colors of higher numbers. But watch out! Special action cards can let other players steal cards or change the board layout. The player to play the last card in the grid wins the round and the win card. First player to win 4 cards wins the game!

Players try to match colors and numbers as in the classic game, but with a unique Bullseye rule that directs a player to decide who gets hit with an action card. The smaller package and cards make it easy to take the game anywhere for fun on the go. And when down to the last card, don't forget to yell "UNO!"

Draft seven cards from a random deal, then compete to earn status points. Play two cards: same 'family' and the cards add. The winner loses the highest card played, all other chose which card to discard.

5 rounds. Family cards range from 1 - 11. (Four families). Status cards from 14 to -6, so swings can be large.

boardgame: Bonnie and Clyde, A Roadmap Through History
description:
Following Bonnie and Clyde: Love and Death and its postal game prologue Love at First Shot, this expansion introduces a new layer of depth to the adventures of the infamous duo.

Using historically inspired maps specifically adapted and scripted for the game, players can now immerse themselves in the geographical reality of Bonnie and Clyde’s journey across the southern United States. This expansion offers a fresh perspective, bringing their legendary story closer to its historical context.

Gameplay

The expansion replaces the route and town cards from the original game with a detailed map where all locations are pre-defined. Players plan their choices in advance, charting their path as they explore roads, rob banks, steal cars, and evade the authorities. The map serves as the foundation for your decisions, guiding your actions and shaping your escape.

This expansion adds a strategic dimension to the game, encouraging thoughtful planning and a deeper connection to the setting.

—description from the publisher

A twisted survival card game for adults.

On the face of it, this game is about telling a story: 
You and those around your table, coming together in an attempt to avert, or at least survive, disaster. 
And that's what it is... mostly...

The aim of the game is very simple – make it through the disaster alive. You will play as a character who has their own motivations and objectives, both prior to and in the aftermath of the disaster. Use their motivations to guide your decisions as you narrate your turn before you then turn a playing card which will affect the direction of the story, and pass play to the next player to dictate how the twist affected the last character.

All you need is the rulebook, a set of standard playing cards, and optionally you can add the Chaos Gauge and Disaster Tour Guide hat.

—description from the publisher

Following the successful Musket & Pike Dual Pack reprint of This Accursed Civil War and Sweden Fights On, we are excited to offer Musket & Pike Dual Pack 2 on P500 that includes the reprinted and updated Under the Lily Banners and Gustav Adolf the Great. As with the first Dual Pack, it has eleven battles in one box! The award-winning Musket & Pike Battle Series was launched in 2002, and Volumes III and IV have been out of print for years. In that time, the series had two more installments, and the Dual Pack reprint of Volumes I and II have attracted many new players. In this box, you will get the most popular volumes of the MPBS including:


    An additional battle for Under the Lily Banners, Wittenweier 1638
    For all battles, counters will not be shared between battles for ease of setup and storage
    The battle specific cards introduced in Saints in Armor with be provided with Turn Track, Victory Conditions, Dead and Pursuit boxes to improve their utility (5 backprinted cards)
    The counters will feature formation icons as seen in the first Dual Pack and requested by so many players over the years!


In the award-winning Musket & Pike Battle Series, armies are divided into several wings. Each has a commander and an order that limits the types of actions that units of that wing may perform. Victory goes to the player who can coordinate the actions of their wings in the heat of battle. Units are infantry battalions or brigades, cavalry regiments, and artillery batteries. Rules include cavalry charges, cavalry pistols for skirmishing or close combat, Polish lances, musket salvo fire, and grazing artillery fire. Play is highly interactive, and most battles can be played through in one sitting.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: D.E.I.: Divide et Impera – Talent Scout Promo Mission Card
description:
A promo mission card available in the "Ashes Special 6 – Essen 2022" magazine from Ludus Magnus Studio.

Description from the official page: 
"– 1x Mission Card (to play the new Mission “Talent Scout” in D.E.I. Divide et Impera)"

This mission gives supply based on the amount of unlocked feats tokens.

In the enchanting world of Wondrous, immerse yourself in a captivating battle for the very heart of the spiritual realm. Wondrous offers a unique and enthralling gameplay experience where one player takes on the role of the courageous underdog Mage, fighting to preserve the balance of magic and protect the realm's ethereal beauty. Meanwhile, the other player assumes the mantle of the formidable Mechanic, hungry for power and determined to seize control of all magic. With the fate of an entire realm at stake, Wondrous unfolds in a duel of wits and strategy, where every card played and every move made reveals the intricate dance between light and shadow. Will the Mage make their journey or will the Mechanic cover the land in oil?!

—description from the designer

On the set of her latest movie, Hollywood sweetheart Ruby Bloom is found dead. As an LAPD detective, it’s your job to uncover what happened … and you suspect murder. The starlet certainly had a reputation, and there’s no shortage of suspects, but who hated her enough to steal her final breath? Set in the Golden Age of Hollywood, this tabletop murder mystery game invites you to investigate the crime scene, question suspects and link evidence to form your conclusions about the murder. Can you see past the glitz and glamour to collate the facts, accuse the correct killer, and bring them to justice? Be warned, Detective, the case won’t be easy—after all, in Tinseltown, a lie isn’t a lie … it’s just good acting.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Natural Killer: El Cuerpo Bajo Ataque
description:
Natural Killer: is a hidden-role card game that thrusts you into the intense conflict between the immune system and infectious viruses. Each player starts as a defensive cell (such as T or B lymphocytes, Natural Killers, etc.), but a dangerous Virion can infiltrate and convert anyone into an agent of the Virus.

Throughout the game, players draw and play cards representing immune system actions—revealing symptoms, exchanging hands, or even stealing cards from others. Symptom Cards are vital for identifying which virus has invaded: as more symptoms are placed in each player’s “Antigen Zone,” it becomes clearer which Virus is at work. However, infected players can bluff or manipulate card exchanges to secretly spread the disease.

The Immune System seeks to deduce the correct Virus and use the specific Treatment to neutralize it, ensuring no one remains infected. Conversely, the Virus aims to infect all or leave only a few immune players while covertly passing the Virion card. Special cards like Interferon can cure an infected player, but there are restrictions that add to the game’s tension and strategic depth.

With shifting roles, deduction through Symptom analysis, and the ever-present threat of infection, Natural Killer delivers a blend of strategy, negotiation, and bluffing. Every choice matters—one misstep could doom the entire body, while careful play can save it from total collapse.

—description from the publisher

In an age where everyone strives to be extraordinary, we have forgotten how good it is to just be, well.... average. Enter our new game Average Joe, a riveting card game which is all about “playing it safe”, where being too right or too wrong can cost you the game... Combining the fascinating world of statistics with strategic guesswork, Average Joe is not about standing out, instead players must strike the perfect balance and stay in the middle ground to secure victory!

—description from the publisher

Give your creative brain a spin with the fast-paced drawing game that turns as you doodle! Roll the die to determine your category – and the speed of the spinning board – then draw a card to see what you’ll doodle. The clues may seem simple, but once the canvas starts to spin, you’ll be laughing out loud as your teammates guess your dizzying doodles for points.

Creating can be fun even if you have no actual artistic skill — gather your friends for a TEAR-A-PARTy and you’ll see!

Give free rein to your fantasy: rip, crumple, fold and twist a small piece of paper to make the other players guess your secret concept. Most probably you won’t become a renowned artist in the process, but you surely will have tons of fun (and maybe even TEARS of joy) while creating your artworks. Get started and TEAR it up!

Score as many points as possible by depicting your secret concepts using nothing but a small piece of paper and do your best to guess what your opponents were trying to show.

—description from the publisher

A set of three small expansions for the base game Outback Crossing. All bundled together in one pack.

The Koala: This is a new type of animal tile. The Koala generates points based on the number of other animals around it.

The Termites: This is a new type of arrow marker. The termites block a path and make it impossible to generate points in the direction of the arrow.

The Nationalpark: This is a new type of marker. Each player gets a plus-shaped marker in their respective color. Players can use the marker to secure one of their arrows, so other player can´t manipulate the arrows or force a player to move it.

—description from the publisher

Who can decorate their Christmas tree the fastest? Every child receives a Christmas tree card with illustrations of 5 colorful baubles and a topper. The children take turns tolling the colour die. Every colour on the die corresponds to a bauble with a matching colour, which can then be placed on the Christmas tree card. Whoever fully decorates their tree first is the winner.

The ultimate game for women's football fans! Create your own team with the world’s top players and compete to win Liga F.

You, along with a friend or up to five friends, will manage a women’s football team. Each manager receives a football game system and a budget to build their squad.

The game consists of two phases: the "Transfer Window" and the "Match Days." In the Transfer Window, managers buy players that best suit their team’s style of play. During the Match Days, teams compete in a league format with 4 to 6 teams.

After all the matches are completed, tally the points each team earned across the matchdays. The team with the highest score wins Liga F.

boardgame: What Do You Meme?: UK Edition
description:
Party games are really popular right now, especially those containing edgy content. What Do You Meme: UK Edition certainly falls into that category.

This UK Edition of What Do You Meme? is bigger, better and naughtier than the original game. It brings 435 new cards to the table, including:


     360 Caption Cards.
     75 Photo Cards.


The rules could not be any easier. A player selects a Photo Card and everybody else puts down (face-down) the Captions that they believe best suits the photograph. The Judge of that round then chooses their favourite Caption from those put down and points are awarded to the winner.

Next round. New Judge. New Picture. New Captions. Repeat this formula until you can’t laugh anymore

—description from the publisher

Mexican Train is a popular domino game played with a Double 18 set, offering a fun and engaging experience for players of all ages. The game can be enjoyed by 2 to 12 players, making it ideal for family gatherings, game nights, or casual get-togethers. Its name comes from the unique "train" feature, which allows players to build their personal trains of dominoes while contributing to the central "Mexican Train."

The goal of the game is to be the first player to play all your dominoes or to have the lowest score when the game ends. Players take turns matching dominoes end-to-end, following numerical sequences. Strategic moves and the use of special markers add an exciting twist, as players can block opponents or extend their own trains.

Mexican Train is easy to learn but offers plenty of depth, making it appealing to both beginners and experienced players. With the Double 18 set, the game introduces more tiles and longer rounds, providing an extended and more challenging version of this classic favorite.

—description from the publisher

A head-to-head battle for the Sovereignty of Solara!!!

The Duel Expansion brings replacement cards for 12 already known characters with actions adjusted to be better played with 2 Players. Plus alternative arts for all cards.

Who will you face in this 1x1?
family: Versions & Editions: Two-Player Versions of More-Player Games

description:
Players draw cards until somebody draws the Exploding Kitten at which point they explode and lose the game.
Unless they can defuse the kitten with things like a laser or belly rubs.
Use action cards to skip your turn, secretly peek at a card before drawing, or force the other player to draw instead of you. Play cards, draw cards, try not to explode.
Includes 20 Battle Brackets!
Face off with up to 8 friends in a tournament mode!

—description from the back of the box
family: Versions & Editions: Two-Player Versions of More-Player Games

description:
RollDown is a 1 on 1 strategic dice/card game. players pose as gods acting out holy wars from a heavily abstracted point of view, all just to score points against one another. Each player commands six martyrs represented by dice of ascending sizes. players also receive three blessing cards that they can use for powerful effects that can directly change dice values or reroll multiple dice at once.

Capturing an opponent’s die requires you to sacrifice one of your own. you and your opponent will always have an equal number of dice in play. The player who makes the final capture takes all the glory, winning the round.

Use cards and rerolls to strengthen your position or to weaken your opponents. Try to set up the best trades possible so that the final odds are skewed in your favor.

This game is fully playable with just a set of playing cards and Two sets of polyhedral RPG dice. It allows for several aspects of casino advantage play such as card counting, bet manipulation, luck pushing, and bluffing.

—description from the designer

At the Notre-Dame-of-Gossip high school, rumors are flying: a student allegedly cheated on an exam. The goal of the game is to spread rumors among the other players in order to unmask the guilty student (character), the exam on which he cheated (subject) and how he succeeded (cheating). All this, without taking notes! Fortunately, players are given special talents at the beginning of the game to facilitate and spice up the investigation.

This game promotes:

     Memory
     Deductive thinking
     Concentration


—description from the publisher (translated)


                French****
            
        
    


À la polyvalente Notre-Dame-du-Commérage, les rumeurs vont bon train : un élève aurait triché à un examen. Le but du jeu est de lancer des rumeurs auprès des autres joueurs afin de démasquer l’élève coupable (personnage), de l’examen pour lequel il a triché (matière) et de la façon dont il y est parvenu (tricherie). Tout ça, sans prendre de notes ! Heureusement, les joueurs se voient attribuer en début de partie des talents spéciaux afin de faciliter et de pimenter l’enquête.

Ce jeu favorise :

     La mémoire
     L’esprit de déduction 
     La concentration


—description from the publisher

Toby Malito is a family-friendly board game released exclusively in Spain in 2013, resembling the classic game Operation. The game centers around Toby, an electronic dog who needs medical attention. Players take on the role of veterinarians tasked with removing various ailments from Toby using five included medical instruments. The game combines skill and humor, offering an engaging experience for children and adults alike.

The gameplay involves carefully extracting objects representing Toby's ailments without triggering the sensitive areas of his electronic body. If a player touches the wrong spot, Toby reacts with sound effects like "Ouch!" and "Woof Woof!" This adds a layer of tension and excitement, as players must steady their hands and focus to avoid making Toby bark in protest.

The goal of the game is to successfully remove all the ailments without causing Toby too much discomfort. Players take turns performing these delicate operations, and the one who completes the most successful extractions wins. The game emphasizes precision, patience, and hand-eye coordination, making it both fun and educational.

The addition of realistic sound effects enhances the immersive experience, creating a sense of empathy for the adorable electronic dog. While the game requires batteries to power its sound and interactive elements, the exact type and number are not specified. Toby Malito has remained a nostalgic favorite in Spain, combining traditional board game mechanics with a playful twist.

Evokers is a 2 player card in which Evokers duel through contracting and commanding demons.
Simply defeat your opponent’s demons before they yours and victory shall be yours.
Every single one of the 120 demons are unique individuals, each with their own charm and wit. 
And when an individual demon is not enough for you, fuse two together to create an entirely new and unique demon, limited only by your imagination.

—description from the publisher

Work together as Researchers using dice to build experiments, test hypotheses, and ultimately publish theories to uncover a hidden Law of the Universe.

Setup:

     Place the dice within easy reach of all players.
     Decide which player will be the Universe, who will be responsible for creating a secret Law only they know. They take the Result Markers.
         Their goal will be to present the other players with a challenge while silently guiding them to success.
    
     All other players take on the role of Researchers. Place the four Publication Tokens within easy reach of them.
     The Universe shuffles the Laws of the Universe cards and draws one.
         This card must be kept secret, but the Universe may refer to it at any time.
         Example Laws: “There must be at least one orange die.” “The sum of all dice must be ten.” “There must be more odd dice than even dice.” “There must be exactly three dice.”
    
     To begin the game, the Universe builds 2 experiments, each using 4 or fewer dice: One that follows the secret Law described on the card they drew, and one that does not. The Universe then places an appropriate Result Marker next to each: O for follows, X for does not follow.
         An experiment is any grouping of dice created by a player.
    


Play
The Universe takes no turns of their own. They set up the game and respond to the actions of the Researchers.
Researchers take turns either building experiments for the Universe to evaluate or publishing theories in an attempt to win the game.
On their turn, the active Researcher may either Build an Experiment or Publish a Theory.

—description from the designer

When the planet Shinju was discovered on the other side of the Abraxas opening, it sparked a chain of events which has set the human sphere on course for conflict. As the great powers scrambled to route ships to the new planet, it was a near-obsolete attack craft, the POS Thunder Child, run by a trainee Japanese crew, who arrived first.

What they found on the sand-blasted hell hole is set to change the balance of power in the Human Sphere, and beyond, forever…   Welcome to Operation Sandtrap.

Operation Sandtrap is the first two-player battle pack for Infinity N5. Featuring two, complete forces from new sectorial armies for PanOceania and the Japanese Secessionist Army (JSA).

—description from the publisher

Muriel Anderson Music CD with Board Game

In the board game you will sail from the south shore of Long Island to the central coast of Maine. It is unique in that there are multiple winners and multiple ways to engage with your fellow players. You’ll also learn something about sailing, something about the area, and something about the music. Fair winds!

Included are: multiple choice and experience cards, fish and lobster to catch, a piece of cord in case you need to tie a sailor’s knot, a spinner in which the point of sail determines how many spaces to proceed, a professional fold-out game board, CD with the music on it, colored wooden sticks as experience tokens, and shells from the actual beaches represented (which become your sailboat playing pieces).

The music was inspired by a sailing trip from Long Island, New York to Penobscot Bay, Maine—the very trip that you will be taking in the Sailing Dreams game while listening to the music. Guest musicians include Phil Keaggy, Suzy Bogguss, Mark Kibble of Take 6, and vocal cameos by Tommy Emmanuel, Cap’n Bryan, and shanty singers.

Just in! New and improved version which includes “pirate cards,” updated board and new deck of cards with lots of interactive options. All parts are made in the USA, using natural materials where possible.

—description from the designer

Are you ready to alienate every relationship you've ever made to shill an overpriced product in a market with too many sellers?  Then it's time to join a Multi-Level Marketing (MLM) Company where the failure rate is over 99%!  Your life is finally about to begin!

Boss Babe The Game is a sarcastic board game that shows the reality of what it's like to work for an MLM. Your goal is to do whatever it takes to add 5 Huns to your downline. Only then can you join the elite MLM ranks and make the coveted $24,000 per year that most #bossbabes can only dream about earning.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Worst-Case Scenario Card Game: Apocalypse
description:
There are a thousand ways the world could end and the Worst-Case Scenario Card Game APOCALYPSE is jam-packed with 225 of the grittiest apocalyptic scenarios for players to rank. How do you compare being captured by raiders to touching a zombie's butt-cheek, discovering your pet is a flesh-eating alien, or seeing grandma flattened by an asteroid? The apocalypse is not far away, and now is the time to start thinking about which scenarios are bad, very bad, awful, horrible, or simply the worst!

Take turns playing “The Victim”. When your fellow players match how you rank 5 apocalyptic scenarios, they score points. The Victim Die decides your fate on every turn, but when disaster strikes… all that matters is who thinks alike!

Based on the New York Times Bestseller, The Worst-Case Scenario Card Game APOCALYPSE has very easy rules, but difficult choices. It's a fun easy-to-play adult game for ages 14 to adult for 3 to 6 players.

—description from the publisher

Soldier’s Chess is a military strategy game.

Soldier's Chess places you in charge of an infantry platoon, just as it makes contact with the enemy. Every decision you make will be crucial to the outcome of the battle.


    In solitaire mode, you can achieve victory according to your mission objectives (the parameters of the scenario).
    In duel mode, the first player to reach 20 victory points wins (1 point per enemy wounded and more points per enemy KIA depending on the type of unit).


With seven different unit types (each with unique traits and abilities) and no dice, you can play each game according to your strategy - but be careful, you must contend with the will of your enemy. The 7 unit types, each with distinct movements, attack ranges, and special abilities, include: Private, Machine Gun, Sergeant, Medic, Mortar, Sergeant First Class, and Lieutenant. Soldier's Chess is played on a 16x16 square board, four times the size of the board in traditional chess. The game is also played with 6 terrain tiles, which hinder or benefit nearby units.

Some key game mechanics of Soldier's Chess include: Players may order three units to act per turn. Movement and offense are separate actions, and units can also perform special actions. Pieces interact: Sergeants can lead formations, Medics can heal wounded units, Lieutenants can inspire other units, and Sergeants First Class can lead grand formations.

—description from the designer

Play a game of fun and science with Poop Attack! Travel the world and explore 54 stunning photos of poo from alpaca to zebra. 
Learn more about the animals and their habitats with fascinating facts and stats - not to mention, an incredible visual experience. It's an unprecedented look at the wonders of the animal world! 
Players will battle each other with poop stats from Frequency, Hardness, Length, Width, Smelliness, and Yuck Factor .. euh, I cannot believe we're talking about this right now... but it's a messy job and somebody had to do it. 
Loser picks up all the poop. Not it!

—description from the designer

Snoop around as Detectives Jake Peralta, Amy Santiago, Rosa Diaz, Terry Jeffords, Charles Boyle, or Captain Holt to clamor around the precinct for a hidden trophy that will deem which officer is most worthy! Using six collectible tokens for each suspect’s telltale item, players will track and narrow down WHO among the thieving workers hid the prize, WHICH item is the trophy they’re looking for, and WHERE in the precinct the ultimate trophy was stashed!

Get to the bottom of the heist by searching nine well-known Locations from the precinct, including The Bullpen, Captain Holt’s Office, The Break Room, Evidence Lock-Up, and more. Along with the Intrigue and Rumor cards that will put you on a “Cool, cool cool cool cool” path, Power Cards boast a unique ability for each player to get one shot closer to the answers.

Was it Rosa who hid Holt’s Watch in The Elevator? Or did Santiago bury Scully’s Medical Bracelet in the Copy Room? Use your custom Brooklyn 99 Clue sheets to track accusations and know where else to look before you hit the Bingpot!
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

description:
Dive into a wacky world where the most absurd conspiracy theories come to life. Your mission? Be the first to collect 7 cards from the same category (Space, Technology, History, etc.) while outsmarting your opponents. But beware: with devious Action cards, chaotic Events, and the mysterious Shepherd and his Sheep, everything can change in an instant. Cunning, strategy, and a dash of luck will be your best allies to outmaneuver the competition!

Simple, explosive, and fun for everyone!
With rules that take less than 2 minutes to learn, this hilarious party game is perfect for both beginners and seasoned schemers. Short games (15 to 30 minutes) promise laughter, unexpected twists, and a lively atmosphere. Ideal for game nights with friends or family!

Chaos and hilarity: Meet the cards that will define your evening.
Build your hand with ridiculous Conspiracy Theory cards (“The stars are alien surveillance cameras”) or sabotage your rivals with ruthless Action cards like “Government Censorship”. Unpredictable Event cards add an extra layer of madness: how will you cope if “The Internet Collapses”? And if you manage to gather the Shepherd and his 4 Sheep, you win the game instantly—but can you take that daring risk? Get ready for surprises at every turn!

—description from the designer

Before starting: Shuffle the cards and deal 5 cards per player. The other cards form a face-down pile on the table.

Rules of the game: The player who starts the game places the card of his choice on the table. The other players take turns placing a card that matches either the colour, number, or suit of that card. If a player cannot place a card, he draws from the pile. If he has not drawn a good card to play, he passes his turn.
When there are no more cards to draw, he must shuffle the cards already played and form a new face-down pile on the table.
When a player plays his penultimate card, he must say PLAYMO out loud. If he forgets it, and one of his opponents notices it and points it out to him, he must then draw 1 penalty card from the deck.

End of the game: The first player who has no more cards wins. When a player plays his last card, he must announce PLAYMOBIL. If he forgets, he must draw 1 penalty card from the deck of cards.
Joker cards: These cards can be placed on top of all other cards, regardless of colour, number or suit. The player who has just used this card can then choose the colour of the card that will be played immediately after.
Result of the game: Each player (except the winner) counts the points of his remaining cards. The fewer points, the higher the player is placed in the ranking.

A soccer board game for kids.
Start at the space marked KICK OFF. In your turn, throw a die and move forward by the number on the die. If you land on a space with a blue instruction you must follow the instruction. Play continues until a player reaches the space named GOAL. and wins the game.

This adventure takes Characters to a very unique part of Evershade – the Drysea.

The Drysea was once a giant ocean that upon the Breaking of the Sun was cleaved in half. Half of it remained under the disc. Half of it stayed outside, evaporating and disintegrating in the corrosive light. Even Luada could not staunch this, and eventually, the entirety of the ocean drained away into the Fadalends, leaving behind the region now known as Drysea.

And in the distant part of the Drysea lies a reclusive island nation of Albartia.

Once rulers of the waves, now they preside over a never ending desert of mud, salt, and decay. They built walls all around the island to ward off dangerous fauna of the Drysea and any possibility of the ground invasion. They are a strange, isolationist culture. And yet, this is exactly where your party will have to go, to mount a desperate rescue operation.

Drysea Odyssey is a highly replayable Advanced level Adventure that takes 10-15 hours for a single playthrough across 3 Chapters / 6 Acts.

—description from the publisher

Linda Belcher is hosting a murder mystery! She's invited Bob, Tina, Gene, Louise, and Teddy, and insisted they all dress up like different characters. She tells them her friend "Ned Boddy" is running late because he's been murdered somewhere in the apartment!
It's up to you to find out who could have done this? And where did they do it? And what did they do it with?
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

description: The Griffins host a dinner party and the Giant Chicken gets killed. Who killed the Giant Chicken, with what weapon, and where in the Griffin House did it occur? Features a custom game board and 9 custom weapons: Stewie's Ray Gun, Brian's Leash, Peter's Pirate Sword, Death's Scythe, Poisoned Clam Chowder, Stewie's Baby Bottle Nunchucks, Peter's Thinking Grenades, Joe's Night Stick and a Towel Rod.
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

description:
In Murdio Island, you take on the role of a police student, explore the locations on the game board step by step and listen to an atmospheric radio play with clues to your murder mystery case via the Murdio Island app. Use these clues to crack codes, solve puzzles and solve the case.
  
Murdio Island is great entertainment with an exciting radio play for children aged 10 and over, crime fans, their friends or the whole family - you can either puzzle, combine and solve the case alone or together with friends.

Your Murdio Island game comes with 1 large game board, 50 location cards, 17 puzzle cards and the Murdio Island app. This allows you to play through your first case with 1-8 players in approx. 60-75 minutes.
    
Your decisions during the game influence the course of the murder mystery. You can puzzle through your first case again to reach your goal in a different way. If you want to play more cases, you can buy new mystery stories for your board game in the Murdio Island app. The Murdio Island game material remains intact, so you can easily pass it on to your friends.

—description from the publisher
Clue with rumor/intrigue cards and a Seinfeld theme.
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

description: This Swiss edition contains different songs than the original HITSTER.

boardgame: Murder Mystery: Murder at Lake Como
description:
Host your own murder night for an evening of adventure,fun,and detective work.
Set in the 1960's there has been a murder Bellagio has been found murdered at a local villa while on  vacation in lake Como.  Tourists and a few locals have to remain inside the villa while the local police investigate.
Play your assigned roles this hilarious murder mystery game will provide you with a  night you will not forget

boardgame: Sgropin: Queo che no sofega ingrassa
description:
Sgropin is a card game in which 2 to 4 players combine ingredients to create traditional dishes from the Veneto region, matching them with the right drinks to score more points.

There are 6 different traditional dishes whose recipe requires the combination of 2 or 3 ingredients. Each dish can be created a limited number of times and recipes scores from 1 to 10 points.

Each player is dealt a hand of 6 cards representing ingredients and drinks. From the deck, 5 cards are dealt face up. On the turn, the player takes 1 face-up card from the pool or 2 cards from the deck, combines the ingredients to create a dish and - since only 6 cards can be held in the hand - discards the excess cards by placing them on top of the face-up cards in the pool.
Special card "Polenta" give the chance to move the face-up cards in the pool and special card "Sgropin" let take a card from an opponent when he creates a dish.
Drinks give additional points when creating a dish, while possession of drinks at the end of the game gives negative points.

The game ends when the deck is empty and victory goes to the player with the most points.

Sgropin takes its name from the typical after-meal that can be found in Veneto, but also from any generic digestive or convivial moment accompanied by other classy spirits.

boardgame: S.T.A.L.K.E.R. The Board Game: Stretch Goals Box
description:
The Stretch Goals Box contains all the rewards that were unlocked as part of the Gamefunding campaign on Gamefound.

The Stretch Goal Box, for example, adds several new playable missions, new weapons and artifacts, additional Stalker miniatures, plastic markers, new monsters with their miniatures and related equipment, Maps & Stickers Recharge Pack, Magnet Artifact Die which allows you to customize your anomaly exploration by rolling the die for specific effects, opening new possibilities and increasing your chances of extracting valuable artifacts from the depths of the Zone, and much more!

boardgame: S.T.A.L.K.E.R. The Board Game: Zone Survival Mode
description: In this mode, you will be able to play alone (as a true Stalker!) or with a group of friends a fully fledge scenario that will unveil before you and create a unique storyline from the combination of gameplay events and special story scripts.

boardgame: S.T.A.L.K.E.R. The Board Game: Duty & Freedom Faction Pack
description:
Introducing the Duty & Freedom Faction Pack, a comprehensive expansion that brings new elements and strategic options to your gameplay. This add-on will allow you to align your goals with one of the factions: Freedom or Duty.

This pack includes 4 detailed Duty miniatures and 4 versatile Freedom models, allowing you to immerse yourself in the conflict between these factions. They will be designed with visible Faction patches and produced in two different colors to make them more immersive and easier to distinguish!

Among other components you will also find a dedicated Campaign Book, offering a rich narrative experience as this add-on comes with THREE new missions that you can play outside or between the core campaign.

The Duty & Freedom Faction Pack will also expand your tactical options known from the core game by introducing 8 new Enemy cards! With 3 sets of 25 Enviro cards per mission, you'll face a variety of environmental conditions that add depth and unpredictability to your encounters.
With Factions being in a permanent conflict against each other you will be also able to take advantage of 18 new Weapon cards and provide your allies with enhanced protection using 24 unique Armor cards.

boardgame: S.T.A.L.K.E.R. The Board Game: Zone Companions
description: With this pack, you'll receive 3 new companions miniatures, along with a rules reference card and a dedicated token, allowing you to venture into the Zone with different companions by your side.

boardgame: EXIT Adventskalender: Die Polarstation in der Arktis
description:
EXIT the megatrend now also as an Advent calendar. Day by day, room by room, time is running out. This Advent season will be just as exciting as an escape room. In each of the 24 compartments of the Exit Advent calendar for ages 10 and up, a tricky puzzle challenges the player.

To find the right door for the following day, the puzzle in the room must be solved. There are clues in the detailed illustrated compartments, on a total of 56 puzzle cards and on various puzzle elements. If you combine things cleverly, you will find the solution and thus the symbol of the next door.

That is the mission: The dream of a trip to the North Pole comes true. The puzzlers are invited to accompany the scientists at the Arctica research station on a 24-day internship and support them in their experiments in the ice. There are still unexplored secrets in the pack ice, some of which could be important for the future of our planet. So there is a lot to discover and learn and every day brings an exciting challenge.
family: Misc: Advent / Christmas / Jule calendar game

boardgame: Great Northeastern Railroads of the United States and Canada 1840 to 1889
description:
The board game, Great Northeastern Railroads of the United States and Canada 1840 to 1889, is a turn based building and economic game set in the mid to late 19thc in the eastern USA and southern Canada. Up to ten players can choose to play various railroads providing the whole family with hours of entertainment, while learning a great deal about the actual history of the railroad industry in the middle and late 19thc United States and Canada. — Compete to become the greatest Railroad Mogul of the Age, through this highly detailed historical game which puts you in contact with the real railroads, prices, costs, freight, and passenger clients of the age.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Cattle Barons of the Great Plains 1860 to 1884
description:
The board game, Cattle Barons of the Great Plains 1860 to 1884, is a turn based card drawing and economic game set in the 19th century Great Plains states of the USA. Players compete with others to become the largest cattle rancher of the Great Plains, while providing the whole family with hours of entertainment, while learning a great deal about cattle ranching and economics in the 19thc Great Plains States of the USA. — To win the game, you must assemble the largest cattle herd on the Plains. — An exciting way to learn the real history of the West from the point of view of the heroic cattlemen of the United States.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Pirates and Privateers of the Caribbean 1640 to 1679
description:
The board game, Pirates & Privateers of the Caribbean 1640 to 1679, is a turn based card game set in the 17thc Caribbean and Spanish Main area. Players can choose to be a pirate or privateer from one of the five countries represented providing the whole family with hours of entertainment, while learning a great deal about the political, economic and warfare in the 17thc Caribbean and Spanish Main (New Spain, New Granada and province of Venezuela). This game of high adventure on the seas is won by the first player to earn the highest rank, garner the most victories, the greatest wealth in 5 game – years of play. — An exciting way to learn the real history of famous Pirates of the Caribbean from the actual historical facts of place, ships, cargoes and personalities of the 17th century!

—description from the designer

Aliens have made contact with Earth, but no one can understand what they're saying! You and your friends must use shapes to translate for them.
Over five rounds players take turns being the "alien," who must draw a card and pick one of the three words on the card. 
Using the collection of included shapes, the alien player must try to convey the word chosen in 60 seconds - the harder the word, the more points it is worth if the other players guess it correctly (for example "cactus" is worth one point, "desert" is worth two points, while "dry" is worth three). 
After five rounds, the player with the most points wins.

Unnatural storms, strange beasts and misfortune plague the town of Nordhiem. Odin has been angered, though the people know not the nature of this transgression, only that they were now gripped by dread, fearing that Odin’s judgment could spell doom for their beloved town.

This Saga includes sixteen linked Encounters, and you must face deadly foes and challenges to discover the fell deed that has broken the peace with Odin and set things aright.  
This book includes new foes and custom Challenges and Encounters, maps and the people of Nordhiem.

Yulefather Cometh is an adventure for Warrior Heroes Adventures in Talomir (WHAT) and WHAT Quickplay.

You possess an artefact of terrible power. It whispers foul words to you. Riches, glory and legend await, your path drenched in the blood of the weak. You can manifest creators of nightmare to fight at your side, and you know secrets long buried in the Rotvärlden.

Rotvärlden: Realms of Carnage is a fantasy warband skirmish game, building on ideas from Mystic Skies, and set in the world first explored in Hobgoblin. Your warband will be between 5-30 models, and the game will both support any fantasy miniature you throw at it, and provide a semi-random warband creator, to provide an in-build hobby-challenge generator. 
Gameplay features a focus on summoning and on your Haunted as a highly mobile commander, who cannot be everywhere at once. Planned to feature a number of distinct game modes, separately optimised for the best head-to-head and multiplayer experiences.

P009 - Catalan Promo Card

Geneta:
Pawed animal
Scores 5 points
Take one card

Teixó:
Pawed animal
