You may play a pawed animal

Are you ready to harness the raw power of wild magic, raise your own gleaming towers, and assert your dominion over the mystic deserts? Enter the world of Mystic Skies and prove yourself as the most skilled sorcerer in this epic battle for arcane supremacy. The fate of Kummeria lies in your hands!

Mystic Skies is a stand-alone tabletop miniatures game in which you take on the role of a mighty sorcerer riding atop a magical carpet, navigating the desert landscapes to carve out mystical kingdoms of your own. Your primary goal is to topple your enemies towers, tapping into wellsprings of power and safeguarding your territories from adversaries.

Assemble your loyal band of men-at-arms, rogues, and guards who are ready to defend your fiefdoms against the onslaught of monstrous foes, bandits, and rival sorcerers. In this high-stakes game of cunning strategy, you must use a clever combination of sorcery, manipulation, swiftness, and force to achieve victory.


     Play with what you have: Use any miniatures in your collection to represent your forces, and the wandering monsters of the desert. 
     Dynamic Spellcasting: Unleash your sorcerous might and cast spells to summon minions, control fearsome creatures, raise formidable defenses, and launch devastating attacks upon your foes.
     Unique Tower Defense Gameplay: Your wizard zooms across the battlefield commanding minions, monsters and more in an attempt to wreck the opposition's Tower of Sorcery before they, or the endless rampaging monsters, wreck yours.
     Rich Lore and Setting: Immerse yourself in the rich lore of the Deserts of Kummeria, a world teeming with untamed magic, ancient tombs, and hidden sources of mystical power.


What’s in the book:
    Mystic Skies (stand-alone game, as seen in Blaster Vol: 02)

What do you need to play?

Each player should have: 
    A wizard on a cool flying mount, ideally a magic carpet or small monstrous beast. Wizards must be mounted on a base no larger than a 50mm circle.
    A Wizard’s Scroll card (included)
    An item of scenery to represent the wizard’s Tower Of Sorcery
    A small collection of miniatures to represent their wizard’s minions

Between the players:
   One set of Gaslands movement templates (Available for free download from Mystic-Skies.com)
    A dozen or so D6. “Dice” are always D6 in this game.
    A small collection of miniatures or tokens to represent wandering monsters (see “monsters”)
    Coloured tokens to mark monsters’ current controller
    A way of tracking wounds on models, either tokens or spare dice­

It's 1962 and the space race is on! Complete the 5-4-3-2-1 countdown, find a test pilot and BLAST OFF!! to win the round. But, if another player has the most matching rockets in their countdown, they'll cut into your profits. Always keep an eye out for imposers. If you capture them, the round will end immediately, and you'll take ALL the profit chips for that round!

BLAST OFF!! is played over 3 rounds. Each player starts with a countdown row of 5 cards facedown, and a hand of 2 cards. Each turn, a player draws one card and may place a rocket number they still need in their countdown row. Then, they must discard back to a 2 card hand. It's a constant grid deciding what to hold onto, and how to avoid handing the next player a card they need.

The throwback art and simple mechanics disguise a challenging pub-weight good time with a little bit of take that.

—description from the designer

Two crooks pretend to make clothes visible only to the intelligent people, deceiving the emperor and his court. In the final parade, a child reveals that the emperor is naked, exposing the fraud to everyone.

The Emperor's New Tricks is an avoidance trick taking game for 3 to 5 players. The objective is to be the player with as few points as possible in 3 rounds. In this game you as a player will play 2 cards in each trick: one of them will be the number and the other one will be the color (suit).

Inspired by the tale: The Emperor's New Clothes.

—description from the designer
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: The Emperor's New Clothes

description:
Restoria is a tabletop game in which you take on the role of an ecologist working to restore grasslands. Your challenge is to build a species diverse plant community that is resilient to wildfires, drought, and other events.

Draw from 31 different Plant cards, each with abilities and characteristics based on real-life research. Each plant species has different interactions with adjacent plants, so where you place each plant matters. Play Land Management cards, such as prescribed burns or grazing, to remove Invasive Species or nurture your community in other ways.

The game’s mechanics incorporate the four high-level processes of community ecology: selection, drift, speciation, and dispersal, making Restoria not only a strategic challenge, but an educational experience, too.

—description from the publisher

The PA system overhead crackles and barks suddenly to life, as a she'epish techie steps away from the nearby signal box with a screwdriver. 
...begins in 4 minutes.
Message Repeats.
Dear valued employees,
As you have no doubt read in the minutes of the annual shareholders meeting, and in accordance with our stated 2021 mission, Rutherford Inc. are proud to report Mars-side production has now exceeded targets for all priority supply chains. The board are delighted to announce this represents the completion of Project Remus, sixteen months ahead of schedule.
It is with sincere gratitude that we thank you for your contributions, and with deep regret that we announce the closure of all Earth-side manufacturing and logistics facilities, with immediate effect. Automated decommissioning begins in 3 minutes.
Message Repeats.

Discover Unique Stats: It’s a thrill discovering your toy car's unique identity. Each named vehicle comes with distinct stats and weaponry, making every car a one-of-a-kind asset in the high-stakes battles of the post-apocalyptic wasteland. Simply type the name listed on the bottom of your toy car into our Gaslands: Legacy generator to uncover its unique stat line.

Evolving Narrative Campaign:  With legacy rules at its core, Gaslands: Legacy brings evolving narratives to the forefront. As players engage in heart-pounding battles, their decisions impact future confrontations and shape the story. The game evolves with each playthrough, ensuring no two experiences are the same.

Survival in a Mad World: Gaslands: Legacy's world is a lawless, apocalyptic wasteland filled with deconstructing nanobots, radioactive slime, and cybernetic dinosaurs.  
Players must navigate dangerous obstacles and outmaneuver opponents to be the last racer standing.

What’s in the book: 

     Gaslands: Legacy Part 1 (as seen in Blaster Vol: 03)
     Gaslands: Legacy Part 2 (as seen in Blaster Vol: 04)


What do you need to play? 

     A copy of Gaslands: Refuelled: Post-Apocalyptic Vehicular Mayhem 
     Each player will need two toy vehicle 
     A legacy dashboard
     Set of Legacy Templates
     3 Corrosive Pools medium terrain pieces
     Some toy dinosaurs

A game full of fun and curiosities!
With “Half the Truth” you can expect a card game that offers Levi Penell's unmistakable mix of humor, clever facts and surprising twists in every turn. Designed for anyone who likes to guess, bluff and challenge their fellow players - nothing here is as it seems!

Easy to understand, immediately playable! 
No long explanations necessary: “Half the Truth” is explained in a flash and can be played straight away. With minimal preparation time, you can get started with just 2 people. The aim is simple: fool, bluff and collect points by skillfully misleading your fellow players. Whether you play honestly or get sneaky - you decide the course of the game!

Uruguay did not rest on its laurels after trailblazing the country's conversion to renewable wind and solar energy. Now they're working to convert the transportation and heavy industry sectors to renewable hydrogen energy! Code-named Project Verne, this effort will once again set a new clean energy standard for the world!

The Project Verne expansion to Pampero includes several new ways to play. Use some or all of the rules and components, including:


     CO2 - An abundance of cards and tiles to support the introduction of CO2 into the game. Gain CO2 tokens when building wind farms and use those tokens to help pay for electrical towers. Tokens can also be spent after 1st and 2nd scoring for various bonuses.



     H2 - Used together with the CO2 rules and components, H2 provides numerous rules and components to support production of H2 fuel containers that can be delivered to local markets or exported via tanker ships.



     Innovations and Freelancers - Hundreds of cards, wooden tokens, tiles, and other components! New bonus actions can be combined to explore different strategies and create powerful combos.



     Low-Tech Towers - Tiles are not added to the tower bonus board during setup. Instead, new electrical tower bonus tiles are placed beside the board at the start of the game. Players take the board's pre-printed bonus, then add the new low-tech tower tiles. These new tiles remain on the board and provide a permanent bonus like the pre-printed bonuses.



     Events - Event tiles are added to the board under the first and second scoring and provide new effects after scoring.



     Lots, lots more!


—description from the publisher

Add some seasonal sparkle to That Sound Game with the Festive Pack.

Each card is inspired by end of year holidays from around the globe.

Simply mix these additional 65 cards into their corresponding categories and get ready to spread some joy. Oh and as a special gift from us, you also get to be uncuffed, now that’s a treat.

—description from the designer

Happy Habitat is a fast-paced card game set in a world of fantastical flora and fauna. Race your opponents to fill your habitat with a beautiful array of otherworldly birds, insects, flowers and critters. Larger sets of the same species earn more points, but you have to plan carefully because only one bird, insect, flower or critter can be placed in each row and column.

The game ends when one player adds the 16th card to their habitat. Players total their points to find out who has the happiest habitat! Happy Habitat offers a puzzly twist on card drafting and set-collection and includes gorgeous art by Varvara Alay. Solo variant included.

—description from the designer

Build your ‘Fourcast’ hand to include four times of day:  ‘SUNRISE’, ‘MORNING’, ‘AFTERNOON’, and ‘EVENING’. Then, your score will come from matching weather conditions or temperatures:  4 of a kind, 3 of a kind, or 2 pairs.

Keep an eye on the 4 ‘Prediction’ bonus cards added each hand. You might need to go out early to grab one before another player does.

After 4 hands, the player with the highest score is the winner!

Cozy weather theme and familiar poker-style scoring make this non-gamer friendly. Prediction bonus cards and open hands give gamers something to crunch on. A great after dinner game that balances luck and skill.

—description from the designer

You are all children who have been brought to the State Fair on a crisp fall afternoon.  Papa has put several bills in each of your hands and given you a list of delicious treats to go and find along the Midway.  Running excitedly into the hustle and bustle of the fair, you can't help but take in the sights and smells of the food vendors selling their tantalizing food up and down the rows.  You begin buying the things on Papa's list but you had better be careful as your arms fill up with your sweet smelling purchases, it will become harder and harder to get at your money and buy what you need as you come across it.  Do your best and hurry before the Midway closes down at the end of the evening!​

In the game Fair Food Frenzy, you begin with a handful of cash and the teddy bear your mother told you to leave in the car at least three times.  In this deck builder turned on its side, this becomes the starter deck you use on your turn to buy things from the Midway row placed in the center of the play area.

On your turn, you can buy a food item if you have the money in your three card hand to do so or you can spend time shopping up and down the Midway rows, allowing you to redraw one of the treats presented on the Midway and perhaps find that special something you were looking for.  Lastly, you can brave the trash barrel and see if luck will present you with what you seek.  After all, Papa didn't specifically say his corn dog couldn't be from out of the trash, did he?

As play continues, your deck will fill up with your purchases, making it more difficult to hold a hand of cards that will give you the buying opportunity you might need.  Carefully shift around your carried items to get the money you need in your hands to buy the tantalizing treats your Papa ordered.  As the Midway deck shrinks, you must work on fulfilling as many of the requests on Papa's list as you are able.  Scoring smaller groups of fair food if you can get them all together in your hands will allow you to remove the purchases from your deck and thin it out. Your larger food orders will have to score at the end and may come down to making sure you remembered to buy everything on the checklist so you can grab even more points after the fair closes down for the evening!  Will you focus on the orders you start with or take on more to earn extra points and maybe risk not being able to get all your purchases made before the carnival lights begin shutting down?

This fun twist on deck building is medium to light in weight, easy to learn and teach, and gives players a number of fun, strategic ways to chase points for their end of game score.  It also presents a fun way to learn how the deck building mechanic works for those who have not seen it before, all wrapped up in the indulgent fun of Fair Food!

—description from the publisher

Each round there is a drawing prompt (examples: Kermit, an Ewok, Harry Potter, a Venus Flytrap) which all players but one take a couple minutes to draw. The player not drawing this round is the judge who checks the deck of judging criteria (primarily fun variations on either "most accurate" or "most hilarious") and picks the winner based on the criteria.  It's a silly game with lots of laughs! The rules for competition deem the winner the first to ten times having your drawing be picked, but you can also play simply for laughs.

boardgame: Line of Battle: Clear the Decks! Duel
description:
Line of Battle is a stand-alone competitive naval battle card game from the same world as Clear the Decks. Two teams of 1 or 2 players command a gun crew as part of a squadron of three ships.

Each player has a cannon and cards include attack, recovery, officer, misfortune, and events. Players win by destroying their opponent's three ships before their ships are destroyed.

Introduction:Cropa (from Old English: croppa, meaning "group") is a drawless unification game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 7 cells per side, but boards with 5 or 9 cells are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones in their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is the maximal set of connected stones of the same color. A single stone is also a group.
     The size of a group is the number of stones it contains.


				
				
					Turns:
     On the very first turn, Black places one stone on any empty cell.
     From then on, starting with White, the players take turns placing exactly two stones, one by one, such that each group of stones placed is at most one stone larger than the size of the largest group of any color, excluding itself if before placement it was the largest group.
     If you cannot place both of your stones, pass your turn; otherwise passing is not allowed.


				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when both players pass in consecutive turns. The player with the fewest groups of size 1 wins. If there is a tie, groups of size 2 are compared, and so on, until there are no more ties.

—description from the designer

boardgame: For One: Mensch ärgere Dich nicht
description:
Schmidt Spiele's long-lived game Mensch ärgere Dich nicht has been reborn as a solitaire game in For One: Mensch ärgere Dich nicht.

To play, you roll the dice and move your game pieces as usual, trying to reach the finish line quickly, but on the way you have to beat lots of tiles and complete other tasks. Choose the pieces cleverly, hope for luck with the dice, and (as always) don't get angry!

As with other For One titles, this design features multiple levels of gameplay to present you with new challenges over and over again.

The game contains 64 playing cards with beautiful illustrations of items we use on expeditions, meetings and camps. On each card you will find interesting information about the origin of the inventions and their historical development.
Each card shows the year of the invention and the player's task is to place the card correctly on the timeline created by the cards already placed. The first player to get all of their cards wins.
publishers: český skaut, Tiskové a distribuční centrum, z. s.

description:
JINX is a high-energy, quick-thinking card game that brings friends and family together for non-stop excitement.

     EASY TO LEARN: Engage in a thrilling race to play all your cards while simultaneously counting from 1 to 10. Beware, if two or more players call out the same number, that's JINX - they have to pick up! Strategic power cards add twists and turns, keeping everyone on their toes.
     FAMILY-FRIENDLY FUN! Perfect for kids 7+, teens, and adults alike, JINX offers endless entertainment with 3-5 players in just 5 minutes.
     PLAY ANYWHERE, ANYTIME! Compact and portable, JINX is the perfect companion for fun on the go - whether at home, a party, or on vacation.


—description from the publisher

As the Twentieth Century dawns, the world is forever changed. Fueled by the Uranium Revolution, a new era of prosperity is here. The rich, the powerful, the free spirited... They all want a slice of the new life. Lucky for them, the energy consortiums working all over the planet, from Saxony to Australia, are more than happy to sell a few shares for extra investments. With these seemingly inexhaustible new sources of funding more and more entrepreneurs dared to set up their own experiment, and set out to make their own glowing bright green path to success! But no one can reach their heights with ambition only. Powerful Patrons are here, offering their wealth and knowledge to help you reach a new level and become the most respected entrepreneur of the time!

Nucleum: Energy Research Institute is an expansion to the base game of Nucleum and brings to life 8 new Experiments, and Patron cards with powerful abilities. All new Experiments are also compatible with Nucleum: Australia, Nucleum: Court of Progress, and Nucleum: Patrons Set.

—description from the publisher

Trade flourishes, your clan grows in power, and the coveted title of Minos feels within your grasp. Yet, in the intricate web of life, nothing is achieved without the favor of the gods. To earn their attention, you offer gifts, orchestrate sacred dances, and commission a magnificent bull’s head adorned with golden horns for your household altar. You pray, hoping for the blessing of the Great Mother Goddess. For you know, that without divine favor, life would be far more uncertain, its challenges unbearable. You are worthy and you will prove it. It is your conviction. Your faith.

Minos: Dawn of Faith is an expansion for Minos: Dawn of the Bronze Age that introduces new dice allowing players to gain favor of the gods. It brings new strategies to the table as well as new abilities for the players, and various other improvements.

—description from publisher

Ferabun (from Scottish Gaelic: fearann na buidhne, meaning "territory of group") is a strategy game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 7 cells per side, but boards with 5 or 9 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a maximal set of connected stones of the same color. A single stone is also a group.
     The size of a group is the number of stones it contains.
     A territory is a maximal set of connected empty cells. You own a territory if the largest adjacent group belongs to you, and in case of a tie, the next largest groups are compared; if all are the same size, the territory does not score for either player.
     The value of a territory is the number of empty cells it contains.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty cell not adjacent to groups of any color, unless any adjacent enemy group is exactly the same size or exactly one stone smaller than the group of the placed stone.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when no player can place stones, and the player with the territories of the highest value wins. To prevent ties, half a point is awarded to the player with the largest group. In case of a tie in size, the next largest groups are considered.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a black stone on an empty cell, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer

What Happened?

The rumor mill is churning: A mysterious criminal is planning to rob the largest casino in Las Vegas during the legendary Christmas poker tournament. Everyone is a suspect – from the employees to the guests.

Slip into the role of private detective Frank Hunt and dive into the dazzling Las Vegas of the 1970s. Uncover the dark secrets of the glittering city and become captivated by greed, love, and crime.

Solve a puzzle for each of the 24 days to prevent the casino robbery.

LOST in an unknown GALAXY!

Your goal is to find your way back home while navigating through asteroid fields, encountering alien lifeforms, and blasting enemy ships!

Paper Apps GALAXY is the third game in the Paper Apps series of procedurally-generated game notebooks.

—description from the publisher

Paper Apps Galaxy is a small, uniquely generated, notepad where each page is a sector of a larger galactic map. Draw lines and spend fuel to navigate your ship around the hexes. Stay out of the line of enemy fire, avoid asteroids and mines, and don't run out of fuel. Grab some extra supplies, too because you only have limited shields and missiles. Use the credits you win and scavenge to make your ship more nimble and more powerful, or go for prestige instead. Once you collect all six missing warp cores you can triumphantly return home!

—user summary

On a beautiful Friday morning, someone called 112. One of the firefighters received a call about a fire. The entire team got into the fire truck and went on a dangerous mission to save the residents of the building. Who will demonstrate speed and tactics? Who will save the most people from the burning tenement house? The instructions have two game variants - cooperative and competitive.

—description from the publisher (translated)

When the building catches fire, players must put out the fire and save all it's residents. During turn, player draws top card from the stack and moves the firefighter figures based on the card drawn. When a firefighter reaches a burning window, a roll of dice decides if the fire is put out and resident is saved, or if the fire is too string and firefighter has to evacuate to a neighbouring window.

In competitive mode, each put out fire is given a score based on if and what resident was saved from that fire. The game finishes when all fire is put out and the player with highest score wins. The cards are reshuffled if they run out.

In cooperative mode players all win together if they manage to put out all fire before the movement cards stack finishes or lose, if the cards run out first. Points are disregarded in this mode.

—user summary

boardgame: Enormity: Gouger of Hopes Campaign Expansion
description:
Descend to the barren world of Gough-Groth and into the blinders warren-lands. The Tyrant awaits at the planet core. It is not too late to turn back. All you have to sacrifice is... everything.

This is a new Campaign Expansion, introducing a new narrative campaign, new Intruders, new Boss, new Spacers and Suits, new Gear, new Locations and much much more. All content from the Expansioncan also be used in modular custom campaigns.

Roll up Screwball Scramble fans! That’s just what you’ll do in this new version of everyone’s favorite marble maze game that swaps from horizontal to vertical for a gravity-defying challenge. Starting right at the bottom, you need to make your marble climb on up the frame, tackling over a dozen different obstacles and visiting some of them twice! On the base are just three controls for you to jab, twist, and tease to inch your way up the corkscrew, hopscotch over the bridge one step at a time, and snake your way up the ladder by skipping up the rungs. Still have control of the ball? Get ready to sweep over the swinging passage, hop through hoops, roll down a chute, and then back up to the throwing cup. Catapult that pesky marble over to a pair of floating mazes, tip-toe over the saw blade bridge and catch your breath as you soar up to the top using the elevator. Don’t stop now!

This amazing board game still has steps to scale, a cloud to reach for, and a tricky, teetering top rail. Then drop down the funnel for that final Screwball magic as you ding the finish bell below! If you love Screwball Scramble I and II (sold separately), you’ll be itching to Level Up and reach for the top in this exciting new version. As you lunge and stretch up the cliff edge, timing and dexterity are tops as you mix patience with speed and cat-like reactions. Play solo to see how fast you can beat the maze or go head-to-head with family and friends. Fastest up the maze wins! Suitable for 1-4 players aged 5 and over. No batteries required.

Classic Game with a New Twist: All the fun of previous Screwball Scramble games, but this time it’s vertical – make your marble climb up the maze from the bottom to the top
Beat Gravity, Beat the Clock: Use just three controls to operate over a dozen obstacles, inch your way up, but don’t let the marble drop! You’ll have to start over as time keeps ticking by
Test Your Hand-Eye Coordination: Helping hone motor skills for younger players, adults will also love mastering the controls, guiding the marble patiently up the wall
Play Solo or Head to Head: You’ll need skill and speed to set your best time, or beat others to the top – the fastest player wins!
Great Family Game: Action game for 1 player or more ages 5 and up

—description from the publisher

Magi Kidz is a cooperative board game designed for children aged 5 and above. Players take on the roles of young wizards who will duel eachother. They can summon magical creatures, equipment and to use powerfull spell. The game emphasizes teamwork, problem-solving, and creativity.

Featuring colorful artwork, straightforward mechanics, and engaging gameplay, Magi Kidz is perfect for introducing children to collective card gaming. Its simple rules ensure accessibility, while the enchanting theme keeps young players immersed in the magical world.

It is a turn based game, where each player can "summon" the creature that are from its world: Pirates, fantasical beast, skeleton and zombies or fruits and vegetables.
At the beginning of the game thy draw 5 cards from their specific deck. The cards are very simple and they belong to two categories: the "concentration" that provides you the power that you need to summon and the "creature" that are cards with its "concentration" cost and have the points on its side that define its power and defense.
you draw an extra card per turn, during the "battle" you are facing different creature that the other mage has evoke. each card can deals damage and it is able to absorb the damage. If at the end of the turn it has some defense left, it will regenerate otherwise it is defeated.
Once you evoke your creature you can attack your "duellist"nad deal the damage, each of players has 10 hearts, if the counter sign zero... You are defeated... So it is time for a second duel!

—description from the publisher

DYNAMIC STRATEGY GAME: Dive into the cosmos with Vediic, a unique strategy board game where players harness planetary dynamics to outwit their opponents. With 2-7 players and a playtime of 45-90 minutes, Vediic challenges players to align with planets, capture powerful benefits, and extend their pawns' capabilities. From teleporting and avoiding setbacks to attacking and stealing cards, each turn offers deep strategy and exciting opportunities.

OPTIMIZE IN SPACE AND TIME: Perfect for gamers who enjoy intellectual challenges and creative strategy. To win, align pawns with the wandering planets in the Zodiac, and ascend your pawns by making strategic donations to planet of justice, acquiring enlightenment cards. The first player to ascend both pawns achieves ultimate victory, completing their journey. Vediic offers layers of strategy, competition, and thoughtful gameplay that reward deep thinking, making it perfect for board game enthusiasts and strategy lovers alike.

UNIQUE BENEFIT CARDS THAT HARNESS PLANETARY POWERS! In Vediic, planets offer special cards with unique advantages:

     Venus: Love - coexistence benefits.
     Jupiter: Wisdom - grants extra turns.
     Mercury: Speed and adaptability boosts.
     Mars: Power for offense and defense. 
     Saturn: Stealth - steal cards.
     Moon: Teleportation to key positions.
     Sun: Shields against retrograde effects. But in retrograde, these benefits turn into penalties, creating sudden twists. Strategize wisely to master the cosmic game!


EVERY MOVE IS STRATEGIC: Navigate a dynamic cosmic landscape to promote your pawns and ascend to victory! Compete for key spots on the board in a thrilling race against time. Use benefit cards strategically for both offense and defense to outwit opponents and gain the upper hand. Every decision counts in a race against time!

SPECIAL BEGINERS RULES: The QR code on the game box points to a simpler ruleset for beginners to get started quickly. Beginners can simply skip retrograde rules and pawn promotion end game and simply earn three enlightenment cards to win. Flexible rule variations make it perfect for pre-teens, casual players, or family fun. For hardcore strategists, the full game depth rivals that of chess, offering endless challenges and layers of complexity.

REPLAYABILITY: With millions of unique planetary configurations, no two games are ever the same!
PLAY DURATION: Designed for 2-7 players, with optimal gameplay for 2-4 players. Expect a playtime of 45 to 90 minutes for 2-4 players, with an additional 15-20 minutes for each extra player. The game includes a comprehensive 20-page manual to guide you through every detail of the gameplay.

A SIMULATION OF ANCIENT WISDOM: The game takes inspiration from the ancient practices of Astrology and cosmology in Indian (Vedic), Greek, Babylonian, and Egyptian civilizations. These traditions viewed planetary movements as influences on human events and well-being. Vediic distils this rich heritage into a structured and strategic rule set, creating a fun and immersive simulation. It bridges ancient wisdom with modern gameplay, blending celestial themes with strategic decision-making for an engaging experience.

—description from the publisher

You are geology specialists examining a stratum filled with lots of fossils. Find the fossils by looking at the distribution list based on the preliminary survey. However, the deeper the stratum, the more resources you will need to dig. Furthermore, the survey schedule is too short, in other words, only 3 turns. Find the most valuable fossils possible within the limited amount of digging!

Players each draft a tableaux of six, face-down fossil cards. On their turn, they reveal a card in another player's tableaux then attempt to reveal a matching fossil in their own tableaux. If the cards match, the player scores points depending the rarity and difficulty of digging that fossil. Each revealed fossil also reduces the number of rarity of fossils a player can attempt to dig on their turn. The winner is the player that has successfully dug the rarest set of fossils.

Genre: Memory x Resource Management

Quarks is a fast and fun tile placement game. Strategically match colors to score points, but watch out — the board is constantly shifting as tiles turn and connections change.

On your turn, draw a tile — lime green, red, or blue — and place it on the board. Then rotate the board:

Green – Go (clockwise)
Red – Reverse (counter-clockwise)
Blue – your choice

After each rotation, score points by creating chains of matching colors connected to your tile. The board is always changing, so no two turns — or games — are ever the same.

The player with the most points after all tiles have been placed, wins.

—description from the publisher

Catch the Chameleon, again! 40 brand new Topic Cards to freshen up your next game of The Chameleon, including Fast Food Chains, Songs, and Things That Get Flushed Down the Toilet!

—description from the publisher

This pack adds a menagerie of new NPC creatures to Maladum, from the relentless Split-Tail Rats to the bulking Crag Troll, each with unique mechanics to present a new challenge for your Adventurers. They can be integrated into any quest or campaign simply by shuffling in the new Event Cards included in the box. The beasts are accompanied by a selection of themed equipment tokens and a new
Hidden Location for your Adventurers to find...

—description from the publisher

- This was included inside the Core Box of the Kickstarter Edition

PICK IT, SPOT IT!
Beware! Gameboard packed with 300 of the world's deadliest and most dangerous animals! Can you be the first to spot the death stalker scorpion? How about the wolverine or great white shark? Take a chip from the box and see who can find the picture first!

—description from the publisher

These are four additional cards; one for each faction.

On top left corner is a crystal and right down on the bottom three pictures of the corresponding faction.

Crystal: Go one step on the crystal path.

Headquarter: Bottom left with (Colour in the background). You are not allowed to cover it. It has to be free all the time.

Draw My Face is a drawing game for all artistic abilities (or none!). Can you draw your best friend with your eyes closed? How about your brother using only triangles? Your grandma as an astronaut? Whether you produce fine art or art that is far-from-fine, Draw My Face is sure to get everyone laughing.

In each round one player is the 'model' - they draw two category cards and chooses one to apply to themselves. The other players are the 'artists' - they have 60 seconds to draw the model using the added descriptor, then the model must compliment all the drawings that have been made of them. After everyone has taken two turns being the model, all drawings are laid out and each player is given three tokens to vote for their favourites (-you cannot vote for your own drawings). The player whose drawing receives the most votes is the winner, with their drawing declared a 'priceless artwork'.

Byte Club is a strategic card game that makes cybersecurity concepts easy and enjoyable to learn. Simulate real-world cyber threats and defense strategies, familiarizing players with essential cybersecurity frameworks used by attackers and defenders alike. During gameplay, players gain practical insights into safeguarding digital information, countering cyberattacks, and understanding key concepts like the Cyber Kill Chain (attack) and the NIST Cybersecurity Framework (defend).

On your turn play any number of attack and/or defend cards in your hand. Resolve the actions on your card 1 at a time. End your turn by drawing a card.

A player wins by achieving either of the below end game conditions:
(1) Have the Data Flag card in their hand when the Times Up card is revealed
-OR-
(2) Collect the complete sets of attack and defend cards by having them played in front of them.

—description from the designer

The Escape Game phenomenon becomes a game for the little ones too!

New adventures designed to introduce children to the world of puzzles, logic and lateral thinking.

Each card hides a clue to continue the adventure. The game includes a deck of puzzle cards and useful objects to find the clues.

An adventure full of puzzles and a journey to enjoy by recovering all the clues and solving the puzzles, training the mind and lateral thinking.

A game with cards and many mysteries to solve, just like in a real Escape Room!

—description from the publisher (translated)

1880. The British have suffered a major defeat by the Afghans at Maiwand and the British garrison at Kandahar is under siege. A British relief force is sent to relieve the Kandahar garrison and smash the Afghan forces under Ayub Khan. The Afghans have prepared impressive defenses utilizing the rugged terrain and have as much artillery as the British. The Afghans expect to impose another crushing defeat against the relief column.

The Battle of Kandahar is a low-to-moderate complex tactical game covering the British attack against the Afghans at Kandahar in 1880. The game takes about 90 minutes to play. One player commands the British forces and the other player commands the Afghan forces. Cards vary game play.

—description from the publisher

Phase Four is an expansion for the core rules of Alien Sanctuary. It adds a phase four to the game and brings in external influences. Many of these influences can be problematic but an astute Sanctuary manager may well turn them in their favour. Face activists, government officials, disgruntled celebrities, natural disasters and unexpected alien incursions!

boardgame: Guess the Sum (or Plus or Minus)
description:
The game is played on a 8x8, or 10x10 board, or as a TV show.
The game contains 400 coins/checkers with numbers from 1 to 100 on one side, and the sign Plus or Minus on the other side.
The numbers fill the board, and players will have to make accurate guesses for the sum of a certain row or column, without knowing the sign of each number.

The total permutations for this game are incredible!
On a 8x8 board = 5.94*10^173.
For a 10x10 board = 2.58*10^241.
The game is full of suspense because players have no way to check that many permutations.

boardgame: Happy Little Dinosaurs: Emotional Support Log Promo Card
description:
Your favorite existentially-distressed dinosaurs are now collectible vinyl mini figures!

The Happy Little Dinosaurs Vinyl Mini Series features eight of your favorite dino characters, plus two new mystery characters. Each box comes with a vinyl figure and a promo card you can use in the game.

Plug the new card into your Disaster deck to play with your Happy Little Dinosaurs base game and expansions.

Buy one figure at a time for a mystery surprise, or get the pack of 18 with a guarantee that you'll find all 10 figures! The surprise is half the fun, so Vinyl Figure Series sales are final.

Cry Ceratops vinyl mini figure and Emotional Support Log promo card.

Card text:
EMOTIONAL SUPPORT LOG
If you would add this card to your Disaster Area, move it to the bottom of the Disaster deck instead, then collect 2 points for each Emotional Disaster card in your Disaster Area.

Sky Totems (スカイトーテム) is a 3-dimensional strategy game, where players compete to build the best altar by completing the requirements of altar cards while acquiring the blessings of one or more powerful totems.

Each round, wooden pedestal cubes are drawn from a bag to form several stacks on the central board, and players take a stack of cubes to use for their own altars. Once taken, pedestal cubes can be placed on the cloud tiles that make up an altar's foundation, stacked atop a matching cube, or exchanged for more cloud tiles to build upon later on.

As players build their altar, they will aim to complete the missions given by various altar cards while also attracting totems that cap off sections of the altar, each with its own requirements for attraction, scoring bonus, and helpful ability to further enhance their altars.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The game is a competitive fishing adventure where players take on the role of fishermen, exploring various fishing locations and attempting to catch the most valuable fish. Players use dice rolls, strategic card play, and bait management to outwit their opponents and maximize their score. Each fishing location is represented by a stack of fish cards, and the game board provides multiple areas for players to compete over prime spots.

On each turn, players can perform one main action, such as casting their line by rolling dice to form sequences that match the requirements of fish cards. Players may also move to different locations, displace opponents for bait, or reorder fish cards at a cost. Special action cards add a layer of strategy, allowing players to manipulate dice results, protect their position, or gain additional advantages. Resources like bait are essential, as they enable rerolls, access to new cards, and tactical plays.

The goal is to score the highest points by catching valuable fish and utilizing resources effectively. Points are earned based on the fish caught, with additional opportunities to exchange fish for bait or influence the game board. The game ends when key conditions are met, such as depleting fish stacks, and the winner is the player with the most points.

Players must move their pieces along the board and discover the various cards representing animals or peoples present on Earth.
The objective is to complete missions that require you to find animals or people that have certain characteristics (where they live, what they eat, what type of animals they are or what economy the population is based on).
Children compete and learn new things with every game.

New challenges in your ascent.

Firefly is an expansion for the game Solstis. Discover four new mischievous Spirits and go in search of the mysterious fireflies, which can give you an instant victory.

春秋 (Spring and Autumn) is an area control board game featuring a unique political system-building mechanism. In this game, players assume the roles of one of the Five Hegemons, utilizing three types of actions—military, diplomacy, and internal affairs—to visit the Zhou Dynasty court, convene alliances, wage war against major states, and establish ties with minor states. The goal is to earn the most Mandate of Heaven and become the supreme ruler of the Spring and Autumn period.

—description from the publisher

《春秋》是一款带有政体构筑机制的区控桌游。在本桌游中，玩家将扮演五霸之一，通过军事、外交、内政三种行动，觐见周王朝、举行会盟、攻打大国、结交小国，争取获得最多的天命，成为春秋时期的天下共主。

—description from the publisher (Chinese)

This game contains 64 double sided coins placed randomly on a regular chessboard.
Each side of the coins has a letter representing either the Signal, or the Noise.
Signal = 64 = one letter per coin.
Noise = 64 = one letter per coin randomly generated. 
The Signal is an encoded message leading to a hidden treasure.
The Noise adds additional confusion and complexity. 
The coins are placed in a bag and scrambled.
Then, they are placed randomly on a regular chessboard.
The task is to flip and move the coins around in order to uncover the original message.
However, the number of total permutations are so high that decoding/solving the game seems almost impossible.

If we increased the number of coins to 300, not even the most powerful computer in the world will be able to solve the puzzle.

This game can be played with any number of coins from 10 to 300!
New achievement board that is part of the French Magazine Plato number 170 (november 2024).

Watch out! There are 300 of the deadliest and most dangerous animals in the world on this game board!
Who will be the first to spot the yellow Mediterranean scorpion? Or the great white shark? Draw a chip from the box
and find the depicted animal faster than your fellow players (dear parents, don't be surprised, your
your children will surely beat you!) This game is easy to understand, but be careful - it's addictive!
addictive! Lots of family fun guaranteed.

Three Sisters Harvest Edition is a strategic roll-and-write game about backyard farming. It is named after an Indigenous agricultural technique still widely used today: planting three different crops close together. Corn provides a lattice for beans to climb, the beans bring nitrogen from the air into the soil, and the squash provides a natural mulch ground cover to reduce weeds and keep pests away.

Three Sisters Harvest Edition features all the award-winning gameplay of the original edition plus a twelve-chapter campaign mode with new content, gameplay, and components. The campaign is for both group and solo play. The new content can also be used as expansion modules for an individual game of Three Sisters.

Three Sisters Harvest Edition is a fully standalone replacement for Three Sisters.
family: Series: Loaded Roll and Write (Motor City Gameworks)

boardgame: MonToons: Guardians of the Coral Isles
description:
In this modular expansion for MonToons: Clash of the Coral Isles, ancient island Guardians who have slumbered for eons awaken to forge powerful bonds with your MonToon characters.

As the Waystone’s energy fades and Kava’s blight spreads like an unrelenting wildfire, these Guardians rise once more, bringing a surge of hope to purify the blight and defend the dying Waystones. Together, they stand as the last line of defense against the blight threatening to consume the Coral Isles.

This expansion brings you new Guardian sheets that give you additional gameplay, exciting new mechanisms, new character abilities to try, battle maps and so much more.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Strawberry Shortcake: Berry Besties Bakeoff Card Game
description: In Strawberry Shortcake: Berry Besties Bakeoff Card Game, players bid for cards featuring familiar Strawberry Shortcake characters and items, trying to place them well to complete their cake recipe and out-bake their opponents. The cards bid in one round become the pool of what's available in the next one. Whoever builds the best shortcake and scores the most flavor points wins.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Welcome to Susberg is a cooperative deck-building game. Join your friends in a problem solving adventure where each player has a unique style of play. Buy cards and manipulate your deck to create the best possible combinations for you and your friends.

Each player chooses one of four characters. The athlete, who trashes cards and removes them from the game. The artist who mixes paints together and stashes cards aside for later turns. The musician who makes and spends music. The inventor who grows their energy, and puts cards back into their decks.

A deck of 18 foes is randomly stacked and the players must defeat them all to win the game. If each player loses their 5 health, it's game over.

Each turn follows the same pattern. You attack. You defend. You buy. You'll first combine your power cards to defeat foes. Then you defend from foes using your block cards. Then you buy new cards with the coin earned from defeated foes.

—description from the designer

Relive the magic of the 90s with Gift Republic's Totally 90s Board Game, the ultimate nostalgic trivia experience for fans of this iconic decade. From Tamagotchis and Furby to the Fresh Prince of Bel-Air, this game brings back all the feels with 500 curated questions spanning TV, movies, music, cultural trends, and sports.

Designed for 2-6 players, it’s perfect for family game nights, friendly competitions, or retro-themed parties. Whether you're a 90s kid or just love the era's vibrant pop culture, you'll enjoy hours of laughter and reminiscing as you battle it out to prove who knows the most about this unforgettable decade.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Until Proven Guilty: Thirst for Justice
description:
Until Proven Guilty is a narrative co-operative game inspired by court-themed visual novels, television series, and video games.

Thirst for Justice is a standalone case in which the theft of money from a well-known nightclub hides many uncertainties. Using the available evidence in your role as attorney Peter Howard, sway the jury to your side and convince them of your client's innocence.

You've heard of f*** marry k*ll? This is like that, except you can print the name on a box. Date Fate Eliminate is an absurd card game that gets you to pick between Experiences, People, and Things to determine your favorites. Trust us, it's harder than it sounds to compare "your dentist" to "the cheesy smell of an old book". Win points when you correctly guess your friends' rankings.

—description from the publisher

Pack O Game is a series of easily portable card-based strategy micro games in a gum-sized tuck box with a variety of mechanics, complexity and theme. Each game is 30 cards size 1"X3" and a 1 sheet rules.

Pack O Game: Set 3 includes all 8 games from the 3rd set of Pack O Game from Perplext. This set includes: WIN, YET, MAD, NUT, BOG, FAR, IRK, and SLY.

DECOY DOLLAR

Hazardous Movement Pack 5: Firestarter follows in the footsteps of Pack 4: it's chock full of thoroughly playtested, fun, tournament-sized scenarios that feature a wide variety of nationalities, terrain and theaters of war. In addition, Firestarter includes a new two-sided Hazmo map (HZ3a/3b)!

—description from the publisher

Discover the third adventure in the BACKSTORIES Saga: The Blaze

How to Play ?
Backstories is a game with mechanisms as simple as they are revolutionary: a bit like you would do in a Point & Click video games, interact with your environment as you progress through your thrilling adventures. Some cards (Panorama) represent the environment in which you’re evolving. Other cards (Actions) are perforated, and represent the actions you can perform (Talk, Search, Objects in your possession, etc.). To interact, all you have to do is place a perforated card on the back of the Panorama card you’re interested in, then turn the card over and read the result of your choice.
Be careful, though: in every scenario you play through, each of your choices will have real consequences… Be ready to face them!

In this scenario, you play as a former militiaman who left his violent past behind to take care of his family. But in this post-apocalyptic world, the past is quick to catch up with you…

Remember: Your choices, Your Adventure!

This box of alternative enemies includes 7 PVC miniaturs of 3 new type of red caps for your Warcrow Adventures games.

Contents:

3x Osseous
    3x Sightless
    1x Rumpy Ride
    Enemy cards (both in English and Spanish)

boardgame: Warcrow Adventures: They Came From the Fog
description:
This box of alternative enemies includes 9 PVC miniatures of 4 new types of adversaries.

Contents:
    3x Needles
    3x Marked with crossbow
    2x Orc husks
    1x Harvester
    Enemy cards (both in English and Spanish)

Something fearsome rests underground in Hawthorn Point, though its citizens are completely unaware of it. The most monstrous and fearsome creation of the Sculptors of the Scions of Yaldabaoth, the Giant Mornmab, is a hulking beast of living flesh that waits chained beneath the coastal town's buildings. Now it has awakened and is incredibly angry and threatens to destroy everything in its path.

Our heroes will have to join forces and face the terrible threat of the Giant Mornmab to save Hawthorn Point and its inhabitants!

The Giant Mornmab is an expansion that includes a large 165mm miniature, accompanied by a side-quest where you will face this fearsome creature. The box comes only with the miniature, since the enemy card and its initiative token are included for free for all backers of at least the Core Pledge.

boardgame: Lost in Adventure: The Curse of Jack Parrot
description:
In the co-operative game Lost in Adventure, you and your fellow players will explore an unknown world where your actions directly shape the unfolding narrative. As you progress, you reveal new areas by placing Scenario cards side by side, interact with characters, gather clues, and use objects strategically. Each decision can affect the outcome of the story, leading to one of multiple possible endings. Your ultimate goal is to complete all feats and finish the adventure with the highest possible reputation.

In Lost in Adventure: The Curse of Jack Parrot, players follow the trail of a legendary pirate whose name is wrapped in mystery and misfortune. Set in a tropical archipelago filled with secrets, the game introduces a branching narrative full of dangers, puzzles, and discoveries. The modular map grows as you play, revealing new scenario cards and interaction opportunities tied to both story and mechanics. Unique object-based interaction rules allow players to resolve situations based on item matching and contextual clues, rewarding observation and teamwork.
This title builds on the core system introduced in Lost in Adventure: The Labyrinth, adding new gameplay elements, a thematic shift to a pirate adventure, and increased narrative complexity.

The Hostage Negotiator: Ultimate Box contains all of the gameplay products for Hostage Negotiator! The content within was originally released as separate products produced and released over the course of several years, but now you can get it all in one place for a great price!

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Blackbeard, the strategic trick-taking card game from Mindrax for 3-6 players.
Players are transported into the Golden Age of Piracy.
The colonial powers of England,  Spain and France fight foor safe trade routes at sea. But beware: the colonial powers are threatened by pirates and there is a traitor in their midst...

THE GOAL
The goal of the game is to get as few tricks as possible with pirates and especially Blackbeard. Going around, every player. discards one card in the middle of the table - these cards are the trick. The tricks are repeated for so long until all cards have been put down. This marks the end of a round. After each round you receive 1 point for each Pirate card and 10 points for the received Blackbeard card. The player with the least points after 6 rounds wins the game.

THE CARDS
There are 4 nations in the game:
British (red) 1-15, including Blackbeard (13)
French (blue) 1-15, without 7
Spaniard (yellow) 1-15, without 7
Pirates (black) 1-15
There are also 2 Jokers.

THE RULES

     ﻿﻿If a player has the starting nation on his hand, it must be discarded.
     ﻿﻿If a player has no card of the starting nation in his hand, any card can be played - including a Pirate or Blackbeard. The player can no longer receive the trick. Example: The first player discards a French card, but all other players have no French cards left in their hand. The player with the Frenchman will get the trick and probably collect a lot of points.
     ﻿﻿Cannon Fire: If the first card of a trick is a Pirate, all players may discard any card of any nation. The trick is awarded to the player with the highest card, regardless of nation. If two players discard an equivalent card - e.g. British 9 and Spaniard 9 - the player who last discarded the highest card receives the trick.
     ﻿﻿Jokers can always be discarded and are used to bypass a trick. If the first card of a trick is a joker, the next card determines the nation.
     ﻿﻿Blackbeard is the special card of the game! He disguises himself as British and must therefore be discarded with the British cards. Whoever receives Blackbeard starts the next round by discarding the first card.


POINTS

     ﻿﻿If a player receives a trick, the cards are placed on a pile on the side of the game. If Pirates or Blackbeard are among the cards received, the player places them directly in front of himself. At the end of each round, the players add up their received cards. For each Pirate they get 1 point and 10 points for Blackbeard.
     ﻿﻿The captain records the points of all players after each round. The cards are then shuffled again and distributed to the players. From the 2nd round onwards, the player who previously received the Blackbeard card always begins to discard the first card. To relieve the captain, the cards can be shuffled and dealt clockwise.

A game that take place on a fantasy World called Crysalia,and you will become Crysalis, the one that can control the power of Xtall.

With FPS [Full potential System] In Crystales Storia you can choose between 4 Episode to play. Each Episode have different gameplay and objective to win.

1. Main Episode : Joyfull Festival, in this game you compete with other Crysalis to gather Mysterious Xtall Data Chest, that can be found on Ruin, Monster drop reward, Restoring Field Reward. During the game you can manipulate the value of each type of chest, so no one can predict the value of the chest they have until the end of the game.

2. Main Episode : MonHunt Day, In this game you and other Crysalis must co-op to hunt monster and get good achievment.By slaying a monster you will get Contribution Point, try to get as much as Contribution point and Become MVP.

3. Sub Episode : 3Xtall Crash, this is mini game that can be played around 30 mnt. Your goal is to match 3 Xtall with same type to get point, other player can steal your point by matching 3 Xtall with same type of point want to steal.

4. Sub Episode : Poker XChest, this also mini game, the goal is to have higher Chest Value than the other player, try to bluff other player to raise the bet.

Each of Episode is refer to your daily life story as a Crysalis.Enjoy your real life and enjoy your fullest life as a Crysalis

If you go wild for casöla and think nothing in the world is better than a plate of risotto with luganega sausage, then this is the game for you.

Schiscèta is an easy and fast card game, perfect for having fun with friends and family while celebrating the classics of traditional Brianza cuisine.
Enjoy creating new combinations and putting together unique flavors to outsmart your opponents.
Honor the genuine and rich tastes of your Brianza!

In Schiscèta, you will score points creating dishes using the ingredients cards in you hand. Additionally, you may play a wine card when creating a dish, to score extra points.

Every turn, you will either draw two cards from the ingredient deck, or one from the face up card stacks. You will be able to manipulate the card stacks using the schiscèta cards.

At the end of the game, you will add up all the points in the dish cards you have created, adding all the wines card you manage to play with your dishes. Who scores the most points is the winner.

The Creator of the world of Azvaltya hires you as a guardian angel to take care of Marcel, his little protégé.

But beware, Marcel has free will, and you cannot force him to make the right choices. You can influence his journey and his moods. Paradoxically, at times, you may even have to provoke incidents and problems (but not too much, okay!?) if it helps him avoid the worst!

A stressful yet undeniably fun challenge.

Throughout the game, you will have to manage the partition of resources between players and come up with a strategy tolerating the unknown cards that might change the direction Marcel will take, and all of that in two minutes not more! Once the unexpected finally happen, prepare to deconstruct all your previous plans and come up with new shortcuts and ideas. All of that can be combined with challenges like "Everybody is called Marcel" or "You are only able to communicate through singing" that are sure to raise the party atmosphere!

In the end, if you did not spend too much time going in the wrong direction or failing challenges, you'll have enough gas for Marcel to drive to the end of the road and reach Azvaltya! If your strategies were not flexible enough you will likely break the car or run out of gas! As a collaborative game, you'll all share the victory!

What’s more, this game makes you think!

It invites you to see your own life circumstances from a new perspective. A fun and philosophical experience not to be missed.

This quirky strategy game might just change the way you see life! Guide Marcel to the finish line by placing the right paths in his way and anticipating his emotions, which can turn everything upside down!

boardgame: Happy Little Dinsoaurs: Feathered Fury Promo Card
description:
Your favorite existentially-distressed dinosaurs are now collectible vinyl mini figures!

The Happy Little Dinosaurs Vinyl Mini Series features eight of your favorite dino characters, plus two new mystery characters. Each box comes with a vinyl figure and a promo card you can use in the game.

Plug the new card into your Disaster deck to play with your Happy Little Dinosaurs base game and expansions.

Buy one figure at a time for a mystery surprise, or get the pack of 18 with a guarantee that you'll find all 10 figures! The surprise is half the fun, so Vinyl Figure Series sales are final.

Rare mystery vinyl mini figure and Feathered Fury promo card.

Card text:
FEATHERED FURY
If you would add this card to your Disaster Area, move it to the bottom of the Disaster deck instead, then search the Discard pile for an INstand card and add it to your hand.

In Chytré kostky - Doprava one player rolls four different die (length, weight, type, etc.), then everyone tries to find vehicle matching most rolled symbols. After every player has chosen one vehicle every picked vehicle is turned to other side and each match equals one point.

Once each player rolled once (or twice in two player game) it is end of the game and player with most points wins.

boardgame: Happy Little Dinosaurs: Flying High Promo Card
description:
Your favorite existentially-distressed dinosaurs are now collectible vinyl mini figures!

The Happy Little Dinosaurs Vinyl Mini Series features eight of your favorite dino characters, plus two new mystery characters. Each box comes with a vinyl figure and a promo card you can use in the game.

Plug the new card into your Disaster deck to play with your Happy Little Dinosaurs base game and expansions.

Buy one figure at a time for a mystery surprise, or get the pack of 18 with a guarantee that you'll find all 10 figures! The surprise is half the fun, so Vinyl Figure Series sales are final.

Pterry Dactyl vinyl mini figure and Flying High promo card.

Card text:
FLYING HIGH
If you would add this card to your Disaster Area, move it to the bottom of the Disaster deck instead, then choosea player and look at that player's hand. Choose a card and add it to your hand.

Monopoly WOA, Wacken Open Air Festival Edition

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

With this edition you get into the world of the legendary Wacken Open Air Festival. Not only for Metalheads.

boardgame: Happy Little Dinosaurs: Self Defense Promo Card
description:
Your favorite existentially-distressed dinosaurs are now collectible vinyl mini figures!

The Happy Little Dinosaurs Vinyl Mini Series features eight of your favorite dino characters, plus two new mystery characters. Each box comes with a vinyl figure and a promo card you can use in the game.

Plug the new card into your Disaster deck to play with your Happy Little Dinosaurs base game and expansions.

Buy one figure at a time for a mystery surprise, or get the pack of 18 with a guarantee that you'll find all 10 figures! The surprise is half the fun, so Vinyl Figure Series sales are final.

Easygoing Anky vinyl mini figure and Self Defense promo card.

Card text:
SELF DEFENSE
If you would add this card to your Disaster Area, move it to the bottom of the Disaster deck instead, then choose a player. Move that player's Dinosaur meeple backward 5 spaces on their Escape Route.

This game resembles the political landscape and the struggle for power.

The game is played on a pyramid of 561 hexagons, or circles arranged hexagonally. 
The pyramid has 33 hexagons/circles at the base, and 33 ranks/files to the top. 
Each player has 32 checker pieces, black or white. 
The pieces are placed in stacks of two at the base of the pyramid. One player has his pieces on the left side, and the other on the right side.
An empty hexagon should separate the pieces of both players in the middle of the first rank. 
The goal is to reach the top of the pyramid. The first player to place one of his pieces at the top wins the game.
Players use two dice and take turns rolling the dice and moving pieces according to the numbers on the roll. 
Players are not allowed to move the same checker piece more than once per turn. This means that, rolling two dice means that two pieces have to be moved. Rolling a double means that four pieces have to be moved. 
Pieces get captured by landing on another piece of opposite colour.
Captured pieces are put back on the starting rank on a cell chosen by the owner on his own half of the board. 
Players are allowed to place two pieces, but not more, on the same cell.
Two pieces on the same cell makes them uncapturable.

This game can also be played as an "election game" with checker pieces becoming "candidates", each checker piece having a number, or letter, representing a certain candidate. The two colours can represent the two sides of the political landscape. 
If there are fewer "Candidates" than the checker pieces, a candidate can be represented by multiple numbers. Of course, the numbers will have to remain face down until the end of the game.

This game is a great way to get rid of corruption and endless scandals in the political world.

—description from the designer
Originally released as an exclusive 5 card expansion for OrganATTACK! if you purchased the 'Fat' Plushie from Awkward Yeti's website.

As a Will Street Detective, you normally use your skills to locate serial killers, track down stolen items, and even tail a cheating spouse or two, but the newest case may be the strangest of all. A pirate radio station has started operating somewhere in Valley Falls. Only, instead of playing unlicensed music, the station has been broadcasting bizarre and odd rants coming from someone calling themselves The DJ. Even stranger. for the last three days, the broadcast has been the exact same. While it seems innocuous, nothing in Valley Falls is ever what it seems.

Will you be able to figure out what the mysterious broadcast is about or who is behind it? Tune in to Dead Air!

As with all Deadbolt Mystery Society games, you step into the role of the detective. Examining the evidence provided, visiting different locations and using the password protected QR mechanic to uncover the truth.

Players win once they catch the killer, find what's missing or whatever else is needed to solve the case at hand.

Valley Falls is known for a few things: an abnormally high murder rate, an overwhelming number of secrets, and winning at high school football. Coach Bobby McCall has led the Valley Falls Druids to their last three state championships, and this year is off to a similar start. In tonight's game, the Druids are facing off against the Crowley's Point Jackals. The Druids are up by seven points at halftime, and things are looking good as the team heads to the locker room for their mid-game pep talk. Coach McCall is right in the middle of a fiery speech about winning when he clutches his heart, collapses, and utters the words "I was murdered" in front of a shocked team of Druids before taking his last breath. Now, it's up to you to muster some school spirit, examine all of the secrets
beneath the Friday Night Lights to see what is revealed, and discover who committed this Halftime Homicide.

As with all Deadbolt Mystery Society games, you step into the role of the detective. Examining the evidence provided, visiting different locations and using the password protected QR mechanic to uncover the truth.

Players win once they catch the killer, find what's missing or whatever else is needed to solve the case at hand.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Clash Of Steel: War of Unification – Soviet vs British Complete Starter Set
description:
Welcome to Clash of Steel. It’s 1948 and the war has started again.

Based on an Alternative History where the war in Europe finishes in 1944 with the surrender of Germany, but restarts just four years later in the face of Soviet aggression.

Players take to the tabletop with one of four initial nations in Epic Tank-On-Tank Combat using Highly Detailed Multi-part Plastic Miniatures, battling over objectives in an attempt to control the battlefield and defeat their enemies.

Featuring Dynamic Mission Based Game Play to ensure that every battle is unique and cinematic.
How will you re-write the history books?

The Clash of Steel Starter Sets contain everything a player needs to get started, including rules, miniatures, dice, tokens and much more…

—description from the publisher

Two coalition naval forces are on the hunt for enemy subs. Though working together, each navy wants the bragging rights on how many subs they can destroy. Using their radar, ships will position themselves just above an enemy sub and release a depth charge in the water below. Whoever destroys the most subs, gets to brag!

Depth Charges is a two player, grid game that pits each player against each other to see who can destroy the most subs (cards) in the game. With each hit, you score victory points (VP). The deeper the sub is in the water, the more points you can score. Whoever has the most victory points at the end of the game, wins!

The game will be set up with a 3 X 4 grid. These represent the sub cards. On the outside of the grid are the coordinate cards. The are the cards you will alter to target a sub. To the left and the right of these cards, there will need to room to place cards down to represent both player's hit.

Each turn players will find a card you want to attack. Find the cards, outside of the grid, that intersect with that grid card. The player will then find a card from their hand that can mathematically be placed in such a way that a card value can subtract from the other to equal the value of the card in the grid. You will then claim that card and earn the points, at the depth (1,2,3 or 4). The player with the highest total, wins!

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Happy Little Dinosaurs: Sweet Surprise Promo Card
description:
Your favorite existentially-distressed dinosaurs are now collectible vinyl mini figures!

The Happy Little Dinosaurs Vinyl Mini Series features eight of your favorite dino characters, plus two new mystery characters. Each box comes with a vinyl figure and a promo card you can use in the game.

Plug the new card into your Disaster deck to play with your Happy Little Dinosaurs base game and expansions.

Buy one figure at a time for a mystery surprise, or get the pack of 18 with a guarantee that you'll find all 10 figures! The surprise is half the fun, so Vinyl Figure Series sales are final.

Stego vinyl mini figure and Sweet Surprise promo card.

Card text:
SWEET SURPRISE
If you would add this card to your Disaster Area, move it to the bottom of the Disaster deck instead, then collect 5 points if you have at least 1 Meteor card in your Disaster Area.

Welcome to Kirlo, the board game for all occasions! What does "Kirlo" actually mean? Well, it's Esperanto and means whirl or vortex. If you take a look at the game board, you'll immediately see why the game is called that - the arrows between the playing fields move around and towards the center, just like in a vortex.

Gameplay
As soon as it is your turn, carry out the following steps.
Roll the dice: Take your dice and roll once.
Move: Move a piece of the same color forward by the number rolled.
Arrow direction: You may only move the stones in the direction of the arrows. At junction in the path, you are free to choose which path you want to take with your piece (provided the path is not blocked).
Blockades: You may not jump over other pieces with your piece.
If a piece blocks the path, you must move your piece along
another free path.
Hit: You may hit a piece with an exact roll and place
it back on a starting position. You can now roll the
dice again and make another move.
Black field: You may only move to the black target field in
the middle with an exact roll. If you manage this, you can
pick up the piece and place it in front of you. If this was your last piece, you win!

You can play Kirlo in several variants with different levels of difficulty. Kirlo is a game with simple rules and a short playing time, lots of interaction, lots of fun and exciting to the end.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Chronicles of Avel: Animal Companions – Unicorns
description:
Packaged inside the publisher's new game, Dungeon Legends.
Four unicorns are added in this mini expansion. Each unicorn has a special ability.

Victory Rummy is played with 63 cards made up of dots (10), dashes (4), and V cards (4) in each of three colors (red, white, and blue).

The object of the game is to create combinations of cards to create Victory or Grand Victory sequences.

First person to score 250 points after creating a Grand Victory wins the game.

boardgame: Old School Tactical: The Khalkhin Gol
description:
6 scenario expansion for the Pacific Volume 3 & Eastern Front Volume 1 (1st or 2nd editions). Covers the fighting between Japan and Russia in 1939 in the disputed border region along the river, The Khalkhin Gol.

Scenario 1: Forgotten Outpost, Border region, Manchuria. May 20th, 1939

Scenario 2: Bain Tsagan Heights, Border region, Manchuria. July 3rd, 1939

Scenario 3: Poking the Bear, Border region, Manchuria. July 3rd, 1939

Scenario 4: Paper Tigers, Border region, Manchuria. July 26th, 1939

Scenario 5: A Bloody Test, Border region, Manchuria. August 3rd, 1939

Scenario 6: The Setting Sun, Border region, Manchuria. August 29th, 1939
family: Series: Old School Tactical (Flying Pig Games)

description:
UNO Junior Move! is a child-friendly adaptation of the classic UNO game, featuring simplified rules and engaging movement mechanics. Players match colors or numbers on cards while navigating a fun gameboard with their pawns. The first player to reach the finish line wins! Perfect for younger players, UNO Junior Move! combines the excitement of UNO with the thrill of a race.

—description from the publisher

Cincogó Egérfogó is a fun memory game for children aged 3 and up. Players take turns flipping cheese cards to collect as many as possible while avoiding the sneaky cat. The game supports 2–4 players and features colorful components, including 32 cheese cards and 4 squeaking mice. Simple rules and engaging gameplay make it perfect for young players and family game nights.

Prepare for the most ambitious and inventive gameplay to date! This expansion merges the mechanics of Hildegard, including Target Cards, into the framework of Spire's End, creating an ultimate fusion of strategies. Additionally, the Shot and Wild Shot Dice from Hildegard are seamlessly incorporated into the Spire's End encounter mechanics, offering a thrilling new way to engage in combat with two new characters.

Marin's Collection is a 38-card expansion that provides a morose new beginning to Spire's End. This character vignette serves as a vital bridge between the stories of Hildegard and Spire's End: Volume 1, enriching the narrative and deepening the lore of both games.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A game about quickly grabbing creatures that are totally different & counting your BEETROOTS
description:
Do you like quickly grabbing things? Getting a bit stressed? Beetroots?

“A game about quickly grabbing creatures that are totally different and counting your beetroots” is a twist on a typical speed-matching game!

Everyone is grabbing their own stack, getting some satisfaction/ownership. 
Rather than looking for matches, you AVOID them! 
You can grab 5 vegetable cards. But when do you do that?

Everyone is grabbing cards as quickly as they can! Cards with creatures may have 4 different heads (0, 1, 2, or 3 eyes) 3 different bodies (2 tentacles, 4 arms, or 6 wings) and 3 bottoms (1 tail, 2 legs, 4 legs).  Each card you grab has to be TOTALLY different to the one just before - if any of the sections are the same, it's a foul!

You also get some cards with root vegetables. Those are totally different to everything else. So they make it easier for you to grab more cards!

However, you cannot grab 2 vegetable cards in a row. And there's a limit! If you grab more than 5 total, it's a foul!

At the end, pass your cards to the player on your left. They will check you didn't foul by collecting 2 similar cards in a row, or take more than 5 vegetable cards. If you didn't foul, you win! But the person with the most cards won the most.

Combine decks to play with more folk.

HCG Pro Bowling Board Game

Enjoy the excitement of the professional bowling tour on your table top. Play an entire tournament or a single match between today’s top bowlers or dozens of all-time greats using dice, charts, and player cards.

Each bowler is rated based on his or her performance in a particular season. Today's top bowlers like Jason Belmonte will reach match play more often and win more tournaments than lesser bowlers.

Bowlers are rated for strike%, one-pin conversion%, spare conversion%, and performance in qualifying play and match play.

Each tournament is rated for scoring difficulty.

Qualifying rounds are conducted using a quick-play format. Match play is conducted using a roll-by-roll format. This enables tournaments to be played swiftly.

The ratings of bowlers included in the all-time great bowler's sets are normalized to produce averages of today's bowlers.

The ratings of bowlers included in the past season sets will produce the averages of those eras.

Apartment Architects is a strategy board game that combines polyomino tile placement and set collection mechanics. Players take on the role of architects designing apartment complexes by arranging Apartment and Amenity tiles on their player mats.
