Victory Points are earned through multiple methods: fulfilling Renter preferences by matching desired scenic views (Happy Family bonuses), forming sets of identical Apartment or Amenity types to gain Reputation, and completing specific objectives to secure Awards. The game includes 114 development tiles (featuring Apartment types like Studio, Condo, Loft, and Penthouse, and Amenities such as Gardens, Playgrounds, and Pools), along with Renter cards, meeples, Reputation tokens, and player mats. Designed for 2-5 players, the game focuses on spatial planning, resource optimization, and set collection to achieve the highest score.

—description from the publisher

Foreshadow is a trick taking game with the twist of having a shared hand in the center of the table that is visible to all players. The hand in the center of the table always leads each trick. Players have to be smart to utilize this hand to gain themselves points and thrust negative points upon their opponents.

—description from the designer

The war is over, the invaders and their fearsome dragons are gone.  As a sign of supremacy, the Emperor has annexed an uninhabited piece of land from the invading country and is now tasking you with populating it.

This is a cooperative scenario for The Colonists designed for two players.  Your goal is to build a "pyramid" of residences on a neutral community board while upgrading its storage and making your own communities autarch.

Unleash your creativity and competitive spirit in Uttarayan - Festival of Kites, a delightful card game that celebrates the art and joy of kite flying. Set against the backdrop of the vibrant Indian festival of Uttarayan, players step into the shoes of skilled kite makers, vying to craft the most stunning and aerodynamic kites.

Strategize your way to success by collecting Paper Cards, Threads (Manja), and Sticks, combining them to create unique kite designs. Each turn presents a critical decision: stockpile essential materials or construct and fly your masterpiece to score points. As the game progresses, the sky fills with your creations, showcasing your ingenuity and craftsmanship.

But beware! Gusty winds and rival players can disrupt your plans. Adapt to changing conditions and outwit your opponents to become the ultimate kite artisan. With its blend of strategy, creativity, and festive charm, Uttarayan is a game where every turn feels like a celebration.

Will you master the wind and claim the title of the champion kite maker? The sky's the limit in Uttarayan - Festival of Kites!

—description from the publisher

Be the last elf standing!
Get ready for a blustery battle in this super snowball fight card game! Have a ball defeating your opponents while special cards to power up your attack.

Players get elf cards depending on the player count. Elf cards have a strength value of 1 to 4. On a players turn they pickup a snowball card and put it on top of a opponents elf card. If the total value of snowball cards are bigger then the elf cards strength, the elf is eliminated from the game.  There are some special cards in the snowball deck which allow a player to play two cards, get a new elf or throw a snowball at one elf of all other players etc.  Last person with a elf in front of them wins.

boardgame: Blanc Manger Coco DUO: Plaisir à deux
description: Plaisir à Deux: “duo” version of Blanc-Manger Coco. Try to beat the deck over 10 rounds. Each time you succeed, you move to the next, more difficult level, while unlocking new, more spicy cards.
family: Versions & Editions: Two-Player Versions of More-Player Games

description:
The party game you can play anywhere

Guess the number - but don't go too high!

Lighten up the mood at any game night, dinner party or couples therapy session. Play it during business meetings, datenight your uncle's funeral - or all three at once!

Gameplay:
One player reads a question (all answers are numbers). The active player must guess a number that is close to - but not higher - than the correct answer. Players take turns either making a guess that is higher than the previous player, or calling out a DARE if they believe the previous player guessed too high. When a Dare is called the answer is checked and the player who was wrong must keep the card for "Silly goose points". Once a player has collected a certain number of cards, the game ends, and the player with the most silly goose points loses. Everyone else wins.
Nearly no table space is required, and the game therefore works well as a party game in nearly any setting.

"How Dare you?" comes with 2 optional advanced rules that spice up the gameplay:

"Doubling": If you guess a number that is at least twice that of the previous player, you gain a doubling card. This card negates one "silly goose point". This variant tempts players to make higher guesses.

"Double Dare": If you are dared, you can either accept the dare and check the answer like normal, or you can reply with a "Double Dare". The player who is wrong will now get double punishment (two cards instead of one). But the player who initiated the dare, now has the option to chicken out and withdraw their dare. If they do, they must instead guess a higher number before the round continues as normal.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Goede Tijden Slechte Tijden Party Quiz
description:
Dutch-only quiz game in trivial pursuit style about the Dutch Soap Opera 'Goede Tijden, Slechte tijden'

Go round the board answering trivia questions, or guessing the word or phrase being drawn or mimed to score points. First to 15 points wins the game. The game is best played in teams, but can also be played head to head.

—user summary

Maak kennis met de wereld van GOEDE TIJDEN, SLECHTE TIJDEN op een originele manier in dit veelzijdige gezelschapsspel. Speel met de hele familie, individueel of in teams.

—description from the publisher

Embark on an adventure with Rychlé šípy to uncover the secrets of Jan Tleskač.

An escape card game inspired by the stories of Rychlé šípy by Jaroslav Foglar.

This escape game is a card-based variation of popular escape room games, where a group of friends or colleagues is locked in a room full of puzzles and must escape within a time limit.

The card game version is also packed with puzzles you need to solve to succeed. And you will either win or fail together—this is a cooperative game, meaning all players work together rather than compete against each other.

Players progress through the deck of cards, unveiling new rules and challenges. Some cards contain puzzles, others offer hints, and some provide items that will come in handy later.

Will you solve the unique puzzles of this adventure in time?

boardgame: Ink & Clues: Secrets of the Holloway Manor
description:
Dive into the gripping mystery and develop your theory about the serial killer's identity and motive. Analyze suspect conversations, letters, articles, evidence, and more. When you're ready, open the solution PDF to check your deductions and calculate your score. Can you solve the case and achieve Legend status?

On September 16th, 2015, four teenagers set out to explore the eerie, long-abandoned Holloway Manor near their small town of Stonebrook. Rumors of ghostly apparitions and hidden secrets had surrounded the mansion for years, drawing the teens in. Inside, they uncover something far worse than they expected: a hidden chamber filled with the remains of multiple victims. Now, as the detective, it's your job to uncover the victims' identities and the dark history behind their fate. The truth lies within the walls of Holloway Manor and the quiet town of Stonebrook.

Digital Product (PDF), instant download. Print and play or play on screen, no internet needed

—description from the designer

Mordon Mord, the Wizard and Lord of the Dark Forces, has kidnapped the daughter of King Satori, the beautiful Griselda. The sorrowful king offers half of his kingdom to anyone who can rescue his daughter from the evil wizard’s grasp. Hundreds of knights have already tried and failed, for the mission is nearly hopeless. The road to the wizard’s castle is unknown and winds through wild lands, where a thousand dangers lie in wait for the brave traveler, from treacherous swamps and venomous spiders to bandit hordes. And even if someone reaches the wizard’s castle, they must outwit the vigilance of his spies, get past the heavy bronze gate, battle Mordon's war bats, and only then will they face the Dark Lord himself. Now, it’s your turn.
Be a warrior, roll the dice, and collect the equipment needed to defeat the wizard and survive the battles along the way through the different lands.

The rules are designed to recreate the small clashes that took place in the Italian wars of independence, from the unfortunate undertaking of King Joachim Murat’s Tolentino (1815), to the Third War of Independence (1866). It focuses on the War of 1848.

It simulates clashes that involve the use of forces from a platoon to two infantry companies on each side, with small cavalry and artillery elements to support.
It includes scenarios:
Ponte di Goito – 8 April 1848
The charge of Pastrengo – 30 April 1848
Night raid: raiders attempt to destroy some artillery positions under construction...

Scale 1 soldier = 1 real soldier
Rules designed for 1/72 but adaptable to 28mm or 15mm

Rules in Italian

Medium: The Hand of Fate invites you to harness your psychic connection and find the word that bridges two cards. Say it together, at the very same moment. The stronger your mind meld, the greater your reward.


This standalone version adds new ways to sync minds and predict the future. Whether playing in a large group or facing Madame Fortuna in the new 2 player variant, the only right answer is what your partner is thinking. Best of all, this game can be combined with any other Medium title to explore limitless possibilities.

In Medium: The Hand of Fate, players take turns pairing up with the person on their left and attempting to find the single word that connects two word cards.

Each turn, you’ll:
•    Play a card face-up in front of your partner, who plays a card next to yours.
•    Together, count down: THREE… TWO… ONE… and say the connecting word—the Medium—at the exact same moment.
•    Match on your first attempt and earn a high-value Mind Meld token. Miss? Your spoken words become the new clues for a second attempt, then a third.

When the Crystal Ball card surfaces, the round ends. The team with the highest combined score wins!

Optional modules—ESP cards and Prediction tokens—add extra twists: steal rival words, ban an opponent’s answer, or bet on your opponents’ fate with the Hand of Fate tokens

—description from the publisher

What is Kombat Kangaroo!? It's a fun card game for 3-5 players where your kangaroos attack other kangaroos with peas!

The aim is to be the last Kard Kombater still hopping. During the game you place Kangaroo cards into Kard Kombat, with both attack and defence values.

Each Kard has a unique kangaroo design which is personally created by the game creator.

boardgame: Solacia 2: The Ice Demon – A Solitaire Fantasy Game
description:
Solacia was at peace until the Ice Demon arrived. He spread evil across the land, conjuring up all kinds of horrors and freezing the land permanently so that nothing would grow. The Queen has called for a group of Heroes to find and kill the Ice Demon, and to return the land of Solacia back to its beautiful former glory.

Solacia 2: The Ice Demon places you in control of that party of Heroes, consisting of Warf, a tough fighter; Erica, a skilled spear thrower; a mysterious Mystic; and Tooth, a huge powerful beast. The game should be played as an Adventure (campaign) which strings together a series of five quests for your party to undertake. Quest selection is randomised to keep the game fresh and different on each Adventure attempt. In addition to random quest selection, the quests themselves play out very differently each time, adding to replayability.
Victory is achieved if you manage to reach and win Quest 5 and destroy the Ice Demon.

This game is a solitaire hex and counter fantasy game. You play the game by controlling what your Heroes do, and the game will provide Enemies and other challenges for your Heroes to face. You will need two, or preferably three, standard six-sided dice to play.

—description from the publisher

Wallace Gordon Parks (January 23, 1913 – September 28, 2007) was the founder, president, and chairman of the National Hot Rod Association, better known as the NHRA. He was instrumental in establishing drag racing as a legitimate amateur and professional motorsport. In 1951, he founded the National Hot Rod Association, which stands today as the largest motorsports sanctioning body in the world. The winners of National Hot Rod Association national events are awarded a trophy statue nicknamed the Wally. Wally Drag Racing is a tribute to Mr. Parks for establishing this awesome sport of drag racing, and is a fun and exciting easy to play solo dice game replaying seasons.

—description from the publisher

Strašák ("Scarecrow") is a children's observation game in which young players try to find the correct color of the wardrobe of a friendly scarecrow on the field among their cards. Their concentration is occasionally disturbed by a flying black crow.

The game is based on cards with a picture of a scarecrow, which always has different colored parts of its clothing. One part is always white.

One card is laid out in the middle of the table, the other cards are in the players' hands and their goal is to get rid of the cards. The laid out card determines what the players will look for. They must always focus on the white part of the clothing and determine by elimination which color is missing on the card - for example, if green is missing and the laid out scarecrow has white pants, they must find a scarecrow with green pants among their cards.

The card found in this way becomes a new laid out card. The change occurs as soon as the card with the crow enters the game.

boardgame: Age of Steam: The NYC & The Erie Canal Express
description:
A magazine insert map published by TraXX for Mainline magazine Issue No. 3 - Spring 2024.

This is an expansion map for Age of Steam that is set in the region of the New York Central railroad and the Erie Canal. This is an express map with fewer turns and less rules for a shorter play length. This expansion has a new phase, Water Movement, that allows players to use the Erie Canal to ship goods between Buffalo and New York City.

Trivial Pursuit Voyage Party is a boardless trivia game featuring 600 questions divided into six categories: Music, Travel, Miscellaneous, Cuisine, Movies, and Who Am I? The game is compact and portable, designed for quick play during travel or social gatherings.

Gameplay
Players use a colored die to determine the category of question to be asked. The objective is to collect a total of six cards. On a player's turn, they roll the die to select a category and are asked a question. If answered correctly, the player may reroll the die and answer a new question. If this second question is also answered correctly, the player gets to keep a card. A player's turn ends immediately upon answering any question incorrectly. The game supports multiple players and has a recommended age of 16+.

Goal
The primary objective is to be the first player to collect six cards. Each card features questions from different themes, requiring players to demonstrate knowledge across various topics. This gameplay dynamic creates a challenging and engaging experience where players must successfully navigate multiple category questions to win cards.

Extraordinary courtiers is a mini-expansion for El reyno. It presents four new personalities with shocking actions that will bring fresh intrigue to your games.

New members of the elite have entered the court scene.


     The Jester, this apparently secondary actor hides in satire his skillfull handling of palatial entanglements.



     The Priestess, a mystic expert in the dark arcane arts, capable of scrutinizing what the future holds.



     The Ambassador, the representative of a lush and rich foreign power, possessor of an irresistible "charisma".



     The Inquisitor, a relentless judge and jury in charge of enforcing the law, with the authority to sign the death sentence of transgressors.


This expansion first appeared as a Stretch Goal from the base game crowdfunding

boardgame: The Ultimate Date Night Game for Fun Couples
description:
What's Inside: This game includes 300 cards, divided into five categories, along with a spinner and instructions. Get ready to have fun together while building bonds of love, intimacy, and friendship.

How to Play: Flick the spinner. Draw from the deck. Then just do what the card says. To win, be the first to collect 25 cards. In terms of date night ideas, it's easier than picking a spot for dinner.

Grab Your Person: This couples game for adults is designed for 2+ players, ages 17+. From sharing new stories to celebrating your history, this game will become one of your new favorite couples activities.

—description from the publisher

Introducing a Target-exclusive fun family game for movie buffs, fans of blockbusters, or has seen any movie! In round one, each team will choose one of their members to go head-to-head in a quick-fire buzzer battle. From ‘Famous trilogies to ‘Movies with a zombie in it’, these two players must think quick, shout out a movie and slap the buzzer app to put pressure on their opponent. The first player to run out of ideas loses the round. Part 2 is triple charades jeopardy, featuring new Movie Hero round. The player that won the buzzer battle picks up six movie cards, gives the hardest three to their opponent and keeps the rest for themselves. They’ll get 30 seconds to get their team to shout out the movies by using three tricky techniques – acting out the film, making up a quote and describing them with just one word. Get them right quick enough and you’ll have a chance to steal from your opponents. Too slow and they might get a chance to steal from you. Spin Master brings the fun home by offering a range of games for adults, card games for adults, adult games, kids toys & games including baby toys, toys for girls, and board games. Collect your favorite stuffed animals, fidget toys, RC cars, action figures, dolls & accessories + more. From special gifts for men to baby & toddler toys for kids at heart, make your birthday gifts a breeze by shopping our diverse catalog selection. Still hungry for more, explore kids games, pool toys, outdoor games, and monster trucks for any time of the year. Get creative with arts and crafts, building toys and RC cars and take the action to the table with jigsaw puzzles for adults or card games.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Mission to Planet Hexx!: 2025 Satellite Pack
description:
The 2025 Satellite Pack is a limited expansion to the Mission to Planet Hexx core game.

Satellite Packs are a natural extension to the game, which already bears a lot of similarities to existing CCG/LCGs. The 2025 Satellite Pack contains twenty-two cards that are balanced and ready to use. The contents can be shuffled into the core game deck or any other balanced custom deck and played immediately.

The print run for this set was very limited to a few hundred. The cards incorporate rules from the existing sets at the time of printing, (so for 2025, this includes the Core Game plus the Beyond expansion game), as well as a few new mechanics. The inside of the pack contains rule reminder text for mechanics such as Boosters, Tokens, Rolling on the Die, Continuous, Hybrid cards, and others, as these rules pertain to the cards inside the pack.

Twenty-two brand new cards. The set includes cards that:


     affect the turn order
     use an additional Ship
     manipulate the Deck
     Upload from an Opponent's Hand
     Move during an Opponent's turn
     shut down Planet abilities
     win instantly!

Basketball Chess is a basketball-themed boardgame that allows you to coach your team's offense and defense and compete against your opponent, similar to a real basketball game. Each game piece has different statistics that determine how well he can make passes and shots. Players can use strategy to set up open shots, or they can shoot half court shots any time and hope to score. With multiple variations and addons to the game (You can combine two boards to play a "Full Court" version), Basketball Chess can turn into a full tabletop game of Basketball.

boardgame: Kamigami Battles: Bahamut, Lord of the North Wind
description:
Released via the Japanime Games patreon.

Bahamut, Lord of the North Wind is promo card # KBP-013

Act: Declare a color. Perform Adjudication. If the primary color of the revealed card is the same as declared, add it to your hand.
React: Pay 1 Energy to add 2 to an Attack.

IntroductionEneda (from Proto-Brythonic: enėd, meaning "breath" or "soul") is a drawless territorial game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square board. The recommended board size is 15 points per side, but boards of 11, 13, 17 or 19 are also valid. There is also a location off the board called the prison. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

This game is based on Anda.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a maximal set of stones of the same color connected orthogonally. A single stone is also a group.
     A supergroup is a maximal set of groups of the same color connected diagonally. A single group is also a supergroup.
     A breath of a supergroup is a maximal set of orthogonally connected points that does not include points occupied by the supergroup.
     A group is smothered if all breaths in its supergroup have at least one path to exactly one enemy supergroup, and that supergroup is the same for all breaths in that group. The path must be composed of orthogonally connected empty points, can be of length zero, and need not be a straight line.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then players alternate. On your turn, perform these actions in the following order:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty point, then move all smothered enemy groups to the prison, followed by moving all smothered friendly groups to the prison. If it is your first turn, place two stones of your color on empty cells on opposite edges.
     Remove an enemy stone from the prison. If the prison contains stones of both colors, remove pairs of stones of opposite colors until only stones of one color remain or none remain.


At the end of your turn, the placed stone may not create a new group unless it changes the board state, and the group of the placed stone cannot smother a group that, in the previous turn, had smothered exactly all of your groups. For better visual clarity, although capturing is mandatory, it is recommended that beginners start playing with optional captures.

				
				
					End of the game:The last player to perform an action wins.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a number of black stones in the prison, and then the second player chooses a side. For handicap games, the weaker player chooses black and begins by placing a number of stones in the prison proportional to the difference in skill between the players. This balancing method is called the komi pie rule.

—description from the designer

A game in Cypriot dialect and 18+. Players need to say Villos Komposto Arxidia Mple Karamele and if that matches the card that they draw, they need to act fast. A lot of special cards make the game very interesting and different! The first board game that Andreas Vasileiadis designed. Not for commercial use, just for personal use.
family: Versions & Editions: Adult Versions of Family-Friendly Games

description:
"Entrepreneurs" experience Bizness type scenarios while trying to gain control of all 5 "Stock Chips" for each Company to gain the Complete Ownership required to win this game. Players may buy each other's Stocks when Landing on owned companies, however Fate cards can cause "Open/Free" stocks to be lost back to the Bank or Players and available again ... Originator: Wishing You Good Luck!

—description from the designer

SURMOUNT 
Introduction: Surmount is a somewhat bold attempt to improve on an already great game; Mark Steere’s Oust. Surmount takes from Oust its fundamental grow larger to capture mechanism and re-imagines it as a game where you can play anywhere you like, even next to your groups. Those familiar with Oust will wonder how you can allow unchecked growth without the game being an automatic first player win. Surmount manages this by allowing players to grow very quickly in a single turn to become as big as an adjacent enemy group. Whereas Oust, in true Steerian fashion, becomes a minefield of either banned placements or legal placements of which many are strategically equivalent, narrowing down the game tree and alienating players with stifling coldness and counter-intuition. Surmount comes from the philosophy that play should be as free as possible, giving players creative opportunities. In General, because the entire board remains open and most moves lead to different strategic outcomes, Surmount can be played competitively at a much smaller size than Oust and makes much better use of space while being inviting to new players with a comparative hotness.

Equipment: Surmount is played on a size 4-7 hex hex board with an unlimited supply of stones in two colors.

Gameplay: Turns alternate starting with Green. A turn consists of a basic move: adding a single stone to any empty cell without capturing, or else adding one or more stones to execute a capture. A turn on which a capture is made is typically broken down into three stages, the initial placement (which contacts at least one enemy group and expands one friendly group on the board), the intermediate placement/s (which make the expanding friendly group larger) and the surmounting placement (which captures an adjacent enemy group by replacing one of its stones).

Initial Placement: The initial placement must contact at least one enemy group while expanding some friendly group already on the board. If you happen to make a move that qualifies as an initial placement, you are not obligated to make further moves and you may conclude your turn as if you were making a basic move.

Intermediate Placements: If you want to make additional placements on your turn, you must end your turn by capturing one of the enemy groups you are adjacent to as a result of the initial placement. This may involve a number of intermediate placements to grow to be the size of one of these groups. If you need to make intermediate placements these are much more constrained than the initial placement in that all intermediate placements must not contact any additional groups of either color! When making the intermediate placements you must expand to be exactly the same size as one of the enemy groups you contact.

Surmounting Placement: The surmounting placement involves expanding into one of the  enemy groups by capturing a stone in it by replacement, thus capturing the entire enemy group and removing it from play. You must create a larger group than the group being captured with the surmounting placement. Like the initial placement, the surmounting placement can also be your only action on a turn, as long as you expand a group into an enemy group that is smaller than the group being created.

Forced Passing: If you have no legal move on your turn, your turn is skipped over. Voluntary passing is strictly forbidden in Surmount.

Tournament/AbstractPlay rule: when capturing a strictly smaller group all adjacent enemy groups smaller than the created group are captured as well
 This rule only exists to prevent a tedious wrap-up phase once the game has already reached a conclusion and it would be polite for one or the other player to resign

—description from the designer

Help Your Neighbor is a family friendly fast paced card and dice game. In this game you roll 2 dice, add them up and turn over the card they add up to. You don't want to roll a 7! The winner is the first to turn over all their cards. A simple game instruction card with a video link is included in the game.

Help Your Neighbor is a great party game and fun for all ages. Takes just minutes to learn. Each game takes around 15 minutes to complete.

Help Your Neighbor plays great for 2-6 players. The game can be expanded with additional games for unlimited amount of players.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer Quest: Cursed City – White Dwarf Exclusive Grombrindal Card
description: The 500th issue of White Dwarf magazine included this Warhammer Quest: Cursed City Fighter Card for Grombrindal, the White Dwarf. A special miniature of Grombrindal was released in conjunction with the 500th issue of the magazine. The magazine also included a special rules for playing with Grombrindal in Warhammer Quest: Cursed City.

A game where you play cards from your hand to aim for the majority in each mission.  
Observe what your opponents play and hold, maneuver strategically, and seek missions that suit you best — all within the theme of space combat units.

—description from the designer

They say everyone has a novel inside them, just waiting to be written. Put the old adage to the test in a race to the creative conclusion in Novel: The Board Game.

Each player chooses an author with asymmetrical talents and one of five fantastic genres. Start with a setting and a scene and boost your abilities with resources like imagination, research, and narrative genius. Boldly write chapters that move your main character through your unique storyline.

But beware. All is not as smooth as it seems as you encounter writer's blocks and conflicts. To win, you'll need to take inspiration from the Muses and create sideplots to gain critical advantage over your authorial opponents.

The end is triggered when the first player writes their final chapter. Have you covered all your plot holes? Is your story true to your genre? Ultimately, the author with the most points takes their place on top of the bestseller list and wins the game!

—description from the publisher

The grand prophecy of Asuholov is a trick-taking game for 2-4 players that takes about 15 minutes to complete. In this game, players must avoid drawing the “Book of Apocalypse” card from a set of eight cards, as drawing this card results in an immediate loss.
 The probability of drawing the “Book of Apocalypse” card from the eight cards is less than 13%. As a prophet, you might think this chance is trivial, but if you lack confidence, you can use your hand cards to peek at the cards in play or force others to take the cards.
 However, as the game progresses, the probability of drawing the “Book of Apocalypse” increases. Deciding when to use your cards and when to draw becomes a challenging and intriguing aspect of the game. This dilemma adds to the excitement and enjoyment of the game.

German:
Scotland Yard braucht deine Hilfe: Ein Mitglied der Londoner Oberschicht ist bei einem Giftanschlag ums Leben gekommen.

English (user translation):
Scotland Yard needs your help: A member of London high society was poisoned.

Yummy Kitchen is always filled with the delightful scent of various cakes and pastries! Many animals come to this bustling and lively town daily to purchase their favorite pastries. As a pastry chef in this town, you need to prepare the pastries ordered by the animals quickly and try to sell them at a good price.

In this game, players must swiftly fulfill orders to accumulate funds and outshine their competitors. With each round, players need to finish all ordered pastries on their plates and place them in the delivery area for the animals. The pressure mounts as you race against time to secure your earnings. The first player to reach $15 can hit the tap bell and claim victory as the top pastry in town. Are you ready to show your pastry skills? Let’s begin the competition!

'—description from the publisher

In this game, you and your fellow players are thrown into an adventure at a mysteriously ruined magic school. Your mission is to make your way through the magic school's secret corridors, professors' offices and libraries to find an elixir, which is rumored to be the solution.

This game can only be played once.

A surprise party at a lake in 1999 ends in tragedy. One of the partygoers is found brutally murdered in a very strange location.

The game includes: Crime scene investigation, witness interviews, developed camera images, mysterious finds, a map, etc.

Difficulty: Very hard.

A nice wedding in the summer of 2018 turns into a nightmare when one of the guests is found murdered on a balcony. Among the large crowd of guests and staff, a murderer is hiding. But who is it really? And what evidence and clues have the police failed to identify after so many years?

This murder mystery requires access to the Internet via smartphone/computer.

Difficulty: Medium.

On a warm summer evening in 2024, a man is found murdered in the hallway of his home. This after a stalker has plagued the small Swedish town of Jungabro for several months, where the murder victim has been a target, among other things. Who is the stalker? And who committed the murder of Hannes?

The game includes: Police interrogations, lots of pictures, recording of emergency calls, maps, mysterious finds, etc.

This murder mystery requires access to the Internet via smartphone/computer.

Difficulty: Medium.

The year is 2012. In a quiet small Swedish town, a person suddenly disappears. What at first is a mysterious disappearance turns out to hide so much more about some of the inhabitants of the small town. Can you solve the disappearance?

This game is extra long as it contains two parts; first solving a disappearance and then a murder case.

The game includes: Police interrogations, audio recordings of testimonies, detailed maps, crime scene photos, mysterious finds, news clippings, etc.

During a book fair in Örebro, Sweden in 2023, the unthinkable happens.

Sweden's most famous crime writer is murdered on the same day she has a book release for a new crime novel. Horrifyingly, the murder seems to be inspired by her own page-turners. Can you stop this literary assassin before the next chapter is written in blood? Fiction becomes a terrifying reality in this murder mystery.

The game includes: Police interrogation, polaroid photos, preliminary investigation, findings from the crime scene and other mapping material needed to investigate the murder.

The year is 1995. Along a highway in the United States, several murdered people are found during the summer — all of whom have the same symbol tattooed on their forearms. What do the people have in common? And who has killed them and escaped after so many years? What details did the police miss in their investigation? The questions are many, but so are the number of mysterious finds and evidence that exist. It is now up to you to figure out who the murderer is!

The investigation includes: Police interrogations, maps, pictures, mysterious objects, polaroids, news clippings, etc.

This murder mystery requires access to the Internet via smartphone/computer.

The year is 2002. On a cold November night in a forgotten hotel along a highway in Sweden, a murder occurs. A man is found dead in his room early one morning. But who could have wanted him harm? And why did he check in under a false name?

The game includes: Police interogations, pictures, mysterious objects, telephone conversations (recordings), maps, news clippings, etc.

This murder mystery requires access to the Internet via smartphone/computer.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
One Saturday night in 1994, a young man is found murdered in a field in a quiet Swedish village. Almost 30 years later no one knows who murdered him. It's your job as private detectives to finally crack the case.

The game contains: Police interrogations, pictures, mysterious objects, emergency calls, maps, news clippings, etc.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The Advanced Game System IV is an EXPANSION for the famous Board Game: HeroQuest. It is compatible with the core box (new and old edition).

With the Advanced Game System, you can choose a "Class" and "Level Up" your Hero. You can also purchase "Advanced Equipment" during the game.

This deck allows you to play with the following expansions (new and old editions):


     "Against The Ogre Horde"
     "Jungles Of Delthrak"

Welcome to the Arena! As the lead luchador (wrestler), you will guide your team to outcompete your rival with muscle and wits. Tackling, bumping, luring your rival into traps, all cool moves and other tricks guarantee a contest full of twists and turns!

As you embark on this game, players take turns to move their Luchador pieces to Lucha! and try to form as many of their advantage stacks as possible. After moving one of the advantage stacks through several empty spaces until it Lucha, the moved stack will stack onto an opponent's advantage stack and resolve the outcome. The game ends when neither of the players can Lucha! anymore. Then calculate how many cheers each player has received; whoever receives the most cheers wins the game and the Gold Belt!

LUCHA LUCHA 345! encourages players to develop Spatial Awareness Intelligence, which is based on the well-known ‘theory of multiple intelligences’ proposed by American psychologist and Harvard Professor Howard Gardner. Easy to learn but tough to win, this game is perfect for strategic minds and casual players alike!

—description from the publisher

In Journey to The West, the master and his three disciples went through 81 perils with great willpower and determination. In the end, they finally overcame all obstacles and attained the true scriptures.

In Drawing to the West, you need to familiarize yourself with the scenes from the 81 stories and accurately use techniques such as connecting lines, drawing dots, and circling to complete each peril. After overcoming one peril, overlay your drawing board onto the original illustration to verify whether you’ve successfully cleared the level and earned points to achieve victory. The level choice and difficulty of the game are varied and entirely up to your decision! This is a test and challenge of your observation skills and hands-on abilities. Gather your friends and conquer the 81 perils together in Drawing to The West!

An immersive two-player board game where deduction skills and strategic thinking are key to victory. In Werewolf: Duel!, you and your opponent take on changing roles that belong to the Werewolf Faction or the Human Faction, where identities shift and bluffs are everywhere.

Each round, you’ll secretly choose one character card from a hidden hand, each with a unique ability and initiative number. At the same time, you must guess which character your opponent has chosen. The twist? You can spend precious Hourglass Markers to change the initiative, turning a sure defeat into a stunning victory.

The game ends when you deplete your opponent’s health or collect enough tokens for the Werewolf or Human faction. Will you outsmart your opponent, or fall victim to their cunning? Let the duel begin!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Swear Snap: A Card Game for Unashamed Adults
description: This X-rated version of the classic game of Snap is all about quick reactions and shouting obscene language in your opponent's face.

The Crown Prince has sent out a call for help and you are a commoner looking for adventure. You are one of few who become a “Volunteer”...those who decide to answer the call.

The goal sounds simple enough: Make your way to Oakhaven Castle to meet the Crown Prince and hear his request. Ancient scrolls uphold the Curse and by finding the scrolls, they can be destroyed and end the Curse once and for all. However, the last six ancient scrolls are guarded by Dark Guardians, creatures transformed by the Curse to protect the scrolls. No one has been successful in retrieving these last six scrolls, let alone finding them.

As you journey through Oakhaven you will meet others and learn details that help you learn of its history, the Curse and ultimately reach a conclusion about what is really happening within the kingdom. The path through Oakhaven will bring you to a final confrontation where the decisions you've made over the course of the game will change the kingdom forever.

Dispel The Darkness is a cooperative, dungeon-crawling adventure game for 1 to 3 players with a CYOA story-paths that will effect the outcome of the game.

—description from the designer

Pets can be great companions, but they have minds of their own. Work around your attention-seeking pet as you prepare your tea.

Pets 4-card expansion adds a menagerie of adorable pets. Whichever one you have, you’ll need to split your focus between your tea and your pet’s interference.

Choose a random pet and place it above the row of tea cards. Each pet has its own movement pattern as well as its own restriction which makes the game more difficult. Play and score as normal.

It’s impossible to relax when you’re hungry. Grab a sugary snack while you’re waiting for your tea.

Something Sweet 5-card expansion adds a variety of delicious sweets. While a quick sugar boost can help you stay on track while preparing your tea, make sure to finish your snack before you sit down!

Shuffle the new cards and place them nearby, removing one from the deck without looking at it.

Instead of swapping two tea cards on your turn, you may reveal the top sweet card and immediately resolve it. Each sweet has a specific effect that flips, rearranges, or otherwise manipulates your teas and worries.

At the end of the game, lose points for each uneaten sweet.

The sixth release in TANSANFABRIK's Death Game Card series, but a cooperative one rather than attempting to feature player elimination.

Death Game Card: Hack is a binary deduction game. The game includes cards numbered 1 through 15, and one is chosen at random as the number the players need to deduce. Each player is game some of the other cards, and a few are reveled face up.

Players take turns giving the other players clues about the binary digits of their cards. Once the clues have run out, the players each take a turn to declare one of the digits of the hidden card.

Once all of the digits are guesses, if the player are correct, they win!

This is a journaling and stationery game for two 
people played across both space and time. One player 
will be the Reminiscer; the other player will be the 
Rememberer.

To play, the Reminiscer will need a Memory, a 3x5 
or 4x6 photograph, a writing utensil (or few), and a 
stamp appropriate for mailing to the Rememberer. 
The Rememberer must have a mailing address they 
can check regularly.

The Reminiscer places the photograph face up in the 
middle space of the Memory, folds one of the three 
spaces without the tab over the photograph, writes 
a Fragment on the edge of the space, and draws or 
writes in the space using the Fragment as a prompt. 
The paper can be used to trace. The Reminiscer 
repeats this process using the other two spaces 
without tabs using different Fragments. Finally, 
the Reminiscer folds the tabbed space, writes the 
Rememberer’s address on the lines, places a stamp 
in the corner, tapes or glues the tab, and places the 
Memory in the mail.

When the Memory arrives, the Rememberer slices the 
tab and unfolds the spaces slowly, one by one, using 
the Fragments as hints for recalling and processing 
the Memory. As the photograph is revealed, the 
Rememberer can use the Fragments to see the 
Reminiscer’s perspective of the Memory.
If the Memory is processed and spoken or written 
about from Rememberer to Reminiscer, both players 
win.

It is recommended to play with Memories both 
players might enjoy recalling and processing.

A straightforward lock-and-reroll dice game. Players take turns to attempt to roll a combination matching one of the currently available cards.

The game deck consists of 40 cards, each showing a tourist point of interest in Lithuania and a set of 2–4 die rolls that are required to successfully visit it. Cards with 2, 3, 4 dice on them are worth 1, 2, 3 points respectively (this point value is also shown on the card).

3-5 cards (one more than the number of players) are dealt face up and on their turn a player chooses a tourist site card to try to visit. They roll the five dice up to three times, keeping any dice that match the rolls on the card and rerolling the others to try to match the whole card.

Dice that show a 6 at any point cannot be rerolled for the rest of the player's turn. If the player successfully matches every roll on the card, they claim the card and add the corresponding number of points to their score; a new card is dealt face up from the deck. Otherwise the card remains and the turn passes to the next player.

The first player to score 15 points wins.

Dangerous Space is a dungeon crawl experience in futuristic starships.

This solo, roll & write game has a tactical emphasis on dice management and careful placement.

With puzzle-optimizing challenges combined with a hero tech tree, you’ll find yourself racing the clock, dodging enemy laser blasts and confronting some deep space terrors.}

2025 Core Set

The 2025 Core Set introduces 4 more mission types to the original Core Set.

When the lights went out it didn’t take long for civilization to start crumbling. After years of scavenging your fledgling society has found a way to restore power to the settlement. But the evil forces thrive in the darkness and they desire to keep it that way. You’ll need to outrun the Creep before it overtakes your settlement, dooming you and your fellow survivors and maybe all of humanity!

Power Creep offers a new spin on the single-page, six-dice game thrusting you into the role of a survivor who must restore the settlement before an evil force overtakes humanity.

The third game in the Dungeon Pages system takes players into a post-apocalyptic future filled with dangerous wastelanders and an ominous dark threat, and most importantly a lack of stable power. Players will craft items, and attempt to power buildings, giving them long term special abilities that can assist throughout the entire game.

Power Creep features:

     All new structure blueprint and crafting mechanics.
     Streamlined gameplay pulls the best puzzle-like play from Dungeon Pages, combined with the tactical choices of Dangerous Space.

Nuh-Uh! is a fast-paced, frantic trivia game with a twist: as well answering their own questions, players can interrupt each other and steal questions… so long as they’re willing to gamble their own cards in the process.

Adding to the chaos are various “Power Cards”, which grant players special abilities. “Flip-Flop”, for example, allows you to change your answer to a question at the last-minute while “Pride” allows you to double-down on an answer when you’re interrupted.

The game features rulesets for multiple styles of play, including shorter casual games that eschew complicated powers or longer, more competitive set-ups.

The trivia cards themselves cover a wide range of topics with an emphasis on obscure and wacky information. Expect to answer questions concerning the date the first ever selfie was taken, the proportional strength of ants, and which nation consumes the most ketchup annually.

—description from the designer

You can either simply tap the cards with the pen, then the pen tells you information about the individual wonders of nature, or you can start a quiz duel with the Captain Quizzo card. The players take turns to answer questions correctly or find the right cards. They get points for correct answers. Whoever has the most points at the end wins the game.

—user summary

Wie entstehen Jahreszeiten? Was ist ein Tornado und wie schnell kann er werden? Wie entsteht ein Wasserfall? Sammelt in diesem Lernsystem wertvolles Wissen über die Wunder der Natur und vertieft es mit spannenden Quizfragen und Kartenspielen.

Das interaktive Lernspiel für kleine Entdecker: Mit dem tiptoi Stift werden die 36 bunten Spielkarten lebendig. Lernen wird dadurch nicht nur spannend und abwechslungsreich, sondern auch zum Kinderspiel.

Mit dem 3 in 1 Prinzip Lexikonwissen, Quiz- und Kartenspiel erfahren Kinder in diesem Wissensspiel von A bis Z alles über die Wunder der Natur - das ideale Geschenk für Kinder ab 6 Jahren zu Weihnachten oder zum Geburtstag.

Das Wissensspiel Wunder der Natur von 6 bis 10 Jahren für 1 bis 6 Spieler kann mit allen anderen Wissen & Quizzen Spielen von tiptoi kombiniert werden und eignet sich sehr gut für die Autofahrt.

Durch 26 Wissenskarten von A - Z und 10 Sonderkarten ist auch unterwegs lang anhaltender Lernspaß garantiert. tiptoi Stift muss separat erworben werden.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Maladum: Of Ale and Adventure Expansion
description:
This expansion for Maladum builds the story and develops the world around your party. When you visit the town between games, you’ll find a whole host of new characters, equipment and objectives to interact with. Every feature of this expansion builds on the core gameplay and can be integrated into all games of Maladum–even an existing campaign!

- This was included inside the Core Box of the Kickstarter Edition

BE A DETECTIVE: The Gothic Tales, a sadly famous hotel in the small village of Brandonsbury, is the scene of a new mysterious crime. Establish the chronology of events, compile a list of suspects and conduct an investigation to shed light on the case.
A FULL CRIMINAL UNSOLVED CASE FILE: Contains witness statements, brochures, a hotel plan, and much more clues... Everything becomes cryptic killers can be everywhere...
ESCAPE ROOM GAME: Solve puzzles to unlock 4 envelopes containing additional information.
DETECTIVE 2.0: Internet access is required to check the Brandonsbury site, check movements information and more..
A CO-OP MYSTERY GAME: 1-6 players aged 14+, 1.5 – 2 hours per game

—description from the publisher (translated)

DIVENTA UN DETECTIVE: Il Gothic Tales, hotel tristemente celebre del piccolo borgo di Brandonsbury, è il teatro di un nuovo crimine misterioso. Stabilisci la cronologia degli eventi, compila una lista di sospetti e conduci un'indagine per fare luce sul caso. 
IL FILE COMPLETO DI UN CASO CRIMINALE IRRISOLTO: contiene dichiarazioni di testimoni, brochure dell'albergo, una planimetria e molti altri indizi... Tutto diventa criptico, gli assassini possono essere ovunque...
GIOCO ESCAPE ROOM: risolvi gli enigmi per sbloccare 4 buste contenenti informazioni aggiuntive.
DETECTIVE 2.0: è necessario l'accesso a Internet per controllare il sito di Brandonsbury, verificare spostamenti e molto altro. Controlla i suggerimenti se rimani bloccato!
UN GIOCO MISTERO CO-OP: 1-6 giocatori dai 14 anni in su, 1,5 – 2 ore per partita

—description from the publisher

"Is there anybody willing to undertake a journey this summer from Beijing to Paris by automobile?"
Five daring crews with their cars left Peking for Paris on June 10th, 1907, to accomplish this incredible adventure: a 10,000 miles long race over trails and mule tracks, across the mountains of China, the Gobi Desert, and the Russian steppes.
Pékin-Paris is a card-driven boardgame for 2-4 players that puts you behind the steering wheel of a vintage car, starting your journey with very limited resources.
In this fast deck-building game you will use your resources and coins to get new cards, to communicate by telegraph the progress of the race to the Western press, to improve your vehicle's capabilities and to draw a path to Paris through uncharted territories. The driver with the most VPs at the end of the race will be the winner.

—description from the designer

Do you know all the great classics of Italian and non-Italian auteur cinema? Do you never miss previews and reviews and follow festivals and awards dedicated to the seventh art live? Here is the perfect box set for you and for all the cinephiles who want to challenge each other with quizzes. 100 cards with lots of questions about films, plots, actors, directors and curiosities about the beloved and talked about world of cinema.

In Mamutensão, you lead a team of scientists tasked with looking after the mammoths created by genetic engineering. Make efficient use of the resources available to ensure the healthy growth of the mammoths, cure them of possible illnesses, rescue them if they escape from the laboratory and face other challenges.

The goal of the scientific teams is to transport the adult mammoths to Siberia, where they will repopulate the tundras and prevent the catastrophic melting of the permafrost. However, beware: the Mammoth Reaper is on the prowl, ready to take the mammoths to the realm of the phantom pachyderms!

During the game you can carry out some “plans” to delay the other teams and thus make your team earn more points. At the end of the game, the team with the most points for mammoth care, transportation and successful plans will be the winners and we'll meet the new DCM: Chief Scientific Officer!

—description from the publisher

Each turn, the active player can roll 3 green action dice or keep the previous result, but must roll a red event and/or action die. The result of the red die can trigger certain events, such as the death or escape of a mammoth. If it doesn't, it acts as a green die.
Then, from their hand cards, the player chooses one to put down on one of the growing mammoths, representing care for it. The downed cards also bring special actions.
In addition, the player can use the value of the green dice to make the mammoths grow, send the grown mammoths to the tundra or trigger extra actions on the cards.
When a mammoth is sent to the tundra, after it is fully grown, it will bring points to all the players who controlled its care.

Embark on an exciting adventure as a ship captain and conquer the ancient greek islands !

In that game, which takes place in Antiquity, you are the owner of a ship and travel in the Mediterranean Sea. You carry some money with you which you can use to acquire islands and develop agriculture and trading. Protect your islands or set out to conquer new territories with warriors. At the end, the winner will be the one who has amassed the greatest amount of treasures or who has under his protection the greatest number of inhabitants. Make teams before the beginning or during the game, become 'pirate', play a cooperative game...
During the game, face realistic events such as storm, bad harvest, etc... 
Upgrade the Classic Edition with an extra fleet for more playing possibilities and play with up to 8 players !

—description from the designer

Shaffle is a strategic and engaging board game where players aim to create pairs or couples of animals by matching pieces of the same type and colour. The game is played on a 21x21 board divided into 49 Colonies. Each player has 196 pieces that represent 49 different animal types. The goal is to form as many pairs or couples as possible, with rarer animal types earning more points. The game combines elements of hidden information, strategy, and movement to create a unique and challenging experience.
Board: The board is a 21x21 grid, divided into 49 Colonies, each consisting of a 3x3 section of squares.
Pieces: Each player has 196 pieces representing 49 different animal types, each type having 4 pieces.
Animal Categories: Animals are grouped into 7 categories, from Domestic animals to Iconic and Legendary animals, each with a different point value when pairs or couples are created.
Starting Conditions: Pieces are placed face down during the initial dropping phase, and only 8 pieces can be placed in each Colony. The pieces are then flipped over at the end of the dropping phase.
Dropping Phase: Players take turns placing pieces on the board, one piece at a time. A maximum of 8 pieces can be placed in each Colony, leaving one square empty in each Colony. A total of 49 empty squares will remain on the board after the dropping phase. All pieces are placed face down and their identities are hidden.
Flipping Phase: After all pieces are placed, all pieces are flipped over, revealing their identities.
Movement: During the movement phase, players move their pieces orthogonally like a Rook in chess (one or more squares, provided the destination is empty). The goal is to move the pieces strategically to form pairs or couples.
Forming Pairs or Couples: A pair or couple consists of 2 pieces of the same animal type and colour placed in the same Colony. The player who forms a pair or couple scores points based on the animal's group:
Group 1 (Domestic animals) = 1 point
Group 7 (Iconic and Legendary animals) = 7 points
Winning: At the end of the game, players tally the points based on the number of pairs or couples they have formed. The player with the most points wins.

—description from the designer

Incohearent: On the Go! is the travel edition adult party game of Incohearent by What Do You Meme that will get you talking!

Each card has a combination of words on the front that look like nonsense but, when read out loud, sound like the hidden phrase on the back,

Try reciting "boy full leash awe" out loud. Can you hear the hidden phrase? Listen closely... you've been saying "bye felicia!"

Compete to decode the hidden phrase from three categories: pop culture, scandalous and party

Perfect for parties, game nights, holidays, reunions and more.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

description:
An additional hero from the Marvel United: Multiverse Kickstarter campaign.

Contents:
1 Iron Lad figure
12 Iron Lad hero cards
1 Equipment card

Cooperative or Deathmatch gameplay pits you with, or against your friends in a challenging adventure filled with heroes, monsters and magic.

—description from the back of the box

A self-published version of Dungeoneer in a ‘Special Limited Edition’ for $10.

Very different play than the more broadly published Dungeoneer game. Map is made in a three by three grid, and the primary play is player vs player, each with their own card deck. The rules hint at cooperative without really specific rules.

Printed on rough, colored paper, the cards are impossible to easily shuffle. Different boxes contain different character types.

Pirates are searching for a treasure by finding an appropriate tokens. In a player's turn one should flip 3 tokens and may take 1 (if one appropriate is found). Be the first to find all your color tokens to win the game.

Soccatome is a round-table game for any number of players, based largely on the principles of, and terms used in association football. Each player represents a particular football team, and matches are played, for goals, between two opposing teams. From one to four independent and distinct matches can be played simultaneously on one board. Moving on the board is determined by drawing cards. There are some tactical decisions possible. Separate matches may be played, also drives or pool forecast games.

—user summary

This is a game for friends and family, in which your knowledge about the music from the Top 2000 is tested. Object of the game is to score more points which you can win by answering questions about pop music. It's based on the annual Dutch radio and tv program Top 2000, in which the public votes for the best pop songs of all time.

The game contains of 49 question cards, each containing 6 questions, and an extra 5 cards with tie-breaker questions.

There is also a separate Expert edition of this game for the real music knowledge experts.

Minus vs Plus is an abstract strategy game for two players in which the objective is to score 24 or more points before the opponent in while executing addition and subtraction operations between integers ranging from -6 to -1, for one side, and from +1 to +6, for its opposite.

Each player starts the game with twelve dice-shaped pieces of six faces each. Each piece contains integers from -1 to -6 or from +1 to +6 drawn on their faces. The values are distributed among the pieces in such a way that there are only two pieces of each value on each side of the board at the beginning of the game. A player will have the positive pieces and his opponent the negative ones.

The players will take turns to move their pieces on the board and to perform addition or subtraction operations between the values of their respective pieces while scoring points with each subtraction and with each displacement that ends in the last row of the board. In this way, their respective strengths are wearing out and, at the end of the game, the player who has reached the score of 24 points first will be the winner.

It will also be possible, for each player, to regain strength in the game by capturing neutral pieces of zero value, which will appear when two pieces with the same absolute value are subtracted from each other. It will be important to make an appropriate assessment of the game in each situation and manage the resources well to win, because an initial disadvantage in the score can be traced if the resources have been wisely managed during the game.

—description from the designer
designers: Alejandro González de las Peñas Lima

description:
Birds as it is colloquially known, is an Australian bird-themed board game with elements of strategy, sabotage and luck. The goal is to win hands and collect points to get your piece to the end of the game board (80+ points) first.

There are 105 cards with different Australian birds and these have all been given two rankings; their fighting value and their twitching value. You will be dealt 7 cards that makes up your flock for two hands before being replenished. You will use two birds per hand to try and have the highest combined fight or twitch across your cards. in the centre of the board is a spinning Wedgie that will determine in a 50/50 spin whether twitch or fight wins the points. There are 9 Power birds that change the nature of each hand.

The Rulebook describes the game in four levels, each adding skill and complexity. The video tutorial jumps straight into playing at level 4.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Borrowing The Army: Napoleonic is a conversion ruleset for my grand tactical American Civil War ruleset Borrowing the Army. 
Please note, you will need the base game to play this. There are four pages of rules additions or changes to show the operational and tactical changes from warfare in 1861 to the fighting fifty years earlier. 
Railroads are out, while crucial roads, heavy cavalry and Guards units are in. 
I have also changed rules to include Napoleon himself as a commander, and to reflect the fact that close combat in the Napoleonic era was often at the point of the sword.

Of course, just including a ruleset without the means of testing it wouldn't be as fun. Therefore, you also get the scenario Nearest Run Thing to refight the Hundred Days campaign which culminated at Waterloo. Orders of battle for the Allies Coalition (led by Wellesley and Blücher) and the French (under Napoleon himself) are available, along with a campaign map to fight two weeks of combat. 
Can you win a more significant victory that Wellington and Marshal Forwards at Waterloo, or as Napoleon will you defeat each Coalition army separately, buying more time to hold back the Coalition hordes?

—description from the designer

Descend to the glacial planet GSC097011-002209 and into the perpetual whiteout, in search of the legendary corpses of giants upon which the Harvesters feast. Beware. The ice is thin and runs red. This is a new campaign expansion, introducing a new narrative campaign, new intruders, a new boss, new spacers and suits, new gear, new locations and much much more. All content from this expansion can also be used in modular custom campaigns.

The Hungarian "exlusive promo cards" includes 22 cards from the three available promo packs:
- Exlusive Promo Cards (without scenario cards, only the 10 Deep cards)
- The Deep – Promo Pack 1 (6 Deep cards)
- The Deep – Promo Pack 2 (6 Deep cards)

The cards are non-foiled.

Recruit reinforcements to the battle with 4 additional gods and 4 additional monsters! These characters boast advanced abilities such as adding another character to the board during the game or pressuring your opponent with an alternate victory condition. Add the Gates module to your play session to spice up the board with powerful tile abilities. When a character passes through a gate, they trigger its power.

—description from the publisher
Double your instrument variety with 6 more instruments (3 double-sided boards) with unique scoring objectives!

The Festivals Expansion includes a double-sided game board featuring two additional festivals: The Longchain Beach Annual and Point City's Jazz'n' Juke.

—description from the publisher

In Rat King, you'll compete to dethrone or defend the tyrannical Rat King. All the while, you must either hide your identity as a Conspirator, sniff out schemes as a Loyalist, or execute suspicious players as the Rat King himself.

The game centers on the Offering, a row of hidden cards placed over the course of a round. Loyalists must try to place Shields into the Offering, while Conspirators must sneak Blades. If the Offering is revealed and every Blade in the Offering is blocked by a Shield, the Loyalists and the Rat King win - otherwise, the Conspirators have achieved victory.

Alongside Offering cards, use Action cards and Hand cards to gain information, get new cards, or even change your hidden role.

—description from the publisher

Play, learn and challenge with Número Perdido (lost number).

A card game that will test your numerical skills. Take on your friends in exciting turns where mental calculation and strategy are the key.

Win rounds by solving operations and show who is the real finder of the Número Perdido.

—description from the publisher

Using number cards and mathematical symbols you have to get a previously specified number, or if the exact number is not possible, get as close as possible without going over.

Welcome to Agrabah, a city of a thousand wonders, dizzying adventures and cunning intrigues! Your task is to succeed at the court of the great sultan, earning glory points. The heroes of the film will help you with this: brave Aladdin, willful Jasmine, roguish Abu, faithful Dalia, insidious Jafar and the all-powerful Genie. Decide which of the heroes to turn to for help, and try to guess who your opponents will choose: this often determines whether you will be able to carry out your plans.

Each turn, players play hero cards that allow them to receive treasures and glory points.
The game ends when at least one player has scored
15 points. The winner is the one with the most glory points.

Добро пожаловать в Аграбу – город тысячи чудес, головокружительных приключений и хитроумных интриг! Ваша задача – добиться успеха при дворе великого султана, зарабатывая очки славы. В этом вам помогут герои фильма: смелый Аладдин, своевольная Жасмин, плутоватый Абу, верная Далия, коварный Джафар и всемогущий Джинн. Решайте, к кому из героев обратиться за помощью, и попытайтесь угадать, кого выберут соперники: от этого часто зависит, удастся ли вам осуществить задуманное.

This promo pack contains 4 additional tribes and rules. It was a giveaway for buying the game on release.

The Cat Monks places additional cards in the Ally Pool and you may take up to two of them. But a party of Cat Monks score points at the end of the Age like they were one card smaller

The Platypus Sailors come with an additional board (like the Merfolk from the first edition). Whenever you play a Platypus Sailor party you advance on their board. You can score extra points at the end of the Age or trigger bonus spaces, where you can move control markers from one region to another.

The Opossum clan works like the Haflings from the original game. They have twice as many cards as the other tribes, but you are not allowed to place any control marker with a Opossum clan party.

The Mole Miners work like the Fairy promo from the original game. You may switch your Mole Miner Party for an already played party same size or smaller, and use their special effect instead.

In this boardgame, The Three Wise Men, Santa Claus, Tió de Nadal, Olentzero and Pendigueiro will participate in a friendly competition in order to see who is the most important character of Christmas, specially when refearing to send gifts.

The game includes a solitary version.

You will play the game through 5 rounds, with 4 actions in each one. With your action, you will pick between "collecting cards" or "doing actions".

The types of cards you can collect are wrapping paper, items, energy or extra actions.

The actions you can do are: wrapping, decorating or delivering gifts.

Earn more points than the rest by delivering gifts and taking advantage of the bonuses of the round, your character or special cards.

boardgame: はらぺこオバケは料理がまてない (Hungry ghost can't wait for food)
description:
Paul's preferences and waiting time change every time

The person who drew dishes from the deck and was the first to make a menu with the desired dishes within the time limit wins! The winner will receive a thank-you dessert from Paul.

Repeat 5 rounds and the winner is the one with the highest score from the collected desserts!

Summoner's Rift was created under Riot Games' "Legal Jibber Jabber" policy using assets owned by Riot Games.  Riot Games does not endorse or sponsor this project.

Summoner's Rift is the board game implementation of the popular video game League of Legends.

The game is played in a team-vs-team (MOBA-like) setting, where teams compete against each other, primarily aiming to secure objectives to upgrade their champions more efficiently than the enemy.

The team that defeats the Elder Dragon wins the game, although matches often end earlier with one team choosing to surrender.

The card game for more humor and joy in the Christian faith - with original quotes from the Bible. Perfect for game nights, small groups and family fun.

Each round, one player takes the role of judge and plays a statement card, after which everyone else plays a Bible quotation card as answer. The judge decides the funniest pairing, and whoever played it wins the round.

Repeat until one player scores 5 points in total.

Tancas is a negotiation game, close to Monopoly, set in Sardinia. Players will be able to travel their pawns through towns and locations in Sardinia, buy land and build buildings to collect income. The objective of the game is to become the richest at the table.

Trickster Gods is a trick-taking game where players ally themselves with trickster gods from around the world to manipulate the fundamental law governing the universe--the cosmic scoring equation.

By winning tricks, players will be given the choice between adding cards to their sets or using cards to alter the scoring of those sets. By leveraging clever play and algebraic trickery, prove yourself worthy of the pantheon!

—description from the designer

Not all wars end, nor do the good guys always win. There are eternal battles where sometimes the bad guys win. Good versus evil is one of these endless confrontations... until today:
The day when you can put an end to this endless bickering, or let it continue again and again until the end of time.
Good & Evil is a simple and fun, fast-paced card game in which 2 players choose a side—Angel or Devil—to play against each other. The goal is to capture as many enemies in their cages as possible.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Become a wizard who summons Ijin from around the world and create a deck that is stronger than your rivals!
Ijinden is a competitive trading card game. Summon Ijin from all over the world, win battles, and win the game.
Each player will prepare their own deck.
A deck is a pile of Ijinden cards.
When making a deck, please follow the following rules.

     Make a deck with 40 or more cards.
     You can put up to four cards with the same card name.


In this game, you aim to directly attack your opponent while supporting Ijin summoned with Marik with Mahou and Hikei. Block your opponent's attacks with Ijin and Guardians and aim to win with your own deck!
You win the game if your attacker's attack is not blocked in the battle of your turn. Or, you win the game if your opponent has no cards in their deck when their turn starts.

—description from the publisher (translated)

世界のイジンを召喚する魔法使いとなって、ライバルよりも強いデッキを作りましょう！
イジンデンは対戦型のトレーディングカードゲームです。世界中で活躍していたイジンたちを召喚し、バトルを制してゲームに勝利しましょう。
プレイヤーはそれぞれ1つずつ、自分のデッキを用意します。
デッキとは、イジンデンのカードを集めた束のことです。
デッキを作る際は、次のルールを守ってください。
●40枚以上のカードで作ります。
●カード名が同じカードは4枚まで入れられます。
このゲームはマリョクで召喚したイジンをマホウやハイケイでサポートしながら、対戦相手に直接攻撃することを目指します。相手の攻撃はイジンやガーディアンで防ぎ、自分のデッキで勝利を目指しましょう！
自分のターンのバトルで、アタッカーの攻撃を防がれなければゲームに勝利します。または、相手のターンが始まるときに、相手の山札にカードが1枚も無ければゲームに勝利します。

—description from the publisher

Mágikus erdő ("Magic Forest") invites you on a fun-filled adventure as you find homes for all the magical creatures in this enchanted woodland! And it’s not going to be easy, because these new residents are really fussy about who lives next door! Can you find the perfect spot for all these special creatures?

During the game, players draw six creature tiles from a bag and simultaneously choose two to place immediately on any empty spaces on their 6x6 forest board. Then, the remaining tiles are passed to the player sitting next to them. This process is repeated once more, so all tiles are placed on the boards. After that, everyone draws six new tiles, and the game continues.

The game ends when the bag is empty, and all players have completely filled their 6x6 forest boards. At the end of the game, players score points based on which creatures they placed and where they placed them. (For example, a witch is worth 4 points on its own, but if placed next to another witch, it scores 0 points.) The player with the most points wins the game.

boardgame: Kircholm: Riga in the Balance 27 September 1605
description:
The Polish-Swedish War (1600-1611) was five years old when Sweden's Charles IX led an army to besiege the Latvian port of Riga. The bickering Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth cobbled together a relief force, which took station near the town of Kircholm. Charles put his army through a miserable night march to find Poles awaiting him.

Kircholm uses a simplified variant of the Pike and Shot system to portray combat. Massive pike blocks form the basis of each army, with thin lines of musketeers for firepower and tightly packed cavalry for shock power. Artillery is only an adjunct-powerful but immobile. Battles focused on maintaining the dense formations. Once broken a unit was easy pickings, a single broken unit could snowball quickly into an ignominious rout.

—description from the back of the box
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: クイズ　トリックテイキング (Quiz Trick Taking)
description:
In Quiz Trick Taking, the players are tasked with determining what seat wins the last trick.

The game uses a subset of a standard deck of cards - that you need to provide. An area is set aside for the different players and rules for the hand, and then cards are dealt face up in a grid - showing you the cards that each player played in each trick.

The rules for a hand are randomly determined - a tie break, a trump suit, and 3 additional rules (e.g. lowest number wins.) The additional rules are revealed simultaneously and the players race to mark their guess, with the earlier correct players earning more points.

The first player to meet a certain threshold after a series of rounds wins.

It’s a monster mash-up! Construct colorful creatures to match the pictures on each card. For an added challenge, hide the pictures and build from memory. Then do the Monster Mambo - arms and legs wiggle so you can boogie-woogie with your beasts!

Object: use your keen sense of observation to assemble monsters (solo or with a parent) to match the images on the cards!

How to play: to start, select one of the monster cards. Once you’ve chosen, begin pulling aside the pieces to assemble your monster. But look closely, each piece is double-sided, and some pieces that look similar aren’t actually the same.
after you’ve assembled your monster, compare it with the illustration on the cards to see if they match.

“Memory” Variant: for an extra challenge, once you’ve picked your monster cards, look at it for only 10 seconds then turn it face down. Assemble your monster, then turn the card over and compare

“Cooperative” Variant: grab a buddy and see if the he two of you can assemble the monster together

Heal or Steal introduces exciting new mechanics to the game, allowing players to strategically heal themselves or steal from opponents. This expansion also includes six unique custom class dice, adding variety and replayability to every game. No two matches will ever feel the same!

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Hell in Microcosm. 
This set of rules has the battalion as the manoeuvre unit. Stiffened and supported by the specialist troops from within the division, the battalions are the cutting edge of your force. Aimed at 6mm and 2mm figures, once you get used to the rules you can contemplate deploying divisions.

When we originally used these rules, we fought battles ranging in time from the first months of the First World War to Vietnam. You can try and smash your enemy’s trench lines with artillery before launching yet another attack, or you can drop your blocking force into place with helicopters (playing the Ride of the Valkyries is purely optional).

You can have beach landings, armoured trains, and cavalry. You can bomb towns to stop the enemy advancing through them, or infiltrate your irregulars through weakly held parts of the front.

But now in this second edition I took the plunge and brought in a science fiction section as well. Whilst you can use with rules without ever turning to the Sci-Fi section; if you want you can now add mecha, giant war machines, droids, and drones to the mix. Indeed I’ve looked at transporting troops and landing them on planets who don’t necessarily want you there. So we have the systems in place to help you invade whole new worlds.

Finally there are even rules for fighting your battles directly on a 1:50,000 or similar map. We found that this gave you terrain far more intricate and interesting than anything you might produce on a wargames table.

—description from the publisher

Detention is a comedy/horror card based dungeon crawler. Two players take on the role of students trying to make their way through a series of monster infested school rooms. You and one other player will work together to win the game by making it through the school before you run out of supplies and get trapped!
