This is a two player cooperative game inspired by horror and teen movies. There are four decks of cards. Each player chooses a character card. Each character card has a special ability. A player's hand is 3 cards. There is a dungeon of 5 room cards. Each turn, a monster card is placed on a room card. Players place items together to defeat the monster and move to the next room. The game is won by clearing all of the rooms of monsters. The game is lost if you run out of cards before all the rooms have been cleared.

This is an expansion for Die Siedler von Catan (The Settlers of Catan). This scenario was  first released as this promotional poster and went on to be included in Die Siedler von Catan: Historische Szenarien in 1998. Play requires bits from the base game and the Seafarers expansion.

The scenario simulates the pharaoh Cheops commissioning the royal families (the players) to build a great pyramid. Players build trade routes, collect resources (including gold) and contribute pyramid blocks to the great work. Cheops awards extra points to the players contributing the most and the least to the pyramid.

Star Trek: Tribble Match can be played like a regular memory game, with players flipping over two tiles at a time in search of a match, which allows them to claim the tiles. Whoever collects the most tiles wins.

Alternatively, you can play with tribble rules, with players trying to collect the fewest tribbles from the tiles that they pick up.

This game requires dexterity. The ball is a chip that the players flip into the goal.
This game is the version in which you compete with 7 players per team instead of 11 players like in the original version of the game.

In a utopian future, financial criminals face an otherworldly punishment: a one-way journey to outer space. As a cunning travel agent, your role is to orchestrate the bookings for these fortunate lawbreakers. Aim high, for the richest offenders bring the most lucrative commissions. In Yeet the Rich, your stellar skills and strategic maneuvers determine who sails among the stars.

Players play cards to move possible launch victims closer of farther from the end of the line. When a rich jerk gets to the end of the line, players collect them to their pile and earn points based on how big of a jerk they were. Players manipulate the line, play power cards, and stop their opponents from collecting the most valuable victims for three rounds and then scores are totaled. The player who launched the biggest jerks wins.

boardgame: Ostia: Pirates Expansion – New Supporters Expansion
description:
Add on during the Ostia Kickstarter campaign.

4 tiles (1 side is in English, the other Japanese)

2 Homecity Tiles:
Petra (disgrace 1) - When you Corbita stops on the Transit tile, double the benefots of the transit tile.
Syracuse (disgrace 0) - Production Phase: Each Ponta ship produces 3 resources instead of 2.

2 Supporter Tiles:
Cornelius Palma (disgrace 2) - When you repel a pirate ship: You may pay 3 gold to place 1 resource cube on this tile. Each cube on this tile is worth 6 VP at the end of the game.
Trajanus (disgrace 1) - Only useable with the Merchants expansion. During setup, place a value 1 resource cube in the 0 space of this tile and take an unused destination tile.
Place the destination tile to the right of this tile. If you perform the trade action during the game, move the cube on this tile one space to the right. If you perform the trade action with the cube on the 4 space, move the cube to your personal destination tile. If you have a cube on your personal destination tile at the end of the game, you gain VP as shown in the upper side of the tile.

Four is a level-based card game designed to be simple enough for a child to enjoy and fast-paced enough to play a couple games over lunch at the office.

Collect Points Cards to advance from Level 1 to Level 4. Use Action Cards to help yourself or sabotage other players. The first player to reach Level 4 wins the game.

—description from the designer

Have you always dreamed of defeating the Bandit but never managed to do so? Have you managed to win a game in 20?

Your time has come. Now go!

To give you a little help in your games, the promotional cards "Now Go!" have only 4 exits (unlike the original game where there are 5 or 6 exits) and greatly improve your chances of capturing the bandit.

Use one of the 5 "Now Go!" cards as the starting card of your game, instead of the original card and try to win with this "little help".

Do you play with children? Use the cards to simplify your games!

Numbered from 1 to 5, these cards are distributed separately. Collect them all!

Step into the ancient world of the Mahabharata with Chakravyuha, a strategy board game inspired by the legendary battle formation. This asymmetric game pits daring infiltrators against the wily orchestrator, recreating the tension and tactics of the fabled labyrinth.

Game Overview:
In Chakravyuha, players embody the roles of infiltrators striving to breach the spiral formation and a single orchestrator defending its heart. The game masterfully combines elements of strategy, bravery, and manipulation of space. Every move counts as the infiltrators navigate layers of defense, trying to create gaps and press their advantage, while the orchestrator counters with soldiers, formations, and special powers to trap the invaders.

Key Features:

Asymmetric Gameplay: One orchestrator vs. up to three infiltrators. Each side has unique abilities, objectives, and strategies.
Dynamic Spiral Formation: The board evolves with every turn as soldiers move to close gaps or intercept infiltrators.
Power-Up Cards: Both sides wield game-changing powers like health boosts, new soldiers, and surprise moves, adding layers of unpredictability and depth.
Dice-Based Actions: Strategically roll and allocate dice to attack, defend, or move. Critical decisions are key to victory.
Historical Immersion: The game's theme and mechanics are steeped in the lore of the Mahabharata, capturing the valor of Abhimanyu and the brilliance of the Chakravyuha.
How to Win:

Infiltrators: Break through the spiral, reach the center, and confront the orchestrator.
Orchestrator: Defend the formation by outlasting or incapacitating the infiltrators.
Gameplay Highlights:
Every turn is a balance of risk and reward. Will the infiltrators focus on creating gaps to advance or conserving health for a final push? Can the orchestrator predict their moves and adapt defenses? The tension rises as the game progresses, with each layer of the Chakravyuha peeling away under the players' strategic choices.

Why You'll Love It:
Chakravyuha is perfect for fans of tactical games, mythology enthusiasts, and anyone seeking an intense, immersive experience. Its rich narrative foundation and innovative mechanics ensure that no two games feel the same.

Join the Battle:
Whether you stand as the orchestrator, weaving a nearly indestructible defense, or as the infiltrators, embodying courage and cleverness, Chakravyuha promises a gaming experience like no other. Can you outwit the maze or hold its secrets till the end? The challenge awaits!

—description from the designer

Neural connection refers to the way neurons (nerve ells) in the brain communicate with each other through electrical and chemical signals. These connections allow information to be transmitted within the brain, enabling functions such as cognition, movement, emotion, and memory.

Components
17 white pieces, 17 blue pieces, 1 game board, 1 Rules

Preparation
Place white pieces on the board where there are white dots, and blue pieces on the board where there are blue dots (10 of each). The remaining 7 pieces are kept in front of each player.

The Object of this game:
The white player aims to connect the opposing sides with their pieces vertically, and the blue player aims to connect the opposing sides horizontally. The moment you connect, excitatory neurotransmitters flow in your brain and you win.

How of play
The player whose neuron was most recently connected go first. If you are unsure, just decide. The player who goes first use the white pieces, and the player who goes second use the blue pieces.

Deployment Phase: For the first 7 moves, players take turns placing your pieces in any empty space on the board.

Movement Phase: Once all of your pieces have been placed, you move your pieces. Moves can be done by sliding or jumping.

Slide Move
Move in a straight line any number of spaces (same
move as a queen in chess). You cannot jump over other pieces.

Jump Move
Jump over adjacent pieces and land on the next
empty space in a straight line. You can jump over any number of pieces (your own or your opponent's).

Reel-Time Pro Fishin’ Game is designed to re-create the feel of real-life fishing. The “tournament” setting is mainly for competitive reasons. At its core, the game is about catching fish, by yourself or with others. The bigger the fish, the bigger the thrill. The more fish you catch, the bigger your boast! Use the items in your tackle box wisely in order to maximize your catch.

—description from the publisher

A fun bingo game from orchard toys with 3D food items.
The first player to cover their board in food items is the winner.

Wasted Worms is a push-your-luck card game of "higher-lower", where players try to get rid of their deck of cards, playing them blind on the "bar". But if you guess incorrectly, you get all cards on the bar and your turn ends. You can guess up to 5 times. But there are also upgrades, downgrades and other "take that" cards in the game that give the game a fun, competitive, party game feel.

Asinel is a strategic 10x10 board game where players lead an army of animal-themed pieces, each moving like their chess counterparts, to guide their Donkeys safely to the opponent’s last rank. Each player begins with 24 pieces: 10 Squirrels (Pawns), 4 Cats (non-Royal Kings), 2 Dogs (Bishops), 2 Wolves (Rooks), 2 Hunters (Queens), and 4 Donkeys (Knights). The Donkeys, carrying a valuable load, are central to victory and must reach the opponent’s final rank. Squirrels, which move like pawns, can promote to any piece except Donkeys when they reach the last rank.

Pieces are placed in three rows: the third row holds the Squirrels, the second row contains all other pieces except Donkeys, and the first row is reserved for the Donkeys. Victory is achieved by advancing a Donkey to the last rank or capturing all opposing Donkeys. If only one Donkey remains for a player, it becomes Royal—immune to capture but subject to checkmate. All other pieces can be captured, including the powerful Hunters. Cats move like chess Kings but are not Royal, serving as defensive and tactical pieces. Asinel combines familiar chess mechanics with a unique race and survival element, creating a thrilling game of strategy and suspense.

The name Asinel combines the Italian word for Donkey (Asino) with the Romanian diminutive suffix "-el", which can mean "of God" or "Godly" in Hebrew. This choice adds a sacred association to the Donkey, emphasizing its pivotal role in the game. While Donkeys are humble in nature, their significance is elevated, symbolizing something essential and almost divine in their importance to the strategy. The name reflects both the Donkey’s importance and its sacred connection within the game.

boardgame: Host Your Own Murder Mystery: At the Circus
description:
"Roll up, roll up and join us for an unforgettable night at the world-famous Starlight Circus!" The lights are low and the acts are limbering up under the Big Top - but who is lurking in the shadows? The real show is only just beginning as a trick of hand turns a night at the circus from dazzling to deadly. Has anyone seen Rollo Upp the ringmaster? A thrilling activity for you and your friends, host a murder mystery night at home with this circus themed murder mystery game kit. Stretch your muscles and your mind as you and your guests piece together the clues to identify the cunning killer amongst you.

—description from the publisher

Happy Haven is the first game that is scientifically proven to make people happier.

Can you replace the lifeless desert with a flourishing oasis? Complete challenges, connect with others and work together to create your own Happy Haven.
The creator of the game conducted a real research study, measuring players' happiness before and after playing the game.

The statistical analysis that followed showed significant improvements in players' happiness, making Happy Haven the first game that is scientifically proven to make people happier!

In Happy Haven, players cooperate to complete tasks from 5 different categories of cards. All cards are shuffled, and players flip the top card and complete the task on the card. Tasks include a mix of group challenges and conversation prompts. Once tasks are completed, the card is placed face down on the board in the correct slot based on its category.

The back of the cards on the board slowly create the image of a thriving oasis in the desert. If players cooperatively complete the oasis before they fail two blue cards, the group wins!

—description from the designer

Are you ready to pick the souls?

You should calculate each move carefully and pick the souls from monsters as quickly as possible by using your inventory cards and your character's special power.

Variable map design will push you to different strategies.
Build a different strategy every game!

The first player to come to the starting square with 3 souls wins the game!

—description from the publisher

Tricky Dragons: Where Strategy Meets Chaos!

Step into a world of Tricky Dragons, unpredictable twists, and thrilling battles! In Tricky Dragons, 2-6 players compete to outsmart their opponents by mastering the power of Fire, Water, and Grass. Each turn brings a new challenge as players draw cards, unleash game-changing effects, and aim to be the first to score 7 points.

But beware—the valley’s dragons are as tricky as the players! With 100+ cards and 50+ unique effects, no two games are ever the same. Every play is a mix of strategy, surprise, and a dash of chaos that keeps everyone on their toes.

—description from the designer

Elements Tricks is a trick-taking game where players want to win tricks containing the cards worth the most points, and lower ranked cards are worth more points.

Each suit has a specific hierarchy and adjusts the order of suit strengths for the trick. Additionally, this hierarchy determines which suits a player may not follow the lead suit with.

The player with the most points after a series of rounds wins.

The Six is a fast paced card collection game. The aim is to be the first to collect 6 of the same character to win, with the current characters being spooky themed.

2 to 6 players can play the game and there are 88 cards in a standard deck. Each player starts the game with 6 cards in their hand, drawing from the main deck, discarding cards and playing action cards to prevent other players from winning.

—description from the designer

Spela Snabbare ("Play Faster") is a Party Game for people who like punk, metal and hardrock. During the game you listen to music and create timelines. Each player has two timelines which are separate from each other. The first timeline has a starting card on it with a starting year, and by placing a card correctly here they receive 3 points. The second timeline has no starting card which makes it easier to place cards, but you only receive 2 points for placement.

Each round of the game the player to the right of the active player assumes the role of DJ, scans the QR code of the topmost card in the draw pile with his phone, which then plays a song via Spotify (account needed).The active player then places the card on either of his timelines. If he also guesses artist+songtitle correctly he receives an bonus point in the form of a guitar pick. Then the card is flipped to reveal the songs year of release. If the year is correct, the card stays, otherwise it is discarded.

During listening the DJ may use either of his two DJ cards, green or red, to guess if the active player will correctly guess artist+songtitle. If the DJ's guess is correct, he receives one bonus point. Other players may also use their "Spela Snabbare"-card during listening. If they do, and the active players artist+songtitle guess is wrong, they get to guess as well.

The first player to reach 30 points is the winner.

Slow and deliberate, you might be forgiven for thinking these lumbering lizards are all brawn and no grace. The truth is, the Matsudan believe in picking their moments – when they commit to a play, their earlier deliberation is replaced by lightning-quick movements. No-one can stand in the way of a Matsudan when they slam into action, pushing their foes about the pitch with thunderous strength.

最善, roughly translated as "Best", is a trick-taking game where hands are only dealt once, and the conditions of the game will be adjusted by the players as the game progresses - and are specific to the suit being led.

In general, larger numbers are stronger, nothing special happens if the same ranks are played, you must follow, and you must play to win.  Players earn one point per trick.

However, at the start of each round, a player can irrevocably shift these conditions for a specific suit. For instance, if spades is led, the trick could become may follow. If hearts is lead, and two players play the same rank, it may trigger those players to play extra cards.

After one hand is complete, the hands are passed to the player on the right and another round is played.

The person with the most points after a series of rounds wins.

In 明暗, roughly translated as Light and Dark, there are no suits and cards show their rank on the front and back.

Players will make a bid from the cards in their hand at the start of the round, and if they win exactly that many tricks, they earn a corresponding amount of points.

Depending upon which side of the card they bid with determines the penalty if they miss their bid.

The person with the most points after a series of rounds wins.

Services Spéciaux - Opération Humint is a competitive Board Game in which 2 to 4 players confront each other during games of roughly 50 minutes.
Each player represents one the major Secret Services: CIA (USA), Guoanbu (China), SVR (Russia) or DGSE (France). In order to win, the players needs to gather new intelligence faster than the others. Each player has two units that can be deployed on the city map by playing cards, and ultimately to secure and recruit new assets (« Human Intelligence »). But of course, other services will do everything to prevent the recruitments!

Negaeri Friends is a trick-taking game where the suits are visible on the rear of the cards and the trump is randomly changed each trick.

When a player follows suit, they can choose to play face up or face down - but a person playing off suit must play face up.  The winner of a trick only collects the face up cards, with face down cards moving to a face down pile in front of the person who played it.

At the end of the hand, all cards won in tricks are worth 1 point. Face down cards are revealed and the person with the highest sum of ranks in each suit earns 2 points.

The person with the most points after a series of rounds wins.

Updated version of Chinese peg game ji'n-shizi (later called "Nim" in English) played on a 9-pointed star board. Players take turns removing pegs (either one peg or two pegs if both are connected by a line on the board), with the loser being the last player to take a peg.  Includes variant rules for playing traditional Nim using the same components.

You and your friends were going on a picnic, but your car died in the middle of nowhere. You find a cabin, but its inhabitants want to eat you! These are real zombies, and some of them seem to have super powers — there must have been some kind of mutation in this group of circus artists. Anyhow, you need to get out of this wilderness now!

In the Zadventure each turn, you flip different tiles on the game board and find weapons and other useful items, but you will also be facing zombies and it is your job to fight them off. In order to win, you need to find the keys and the gas can to start the escape car and flee!

It is a cooperative game, so you either win or lose together.

—description from the publisher

Raise the Risk is a may follow trick-taking game where players can use organ cards to adjust the conditions of the trick - such as the low card winning, rotating cards, or choosing the lead player of the next trick.

Players earn points by collecting the cards with coins on them, and the first person to collect 5 coin cards or have the most after 3 rounds wins.

With about fifty states, and as many armies, it would be impossible to draw up army lists for each African nation and there would be the risk of creating just copies. 
To avoid this problem, the armies have been divided into three generic groups: these categories are not rigid and represent only a general idea to which players can toy with and make changes at will, even through customization through the sets of specials rules provided in this supplement.

Tier 1- Main: Egypt, Algeria, South Africa, Nigeria, Morocco
Tier 2- Main: Ethiopia, Angola, Sudan, Tunisia, Kenya
Tier 3. All the others like Congo, Mali, Chad, Zimbabwe...

—description from the publisher

Army lists for:

Yugoslav People's Army (JNA) 1991-1992
Croatian Defense Council (HVO) - December 1992-1995
Croatian Armed Forces (HV) - December 1991-1995
Armed Forces of Bosnia and Herzegovina ( ARBiH ) - 1992-1995
Army of the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia - 1992-2000
Armed Forces of the Republic of Srpska (VRS) and Krajina (RSK) - 1992-1995
Slovenian Territorial Defense Forces (TSO) 1991
Croatian National Guard (ZNG) - May / November 1991
Kosovo Liberation Army (ELK) 1998-1999
Italian Army (EI) -1990 (To use in What-If scenarios)

UMS: Scenario pack 1 brings four more regulars vs regulars scenarios to the ones already found in UMS: Modern Age corebook:

     Sudden strike
     Raid
     Blind Attack
     Beyond the trenches


Those missions are also compatible with the Yugoslav Wars module.

—description from the publisher

UMS: Dragons and Demons is a module for UMS: Modern Age

Inside Dragons and Demons you can find an accurate point system to assemble and field armies from China, Japan and North and South Korea into your UMS: Modern Age battles!

—description from the publisher

In Trick Taking Dungeon, the cards played to the tricks are placed face down in a grid by the winner of the trick to create a dungeon. Sheets are provided for the players to take notes.

Once all of the tricks have been played, the players take turns exploring the dungeon - revealing swords, monsters, trap, etc.

Once the exploration is finished, players create pools of loot, based upon how many tricks they won in the first phase.  Then, one final trick is played to determine who chooses from the loot first.

The player with the most points wins.
publishers: iop games (イオピーゲームズ)

description:
Exclusive Stretch Goals of Starship Interstellar which include:

     Module Leaders: 5 Interstellar Module Leaders which allow player to unlock special technologies and effects.
     Alternative Suns: 2 Alternative Sun Boards showing 3 Alternative Suns (printed on both sides). In the Alternative Suns variant, the players will find themselves in new situations where they will have to pay more attention to the Solar Spiral and the events linked to our Sun.
     Special Solar Events: 20 new Solar Event Tokens. When you use the Special Solar Events variant, there will be even more pressure on the World Government, as they will have a greater impact on the building of the Interstellar and on the search for the best route to Trappist.
     Blue Print: A 93x35x14 inches poster that can also be used as a play board when one sector of the colossal Starship Interstellar.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Champions of the Hundred Halls: Gadgets & Gauntlets
description:
The Gadgets & Gauntlets expansion for the asymmetrical cooperative strategy card game Champions of the Hundred Halls will add 2 new players, 1 new realm, and 3 new scenarios.  Both base game and expansion are for 1-4 players and include a solo mode.

The game sets up quickly, is simple to learn, and lasts 50-90 minutes with various levels of challenge available.

The Gadgets & Gauntlets expansion puts the spotlight on two new players: Lennie Fane and Hannah Binder.

—description from the publisher

Shoot the puck and strike the lights! Tabletop dexterity game, similar to air hockey, but portable with multiple game modes and objectives. Includes four 2 player modes to be quicker than your opponent, and one solo mode to reset your own records.

This trick-taking card game was copyrighted in 1903 by Jas. W. Tavenner and published in 1904 by the Read & White Game Co. of Bloomington, Illinois.

Cards in the deck have two numbers in the middle and the sum of these two numbers is presented in the corner. Some cards in the deck are special cards important for scoring (Trix, Prize, Prize Trix, and Trixie)

Play begins with the dealer shuffling the deck and dealing four cards face down to each player. The remaining cards are placed face down to form a draw pile. The person to the left of the dealer plays a card face up from their hand and then draws a card from the draw pile. The next player plays a card from their hand, which may have a sum greater or less than the sum on the previous card played. After each person has played one card, the winner of the trick is the person playing the highest sum card - who keeps those cards. The person winning the trick sets aside any special scoring cards and then plays a card to begin the next round.

Play continues as above until all cards have been drawn from the pile and all cards in hand have been played. Each person counts their scoring cards and if no one has reached the required 500 points to win the game all cards shuffled together to continue the game.

The game can also be played with partners.

boardgame: How The Grinch Stole Christmas: Memory Master
description:
The Grinch Memory Master Card Game is a card game for the whole family. Put your memory to the ultimate test with your friends and family at your next party or game night with this incredibly entertaining card game that the whole family can enjoy.

Memorize, switch, match and win. Partner up and compete in a game of memory at home or on the go. 5 Minutes to learn the game, 30 minutes to play, 4 players 2 teams, ages 6 and up.

Players are dealt hands that they must quickly memorize then switch with their partners from which they then select a card that they think will match with each other.

Players compare their chosen card with that of their partner. The goal is to match colour and/or character. Matching either just the colour or just the character scores that team 1 point. Matching both colour and character scores 2 points for that team. No points are awarded if there is no match. Keep track of points using the scorecards provided

First team to achieve 12 points wins!
family: Books: How the Grinch Stole Christmas

description:
Today is a good day to die… for the enemy crew!

Battlefront Valkyrie is a groundbreaking 3D printable wargame that delivers the thrill of epic fleet battles right to your tabletop, all at a cost that’s just a fraction of traditional games!

This expansion set includes:

Large Space Station Wreckage

Small Space Station Wreckage

Kurgun Sensor Satellite

Terran Sensor Satellite

Two New Game Scenarios

Technology Card Deck

All models are optimized for printing on FDM printers and print without supports.

—description from the publisher

Each player wants to have the most swagger, but your plans will break down as your crew will be stolen, killed, or might just be unhelpful.

Take turns playing your hand of 5 cards, ordering them to your benefit to be left with most swagger when all cards are played.

—description from the designer

The Fate of the 9 Worlds is a Area Majority Duel game in Scandinavian Mythology setting where one player playing Asgard side and the other one playing for Helheim side.

During the game you will receive cards, the events of which you may be familiar with from Scandinavian myths and sagas.

You will also take control of various Worlds, outstripping your opponent in terms of influence in them, as well as trying to invade, capturing them by force and getting glory points for winning in battles.

If in the first half of the game you will build your strategy, trying to control the Worlds you need, then in the second half Ragnarok awaits you. The time when no one spares anyone and the battles will become bloodier.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Iago is trying to escape with the Magic Lamp! Can Aladdin and Abu catch him, or will end up in the Cave of Wonders?
Cut out the two counters and place them at the start. Now flip a coin. If it's heads, then move the Tiger God counter one space. If it is tails, then move the magic lamp counter. Keep flipping the coin to see where Aladdin and Abu end up.

Experience chess like never before with Chess-à-Tête: Classic Edition, the thrilling new version of the original game. With refined mechanics and enhanced gameplay, this edition delivers a bold twist on the timeless classic, blending deep strategy, tactical card play, and fast-paced action into a truly unique experience.

Command legendary chess pieces, each with dynamic abilities and powerful synergies. Draw your cards, build your board, and launch an epic assault on your opponent. Unleash devastating combat cards, shift momentum with tactical action cards, and outwit your rival using your pieces’ special abilities. Victory is yours if you can reduce their king to zero health, deliver a decisive Checkmate, or exhaust their deck.

With its innovative mechanics, Chess-à-Tête: Classic Edition reimagines chess for a modern audience, appealing to strategy enthusiasts and card game lovers alike. Perfect for two players, it’s dynamic, unpredictable, and endlessly replayable.

Whether you're a returning fan or new to the game, Chess-à-Tête: Classic Edition invites you to redefine the battle of wits in the ultimate evolution of the world’s most iconic game.
Are you ready to make your move?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Nuh-Uh!: Science and Nature Expansion Pack
description: A Science and Nature themed xpansion pack for the "Nuh-Uh!" trivia card game. Adds over 200 additional qustions to the base game themed around Science and Nature.

De zoete mix ("The Sweet Mix") is a fun and strategic card game where players collect baking ingredients by combining patterns. By completing various recipes, such as cakes and cookies, players earn points. The game challenges players to plan ahead, manage their ingredients wisely, and outsmart their opponents to create the most delicious sweet treats and score the highest points. Perfect for anyone who loves baking and a bit of friendly competition!

—description from the publisher

With this expansion, 36 unique influencers come to your park. You also get the green influencers from the base game to save on sorting... or simply to be able to hire even more influencers! So, put on your best smile and advertise your theme park!

—description from the publisher

Duetwoorden is a party game in which two players simultaneously act out the two parts of a compound word—without knowing what the other is portraying. The guessing players only see the combined performance and must identify the full word.

Each card contains a compound word split into two separate nouns. One performer acts the first part, the other the second part. No talking, pointing or sounds are allowed; players can only use body language, gestures and facial expressions.

Duetwoorden can be played competitively or cooperatively. In competitive games, teams take turns sending a duo forward while the rest of their team guesses. In cooperative play, all players work together to score as many correct guesses as possible within a set number of rounds.

The game scales well for groups and is suitable for families, friends and casual players who enjoy creative acting and light deduction.

Hey, adventurers! ?

We're Studio Lyra, a passionate new indie team creating Guardians of the Abyss, a tabletop adventure game that combines strategic gameplay with an immersive narrative.

In Guardians of the Abyss, you’ll:

     Battle dark forces and survive the abyss.
     Uncover a rich story with every choice you make.
     Strategize your way to victory with simple yet deep mechanics.


Why You'll Love It:

     Accessible for both beginners and seasoned players.
     A fast-paced, 40-60 minute gameplay experience.
     Available in both Standard and Special Collector's Editions!


—description from the designer

Pizzachef - Mijn pizzafeestje is a fun and interactive card game for kids where players actually make pizzas in their kitchen while learning everything about pizza through quiz questions, fun facts, and special pizza experiments. It's the perfect game for combining cooking, learning, and lots of pizza-making fun!

—description from the publisher

This is Print & Play expansion to "Show Business". Outsmart your rivals and achieve three goals to win the game before your reach final end game scoring.

—description from the designer

Two thousand years have passed since the collapse of civilization. The great nuclear war obliterated nearly all traces of humanity, leaving the planet scorched and desolate. The resulting climate upheaval reawakened dormant volcanoes, reshaping the Earth’s landscape. In this harsh new world, humanity has reverted to its primal roots, becoming hunters and gatherers once more. Yet, in their resilience, they have constructed enormous cities on wheels, endlessly roaming in search of vital resources to sustain life and gain power to rule the world.

Steam Cities is an open-world, adventure game for 1-4 players designed by Hugo Silva. Steam Cities features an entire world to explore, with players trying to survive while scattered across the world.

You begin each game of Steam Cities with a City that you will develop along the time an heading towards an uncharted world, you have complete freedom as to how you explore, discover, and interact with other players by sabotage or trade. Create influence and gain votes by improving your city and gather resources be the first to won the election to rule the world that is in chaos.

—description from the publisher

A treasured photo to retrieve, two ex-lovers, two finely tuned brains battling it out! Will it be you, Sherlock Holmes, in this reimagined Sir Arthur Conan Doyle classic, who will prevail as always? Or will you, Irene Adler, inflict on the king of detectives the one, searing defeat of his career? In a SherBlock, you get to play as many as two protagonists! Choose sides, flip the block and play!

A sinister criminal rocks London! Venture into this gripping story of investigation, chases, deductions and puzzles. Unravel the mystery and solve the case! In one SherBlock, you get to play as many as two protagonists! Coordinate the investigations of Sherlock Holmes and his trusted friend, Dr. Watson. Flip the block over and play the game!

In Mankind Madness, a secret nationality, a trip around the world, and a bunch of prejudice cards you can use to your own advantage or against others help you race to the cities of the world. But beware, your actions can also damage the environment and ultimately lead to Armageddon, thus "Game Over" for everyone.

Each player in Mankind Madness starts with a secret identity and a random Ideology - which gives specific player traits.  The goal of the game is for players to visit a number of cities and be the first to return to your home city.

The gameplay is turn-based and begins with the first player. Turn-structure:
1. Draw-Phase: You always draw an Action and a Prejudice card to your hand.
2. Game-Phase: Now you can move your figure one space (for free), can play as many Prejudices or Action cards as you like and activate the ability of your Ideology. All activities in the game phase are optional, and you can carry them out in any order.
After you are done, it’s the next players turn in clockwise order.

If you have at least 6 victory points and your figure is standing on the capital of your nationality, you can reveal your secret nationality and win the game. But if the Armageddon card gets flipped before someone wins the game, its “Game Over” for everyone.

—description from the publisher
Mime Battle is a party game in which two teams try to guess a mime — but both teams are playing simultaneously, using words that are related...or even identical!

REFINED is an economic dice game where the various components of crude oil are represented by 100+ custom dice. In the game, you will be charged with the safe and efficient operation of your custom refinery. You will need to purchase processing equipment upgrades to expand your refinery's dice handling capabilities to optimize your production capacity and maximize your profits over several ERAs. Your strategic planning, puzzle solving and process management skills will be put to the test as you try to refine the most crude oil dice without spilling any dice and causing environmental damage. Optimize your refinery's output to produce the correct distillates required to fulfil specific real-world contracts and historic ERA demands. If you can successfully navigate the historic and operational challenges and generate the most profits, you will be crowned ... Refinery Tycoon!

—description from the designer
In Shapely, players give clues by making shapes out of abstract pieces, then guess the shapes created by their opponents.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Equestrian - Horse Show is a strategy game where you need to train your horse and get him in the best performance to win the show competition.

With a deck building and work placement mechanisms, you will compete with your fellow friends to buy a horse train and get prepared for the season by upgrading your Equestrian Centre.

You can purchase horses from different breeds, buy tacks to improve the horse performance and strengths, upgrade your equipment to allow you to get more chances to win the Horse Jumping Competition.

Try to win the most competitions, get better horses and be the World Best Equestrian Center.

Points will be achieved through competitions prizes.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dzieci kontra rodzice: Czego o sobie nie wiecie?
description:
Kids and parents have a separate decks of 25 cards with 4 questions on each card.

The decks are named "Kids ask parents" and "Parents ask Kids" and both contains questions designed to match with knowledge of each side of duel.
One side draws a card from opponents deck, picks a question and chooses 1 of 3 given answers. After a correct answer the card is given and 1 point is awarded.
In case of drawing a trap-card you lose your turn and a chance to answer a question.
Then other side can draw a card.

3 games in 1.
Firstly the unicorn party, move around the board to collect equipment to attend the unicorn party (watch out for cheeky trolls that steal your stuff). When your ready you then ascend he rainbow to get to the party, first person to the party wins.
Secondly, roll the dice to dress your unicorn.
And thirdly, roll the dice to move round the happy/sad cloud board, happy clouds move you forward and sad clouds send you back. First to the finish wins

Kids and parents have a separate decks of 25 cards with 4 questions on each card and 1 extra trap-card.

The decks are named "Kids ask parents" and "Parents ask Kids" and both contains questions designed to match with knowledge of each side of duel.
One side draws a card from opponents deck, picks a question and chooses 1 of 3 given answers. After good answer the card is given and 1 point is awarded.
In case of drawing a trap-card you loose your turn and a chance to answer a question.
Then other side can draw a card.

Universe A1 takes place in different universes, each having their own landscapes and locations. Each set is based on its own universe. This game is a one versus one deck building trading card game, where your goal is to have your opponent's graveyard reach five characters. You can achieve this by having your characters attack and defeat your opponent's characters. Other card classes, like items, boosts, and gears, may assist you as well. Defeating an opposing character doesn't just help with your end goal either, there is a mechanic that allows you to play a boost card for defeating an opposing character. This boost may be used every turn until the game is over without discarding it.

Universe A1 also hosts a variety of deck building strategies and synergies. Some examples include matching attribute decks, location decks, tempo decks, cheap decks, and sweep decks.

Like other card games, this game has a "resource" that must be managed in order to play cards, use attacks, abilities, etc. This resource is referred to as "action points". Action points are given each turn from characters you have in play. Other items and boosts may assist in giving action points as well, but the primary way is from your in play characters.

—description from the designer

Experience the festive season with Hitster – Christmas! Immerse yourself in a world of Christmas sounds and nostalgic hits as you and your friends try to find the right placements on your personal timeline.

In Hitster – Christmas, you take on the roles of music lovers navigating through listening to Christmas songs. Take turns choosing songs from different decades and trying to guess their correct place on the timeline. Good hearing is crucial to score points and emerge as a hitster champion.

Hitster – Christmas is perfect for players aged 16 and over and is ideal for families, friends and parties. With a playing time of around 30 minutes, it offers an ideal mix of fun and challenge and guarantees a high level of replayability thanks to the variety of songs and combinations.

Put your Christmas music knowledge to the test and celebrate unforgettable gaming moments together with Hitster – Christmas!

—description from the publisher

THE DUNDER MIFFLIN SCRANTON BRANCH is in danger of closing!

In this team hidden identity game, players will perform any-and-all tasks thrown at you by the various members of the office to help them succeed in winning back Scranton for Dunder Mifflin. But be on the lookout. It’s rumored that Osprey Paper has bribed certain members of your class to sabotage your team’s hard work. Be more than a little stitious when trusting your fellow interns. Good luck! The fate of the branch is in your hands.

On the active player's turn, they will reveal a Task Card, Gather their Team, and prepare for next week as they try to determine the allegiances of those around them.


     Spend Schrute Bucks to sneak a peek at your opponent's card 
     Spin the Chore Wheel for Rewards 


The game ends after 12 weeks ... unless the interns fail 5 tasks.

—description from the publisher

The game is played with a football-like die with numbers and arrows on the sides. The player who throws a number with an arrow pointing forwards can rush forward. The player who throws a number with an arrow pointing backwards must run back. The player who throws a number with an arrow pointing to the right or left can run sideways.
The player who throws the highest number has the kick-off. 
It is not permitted to jump over players in the opponent's goal area. "Walling" in front of your own goal is permitted. However, no other player may be in the goal other than the goalkeeper; however, the goalkeeper can leave the goal. If the players get stuck, they must take turns rolling the dice until one or the other player can rush forwards or sideways again. In any case, there is a forced move.
The player who has scored an opponent's goal must return to any space in his own half of the field. All other players remain on the field.
The winner is the first player to score 10 goals.

—user summary

You are a gambler sent to take down a certain casino.
Your goal is to hire gamblers to gamble and earn money.

Each gambler has a "winning number", and if the total of the numbers on the "Destiny Card" chosen by each player in each round is the same as the "winning number", you will receive money.

There are now more opportunities to hire gamblers, and special gamblers can only be purchased when you have 0 money.

Various adjustments have been made, allowing players to enjoy a flashy gameplay experience with large amounts of coins flying back and forth and tension building until the very end.

Enjoy this remake by game designer Miyano Kaya, which took nine years to complete.

The creators of the card game "SideQuest: Aquí no hay Héroes" launched a crowdfunding campaign on the Verkami platform to fund the production of the game. The campaign was successful, surpassing its initial funding goal of €4,500 and raising a total of €6,133 from 123 contributors. The campaign offered a variety of rewards to backers, including digital and physical copies of the game, art books, keychains, stickers, and physical and digital illustrations.
The campaign highlighted the game's unique theme and gameplay. Instead of traditional fantasy heroes, players take on the role of merchants in a bustling fantasy marketplace. The goal is to earn the most gold by fulfilling the requests of fantasy characters who visit the market and using magic items to sabotage their opponents. The campaign emphasized the game's simplicity, fast-paced gameplay, and its inclusive and diverse cast of characters.
To keep backers informed, the creators posted regular updates throughout the campaign, providing information on the game's development progress, production plans, and upcoming events. The creators also actively engaged with backers on social media, announcing raffles and sharing insights into the game's design and artwork. The campaign updates also highlighted the positive feedback received from playtesters and reviewers, which helped build excitement for the game's release.
The successful crowdfunding campaign enabled the creators to produce and deliver the game to backers in November 2024, marking a significant milestone for the SideQuest. Games team and paving the way for the development of future projects.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Sometimes you want a hippopotamus for Christmas. Only a hippopotamus will do...

Hippo Hero is an 18-card deduction game for 3 or 4 players. You are trying to give each other the best possible Christmas gifts. Each of you secretly has one item that you want more than anything else in the world. You also have one item that would ruin your day.

Each round players will exchange gifts and then (possibly) point to one of the others they'd prefer over the gift in front them.

Once all the gifts have been given, each player will reveal their heart’s desire to determine if this Christmas was merry or just meh.

Movie Connection Game, similar to 6 degrees of separation. Each player will start with 5 cards. The remaining cards are placed face down on the table, with the top card flipped to begin as the 'active' game card.

Players compete to connect their cards to the active game card through six degrees of separation or fewer. When a player finds a connection. The connected card is then placed face up on the active card, which becomes the new active card. Play continues until a player has no cards left.

3 of the main actors from the movie are listed on the card but ANY actor from the movie can be used in the connection not just what is on the card.

There is also a QR code on the card linking to the IMDB page for the movie to get the full cast if required.

—description from the designer

Throw stones into Lake Llanquihue and make it bounce on the water as many times as possible!

This is a short-term memory, information retention and working memory skill game.

You will put the duckling cards on a grid that grows after each of the three rounds. These cards will have numbers that you must remember, as they will then be turned face down. Next, you must look for a suitable stone to throw and when you find it, you can turn the duck cards face up only in ascending numerical order, if you go down in number or reveal a botch card, the round ends.

Beat your own score in this game that healthily and entertainingly stimulates the executive functions of your brain!

Despite being a solo game, more people can play it to see who wins the most points!

—description from the designer

Lanza piedras en el lago Llanquihue y haz la mayor cantidad de patitos sobre el agua!
Este es un juego de habilidad de memoria a corto plazo, retención de información y memoria de trabajo.

Pondrás las cartas de patito en una grilla que crece tras cada una de las tres rondas. Estas cartas tendrán números que deberás recordar, pues luego se voltearán boca abajo. A continuación, deberás buscar una piedra adecuada para lanzar y, cuando la encuentres, podrás voltear boca arriba las cartas de patito solo en orden numérico ascendente, si bajas de número o revelas una carta de pifia, la ronda termina.

Supera tu propio puntaje en este juego que estimula sana y entretenidamente las funciones ejecutivas de tu cerebro!

Pese a ser un juego en solitario, pueden jugarlo más personas para ver quién gana más puntos!

—description from the designer (Spanish)

boardgame: Rad Pirates of the Toxic Sands
description:
Rad Pirates of the Toxic Sands is a game of post-apocalyptic piracy upon the Mediterranean desert sands. Command a small flotilla of junkers as you attack tanker caravans, raid oil rigs and plunder the black gold.

You are a Rad Pirate captain with a small flotilla of sandships. You make a living trading with rigs or raiding them if they hike their prices up too high), salvaging scrap and fuel from wrecks and abandoned rigs, and attacking other sandship and tanker flotillas.

Rad Pirates of the Toxic Sands is my entry to the inaugural One Box Wargame Challenge. 
The rules were simple: make a tabletop game that required only the contents of a single box of miniatures. 
To play Rad Pirates with two players, you’ll need a single box of the Black Seas Frigates and Brigs from Warlord Games. 
You get six ships in the box, and you split them between you.

—description from the designer

Grab some toy cars and flick your way to victory! 
Build a race course from Jenga blocks and flick your two cars around the track. 
If you crash into something, you add a Crash Dice to your cup and roll everything you got. 
Some dice will get discarded, some will give the next play more flicks on their next turn.


     1-6 players, 15-30 mins!
     Pretty much the most fun you can have with toy cars!
     Simple dexterity mechanics! 
     Great with kids!
     Just grab dice, toy cars and a Jenga set!
     Get wrecked, but never stay out for long!


Flick your cars through the race gate in the right order in the right direction to be the champion in this chaotic and tactile game of tabletop racing. 
Road-tested to be perfect for playing with kids from five and up.

—description from the designer

You are a Cat. You live in a House. You share this house with your pet Hoomin. Shower this Hoomin with your Affection, then cause as much Mischief as you can without being thrown out of your House. The first Cat to score 9 Mischief points is the winner of the game!

In this furrocious feline card game of mischievous machinations, you play the role of a Cat doing what cats do best: tormenting their Hoomin pets! Play Affection cards into your House to gain their trust, then wait for the right moment to play Mischief cards to score points.

But be quick about it! The other Cats in your Neighborhood will play Incident cards to slow your progress to give themselves the upper claw.

This remixed, remastered, and renewed THIRD EDITION of our beloved indie title has been refined and remastered with the following updates:


     Streamlined rules for faster, family friendly gameplay at ALL difficulty levels.
     Graphic design and rule enhancements to keep the game fresh for 2025.
     All illustrations have been rescanned to archival standards for more detailed paintings.
     INCLUDES the previously unreleased expansion: "Cantankerous Cats and the Kitten Catastrophe"


"Kitten Catastrophe" includes 13 new cards that can be mixed into the base game for easier scoring and more kittens!

Designed by the original Cantankerous Cats team with refinements by Jason Serrato and Aven Gonzalez.
The stunning watercolor tradition continues with new adorable kitten illustrations by Amanda Kahl, Rebecca Rhodes, and Aven Gonzalez.'

—description from the publisher

Extra Extra! You and your fellow newspaper Barons are trying to get a foothold in the boroughs of New York! Use your Corporate Suit to navigate New York and recruit Newsy's to the most valuable streets to win Blocks for your newspaper.
You only have 6 Actions each game, so choose wisely! Use your resources, Newsy's, and private Hedge Fund to come out on top and push your rival Barons out of the market!

S for Startup transforms players into ambitious entrepreneurs in a fast-paced 30-minute card game, where you'll race to build the next unicorn.

Unleash the power of 110 action-packed cards to hire genius employees, score massive funding, and dodge sneaky competitor takedowns - all while racing to collect glowing rocket tokens that mark your path to startup fame. Perfect for 2-4 future entrepreneurs (aged above +12 years), this game turns every player into a mini-Elon, as you scheme, strategize, and sometimes stumble your way to building the next BIG startup!

Designers Rubini Niveditha and Adithya Hreendranathan have thoughtfully integrated AI into their design process, approaching the artwork with genuine passion and creativity. By embracing AI as a collaborative tool, they're exploring innovative ways to merge technology with artistic vision, demonstrating a nuanced approach to design that goes beyond mere technical application.

This game not only entertains but also teaches valuable lessons about business and investment in a fun and interactive way.

Dive into the investigation, question suspects and analyze a police report, DNA test results, evidence material, and much more. When you're ready, check the solution to see how accurate your deductions were and calculate your score. Can you solve the case and become a legend?

Welcome aboard the SS Royal Line, a luxury ferry sailing between England and Sweden. A passenger, Johan Dahlberg, has been brutally murdered. With the ferry sealed off, it’s up to you to find the killer before time runs out.

Digital Product (PDF), instant download. Print and play or play on screen, no internet needed.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ink & Clues: The Ghost of Crimson Sky Ranch
description:
Dive into the story, analyze reports, interview suspects, and examine evidence. When you've formed your conclusion, turn to the last page to see if you solved the case. Can you bring justice to Crimson Sky Ranch?

Crimson Sky Ranch, once quiet, is now haunted by mystery. The young owner, Samuel Blackwood, was found dead with no visible injuries, leaving the police puzzled. Locals believe in a supernatural culprit, "The Ghost of Crimson Sky Ranch." As the detective, it's up to you to uncover the truth behind Samuel's death.

Digital PDF, instant download. Print and play or play on screen, no internet needed.

—description from the publisher

Step into the role of the lead investigator. Examine autopsy reports, interview suspects, comb through evidence, and study a police sketch. Once you’ve pieced together your theory, reveal the solution to see if your deductions were accurate. Will you solve the case and bring the notorious serial killer to justice?

March 5, 2024. New York City is gripped with fear. Three bodies, each bearing the horrifying signature of a killer known as "The Organ Harvester," have been found. The city's streets are empty at night, as residents fear becoming the next victim. Now it's up to you to lead the investigation, stop the killer, and restore safety to the city.

Digital Product (PDF), instant download. Print and play or play on screen, no internet needed

—description from the publisher

Orbital: The Chemical Card Game is a strategic card game where players play chemical elements onto the table. Each card represents an element and is color-coded based on its group in the periodic table, including Groups 1–8 and the transition metals.

Players compete to match cards on the table and collect sets of elements from the same group. But beware of the transition metals! These cards yield negative points. To succeed, players must carefully manage their hands to maximize sets while avoiding the burden of transition metals.

In twentieth-century Europe, the best Criminals of all time have come together to steal some extraordinary treasures such as the Sword of Napoleon, The Ingots of the Orient Express or The Bracelet of Osiris. Only the most shrewd Detectives will be able to arrest them!
Choose which character to play, including, for example, the magician Zoltan Kovács, the archaeologist Maia Ioannidis, the former police commissioner Zac Clifford and the zoologist Naira Nandez.
Play in the Detective team to give instructions to the Police Officers, find Clues, follow the tracks of the Criminals and try to arrest them.
Wanna choose the Criminals instead? Move in the shadow, but mind no to reveal your moves and have your thugs guard your back while you reach the cities where treasures are kept!

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play
Players: Games with expansions that change player count

boardgame: Kaiser Cucumber and the Gangs of McChanicle Corners
description:
A small box, tile stacking game of Masterminds & Mayhem!

McChanicle Corners is a city brimming not only with cogs and commerce but secrets and secret societies. The masterminds run the city entertaining themselves with hijinks and high society.

The Royal Museum has just received a large donation of priceless art, previously thought lost. Sergeant Bonnie and the rabbit police (AKA the Bunny Bobbies) have been tasked with its protection.

YOU are one of the Masterminds. Your goal: Out maneuver the rival masterminds and get the most points. On your turn: Hide a criminal or drop them into the action.

—description from the designer

Welcome to ZooJourn, a wild adventure where you are a zoologist and your mission is to journey around the world and collect animals of the Animal Alphabet from A to Z.

Journey around the world and collect Color Animal Cards or Mystery Animal Cards. As you collect animals, you create alphabetic animal straights to collect points. Good Luck and Have Fun! (Just watch out for the Pernicious Poacher!)

Along the way, you will encounter many fun challenges against your fellow zoologists.

The first zoologist to earn 12 points wins the game and will be known as The World's Greatest Zoologist - at least until next time!

—description from the designer

Enter the arena and prepare yourself for a dynamic, 3D battle unlike any board game you’ve ever played before! Edge Quest delivers a groundbreaking fusion of tactical strategy and physical action, offering a gameplay experience unlike any other. Players command fully articulating action figures on a 3D battlefield, where positioning and movement are just as crucial as card play and resource management. You'll use the dual-use cards to change your position, footwork, gain edge, and attack!

Mastering your position is key to executing precise attacks and defenses! Adjust your sword’s orientation and placement or adopt a guarded stance. Each move demands the right positioning for success!

Move your figure around the arena using footwork adjustment cards. Get yourself out of reach, or get within reach of your opponent to execute the perfect attack or defense! While there are rules about how to move, this is not a grid-based combat! You can move your figure freely around the arena!

There are several different victory conditions to choose from before you begin the match. Use your cards wisely to propel yourself to the win!

—description from the designer

Moonlight is Roll and Write series of games in a comic noir universe.

Episode 1:  You are Dexter "Sly" Harrigan, a hard boiled detective in Midnight City.  Your partner has been kidnapped and you have less than 3 weeks to save them.  You know who did it: Big Louie "The Claw" Marconi, and you have a plan...

Each turn you'll spend one day from the calendar to roll 6 dice and then spend those dice on various Investigation Actions, Hustle Actions or Searching areas that you've Investigated.  Collect clue sets to level up your skills and deduce quadrants of the map as not containing the hostage.

At the end of each of the 3 weeks you must make an escalating ransom payment. In order to raise those funds you'll need to commit various heists.  When you do so use the Heist overlay board and your skills that you've honed through your sleuthing to maximize the amount of loot you get away with.  Then, in you capacity as a detective the next day show up and destroy and fabricate evidence at the crime scene to frame Big Louie "The Claw" Marconi.

Win the game before time runs out or before the police catch on to your crime spree.  You can do this by incriminating Big Louie, paying the ransom or finding the hostage on the map.

—description from the publisher

Fluffy, floating clouds will get you to the finish, but only if you uncover the two correct tiles.

However, this plan is made more difficult for you by the fact that not all tiles are always available to you as they are locked by gray clouds.

Who of you will eventually make the race?

boardgame: Lying Pirates: Cities of Greed Expansion
description:
The tides of adventure rise higher than ever with Cities of Greed, the brand-new expansion for Lying Pirates! Dive into a world where cunning tactics meet fierce competition as you navigate the treacherous waters of wealth and ambition. Will you reign supreme, or will greed be your downfall?

Cities of Greed adds 7 new towns to visit, influence and take control of as Mayor.

—description from the publisher

Blaster Magnets is an incredible Magnetic Board Game!
You just need to place one magnetic Blaster on any space of your designed color of the board, but be careful not to attract the other Blasters, because if they stick together, they'll be yours!
THE PLAYER WHO RUNS OUT OF BLASTERS FIRST WILL BE THE WINNER!

It has 2 game modes: traditional and extreme, and includes 12 Wild cards, which can either help or hinder you.

—description from the publisher
The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach space 100 wins the game.

The power of the element is flowing and changing all around. Ten unique pieces of the four-element tiles are flipped or pushed each turn. Can you remain focused and manipulate your way to place your elements in the right spot before other players? Put your memory to the test as you become the master of your element.

—description from the designer

Couch Kingdom is an action based and area control card game that plays out over 3 timed rounds.

Players can go solo or split up into teams and move around the game area placing their units on lands to claim them and get money.

Teams only have several minutes to act, and multiple teams can try and fight over land cards! Those players fighting over land only have 30 seconds to try and bluff, bribe, retreat or fight for those lands!

If a team has 15 Gold at any time, they win! Otherwise, control the most lands at the end of 3 rounds!

Setup your house, office, convention area - wherever - and start bluffing and bribing to victory!

—description from the designer

Roll into the action with Sports Dice: Basketball includes all the drama of a real game. Throw your ball at the net and try to dunk to score. Try to stop the opposing team by  stealing the ball in the defensive zone. Win the jump ball at center court and push your team to the offensive zone. The closer you are to the net, the better. Throw the ball on the net and beat the opponent dice number to score! Both teams simultaneously roll all their dice. The symbol that appears the most on each team’s set of dice becomes their play. The team that rolled the most dice with the same symbol wins the roll. Apply the outcome of the winning play by moving the puck accordingly. See the Dice chart for a description of each symbol. The game ends when a team has scored 21 points. '

—description from the publisher

One of a number of German journey games inspired by the classic Game of the Goose, the Eisenbahn-Spiel has players competing to reach the final (70) location first. In a variant on the traditional Game of the Goose, players are required to roll doubles for each number before being permitted to start, movement is determined by a single die (as opposed to the two dice traditionally used), and landing on some spaces will incur fines, with players unable to pay being eliminated.

The German version also included a stipulation that landing on spaces numbered 30 or higher that are divisible by 5 would return a player to space 23. Subsequent Dutch printings removed this rule.

The dating of the original German version to 1897 is an approximation based on the publisher's numbering system.

Enter the game of millionaires, where deals are made not just on the stock market, but also at the table. Form temporary alliances with other players, negotiate secret agreements, and fulfill them to gain an edge over your competitors. But beware - in the world of millionaires, a word is often worthless, and allies can quickly turn into sworn enemies.

But that's not all - the game also has room for chance. Spin the roulette wheel, place your bets, and risk it all to get what you want. And when you've accumulated enough capital, you can make truly monumental deals. Want to buy a whole country? Or perhaps negotiate a monopolistic contract with other players? The possibilities are endless, but the path to victory won't be easy. Are you ready to enter the game and become a millionaire?

boardgame: The Couples Game That's Actually Fun
description:
If you've ever made up a word together or communicated with animal noises, then this is the game for you.

Play this game with your partner. You will draw cards and read questions to each other. There are 3 types of questions: match, best, me or you. You will write your answers in secret and flip over the boards to see how well you know each other!

A Happy New Semester is a may-follow trick-taking game where players attempt to win the students to their lab who are the best researchers.

Each card represents a student and has a certain research value, as well as mostly being part of potential set collection points. Some points and research values are negative.

The player with the most wins after 3 rounds wins.

With the heat in Brazil, it’s no wonder that Brazilian houses are full of cobogós. You are architecture interns and have been given the task of planning a mosaic of cobogós — but you will need to understand your clients’ whims to do a good job. Time to get to work!

Cobogó is a card-laying, cooperative/solo puzzle microgame with only 18 cards. At setup, 3 cards are flipped to show different polyomino to be built with the other cards. The other cards are placed to create those patterns, avoiding covering glass blocks. If the players get all patterns done until the deck runs out, they win the game.

Game #01 of the Piquinim Collection.

—description from the designer

Can you hear the engines revving and tires screeching as the go-carts carve the last corner before the final lap of this deck-building, adrenaline-rushing, momentum-managing racing card game?

Players take turns acquiring cards and managing their momentum so they get the most speed each turn and aim to get over the finish line before anyone else does!

—description from the designer

Today is a good day to die... for the enemy crew!

Battlefront Valkyrie is a groundbreaking 3D printable wargame that delivers the thrill of epic fleet battles right to your tabletop, all at a cost that's just a fraction of traditional games!

This expansion set includes:

Terran C-7A Light Cruiser

Terran D-4A Destroyer

and their Ship cards

All models are optimized for printing on FDM printers and print without supports.

—description from the publisher

Today is a good day to die... for the enemy crew!

Battlefront Valkyrie is a groundbreaking 3D printable wargame that delivers the thrill of epic fleet battles right to your tabletop, all at a cost that's just a fraction of traditional games!

This expansion set includes:

Six transport ships

stellar jump gate

a new game scenario

and ship cards

All models are optimized for printing on FDM printers and print without supports.

—description from the publisher

As you wanted to live closer to nature, you have decided to start cultivating rice in Niigata Prefecture, Japan.

Snakes are commonly found in paddies. These reptiles are considered sacred creatures that help the rice to grow.

The more snakes there are, the greater the harvest will be. However, you will need to control the snake population in order to harvest the rice.

Tanbo is a two-player abstract game with a Japanese theme. The primary mechanic resembles Mancala, which have been played since ancient times. However, in this game, the mancala has evolved into something unusual and mysterious, creating elements that feel like worker placement, expanding options, area majority, and set collection.

Winner of the Rakuichi Fresh Dragon Award in 2023

—description from the publisher

Times are changing, and the wilderness is withering. As resources dwindle and new threats emerge, healing wildlife is no longer an easy task. Parker Rangers must think ahead and act wisely, crafting their potions with prevision and care. Venturing out at the right time and navigating around obstacles are key to success in Potion Perils.

Potion Perils is a strategic expansion that requires the base game, Parks & Potions. In this expansion, players choose specific Time of Day dice to roll, which determine the potions available during those hours. New challenges, such as viral infections and spore outbreaks, create competitive obstacles that players must navigate each round.

—description from the designer

Bugs & Bottles is a mini expansion that features bugs, a new 6th player ability, and offers more combo completions! Heal bugs and get a little something back!

—description from the designer

PMC 2670: The Highest Bid is an expansion for one of our flagship rulesets, PMC 2670, specifically crafted for competitive play. With nearly 1.5 years of development, this expansion introduces new rules and content designed for tournaments, conventions, mini-campaigns, and quick club gaming sessions.

The rules presented in this expansion are tailored for the PMC 2670 system, but with a little work, they could easily be adapted to other rulesets.

What's Inside?

     Special Army Composition: Adjust battle sizes for events, club meetings, or other games played within limited timeframes. Armies built using these guidelines ensure compatibility with the rest of the expansion.
     Two Tournament Points Systems: Choose between the Tactical and Bloodbath scoring systems—whether you favor careful, objective-focused strategies or aggressive, combat-centric tactics.
     Dynamic Army Progress Modes: Select One-off or Army Progress modes, enabling your units to gain Veteran skills and Battle Shocks, adding new layers of depth to your army rosters.
     Contracts: Engage in short campaigns with simplified army management and no economic considerations. Contracts provide an exciting way to compete within your group over a set number of battles.
     Comprehensive Stratagems: Enhance your tactical options with a variety of offensive and defensive stratagems, allowing you to tailor your force to your specific play style.
     Detailed Scenarios: Six new tournament scenarios provide diverse and balanced challenges, complete with specific objectives and terrain guidelines to ensure fair and exciting battles.
     SLAM Battles: Experience rules with card-driven objectives designed for large multiplayer battles. Perfect as a tournament finale or as a one-off big battle with friends.
     Suggested Tournament Titles: Reward players with 12 different titles for standard and extraordinary achievements after the event.


—description from the publisher

Dominion of the Spear is a very simple set of wargames rules allowing one to fight two armies (pre gunpowder) against each other in just a few minutes. 
Although figures can be used, battles can easily be fought on paper (no measuring) using pen or pencil and two different coloured six sided dice. 
196 army lists are included divided into the periods: the Ancient Near East Age, Age of the Greek and Persian Wars, Age of the Romans, the Dark Ages, Age of the Crusaders, Age of Mongol Invasions and the Late Middle Ages. 
Armies consist of 3-6 units or commands and the points system is included. 
There is a complete play through of the Battle of Hydaspes 326 BC between Alexander the Great and King Porus of India.

Designer Notes are included along with a quick reference sheet on the back cover.

from the designer:
The armies are deployed as left flank, centre, and right flank with extra units in the reserve. Then combat in each of these three sectors are resolved sector by sector. A six sided dice is used for each unit to hit and rout it's opponent with missile armed units firing first. Defeated units are replaced from the reserve. The fun and interest comes from the interaction of the different types of units with missile mounted units having an advantage over melee infantry, melee infantry having an advantage over missile infantry, missile infantry over melee mounted, and melee mounted over missile mounted. Then there are outflanking bonuses. A battle can be fought in around 10 minutes or less and with some imagination generates an amusing story. Brief rules are given for scaling up with a bigger battlefield and bigger armies. Really designed to be played solo.
family: History: Greek & Persian conflicts of the 4th century BC
History: Wars in the Ancient Near East
History: Wars of Alexander the Great

description:
In the vale, an ancient evil stirs. It has slumbered for years and years and now, it has taken root in every living being, whatever their magical ability. Can you resist the dreadful influence of the Curse-Tree, or will you seek its dark blessings?
This expansion consists of a main module with new cards to shuffle into the card deck, and Tamer tiles. It can also be used with the other expansion for Vale of Eternity, Artifacts.

Contents:
28 cards

8 Summoner tiles

1 Tree standee

12 double sided Ward tokens

40 Curse tokens

—description from the publisher

Return to 13th-century France, where a cathedral is being built in Chartres, featuring stunning stained-glass windows that continue to captivate people to this day. 
Choose the right locations and assistants to strengthen your reputation in the city, acquire horses and carts, fill barrels with the finest wine, and craft the most magnificent stained-glass windows to make your noble house the most influential in the city.

Through 3 decades (rounds)the players will choose from their hands two cards to play, one of these should activate the abilities of a previous played card that could help to decorate with amazing stained-glass windows the Cathedral, gather grapes to make wine or help to grow the population of the city, among other effects.

By gaining a better status, you could gain reputation, grow your hand limit and make that your actions through the game become more rewarding in the final scoring.

At the end of the game, the House who scored more influence wins the game.

—description from the publisher
Send Hammy the Hamster into a maze to retrieve the food ball. If he brings it back to your side of the board, you win!

Compete with your other librarians to get rid of your hand of book cards. You'll need to shelve them in order by publication date - but if you place your card incorrectly, it'll come straight back into your hand. Never fear, though, as you can use your single use-library card to help, or to hinder your opponents.

Exposure - A Radiating Card Game is a fresh take on the social deduction game genre popularized by Mafia/Werewolf.
In Exposure, you are handed a hidden role where you strive to quarantine the Uncontrolled Radiation - or to see your victims succumb to radiation poisoning.
The game plays over several rounds, with each round consisting of an Exposure phase, a Survey phase, a Quarantining phase, and a Reposition phase.
Exposure features two game modes, play either a classic Versus variant, or a Co-op mode where all players work together to quarantine the Uncontrolled Radiation.

—description from the designer

Embrace joy for once and relive the Rose Family’s best and worst moments with TRIVIAL PURSUIT: Schitt’s Creek! Flaunt your knowledge of the Emmy Award-winning comedy’s characters, quotes, locations, and more with 600 questions from six categories: Love That Journey For Me, Townsfolk, The Roses, Who Said What, Fast Forward to Success, and Where Everyone Fits In. Answer correctly to claim the card and be the first with six to win! Portable wedge case makes this quick-play game easy to pack for last-minute moves and motel room entertainment.

—description from the publisher

Every year, capybaras and capyfans gather in the Capivari River to participate in the National Capivárias Tournament, in which groups of mammals from all over the country compete for control of the waters with the help of their favorite humans. Choose your capybaras, keep an eye out for predators and be careful with the course of the river in this incredible capybara duel!

Capivárias is a duel, 18-card microgame about capybaras going down the river to collect points. At setup, 3 cards are flipped to form the river and showing special scoring conditions for each section (like doubling all odd cards). In each turn, players choose a card to put in their side on the starting section of the river. Cards of the same capybara group come into conflict, and the lower card goes down the river, creating a chain reaction.

Game #02 of the Piquinim Collection.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Il Gomitolo di Solevento: Il gioco da tavolo
description:
Il Gomitolo di Solevento ("The Solevento's Yarn) is a cooperative game inspired by the eponymous book.
It tells the story of a family next-door whose financial situation gets tangled because of ordinary events, whilst teaching some financial concepts and how it is possible to overcome economic distress moments related to debt.
