Guided by two trusted helpers, Giustina and Virgilio, the family discovers some solutions to untangle the confused yarn of their financial situation.
In the game, which explores more in-depth some topics only hinted at in the book, the concepts of responsible consumption, family budgeting, managing the unexpected, and the use of savings and credit also come into play.
Both the book and the game were made upon initiative of the Fire Group.

—description from the designer

Necromancers are craftsmen, artists, and creators. The study of forgotten and lost techniques of animating the dead is mixed with new advances in creating abominations and terrors that mortals could never dream of.

Included below are new Necromantic Minions for your necromancer to build, recruit, and raise. You may select any of these undead when you are building your new horde (as part of the creation process), or after a game when you are attempting to recruit new undead in the Post-Game Process.

Presented in Snarl '24

The members of MAJESTIC 13 are constantly researching, pushing the bounds of technology, often using recovered alien technology. The tangled web of bureaucracy often causes these innovations to be slow in gaining approval and even slower to reach the field operatives on the front lines, defending Earth from the alien threat.

The list below represents new gear you may utilize for your teams in MAJESTIC 13.

Presented in Snarl '24

The war in Hell grinds on and a new cabal has emerged, one that harkens back to the foundations hell was built upon – faith. They move through this desolate inferno, bringing all they can
to their flock. New battles continue to rage, as no cabal has claimed the Oculus Spear and the prophecies remain unfulfilled.

A new philosophy emerges in Hell - create new cabals with this add-on.

Presented in Snarl '23.

boardgame: Reign in Hell: The Broken Door
description:
You have emerged from the Dimensional Vault in the fallen city of Javekx, former home of the Directorate of Hell. The city remains in ruins… the scorching winds of hell continue to blow across the debris. Everything is quiet, only occasionally interrupted by the
sounds of falling rubble from the few ravenous demons hiding in the shadows, too full of fear to challenge your cabal. The quiet is startling, as though the rest of hell doesn’t know that everything has just changed.

Three new solo & co-op scenarios that might get you out of Hell.

Presented in Snarl '24

Forays into the bowels of Space Station Zero are rarely successful, but often it’s the only option crews have. Most crews that venture into the
depths are never seen again. Recently, however, a shipping crew returned from the abyss with fantastic tales of vegetative mutants and haywire
robots. More importantly, they returned with large stores of ancient and mysterious equipment. They have begun selling it to other crews who are hoping to find success in the dark, remote places of the ancient station.

This add-on brings new gear, weapons, and technology to the Station.

Presented in Snarl '23

Take a card and try to find out which region of Brazil the place in the image belongs to. If you get it right, you will be able to answer a question on the back of the card, about various topics linked to Brazilian geography and culture. If you get it right, you get the letter that serves as a postmark for your logbook! In an enriching and pleasurable way, players discover and recognize cities, great monuments and important landscapes as if they were traveling throughout Brazil and experiencing the different cultures that our country offers. Discover Brazil in 90 photos!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Pegue uma carta e tente descobrir a qual região do Brasil o local da imagem pertence. Se acertar, poderá responder uma pergunta do verso da carta, sobre os diversos temas ligados à geografia e à cultura brasileira. Acertando, você ganha a carta que serve como carimbo-postal pra seu diário de bordo! De uma maneira enriquecedora e prazerosa, os jogadores vão descobrindo e reconhecendo cidades, grandes monumentos e paisagens importantes como se estivessem viajando por todo Brasil e experimentando as diferentes culturas que nosso país oferece. Conheça o Brasil em 90 fotos!

—description from the publisher

Tank-on-tank battles have been the norm now for years. Though original ammunition store houses and parts factories were soon exhausted, those who crew and pilot the tanks have figured out how to create their own home-made ordnance and jury-rig most necessary fixes.

It is in this world you find yourself. You are lucky enough to pilot a powerful machine of war across the scorched landscape. The question is, can you keep it?

Post-apocalyptic tank battle minigame for two to ten players

Presented in Snarl '23.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Once every few years... when the moon is just right, when the perfect scent if fungus is on the wind, when the moss grows on the east face of the rocks, when there are just enough goblins around, that means it's time for..
THE GREAT GOBLIN ARGLE-BARGLE

Crazy goblin armies fighting for...reasons - for two to four players.

Presented in Snarl '24.

Roll the dice up to three time to set aside the shot that will win the point or keep the rally going. If you can't do that the balls into the net and your opponent scores. Use the Pickle Chips to keep score. First player to 11 wins the trophy and gets to do a victory dance with the mini paddles. Your opponent gets the pickle juice to get ready for next game.

When rolling the dice, you control your shot selection to keep your opponent deep or drop the ball into the kitchen and put the pressure on them to roll a great return shot.

The fast pace and simple rules will eliminate all those 'questionable' line calls and have you winning tournaments in no time!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Tales from the Red Dragon Inn: Double-Necked Lute Promo Card
description:
A promo card given during GenCon 2024 and the 2024 Extra Life event.

It is a Major Item with the following text:

Action, cooldown 5

Mêlée attack 2 yellow dice plus one, one foe

Harm: Add an Evade token to the Epic Pool. It may be used by any player.

boardgame: Зомби в доме: Заражение – Ботаник (Zadventure: Student)
description:
Зомби в доме: Заражение – Ботаник ("Zadventure: Student") is an expansion of the Zadventure game. It allows up to five players to play, with a new character called Student and two new weapons.

The main advantage of the Student character is its speed. No matter how many moves the spinner has, the Student makes one extra move.

Flip Chess is a pocket-sized, chess-based strategy game. It uses a folding half-sized game board/case with 4 chess dice for each player. Players promote their piece after each move.

Instructions for 4 games are included.

—description from the designer

Archibald, Certainly Not! is a game of World War One aerial combat focused on fun and playability.

The game can be played on any table, completely free of hexes and hex mats using garden variety six sided dice. 
Additionally there is almost no tracking of altitude or excessive clutter. 
Archibald’s clean mechanics lead to great fun and the feel of aerial combat during the Great War (minus the sheer terror), while minimizing the bookkeeping.
A typical game will last from one to two hours, depending greatly on the number of aircraft involved and each player’s tendency to ponder.

The game includes an extensive list of ratings for all types of aircraft, not just sexy Spads and Fokker D.VIIs,  along with a well informed QRS.

—description from the publisher

Sequence of Play (all actions are simultaneous):

     1 - moving
     2 - shooting

After the defeat at Waterloo, Napoleon was banished to the remote British island of St. Helena. The French army was weakened and the constant uprisings in Spain forced Joseph Bonaparte, Napoleon’s brother to abdicate the Spanish throne and flee to America. Once in America, Joseph Bonaparte and a considerable number of exiled Napoleonic generals settled in Texas. From there they began to conspire in secret, forming a large community of expatriates with French sympathies and expansionist intentions. They were discovered by Spanish agents who at that time controlled New Spain. The Spanish authorities sent a substantial number of soldiers to the area, which quickly dashed the conspirators' hopes.

This historical event is the inspiration for this expansion where in an alternate reality the Napoleonic legions are strong enough to compete for the territory of New Spain

More info:

This expansion can be played in solo mode, and two or three player competitive mode.

The Confederation has its own cards, Napoleonic board and VP marker illustrated with a French flag.

Backgammon Dice slims down the classic game of Backgammon into your pocket, and then expands the game play with changeable Pawn Dice and dice rolls! Navigate your Pawn Dice around the board, bear them all off, and aim for the lowest score.

You Choose Your Level of Play with your favorite rules from Simple Game Play for beginners through Basic play and on to Advanced/Tournament Play for seasoned strategists.

—description from the designer

Massive Darkness: Dungeons of Shadowreach takes the epic dungeon-crawling action of its predecessors and brings more thrilling adventures to this beloved setting. Step into the boots of six new lightbringers as you battle against the forces of Darkness in the depths of Shadowreach, a forgotten realm of endless danger and mystery.

This standalone, co-operative adventure for 1-6 players allows you to explore fantastical realms, facing a diverse array of enemies, formidable bosses, and insurmountable challenges. No game master is required for this board game adventure — gather your party and use your skills to survive the twisted caverns, battling vile monsters and mighty bosses in your quest to protect Shadowreach from the forces of Darkness.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Зомби в доме: Заражение – Охотник (Zadventure: Hunter)
description:
Зомби в доме: Заражение – Охотник ("Zadventure: Hunter") is an expansion of the Zadventure game. It allows up to five players to play, with a new character called Hunter who starts with a shotgun and axe.

Also, this expansion introduces a new Zombie Master mode, where one player initially plays as the zombie master against the others. They have all the zombies under their control, as well as Danger cards to harm characters. If a character dies, they turn their sheet zombie-side up and become a zombie character, which is a unique zombie.

The front of each card has words written in gibberish.

When you read it out loud, it reveals a secret phrase.

Compete to decode the secret phrases before time runs out. You'll say nonsense until it makes sense.

boardgame: Bear Mountain Camping Adventure: Tent Camping Mini-Expansion
description: This mini-expansion is a shout-out to all of our tent-campers, allowing you to play the game tent-camping instead of camping in RV's.

boardgame: Bear Mountain Camping Adventure: Pets Mini-Expansion
description: When you adopt a pet, they act as a permanent wildlife bell for the rest of the game, and they look adorable on your camper card!

Step into the world of global trade and high-stakes decision-making with Captain Phillip, the board game that combines the classic thrill of Blackjack with the strategic depth of shipping logistics.

In Captain Phillip, players take on the role of shipping tycoons managing their fleets across busy trade routes. Your mission is to outmaneuver your opponents by striking the perfect balance between risk, timing, and resource management. Will you take a gamble to secure high-value cargo or play it safe with smaller shipments? The choice is yours, but every decision impacts your reputation and financial standing.

Key Features:

Dynamic Mechanics: A unique blend of Blackjack-inspired risk-taking and logistics strategy.
Competitive Gameplay: Manage routes, outbid opponents for contracts, and navigate changing market conditions.
Multiple Paths to Victory: Focus on building the most efficient fleet, dominating trade routes, or amassing the most wealth.
Immersive Design: Vibrant visuals and detailed components bring the shipping industry to life.
Whether you're a fan of strategic planning or enjoy games with a hint of risk and excitement, Captain Phillip offers a fresh and engaging experience for gamers of all levels.

Why You'll Love It:

Easy to learn but challenging to master.
Perfect for casual gamers and seasoned strategists alike.
Encourages interaction, negotiation, and tactical thinking.
Get ready to set sail, manage your fleet, and navigate the thrilling seas of global trade with Captain Phillip!

—description from the publisher

The Face Cube game is a game for 2 or more players. This kit comes with 2 personalized 2x2x2 cubes (assorted colors) and 60 cards with different expressions. The deck must be positioned between the players upside down and the first card must be turned over; Once this is done, the game is already started! Whoever builds their cube with the same expression as the card wins the round and takes the card as a gift. The player who collects the most cards wins!

—description from the publisher (translated)

O jogo FACE CUBE é um game para 2 ou mais jogadores. Este kit acompanha 2 cubos 2x2x2 personalizados (cores sortidas) e 60 cartinhas com expressões diferentes. O baralho deve ser posicionado entre os jogadores de ponta cabeça e a primeira carta deve ser virada; feito isso o jogo já está valendo! Quem montar seu cubo com a mesma expressão da cartinha ganha a rodada e leva a carta de presente. O jogador que conquistar o maior número de cartas vence!

—description from the publisher (translated)

TRIVIA GAME FOR TRAVEL: Looking for a fun road trip activity? You've found it! Buckle up with your friends & family & let the fun begin with 150 questions about unique, quirky & tantalizing places to visit all over the United States
TEST YOUR KNOWLEDGE: Do you know where you can order a $10,000 martini? Or which condiment has its own museum in the USA? Our roadtrip game will have you talking, laughing & learning as you plan your next vacation
FAMILY FRIENDLY: Put the phone down & have some fun with Road Trip America! Whether you're in the car or on a bus, conversations will never go stale with these fun conversation starters & travel family game
TAKE THEM ANYWHERE: Whether you're looking for family games for game night, or a car game for your next family road trip, our convenient & compact Road Trip America trivia cards are perfect to take anywhere you go
THE PERFECT TRAVEL GIFT: Word Teasers makes great stocking stuffers, birthday gifts & travel gifts. No special occasion? That's OK! It's always a great time for a conversation starter!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Elder Scrolls: Betrayal of the Second Era – Deslandra Promo
description: A promotional enemy token distributed by Chip Theory at Gen Con 2024

Satellite is a simple came where players choose a colored wooden ball and toss them onto a board containing colored holes representing planets or other celestial bodies. Each planets is worth a certain score. If a player lands their wooden ball on a planet of a different color then they score the value of the planet. If they can toss their colored ball onto a planet of the same color then they double the planet value. The first to reach 500 points or an agreed total is the winner.

—user summary

Paint It Black is a roll and write game where players try to fill in the boxes of their sheet....in black!

The active player rolls two die, sum them, and all of the players fill in a number of squares equal to the sum, in any of the rows or columns matching either dice.

Bonus actions can be achieved by filling in squares in the shape of + or -.

After 12 rounds, the players sum the number of boxes filled in, and bonus points earned for filling the most in each area, and the person with the highest score wins.

In Seiðruna, two players compete for the favor of fickle spirits  by using elemental runes to craft precise ritual patterns.

On a turn, a player drafts a card from the central market, adding the card and its associated token to the corresponding position on their board. Over multiple turns, the player will draft cards based on various traits to create specific four-card patterns on their player board. With these patterns, the player invokes spirits, with four invocations forming the victory condition for the game.

Cards, once drafted, possess latent magic in the form of their rune tokens. While in a player's position, these runes can be used to manipulate the player's board. On a turn, before or after drafting, a player may "burn" one of the rune tokens on their player board, activating the burn effect of that rune. Some runes allow the player to manipulate their cards and tokens together, while others manipulate tokens while leaving cards untouched. Once burned, a rune token is permanently lost. Careful management of burn effects will enable high impact turns and clever invocations from unexpected positions.

Every rune has a unique and predictable effect, but with a catch: Each rune has two forms, and the form visible to one player will be upside-down from the perspective of the opponent. In this face-to-face game, the two players will be competing over the same set of cards in an open draft format, but the cards available to one player will be mirrored for their opponent. In this way, two players with the same market and the same available spirits will face very different conditions and will need to keep track of both their own options and their inverted counterparts.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 紙ペントリテ (Kami Pen Torite)
description:
In 紙ペントリテ, roughly "Paper and Pen Trick-Taking", players play a trick-taking game, the results of which are used to fill out a score sheet as one might in a roll-and-write game.

After a trick is complete, the winner chooses a card played to the trick, and the other players can all choose any other card. The players mark the chosen card in one of four rows, which look for the diversity of ranks taken, the same rank to be taken, an increasing value of rank taken, and the sum of the ranks taken.

The player that earns the most points in the third round wins.

VIVA! Woodwork is a drafting dexterity game in which beavers try to deliver the most weight of wood to a market.

Each turn players draft a number of sticks from the wood barn, and either place it to dam their river or load it onto their cart to deliver to the market later.

At the end of any turn, if a player is ready to deliver, they push their cart across the river. Any sticks remaining on the cart when it reaches the other side and then placed onto the scale.

The player then loads counterweights - showing how many points they are worth - onto the other side until it weighs too much

The game ends after all players have had a chance to deliver once, or one player has delivered twice. The player with the most points wins.

The Lumecon is part of the traditional folk festival Ducasse de Mons held in Mons, Belgium. On Trinity Sunday (57 days after Easter) 4 different groups take part in a choreographed battle that represents the fight between Saint George (the good) and the dragon (the evil). 
Each player has a character token representing one of the 4 groups and two counters for movement. There are 80 tokens (20 from each faction) that are drawn randomly and placed on the character token. Players use the movement counters to move the tokens around and when the movement is finished any counters that are in the player's area are collected. The first player to collect a specific combination of tokens is the winner.

—user summary

boardgame: Neo SNL: Tilelized Snakes and Ladders
description:
Neo SNL: Tilelized Snakes and Ladders is a game that celebrates the Chinese Spring Festival in 2025, the year of the snake. The game adds a tile-placement mechanism to Snakes and Ladders. During each turn, the player draws and places a tile to complete the gameboard, then rolls a die and plays like traditional snakes and ladders.

Tiles must be placed next to a tile placed, in such a way snake connected to snake, ladder connected to ladder. Edges with gold coins on a tile can connect to either a snake or a ladder. Game strategies will be a decision like "Should I build a snake/ladder or break a snake/ladder? Which will let me go faster than other players".

The game's graphic design combines snakes and cats to create a new image of a "snake." The game uses elements like "big gold nugget" and "Spring Festival Greeting" to generate the icons for extra actions for gameplay.

The player who reaches the end tile first is the winner.

—description from the designer

The Secret Alien Message is a unique and engaging board game where players encode and decode hidden messages using binary grids represented by stones on a Go board. Each 3x3 grid, called a "codeword," adheres to a special rule: exactly 5 of the 9 positions in the grid are filled with 1s (black stones), while the remaining 4 are 0s (white stones). This restriction creates a set of 126 unique codewords, making the game both challenging and mathematically intriguing. Players work together or compete to decipher the encoded messages, bringing logic and creativity to the table.

Setup
The game uses a standard Go board, played on squares rather than intersections, forming a 6x6 layout of 3x3 grids. This setup allows for up to 36 characters in a single message. Players also need black and white stones to represent binary values, and a decoder key that maps each 3x3 grid pattern to letters, numbers, or symbols based on ASCII encoding. Before starting, players decide on the message length and whether tiles can be rotated for added complexity. The encoder uses the start (+) and end (x) markers to frame the message.

Pieces
Each codeword is a 3x3 grid filled with exactly 5 1s and 4 0s, creating a distinct visual pattern for each character. The + and x patterns are special non-rotating codewords used as the start and end markers. The remaining 124 codewords can represent letters, numbers, and symbols. These tiles can be placed as is or rotated (90°, 180°, or 270°) to obscure their orientation. The stones make the game tactile and interactive, as players physically arrange and analyze the grids to decode the message.

Rules
The encoder places the 3x3 grids row by row on the board, starting with the + and ending with the x. Grids are either left in their standard orientation or rotated for increased difficulty. The decoder examines each 3x3 grid in turn, matches the pattern to the decoder key, and reconstructs the original message. The rule of exactly 5 1s in each grid ensures clarity and consistency, adding a layer of strategy. Beginners can start with non-rotated grids for an easier experience, while advanced players can challenge themselves with rotated tiles and longer messages, ensuring 
accessibility for all skill levels.

The alien language
The alien language uses 120 codewords out of the 126 permutations for its unique alphabet and numbering system. The alphabet consists exclusively of capital letters, with 26 letters, each represented by four distinct codewords to add variety and complexity. Numbers are expressed in base 4, inspired by the structure of DNA, and are also represented by four unique codewords for each digit (0, 1, 2, 3). The remaining four codewords are reserved for punctuation: space, comma, full stop, and apostrophe. Additionally, the language includes a Start/Begin symbol (represented by the + codeword) and an End symbol (represented by the x codeword), marking the boundaries of any encoded message.

—description from the designer

Expansion for Cat Packs that introduces 30 new vacation themed Cat Cards and new Card Effects!

—description from the designer

Monkey Thieves gathers from 2 to 8 thieves in the packed Indian city streets. Throughout the game, you will seek to outsmart your opponents with the help of cheeky monkeys to become the most successful of the thieves. To do so, you must skillfully manage your hand of cards, steal and retain valuable items, and perform actions to give you an edge or thwart your opponent's moves.

There are three game modes to choose from, each with its unique objective.

Age of Moon, a fast-paced sci-fi game which takes place in the near future, ii mankind's first lunar city.

Taking on the role of a huge conglomerate, players will advance the development of the moon while participating in various public projects.

However, there is one "rule" to the work of this world.

Workers need to be used for both business ventures and public works projects.

To start, players take a worker cube from a business card in their hand and place it on the same job in a public works project in the center. This reduces one task from the original card.

Once all tasks on a card are completed (i.e., all workers are removed), the plan is finished. Public works projects are displayed in the center and gradually fill up as players add workers.

At some point, players can 'take over' projects that have progressed nicely and turn them into their own businesses—this is called 'selling off.' The taken-over project becomes the player's, carrying over all progress made so far. After a takeover, only the red and green jobs remain from previously filled cards.

If you focus too much on your own company and send too many workers to public works projects, you might end up handing over an "almost finished" project to the player next to you. Make sure to evaluate the value of each card, claim high-return projects, and strive to become the top company on the moon!

—description from the publisher (translated)
publishers: Southern Cross Games (サザンクロスゲームズ)

description:
Believe in Me! (Please) is a satirical strategy game in which 2-4 players embody mischievous, self-proclaimed gods vying for the faith of gullible mortals. Through cunning, persuasion, and divine theatrics, you claim natural phenomena as your miracles to grow your cult and define your godly form by assembling a unique combination of chaotic, serene, or wrathful body parts. Beware of meddlesome heretics, inquisitors, and rival gods, who will stop at nothing to undermine your holy reputation!

With its mix of card-driven mechanisms, worker placement, and over-the-top humor, Believe in Me! (Please) is a battle of wits, strategy, and divine improvisation to see whose cult becomes the world's ultimate religion. Hallelujah!

—description from the publisher

Do you have what it takes to join ‘The 1% Club’? Based on the popular TV gameshow, this brain teasing boardgame will finally determine who amongst you can join the top 1% of quizzers in the nation!

The 1% Club, where the questions have nothing to do with remembering little-known facts, endless reams of dates or obscure quiz trivia, as instead they are all about applying logic, reasoning skills and common sense.

3 - 6 players are whittled down as they compete to make it to the end of the quiz and answer a question only 1% of the country can get right in the hopes of winning up to £100,000.

- description from the publisher

This standalone expansion adds two new asymmetric factions to the deck builder Partisan. Play 1v1 with the two newcomers, combine both games for 2v2 gameplay or go for all out war with 3-4 players!

The first new faction is Venta Belgarum, a religious faction feared by many for their powerful Armada. Their goal is build alliances, although their tendency to lean toward war can make this difficult a times.

The second new faction is Duro Cobrivis, who have long been biding their time, but are ready to make a lasting impact on the world around them. Nothing is more important to them than the morale of their people, so timing their moves will be crucial to success.

Using the same core mechanics as the first game, Partisan: Faith and Factions is a lightning fast deck builder where you manage your military, alliances and morale to once again assert your dominance over the Earth Former Universe.

—description from the designer

Mr. Monopoly has opened the Sale Vault, and there’s more for sale than ever before! The Monopoly Buy Everything Expansion brings a fresh twist to the Monopoly game and takes half the time to play. Just add this expansion to the classic Monopoly gameboard, and everything is for sale. (Classic Monopoly game required to play.

Players can buy every space (including Go to Jail!), and they can roll the Buy die to purchase more ways to win and abilities from the rotating Sale Vault. When all properties are owned, the richest player wins — or a player can win instantly by completing an instant-win Sale card!

—description from the publisher

Press your luck and pile up the cash! The Monopoly Free Parking Jackpot Expansion brings a fresh twist to the Monopoly game and takes half the time to play. Just attach this expansion to the classic Monopoly board, and turn the Free Parking space into a Jackpot. (Classic Monopoly game required to play. Sold separately.) Spin the spinner for a chance to win BIG money! Upgrade to the Deal Mobile token and draw Bonus cards to take free properties and more. The game ends when all properties are owned or when one player has gone broke. The richest player wins!

A FRESH WAY TO PLAY THE CLASSIC MONOPOLY GAME: Spice up the Monopoly game and play in less time with this Monopoly Free Parking Jackpot Expansion! THIS IS AN EXPANSION, NOT A STANDALONE GAME. (CLASSIC MONOPOLY GAME REQUIRED TO PLAY. Sold separately)

PLAY IN HALF THE TIME: Play the Monopoly family board game in about 40 minutes with this easy to learn, quick-playing expansion! It’s great for fitting in fun mischief with family and friends anytime

A FAVORITE HOUSE RULE IS NOW A REALITY: Attach this expansion to the Monopoly game and enjoy the classic Monopoly gameplay players know—plus an easy-to-learn twist! Transform Free Parking into a spin-it-to-win-it jackpot

SPIN FOR A CHANCE TO WIN BIG MONEY: In this fun family game extension, the Chance and Community Chest spaces are now Spin spaces. Players who land on them can spin the spinner to go for the Jackpot

DRIVE THE DEAL MOBILE: The Spinner and Bonus Cards let lucky players upgrade to the Deal Mobile token. When they drive it, they can get free properties and skip out on rent

COLLECT LUXE BONUSES: Draw Free Parking Bonus cards to score all kinds of lavish bonuses—like free properties, free houses, cash, and more

—description from publisher

Turns out, crime does pay! Spice up Monopoly  and play in half the time!

Just add the Monopoly: Go to Jail Expansion attachment to the classic Monopoly board to turn the Jail and Go to Jail spaces into desired locations. While there, players can collect Corruption or Super Corruption cards that let them get away with mischief — and score advantages over other players! The game ends when all properties are owned or when one player has gone broke.

The more time a player spends in Jail, the more time they have to collect Corruption cards, which let them wreak havoc, be sneaky, and gain an edge over opponents

If a player lands on Chance, the law is hot on their tail! They’ll need to roll the Escape die to try to get away. On Community Chest, they can roll the Heist die to steal cash.

Requires classic Monopoly board - not a standalone game.

- description from publisher

A big game packed in a small cassette tape case for the bloodiest zombie experience you can have. Take the role of one of the survivors and try to stop the zombie hordes marching toward the last human town!

Cassette Zombies is a simple, tactical game of survival terror. Face down brutal, brain-hungry zombies and complete unique missions. Are you brave enough to survive the most metal and brutal zombie apocalypse?

Easy but compelling game system for multiple tactical challenges. each character has different abilities and each mission has different difficulty levels for more replayability.

Can be played as a solo or cooperative experience - win or fail together!

—description from publisher

Starts with a magnetic board filled with colors, shapes, numbers and vowels, designed to develop cognitive ability from an early age. Adding cards, it evolves to awaken competitiveness in children from all ages. With the use of dices, it ultimately becomes a challenging and fun game for the whole family.

—description from the designer

In Trollbooth, 2-6 players take on the role of mischievous trolls who build bridges, collect tolls, capture unsuspecting travellers, and upgrade their caves to earn the most victory points!

The game is played over five rounds, each divided into five phases:


     Build Phase: Players add a Bridge card from their hand to the shared Bridge Row.
     Troll Phase: Players deploy their trolls to a bridge to collect tolls (i.e. resources).
     Traveller Phase: Traveller cards are revealed (one for each bridge), and players take turns choosing a traveller to capture.
     Crumble Phase: Players choose an unoccupied Bridge card to add to their cave, increasing the value of their resources.
     End of Round Phase: The First Player token passes to the last player to collect a crumbling bridge, the Bridge Row is refreshed, and players take their trolls back.


At the end of five rounds, players tally their victory points from the resources they've gathered, the special scoring conditions of captured travellers, and their cave upgrades. The player with the most victory points wins the game!

—description from the designer

What if you never get OUT ... of the bocage? ??

Our landing in Normandy and the battle in/through? the bocage.
10 10-day turns. Division level, except in a couple instances including Tigers!
8 miles per hex. Uses many mechanics from my NW Europe 1944.
Allied air supremacy - tactical and strategic.
Will the German player allow us to seize Cherbourg in a coup de main?
(In the very first playtest game, Cherbourg was taken by us, intact!  352. InfDiv was obliterated by naval gunfire - a 1/6 chance - and 4th and 90th InfDivs landed easily.  The 243. Static Div then did get into it first, but didn't have time to Deploy and the port was taken by our guys before it could be blown!)
Can Pvt Roberts and Sgt Culin come up with their "rhino tank" idea to get us through the bocage hedgerows and out-maneuver the Germans still confined/restricted by/in it ... in time?
If they do, and we start our breakout, can the Germans cut it off ... at Mortain or wherever else?

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Complementary Game is a vibrant and innovative strategy game that reimagines chess as a dynamic battlefield of colors. Inspired by the strategic intensity of chess and the psychological twists of cultural phenomena like Squid Game, this game blends familiarity with innovation to create an experience that challenges both the mind and the imagination. Played on a 10x10 board with squares randomly assigned one of four tetradic colors—Red, Blue, Green, or Yellow—the game introduces new rules of interaction based on color relationships. Each piece is tied to a specific color, except for the Queen and King, who remain neutral, anchoring the game in balance amid ever-changing conditions. Complementary Game invites players to adapt, form "complementary bonds," and navigate shifting strategies in a visually stunning and intellectually demanding arena.

Setup
The game begins with a 10x10 board where the colors of the squares are randomly shuffled but evenly distributed. Each player commands an army of 24 pieces: 10 Pawns, 5 Knights, 3 Bishops, 3 Rooks, 2 Queens, and 1 King.

3rd row: 10 Pawns are placed in a single line.
2nd row: The main army is set up in this order: Rook, Knight, Bishop, Knight, Queen, King, Knight, Bishop, Knight, Rook.
1st row: Four additional pieces (1 Queen, 1 Rook, 1 Bishop, and 1 Knight) are placed randomly, leaving six squares empty.
This setup strikes a balance between structure and variety, offering both symmetry and unpredictable openings, ensuring no two games feel alike.

Pieces
The pieces retain their traditional chess movements but gain additional strategic complexity based on the board’s colors and their own color associations:

Pawns (Red): Advance one square forward and capture diagonally. Pawns cannot teleport to the promotion line (opponent’s starting row).
Knights (Blue): Leap in an "L" shape (two squares in one direction, one perpendicular).
Bishops (Green): Move diagonally any number of squares.
Rooks (Yellow): Travel horizontally or vertically across the board.
Queens and Kings (Neutral): The Queen combines the movements of Rooks and Bishops, while the King moves one square in any direction. As neutral pieces, they are immune to all color-based rules, serving as stabilizing forces on the board.
Complementary bonds occur when adjacent pieces of complementary colors (Red ↔ Green, Blue ↔ Yellow) form a special synergy, granting immunity to penalties and unlocking defensive and teleportation advantages.

Rules
The game’s strategy revolves around how pieces interact with the board’s colors:


     Same color: Neutral; the piece continues its movement unaffected.
     Complementary color: The piece teleports to any unoccupied square of the same complementary color (e.g., a Red Pawn on a Green square teleports to another Green square).
     Opposite color: The piece loses its ability to capture until the next turn (Red ↔ Blue, Green ↔ Yellow).
     Complementary bonds: Adjacent complementary pieces gain immunity to opposite penalties, defensive boosts, and enhanced teleportation abilities, reflecting the synergy and resilience of cooperative play.


Players win by checkmating the opponent’s King.

Summary
Complementary Game elevates classic strategy with its bold visual design and unique color-driven mechanics. By tying each piece to a specific color while leaving the Queen and King as neutral anchors, it offers a rich blend of complexity and balance. Whether disrupting your opponent’s bonds, planning teleportation chains, or creating unbreakable defenses, Complementary Game redefines strategic board play in an exciting, modern context.

—description from the designer

A cheeky party/card game about Real-Life S#!T!

Everyone gets dealt some S#!T to Deal With (cards). Players take turns trying to deal with their "S#!T" to earn sweet-sweet good-feels (victory points) and win. Fine upstanding players will play Healthy Ways to Deal cards and spend coins to deal with their S#!T (cards). Players can use cards and coins from their own hand, or negotiate for additional cards and coins from other players. Chaos agents and desperate players might turn to the Unhealthy Ways to Deal cards that will help in the short-term.... but for a PRICE! A little luck, good choices, and teamwork can go a long way, but sometimes you just want to get messy and sabotage the competition.

At the end of the game if you've earned the most Good-Feels, YOU WIN!

Detailed Game Mechanic

Players take turns trying to Deal with their S#!t cards.
On their turn players can choose to 
A. Deal with Their S#!t by playing resource cards that match or exceed the resource value to "deal" on their S#!t card. Once dealt with, players earn the good-feels listed on the S#!t card. Players can spend coins for money and also negotiate for resources and coins with other players. 
B. Discard Healthy Ways to Deal Cards and redraw new cards.
C.Go to Work (earn a coin).
D. Draw an Unhealthy Ways to Deal Cards. These cards can help a player in the short-term but exact a price in good-feels, coins, or cause chaos among the players at the table.

Most Healthy Ways to Deal cards are the basic game resources: Self Care, Medical Care, Mental Health, and Political Action. Some of the additional special cards in the Healthy Ways to Deal cards can increase a player's hand size, earn them coins, or sabotage other players.

After each players turn, play continues with the next player. If a player dealt with their S#!t card, they draw new S#!t to deal with on their next turn.

How to Win

Players can race to a set number of good-feels for the win or play for a specific period of time and see who has earned the most good-feels at the end of the play time.

In addition to victory options, the game includes gameplay variants such as Community S#!T, Teams, Collaborative, or Healthcare Variant.

boardgame: OOrtum: A seasonal folk horror skirmish wargame
description:
For longer than memory the world has been Oortum . 
The ground is thick with rot as amber leaves fall, burying life beneath them. 
Dawn is cold, obscured by mist. 
Each day ends with a tired sun casting long shadows over a dying landscape. 
Each morning, a new sun rises and an old day repeats. Time has stagnated, bent back over on itself so often that years have lost all meaning. 
Warbands of feudal knights in ancient, rusted armour clash with mud covered redcoats, wandering Ronin, tightly ordered legionnaires and hodge podge bands of scattered travellers. 
All lost, all trying to survive and for some, crushed by the inertia of Oortum, searching for ancient knowledge which might change the world.

Oortum is a miniatures agnostic skirmish wargame set in a world trapped in the season of Autumn. 
It plays on ideas from British and Irish folklore, British horror (such as the ghost stories of M.R. James) as well as having a meta narrative and gameplay rooted in paganism and nature based spiritual practices. 
Warbands of between 4 and 10 models struggle for survival.

—description from the designer

A great leader is needed to forge an alliance between the four fleets and unite the galaxy. Players will lead their alliances to victory by conquering planets in vast interstellar battles. The player with the most galactic influence at the end of the game wins.

The game takes place over a series of battles for control of a planet. Players draw cards in an attempt to make the strongest alliance from their hand of cards. Alliances must not contain duplicated fleets or ranks. Remaining in a battle and drawing new cards each require influence to be added to the planet. The victor in each battle wins the planet as well as any influence that has been added during the battle.

—description from the publisher

You are stuck in a time loop and the only way to break the cycle is to rescue the Princess, through love, money, or...dragon? Players will need to explore the village and the castle, collecting items, performing quests and battling bandits to win the Princess' freedom. They will also need to interact with their past selves, maybe passing a valuable item to an older copy, or distracting a guard while a future version sneaks into the castle.

There are 12 locations available at the beginning of the game arranged around a clock-face. On a typical turn a player may wake up a new copy (beginning another iteration of the time loop), move (always forward) to a new location, and perform other actions at their current location.

—description from the publisher

The islands of the pacific produce and value many different resources. Pacifica is a light strategy card game in which players trade these resources throughout the Pacific, trying to put together baskets of goods for sale. On your turn you can take a free resource from an island local to you, trade in the market, or use canoes to take or trade resources from a more distant island.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Second World War at Sea: North Cape
description:
Adolf Hitler launched his sneak attack on the Soviet Union in June 1941. The Red Army reeled back from the assault, losing thousands of tanks, guns and planes. On 21 August 1941 the first convoy loaded with equipment and weapons left Iceland for the Soviet Arctic port of Archangel. For the next two years, the Royal Navy would force through convoys to the Soviet far North against enormous risks and terrible odds.

North Cape covers the Allied attempts to push convoys through the Norwegian and Barents Seas while German aircraft, submarines and surface ships try to stop them. Famous convoy operations like those of PQ12 and PQ17, British carrier raids, German-Soviet destroyer skirmishes along the Arctic coast — all of these and more are included. Like all games in the series, this one come stuffed with scenarios and variations on them.

North Cape follows the same story-arc structure as other new Second World War at Sea games, with history and scenarios interwoven to tell the story. Operational scenarios take place on the huge operational map covering the Norwegian and Barents Seas from Iceland in the southwest to Novaya Zemlya in the northeast, from the Norwegian coast to the Arctic pack ice.

When task forces make contact, play moves to the Tactical Map, where ships fight each other with guns and torpedoes. Battle scenarios start here, and let you get right into the action and shoot at the enemy (if you make explosion sounds, we won’t tell).

Most operational scenarios are very straightforward — each side knows what the other must do. The Allied player has large convoys of vital merchant shipping that must reach their destination. The Axis player has bases in Norway well-placed to intercept them, with submarines and aircraft that can hit them almost continuously. Aircraft are drawn in a semi-random fashion so neither player has perfect intelligence about their numbers and location. German surface ships are few in number, but there’s a lot of open sea in which they can hide and strike the convoys at vulnerable moments. And with war raging simultaneously on many other fronts, the convoy escort forces are stretched very, very thin.

The Germans have the battleship Tirpitz and battle cruiser Scharnhorst, plus cruisers like Admiral Scheer and Admiral Hipper and a flock of destroyers in support. The Allies have powerful British and American battleships like Washington, Duke of York and Alabama, but it's rare to see more than one of them enter the combat zone by herself. They also have aircraft carriers, but when the weather turns nasty, these become little more than inviting targets for U-boat skippers. And the weather's nasty most of the time.

North Cape includes a huge 22x68-inch map, 550 pieces (210 double-sized ship pieces and 340 normal-sized square ones) and 42 scenarios.

Note: North Cape is a thoroughly re-imagined edition of our old Arctic Convoy game.

—description from the publisher

Series: Second World War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

boardgame: Yes or No: The Game Politics, Philosophy & Religion Expansion Pack
description:
Michael Knowles's beloved internet show turned party game phenom has grown once again! With 110+ new cards, you can delve deep into the most animating forces of humanity, finding out even more about your friends and family, and judging their stances on the most ...unifying... discussion topics in American life. Because the original game wasn't controversial enough.

—description from the publisher

Onder de tien ("Under 10") is a card-shedding game from West Friesland in the Netherlands.

Make sure you get below 10 points as quickly as possible
and at the right moment shout: “Under 10!”

The game:

The aim of the game is to have as few points in your hand as possible. Each player starts with 5 cards in his hand and tries to get below 10 points as quickly as possible. You do this by playing 1 or more cards each turn and taking 1 card in return. In addition to single cards, it is possible to discard combinations. For example a pair, 3 of a kind, 4 of a kind, a small or large straight or a flush. This helps you get a low number of points in your hand sooner! But be careful, there are 3 different Joker cards in the game, with which you can put your opponent at risk!

As soon as a player has less than 10 points in their hand, this player may choose to end the round. He then shouts 'Under 10! ́. This player wins the round as soon as he has the lowest score. If this is the case, each player's points are recorded. In this case, the winner receives 0 points. But be careful, if you shout 'Under 10!' and there is a player who has the same number of points or fewer points, you have lost the round. In this case you will receive 20 penalty points plus the number of points you have in your hand. In this case, the player with the lowest score is the winner of this round and therefore receives 0 points.

Raising the stakes on Shark Tank. Draw a card to get a problem or product that only you can solve. The investor gives everyone 2 minutes to come up with their best pitch ideas and then pitch it. Whoever comes up with the best new solution or product for their topic gets A BILLION DOLLARS (okay, a billion dollar chip). The player with the most money at the end wins! But there are other cards that can stop pitches, steal money, allow you to merge with other players, or just earn the money anyway. Play this and you might just come up with the next billion dollar idea!

—description from the designer

Tsunawatari is a 2-player game based on the theme of tuna traveling across the Pacific Ocean from east to west.

In this game, cards representing the ocean are placed between 2 players facing each other, players take turns in moving their tuna closer to the opposite side.  Cards have various effects but only the player who placed the cards knows what they are, use your movement efficiently, and the first player who gets their Tuna starting at one end, all the way to the goal at the other end wins.

Rumian is a competitive strategy board game combining resource management, exploration and high adventure. In the medieval realm of Rumian, players compete to shape the fate of the kingdom according to their vision. But only one will rise to become a true legend and receive the Golden Relic of Rumian - The prestigious symbol of remarkable achievements.

Each player starts the game with 2 secret quests. Each turn, players take 4 actions: gather resources, construct buildings, craft goods, or explore. They can also complete their quests, play cards, or battle to conquer new lands. How well you manage to influence the realm is rewarded with Victory Points.

The player with the most Victory Points at the end of the game is the winner.

—description from the publisher

Let the games begin!

How to win a game of Letterprik?

At the start of the game, a letter is chosen using the pricking board, and a 5-minute timer begins. Each player has a board with 10 categories. During the 5 minutes, you must fill in your board with answers, but each answer must start with the chosen letter.

After time's up, the checking round begins. You’ll compare answers to see who was the most original. If your answer is unique, you earn full points. If one or more players have the same answer, you earn fewer points.

Go through all the categories, and the player with the most points wins the round of Letterprik!

—description from the publisher

Kan jij onder tijdsdruk presteren? In dit party spel ga je proberen de meest originele antwoorden te verzinnen binnen de tijd.

Prik een letter en laat de spanning en gezelligheid beginnen. Of je nou van een spel spel houd, of urenlang door wilt spelen, met dit hilarische gezelschap spel is het mogelijk!

Let the games begin!

English:

Can you perform under pressure? In this party game, you'll try to come up with the most original answers within the time limit.

Pick a letter and let the excitement and fun begin. Whether you're a casual player or ready for hours of non-stop play, this hilarious party game has got you covered!

—description from the publisher (Dutch)

boardgame: Забытые боги: Рёв Рагнарёка (Forgotten Gods: Ragnarok's Roar)
description:
The Рёв Рагнарёка ("Ragnarok's Roar") expansion adds 110 new cards to the 120 cards of the base game, significantly increasing the already impressive number of possible combinations.

Besides new story elements, the expansion introduces another divine character, the giant wolf Fenrir, threatening the extinction of all life. If he remembers his name before the other gods, they are doomed, so this must be prevented at all costs. Remember your name and stop Fenrir before he unleashes the end of the world!

The Crete upgrade for Asterion uses a diamond board.

The game plays the same, with the following changes.

Place the board in the middle of the playing surface. Place the Minotaur tile on the centre space and the Minotaur figure on it.

Each player discards as many cards before the game starts and uses board spaces as follows:

4-player: discard 2 cards, use the whole board.
3-player: discard 4 cards, use the inner 41 squares.
2-player: discard 5 cards, use the inner 25 squares only.

When playing, cards must be placed on board squares only, and adjacent to a previously placed tile.

Refer to the official rulebook for more information.

It can be purchased as a stand alone game or as an expansion to Asterion.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Emperor of the Battlefield:   Napoleonic Wargame Rules for the Revolutionary and Empire Years
description:
Emperor of the Battlefield gives you good reason to use historic tactics in your games because they work. Napoleonic warfare means combined arms to me. I need to see those columns moving up, the lines giving fire, and the reactions to determined cavalry. In this game you pick your target priorities, not me.

They are your guns, sir, not mine.

Emperor of the Battlefield has the Control Test and the Morale Test at its heart. The Control Test deals with the passage of orders within the unit and measures its ability to charge, react and change formation. The Morale Test is for any firing or close combat aimed at your unit.

So, you roll to control and the enemy rolls to react.

Emperor of the Battlefield uses an alternate bound sequence with reaction point options and choices. This makes it suitable for single or multiplayer games where each player controls a Division or a full Corps if they wish. In general, all troops move as you command. The section on orders is both simple and optional.

Extra players add enough command and control issues for me.

—description from the back cover
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
An environmental game with active ideas for young people.

Go Green! has been developed to show young people that saving the environment can be fun.

The winner is the first person to visit nine collection/deposit banks across the board by moving your pawns around the board.  You can walk, cycle or drive between them using dice to control your movement.

Components:

     6 Railway-only Action tiles


Changes in setup:
Place Railway-only Action tiles next to the Action tile offer.
If you are playing without the Court of Progress expansion, return a tile with Court Action back to the box.

Changes in gameplay:
When you resolve a Develop action, you may instead of taking a second tile from the offer, spend 1 Thaler, to gain any Railway-only Action tile laying next to the offer.
You may place the Railway-only Action tile on a link, using a Worker to mark your ownership, and resolve the action according to the standard rules. You cannot use it on your Player board.

Rule Nothing Out: With 200 Rule Cards, this game is sure to be a hit time and time again!

Rules of Engagement (AKA How To Play): Split up into teams of Detectives and Accomplices. Accomplices draw a Rule Card that they must follow for the entire round. Then, Detectives must ask the Accomplices questions to try and guess what the group is up to. Be careful! Detectives only get three tries to guess the rule.

What's Inside: 200 Rule Cards, 6 Guess Cards, 4 Sample Question Cards, and Instructions

—description from the publisher

When the girl woke up, she found herself in a nightmarish world shrouded in darkness. She didn't know why she was there. But she strangely realized something. The pocket watch she was holding had the mysterious power to rewind time. And she knew that by exorcising the darkness of this world, she could return to her original world. The girl set out into the strange world in order to return to her original world.

Alice's Time Labyrinth is a solo deckbuilding game from Komainu Games.

The game comes with 3 play modes:

     Basic mode (30 - 50 minutes)
     Expert mode (100 - 180 minutes, with a save mode)
     2 player mode (30 - 70 minutes, requires 2 copies)
Midnight Stalker is a print-and-play escape room-style game in which you help a detective solve a cold case from the 70s that could save the life of an innocent.

This is a fan-made board game themed around *The Idolmaster: Million Live!*.

Players take on the role of producers at an idol agency.  
Your goal is to turn your assigned idol into a top idol, competing against rival producers.

However, as newcomers, you don't yet have access to major opportunities.  
Producers must promote their idols and expand their activities,  
while idols strengthen their skills through lessons.

Once ready, earn victory points (fans) through live performances and jobs.

At the end of the game, the idol and producer with the most fans will be the winners.

—description from the publisher (translated)

The game concept found in the rules:

"Your transport firm is approached by Santa Claus as a candidate for a major yearly contract. On Christmas Eve he personally delivers to all households, but he needs others to prepare local depots from which to take the gifts. Right now, these gifts are in the central depot for the country and you must get them to the individual city depots. You will rent reindeer sleighs to transport them. This year you will be competing with other candidate contractors. The one who does the best job will get the contract."

Han Heidema gave out specially designed games as Christmas cards; one for every year from 1990 to 1999. This was card #6 and was sent out in 1995.

Players take turns renting sleighs and transporting gifts across a map of the US, competing to make the most money before Christmas Day.

boardgame: Зомби в доме: Заражение – Экоблогер (Zadventure: Eco-Blogger)
description:
Зомби в доме: Заражение – Экоблогер ("Zadventure: Eco-Blogger") is an expansion of the Zadventure game. It allows up to five players to play, with a new character called Eco-Blogger and four new items.

The Eco-Blogger girl has a pet owl. You can use it to distract zombies in order to escape from them. However, the owl will only obey you if you have food. During a fight with zombies, you can drop a feed token and automatically escape and how many moves you take is determined by the spinner.

New items:

     Binoculars allows you to peep at any closed tile on the field.
     Shopping Cart allows you to push zombie off your space and avoid a fight.
     Grapple Gun allows you to grab an open item-tile within two spaces.
     Gamepad allows you to move any zombie on the board instead of your turn.

boardgame: Зомби в доме: Заражение – Учёный (Zadventure: Scientist)
description:
Зомби в доме: Заражение – Учёный ("Zadventure: Scientist") is an expansion of the Zadventure game. It allows up to five players to play, with a new character called Scientist who has a portal gun. Now there are no obstacles for you. Just use the portal near the wall to get through where there was none before. True, it only works twice, but that's better than nothing.

Also, this expansion introduces a new Tamer mode, where one player initially plays as the Tamer against the others. Each turn, he summons a new zombie from the grave and controls all the zombies on the board. However, at some point, he can summon a zombie tiger that is completely immortal. The player character's main task does not change, but the Tamer must use their zombies to capture four special artifacts on the board in order to win.

In Yonmoque Hex players try to be the first to get exactly 4 of their pieces in a row. And no more than that... Get 5 in a row and you immediately lose the game!

Yonmoque Hex applies the 'swapping mechanism' from Othello; opponent pieces that are trapped in between two of your own pieces are turned over so that they become your own. The differences with Othello are that you don't just place tiles on the board, you also move them around. Furthermore, placement and movement is determined by the colour of the fields of the board. There are fields in the colors of each of the players (white and blue), as well as 5 neutral fields. Given that there are less squares for the white player (8 versus blue's 12), the white player always gets to play the first move.

The roar of powerful engines bursts from the dark mouth of the Pyrenean tunnel just moments before gleaming cars streak past. One mistake could mean certain destruction, but the lure of the Spanish Grand Prix keeps the drivers focused and aggressive.

Heat: Tunnel Vision introduces two new racing tracks. The Spanish Grand Prix takes your car through treacherous tunnels, forcing you to trust your instincts in the semi-dark. The Netherlands track allows you to open your engine to full throttle before throwing you into a succession of treacherous corners.

Take part in the 1965 Championship season and install the new Front Wing upgrade to draft off the other drivers and charge to victory.

This second expansion brings chicanes and tunnels for more technical and high-intensity races, front wing upgrade cards, and new event cards, in addition to expanding the player count to seven by adding a purple driver (or eight with the Heavy Rain expansion).
family: Game: Heat – Pedal to the Metal

description:
In Thieves of Eldris, fortunes are forged in the shadows, one stolen coin at a time.

Amid the chaos of bustling merchants, you lurk in the shadows, eyes locked on your target, ready to slice their coin purse and make off with the spoils. The Thieves' Guild has taken notice of your talents, but earning your place among them won't be easy. To prove your skill, you must outwit your rivals, steal from your targets, and keep your true identity hidden.

Roll the dice to navigate the crowded square, rearranging townsfolk and setting up daring heists. Slip past rival thieves undetected, and when the time is right, liberate your targets of their precious coin. But beware, one wrong move and your rivals might expose you, stealing not only your newfound riches, but your invitation to the Thieves' Guild.

Will you become the guild's newest initiate, or will your ambitions leave you penniless in the gutter?

—description from the publisher

The roar of battle echoes across Eldris.

The feud between the Elkyr and Lycarans has raged for generations, but this clash could reshape the future of Eldris forever. Townsfolk across the land are watching, ready to lend their skills to the clan they trust to lead them into a new age.

As the leader of your clan, you’ll command your Captains to bid for powerful recruits, deploy warbands to contested regions, and inspire your forces to push the Roar Track in your favor, intimidating your rival and hindering their plans. Unleash the unique class abilities of your recruits to outmaneuver your opponent and seize control of the battlefield. Each victory raises more of your clan’s banners, bringing you closer to claiming the throne of Eldris.

Only one clan will rise to power, will it be yours?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Robot Quest Arena: Blaze & Matilda Pack
description:
This expansion pack for Robot Quest Arena includes the Blaze robot mini and player board, 4 tiles, 20 cards for the Shop Deck, a 10-card starting deck, 3 gems, 2 blue cubes, and 10 red cubes.

This pack's shop cards are all about speed! Move your robot around the arena while refilling your hand with even more firepower to obliterate your opponents!

boardgame: Robot Quest Arena: Nozzle & Markus Pack
description:
This expansion pack for Robot Quest Arena includes the Nozzle robot mini and player board, 4 tiles, 20 cards for the Shop Deck, a 10-card starting deck, 3 gems, 2 blue cubes, and 10 red cubes.

This pack's shop cards are all about manipulating the shop! Get discounts and free cards and re-use your already-played cards.

boardgame: Robot Quest Arena: Tack and Inez Pack
description:
This expansion pack for Robot Quest Arena includes the Tack robot mini and player board, 4 tiles, 20 cards for the Shop Deck, a 10-card starting deck, 3 gems, 2 blue cubes, and 10 red cubes.

This campaign-exclusive pack's shop cards introduce an exciting new ability! Players may spend energy to activate secondary effects on their cards. Double the power!

boardgame: Robot Quest Arena: Bubble & Tito Pack
description:
This expansion pack for Robot Quest Arena includes the Bubble robot mini and player board, 4 tiles, 20 cards for the Shop Deck, a 10-card starting deck, 3 gems, 2 blue cubes, and 10 red cubes.

This pack's shop cards are all about scrapping your deck for maximum power! Trash those unnecessary cards and power your robot to its full potential!

boardgame: Robot Quest Arena: Bot Battle Promo Pack
description: This expansion includes 43 new Shop cards & 13 new tiles. Cards are a mix of campaign exclusives and convention promotions that are sure to amp up the fun!

boardgame: Robot Quest Arena: Big Arena Pack
description:
The Big Arena Pack includes: a double-sided player board with one side accommodating up to 4 players on the 7x7 Arena, and the other 5+ players on the huge 9x9 Arena. Also includes additional starting deck cards, cubs, and double-sided shop board.

—description from the publisher

Time HAMMER The Dual is for 2 players age 12+ and takes about 30-45 minutes. You will: play Gadgets (unique cards), Attack rival Agents in the past or future, Jump Agents to other Decades, repair HAMMERs that infect decades and Buy stuff.  Players will also set Agents in past and future decades for command of assets & powers, to gain Skill and raise their Repair Bonuses (to win the game).

You Get: One rules booklet, 1 Die, 2 Historians, 12 cubes, 72 Poker size cards made of: Agents (Human, Fido & Bot[robots)), Commands, Epochs, Gadgets, HAMMERs, Jumps, Quarks and Info.
 Cards also generate random numbers like an eight sided die, so you will often,ﬂip the Draw Decks’ top card, to reveal a Flux  or Stasis when a random result is needed.

—description from the publisher

The era is the Universal Century. Having developed interstellar navigation, humanity discovered a habitable planet in a star system several dozen light years from Earth. Blessed with rich mineral resources, the planet was named Deloia, and gradually developed as its population grew. However, the Earth Federation Government, which ruled over Deloia, did not recognise its autonomy, and continued to oppressively rule over it with its powerful military force.
In the 152nd year of the Universal Century, the people of Deloia rose up to break free from the rule of the Earth Federation. They rallied under the leadership of Dr. Samarin and began their struggle to break free from Earth's rule.
Liberation of the Colonised Planet is a two-player simulation wargame that recreates the tactical battles of the Deloia War of Independence. Players take control of the Deloiean Liberation Army and the Earth Federation Forces respectively, and fight with the aim of achieving or preventing Delorean independence.
The game includes five scenarios, including the fierce battle at Stanley Heights, which was a turning point in the war, and the Battle of the Carnac Mountains, the largest battle of the war, and it is also possible to create your own scenarios.

—description from the designer

Carrier Battle! South Pacific 1942 is a game that recreates the battles between the Japanese and American navies in the South Pacific during the first half of the Pacific War, at the tactical and operational level.
The game features the warships of the Japanese and American navies that were active in the South Pacific, each represented by a single counter.
Aircraft are also represented by type, and the main aircraft such as the Zero fighter are rated by model (the difference between the A6M2 and A6M3). The system is innovative, with an emphasis on search and planning, and the game recreates the essence of aircraft carrier battles.
There are 9 tactical scenarios and 7 operational scenarios. As well as historical naval battle scenarios such as the Battle of the Coral Sea, Battle of the Eastern Solomons and the Battle of the Santa Cruz Islands, there are also virtual battle scenarios, in which the battleship Yamato and the escort carrier Sangamon make an appearance.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Victory or Death is a scenario supplement for the rules Live Free or Die. This PDF supplement includes 10 of the most famous battles fought in the American War of Independence. Battles include Bunker Hill, Harlem Heights, White Plains, Trenton, Brandywine, Saratoga (Freeman's Farm), Monmouth (the morning), Monmouth (the afternoon), Guilford Court House, and Eutaw Springs. Note that this scenario book does not include a copy of the core game rules, Live Free or Die, which is available separately and includes scenarios for Princeton, Germantown, and Camden.

—description from the publisher

In the Pacific War that began in 1941, aircraft carriers and their aircraft became the main players in naval battles. However, during the night when aircraft could not fly and in bad weather, cruisers ans destroyers fought fiercely with their guns and torpedoes.
The Solomon Naval Night Battle game recreates the surface battles between the Japanese and Allied navies in WW2. There are 16 scenarios, which recreate battles such as the Battle of Guadalcanal, the Central Solomons, and the Battle of Leyte Gulf.
The system is a fusion of a precise tactical level and a fleet command system, and the hit judgment is carried out for each shell and torpedo. The power of the guns and the thickness of the armor are also made into rules, but on the other hand, the playability is high, and most scenarios can be completed in less than an hour.
Game Scale
1Hex = 1.5 km
1Turn = 5 minutes
1Unit = Individual warship

—description from the designer

boardgame: Naval Battle in the European Seas
description:
In naval battles during WW2, aircraft carriers and submarines played the leading roles. However, this does not mean that battleships and cruisers were no longer used. In particular, in the Mediterranean Sea and the seas around Europe, battles were fought many times between surface vessels of the Axis and Allied forces.

Naval Battle in the European Seas is a game that recreates the surface battles between the Axis and Allied navies during WW2. There are a total of 16 scenarios, including the famous Operation Rheinübung, the Battle of the Barents Sea, the battle between the British and Italian navies in the Mediterranean, and the battle near Norway.

The system is the same as that used in ‘Solomon Night Naval Battle’, and combines a detailed tactical level with a fleet command system. Shells and torpedoes are judged on a per-shot basis. The power of guns and the thickness of armor are also regulated, but on the other hand, the playability is high, and most scenarios can be completed in less than an hour.

Game Scale
1Hex = 1.5 km
1Turn = 5 minutes
1Unit = individual warship

—description from the designer

The Decisive Battle! "A Baoa Qu" is a simulation wargame that recreates the Battle of A Baoa Qu, the final battle of the One Year War (the Zeon Independence War).

Game Scale:
1 unit = approx. 4 space battleships, approx. 12 mobile suits/space fighters
1 turn = approx. 10 minutes

—description from the designer

Embark on an exciting journey through the vibrant world of trade, conquest, and adventure. Set in the Golden Age of the Islamic Caliphate, our game offers players an immersive experience filled with strategic decision-making, thrilling quests, and epic battles.

Gameplay Overview
Merchants of the Caliphate invites players into the vibrant and competitive world of trade during the height of the Caliphate. Players take on the roles of ambitious merchants, navigating treacherous trade routes, bustling markets, and historic landmarks in a quest to amass wealth and prestige.

Strategic movement, savvy trading, and the completion of lucrative trade quests are key to success, but the journey is filled with uncertainty. Players can invest in utilities like horses and camels to enhance transport and speed across the board, or in cavalry and camelry to defend against warfare events and seize raiding opportunities. These tools can turn the tide, offering crucial advantages as merchants compete for dominance.

As merchants race to fulfil their quests, every decision shapes the outcome of the game. The ultimate goal? To rise as the Master Merchant and secure a legacy of unmatched influence and prosperity.

With its dynamic mix of strategy, chance, and player interaction, Merchants of the Caliphate offers a thrilling and immersive gameplay experience where no two sessions are ever the same. The race to dominance is on - will you claim the title?

—description from the designer

Draw a card, then take turns to select a coloured token and place it in a octagon-shaped grid. When tokens in the grid match the pattern on your card, declare the card as complete. Complete 5 cards to win.

Stocklab is a stock market simulation game for the general public. Players compete to build assets by investing in several stock sectors and mutual funds, and using various actions optimally to become the investor with the most coins at the end of the game.

For the game setup, shuffle and arrange 4 stock cards in a row from left to right, place 4 stock tokens on the number 5 (starting price) of each sector as a marker for the stock price of that sector, then place the mutual fund card between the 2 stocks in the middle. After that, shuffle the pile of economic cards and place 6 cards on top of each stock card, return the unused economic cards to the box. Distribute the initial capital of 15 coins to each player, a screen divider to store and keep the number of coins secret, and random order cards. Shuffle the pile of action cards and place them in a place that is easily accessible to all players.

There are four phases in a round: bidding phase, action phase, selling phase, and economic phase.

1. Bidding phase
Open the action cards twice the number of players. Players auction their turn order, each player fills their fist with coins secretly, with minimum 1 coin. After that, players open their fists simultaneously, then compare the number of coins with other players and redistribute the order cards starting from the player with the most coins. If it's a tie, compare the order cards that have been previously owned. Pay all coins used for bidding to the bank.

2. Action phase
Each player takes 1 card in turn, starting from player with order card 1 until all action cards are gone. Players can keep the action card as stock, and the card effect is lost, or activate the effect to use that card's action, then the card is discarded.

3. Selling phase
Depending on the order, each player can sell as many shares of one type as they want.

4. Economic phase
Open the top economy card of each sector. Execute the effect of each card, then discard all activated economy cards.

The game ends when the last economic card is revealed. Each player then calculates the total assets they have from all stock and mutual fund cards at current prices, plus the number of coins they have.

The player with the highest total asset value is declared the best investor. In the event of a tie, the player with the smaller order number in the last round is the winner.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Travelers plan a route around the world with their animal travel companion.  The winner of each trick gets first dibs on the die pool which are used to travel between placed terrain cards.  The most efficient route created after 4 rounds wins the game!

This game involves trick taking, dice selection, and a bit of math to maximize card and dice placement.  Bonus points to those who can predict their trick taking savvy at the start of each round.  Be careful as the cards you use for winning tricks are also used for making your route so your plans can be disrupted at any turn by your opponents played cards.

Rolling Bears is a dice-drafting game full of player interaction and high tension! Rounds are quick to play! Try to outscore your opponents by collecting the high-scoring dice and also by ending the round with the "Best Animal Set". Rolling Bears is two games in one, the two variants being called "Wuppertal Rules" and "Hollandica Rules". Players take turns to roll dice into the "Pool" (i.e. the centre of the table), and then take selected dice back out. The sets taken out must form either a "matching set" or a "running set". Dice extracted in this way are then placed on the player's special 'Enchanted Forest' scoreboard. This scoreboard is cleverly designed to work a bit like an abacus and it constantly keeps track of the player's score. The value of a die depends on its colour, and there are dedicated columns on the scoreboards for each colour to be placed on. Animal dice (Fish, Hedgehogs, Ravens, Wolves and Bears) are all stored in Animal pens, instead of the 'number' columns used for the rest of the dice. At the end of each round, the player with the Best Animal Set (ie the largest number of a particular type of animal) will move these animals to the Best Animal Set column, where they will score bonus points for that player. There is always huge rivalry and player interaction involved in the battle to have the Best Animal Set! At the end of each round, each player reads the scores indicated on their scoreboard and these are recorded and a total is kept across all rounds played.

Rolling Bears comprises thirty 16mm D6 'logo' dice. The 'logo' side of each die contains the engraved image of an animal and the full set consists of six 'Fish' dice (green), six 'Hedgehog' dice (ivory white), six 'Raven' dice (sky blue), six 'Wolf' dice' (Grey) and six 'Bear' dice (black).  Each scoreboard is double-sided, one side for each of the two rulesets (Wuppertal Rules and Hollandica Rules). Please note that exactly the same set of 30 dice is used for both versions of the game, but in an altogether different way for each.

Wuppertal Rules: When using Wuppertal Rules, the first five rolls of the dice involve players rolling the dice into the Pool, by colour, six at a time. The first player will roll in all six green dice, and then create a set using these green dice only. Then the next player will roll in all the white dice and create a set from the Pool, using white and green dice. Then the next player will roll in the blue dice and create a set. Then the next player will do the same with the grey dice and the following player will do the same with the black dice.
