Now all the dice will either be in the Pool, or taken out and placed on players' boards. Now we move into a new phase of the game. The next player (after the one who rolled the black dice) now has the opportunity to re-roll a number (up to six) of the dice that currently remain in the Pool and to make a set after re-rolling. At this point, the real tactics start to enter the game. Players have to make difficult, and crucially important, decisions about which dice to re-roll. These can involve giving that player the best probability of obtaining a valuable 'set' to be extracted, while at the same time ruining potential opportunities for that player's opponents. One such tactic is to re-roll an animal which would have been useful or valuable to an opponent. But there are many more ways to be tactical with your re-rolling. Wuppertal is essentially the 'basic' game, where "matching sets" consist of dice whose numbers AND colours match, while "running sets" are dice which form an ascending sequence of numbers, while all of these dice MUST be a different colour. An "animal set" is a special case of a "matching set" where ALL the dice in the set are identical animals. Animal dice are always placed in the Animal Pen and will remain there until the end of the round. If they form the Best Animal Set at the end of the round, they will be moved to the Best Animal Set column and will score 8 points each. Otherwise, they will remain in the Animal Pen and score no points for that player. While there is a certain luck element (it's a dice game after all, and that makes it exciting and competitive) Wuppertal is also very tactical and skilful. One of the key elements is learning which dice to re-roll at the beginning of your turn!

Hollandica Rules: When using Hollandica Rules, the first SIX rolls of the dice involve players rolling the dice into the Pool, five at a time. On each of these rolls, the five dice consist of one of EACH colour, called a 'Clutch' of dice. The first player will roll in the first Clutch, and then create a set choosing from these five dice only. Then the next player will roll in the next Clutch and create a set from the dice in the Pool. Then the next player will roll in the next and create a set. Then the next player will do the same. And then the next and then the next.  Now all the dice will either be in the Pool, or taken out and placed on players' boards. Now, as for Wuppertal, we move into a new phase of the game. The next player (after the one who rolled the last Clutch) now has the opportunity to re-roll a number (up to six) of the dice that currently remain in the Pool and to make a set after re-rolling. At this point, the real tactics start to enter the game. Players have to make difficult, and crucially important, decisions about which dice to re-roll. These can involve giving that player the best probability of obtaining a valuable 'set' to be extracted, while at the same time ruining potential opportunities for that player's opponents. One such tactic is to re-roll an animal which would have been useful or valuable to an opponent, but there are many more ways to be tactical with your re-rolling. Hollandica is essentially a far more complex and tactical game than Wuppertal, where "matching sets" consist of dice that are ALL different colours, but showing the same numbers, while "running sets" are dice which form an ascending sequence of numbers, AND are all the same colour. Please note that in Hollandica, the values of the dice colours are not predetermined. You can decide how much the colours are worth (to you) as you collect them! This is all done via your scoreboard. Please also note that there are Bonus Points scored in your number columns if you collect more than three 'number' dice of the same colour (4 dice: 10 points, 5 Dice: 20 points, 6 dice: 30 points). But these extra points will be automatically included by your scoreboard, so you don't need to add them on yourself! There is no such thing as an "animal set" in Hollandica. Animal dice are collected as 'wild cards' to extend or connect "running sets" of the matching colour (so, for instance a Raven could be used as {any number} within a sky blue "running set". When transferring such a "running set" to the scoreboard, all the number dice get placed in the appropriate number columns, while the animal die is placed in its very own Animal Pen. You can never use more than one animal in  a "running set" and you can never collect more than one of any type of animal. So if, for example, you have a Raven already, you are not permitted to use a second Raven in another sky blue "running set". So, each type of animal has its own Animal Pen and when captured will be placed in this pen on its own and will remain there until the end of the round. At this point, the player with the most individual types of animals will be declared the one with the Best Animal Set. These individual animals will then be moved to the Best Animal Set column and will score 10 points each. Hollandica still has a certain luck element, but it is vastly more tactical than Wuppertal and so is very challenging and has an extremely high replayability factor.
   
—description from the designer

How well do we know our friends? The frank and intriguing game "Truth Detector" will help you forget about shyness, bare your soul, discuss secrets without fear of being revealed. Ask - how is it? You anonymously answer "yes" or "no" to questions, and then guess how many people in the company answered affirmatively. Politics, relationships, sex, bad habits and everything else with an 18+ restriction. Only scandalous questions, only hardcore!

Phrasium comes packed with 276 Phrasium Cards, each featuring a well-known phrase and three possible origins. Teams can score big based on their knowledge and intuition! Think you know the answer? Go for 3 points. Feeling a bit unsure? Play for 2 points and let the other team read out three options—one right, two wrong. Totally stumped? No worries! Go for 1 point and get a helpful 50:50, with the right origin and one wrong one to choose from. It’s all about insight, strategy and loads of fun!

The game also comes with 3 different types of Challenge Cards to shake things up! You can swap out a phrase you don’t like, make the other team go all in for 3 points, or pick a specific player to answer. The key is keeping your opponents on their toes!

—description from the publisher

Daqin is a masterpiece of abstract strategy where innovation meets timeless tactical depth. Drawing inspiration from the fusion of Eastern and Western philosophies, Daqin reimagines the dynamics of strategy with hidden identities, transformative stacking mechanics, and the intriguing influence of neutral Stones.

Every decision in Daqin ripples across the board, creating opportunities for bold maneuvers and unforeseen shifts. With its unique blend of mystery and strategic complexity, Daqin challenges players to think beyond the ordinary and embrace the thrill of discovery with every turn.

Setup
Daqin is played on a 10x10 board. Each player begins with 24 pieces: 10 Pawns, 5 Knights, 3 Rooks, 3 Bishops, 2 Queens, and 1 King. Additionally, 4 neutral Stones are placed on the 4 central squares of the board.

Pawns are placed face-up in the second row. The other 14 pieces are shuffled and placed face-down on the first row, with 4 forming hidden stacks. Face-down pieces remain hidden until they move, ensuring the opening phase is filled with intrigue and unpredictability.

Pieces
Regular Pieces
Face-Down Pieces: Hidden pieces cannot capture, ride, or carry and are revealed only after their first move.
Pawns: Pawns move and capture as in traditional chess. They can ride or carry but cannot promote while part of a Stack.
Knights, Bishops, Rooks, and Queens: These pieces retain their classic movements. They can participate in Stacks and execute the game’s unique Rider and Carrier mechanics.
King: The King cannot participate in Stacks as a Rider or Carrier and retains its central role in the game.
Riders and Carriers
Stacking Rules: A friendly piece can move onto another friendly piece to form a Stack. The bottom piece becomes the Carrier, and the top piece becomes the Rider.
Pieces of the same kind cannot ride each other.
Stacks are powerful but vulnerable: capturing a Stack removes both pieces from the board.
Movement and Capturing:
The Carrier loses its ability to capture but can move normally.
The Rider can dismount during the Carrier’s move if riding a long-range piece (Rook, Bishop, or Queen) and capture independently after dismounting. Other Riders dismount at the Carrier’s destination.
If the Carrier has moved at least one square, the Rider cannot capture immediately upon dismounting.
Neutral Stones
Movement: Stones move one square orthogonally per turn. They cannot return to their previous square in the same turn. Moving a Stone counts as an optional second move.
Interactions:
Stones can push both friendly and enemy pieces or Stacks.
Stones can swap places with adjacent friendly pieces but not with enemy pieces or Stacks.
Stones cannot capture or be captured.
Rules
Revealing Pieces: Face-down pieces are flipped over upon their first move. They cannot capture, ride, or carry while hidden.
Stacks:
A Stack consists of two pieces. Pieces of the same kind cannot form Stacks.
Capturing a Stack removes both pieces from the board, making them powerful yet risky.
Rider and Carrier Mechanics:
The Carrier moves the Stack but cannot capture while carrying.
The Rider can dismount along the way (if riding a long-range piece) or at the Carrier’s destination and capture independently.
Neutral Stones:
Stones can push pieces and Stacks but cannot swap places with enemy pieces or Stacks.
Moving a Stone is optional and counts as a second move per turn.
Victory Condition: The game is won by checkmating the opponent’s King.

Exploring the importance of thought-balance and the philosophy of Gross National Happiness (GNH). Rollpa is about the philosophy of GNH and how it relates to personal and societal well-being. You’ll also discover the role that ‘balance’ plays in being happy.
Rollpa is the amalgamation of GNH philosophy, Shamatha meditation symbolism and UN Worldly Events. 
GAMEPLAY​ :

     Players will be competing to Win the most number of Pyramids.
     Pyramids will be won by earning Merits for your Domains of Wellbeing by moving around the GNH board and hosting UN World Days. 
     And hope not to meet an Indulgent Monkey or an Abstinent Elephant.


The mechanics used are to present the 9 Domains of Human Wellbeing from the Philosophy of GNH. The game is balanced by the Monkey and Elephant symbolism from Shamatha meditation, to show our minds’ interactions to the world around us. Rollpa is powered by 39 United Nations Days to raise awareness on the Global Sustainability Agenda.

—description from the designer

The ukiyo-e woodblock prints belong to the most unique art Japan has to offer. By playing Kōshodō, you immerse yourself in the 'floating world' of 18th and 19th century Edo (current-day Tokyo). You assume the role of an ukiyo-e publisher, tasked with bringing the most valuable set of prints to the Edo market. You try to appeal to your customers with images of landscapes, city life, 'beauties', actors and much much more.

The players try to collect their ukiyo-e by strategically trading with opponents and with four central stacks of cards. Points are earned by collecting the most valued prints, the 'beauties' and 'actor portraits' in particular. Meanwhile bonus points can be gathered by placing a series of similar cards next to each other. However, as not all cards are visible, you will need to guess who of your opponents is in the lead and who is lagging behind. And take care: ghosts, warriors and censorship will offer unpleasant surprises!

All the cards in the game are digitized copies of historical ukiyo-e  prints, kept in the collections of the Royal Library of Belgium, the Royal Museums for Art and History in Brussels and the Rijksmuseum in Amsterdam. As such, you are playing with the exact same images that were sold in Edo!​

—description from the publisher

Bees are among the world's most important pollinators. They enable plants to reproduce, help crop production, and make delicious honey. However, bees face threats and must compete with other bees to survive.

In Apis Mellifera: the Bee Game, you play as a beehive. Your goal is to become the strongest hive and survive the year. To do so, you will face numerous challenges, including pests, animal attacks, harsh weather conditions, and other beehives vying for your resources. You have three rounds to grow your hive, gather nectar and pollen, make honey, fend off the threats to your hive, and see if you can become the strongest hive at the end of the year. The hive with the most victory points wins.

This bee-themed worker placement introduces actual bee facts and science into its gameplay. Do you have what it takes to ‘bee’-come the strongest hive?

—description from the designer

Welcome to the Librarium, a world where a word is more dangerous than a bullet, and the possession of books, which give their owners powerful powers and abilities, gives dizzying power. That's why the most dangerous and powerful figure is the Librarian, who has unlimited access to the knowledge of the Ancients. He stands at the helm of the secret society “Librarium”, which rules the world order. Having spent years honing the art of putting words together from letters and turning them into powerful spells, the Librarian believes he has no equal.
But the brave Conspirators are up to the challenge: they've started a rebellion - they're not happy with the current state of affairs.
The Librarian, with the help of his loyal Guardians, must put down the rebellion if he is to maintain his dominance of the Librarium. In turn, the Conspirators seek to overthrow the usurping Librarian, and the Outcast, disillusioned with the Librarium, plans to destroy all of its members.
Use the full power of your vocabulary in the struggle for power in the secret society of Librarium. Act according to your role, attack your rivals by making spell-words out of letters. But try not to accidentally hurt your own in the heated battle, if, of course, you still have supporters...

The goal of the game is to attack and destroy enemies by making spell words from cards with letters.
Each player's strategy depends on what role they have been assigned.

     The Librarian wins if he is alive at the end of the game and all of the Conspirators and Outcasts are dead.
     The Guardian wins if the Librarian is alive and all Conspirators and Outcasts are dead.
     The Conspirators win if the Librarian is dead and at least one Conspirator and Outcast


the Librarian is dead and at least one Conspirator is still alive.

     The Outcast wins if he is the only one left alive.


—description from the designer

It's getting dark. After an eventful day the horses are going back to the stable. Help them get back to their respective box stalls and gather delicious apples which the horses love.
During their turns, players alternatively pick horse cards and place them in the stables - their goal is to collect enough points to close the box stalls. For the right arrangements of cards, the players win rosettes to collect additional victory points.

—description from the publisher

Body Be Gone is a lighthearted card game where players take on the role of cleaners tasked with disposing of bodies in the most creative and humorous ways possible.

Navigate quirky scenarios and laugh-out-loud moments as you strive to become the latest franchisee in the world’s premier corpse removal service. This game offers a unique blend of storytelling and gameplay that will keep you entertained for hours.

Gameplay takes place in two phases: a card drafting round, and a card resolution phase. In the card drafting round, players pass cards around and hope to collect cards with matching symbols. In the card resolution phase, players lay out the cards they collected - telling a story using all of the elements on their cards, and gaining points based on how well the symbols match. The player with the most points wins!

Rex, Felix and Bunia are happy pets that love fun. Today, they are going on a trip to the park. Turns out numerous adventures await them on the way!
Which of the friends will get to the park first?

The game contains 2 variants: 
Variant 1 for children from the age of 3 is a competitve game:
The player's task is to collect a set of 3 tokens for their pet for their pet, place them in their board and then reach the park which is the finishing line.

Variant 2 for children from the age of 4 is a cooperative game:
All players work together to bring all animals to the finishing line.

There is a great commotion at our kindergarten today!
We will play hide-and-seek - with children's toys!

This is a cooperative game for children. The goal is to reach the multicolor field and collect 8 tokens on the way. Therefore, the children roll a dice and move the piece to the nearest field in the colour indicated by the throw.

boardgame: Die Eiskönigin: Völlig unverfroren – Das verdrehte Wettlaufspiel
description: Who is the Queen of Arendelle? Where does Olaf get his carrot nose and who grew up with the trolls? 1 - 4 players aged 4 and up face the tricky questions about Elsa and Anna. For each picture, tiptoi tells the appropriate story from the Disney film "Frozen".

LIMITED COMMUNICATION, UNLIMITED HAVOC! 
Omega Roboto is a limited communication co-op game  inspired by classic super sentai shows  where each player pilots a different part of the same giant robot.

TERRIFYING MONSTERS 
Face 6 GIANT MONSTERS , in 18 missions each with its mechanics and a final form that flips the strategy upside down!

C-C-COMBOS! 
Combo cards require players to work together to be activated! Each has a SUPER SENTAI power pose.

UPGRADE YOUR ROBOT
Between episodes, you'll gather new ACTION and EQUIPMENT cards to face stronger enemies.

Don't just stand there! The city needs its heroes:
OMEGA BEATDOWN: INITIALIZE!

—description from the designer

Tsar reimagines the reign of Nicholas II, the last Tsar of Russia. The game begins with his accession to the throne in 1894 and his fate depends on you. Through a realistic simulation of Russian society, Tsar explores the inner workings of autocratic regimes, with a lifelike portrayal of official corruption, the cult of personality surrounding the Tsar, the violence of oppression and resistance, and the conditions that led to revolution. The game’s narrative may follow historical realities or may diverge from the historical record into alternate histories. Depending on your decisions, Russia may pursue liberal reforms or maintain a strict autocracy, experience victory in war or defeat, industrialize its economy or stagnate, stabilize its political regime or witness the collapse of the Romanov dynasty.

Players occupy the center of the Tsar's regime, advising and influencing him while competing to advance their own factional goals. In multiplayer mode, the game is semi-cooperative with shared resources, a single hand of cards, and overlapping VP objectives. Each player controls a different Faction within the regime with multiple Characters based on historical figures, and players gain their decision-making capacity through the placement of their Characters. Tsar is mostly card-driven, with shuffled decks generating randomly-timed events and a "Coded Deck" of unique numbered cards generating events at specific times and in response to specific conditions. Other mechanics include worker placement, resource management, and a hybrid system of action points and bidding. With its built-in automation for Non-Player Factions, Tsar easily transitions from multiplayer to solitaire play, with no extra components and minimal rule adjustments.

—description from the publisher

You play together for the entry to the fairytale castle of the princesses Abra and Cadabra. By cleverly using your Fillys, you quickly collect the necessary symbol keys before the traps block your way to the castle and the magical 13th hour strikes.

Roll the dice. Decide together which horse will be moved. Move the horse no more than the maximum distance indicated by the dice. Discover symbols on the hidden chips and use the special actions!

To win, find all 6 symbol keys, place the traps around the castle and move a filly in front of the castle.

Fillys glow in the dark and are made of Swarovski Elements!

Imperius is a game of intrigue and conflict for 2-4 players. Outwit and outmaneuver rival factions to score victory points. Play continues until one or more houses earn 20 victory points, which will trigger the end of the game. The house with the most victory points at the end takes the Crown Imperius and rules over the Known Universe.

The kickstarter edition comes with both expansions in their own tuck boxes packaged inside the base game. Includes alternate art slipcover and additional cards.

Artist Alley is a vibrant board game where you and your friends compete as artists trying to make the most money at a bustling convention. Each round, a unique client with a limited budget arrives, ready to spend on the best artwork. But in the competitive world of conventions, every sale is a battle of strategy, pricing, and adaptability. Can you outsmart your rivals and become the top-earning artist?

Strategic Sales with a Twist
Every player secretly selects one product card to offer, revealing their choices simultaneously. Clients are picky, with three key rules: they start buying from the lowest-priced items, they haggle when they spot two identical products, and they skip entirely if three or more artists present the same thing. Balancing pricing, originality, and predicting your opponents' moves is the heart of the game.

Advanced Mode: Unique Clients, New Challenges
For those looking to spice things up, the advanced mode introduces special clients with unique buying behaviors. From collectors hunting for variety to bargain hunters seeking deals, each client changes the rules and forces you to rethink your strategy. This layer of unpredictability ensures no two games of Artist Alley are ever the same.

The Perfect Mix of Fun and Strategy
Artist Alley is easy to learn but packed with strategic depth, making it ideal for casual gamers and seasoned strategists alike. Whether you’re an art lover or just love outwitting your friends, this game delivers a colorful and competitive experience in every round. Gather your products, plan your prices, and claim your place as the ultimate convention artist!

—description from the designer

Celebrate and travel through the magical land of Neopia with this special Neopets edition of MONOPOLY! Line your pockets with Neopoints as you acquire property throughout; the many iconic Neopian lands. Build Neohomes and Neolodges, invest in Neopian resources, and traverse the many locations of Neopia on four unique Neopian transports. Encounter exciting events through the Money Tree and Wheel of Excitement cards. From the streets of Neopia Central to the glittering towers of Faerieland - and everything in between - you'll have a blast while exploring Neopia and growing your hoard of Neopoints. There are investments to be made, so don't wait - roll the dice and enjoy this Neopets edition of MONOPOLY!

Under the white sand of Pesky Island there are many treasures to discover. Explore the entire island and collect as many precious stones as you can. Will you manage to have more than your opponents before the loot runs out?
Roll the dice, and pick up the precious stones, but take care with the other pirates and the skull, they try to pick yours!

—description from the publisher

Bajo la blanca arena de Pesky Island se encuentran numerosos tesoros por descubrir. Explora toda la isla y recoge las máximas piedras preciosas que puedas. ¿Conseguirás tener más que tus adversarios antes de que se termine el botín?
Lanza el dado y recoge las piedras preciosas, pero ten cuidado con los otros piratas y la calavera, ¡intentarán quitarte las que hayas cogido!

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

This is a compact version of Nice Buns that uses cards instead of tokens.  This version only supports 2-4 players.  There are only 14 of each of the colored bun cards and 8 of the fish head bun cards.

Includes 210 trivia cards featuring a wide array of movies and TV shows, from 'The Godfather' to 'Game of Thrones'.
Designed for 2 or more players, perfect for game nights, parties, or family gatherings.
Compact and portable design makes it easy to carry for trivia fun anywhere.
Each card offers a mix of challenging questions and fun facts to enhance your media knowledge.
Made in China, ensuring high-quality production and durability.
This trivia game is not only a source of entertainment but also a great educational tool, helping players discover fascinating facts and trivia about their favorite films and series. It's an excellent gift for movie lovers and trivia enthusiasts alike. Whether you're looking to host a thrilling trivia night or just enjoy a casual game with friends, this game promises to deliver excitement and competition.

The ALL IN Tiwanaku Add-ons box is packed with upgrades:

+  119 Wooden Tiles, engraved and stained in the matching colors;

+ 50 Note Pads, printed with the board layout to facilitate play; and

+  20 Additional Scenario Discs, to extend game play even more!

The wooden tiles box is within this expansion, and requires the base game for play.

TONS OF FUN: Looking for awesome family games? ReactSee brings the whole family together with an engaging, loud, energetic, fast-paced fun activity. This is the perfect pick from games for family game night with kids.

GREAT FOR KIDS: ReactSee is the best choice from card games for families, designed to give children an advantage due to their quick reaction times and reflexes. It greatly improves reading, cognitive and comprehension skills in a form of play.

WINNING: players must react quickly and accurately to end with the least cards and win the game.

Gundam Assemble is a tabletop miniatures game that uses approximately 2-inch-sized Gunpla for battles.

Each unit has abilities that reflect their unique characteristics, allowing players to enjoy scenarios that follow the original story, or diverge into situations that transcend the world of the Mobile Suit Gundam series; thus, providing both a sense of recreation and distinct battles.

Totika Kohatu (pronounced "Toe-tee-ka Ko-ha-two") is a challenging rock stacking competition for 2 or more players. These beautiful wood rocks are irresistible and they are going to be very popular at the next game night. No set up, simple to learn, each round 3-10 minutes. Pronounced "Toe-tee-ka Ko-ha-two," this wood rock stacking game is challenging for children, teens and adults.

Players take turns rolling the die and balancing one rock, stone or gem of the rolled color on top of the stack. Knock over the stack and lose that round of play. Stack the last rock and win.

Claws Out Card Games - The hilarious, fast and fun card games for adults, teens and kids! Choose between the 2-4 player or 2-8 player games. Claws Out is a wildly fun cat themed card game for families and friends, and they're pawsitively clawsome cat games for kids 8-12 +. Claws Out Card Games are the purr-fect cat themed gifts for cat lovers, and claw-some holiday games for family fun!

Bring Your Claws - Your friends and family are probably awesome, but it’s time to bring your claws and win this card game! Players race to get rid of all their cards by matching color and number. Use special cards to shift the game into unpredictable territory and snatch victory from the other players!

Wildly Addictive Fun - As the game progresses, it becomes increasingly frenzied fun to the finish line! Every round plays differently and is riotous fun! Get rid of all your cards first and yell “Claws Out” to win! WARNING - Gameplay is about 20 minutes, but can lead to endless hours of play and wildly addictive fun!

Easy to Learn, Everyone Can Play - Takes only a few minutes to learn and is easy to play! Great family games for kids and adults. Both written instructions and YouTube video instructions code are included. The best choice for exciting kids games, adult party games, or board games for kids 8-12 +

Play It Anywhere! - In your home or on a trip, in a bar or on a ship! Claws Out Card Games are small and easy to carry! They’re great travel games for kids and adults, game night party games for friends, fun board games for adults and family, and fantastic table games for kids 8-12 +

—description from the publisher

The year is 1842. Oxten, once a sleepy coastal hamlet, now buzzes with newcomers and the need for quality wooden goods is increasing. From dining tables to rocking chairs, the villagers desire pieces that will last a lifetime. With honed skills and experience, the woodworkers of Oxten fulfill the villagers' demand for handcrafted heirlooms and gain notoriety along the way.

In Joinery, players compete to become the most renowned woodworker in Oxten by fulfilling woodworking contracts for villagers to provide them with various goods - wooden bowls, benches, cupboards, and more. In doing so, players may receive coins to upgrade their workshop and buy materials as well as gain Mastery Points for completing more complex contracts.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Tiny Epic Game of Thrones: Heroes Pack
description:
The Heroes Pack introduces a captivating selection of alternate leaders for the seven playable houses in the Tiny Epic Game of Thrones. Each leader offers unique strategies and a wealth of possibilities as you delve into the game's epic world. Will you choose to embody the ruthlessly pragmatic Cersei Lannister, or will you lead your armies with the unwavering honor of Robb Stark?
The additional content from the Heroes Pack is compatible with all modes of play, including competitive, cooperative, and solo.

-From the original Kickstarter page

In The Ultimate Breakfast Scramble!, players pick a menu card, then race to collect breakfast cards to try to be the first to collect all of the items listed. Special action scramble cards shake things up.

In Space Dogs: Galactic Strike, you want to trade space dogs, avoid alien invasions, and beware of the black hole. Try to be the player with the fewest points in your "space station" when the black hole comes to suck you in.

In Photobomb, nobody knows the secret image except for the dealer with the picture card, who is trying to pretend they don't know. You're objective is to catch the photobomber and earn points doing so. The first player to 30 points wins.

In the party game Group Chat, you build text threads, then anonymously vote with thumbs down and hearts to decide who the best and worst texter was. Whoever gets the most red hearts wins the round, and whoever gets the most thumbs down gets kicked out of the chat next round. Play until someone wins seven rounds.

Survive The Island: Monster Pack contains three new monsters to bring twists to your game: the pterodactyl can lift you off the ground and leave you stranded far from safety; the whale creates chaos by tipping over boats, forcing you to rethink your escape strategy; and the unpredictable octopus can snatch players when they least expect it, making the race to escape even trickier.

boardgame: The Burning of Kingston Card Game
description:
It is October 1777. The Continental Army has just won its first major victory at Saratoga, and the British “Redcoats” are furious. Looking for retaliation, Lieutenant-General John Vaughn is leading forces to New York's current capital, the city of Kingston, with orders to burn it to the ground. And, although most of the town's soldiers are away fighting in the south, a few brave colonists have stayed behind to salvage what they can before the town is destroyed.

This is our history. This is the story of our game.

In the Burning of Kingston Card Game one player will play as the Kingstonians: everyday people who, upon hearing of the British ships docked at the Rondout, race to rescue valuables, keepsakes and important documents from the doomed town.

The other player will play as the Redcoats, British soldiers, who have been ordered to march on the town and burn down its buildings.

—description from the publisher

How to Win: Get all nine cards of one African crop and one ASANTE! card first.

Basic GamePlay:
Once the dealer shouts, TUANZE! (too-WHAN-zeh!), Go! Players trade ALL AT THE SAME TIME—shouting the amounts and swapping the cards in Swahili—

MOJA (MOE-dyah)
    MBILI (mBEE-lee)
    TATU (TAH-too)

OVERALL RULES:

To start, players are dealt with a mix of different crops and Asante! cards.  Sort the cards to help determine which of the ten crops you will collect along with one Asante! card.

Let’s Go!

Without showing what you have, trade 1, 2 or 3 cards of the same crop for the same number of cards with other players.  Trade what you don’t want and keep what you do want.

Score!

Once a player has gotten all nine of one African crop and one Asante! card, tally the points and play again.  Continue playing as many rounds as you like.

Win!

The player with the highest score wins!

Winning The Game:
Got it? Yell NIMESHINDA! (nih-meh-SHEEN-dah!) and all trading stops. Once proven, that player wins!

—description from the publisher

Shadows of Ruin is set in the dark age of Arathian history, long after an event known as The Great Cataclysm laid waste to the surface world of Arathia and caused the downfall of the great empires that once ruled the land.

You and your fellow adventurers enter this tumultuous world, each with your own motivations: some seek gold, some seek power, but all seek answers. What will be your claim to Arathia's history?

Shadows of Ruin is an old-school dungeon crawler steeped in lore and nostalgia for the golden era of board games. Create a party, choose a quest and enter the dungeon!

With simple yet strategic combat, emergent narratives and options for roleplaying, your party of heroes will rid the world of the evil that has risen!

—description from the designer

A Witch in Time Troupe Box

3 Spirit miniatures made in fine quality resin.

Contains:
1 x Antonia the Noonday Witch Miniature and Stat Card
1 x Zorya the Dawn Witch Miniature and Stat Card
1 x Danica the Dusk Witch Miniature and Stat Card

Taxi is a light strategy, route building, tile placement game with a little bit of take that mechanics mixed in.  Players play the role of taxi drivers, navigating a growing cityscape. The goal is simple yet challenging: create an unobstructed route from a starting location to an ending location, as shown on your Fare card, using a variety of road segment cards laid out in a shared grid.

But the road to success is filled with unexpected detours! Your opponents are crafty, setting up roadblocks like parades and traffic jams. And if that wasn't enough, watch out for those special cards that could have you sidelined for speeding or making way for an ambulance.

It's a race to be the first to build an unobstructed path, and maintain it for one round of play.  The first player to do that, wins.

—description from the publisher

Take it to the hoop in this quick-paced game of slam dunks and defensive strategy! Battle for position as you work your way from rookie to MVP and lead your team to the league title. Will you take home the trophy or sit out on the bench? Test your skills at Double Dribble and find out!

—description from the back of the box

Foolish Fool is a goofy party game for folks who want to have silly fun together. Each turn you read the rule on a new card. From then on, you must follow that rule, which gets harder and harder since you get a new rule each turn! Some rules apply only to you, while others apply to the whole group. Get caught breaking the rules, and you lose a token; lose all three of your tokens, and you lose the game.

—description from the publisher

Slip on The silly see fingers to become a stacking octopus...a Stacktopus. Stacking these cups should be easy, but when you're wearing octopus arms on your fingers, it becomes silly, competitive, fun! Race to match your stack of cups to the card shown to win!

Introduction: Abacus is a game of about setting up supply chains to minimize the amount of resources you expend on the board. Players will try to interfere with eachother's supply chains while transferring stones between their own. Is Abacus a Realm-like? Its a matter for debate I suppose. Its certainly less blatantly realm-like than Gliss for instance. Abacus is Corey L. Clark's first game of 2025. A game of Abacus cannot end in a draw.

Setup: To play Abacus you will need a hexhex board between size 4 and size 6 and an unlimited supply of stones in two colors. size 5 (61 cells) is recommended for a standard game and the 37 cell size 4 can be used for a light game.

Gameplay: On a turn you must either place a stone of your color on any empty cell or move a stone in one of the six cardinal directions, in a straight line, through any number of empty cells such that the group the stone moves into is smaller after the movement than the group the stone originated from prior to the movement.

Objective: The objective of Abacus is to have fewer stones than the opponent when the board is full.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Andromeda's Edge: The Dice Tower Promo Cards
description:
Originally available as part of D Pack from the Dice Tower 2025 Gamefound campaign.

Luke and Maximus have teamed up with Sergio to come up with a stellar promo card! There's one copy for each event deck, so you can play it with your favorite set of game consequences! Special note - this card is a fun one-off and not meant to replace any core gameplay.

Each player rolls all 6 of their dice. In turn order, each player selects a benefit matching on of their results.

Includes 4 identical copies.

The players take turns to roll and move. They start at space 1 and have to get to space 90 to win the game. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. One player plays the thief, the others play the pursuers.
If a pursuer gets on the same space as the thief, the thief escapes and advances 6 spaces. If the thief gets on the same space as a pursuer, he retreats to the space he just left to avoid being discovered.

—user summary
publishers: Verlag von Auerbachs Deutschem Kinder Kalender

description:
The players take turns to roll and move. They start at space 1 and have to get to space 60 to win the game. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players.

—user summary
publishers: Verlag von Auerbachs Deutschem Kinder Kalender

description:
The players take turns to roll and move. They start at space 1 and have to get to space 40 to win the game. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players.

—user summary
publishers: Verlag von Auerbachs Deutschem Kinder Kalender

description:
Carver's Park is a domino climbing game for 3-4 players.

Carver's Park uses 2 double 6 domino sets, and adapts the Slalom mechanism from Jass, where the strongest dominoes alternate between the low and high end every trick.

Win by shedding out by navigating the Slalom, in addition to the speed limits, which dictate the maximum combination size per trick!

The players take turns to roll and move. They start at space 1 and have to get to space 60 to win the game. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players.

—user summary
publishers: Verlag von Auerbachs Deutschem Kinder Kalender

description:
Reaper’s hideout is a print&play, polyomino roll&write, puzzle, exploration, role-playing game. For 1-∞ players, ages 8 and up, 30 minutes long. Game objective: explore the map, collect treasures, gain experience, fight monsters and become the first hero to defeat boss monster.Game components per player: One-page game sheet, two D6 dice, and colored pencils. Blue, red, yellow, silver, black and green. Each turn, players explore the dungeon using polyomino shapes, collect colored treasures for character attribute growth and then face creatures when they are ready. All players are playing at the same time so there are no delays.

—description from the designer

In Ringbahn Roulette, your mission is simple: ride the Berlin Ringbahn all the way around to get back home. But be careful—all your friends have the same goal, and they will do anything to stop you.

Each roll of the dice will bring you all sorts of crazy, suprising and funny challenges. Letting you experience all the things you love and hate about Berlin.

—description from the designer

ButtChess is a fast, easy-to-learn, and highly addictive 2-player game that adds a fun, quirky twist to the classic game of checkers. With vibrant pieces and engaging strategies, ButtChess offers a refreshing and lighthearted take on traditional gameplay.

Whether you're a seasoned player or a newcomer to board games, ButtChess accommodates all skill levels with adjustable difficulty settings. Choose your challenge and dive into a fast-paced battle of wits.

The aim of the game is simple, get 3 of your pieces to the other side of the board first, or, take 4 of your opponent's pieces.

—description from the publisher

A trivia game played by 2-7 players. The players will answer questions from the Hungarian history. Each answer will grant you a specific ammount of movement points depending on the difficulty of the question. 
 The questions you have to answer depends on the tile you are standing on. The further you go, the later the question's year from history is going to be.

You have to "answer yourself through" the different times of Hungarian history, starting form the "Honfoglalás" era to the revolution of 1848.

The game has 3 different playstyles included in the rulebook.

No obscure books or random movies, just 700 well-known phrases to act out across three levels of difficulty.

Fabulous, family-friendly and fun to play anywhere and everywhere. And with eight different ways to play, there’s always a new challenge.

Gather your friends, unleash your inner actor, and let the games begin!

—description from the publisher

The ultimate trivia game that tests your quick wit and rapid recall across five captivating categories: Movies, People, Literature, Slogans and Music.

Race against the clock as your team tries to identify four of the 800 classic one liners, from iconic song lyrics and memorable movie lines to timeless literary excerpts, catchy advertising slogans and legendary quotes to infinity and beyond.

Don’t worry if you are stuck…there are clever clues, so it ain’t over ‘til its over! And if you beat the clock there’s a bonus question to score 10/10. There are no complicated rules, no boring questions – just endless fun and fierce competition. Are you ready? Then let’s begin…because you can’t light a fire without a spark.

—description from the publisher

The purpose of this game is to collect antique objects by trading, advertising, buying, selling and exchanging objects, preferably in sets of 6 or 4 of the same colour, as a complete set of 6 of the same colour is worth 3 times the catalogue price, a set of 4 is worth twice the catalogue price. When you have the foruth or the sixth card of one set in your hand, and only then, you may finish the game. In order to prevent losing one of the four cards in a set you can insure them against fire, robbery, etc... (caused by risk cards). As soon as somebody finishes the game, then everybody's cards are counted. The player with the cards of the highest value win the game.

Surrounded by water, no dry land insight and a worn down sailboat that probably won’t last another year. There has been some rumours circling that if you follow the constellations in a particular order you will find the land you have been looking for!

The night sky will aid you in this adventure… but not everything is smooth sailing. The landscape has changed drastically after all the flooding, if where used to be mountains now it’s sea.. what else could have changed?

The games is played over 16 rounds, in each of them the players can take a total of 3 actions. These actions vary from moving the ship in the map, plant crops and fish/collect water.

Printable components:
- Player Sheet 
- 16 Seasonal Objective Cards
- 4 Sky Cards

Additional components:
- Pen or Pencil
- 1 D6
- 3 Small Cubes (or other small objects, such as beans or even small legos)

—description from the designer

Project Revolution is a Japanese trading card game printed with characters of manga, novel, TV animation, game software, etc.
In this game, both sides should utilize their own decks to summon different unit characters, and with the coordination of various traps and strategies, plan out different tactics on the battlefield to defeat the opponent.In the game, cards are divided into three different categories: "Units (ユニット)", "Traps (トラップ)", and "Strategies (ストラテジー)", units can move in a 3x3 grid, two units in the same grid will trigger a battle, and units in opponent's grid or central grid can deal damage to the opponent, the player suffers 7 damage will lose the game.

Das wurmt is the short card game about long worms that get longer until they are short enough.

There are worm ends on the playing field, each with a head card in the same colour. If you turn the head, you ‘discover’ a worm appendix, which makes this worm grow longer. However, the actual ‘growth’ only becomes visible when the next head is played and the previous head is turned over again. As not all worm appendages are the same, some worms can grow faster and others slower, depending on which card is played. Worms that appear at the ‘ideal length’ earn their discoverer bonus points, while worms that are too long can be quite ‘wormy’ and result in hefty point deductions.

—description from the publisher

Little Dragon Whirlwind is a lively game of running and dexterity in which everyone wins together when they create good weather with the wind ball.

Storm threatens! Rain goblins hijack the cloud bridge to make the weather gloomy. But high above, the Little Dragon Whirlwind swings his wind ball to stop their flight. 
Together, the players make sure that 4 sun wizards reach ‘Cloud 7’ before the grumpy bad weather gnomes. New goblins keep arriving to prevent magical sunshine. But with the help of his spherical pendulum, which all the players get swinging, the Little Dragon Whirlwind ‘blows’ the cloud bridge free again and again – making the sun shine for everybody.

—description from the publisher

Mach die Flatter is a colourful memory game in which everyone causes confusion in order to keep track themselves.

Who is that sitting on the washing line? A parrot with a blue head and yellow wings, of course … and behind it sits a red one … er … no, it’s already fluttered away … over to the fence … or … on the roof … or … somewhere else …?! 
The players let colourful parrots land where another parrot has just been seen before. They should not miss which of the five places the feathered fluttering artists have settled as soon as a spyglass card asks to search for a certain colour combination. Whoever moreover finds the jolly good-luck bird – without encountering the grouchy bad-luck bird – completes their high-flying observations as an outstanding birdwatcher.

—description from the publisher

Dangerous Skies is a fast play set of rules that represent aerial warfare during the Great War. Taking place just a decade after man’s first tentative ‘hops’ into the air, at the start of the war, early aircraft were fragile and prone to structural and mechanical failure. It took a very special breed of men to fly these aircraft, let alone to do anything else whilst actually in the air. Indeed, more crew died as a result of accidents or mechanical failure than enemy action. However by the end of the war, most modern strategies and tactics had evolved, many of which can be traced to modern times.

The rules work well for any number of players up to 20 per side, though approximately 12 players works best. Players each fly a single aircraft, pitting their wits against opponents. Novices can be easy prey to the more experienced pilot, but even they get lucky sometimes, and beware the two-seater observer’s arc of fire.

The rules include nine scenarios representing real events that happened during WWI (which were often one-sided either in numbers, skills or both). Can you do better than the aces mentioned, or will you be another victim of the Red Baron (and become another commemorative cup).

The rules include statistics for 44 Allied and 30 Axis aircraft covering the various theatres of the First World War (more can be requested). The game needs a hex mat (sized according to the aircraft models used) and sets of red and white dice, preferably in closed containers.
Why not research into this fascinating period of aviation history and create your own battles and campaigns.

—description from the designer

family: Series: The Chicken Family of Zoch
Versions & Editions: Dice Versions of Non-Dice Games

description:
Deep beneath the Swiss Alps is a cave system carved out long ago by Gnomes. The gnomes loaded the caves with treasure, but only for those who weren't too greedy. Venture through the caves, collecting treasure along the way, while negotiating with the gnomes to prove you're worthy to take home what you've collected. The gnomes don't take well to greed and will turn on you if you try to take too much of their treasure. Know when to say 'Gnome Ore'.

Gnome Ore is a push your luck dice rolling game where you venture into a cave system. Each roll gets riskier and riskier as you have fewer dice to achieve your goal.

Knowing when to stop awards you with forge cards and allows you to work with the gnomes to turn your collected ore into precious gems. The player with the most gems after the mine collapses wins the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Raksha Bandhan: Celebrating the Bond of Siblings
description:
Embark on a celebratory adventure with Raksha Bandhan: The Journey Home, a captivating two-player board game. Begin by placing your pawns on the edge of the board and rolling the dice to navigate towards the centre, where Rakhi tokens await. 
The youngest player starts, moving their pawn according to the dice roll. Upon reaching the center, collect a Rakhi token and begin your return journey, but with a twist—move only half the number rolled on the dice (rounded down). Beware, as your opponent can use special bead sections for shortcuts and may intercept you to steal your Rakhi token. If attacked, return to the start and try again while the attacker continues their journey back. The first player to successfully return to their starting space with a Rakhi token wins. 
This engaging game blends strategy, competition, and the festive spirit of Raksha Bandhan, making it perfect for family fun.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Fireblade: As Simple and Fair as Possible – Tabletop Wargame Rulebook
description:
In the future, humanity fights for the fate of the universe.

After barely surviving a devastating apocalypse, humanity builds a new civilisation in the future. This civilisation has a bold new purpose; to fight for the weak and downtrodden across the entire universe. To fulfil this new purpose, the mighty Space Knights are created. An unstoppable force of super soldiers, they fight for justice for all.

Fireblade is a tabletop wargame that uses fierce armies to fight in mortal combat. The main goal of the game is to be as simple and fair as possible. It is a great way for new wargamers to learn how to play on a tabletop. Because it's easy to learn but hard to master, it's also great for wargaming veterans.

In Fireblade, you can use any models you want, so if you have an existing army, you don't have to go and buy a new one. However, for those who enjoy painting and collecting miniatures, Fireblade will be offering original models in future. These models will be based on the design and art in the Fireblade rulebook.

One of the main reasons I designed Fireblade was because I found that, some other mainstream wargames were not simple or fair enough for my liking. Their rules could be very complicated and ever changing, making it hard to play the game casually. The rules didn't seem fair in the sense that, some armies were stronger than others and the strongest armies were constantly changing. This was bad when you had to collect and paint a whole army that was once strong but then became weak. The same could be said for individual units. With Fireblade, I have made all of those aspects fair to all players. A beginner has the same chance of winning a game as a veteran. Also in Fireblade, no-one goes first, we fight simultaneously so there is no unfair advantage.

I have also created an entire back story, universe and in depth art which is in the Fireblade rulebook. Fireblade has its own rich universe but you can use any models you want because the universe is a big place.

—description from the publisher

Assemblea de Majaras ("Assembly of Madness") is a tabletop game set in current social and political movements, as well as in the reality that surrounds us. It's a game where you can laugh, cry and (self) criticize, all at the same time.

The game will have so many cards that you won't run out of them. There are two types: some that pose very different situations, and others that respond to these situations. Resulting in fun, but also crazy combinations, others terribly real, sarcastic and even cancelable.

The last person to take minutes in a meeting will show one of the cards that raise an unfinished situation. This situation can be completed in two ways. Either coming up with response letters that give the funniest and funniest situation possible, or completing the sentence with the most accurate (self)criticism ever made.

It will be up to each of the players to choose a card from their hand that completes the sentence and gives the best possible answer!

(yes, that's it, great. If that sounds like Cards Against Humanity, it's because we were completely inspired by it)

—description from the publisher

Un joc ambientat en les dina`miques dels moviments socials i polítics transformadors i, en general, en la realitat social que ens envolta. Humor i crítica ben barrejades per a enaltir les virtuts dels col·lectius i moviments anticapitalistes, o recrear-se en les seves merdes i en les del món on vivim.

El joc és senzill. A cada ronda es plantejarà una carta de situació, i sereu vosaltres qui decidireu respondre-hi amb les vostres millors cartes.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

Grab, Trap and Attack your opponents' crab armies and be sure to defend yours. Strategize to be the last one standing in this clash of crabs!

—description from the publisher

Imagine Angela Merkel, Mona Lisa, and other celebrities are now looking for matches on dating platforms — what would their profiles look like?

Each round in Match Me If You Can! a new celebrity card is revealed, whether a figure from history or a star from today. Use your text cards to create the best profile bio possible, whether charming, absurd, or romantic.

In Simsala Spin, little witches and wizards get to work with cauldrons, but before the best potions can be created, the right ingredients are needed! These ingredients are well hidden, though, and must first be found and uncovered in line with the recipe.

In this co-operative memory game, the position of the potion ingredients you are looking for changes during the game. Anyone who still manages to find all the ingredients for a potion recipe can unlock valuable bonus effects, but you win together only when all the potions have been brewed!
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

boardgame: Kreuz und quer zu jeder Stadt, die ein C&A-Haus hat
description:
The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach space 100 wins the game.

The game was published as a promo giveaway in the 1950s. The promo figure "Onkel Canterbumm" was used till 1957.

—user summary

In Hokus Pictus, you are sorcerer's apprentices who are being taught magical painting, specifically painting with colors that you can't even see!

With the help of a magnetic magic wand and a magic ball, you gradually make the outlines of the picture appear on the easel. The first audience member to guess which motif it is from the magic books wins a star. Once everyone has been artist four times, the player with the most stars wins.

WTF - Write Things Fast
The Game of Wits, Words, and Wild Creativity!

In WTF - Write Things Fast, players flex their creative muscles to craft hilarious, clever, or downright absurd phrases that match a theme—but there’s a twist! Each phrase must fit a random acronym, making every round a mix of quick thinking, word mastery, and unfiltered fun.

How It Works

Each turn, the player in charge flips over three cards, each with a letter and a variety of themes. Using those cards, the player chooses a theme for the round—anything from "Title of a Song" to "Movie Tagline" or "Worst Dating Advice." Once the theme is announced, the challenge begins:

Players must create a phrase where each word starts with the letters on the cards in the order they appear.
Need some flexibility? You’re in luck! Free Words like "and," "the," or short connectors (e.g., “in” or “on”) can be used anytime to help smooth out your masterpiece.
For example:
If the cards reveal R, D, and G and the theme is "Title of a Song," players might come up with:

"Rowdy and Drunk in Germany"
"Radio Destruction with Grenades"
"Rachel's Dances with Greg"

The possibilities are endless—and the fun only grows as the table erupts in laughter over the creative (or chaotic) submissions.

Storytelling for the Win

Once everyone has written their phrase, the spotlight shifts to storytelling! Each player takes a turn sharing their phrase and weaving a quick, creative, and (hopefully) convincing backstory. This is your chance to sell your idea: Why is “Rachel's Dances with Greg” the next viral hit? Why does “Rowdy and Drunk in Germany” belong on every karaoke playlist?

More creativity = more votes = more points!

Vote for the Best

When all the phrases and stories have been revealed, players vote on their favorite. Voting is fast and simple:

On the count of three, everyone points to the player whose phrase they think deserves the win.
The winner collects the theme card as a point.
If there’s a tie, the player who picked the theme gets the final say (and no, they can’t vote for themselves!).

Ramp Up the Chaos

Want an extra challenge? Add a timer! The ticking clock forces players to think on their feet, turning every round into a high-pressure sprint for the most creative (or hilariously bad) phrase.

Winning the Game

Decide your own win conditions:

First to collect 5 points wins.
Play through the entire deck of cards.
Keep going until drinks run out or laughter turns to tears (from joy, of course).
Why You’ll Love WTF

Endless Replayability: With a mix of random letters and a huge variety of themes, no two rounds are ever the same.
Perfect for Any Group: From quick-witted wordsmiths to wild storytellers, everyone can join the fun.
Hilarious Moments: The combinations of letters, themes, and creativity lead to phrases and stories you’ll be laughing about long after the game ends.
Flexible Play: Whether it’s a quick game to five points or a marathon session, you control the experience.
What’s in the Box

Letter Cards: Packed with endless possibilities.
Theme Prompts: Hundreds of options to keep the game fresh.
Rulebook: Clear and simple instructions to get the fun started in minutes.
WTF - Write Things Fast isn’t just a game—it’s an unforgettable experience that challenges your mind, fuels your creativity, and brings everyone together in fits of laughter. Whether you're a word nerd, a creative thinker, or just someone who loves a good time, this game is guaranteed to deliver.

Get ready to write things fast and let your mind go wild!

—description from the publisher

3, 2, 1 — go! In Aliens Unboxed, everyone competes at the same time, trying to form predetermined combinations of the eight different, brightly colored aliens as quickly as possible. To do this, fold and rotate your UFOs — unknown folding objects — until you can fulfill one of the boarding cards on display. Depending on how difficult it is to put together certain alien crews, you will receive 1-5 points. Whoever has the most points at the end wins the check-in challenge.

In City Flip: Roma, all roads lead to Rome...and to St. Peter's Basilica, the Roman Forum, and so on!

In the game, you work together as city planners. Your job is to lay out the cards in a 7x7 grid so that the ten sights are all connected by their streets. When it's your turn, swap the face-up card in your hand for a face-down card, which then becomes the next swap card.

In addition to the direction specifications are various building specifications for individual columns and rows, which vary from game to game. This allows you to rebuild Rome again and again and make the game increasingly difficult.

Things are happening in the laundromat! Who can keep track of everything and say where the right items of clothing are? Are all the T-shirts really in washing machine 3? And where are most of the red items of clothing?

In Waschsalon, you want to carefully watch what is happening in the washing machine drum, but this would be much easier if the other players didn't want to wash their dirty laundry, too.

Since everyone has to wash their laundry, the box also contains a simple version for children and a slightly more challenging version for real washing professionals.

Hands off! is a game that requires quick reflexes and visual discrimination. You compete to find and touch the card that matches the combination rolled on two dice: one for numbers and one for candy. As the game progresses, more and more obstacles are placed in your path, making it difficult to progress and, on the other hand, offering other players the chance to catch up.

With a variable difficulty system, Hands off! ensures balanced gameplay between people of different abilities, making the game accessible and fun for everyone.

Paper Apps GOLF is a pocket-sized golf game.  Each notebook is randomly generated and completely unique. They are 3x5 inches with coil binding, making it a perfect solo roll-and-write travel game!

Each notebook contains 3 golf courses, each with 18 holes, as well as a brief rules section to show you how to play.

Gameplay is simple, yet engaging.

Each hole has randomly-generated terrain, including fairway, rough, water, sand, and trees. You must navigate this to get your ball to the hole in as few strokes as possible. Your path can go over water, but the ball can't land in it. Hitting from the fairway gives you a boost, while hitting from sand shortens your distance.

—description from the publisher

8 power
Opponents cannot play cards with power 4 or less from their hand.

P5/2024

Collect the biggest sets, while cunningly moving the chopsticks to stop other players grabbing the sushi they want. Create the same set as another player to steal one of their cards … but be careful not to lose points by getting the angry wasabi!

—description from the publisher

This is an expansion for Killer Katanas 2.  It deals with the Daimyo Date Masamune and includes information about the Lord, his armies, organization, uniforms, and also includes two scenarios.  There are also several full-color plates showing uniforms, armor, and sahimono/nobori flags.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
This is an expansion for Killer Katanas 2. It deals with the Imjin War, Hideyoshi's invasion of Korea from 1592-97.

The book contains information on the organization, uniforms, armor, and flags of the Ming and Joseon armies, and gives details for how to use them in KK2.  Also included are 7 scenarios.

In Wool Street, you buy, sell, and trade colorful woolen goods in order to end up with the trendiest items in your wardrobe and lose as little as possible on the stock market. Analyze the market in order to act cleverly at the right time!

The wool products that sell fastest in New York are considered the trendiest. The player who has the trendiest wool products in their wardrobe at the end of the game will be the winner.
Setup: Distribute 10 wool cards to each player, two of each colour, and place them on the table in front of you. This is your wardrobe. Shuffle the New York cards, players secretly have a look at some of them, then form a drawing deck and place it in the middle of the table.
Game: Starting with the first player and then clockwise the active player must perform two mandatory actions:
- Draw a New York card from the drawing deck and place it in the middle of the table next (right) to another New York card of the same colour.
- Sell or buy a wool card. To sell, take one card form your wardrobe and place it in the middle of the table, next (left) to the row of New York Cards of the same colour. To buy, take one wool card from the middle of the table and place it in your wardrobe.
The New York cards in the middle of the table represent the sales graph of the various products. As soon as the seventh New York card of the same colour is placed in the middle of the table, assign the highest remaining value card (+2,+1,+1,-1,-2) to that colour.
End: As soon as the drawing deck runs out the game ends. Each wool card in their wardrobe scores points corresponding to the value card assigned to the respective colour. The player with the highest final score wins the game.

Pizza Panic is a Shape-Matching Game for kids with a tasty pizza theme. Kids race against the timer to place 25 different pizza topping pieces onto their matching spaces on the pizza base. Toppings include favourites like pepperoni, mushroom, cheese and bacon, and lets kids prepare make-believe pizzas while practicing shape and pattern recognition skills. Press the centre button to start the timer, then quickly match the different shaped toppings to their spaces before the pretend pizza goes POP!

—description from the publisher

"The defence sets their formation. The offence calls the play and the dice is rolled. Was it a pass or run? Was the play successful, or was there a turnover? The outcome is determined only by the luck of the dice and the skill of the cards."

In Canadian Style Football, players use dice and offense/defence cards to score a touchdown, return a punt, sack a quarterback, or make an interception, all using Canadian football rules to win the game.

Players can choose between a variety of different plays - both offensive and defensive - and roll dice to determine whether or not the play stands and, if so, the resulting yardage.

If the offense fails to gain ten yards in three downs, the play is turned over the the defense.

The game is played until each team has had three offensive possessions which would make up one quarter. The winner is the player or team with the most points after twelve possessions (or four quarters) of play.

The year is 1920 and Spiritualism is sweeping across America, offering curious souls the chance to speak with the dead. The veil between worlds is especially thin at Ouija Manor, where a family of investigators communes with restless spirits, seeking to learn who they were in life. Fragments of obituaries lie scattered among the family's curious possessions. Only by reassembling a spirit's obituary will the investigators discover their identity and help them find eternal peace.

Step into the mystic realm between the living and spirit worlds in this cooperative deduction game. One player takes on the role of a spirit beyond the veil, helping to solve the mystery of their untimely passing - but they cannot speak aloud. To guide the others, they must align their psychic intuition to decode hidden truths throughout the manor and reveal cryptic messages using the Ouija board. Players must determine what caused the spirit’s unfortunate demise to win and bring this lost soul to rest. Recommended for 2-5 players, ages 10 and up.


     Features an easy-to-assemble 3D manor with a stunning Art Nouveau flourish
     Moveable standees let players point to mysterious clues in each room
     Use a real miniature Ouija board to answer questions and decode hidden truths
     Solve the mystery by selecting the correct obituary cards after asking a series of questions to the spirit beyond our realm


—description from the publisher

Players learn about Five senses and which One is relevant to aspecific task. Great for learning to explain attributes of objects!

Each student is given a game board and in lotto-style game format matches the appropriate card to the game board
Great for sensory awareness and for help with describing objects
Grades Pre-K and older.

—description from the publisher

Players travel west to east collection states by answering trivia questions. If you get to the east coast and have the most states, you win! Otherwise you travel up and down the east coast until someone on the east coast has the most states.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Supply and Steel:  A WW2 Logistics Micro Wargame
description:
S&S has been revised.  FCI accepted my suggestions, and the board is bigger - 2-piece - on the pdf, and Mortain, Domfront, and  Prepail are now bocage points as well.  They re-edited their rules.  And FCI added a Luftwaffe piece!

This is a general logistics oriented game about getting supplies to units to fight for or against an Allied breakout.
It starts after D-Day - Cherbourg is US occupied in the setup, shown - and the game is about getting supplies to units for their replenishment after attacking or taking defensive losses.
Supplies are moved by sea and road for the Allies and by road and train for the Germans.  (Trains move them faster but are more vulnerable to air attack.)
Both USAAF and RAF tactical units and also strategic airpower units are in the game, to devastate German ground units and interdict their and their supplies' movement.
Combat units are generic.  There are 4 US infantry and 3 US armor pieces which are to be assembled double-sided - full strength and "spent."
3 British infantry and 2 British armor and 5 German infantry and 3 German panzer/armor.
There are 24 Allied and 18 German supply markers.
To win, the Allied player must capture Caen, Avranches on the western edge and 4 other starred towns on the map which are considered evidence of a breakout.  Any fewer is a German win. 
The game is free to Fight Club International members - and membership is free - to print off and play our own personal copies, but it is not public domain, and the copyright statement says it is not to be "amended" - revised?

I did this BGG entry for the game as a service to Fight Club and the hobby.  I did not design it.
Lou Coatney
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: This is a supplement for Killer Katanas.  It deals with Mori Motonari and incudes details about his life, campaigns, his army, organization, and uniforms.  It includes two scenarios and a few color plates of flags.

This is a supplement for Killer Katanas.  It deals with Shimazu Yoshihisa and incudes details about his life, campaigns, his army, organization, and uniforms.  It includes two scenarios and a few color plates of flags.

This is a supplement for Killer Katanas.  It deals with Tokugawa Ieyasu and incudes details about his life, campaigns, his army, organization, and uniforms.  It includes two scenarios and a few color plates of flags.

This is a supplement for Killer Katanas.  It deals with Toyotomi Hideyoshi and incudes details about his life, campaigns, his army, organization, and uniforms.  It includes two scenarios and a few color plates of flags.

This is a supplement for Killer Katanas.  It deals with Date Masamune and incudes details about his life, campaigns, his army, organization, and uniforms.  It includes two scenarios and flag details.

This is the first version of the book, and it was redone as Date's Battles 2 with color plates.

Grandfather's Gardeners see the sweltering innards of Embergard to be ripe for transformation into a seething cauldron of plagues – especially with the emberstone drawing in so many potential hosts...

The disease-ridden Phleghmus Potbelly leads this warband and sees his duty as a simple one – to spread the blessings of Nurgle across the realms. Helping Phleghmus cultivate such gruesome cycles are his fellow Plaguebearers Slunge, Maggoty Strewg, Bug-eyed Dripterus, and the daemon-fly Squort, who revels in injecting vileness directly into living bodies.

Grandfather's Gardeners are a Mastery-style warband which would synergise well with a Mastery Rivals deck (available separately).

This kit includes a full Grandfather's Gardeners warband for use in games of Warhammer Underworlds, along with fighter cards and warscroll cards for the warband.

—description from the publisher

A thrilling Thai horror-themed board game that will keep you on the edge of your seat!
In a scenario where anyone could be the killer, your last hope lies in the spirit trapped within the glass!

When an old high school friend becomes the victim of a mysterious murder,
a séance using the spirit glass board becomes the key to solving the case.
Will unity and teamwork lead to victory, or will more victims fall?
If you're ready, let the ritual begin!

Spirit Glass is a secret role deduction game perfect for analytical minds.
You'll need cunning, strategy, and sharp wit to gain the upper hand.
But be careful who you trust—each player harbors their own secret agenda.
This game will show you what it truly means to be a 'Friend to the Death.'

Game Play
Setup:  Place the Spirit Board and Glass Cup. The Ghost is revealed, and the Ghost secretly identifies the Murderer.

Phase 1 & 2: Asking and Communicating:  The player with the Funeral Favor Card asks 
an open-ended question. All players place a finger on the glass and close their eyes. 
The Ghost moves the glass to give a clue, using a letter count determined by a die roll. 
The Murderer can secretly attempt to "Suppress the Spirit".

Discussion:  After the clue, players open their eyes and discuss for 60 seconds 
(Sand Timer).

Phase 3: Final Vote:  After everyone has asked, the Ghost initiates the vote: 
"3, 2, 1...Vote!". Players point to their target. The player with the most votes 
is determined, and roles are revealed to check win conditions.

—description from the publisher

A party game about mind reading. One player is asked a question. Other players provide their guesses, and the answering player then ranks those answers. Finally, the other players try to guess the rankings of the answers, and the closer you get, the better you score.

A new Japanese expansion pack for the game 3 Second Try.
The expansion adds 15 new cards with new challenges themed around Osaka.

In 3 Seconds Try, players face either physical or brain challenges in under 3 seconds to score the most points.  
The first player (called "Master") chooses a "Physical" (Dexterity) or "Brain" challenge and reveals the card.
Placers bet on how many times they think they can do the challenge in under 3 seconds, and the highest bidder gets to do the attempt.  
If they can complete the challenge before the ball reaches the end of the slope they win the card.  If not, the next highest bidder gets to try the challenge.

The first player to earn 2 cards wins.

—description from the publisher

Kurnik 2 takes place in a chicken coop. Hens, mice, chicks, and geese are arguing about which season is the best. Players' task is to find as many animals in the coop as possible that enjoy specific seasons. To win, you'll need to rely on a good memory!

—description from the publisher

Nowa gra z serii „Kurnik” – każda z nich ma inne zasady!
Akcja gry toczy się w kurniku. Kury, myszy, kurczaki i gęsi toczą spór o to, która pora roku jest najlepsza. Zadaniem graczy jest odnalezienie w kurniku jak najwięcej zwierząt lubiących odpowiednie pory roku. Aby wygrać, będzie trzeba wykazać się dobrą pamięcią!

—description from the publisher (Polish)

Daga is an agnostic, multiple-setting, symmetrical miniatures game system that offers players the opportunity to create compelling stories and games by fielding a wide variety of miniatures and settings. Thanks to a flexible rulebook, Daga allows you to stage any kind of confrontation, from a zombie apocalypse, to a knightly duel, to a battle between bacteria! Each miniature comes to life, bringing with it an entire fictional universe.

It is the players who choose the setting of their games based on a few simple rules, thus allowing anyone to play with the models they prefer without any limitations. The rulebook also offers a campaign system, to combine multiple confrontations, with rules for evolving one's warband from game to game, similar to a role-playing game.

—description from the designer
